ISRAEL S REASON FOR PRAISE 1:50-55 He is Merciful Mary was able to envision something of the redemptive plan of God Fear: a wholesome dread of offending a holy God 1 Peter 1:17 2 Corinthians 7:1 Puritan Charles Bridges It is that affectionate reverence by which the child of God bends himself humbly and carefully to his Father's law. Mercy gives attention to those in misery Prophetic Aorist God s activities are so certain to take place that it is spoken of as already have taken place. Daniel 2:34-35 Revelation 19:15-16 God will turn the order of society upside down He is Faithful A promise to bless all the families of the earth Malachi 3:6 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(11).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/16/2021 9:16:38 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE HAND OF GOD Luke 1:57-67 INTRODUCTION Mary s visit to Elizabeth gave her Confirmation: Personal, Physical, and Prophetic Her 3 month stay was longer than customary in today s standards. We are not told what happened during the last 6 months of Mary s pregnancy. We can be sure this was a time of special fellowship with the God of her fathers. She gained the confidence and assurance of Joseph as God spoke to him. Joseph protected her from the cruelty of the idle gossipers. Any marriage goes through adjustments; some more than others. This marriage had accepted an immense challenge; the earthly parents of the Messiah It was a special time for these newly-weds. Our narrative takes us back to just outside of Jerusalem and the birth of the forerunner. THE BIRTH OF JOHN 1:57-58 The prediction of Gabriel was fulfilled The birth of any child is a joyful experience but this birth has a little extra. Parents who were far along in years ; a visit from an angel With all of that you can only imagine the thrill of this mom to hold her newborn Her humiliation and shame were gone; no one can doubt her sincerity before God We wonder how many people never heard Elizabeth was even pregnant 1:24 She had hid herself for 5 months We are told the birth of a boy in Palestine was an occasion for great joy. Musicians came to the parent s home Relatives and friends gathered to wait the baby s arrival After the birth, congratulations were mingled with singing and dancing. It is was announced that a girl was born, the party was over. Musicians sadly departed Elizabeth knew double joy; a birth and a birth to a boy 58 it was evident that the Lord was magnifying His mercy Much rejoicing was taking place 1:14 many shall rejoice at his birth THE NAMING OF JOHN 1:59 Circumcision was carried out according to Hebrew regulations In the OT, children were named at birth; this is the first record of naming at circumcision The relatives proposing the name of Zachariah was appropriate, especially considering the circumstances. In normal circumstances, Elizabeth would have probably accepted such Her reaction probably took them by surprise, NO! ; never, not indeed Momma put her foot down and said that is not happening. The name John means God s grace; to be revealed in the birth and ministry of the Messiah 61 Have you ever questioned the naming of a child? What were they thinking? Natural confusion from the family; a great departure from tradition. At that time the name John was not all that common 62 they played gestures they made signs Reminder of Zachariah s unbelief; an unbelief he had to live with for 9 months A constant reminder as he watched the belly of his wife and watch the growing belly of Mary. THE FATHER OF JOHN 1:63-64 We can imagine again the anticipation of those present as Zachariah wrote out the words, John is his name Zachariah corroborated the position of his wife, but his words were even more emphatic Elizabeth, He shall be called John Zachariah, John is his name It was not a matter of debate. The name was God s choice, not his or hers God had named the child through Gabriel before his birth The naming caused the people to marvel, wonder; a word that includes admiration Not wonder as in what is going on; but astonished The expression of faith released his ability to speak The result was spontaneous praise to God The verb indicates he began to speak in praising God and kept on praising God Praise is always the response of a heart in tune with God. The naming of John brought such a reaction; how much more when the dad speaks. THE REACTION TO JOHN 1:65-67 The news of Zachariah s returned speech shook the area; fear It became a topic of discussion in the hills around Jerusalem The same people were probably part of the gossip group when Zachariah exited the temple. For 9 months the people have noted the events Now, his speech is restored and he is adamant about using the name John These are the same people that in another 3 months were going to hear shepherds declare of angels in the nighttime sky with news of another baby boy. They may have still been talking about Zachariah, Elizabeth, and John. 66 There were many things about John that marked him as a special person. The miraculous appearance of the angel Zachariah s affliction of dumbness The miraculous conception of Elizabeth The determination of the parents to name their son John The sudden healing of Zachariah s dumbness The soon to be presented prophetic utterance 67 Praise recognizes God for who and what He is It is a sacrifice that greatly pleases Him (Hebrews 13:15) Here is a spirit of anointed prophecy Speech had been restored to the priest and although he could not present a benediction when leaving the temple , the Holy Spirit anointed him to speak all that was in his heart. THE GOD OF JOHN One thing to remember throughout the narrative: not about Zachariah, Elizabeth, John We need to see God in the whole picture. the hand of the Lord was with him God of Promise Promise to Zachariah Joshua 21:45 (ASV) There failed not aught of any good thing which Jehovah had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass. 1 Kings 8:56 (ASV) Blessed be Jehovah, that hath given rest unto his people Israel, according to all that he promised: there hath not failed one word of all his good promise, which he promised by Moses his servant. Great is Thy Faithfulness We live by promises, not explanations God of Mercy Celebrating God s lovingkindness Baby for Elizabeth Removal of judgment for Zachariah God of Grace Definition of name THE HAND OF GOD Luke 1:57-67 THE BIRTH OF JOHN 1:57-58 Elizabeth knew double joy; a birth and a birth to a boy THE NAMING OF JOHN 1:59 John means God s grace THE FATHER OF JOHN 1:63-64 John is his name The expression of faith released his ability to speak THE REACTION TO JOHN 1:65-67 John as a special person The miraculous appearance of the angel Zachariah s affliction of dumbness The miraculous conception of Elizabeth The determination of the parents to name their son John The sudden healing of Zachariah s dumbness The soon to be presented prophetic utterance THE GOD OF JOHN God of Promise God of Mercy God of Grace Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(12).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/25/2021 11:26:50 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE BENEDICTUS Luke 1:67-80 INTRODUCTION 68-79 The Benedictus; from the first word in the Latin translation Some see these words as the last of Old dispensation prophecy and the first of the New dispensation Like the other songs, we have to be impressed with their handling of the OT What a goal for 2022 to be controlled by the HS which means you must be full of the Word of God Eph. 5:18; Colossians 3:16 Spirit controlled people use Spirit inspired Words. When Jesus did battle with Satan s temptations He did not depend on divine power but divine provisions. Rhema vs. logos Even though a birth is the setting for the prophecy, the new father and elderly priest is directed by the Holy Spirit to give more attention to God s faithfulness and the Messiah s ministry than to John s work as forerunner. Salvation is the theme of Zachariah s prophecy: 1:68 redemption 1:69, 71, 77 salvation 1:74 being delivered THANKSGIVING 1:68-70 His eulogy: blessed ; well spoken of; worthy of praise, adoration Zachariah s song takes on the flavor of many prophets He speaks of the present and future without noting a time lapse he hath visited and redeemed The God-man will soon come as the Messiah; visit; Immanuel: God with us As a believer in Jesus, God has visited you. Not just the idea of visiting but a visit to express concern: Gen. 50:24, 25 take care of He has come to his people and made them free the price has been paid 1 Peter 1:18, 19 rescued them from bondage brought His people deliverance Redemption for Israel will be both spiritual and material, eventually The birth of John was evidence that the work was begun and as good as done (Prophetic Aorist) horn of salvation strange term to us a powerful Savior our victorious Savior a deliverer of victorious power Horn - A description of power, strength, glory Jesus was seen as mild: Matt. 11:29; power seen in arrival on white horse: Rev. 19:11-16 1 Samuel 2:10; 2 Sam. 22:3; Psalm 132:17 The mighty deliverer the Jewish people had anticipated for so long He would be of the descent and sovereignty of David Zachariah and Elizabeth were both of the tribe of Levi The reference applies to Jesus raise up - NET Bible adds "The phrase raised up means for God to bring someone significant onto the scene of history." Elizabeth had already voiced so in honoring Mary Now the same Holy Spirit confirmed this through Zachariah Prophets have been talking of this through the ages Now witness is being given that the time has arrived DELIVERANCE 1:71-75 Think of the enemies Jesus came to conquer: sin, Satan, death. Spiritual enemies are far more deadly than mortal. Israel was surrounded by enemies; a strange hatred lasting to the present time They were only looking for physical deliverance from Rome, hence, they missed the time of their visitation ; He came to His own and His own received Him not A unique challenge for us: How we interpret the events in our own country. We sense losing privileges these people never realized. But do we understand spiritual enemies are more deadly? Are we more concerned with freedom of speech or freedom from sin, Satan, and death? Would be natural to assume Zachariah was speaking of deliverance from Rome But the Holy Spirit is speaking here The overthrow of enemies in a physical sense must be referred to the second coming of Jesus. God would perform His promise to Abraham Genesis 22:15-18; the promise of a seed to bless the earth This promise has been the Jewish hope in times of near extinction All the OT prophets spoke of salvation, a deliverance, a performance of God s mercy and faithfulness that would carry out the oath and promises given to the patriarchs God will deliver He would keep them so they could serve and worship Him without fear. we should serve not the word for slave, but to minister, perform religious service 21x in the NT: Luke 2:37; 4:8 Hebrews 12:28; Revelation 22:3 Zachariah, like other OT prophets, did not see a time gap between the two comings God s purpose in bringing deliverance through the mighty Savior was that they might serve and worship Him in holiness and righteousness. Spiritual deliverance was the first and primary work of the Lord Jesus MINISTRY 1:76, 77 Now a little attention to the baby. Imagine dad stooping down to lift the baby from the cradle. What parent does not want wonderful things for their child when we have no idea? Here is a parent who knows God s calling upon his son. But the emphasis was not on whose son John was Dad addressed the baby simply as child The human relationship was not as important as John s ministry the prophet of the Highest John s function: prepare hearts God was coming back to His people in salvation, restoration, and blessing The aim of John s work: to give the people knowledge of salvation Salvation is the remission of sins Forgiveness: to send away, to dismiss (a debt) But drops of grief can ne er repay, the debt of love I own The people had wrong ideas about God s promised salvation Many supposed God would save them simply because they were His chosen people The real need: to know the Messiah would deliver them from their real problem: sin Holiness: partaker, practical That is why John s message was one of repentance The people must make ready SALVATION AND MERCY 1:78-79 John would accomplish his mission because of God s mercy tender mercy gives us a great Greek work: splagna, spleen; bowels Inward affection; the emotions which come from the gut This is the first occasion in the NT and only one in the gospels; 11x in the NT Colossians 3:12; 1 John 3:17 This same deep compassion would cause the Messiah to come from on high as the Dayspring, the sunrise, the dawn He would bring light into the darkness of human life Christ is that light Light would reveal the horror and stench of sin Light would reveal the way to escape and turn to God Light that would direct to the way of peace: a right standing with God SUMMARY 1:80 John was led into the sever hardship of life in the wilderness; the most barren part of the country He completed his growing up years in the deserts This was his training ground. This was his place of preparation. THE BENEDICTUS Luke 1:67-80 Spirit controlled people use Spirit inspired Words. Salvation is the theme THANKSGIVING 1:68-70 As a believer in Jesus, God has visited you. Redemption for Israel will be spiritual and material Horn - A description of power, strength, glory NET Bible adds "The phrase raised up means for God to bring someone significant onto the scene of history." DELIVERANCE 1:71-75 Jesus came to conquer sin, Satan, death. Spiritual enemies are far more deadly than mortal God would perform His promise to Abraham Genesis 22:15-18 Serve: to minister, perform religious service Luke 2:37; 4:8 Spiritual deliverance was the first and primary work of the Lord Jesus MINISTRY 1:76, 77 The human relationship was not as important as John s ministry Salvation is the remission of sins Forgiveness: to send away, to dismiss Holiness: partaker, practical SALVATION AND MERCY 1:78-79 tender mercy: Inward affection Colossians 3:12; 1 John 3:17 Light would reveal the horror and stench of sin Light would reveal the way to escape and turn to God Light that would direct to the way of peace Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(13).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/31/2021 6:59:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE GOD OF ALL THINGS Luke 2:1-7 INTRODUCTION One of the most familiar passages in Scripture and also one of the most unappreciated It was read last month in about every church, Catholic or Protestant Luke 2: the great miracle of the world; the pinnacle of miracles It is one thing for Christ to be raised from the dead: how can God be dead? It is a far greater wonder that God became flesh. 1 Tim. 3:16 Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh. . . We do not know the date of Jesus birth We do not know the year of Jesus birth The date we use was decided to try and curb paganism already in practice Unfortunately, rather than the spiritual influencing the pagan, the spiritual fell under weight of paganism. However noble the idea of cleaning up the world system, the all-to-often result is compromising within the ranks of righteous. When we had our Awana program we taught a song called Whatever That was even before whatever became the word of the day The Patch the Pirate version went like this: What, what, what, what, what, what, what, Whatever, whenever, wherever, forever, I m ready to serve my God; Whatever, whenever, wherever, forever; I m ready to serve my God; To serve Him in a foreign land or in my own back yard, I ll give my life to Jesus, if it s easy or it s harder than I thought it would be. What, what, what, what, what, what, what, Whatever, whenever, wherever, forever, I m ready to serve my God WHATEVER Tax; properly enrollment , registration; cf. Hebrew 12:23 So this was not the tax per se, but the record of who could be taxed enrollment - registration You know the drill, here we go again. Another politician wanting to know how much they can get away with? You know it s coming. (murmur, grumble) God used the whatevers Creation: Romans 1 Plagues, drought, disease Signs, miracles Stop the sun; raise the dead God used a walking stick in the hands of Moses Here is God using a potential tax to accomplish His plan you have heard it said, it is never right to do wrong in order for a chance to do right God never does wrong, so be careful before we argue about a tax But more importantly, are you willing for God to use the whatever to accomplish His purposes in your life? Even illness, handicap, death? Psalm 31:15 My times are in thy hand a verse I often use at funerals time is one of those whatever God is using in your life whatever year, month, day, hour, second Luke 2:1 was not an unfortunate set of circumstances but divinely directed by God It was not an accident. Proverbs 21:1 the king s heart is in the hand of the Lord, He turns whichever what He wants WHOEVER Caesar Augustus Caesar: a title like king or emperor; Augustus: highly esteemed Caesar transliterated spells Kaisar Born Gaius Octavius in 63 B.C.; he was a nephew of Julius Caesar who took a real liking to the boy and showered him with presents, adopted him at the age of 20, and declared him to be his successor. One year later, Jules got the et tu brute? treatment and Gaius became ruler Mark Anthony married Octavius sister but left her for Cleopatra. The result was a navy battle which Rome easily won in 31 B.C. Shortly thereafter, Mark and Cleopatra did the Romeo and Juliet plunge Gaius was responsible for the peace and roads of the empire Unbeknownst to him, used by God to issue the decree for the enrollment and spreading of the gospel Gaius died 14 A.D. and ruled for about 45 years Cyrenius Some honest controversy here as he is known to be governor in 6-7 A.D. 3 possibilities Luke made a mistake. If he had it would have been widely exposed The Greek word for first could be translated before Cyrenius was governor on 2 occasions. God used the whoevers Abraham: Joshua 24:2 they served other gods Pharaoh: Ex. 10:1 that I might show these my signs before him Cyryus: 2 Chron, 36:23 he has charged me to build Him a house in Jerusalem Nebuchadnezzar: Daniel 5:27 you are weighed in the balances, and are found wanting. These men were rulers but God was in charge. History is His Story God promised the Savior would be human not an angel: Genesis 3:15 would be a Jew: Genesis 12:1-3 from the tribe of Judah: Genesis 49:10 from the family of David; 2 Samuel 7:1-17 born of a virgin: Isaiah 7:14 born in Bethlehem: Micah 5:2 WHEREVER Bethlehem; the house of David In most of the empire, registration took place wherever people were located In Israel: the genealogical records of families in Judah were traditionally kept in their ancestral home towns We are probably right to assume it was a Roman decree and the gossiping tongues of Nazareth that brought Mary to Bethlehem Bethlehem: house of bread as the site of the birth of the bread of life Micah 5:2 little to be among the clans of Judah From you One will go forth for Me to be ruler in Israel. Rejected but will rule at His second coming How did the Jewish writers interpret Micah 5:2 ? James Gloag observes that even "Jewish writers in general have adopted the Messianic application of this passage. They, however, avoid the reference to Jesus by supposing that Bethlehem is mentioned here as the birth-place of the Messiah only indirectly, denoting merely that he was to be descended from David; and the eternal duration here mentioned alludes not to the person but to the name of the Messiah. According to the rabbinical fancies, there were seven things created before the world existed; and one of these is the name of the Messiah. Other Jewish writers grant that Bethlehem is to be the birth-place of the Messiah, but they regard the prophecy as still unfulfilled, and look forward to its accomplishment in the future." TRANSITION: WHATEVER, WHOEVER, WHEREVER WHENEVER in those days Galatians 4:4 when the fullness of time came The days of Gabriel visiting Zachariah and Mary The days of the birth of John: only 3 months before The days of God directly communicating with man after 400 years with no prophet 500 years since a miracle had been performed the day had come that she should give birth God knew the timing; He directed Caesar to the decree God knew the timing between the giving of the decree and it being carried out in Judea God knew Micah 5:2 God was doing all these things in which few people participated The overwhelming population had no clue about the birth of a forerunner The masses had little regard for what took place in the stable other than an initial wow Folks, we have no idea what God is doing behind the scenes The Jews of Luke 2 only saw taxation without representation The Jews of Luke 2 only saw the major inconvenience of travel The antichrist could be alive and plotting his rise to power Some disease could be presently attacking your body Your departure from this earth could be immanent Do we fret over the unknowable? In no way If there was ever something above your pay grade that it is We know God. We love God. We trust God. We walk by faith. Just like Joseph, Mary and many others we will meet in this gospel Whatever, Whoever, Wherever, Whenever I m ready to serve my God. CONCLUSION There are a lot of people who look at Luke 2 as a nice story, even a great story, but see little of how it impacts the problems they are facing. Other will look at the story and be more interested in what s for lunch than their eternal destiny. IT IS WELL The story doesn t end with the writing of a hymn. Spafford belonged to a Presbyterian church, and they considered the tragedies that his family had gone through as some sort of divine punishment from God. Spafford disagreed. He formed his own group called The Overcomers and in 1881 set out for Jerusalem. They would form what would be known as The American Colony in Jerusalem. For the next seventy years, this group would be used to relieve the suffering of all those living in Jerusalem Jews, Muslims, and Christians, through the running of soup kitchens, hospitals, orphanages, and other charitable ventures. When WWI hit Palestine, they were an important source of aid and were trusted by all sides of the conflict. This group that had 70 years of healthy, productive, compassionate ministry, was birthed by a man who had endured great pain. Beloved, God knows how to get you where you need to be. When things don t make sense or seem out of control, God knows all about it. You can trust Him. THE GOD OF ALL THINGS Luke 2:1-7 WHATEVER enrollment - registration God using a potential tax to accomplish His plan Psalm 31:15 Proverbs 21:1 WHOEVER Caesar: king or emperor; Augustus: highly esteemed Gaius was responsible for the peace and roads of the empire Cyrenius 3 possibilities Luke made a mistake The Greek word for first could be translated before Cyrenius was governor on 2 occasions. These men were rulers but God was in charge. WHEREVER Bethlehem; the house of David Bethlehem: house of bread Micah 5:2 WHENEVER Galatians 4:4 God knew the timing; He directed Caesar to the decree God was doing all these things in which few people participated We have no idea what God is doing behind the scenes We know God. We love God. We trust God. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(14).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 1/8/2022 11:37:02 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHY SHEPHERDS? Luke 2:8-20 INTRODUCTION Some of us remember the days when it took a while for messages to be shared. Even the most significant of messages took a while to get around. We were limited to either snail mail or phone calls. Regular postal service was cheap but still took a few days. Phone calls were fine if local but you were careful of how many long distance calls you made. And long distance could only mean a few miles by road. And for those on a party line, you may have to wait for your turn on the teli. Of course, the quickest way at that time was in the phrase: telegraph, telephone, tell-a-woman. You have learned through trial and error who to avoid if you possess sensitive information. Some people cannot keep news to themselves, even if they need to share it as a prayer request. When you have a message that needs to be swiftly and accurately communicated on whom do you depend? In December 1903, after many attempts, the Wright brothers were successful in getting their flying machine off the ground. Thrilled, they telegraphed this message to their sister Katherine: We have actually flown 120 feet. Will be home for Christmas. Katherine hurried to the editor of the local newspaper and showed him the message. He glanced at it and said, How nice. The boys will be home for Christmas. Another part of the incarnation story that has been read and performed in a play is the account of the angels visiting the shepherds. It is to these outdoorsmen that the message of Jesus arrival is given. The question that has entertained my thoughts these days of message preparation has been Why Shepherds? Of the possible recipients to first hear the message and specially to receive a visit from angels, why these guys? Why depend on the lowly shepherds? Why depend on those who were unlearned, unskilled, smelly from sheep, campfires and poor hygiene? If you really want others to hear and respond would not those who are somewhat in authority or at least respected by the most desirable? The core of the message is 2:10, 11 Be not afraid; for behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy which shall be to all the people: for there is born to you this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord. Our passage yields itself to a simple outline: Receiving the Message; Reciting the Message, Response to the Message But with the question of Why Shepherds? , we will take a different approach. The Picture; The Pursuers; The Proclaimers; The Profiters THE PICTURE Shepherds were told about the Shepherd It is commonly thought these shepherds were engaged the raising of lambs for temple sacrifices. The proximity to Jerusalem and appropriate landscape provided the right terrain for such a job. Many have considered this from the perspective of determining the time of Jesus birth They suggest spring was lambing season so naturally Jesus was born then But because sacrifices were presently year round, the raising of sacrificial lambs took all year. So no determination can be arrived from that Note the wording: Savior deliverer, not a judge, not even a king; 1:77 Christ christos, anointed one, Messiah Lord master, supreme one, Jehovah God the title used most often by Luke in describing Jesus What better group to present the news of Jesus? These were the outcasts; the bottom of the social ladder, only above lepers Ceremonially unclean; their job never permitted Sabbath worship God is not depending on professionals to talk about Jesus You are an ambassador The place of duty became the place of vision If God could use uneducated sheep herders, He can and wants to use you. THE PURSUERS with haste earnestly, speedily, diligently The first Christmas rush. Did not stop to pray about it. They went. found Only 2x in NT; Acts 21:4 They received the message, went and found The sign they were looking for was not swaddling clothes, but a babe in a manger We know the city was crowded We know there was more than one stable in the city God knew He would need someone who was used to going into thistles and brambles to retrieve an animal. Men who were acquainted to losing sleep to protect a flock. Men who traversed into ravines and crevices. Many would have too easily given up the search; would have called it off Too much work, too many obstacles Scripture: Ask, seek, knock (Lu 11:9) Call, I will answer and show (Jer. 33:3) Seek and find (Mt. 7:7) Seek first the kingdom (Mt. 6:33) Following Jesus is about cross-bearing; about losing your life to find it; giving your life as a living sacrifice; walking the narrow road Sweeping out the house until you find that which is lost Why shepherds? They knew about sacrificial lambs; they knew how to seek and find THE PROCLAIMERS 2:17 they made know abroad to certify, make known Only NT usage They made known the utterance (rhema) What is interesting is they did not get caught up in what they experienced It does not say, you should have seen what we saw we saw angels the sky lit up with all kinds of angels The angels were there for the creation of the first Adam And they are there for the birth of the Second. It was not about their experience They were faithful to the message delivered to them They could confirm the substance of the message Shepherds were not permitted to testify in court because of their low status, but God used them as testifiers of the birth. I am not suggesting that no mention was ever made about angelic beings But the text is clear: they made know the utterance Savior, Anointed One, God Your experience as a Christian is important. You have learned things only as you have walked, victories, defeats But somewhere utterance must be given Somewhere the gospel must be presented You can and should model Jesus in your daily life You can and should be able to give thus saith the Lord when the door is open Shepherds were faithful to the given message THE PROFITERS Some wondered marveled, had admiration 46x NT; 42x gospels, Acts, Rev. 1:21, 63; 2:33; 4:22; 7:9 There is a big difference between admiration and belief Mary pondered 2:19 to throw around in your mind; to mentally consider To confer with yourself or others (Acts 4:15) Moms can remember many things about their children. Shepherds glorifying magnify, honor, make glorious; to give a proper estimate of something praising - 9x NT; 7x by Luke 2:13 the work of angels 19:37 entrance on Palm Sunday 24:53 Acts 2:47 Acts 3:8, 9 walking and leaping and praising God There were a limited few who immediately were moved by Jesus appearance We know many are called but few are chosen (Matt. 22:14) We know 1 Corinthians 1:26 how that not many wise men after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called: That to me is why Shepherds . To remind us that we don t have to be a somebody or to obtain some status position God will be absolutely delighted to be the object of your pursuit, the subject of your proclamation, and the recipient of your praise. Lenski adds "The question is still asked skeptically as to why these shepherds should have been selected for the angel s announcement. The answer is as simple to the believer as it ever was: because God found them the kind of people to whom he could communicate such news." Good news which shall be for all people Next to the Incarnation, I know of no more staggering truth than that a sovereign God has ordained my participation. - Elisabeth Elliot Heaven s choir came down to sing When heaven s King came down to save. WHY SHEPHERDS? Luke 2:8-20 THE PICTURE Savior deliverer Christ anointed one Lord Jehovah God God is not depending on professionals to talk about Jesus The place of duty became the place of vision THE PURSUERS with haste earnestly The sign they were looking for was a babe in a manger Following Jesus is about cross-bearing THE PROCLAIMERS 2:17 They made known the utterance They were faithful to the message delivered to them THE PROFITERS wondered marveled Mary pondered glorifying magnify, honor 1 Corinthians 1:26 God found them the kind of people to whom he could communicate such news." Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(15).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 1/21/2022 7:19:41 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message BUCKET LIST Luke 2:25-35 INTRODUCTION DISPOSTION 2:25 And behold worded like it is a surprise, but really just God at work Simeon: nothing else known about him Often suggested he is old (ready to die) but Scripture is silent Just righteous, upright, honest In dealings with other people Devout pious, reverent, religious In relationship to God Holy Spirit on him continually on him; most rare for an OT character Genesis 41:38; Daniel 4:8; Numbers 11:17 An indication not only of his prophetic empowerment but also of his ongoing devout character all of this while waiting for the consolation of Israel constantly expecting the Messiah to come patiently expecting look expectantly toward Waiting an interesting word: to receive, to accept; by implication to await with confidence; 2:38; 15:2 receive Looking for the one who would bring comfort to Israel paraclete = comfort INSPIRATION 2:26-33 Vs. 25-27 Simeon is associated with the Holy Spirit 3 times Simeon is the fourth Spirit-inspired witness to Jesus John, 1:15, 41; Elizabeth, 1:41; Zechariah, 1:67 revealed divine instruction Matt. 1:21 Joseph being warned by God Heb. 8:5 Moses was admonished by God when about to build the tabernacle We are not told how God communicated to Simeon He should see the Christ of the Lord literally the anointed of God Substituting Lord for Jehovah was customary out of reverence The Anointed One, specially empowered, to fulfill the promise of God to Israel through Daivd Led by God to go into the temple area while Jesus was there Led by God to go and say Taking Jesus into his arms Nunc Dimittus = Now lettest thou depart Depart loosing; dismissal; a common picture of death now I can die content thou dost in peace dismiss thy servant servant bondslave Literally: a slave of the despot (master) Simeon considered himself a watchman who had successfully carried out his duty 2:30 The reason for his peaceful state Salvation was not only something Jesus did, but it was who He was Jesus was and is the embodiment of salvation 1 John 1:3, 4 REVELATION 2:32-35 To the Nation 2:32, 33 The song, or prayer, foretells God s extension of His kingdom to include the Gentiles God is to include all peoples: Acts 8:15-17; 10:1-11:18 OT reference: Isaiah 49:6 A light of the nations so that My salvation may reach to the end of the earth Will see again in 3:4-6 as Luke quotes Isa. 40:3-5 Salvation in v. 30 is expressed in terms of a light of revelation to the Gentiles and the glory of Israel Light is used to express revelation Isa. 52:10 This revelation is an unveiling of the plan of salvation to the Gentiles v. 32 a light to lighten - revelation apokalipse the light provides revelation to the Gentiles and reveals the glory of Israel the Jews had a previous understanding of salvation which the Gentiles did not enjoy Isaiah 46:13; Acts 13:46; Romans 1:16, 2:10 John 1:14 The parents marveled 1:21, 63; 2:18 13x in this gospel They already knew the specialness of their Son But it was new information Bit by bit they are putting the pieces together Now they know that Jesus work would transcend Palestine and encompass the whole world To the Parents 2:34, 35 Initial blessing to both then directly to Mary the fall and rising could be speaking of just one people or two If one: unless people lose all pride in their spiritual achievement there is no place for them. They must fall and then they will rise (a word used for the resurrection) If two: Jesus will divide people: those who reject Him will fall; those who receive Him will rise He will be spoken against The coming of Jesus would mean salvation and exaltation for some and judgment and destruction for others Jesus would be a sign of salvation, yet a sign that would be rejected by man The rejection would pierce like the heart of Mary It would be a sword not a little dagger ILLUSTRATION We are also waiters Titus 2:13 looking for the blessed hope 2:11 God has given us the grace to prepare ourselves His program: deny ungodliness, worldly lusts; live soberly, righteously, godly Sounds like Simeon: just , devout 2 Peter 3:12-14 Simeon s disposition did not merit salvation but the result of salvation. Mine eyes have seen thy salvation Do you know Jesus as Savior? Are you fully trusting in His grace this hour? I have a pastor friend of a baptismal regeneration persuasion. Each we week we text a key verse or verses for the Morning sermon. His last week was from Ephesians 2; by grace are ye saved So I wrote back, so are we saved by grace or works? His answer, yes It is not either or. If you are trusting in your own good works, moral life, basically anything other than grace; there is no salvation. Salvation is of God. Period. Am I looking for the return of the Lord Jesus? BUCKET LIST Luke 2:25-35 DISPOSTION 2:25 Just upright In dealings with other people Devout reverent In relationship to God constantly expecting the Messiah to come INSPIRATION 2:26-33 Simeon is the fourth Spirit-inspired witness to Jesus Nunc Dimittus = Now lettest thou depart Depart loosing Salvation was not only something Jesus did, but it was who He was 1 John 1:3, 4 REVELATION 2:32-35 To the Nation 2:32, 33 God is to include all peoples Isaiah 49:6 Light is used to express revelation Isaiah 52:10 To the Parents 2:34, 35 The coming of Jesus would mean salvation and exaltation for some and judgment and destruction for others ILLUSTRATION Titus 2:13 2 Peter 3:12-14 Am I looking for the return of the Lord Jesus? Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(16).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 2/4/2022 3:24:17 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message NEXT WITNESS Luke 2:36-40 INTRODUCTION Last Sunday we looked at Simeon as he looked at Jesus Mine eyes have seen thy salvation many looked upon the adult Jesus and saw no such thing Simeon was looking at a carpenter, a mother, and a baby: his HS vision saw salvation There was no halo over Jesus head It was the eyes of faith that saw the light of the world, the lamb of God Salvation is a person Jesus is set for the rising and falling; A. T. Robertson: Jesus is the magnet of the ages. He draws some, He repels others. Jesus is a sign: a distinguishing mark by which something is known; a pointer to aid insight Here is a family of devoted Jews. Joseph = righteous; Mary = highly favored Offering the appointed sacrifice; paying the proper shekels At the name of Jesus, the thoughts of hearts are continually exposed what think ye of Christ? is still the most important question for anyone to answer Spurgeon - Men s hearts can conceal their thoughts until Christ s cross comes near; then the old enmity rises up, the heart rebels, and we see what is really in men s hearts. HUMBLE LIFE Witnesses: Angels, Elizabeth, Zechariah, John, Simeon Anna is the Greek equivalent of the Hebrews Hannah favored, mercy Note of tribe: tribute to Luke s research Tribe is not really lost Asher is one of the Northern 10 tribes of Israel that head been deported to Assyria in 722 BC but clearly some from this tribe had returned to the land of Israel. The tribe of Asher does not stand out in Old Testament history Simeon was a man who prophesied; Anna was a woman who bore the title, prophetess Lenski: The title does not mean that she constantly foretold the future, or that she put herself forward publicly, but that she knew and interpreted the Word and the will of God This in light of what we call the 400 silent years advanced in many days Married for 7 years; widow for 84 years If married in mid-teens, she would be over 100; maybe 106 Some believe 84 is her age but that is not the obvious reading Such longevity is unusual but by no means impossible she is the perfect refutation to the one who says, But I am too old to serve the Lord In Scripture: God did some amazing things through old people: Abraham, Sarah, Zachariah, Elizabeth Man s chief end is to glorify God, and to enjoy him forever It does not say the young person; healthy person; rich person The elderly of that day were more honored than our society Remember Gov. Richard Lamm of Colorado; (died July, 2021) in the context of limited resources said, old people have a duty to die and get out of the way. (1984) Woman who remained celibate after the death of their husbands and who devoted themselves to God were held in high esteem among both the Jews and the Early Christian Church 1 Corinthians 7:7, 8; 1 Timothy 5:5, 16 Anna appears to be an early example to the honored Christian widows who in their later years were supported by the Church as they performed good works with prayer and supplication. 1Tim. 5:16 Luke often notes the importance of prayer and fasting in relation to divine visitation and revelations; Acts 9:9, 11; 13:2; Luke 3:21, 22 Luke 18:1-8 presents a widow as an example of constancy in prayer who never left the temple literally or figuratively ( kept on not leaving ) imperfect Probably means that she never missed a service; frequented all the temple services and prayed there often Night and day does not necessarily mean an incessant 24 hour day It probably means habitual, consistent, daily worship (Luke s usage in Acts 26:7) Anna was the poster child for a disciplined life fastings and prayers plural; many and often Practiced self-denial; crucifying the flesh Anna was not holy because she fasted and prayed; she did these things because of her holiness HAPPY HOUR 2:38 Luke notes the timing and orchestration of witnesses by the HS At the instant Simeon finished, Anna gave her prophetic Amen Giving thanks to the Lord for the revelation of His Messiah Like the shepherds, the response is praise 2:18 The appropriate response to God s salvation is praise and thanks 17:11-19 Joined the company of witnesses to speak continually; imperfect tense Over and over again she is praising and thanking God It was the natural overflow of her heart Her audience: looking for the redemption of Jerusalem Hearts who were prepared to see Him; those who were looking Here is a group of old saints preparing for the coming Messiah No doubt regular fellowship among them and here is their happy hour Looking for to receive readily; to receive with open arms Same word in v. 25 waiting for Are you looking for or lackadaisical? G Campbell Morgan who said "I never begin my work in the morning without thinking that perhaps He may interrupt my work and begin His own. I am not looking for death, I am looking for Him." Redemption: implies 3 things Bondage, enslaved; a free man needs no redemption Cost: price paid Ownership; a redeemed person in under new ownership A life spent for God is a life well-lived. HIDDEN YEARS 2:39 performed completed, finished not legalist, but law abiders The emphasis: the saintly piety of the Jewish parents of Jesus; Luke makes it clear that Jesus was immersed in the best spirit of Judaism which anticipated the rise of Christianity Acts 2:14-21 The family returns to Nazareth No mention death by infants or flight to Egypt Matt. 2:13-18 40 Summary of Jesus childhood is similar to the one Luke gives for John childhood 1:80 You have to read apocryphal books for fanciful tales about Jesus childhood Scripture is silent Gospel of Thomas is an example: Jesus as a child doing fantastic miracles and terrorizing all of Nazareth The uneventful childhood of Jesus fits in better with the faithless reception that Nazareth gave Him 4:18-32 Increasing became strong; continually increasing Grace: favor and blessing 2:52 Jesus did no miracles as a boy; He grew as a boy; was perfect as a boy He grew physically as well as any body can grow He waxed strong in spirit as well as any could grow He was filled with wisdom as well as any could be filled CONCLUSION Next Witness: it is now our turn. We love old people here. NEXT WITNESS Luke 2:36-40 HUMBLE LIFE 2:36, 37 Anna Hannah favored, mercy God did some amazing things through old people The elderly of that day was more honored than our society 1 Corinthians 7:7, 8; 1 Timothy 5:5, 16 Luke 18:1-8 Anna was the poster child for a disciplined life HAPPY HOUR 2:38 The appropriate response to God s salvation is praise and thanks 17:11-19 Hearts who were prepared to see Him Looking for to receive readily Redemption: Bondage Cost Ownership HIDDEN YEARS 2:39 emphasis: the saintly piety of the Jewish parents of Jesus The uneventful childhood of Jesus fits in better with the faithless reception that Nazareth gave Him 4:18-32 Jesus did no miracles as a boy He grew as a boy He was perfect as a boy Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(17).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 2/12/2022 11:05:38 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message ROAD TRIP Luke 2:41-52 INTRODUCTION No other child had ever been or ever will be perfect in growth at the various stages of childhood, but the Christ-child was. He grew as well as a child can grow: filled perfectly with all the qualities that fill a child. Christ needed to set a striking example for every person, no matter the age, even for children. Phil. 2:3-8 Christ needed to demonstrate a striking truth to all men: no person can enter heaven unless they first become as a little child. Matt. 18:3, 4 Christ needed to experience every situation, condition, and trial of man in order to become the Perfect Sympathizer or Savior. Heb. 4:15 He experienced the most humiliating experiences possible. He experienced conceived by an unwed mother (Mt. 1:18 19 ). being born in a stable, the worst of conditions (Lu. 2:7 ). being born to poor parents (Lu. 2:24 ). having his life threatened as a baby (Mt. 2:13f ). being the cause of unimaginable sorrow (Mt. 2:16f ). having to be moved and shifted as a baby (Mt. 2:13f ). being reared in a despicable place, Nazareth (Lu. 2:39 ). having His father die during His youth (see note, Mt. 13:53 58 ). having to support His mother and brothers and sisters (see note, Mt. 13:53 58 ). having no home, not even a place to lay His head (Mt. 8:20 ; Lu. 9:58 ). being hated and opposed by religionists (Mk. 14:1 2 ). being charged with insanity (Mk. 3:21 ). being charged with demon possession (Mk. 3:22 ). being opposed by His own family (Mk. 3:31 32 ). being rejected, hated, and opposed by listeners (Mt. 13:53 58 ; Lu. 4:28 29 ). being betrayed by a close friend (Mk. 14:10 11 , 18 ). being left alone, rejected, and forsaken by all of His friends (Mk. 14:50 ). being tried before the high court of the land on the charge of treason (Jn. 18:33 ). being executed by crucifixion, the worst possible death (Jn. 19:16f ). DEVOTION 2:41-45 All able-bodied Jews were to attend the Passover and Feast of Unleavened Bread Deuteronomy 16:16 (Passover, Pentecost, Feast of Booths) By this the time of Jesus, it became customary for those at a greater distance to only attend one festival 16:1-4 Passover celebration was 1 week We already saw the saintly piety of this family 2:39 Dad, mom and family went to worship Jewish boys were increasingly exposed to the study of Scriptures and religious ceremonies as they approached the age of 12 when they would take up their responsibilities as men of the Jewish community 42 Jewish boys became sons of the Law at age 12 Then considered adult members of the Jewish community and were obligated to keep the Law Bar Mitzvah: son of the commandments No record if this is Jesus first visit to the temple What we know of the family would suggest yearly visits 43, 44 Strange to us that the parents did not know the whereabouts of their son Probably safe to assume there were other children in the family by this time to occupy their attention They also probably were part of a caravan from the community Men and women traveled in separate groups Group travel would provide some security from potential robbers or inhospitable Jews 45 The continual search is on up and down, careful search The panic of a parent in losing a child A foretaste of the ultimate pain prophesied by Simeon 2:35 Jesus was not being disobedient, not being mischievous; more the presumption of the parents. DEDICATION 2:46-50 Were they looking for 3 days in Jerusalem or for a total of three days: 2 days in travel and one day in the city Jesus was sitting with the teachers; not teaching the teachers Jesus would later be called a rabbi: John 1:38-49; 3:2 The temple was more than the place for sacrifices; also a teaching ministry Teaching was done in the outer courts or rooms of the temple 47 Only here does Luke use didaskalos to refer to Jewish teachers; in the rest of his gospel the term is reserved for John the Baptist (Lk 3:12 + ) or Jesus. No one is called teacher after the title is given to Jesus The teachers were continually amazed of Jesus spiritual perception Amazed: stand out from; they (Joseph and Mary) stood out of themselves as if their eyes were bulging out." (A T Robertson) Luke uses the verb to show reaction to a supernatural event (2:47; 4:22, 25, 38; 8:56: 24:22, 41) No miracle involved but the teachers knew they were not just in the presence of a brilliant young student They recognized wisdom 2:40 says the grace of God was upon Him Kenosis: Phil 2:5-11; Acts 1:6, 7 Emptied Himself to some extent of His divine attributes though not of His divine essence Jesus learned these things through the Holy Spirit 2:40 Jesus learned over a period of time 48 How do you spell relief? Here is a discovery after diligent search Astonished to find Him in the temple Relieved with joy, yet confounded Joy, relief, anger, fear, frustration, confusion; whole range of emotions Rebuke for insensitivity You have acted like a child Grieving, your father and mother searched for you Grieving, distressed, sorrowing; express anguish and torment 49 Jesus first recorded words. why didn t you know where to look? Response may have brought more confusion Second question: added more perplexity Confirmation from Jesus Himself that He is the Son of God It was necessary under divine compulsion; divine timetable must 4:43; 9:22; 24:7, 26, 44 (40x in gospel and Acts) He heard the call of His heavenly Father and obeyed (14:26) Great subject of discussion: when and how did Jesus know about His role as Messiah We now know that by the age of 12 He knew His destiny though still might not know all that it entailed Jesus is setting the priority of His heavenly Father over His earthly family Luke 14:26 Jesus is setting the stage of His eventual departure from the family. John MacArthur comments that Jesus' having to be present in His Father's house "is not only the crux of the passage, but also expresses the definitive reality of Christian theology. This statement is the first time in Scripture that any individual claimed God as his personal Father. The Jews viewed God as the Father of all in a creative sense, and the Father of Israel in a national sense. But no one had the audacity to claim God as his Father in a personal, intimate sense, because of the profound implications of such a claim. In this confession, Jesus made it clear that His first priority was to do the will of His heavenly Father. where else would you expect to find me other than tending to the things of My Father 50 Parents are in a fog. They have heard divine witnesses; they have heard from their Son They did not understand all the particulars The true role of Jesus as Messiah would catch the entire Jewish nation by surprise And even though Mary knew of her Son s position as Messiah, the full scope of His deity was still foreign to her. DUTY 2:51, 52 The true meaning eluded all of Jesus followers until after the resurrection and ascension of Jesus and Pentecost, at which time Mary along with the other disciples received divine empowering Acts 1:14; 2:4 52 Fulfilling all aspects of the Law Commandment 5 Parallel in 2:40 This does not mean that Jesus merely grew taller or matured into manhood A mental, spiritual, and sociological maturity is emphasized as well This is the last mention of Joseph (Matt. 13:55) treasured these things Carefully remembered Simeon told her it would be like this 2:52 is the summary from age 12 to baptism in honoring the deity of Jesus we cannot minimize His humanity kept increasing to cut down in front; remove obstacles that hinder progress Grace: favor Working definition: the desire and ability to do the will of God 1 Cor. 15:10 Even as a 12 year old, Jesus provides a life to imitate Nazareth: It matters not where you grow up but how you grow up What we become is more important than where we are from. ROAD TRIP Luke 2:41-52 DEVOTION 2:41-45 Deuteronomy 16:16 Bar Mitzvah: son of the commandments Men and women traveled in separate groups Jesus was not being disobedient DEDICATION 2:46-50 Jesus was sitting with the teachers; not teaching the teachers No one is called teacher after the title is given to Jesus The teachers were continually amazed of Jesus spiritual perception Luke uses the verb to show reaction to a supernatural event Jesus first recorded words Confirmation from Jesus Himself that He is the Son of God He heard the call of His heavenly Father and obeyed Jesus is setting the priority of His heavenly Father over His earthly family Jesus is setting the stage of His eventual departure from the family. This statement is the first time in Scripture that any individual claimed God as his personal Father - John MacArthur DUTY 2:51, 52 This is the last mention of Joseph (Matt. 13:55) 2:52 is the summary from age 12 to baptism kept increasing remove obstacles that hinder progress Grace: the desire and ability to do the will of God 1 Cor. 15:10 It matters not where you grow up but how you grow up What we become is more important than where we are from. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(18).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 2/18/2022 2:26:39 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE PRESENTATION OF THE SON OF MAN THE FORERUNNER 3:1-20 Introduction to John s Ministry 3:1-6 John s ministry is a carefully worded composition, whereas the Gospels of Matthew and Mark abruptly introduce John. This is consistent with the introduction in 1:1-4 HISTORY 3:1, 2 Names of rulers to assist in dating Names of rulers who were influenced and affected *Philip, Herod s brother, was included in the list because Jesus spent a short time of His ministry in his realm. *Herod was responsible for the imprisonment and death of John who had condemned him for marrying Herodias, Philip s wife. The Roman emperor at this time was *Tiberius; son-in-law to Augustus Hard to be specific on the year even though stated as 15 year of reign Is it dated from elevation to co-regency? Is it dated from the death of Augustus? 25-29 A.D. is where most commentators lean *Pilate: 26-36 A.D. Tetrarch: ruler of the fourth part of a country Itoura?a, ee-too-rah'-yah region NE of Israel Trachon tis, trakh-o-nee'-tis (heap of stones) Abilen?, ab-ee-lay-nay' The chief priests on the list had an active role in Jesus arrest and trial chapt. 23, 24 All done as Luke is a meticulous historian concerned with accuracy Used by the Holy Spirit to produce this masterpiece of literature. The list speaks of the wickedness of the leaders The state of things when John began his public ministry J. C. Ryle: never to despair about the cause of God's truth, however black and unfavorable its prospects may appear. Let us beware of slacking our hands from any work of God, because of the wickedness of the times, or the number and power of our adversaries. " 3:2 Here is a word from God in the days of ritualistic religion In day of self-centeredness and self-gratification Annas and Caiaphas: Caiaphas was the present priest; Annas still exerted some influence John is not called the Baptist but Zachariah s son Reminder of 1:67-79 John is described as Holy Spirit empowered LOCALITY 3:1, 2 When John came Wilderness ministry: also in Matthew and Mark John was filled with the Holy Spirit; 1:15, 44 Now to perform the ministry for which he was called Ephesians and Colossians both state an evidence of being filled with the Holy Spirit is to speak Literally: the word of God came upon John not to John but upon Him Pressed down; pressure from above Not something mystical or miraculous but a strong inner sense Anyone standing behind the sacred desk should have such a compulsion God has given something to say; not the speaker s words but God s Word God s Word is not warm, fuzzy, and popular; not for the pleasing of man but God Not like the politician who responded to a highly charged bill: I have friends who are for it and friends who are against it, and I am with my friends. Word rhema; a specific utterance; a definite meaning Used 17x in gospel; 9x in chapters 1 and 2 John was a preacher with great courage Matthew 11:11 Jesus said there was no one greater than John; we cannot ignore him John Morris: he was the first Christian, the first Christian witness, the first Christian preacher, the first Christian prophet, and, finally, the first Christian martyr. God filled John and John spoke How is your voice? A word about the wilderness (Luke 1:80, deserts; same word) A word meaning desolate, solitary, lonely John was not in the temple when God spoke, but the hot desert A full day s journey from Jerusalem; a strange location to announce the Messiah 1 Cointhians 1:26-29 It is difficult to describe adequately the foreboding desolation and howling barrenness along the shores of the Dead Sea. If there could be fixed in one s mind the image of the almost-painful sterility of the Sahara or of Death Valley, and then multiply that by a factor of four or more, one might come close to capturing the geographical reality to which he is exposed along the shores of the Dead Sea. -Beitzel John is in a literal wilderness seeking to lead others out of their spiritual wilderness In a world where 7 prominent men are listed as exercising control over the political and spiritual landscape, here comes God s spokesman, weather beaten, wild clothes and diet (Matt. 3:4) to set the world aright. obedience to God is the key to lasting influence Our surrender to God precedes His significant work in our life. MINISTRY Proclamation 3:3 How John came Wuest - And he went into all the country around the Jordan making a public proclamation with such formality, gravity, and authority as must be listened to and obeyed, announcing a baptism that had to do with repentance, this baptism, a testimony because of the putting away of sins, Public ministry: John went throughout the region Matt. Mark, John state the people went out to hear John So much for seeker evangelism or the church for people who don t like church John came preaching (imperfect) over and over and over again the baptism of repentance a baptism marked by repentance; a baptism of which the characteristic was repentance; a baptism which involved an obligation to repentance To preach a baptism emphasizes the content of the message rather than the rite itself The preparation of which is the arrival of the Messiah Baptism is the outward sign of a changed heart; whether a change took place or not The fact that the nation turned against Jesus would indicate that few were really repentant There was no baptism in the Jewish system; only ceremonial washings The Jews did baptize Gentile converts to Judaism Thus, those who were being baptized by [John] in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins (Mt. 3:6 ), were publicly acknowledging that they were no better than the Gentiles. Their sins had separated them from the true and living God (cf. Isa 59:2 ) and cut them off from covenant blessings. For Jewish people to place themselves on the same level as the despised Gentiles was astonishing, and demonstrates the power of John s preaching. Baptism: to dip, immerse; used in the dying of cloth Repentance is the human dimension in the experience of conversion; to be distinguished from regeneration which is the divine dimension Repentance turning, a change of heart Those baptized by John were showing their agreement with his message to confess their sins and prepare for the coming Messiah Acts 18:24, 25 Apollos was only familiar with the baptism of John; was given further instruction and received Christian baptism Paul met more of John s disciples in Acts 19 John s baptism was acknowledgment of their sin and need for forgiveness It was a preparatory baptism Citation 3:4, 5 Why John came Quote of Isaiah 40:3-5; used by all 3 synoptic gospels Isaiah is predicting the restoration of Israel from captivity 40:1, 2 Israel s sin was removed and that the way through the wilderness from captivity led to the restoration. Similarly: John cleared the way for God s visitation of salvation by calling the people to repentance. This restoration, the reconciliation of the people to God, was emphasized in the prophecies about John in chapter 1. John s task was not building a road in the wilderness filling up depressions, grading hills, and straightening hairpin curves, rather, he paved the way of the Lord with the changed lives of men and women. Warnings of bad news All have sinned. All need to come to repentance: a changed life-style as well as an acknowledgement of a need for change 3:7-9 Then having told the bad news, John went on to tell the good news that God would soon visit men with His salvation. 3:6 Only Luke quotes the longer version of the Isaiah prophecy by including Isaiah 40:5. Matthew and Mark stop short of this verse. Both Luke s gospel and Acts emphasize salvation to all men: Jew and Gentile THE PRESENTATION OF THE SON OF MAN THE FORERUNNER 3:1-20 Introduction to John s Ministry 3:1-6 HISTORY 3:1, 2 Luke is a meticulous historian concerned with accuracy days of ritualistic religion; of self-centeredness and self-gratification LOCALITY 3:1, 2 the word of God came upon John pressure from above a strong inner sense John was a preacher with great courage Matthew 11:11 Wilderness: Luke 1:80, desolate, solitary, lonely 1 Cointhians 1:26-29 obedience to God is the key to lasting influence Our surrender to God precedes His significant work in our life. MINISTRY Proclamation 3:3 a baptism of which the characteristic was repentance There was no baptism in the Jewish system; only ceremonial washings Repentance is the human dimension in the experience of conversion; to be distinguished from regeneration which is the divine dimension Acts 18:24, 25 Citation 3:4, 5 Isaiah 40:3-5 John cleared the way for God s visitation of salvation by calling the people to repentance. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(19).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 2/26/2022 9:46:17 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message PREACHING OF JOHN 3:7-14 There is not too much preaching today that measures up to Luke 3:7-14 You hear a lot of how to s ; communicate, love, honor, labor There are topical series dealing with a variety of popular subjects All good subjects that need to be address until you get to How to Live your Best Life Now There was nothing fuzzy about John s preaching: he is direct, judgmental, flame thrower Nothing here resembling the easy believism and cheap grace of today There was nothing shallow or superficial about John There was nothing about John s preaching for those who want their ears tickled And this was his message. John came preaching v. 3 over and over and over How could John attract such crowds? People came from all over and continually His message was cutting; his location brutal There was not live broadcast; no FB live or YouTube channel Matthew 21:26 they regarded John as a prophet; the first in a long time John 5:35 he was a burning and shining light; you rejoiced for a while in his light Luke 3:2 they knew a prophet was a spokesman for God Evidence suggests John had crowds but few conversions In fact, Jesus had few converts which should cause us to examine our hearts Many of those who heard only hardened their hearts the more THE CALL FOR BALANCE 3:7, 8 Here is a scathing rebuke from John Luke identifies the crowds in general; Matthew singled out the Pharisees, Sadducees it was not a target audience; John simply targeted the audience before him Mark 1:5 many different groups came to see and hear John John s message did not change no matter who was in attendance There is no charity in flattering the unconverted or smooth talk to damnable sins Generation of vipers - offspring of vipers Brood: to give birth to, beget, what is produced Viper: reputation for being deadly, and deceitful When lying still would look like a twig which could easily be picked up for burning yet strike the person Paul: Acts 28:3, 6 Offspring of the poisonous teachings of their religious leaders The first serpent was Satan; the implication he is their father John 8:44 The true nature of a child of Satan: vicious, poisonous, hostile, deadly Matthew 12:34; 23:33 Do we think of unbelievers that way? Who told you to flee? Snakes will flee the heat of a fire Judgment: Hades, hell, the Lake of Fire Message: repent or face judgment You don t avoid judgment just be being baptized To the Jew, a relationship with God was based on the external keeping of the Law Feasts, sacrifices, alms, formal prayers For them, John s baptism was just another ritual to be performed Warn : to show or point out; used here in negative sense Luke 6:47; 12:5; Acts 20:35 wrath to come God s righteous judgment; Present tense prophetic future Romans 1:18 No place for vipers to hide; or sinners to slide Looking beyond the destruction of Jerusalem in 70 AD Wrath: a swelling that will eventually burst; not uncontrollable anger, but settled indignation God s holiness cannot and will not coexist with sin in any form. Orge and thumos: two Greek words for wrath; thumos is more explosive The demonstration of God s wrath is revealed in Revelation 6-19 The subject of hell is offensive to human nature It is regarded as violent, barbaric, unfeeling, narrowminded No one spoke more plainly about hell than the Lord Jesus Himself J, C. Ryle: Let us never be ashamed to avow our firm belief, that there is a "wrath to come" for the impenitent, and that it is possible for a man to be lost as well as to be saved. To be silent on the subject is dreadful treachery to men's souls. There is no reason to flee until there is a cause to be afraid. No reason to seek heaven until you know you are falling into hell 8 Q. - How do we know a person s testimony of salvation is real? A. It takes time True repentance is evidenced by a change of character Therefore, bring forth fruits worthy of repentance You have heard the expression, no fruit, not root. The last I checked it takes time to produce fruit. More than sorrow of heart; or fear of fire The message from John was harsh, strong, confrontational it was also devastating because John knew how prone the sinner is to superficial repentance. Bring forth bear produce aorist command conveys a sense of urgency Fruit bearing validates heart believing Respond now. There is no certainty of another chance. worthy deserving, suitable; bringing into balance The fruit of one s life should balance the repentance Repentance is a change of mind, but more than that; a change of life s direction Anyone who was coming to be baptized by John thinking the act of baptism would insure forgiveness of sins was tragically mistaken! John is not just pleading for a decision He was putting them on notice that a true decision for Christ would bring about a radical change in their manner of life. Defined as fruit. Does the fruit in our life balance your testimony of repentance? Many can reform for a while. True regeneration will be evidenced over a period of time. Psalm 2:12 "His wrath may soon be kindled. How blessed are all who take refuge in Him!" PREACHING OF JOHN Luke 3:7-14 Matthew 21:26 John 5:35 THE CALL FOR BALANCE 3:7, 8 John s message did not change Generation of vipers - offspring of vipers Viper: reputation for being deadly, and deceitful Offspring of the poisonous teachings of their religious leaders. John 8:44 Snakes will flee the heat of a fire Message: repent or face judgment Warn : to show or point out wrath to come God s righteous judgment Romans 1:18 Wrath: a swelling that will eventually burst; settled indignation The subject of hell is offensive to human nature True repentance is evidenced by a change of character it takes time to produce fruit. Bring forth conveys a sense of urgency worthy deserving, suitable; bringing into balance The fruit of one s life should balance the repentance Psalm 2:12 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(20).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 3/12/2022 10:55:04 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE CALL OF ROCKS Luke 3:8 Luke 3:8 Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance, and don't begin to say among yourselves, 'We have Abraham for our father;' for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones! We defined the word worthy : meaning balance The life you live should measure up to your repentance You were purchased; you were bought; you are not your own Your understanding of Jesus sacrifice as the purchase price for your deliverance will naturally be reflected in your repentant life However, We must be aware of false repentance It happened to these people; many were baptized, yet turned on the Messiah MacArthur: Six progressive marks of true repentance emerge from this passage as necessary. (1) True repenters must reflect on personal sin, (2) recognize divine wrath, (3) reject religious ritual, (4) reveal spiritual transformation, (5) renounce ancestry, and (6) receive the Messiah. John anticipates the response of Jews; Matthew 3: don t say to yourselves . . though we know that was their attitude John 8:32 National identity is no advantage for heaven There was a time in our country when people leaned on their citizenship in the US as security for citizenship in heaven. I m an American, aren t I? Still may be found on a few occasions; it seems only half the country has any national identification John told these folks it is of no value Family identity is of no advantage for heaven John 8:39 Abraham is our father At least in their minds that settled the discussion Coattails don t work; you can t hang onto the life of a parent for heavenly access Each and every person is responsible before God The best any parent can provide is to steer in the right direction, live according to biblical truth, and present God as One worthy of worship Parenting is more than just laying down the law, it is communicating why the law is there You don t have to be perfect; you have to be honest Many children walked away from the faith because of inconsistency in the home Parents faithfully brought their children to church each week, saw that they were in the your group, and exposed them to truth, but failed miserably in their own walk with God. Children see through phoniness. Parents, be genuine. If you blow it and your kid knows you blew it, humble yourself before them and say so. No parent can get their child into heaven, but they can surely be a hindrance for entrance. So, John gets quickly to the heart of his listeners Don t rely on nationality or family But John does not leave it there; he shows just how weak their position was God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones! Options: a play on words: stone in Hebrew is eben and son (or child) is ben. Perhaps in area of Israel s crossing and monument of 12 stones (Jos. 4) Ezekiel 11:19; 36:26 exchange heart of stone for a heart of flesh This statement just really jumped out at me I would think these Jewish leaders (Matthew 3:7; Pharisees and Sadducees) would be highly insulted by now Everything they have trusted in has been shot down: national and family heritage Now John says they are no better than a rock Sometimes used of a Gentile; but probably using what they presently see on the river bank Put it this way: would you be offended if someone called you dumber than a box of rocks? (I trust you have heard the expression) There is actually a kids game by that name; $5.99 online at Walmart Every answer is 1, 2, or 0 Urban Dictionary: describing a person who lacks the collective intelligence of a box of rocks First appeared in a 1859 publication called Chambers Edinburgh Journal of Popular Literature by William and Robert Chambers Originally is was used for a person who could not speak or had little to say An old title: deaf and dumb Now John says if followers is all God wanted He could make worshippers out of stones A similar statement is made on another occasions: Triumphal Entry: Luke 19:39, 40 That is the work of creation is it not? Psalm 19:1 The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork Isaiah 6:3 the whole earth is full of His glory We sing the same: This Is My Father s World of rocks and trees, of skies and seas; His hands the wonders wrought. We know the stars sing. Creation, including rocks, can praise. And maybe better and more pure than us. Lest you think rocks have nothing to add: listen to ringing rocks. [YouTube] So what can we add? Man was made from the dust of the ground; not exactly rock but close Though created from dust; man is a living being; created in God s image We can choose to praise Man was created to give God glory: Revelation 4:11 "Worthy are you, our Lord and God, the Holy One, to receive the glory, the honor, and the power, for you created all things, and because of your desire they existed, and were created!" (WEB) Man can respond to the drawing of God Man can admit he is a sinner; believe the gospel; accept the sacrifice of Jesus for his sin. Man can respond. He can declare his belief in Christ Jesus as Lord. Man can love the Lord with all his heart, soul, mind and strength Rocks can t do that. Only God s image bearer can bring Him the proper level of praise due to Him. It is not about nationality or family ties It is man coming to the place of repentance, turning from his sin to embrace a loving heavenly Father. 1 Corinthians 1:26-31 (ASV) For behold your calling, brethren, that not many wise after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called: but God chose the foolish things of the world, that he might put to shame them that are wise; and God chose the weak things of the world, that he might put to shame the things that are strong; and the base things of the world, and the things that are despised, did God choose, yea and the things that are not, that he might bring to nought the things that are: that no flesh should glory before God. But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who was made unto us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption: that, according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord. Now I do not recommend adding the phrase dumb as a rock to your evangelism presentation. But know this: Jesus is the rock of our salvation the stone rejected by the builders We are living stones. (1 Peter 2:5) The early church asked, what shall I do to be saved? Today s church asks, What can I do and still be saved? The message is repent And what of Abraham? Galatians 3:28, 29 Galatians 3:28-29 (ASV) There can be neither Jew nor Greek, there can be neither bond nor free, there can be no male and female; for ye all are one man in Christ Jesus. And if ye are Christ's, then are ye Abraham's seed, heirs according to promise. THE CALL OF ROCKS Luke 3:8 The life you live should balance your repentance We must be aware of false repentance National identity is no advantage for heaven Family identity is of no advantage for heaven John 8:39 Children see through phoniness. God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones! a play on words area of Israel s crossing Ezekiel 11:19 John says they are no better than a rock Luke 19:39, 40 Psalm 19:1 Isaiah 6:3 Man was made from the dust of the ground Man was created to give God glory: Revelation 4:11 Man can respond to the drawing of God Man can love the Lord with all his heart, soul, mind and strength 1 Corinthians 1:26-31 Galatians 3:28, 29 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(21).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 3/19/2022 12:24:59 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE CALL TO JUDGEMENT 3:9-14 Imminent Warning John was seeking for listeners to become true children of Abraham His message includes a warning of imminent judgment Ax-root; tree: the warning of present and continual judgment is present A continual revolution in life-style prevents destruction already is applied producing is cast all present tense John did not come to simply prune and trim; he came to fell every worthless tree Judgment is one axe throw away; God is an expert woodsman Wiersbe - John reminded them that God gets to the root of things and is not impressed with religious profession. John is picking up where Malachi left off Malachi 3:1-3; 4:1, 2; 5, 6 A few decades later the axe did fall on Jerusalem in 70 AD Foreshadowing a future and final judgment A message serving as a warning for the serious nature of repentance John promised that the baptism of the genuinely repentant would be followed by the Holy Spirit baptism. (3:16) The Holy Spirit is the agent working within The life-style of repentance is the work of the Holy Spirit Jesus draws out new fruits from the convert: (19:1-10) Practical Warning 3:10-14 People were noticeably concerned Their hearts were exposed; they were wrong Now asking the common question: What are we to do? There is a since of urgency. Luke 10:25, a lawyer; 18:18, the rich young ruler; Acts 2:37 Generosity 3:10, 11 The general populace rethinking one s life-style and goals Change the direction of one s life; conduct, behavior If we are mistaken about our view on salvation, we need help Some of you remember the famous line from Love Story Love means never having to say you are sorry Repentance is more than admitting one is a sinner or being sorry for sins. Repentance: rethinking, changing the mind; changing a life-style Includes: trusting in God s offer of forgiveness and acknowledging His lordship and kingdom tunic an inner garment worn under a cloak Practical religion teaches that we are obligated to one another Unselfishness and generosity are fruits worthy of repentance Sharing with the less fortunate James 1:17 pure and undefiled religion . . . An accurate evaluation of the state of our spirituality: K. Hughes Are we generous with our possessions? Do we share our homes, our cars, our clothing, our food with others joyfully? Or are we loath to share? Do we always push for more and then grasp it tightly? Do we enjoy giving to family, friends, and, more significantly, those in need? Do we give regularly and sacrificially to the Lord? If you are a Christian but do not give regularly to the Lord, if you are tight, if you find it difficult to give to God, you are in spiritual trouble, and possibly you are not even a Christian at all! Do you really believe it is more blessed to give than receive? Honesty 3:12, 13 Tax collectors: special sinners Collaborated with Romans Charging outrageous fees If a Jew could scarcely persuade himself that it was right to pay taxes, how much more heinous a crime must it have been in his eyes to become the questionably honest instrument for collecting them. If a publican was hated, how still more intense must have been the disgust entertained against a publican who was also a Jew (Farrar, Life of Christ ) Their question: how does John s teaching apply to them?
