Be honest in your transactions; don t cheat, be truthful, sincere John did not say find another job; something more honorable Did not say get away from those evil Romans Did not say paying taxes was wrong He did say don t get rich on the backs of your countrymen Be fair and honest no more is emphatic John went directly to the heart of the matter Some responded: Luke 7:29, 30 Using Authority 3:14 For John, repentance was to be a way of life; not a one-time life event And nothing about his message promotes salvation Only practical evidences that a life has been change John is not promoting legalism but a relationship with God John went after two of the most hated professions of the day; tax collectors and soldiers Trophies of repentance Some believe these soldiers are Jews who accompany tax collector Soldiers: possessed opportunity to defraud and torment do violence to no man - do not shake violently; to intimidate; shake down Do not take money by force Money and the use of authority do not accuse anyone falsely blackmail, annoy, harass, (2x) Luke 19:8 Evidently, they often complained about their pay scale No word about leaving their position; or finding a more appropriate job Don t use your position as an instrument of oppression Use your role to help Be content sufficient, adequate, satisfied Phil. 4:11 John s message was plain and courageous He was a true herald of his Master. CONCLUSION John s message attacked the idols of money and possessions Kent Hughes: John's answer is somewhat surprising. We might have expected that he would ask them to do something penitential ("Go do four good acts to the orphans in your town") or ecclesiastical ("Commit yourself to your local synagogue") or perhaps devotional ("Pray through the Psalms"). But significantly, John's advice was ethical. He asked them to change how they treated their fellow human beings. A real danger comes with spiritual presumption stemming from exemplary ethical performance True repentance comes with changed thinking and changed behavior. THE CALL TO JUDGEMENT Luke 3:9-14 Imminent Warning John is picking up where Malachi left off Malachi 3:1-3; 4:1, 2; 5, 6 Practical Warning 3:10-14 Generosity 3:10, 11 tunic an inner garment worn under a cloak James 1:17 Honesty 3:12, 13 Be honest in your transactions; don t cheat, be truthful, sincere Using Authority 3:14 Repentance was to be a way of life Some believe these soldiers are Jews who accompany tax collector do violence to no man - do not shake violently; to intimidate; shake down Don t use your position as an instrument of oppression John s message was plain and courageous John s message attacked the idols of money and possessions A real danger comes with spiritual presumption stemming from exemplary ethical performance Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(22).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 3/26/2022 9:03:20 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE PRESENTATION OF THE SON OF MAN 3:1-18 The Forerunner of the Son of Man 3:1-20 The Beginning of John s Ministry 3:1-6 History, Locality, Ministry The Preaching of John 3:7-17 The Call to Balance 3:7 The Call of Rocks 3:8 The Call of Judgment 3:9-14 The Call of Clarification 3:15-18 THE CALL OF CLARIFICATION 3:15-18 Have you ever panicked because you had 30 minutes to prepare for unexpected guests? The phone call announces their soon arrival and you look around to see your house is not suitable for entertaining. At least in your opinion. Reminds me of the guy who brings home another fellow from work for supper without telling his wife. She rails about the thoughtlessness, the gall, the disrespect, the humiliation of having a guest. Her husband simply quiets her with the reply. Well, George here said he was thinking about getting married and I thought I would show him what to expect. Or you have been invited to meet some dignitary and you panic because you have nothing suitable to wear and you have little idea of how to greet someone of that stature. Perhaps that poor illustration will help to understand a little of the people listening to John Their question to him is like that. Wait a minute. Are you talking about yourself? Are you the Anointed One? If so, we need to get out act together right now! Maybe we are already standing in his presence. Just how much time of preparation do we have? They have been asking, what do we do? Now it is more like, how much time do we have? You hear some folks today: I will get right with God before I die. I have time yet. Billy Sunday called such conversions as burning your life for the devil and blowing the smoke in the face of God. How much more time do these folks have before the Messiah comes? Or is John the one they have been looking for? We really need some clarification here! John 3:19-23 Superior in Position 3:15-16a The crowds were in expectancy v. 15 to anticipate; the idea of mental direction 15x in NT; 10x by Luke 1:21 waiting for Zacharias; 7:19, 20 John wondering about Jesus 12:46 a warning for our own mental direction 2 Peter 3:12-14 reasoned in their heart deliberation; KJV muse; wondering Here is a man who did no miracles, was not in the Davidic line; but was so powerful that he made the people wonder if he was really the Messiah Kent Hughes: We need to keep this in mind when we read his preaching because his great eschatological and ethical challenges were delivered in the mighty rush of the Holy Spirit's power. Humbling; perhaps too much preaching today is done in the flesh John did not reprove their expectations Their messianic expectations were correct John also made it clear that he was at best only the servant of the Messiah John, in fact, declared that he did not even qualify to be the Messiah s slave mightier - power. Force The emphasis is on the actual power not just the principle of power Used in the LXX to speak of God; Jeremiah 32:18 (Jer. 33:3) not fit to loosen the strap on His sandals the job of a typical slave The rabbis taught that a disciple should do everything for their master except untie their sandals. That was considered too humiliating to demand. This is the first mention of Jesus by John Guzik comments that "John had many reasons to be proud, yet he was humble. He had a miraculous birth, a prophesied destiny, a man called to personally fulfill great prophetic promises, a powerful preacher, and a man with a great following.. What was the reason, think you, of John s always retaining his proper position? Was it not because he had a high idea of his Master, and a deep reverence for him? Ah, brethren, because of our little estimate of Christ, it is often unsafe for the Lord to trust us in any but the very lowest positions. Perhaps hyperbole; a statement of exaggeration It was language to make it clear that in no way was he the Christ, anointed one This in keeping with the characteristic of John s humility and his spirit of servanthood (Mark 1:7; Matt. 3:13-15; John 1:6-8, 29-37; 3:26-31) Jesus regarded John as the greatest of the prophets; Luke 7:25-28) Superior in Practice 3:16b-17 The baptism with water contrasted with the baptism with the Holy Spirit You can be baptized with one and not the other; but water is of no value without the Spirit An external rite is of no benefit without internal change in repentance External performed by man; only God can do the internal work John is not the Messiah, so He is yet to come John is acknowledging that his ministry may be powerful, but the people had not seen anything yet The Messiah s ministry would supersede that of John s (John 3:22-30) Some legitimate discussion of the verse: are there one or two baptisms? with or in is used only once which leads many to say John is speaking of only one baptism Thus: Fire promises purification and empowerment for believers (D. A. Carson) Water baptism represents the purification of repentance which anticipates the all- consuming baptism of the Holy Spirit the emphasis is upon the empowering aspect of the predicted Spirit-fire baptism a fire or purification at Pentecost; Acts 2 Grammatically this fits. But two things to consider: The fire of tongues in Acts 2 was not a literal fire: only as of fire John s message spoke of a real fire. the context would lead to two events: The baptism of the Holy Spirit for those who are saved The baptism of fire for the unrepentant Fire warns the unrepentant of imminent judgment (Criswell, MacArthur, other) The judgment motif is still strong. John often uses fire as a symbol of judgment Vipers flee the fire (v.7); the tree-fire discussion (v. 9); v. 17 judgment is on the horizon The image of the threshing floor is as sobering as the ax laid to the root The winnowing fork is used to throw the harvested wheat into the air where the wind blows away the chaff from the good grain. The remaining is burned. winnow to reduce a large number of people or things to a much smaller number by judging their quality. (American Dictionary) Note: He will thoroughly cleanse His threshing floor The purging of separation will be absolute and complete Alexander Maclaren (Expositions of Holy Scripture [Baker], Luke, p. 76) explains it this way, Either we shall gladly accept the purging fire of the Spirit which burns sin out of us, or we shall have to meet the punitive fire which burns up us and our sins together. To be cleansed by the one or to be consumed by the other is the choice before each of us. Jon Courson - John preached a message of severity in order that Jesus might come and present a message of serenity. John called for holiness in order that Jesus could give a message of hopefulness. John shone a convicting light in order that Jesus might share comforting love. In other words, John embodied the purpose of the law by showing people they were sinners in need of a Savior. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(23).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/2/2022 3:20:31 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message IDENTIFYING THE SON OF MAN LUKE 3:19-22 Rejection of Forerunner 3:19-20 The Herods were naturalized Jews A naturalized citizen is someone who was born in a foreign country, but took a series of steps with the end goal of being granted citizenship. Herod = Herod Antipas; one of the sons of Herod the Great (prominent at Jesus birth) Herod the Great: an Idumean (Edomite); his wife, Malthace, a Samaritan Herod was a Gentile; but the gospel of repentance was for him as well as Jews Truth is hard because it exposes sin reproved rebuked, convict, tell a fault; to expose or bring to light Herodias was already married to her uncle Phillip; just switching uncles (Leviticus 18:16; Matthew 14:4) Luke inserts the imprisonment here; though it comes later Mark 6:14 [[Matthew and Mark point out that Herodias was responsible for John s death in prison Matt. 14:3-13; Mark 6:17-29]] concerning all the wicked things he had done Herod built the city, Tiberias, on top of a Jewish cemetery He put up idols in public places; another serious offence to the Jews wicked evil same word used for Satan the evil one Matt. 25:41 Folks, if you are going to be a presenter of righteousness, you are going to run into trouble The Gospel is more than God loves you and has a wonderful plan for your life For most people it is, of course, God loves me. I am a lovable person Jesus died for me? I may have my faults, but not as bad as many others. A sinner deserving hell? Don t think so. I am not perfect but l live a moral life. John preached the Law of God; God s standard for morality. In light of that standard, no man measures up; no man does good; none are righteous John did not soften the message; did not alter the message; did not compromise simply because of the position of the recipient A liar is a liar, an adulterer is an adulterer; no matter their status in this world Ephesians 4:18, 19 John was arrested because he was popular with the people and Herod feared what John might tell them to do even to the point of an uprising (Josephus) Proverbs 29:25 the fear of man brings a snare . . . The proverb continues but he who trusts in the LORD shall be exalted You may be thrown in prison and lose your heard, but exalted none the less John s boldness is a direct result of being filled with the Holy Spirit 1:15- The consistent message of Luke throughout the book of Acts 20 The icing on the cake of evil deeds Sin confronted but unchecked often becomes sin multiplied and magnified. Defensiveness in the face of sin is inevitably self-destructive. Unfortunately, the damage often extends beyond the one who is sinning (Bock) 2 Samuel 11; David and Bathsheba is perfect example The Call of God becomes Common; leads to Complacency; Confusion; Cover Up; Casualties; Confession Herod did all he could to silence the voice of conviction Luke records that John was imprisoned, then promptly drops him from the story line until chapter 7. MacArthur: Luke simply tells us the end of the story before it happens Luke does not say that John was the one who baptized Jesus. Once John has defined true repentance, prophesied about Jesus, and predicted the coming of the Holy Spirit, the Gospel of Luke views John s task as essentially complete. The Recognition of Jesus 3:21, 22 Luke s emphasis is clearly on the events which immediately followed Jesus baptism rather than on the baptism itself. The baptism is mentioned in a participial clause The three main clauses are The heaven was opened The Holy Spirit descended The voice came from heaven identifying Jesus as God s Son Only Luke relates that Jesus was in prayer when the Holy Spirit came upon Him Jesus practiced that which He taught His disciples Jesus serves as our model And maybe not so much the frequency as it occurs at key times and places 6:12, choosing of the 12; 9:28, 29, at the transfiguration; 11:1, taught the Lord s Prayer; 18:1, pray and not lose heart; 22:40, 46, keep from falling into sin; 22:32, to keep from falling into apostacy The opening of heaven indicated that divine revelation was about to take place. It is interesting that the heavens were opened to mark the inauguration of Jesus' earthly ministry to accomplish redemption of mankind, while in Revelation 19:11 + the heavens are opened to mark the return of Jesus to accomplish His redemption of the earth An open heaven; God was breaking into human history Stephen saw the heaven open; Acts 7; Peter s witness, Acts 10:11 The Spirit s descent: the gentleness and purity of the Spirit; the Trinity participation The Voice: Psalm 2:7; Isa. 42:1 If God is pleased, I should be thrilled What had Jesus done to please the Father? No miracle had been done, to public teaching, no leper healed; nothing that we would associate with greatness increased in wisdom and stature and favor with God and man What is done in the silent place with God is what matters. Rather than begin His public ministry by going to Jerusalem and identify Himself with the established religious leaders, Jesus went to the river and identified with those repenting of sins. Why was Jesus baptized? Jesus had no need of repentance; the voice from heaven is testimony John s hesitancy is testimony (Matt. 3:14) Matt. 3:15 to fulfill all righteousness is the only explanation we have. It was the right thing to do He was Identified by the Forerunner (Isa. 40:3) He was Identified as the Sacrifice John the Baptist (son of Levite who presented sacrifices) pronounced Jesus as the perfect sacrifice (John 1:29) He identified with Sinners. He was identified with the sinner s repentance. Our baptism identifies with His righteousness It was the perfect embodiment of the Triune God It is not the unity of God displayed in 3 different momentary forms (modalism) Luke s emphasis on the Holy Spirit and Jesus sonship manifests itself quite clearly in how his Gospel recounts the story of John s baptism in contrast to the other Synoptic Gospels. Mark shows how the baptism confirmed Jesus messiahship. (1:1, 7) Matthew s Gospel emphasizes the supremacy of Jesus over John (3:14, 15) Luke s (like John s) Gospel concentrates on the coming of the Holy Spirit IDENTIFYING THE SON OF MAN LUKE 3:19-22 Rejection of Forerunner 3:19-20 reproved to expose or bring to light Herodias was already married to her uncle Phillip wicked evil same word used for Satan John preached the Law of God Ephesians 4:18, 19 Sin confronted but unchecked often becomes sin multiplied and magnified. Herod did all he could to silence the voice of conviction The Recognition of Jesus 3:21, 22 Jesus practiced that which He taught His disciples The opening of heaven indicated that divine revelation was about to take place. What is done in the silent place with God is what matters. Why was Jesus baptized? He was Identified by the Forerunner He was Identified as the Sacrifice He identified with Sinners It was the perfect embodiment of the Triune God Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(24).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/8/2022 2:37:43 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message OUT OF THE SHADOWS Luke 3:21-38 We are looking at IDENTIFYING THE SON OF MAN. Prior to this, what we know of Jesus is found in 2:52. He has operated in the shadows; in obscurity. John has fulfilled his call as the forerunner. He has preached the message of repentance. He has presented the warnings of judgment. From the gospels, he has provided the identifying marks of the Messiah; John 1:29-31 Mark 1:7 Matthew 3:14 In the history of Christianity, today is recognized as Palm Sunday. Zechariah 9:9 (WEB) Rejoice greatly, daughter of Zion! Shout, daughter of Jerusalem! Behold, your king comes to you! He is righteous, and having salvation; Lowly, and riding on a donkey, Even on a colt, the foal of a donkey. The King came. He presented Himself. The people excitedly participated with the waving of palm branches, declaring Hosanna to the son of David. Blessed is the King that comes in the name of the Lord. Maybe some of the same people who were baptized by John. Many of the same people who by the end of the week, called for the King s crucifixion. In Luke 3, the writer presents Jesus in the beginning of His public ministry. We are going to look at two ways. First of all, we want to finish from last week. SPIRITUAL HERITAGE 3:21-22 In the baptism of Jesus, His identification is made known from heaven. As mentioned last week, Luke s purpose is the things that took place at the baptism The baptism is mentioned in a participial clause The three main clauses are The heaven was opened The Holy Spirit descended The voice came from heaven identifying Jesus as God s Son Heaven was open. The opening of heaven indicated that divine revelation was about to take place. God is breaking into human history Acts 7 Stephen Acts 10 Peter; 10:11 The heavens were opened to mark the inauguration of Jesus' earthly ministry to accomplish redemption of mankind, while in Revelation 19:11 the heavens are opened to mark the return of Jesus to accomplish His redemption of the earth The Spirit Descended the gentleness and purity of the Spirit; the Trinity participation The Voice: (Psalm 2:7); Isaiah 42:1 What had Jesus done to please the Father? No miracle had been done, to public teaching, no leper healed; nothing that we would associate with greatness increased in wisdom and stature and favor with God and man What is done in the silent place with God is what matters. Why was Jesus baptized? Jesus had no need of repentance; the voice from heaven is testimony John s hesitancy is testimony (Matt. 3:14) Mathew 3:15 to fulfill all righteousness is the only explanation we have. It was the right thing to do He was Identified by the Forerunner (Isa. 40:3) He was Identified as the Sacrifice John the Baptist (son of Levite who presented sacrifices) pronounced Jesus as the perfect sacrifice (John 1:29) He identified with Sinners. He was identified with the sinner s repentance. Our baptism identifies with His righteousness It was the perfect embodiment of the Triune God Mark shows how the baptism confirmed Jesus messiahship. (1:1, 7) Matthew s Gospel emphasizes the supremacy of Jesus over John (3:14, 15) Luke s (like John s) Gospel concentrates on the coming of the Holy Spirit TRANSITION: The spiritual identification of Jesus NATURAL HERITAGE 3:23-38 Why two genealogies? There are major differences Matthew works forward in time while Luke works backwards Matthew starts with Abraham; Luke goes back to Adam Many of the names are not identical Matthew follows the family of Joseph; Luke the family of Mary The differences, of course, is the reason critics discredit the whole thing Luke did not fabricate a document. 1:1-4 I think we can see two motives for the genealogies The Theological Motive of the writers Matthew: emphasized Abraham and David as the crucial ancestors His audience was Jewish Luke: progresses from Joseph to Adam to God 3:23 as was supposed the son of Joseph ; confirming the virgin birth Luke provides an excellent opportunity to look at the human and divine nature of Jesus Psalm 2:7 the king was considered begotten by God on his coronation day. Common concept among ancient Near Eastern civilization. The king was not considered the genetic offspring of the chief-god of the people, but he filled the office of sonship which meant that the king acted with the full powers and blessing of the god. Luke s gospel goes beyond the traditional meaning of Son of God and makes it clear that Jesus very nature is from God. Luke 1:35 Thus God Himself was responsible for part of the genetic material which made Jesus Jesus received His human nature from the sanctified genetic input of Mary As a Son of Adam, Jesus was qualified to redeem the sons of Adam Kinsman redeemer had to be related to the one in need of redemption Lev. 25:25, 47-49; Ruth 4:4-6; Jer. 32:6-12 As the Son of God, Jesus had no need of His own redemption By Luke locating the genealogy between the baptism of Jesus and the temptation of Jesus rather than at the beginning of the Gospel as Matthew does, Luke s gospel affirms that Jesus is the Son of God several times in succession. The Genealogical Motive A problem David s dynasty through Solomon arises in Jeremiah 22:24-30. Jehoiakin s disobedience stopped the physical right to rule on the throne There is no indication that the legal right also stopped But it does mean that Joseph s seed would have denied Jesus His kingship. So God is His infinite wisdom still honors His covenant with David, but the physical right now comes from David through his son Nathan. Mary, a descendent of David, and a virgin, wonderfully and miraculously gives to the world the human nature of the Son of God and the physical right to be King. CONCLUSION When Jesus entered the city of Jerusalem the week before Passover, He was the Son of David. He was the Son of God. Scripture is clear. His identity is clear. It is our great privilege to honor Him and give Him His rightful place. There is nothing about this message that will provide much motivation come Monday morning. But what I hope you can walk away with a greater appreciation of the wisdom and grace of God that gave you your salvation. OUT OF THE SHADOWS Luke 3:21-38 SPIRITUAL HERITAGE Luke 3:21-22 The opening of heaven indicated that divine revelation was about to take place. Revelation 19:11 The Spirit Descended The Voice Isaiah 42:1 What is done in the silent place with God is what matters. Why was Jesus baptized? He was Identified by the Forerunner He was Identified as the Sacrifice He identified with Sinners It was the perfect embodiment of the Triune God NATURAL HERITAGE 3:23-38 The Theological Motive Matthew: audience was Jewish Luke: look at the human and divine nature of Jesus Psalm 2:7 Luke 1:35 Jesus was qualified to redeem the sons of Adam The Genealogical Motive Jeremiah 22:24-30 Jehoiakin s disobedience stopped the physical right to rule on the throne Mary, the physical right to be King Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(25).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/16/2022 8:02:17 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE OUT OF THE DARKNESS Job 19:23-26 INTRODUCTION I decided not to begin Luke 4 and the temptations of Jesus as I know it will not be a one week study. Ben will be standing here next week as we recognize the students from Cedarville and will hear from them what their plans are for the summer. We need to hear so we can pray for each of them. So, very uncharacteristically, I would like to share a message related to a special day on the Christian calendar; Resurrection Sunday. I remain convinced that there is no greater day on the Christian calendar and I believe 1 Corinthians 15 speaks to that. For Paul tells us in no uncertain terms that if not for the bodily resurrection of Jesus Christ from the grave what we do here is absolutely useless and we are of all men a most miserable lot. We are liars of God if we say Jesus rose if in fact he did not rise. But we do believe Jesus rose. We believe that in His resurrection Jesus was declared to be the Son of God with power . (Romans 1:4) I also am convinced that believing in the resurrection provides practical applications beyond trusting in life after death. Now, that is a tremendous truth. To know that upon our being set free from the earthly bonds of life in this body we are present with the Lord. (2 Corinthians 5) My cousin had a dog who was tied up for most of the day. The dog was very custom to knowing he could go so far and no more. So much so, that my cousin could pet him for a couple of minutes and in the process remove the chain, walk away, and it took a couple of minutes for the dog to know it had been set free. But when it realized it, off to the races! That is how I picture death; being loosed, being set free. We have lived with the limitations of this body for so long that when set free; LOOK OUT!! But today, I want to apply the resurrection life upon this earth and the hope it provides while we are undergoing some very difficult days. Job is our person of interest. We know his story of loss and great suffering. He lost everything accept a nagging wife. Her testimony was, Curse God and die. (2:9) Through Job we learn that there is hope even in the midst of great darkness. Job 19:23-26 THE ASSURANCE OF THE GREAT HOPE Job says, "I know." He has it, and holds firmly to it Contrast 3:1 What brought about the difference? How does Job know that he knows? By inspiration. It is a God-thing. The greatest truths of redemption and resurrection are from God. He is not in some wild state of ecstasy; he is not on something. It is not about feeling the presence; overwhelmed by some emotion His tone is one of calm, glad assurance. Through the discipline of suffering. Sorrow has trained him to see the highest truth. God s revelation comes to the prepared soul. It is as if God parted the heavens to give Job a glimpse God did not forsake him in his suffering but illumined his mind THE GROUNDS OF THE GREAT HOPE Job had a Goel g 'al, gaw-al' A redeemer; an Avenger; one who will plead his cause and deliver What does he know about Him? Divine Clearly he is thinking of God He flees from the God who is his persecuting enemy to the God who is His friend. He flees from God to God. He is struggling with God s present treatment That same God is the One who will deliver We now know more than Job. His great hope and prophecy is fulfilled in Christ. Jesus is the sinner s Goel, the great redeemer of man Personal. my Redeemer Each must know Christ for himself. But all may know and own Him. Christ not only redeems the innocent by vindicating them which was what Job expected. We now see that He goes further, and He redeems the guilty by saving them even from their sin and doom. Living The Redeemer lives, though for a while we do not see him, We have a living Saviour. TRANSITION: THE ASSURANCE; THE GROUNDS THE SUBSTANCE OF THE GREAT HOPE. A future life. I am not sure how much Job was thinking of a present day cure of his deceased skin and flesh I am not sure if he was looking for some kind of vindication in his earthly life His words seem to go beyond his present day circumstances Job is looking for a future life when his worm-eaten body is left behind Here is an OT message of hope; a triumphant hope Here is his answer to 14:14 There will be a future when this body will be laid aside A vision of God. I shall see God Job had been longing to meet God. His prayer was lost in silence (ver. 7). God's hand was only upon him for chastisement Now he foresees the great apocalypse. (1) This is for the vindication of righteousness. God will then explain the mysteries and put an end to the wrongs of earth. (2) This is itself a great joy. The vision is an adequate compensation for all the sufferings of earth. THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE GREAT HOPE Apart from the earthly body This is no trouble to Job His body has become a loathsome, tormenting encumbrance. "Flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, neither doth corruption inherit incorruption". [1Corinthians 15:50 ] With personal identity Job would not be content to be dissolved into the universe. The future life is one of personal existence. It must be linked by memory to the present life. OUT OF THE DARKNESS Job 19:23-26 1 Corinthians 15 Romans 1:4 2 Corinthians 5 THE ASSURANCE OF THE GREAT HOPE By inspiration It is a God-thing Through the discipline of suffering THE GROUNDS OF THE GREAT HOPE Job had a Goel Divine He flees from God to God Jesus is the sinner s Goel Personal Each must know Christ for himself He redeems the guilty by saving them even from their sin and doom Living THE SUBSTANCE OF THE GREAT HOPE. A future life. Job is looking for a future life when his worm-eaten body is left behind A vision of God (1) This is for the vindication of righteousness (2) This is itself a great joy THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE GREAT HOPE Apart from the earthly body 1Corinthians 15:50 With personal identity Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(26).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/30/2022 10:51:13 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message This will only be an introduction. Your video would be great to play during message intro. THE TEMPTATIOIN OF JEUS LUKE 4:1-13 We have made our way through the chapters 1-3 in our study of the gospel of Luke. Luke s purpose is to present Jesus as the Son of Man; fully human while maintaining His Deity. The main headings of our study are simply this: The Preparation of the Son of Man The Identification of the Son of Man The Instruction of the Son of Man The Sacrifice of the Son of Man Up to this point, we have recorded for us the detailed research of Luke as he lays out for us the first 30 years of Jesus living as a man. From the angel s pronouncement to Zachariah, to Mary, to shepherds; to the testimony of Simeon and Anna; to Jesus visit in the temple at the age of 12; to his submission to Joseph and Mary; to the presenting of Himself to John the Baptist and the testimony of God the Father from heaven; Luke has carefully laid out for us all the behind the scenes There is one more thing for Jesus to experience before He formally begins His public ministry. The Temptation of Jesus in the wilderness. Three gospel writers have a written account: Matthew, Mark, and Luke. Mark s statement is most brief; only two verses; 1:12, 13. Matthew and Luke differ as to the order of the second and third temptations. Most opinions are just that, conjecture of a particular writer. Both accounts are inspired and that is what is important. We know that Luke was not as particular for chronology of events. This is such an important text. It is important because this is where we live. Temptation hits everyone. Oscar Wilde is reported to say, I can resist anything except temptation. Others want to be delivered from temptation but they like it to keep in touch from time to time. But if we want to be godly people, we must learn to resist the temptations that come at us from the world, the flesh, and the devil. If we want to be like Jesus, we will be eager to learn from Him how He resisted the devil. This account must have been shared by Jesus to His disciples as it was a private encounter. Luke is using it a teaching moment for us, but more importantly to confirm Jesus as the righteous Son of Man. Since the fall of man (Genesis 3) various means have been used in the struggle against temptation. Self-inflicted pain, isolation, denying temptations existence; there is no escape. Poor theology of a second work of grace where the sin nature is declared dead does not miraculously take one from sin to just making mistakes. Not even water baptism drowns the devil . Jesus did not fall into temptation. He did not find Himself in an unfortunate circumstance, He did not just happen to be in the wrong place at the wrong time. He has preserved this account for us as a practical example of how we are to deal with our own temptations. I know you can make surveys say whatever you want simply by asking the right questions. But it does not take long in your personal research to know that many Christians are losing the battle with temptation. Pornography, gambling including the lottery, gluttony, alcohol, marital infidelity, stealing, cheating (including on your taxes), premarital sex, laziness, procrastination, so many things coming to us from the world, the flesh, the devil. Or as recorded by John: the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, the pride of life. Surveys say there is little difference between those in the church and those on the outside. The battle wages on. We get tired, we get careless, we rationalize, we sin. James 1:14, but every man is tempted when he is drawn away by his own lust and enticed. Jesus can help. He has shown the way. He has provided the resources. He wants us to win. The outline is simple enough: THE PREPARATION, THE PATTERN, THE TRIUMPH. The outline also leads to three points of application: Am I prepared? I will be tempted. Will I triumph? Here is the template. The temptations of Jesus were uniquely His. The timing was His. The location was His. The particulars are His. Temptation: to entice, to test, prove, examine. Jesus had to pass this test. Luke has just provided numerous accounts to prove Jesus identity. Angel to Zacharias to announce the forerunner, angel to Mary, testimony of John in the womb, songs of Elizabeth and Mary, angels in the heavens, shepherds at the stable, Jesus at the age of 12. Luke provided two more accounts with the baptism and genealogy. God spoke from heaven and the genealogy speaks to His heritage. The temptation is the final word of affirmation prior to Jesus public ministry. The proximity in the written record is significant. The genealogy provided by Luke goes back to Adam. It was Adam who failed the test. It was Adam s disobedience that plunged the human race into sin. Romans 5:12,19 (WEB) Therefore, as sin entered into the world through one man, and death through sin; and so death passed to all men, because all sinned. Jesus is called the Second Adam. Jesus must pass this test if He is going to successfully deliver man from the condemnation brought by the first Adam. by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous (5:19) Our first representative failed. Our second representative was victorious. Our first representative was sought by the devil. Our second representative sought out the devil. Matthew and Luke: Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness Mark 1:12 Jesus was driven by the Spirit into the wilderness drove throw out, to eject Our first representative had no sin, the perfect environment, the perfect provisions, the perfect wife. He had paradise. Adam had the perfect environment.. The second Adam was in the wilderness, surrounded by wild animals, no provisions, rugged terrain, alone. The second Adam was purposely subjected to the worse of temptations for 40 days in the most extreme circumstances. Jesus could have responded like many of us would have, God if you really loved me, I would not be in this situation. Jesus is now able to restore everything the first Adam lost. It is absolutely critical that we know Jesus won that battle. That settles any question that any may have regarding His qualifications to be our redeemer. I want to address a theological question that gets bantered around. I don t think it should even be a question, but it does surface. It even came up at my ordination council. The comment had been presented at the beginning that there were some things of controversy that were not going to be allowed to dictate the questioning and answering. This was one of those topics: Was Jesus not able to sin or able not to sin? Was Jesus not able to sin or able not to sin? seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me John 5:30 But each one is tempted, when he is drawn away by his own lust, and enticed. James 1:14 "Don't think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn't come to destroy, but to fulfill. Matthew 5:17 Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me. John 14:30 The big objection: If Jesus was not able to sin, His temptation was not real. Question: do you sin every time you are tempted? Hebrews 4:15 He was tempted in all manner like we are yet without sin That settles the issue. God said He was tempted. God accepted the temptation as real. There is no place for a yea, but . The temptation of Jesus was real. His temptation went beyond this text. in all manner meaning not just in every manner we are tempted but also at every stage of life: as a child, as a youth, as a young man and middle aged man. 2:52 He increased in wisdom and stature and in favor with God and man We will examine the text beginning next week. CONCLUSION: Jesus can come to aid in your temptation. Jesus did battle with the devil and was victorious. Jesus is the perfect mediator between God and man. THE TEMPTATIOIN OF JEUS LUKE 4:1-13 THE ______________ THE ____________ THE ____________ Temptation: to entice, to test, prove, examine It was Adam who failed the test Romans 5:12,19 Jesus is called the Second Adam Our first representative failed. Our second representative was victorious. Mark 1:12 Jesus is now able to restore everything the first Adam lost. It is absolutely critical that we know Jesus won that battle Was Jesus not able to sin or able not to sin? John 5:30 James 1:14 Matthew 5:17 John 14:30 Hebrews 4:15 Jesus can come to aid in your temptation. Jesus did battle with the devil and was victorious. Jesus is the perfect mediator between God and man. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(27).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 5/6/2022 9:57:57 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE TEMPTATIOIN OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 INTRODUCTION A man was on a diet and struggling. He had to go downtown and as he started out, he remembered that his route would take him by the doughnut shop. As he got closer, he thought that a cup of coffee would hit the spot. Then he remembered his diet. That s when he prayed, Lord, if You want me to stop for a doughnut and coffee, let there be a parking place in front of the shop. He said, Sure enough, I found a parking place right in front on my seventh time around the block! PREPARATION; TEMPTATION, TRIUMPH PREPARATION The Timing Notice the word immediately in Mark's version. There is no lag time between the triumph and the temptation. You are never more vulnerable than when you are coming out of a great victory. Sometimes (often) after we experience some of our greatest spiritual victories, we face our greatest challenges. Adrian Rogers said Be prepared. As soon as God opens the windows of Heaven to bless us, the devil will open the doors of Hell to blast us. There is the blessing and then the battle. 1 Corinthians 10:12 (WEB) Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn't fall. e.g. Elijah 1 Kings 18 19:1-4 won a great battle against 450 prophets of Baal, outran the chariot of Ahab. Less than 24 hours later, he is running from Jezebel, becomes depressed and asks God to let him die. He went from the mountain of victory to the valley of despair. I am not trying to keep you from enjoying the mountain top. But we are most vulnerable when on top of the world We let our guard down when we feel invincible. Israel delivered from Egypt and Pharaoh s army comes after them Hezekiah left the solemn Passover than Sennacherib came against him. No sooner had Paul received an abundance of revelations than he was assaulted with vile temptations." The Influence Full of the Holy Spirit Acts. 10:37, 38 Wuest's paraphrase "Jesus, in the control of the Holy Spirit." One who is filled with is controlled by An important doctrinal position must be discussed Luke has made a point about the human side of Jesus Favorite title: Son of Man The narrative of chapters 1-3 carefully lays that out Jesus is fully man and fully God You have heard the word kenosis From Philippians 2 we know Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes Did not consider equality with God a thing to be grasped He chose to "empty" Himself of His divine prerogatives (something we cannot fully grasp or explain), and thus lived His life as an example of the perfect life Adam should have lived and the "ideal life" to which all believers today should aspire and seek to imitate. Jesus relied on the same Source of power, the Holy Spirit, that you and I have access to today, for the Spirit of Christ indwells every believer The Holy Spirit is a Person. You do not receive him in dosages. Just like marriage; you get the whole thing. Jesus was fully surrendered to the Spirit s control. We have the Spirit but the question is does the Spirit have us? Victory over temptation begins here. Acts 6:3-8 the first deacons and Stephen mentioned as full of the Spirit Acts 7:55 Stephen Acts 11:23, 24 Barnabas Acts 13:8-10 negative filling Acts 19:28- describes much of our society; filled with rage The upshot is that to be filled with the Spirit is to be controlled by the Spirit and thus supernaturally empowered or enabled to accomplish what cannot be accomplished naturally. You are either controlled by the Holy Spirit or your fallen flesh. Galatians 5:16 The Holy Spirit empowers life in all its dimensions: personal, corporate, in the world Gordan Fee: Believers in Christ, who for Paul are Spirit people first and foremost, are variously described as living by the Spirit, walking in the Spirit, being led by the Spirit, bearing the fruit of the Spirit, and sowing to the Spirit. The Spirit is therefore the empowering presence of God for living the life of God in the present. Jon Courson on Jesus temptation: The Spirit led Him into the place of temptation not to do Him in, but to show Him off. the Spirit was able to show off the power and the ability, the holiness and tenacity of Jesus. Life Application Note - Sometimes we feel that if the Holy Spirit leads us, it will always be "beside peaceful streams" (Psalm 23:2 ) Luke 4 tells us that is not always the case You may be led by the Holy Spirit into some difficult situations; some real trials To be sure we get ourselves into enough trouble; if so, confess and move on Matthew 4:1 Jesus was led up of the Spirit ; literally under the Spirit The Spirit is the cause or instrument; submission or subjection is the response Luke affirms that Jesus was now continuously under the guidance of the Holy Spirit. Hence in this same sentence he mentions the Spirit twice." Mark 1:12, 13 Jesus was driven into the wilderness; forced out, urgent necessity Andrew Murray: We have here the reason why the teaching of the imitation and likeness to Christ has so little prominence in the Church of Christ. Men sought it in their own strength, with the help of some workings of the Holy Spirit: they did not understand that nothing less was needed than being filled with the Spirit. No wonder that they thought that real conformity to Christ could not be expected of us, because they had mistaken thoughts about being filled with the Spirit. It was thought to be the privilege of a few, and not the calling and duty of every child of God. It was not sufficiently realized that Be ye filled with the Spirit, is a command to every Christian. Only when the Church first gives the baptism of the Spirit, and Jesus, as the Saviour who baptizes with the Spirit each one who believes in Him, their right place, only then will likeness to Christ be sought after and attained. People will then understand and acknowledge: to be like Christ we must be led by the same Spirit, and to be led by the Spirit as He was, we must be filled with the Spirit. Nothing less than the fulness of the Spirit is absolutely necessary to live a truly Christian, Christ-like life. Andrew Murray You and I cannot face the temptations of the world in our own strength Paul found that out and wrote about it in Romans 8 CONCLUSON In 1346, during the Hundred Years' War, the English army of King Edward III met a French battalion at Crecy, France. The King's son, Prince Edward, led one vital division of the British force while Edward III stood nearby with a strong band of soldiers, ready to send relief if needed. Soon after the battle started, the prince thought he was in danger, so he sent for help. But the king didn't come. Young Edward sent another message, pleading for immediate assistance. His father responded by telling the courier, "Go tell my son that I am not so inexperienced a commander as not to know when help is needed, nor so careless a father as not to send it." How are you preparing for temptation? Are you daily relying on the Holy Spirit? God knows when help is needed. He also knows that the proper resources are in place. Following the lines of least resistance make men and rivers crooked . Seek daily to be controlled by the Holy Spirit of God and you will not yield to temptation. THE TEMPTATIOIN OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 PREPARATION The Timing You are never more vulnerable than when you are coming out of a great victory. 1 Corinthians 10:12 The Influence One who is filled with is controlled by Jesus is fully man and fully God Philippians 2 kenosis Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes Jesus was fully surrendered to the Spirit s control. To be filled with the Spirit is to besupernaturally empowered or enabled to accomplish what cannot be accomplished naturally. Galatians 5:16 The Spirit is therefore the empowering presence of God for living the life of God in the present. You may be led by the Holy Spirit into some difficult situations The Spirit is the cause or instrument; submission or subjection is the response Nothing less than the fulness of the Spirit is absolutely necessary to live a truly Christian, Christ-like life. Andrew Murray Following the lines of least resistance make men and rivers crooked . Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(28).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 5/27/2022 9:38:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE TEMPTATION OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 Review: Our study of the temptations of Jesus began by looking at the PREPARATION FOR TEMTPATION. The Scripture tells us plainly Jesus was driven by the Holy Spirit into the wilderness to demonstrate for us that as the Second Adam He was able to overcome the Devil. Not only did the Holy Spirit direct Him into this battle, the Holy Spirit controlled the responses to each temptation over the 40 day period. And that though the temptations were unique to Jesus, the final three temptations do present to us a category of temptations we will engage. The categories can be defined as The Lust of the Flesh, The Lust of the Eyes, and the Pride of Life. (1 John 2) We suggested last week that the first recorded temptation was a direct attack on the Relationship of the Son to the Father. The declaration was just made at the baptism of Jesus (3:22) and now it is called into question. The question was not whether the Relationship was real, but can the Relationship be trusted. Why would a loving God allow His own Son to be in such a desolate situation? The Father cannot be trusted; He really does not care for His own; you are foolish not to take matters into your own hands and turn stones into bread. You are very hungry and Your Father does not care. Using an account from Israel s history in Deuteronomy 8, Jesus simply states that obeying God is more important than eating. Man does not live by bread alone The only reason you are alive is because God wills it so, not because you have food on the table. Have you ever asked, does God really care? So the first temptation we have here is to question God s provision; you may have to take matters into your own hands because the time is now and God is no where to be found. Take care of yourself; look out for Number 1; serve yourself. TEMTPATION NUMBER 2 3:5-8 The Record 3:5-7 Gospel accounts; temptations 2 and 3 are reversed Luke was more interested in theology than chronology It was important for his purposes to finish at the temple Kingdom Rule The Offer of Kingdom Rule We do not know how this was accomplished Miraculous physical transport; done by spiritual vision The exposure was real. all means all ; not just the Roman empire (Acts 17:6; 24:5) Matthew 4:8 these kingdoms have glory in a moment of time flash, instantaneous The authority of Satan is hotly debated: did he even have the authority to make such an offer? ruler of this world - John 12:41; 14:30; 16:11 god of this world 2 Cor. 4:4 His rule is delegated: Lo, his doom is sure. One little word shall fell him Satan will ultimately find a human ruler in the antichrist; the beast Rev. 13:3, 4 domain sometimes translated dominion, power the sphere in which the power is exercised Acts 26:18; Eph. 2:2 glory a seductive, transient glory; the glory is passing away; 1 John 2:17 for it has been handed over to me when? The sin of Adam Adam lost his kingdom and crown Hebrews 2:8 the earth originally was subject to man; the tempter usurped the crown There was a change in command Jesus, as the second Adam; will restore all the first man lost 1 John 5:19 the whole world lies in the power of the evil one There are in reality two kingdoms; no middle ground Believers are in God s kingdom; aliens and strangers here citizenship in heaven This world: politics, economics, education, entertainment, much of its religion, lies in the power of the evil one; hostile to God and His children The Price of Kingdom Rule Worshipping the devil; to bow down and prostrate oneself Not only a position of worship but just the very thought of The request was to break the first commandment Just one little act of worship all this shall be yours Satan knew Psalm 2; that God would do this very thing He is seeking to take the place of God Jesus did not question or reject Satan s authority to offer What was the hook? Where is the disguise, the deception? Satan never reveals the price tag of following him He is a liar; there is no reason to believe his offer would have been honored The OT prophets predicted that the descendants of David would rule over the entire world and that many people would come to Jerusalem to worship the one true God. This temptation to set up an earthly kingdom that would displace the Roman government meant that Jesus would need to compromise His calling. It meant He would have to use the world s methods to bring men freedom. It meant a crown without a cross It meant spreading the Kingdom by worshipping evil which would lead to ultimate doom The Response 3:8 Worship is an attitude that includes action Worship is intimately linked to service 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.'" Sunday worship is either validated or proven hypocritical Monday Saturday Satan said nothing about service but Jesus knew that whatever we worship we will serve Romans 6 Warren Wiersbe; Service to the Lord is true freedom, but service to Satan is terrible bondage. God's pattern is to start with suffering and end with glory 1 Peter 5:10 , while Satan's pattern is to start with glory and end with suffering. Satan wants us to sacrifice the eternal for the temporary and take the "easy way." Him only thou shall serve Christians are constantly attacked at this point. In the attempt to further the cause of God they sometimes stoop to use less than godly means. Often Christians are as so caught up in ministry that they forget who they are serving. The program is pushed, but the Person is pushed aside If the reason for the Kingdom is forgotten (relationship with God), then the means of building the Kingdom are liable to be compromised. the easy way Jesus scriptural response demonstrates that this was the point of the temptation. The object of worship is whatever or whoever has one s ultimate attention. Jesus relationship with the Father was what enabled Him to conquer Satan Jesus was concerned with the life-style (serve) as well as goals. Quoted Deuteronomy: (6:13; 10:20) Israel in the wilderness The nation was tempted to worship something other than God The nation failed; Jesus succeeded Temptation Number 1 was the Relationship with the Father s Provision; can God be trusted? Temptation Number 2 was the Relationship with the Father s Plan Was there an easier way to accomplish receiving the kingdoms without a cross? The Father s Plan involved great suffering God s will for your life: 1 Peter 5:10 There is preaching today that promote eternal life without a cross There is preaching today that promotes discipleship without taking up your cross God has a plan. Don t look for an easy way out; don t look for a detour THE TEMPTATION OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 TEMTPATION NUMBER 2 3:5-8 The Record 3:5-7 Kingdom Rule Matthew 4:8 ruler of this world - John 12:41; 14:30; 16:11 god of this world 2 Cor. 4:4 His rule is delegated Adam lost his kingdom and crown Jesus, as the second Adam; will restore all the first man lost 1 John 5:19 The Price of Kingdom Rule Psalm 2 It meant a crown without a cross The Response 3:8 Worship is an attitude that includes action Romans 6 1 Peter 5:10 Deuteronomy 6:13; 10:20 Temptation Number 1 was the Relationship with the Father s Provision Temptation Number 2 was the Relationship with the Father s Plan Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(29).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/11/2022 10:48:27 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE TEMPTATION OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 INTRODUCTION The Preparation for Temptation Each temptation was to drive a wedge between Jesus and His Father Each temptation was to drive a wedge in the Relationship Temptation #1: The Relationship of God s Provision Does He really love me? Can I really trust Him to take care of me? Man does not live by bread alone The only reason we are alive is because God wills it so; not because you have something to eat. Temptation #2: The Relationship of God s Plan Satan provided the opportunity to rule the nations without the cross Dr. Edward Judson, son of Adoniram Judson: My father suffered greatly in Burma, and as a result there has come into existence all these great missionary agencies of this day. If you get anything without suffering, it s because somebody else suffered for you. And if you suffer and do not succeed, somebody else will get something because you suffered. Satan offered a throne without a cross You shall worship God and Him only you shall serve Jesus knew you could not worship without serving; note His answer Romans 6:16 (WEB) Don't you know that to whom you present yourselves as servants to obedience, his servants you are whom you obey; whether of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness? Is you serve sin, sin is your master? You cannot worship God without serving Him. Bob Burney spoke about an exchange the woman taken in adultery made Is not it time to exchange the serving of Satan for the worship of Jehovah? TEMPTATION NUMBER 3 3:9-13 The Record 3:9-11 We have already cleared the usage of the word if A class 1 condition: properly defined as since ; not questioning His who He is He led brought: used in 4:1 of the HS A man led by the Spirit is not likely to be misled by the devil Used again in 4:29 led Him to the brow of the hill 4:29 is one of those opportune times of 4:13 Townspeople under the influence; serving the desires of the devil sought to kill Acts 8:32: He was led as a sheep to the slaughter The definite article before pinnacle means Luke had a specific pinnacle in mind Most suggests a place about 450 feet above the Kidron Valley cast yourself down throw yourself down Aorist imperative: sense of urgency: do it now; just do it; don t delay Phillips - Satan was using his powers of persuasion on Christ, just as he does when he tries to trap us. We need to remember that Satan can persuade, but he cannot push. He creates the temptation; we create the transgression. Kent Hughes "By urging (commanding) Jesus to jump, Satan was trying to take advantage of Jesus twice-voiced determination to trust and obey God s Word You are committed to obeying God s Word, so obey this! "....There may even have been a messianic overtone in Satan s challenge because a later Rabbinic saying read: Our teachers have taught, when the King, the Messiah, reveals himself, he will come and stand on the roof of the Temple (Pesiqta rabbati, 36). Add to this the tendency of the godly to be attracted by the challenge to step out in faith and you can feel the tug of the temptation. In fact, refusal or even hesitancy in such situations could appear to be a lack of faith. It was a powerful, beguiling temptation. Satan was saying, in effect, In the first temptations you have shown your trust in God s Word. You are a faithful man Psalm 91 is you! This is what the people are looking for in a Messiah. You are a man of faith, aren t you? Just jump, and the whole world will see who you really are! Really? Jesus thought otherwise. He knew that he had no word from the Father directing him to leap off the temple. He again recognized that as God s Son he must never do anything in his own power, for that would be putting God to the test. Augustine said that Satan can do no more than suggest. The tempted person must perform the act Jesus knew that to start His ministry by dramatically jumping from the pinnacle would be completely contrary to God s will Jesus refused to take this shortcut He refused to let anything break His relationship with God This temptation continued to confront Jesus at every turn of His ministry Anything short of the cross would have made His Messiahship reduced to a mere military operation. 4:10 Note this attack: so you live by the Word of God; here is what God says So You intend to live by the Scriptures, he implied. Then let me quote You a verse of Scripture and see if You will obey it! for it has been written perfect tense; settle forever in heaven Satan left out a portion in all thy ways It is not so much what he left out as how he uses the text Other NT writers also took some liberties in quoting the OT If misquotation was the issue, Jesus could have easily corrected Misapplication of text is Satan at his best 2 Timothy 2:15 rightly divide cut a straight path through The devil knows Scripture; the devil perverts Scripture He took Psalm 91:11, 12 and altered the text from trusting God to testing God Note 91:13 You will tread on the lion and cobra. You will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot. Spurgeon: God will help us in our ways if we keep in His ways. I am convinced we have no idea the times that God has provided His protection. * Anyone who has the firm conviction that he will never be forsaken by the Lord will not be unduly anxious, because he will depend on his providence. -- John Calvin The Response 4:12, 13 The secret of Jesus victory was not in the His rote memory of Scripture It was not head knowledge of Scripture that revealed God s will but Jesus relationship with the Father The prerequisite for properly interpreting Scripture is a living relationship with God Scripture was not quoted as some magical incantation but because He has an already existing relationship with His heavenly Father which these Scriptures reveal. You shall not put the Lord your God to the test as you tested Him at Massah Deuteronomy 6:16 referring to Exodus 17:1-7 If Jesus had jumped off the Temple, He would have "put God on the spot" so to speak, forcing Him to act. This would have been presumptuously "testing" His Father which is something He refused to do. it is never right to do wrong in order to get a chance to do right Testing God is not trusting God We test God by presuming upon His will. We take a swan dive into a relationship and cry out for Him to catch us before we hit bottom we rationalize until our rationalizations become rational lies We presume something to be His will simply because it is something we want WWW "We tempt God when we put ourselves into circumstances that force Him to work miracles on our behalf. The diabetic who refuses to take insulin and argues, Jesus will take care of me, may be tempting the Lord. We tempt God when we try to force Him to contradict His own Word 3:13 Though Satan was the agent of the temptations, God used them as a proving ground for Jesus Peirazo can mean not only to tempt but to try or test one s character . (Psalm 26:2; John 6:6; 2 Cor. 13:5; Heb. 11:17; Rev. 2:2) The devil just waited for the next opportunity Incited the town folk to throw Him off a cliff Incited the religious leaders to stone Him Incited Peter to rebuke Him Incited Judas to betray Him Incited Peter to deny Him All about convincing Jesus that man was not worth dying for. Satan lost that battle, but he continues to fight. Despite his efforts, people are still coming to Jesus. Hallelujah for the Cross The Temptations of Jesus Each challenged all aspects of His existence as a whole person Each of them attacked one common point: they attempted to distract Jesus from or to destroy His relationship with God the Father The Provision of God; The Plan of God; Presuming upon God Jesus conquered the Temptations as a real man using the same resources available to us Because he himself suffered when he was tempted, he is able to help those who are being tempted - Hebrews 2:18 He was filled with the Holy Spirit He used the Word of God Kevin Zuber summarizes Jesus' temptations as "(1) Serve Yourself (Lk 4:3-4 ); (2) Honor Yourself (Lk 4:5-8 ); (3) Be spectacular (be presumptuous and prove Your self-importance; Lk 4:9-12 ). CONCERN FOR PROVISION, take matters into your own hands; issue of trust POWER, avoid the cross and work of redemption PROTECTION COMPARISON OF GENESIS 3 AND LUKE 4 The Appeal of Physical Appetite eat of tree turn stones to bread The Appeal to Power or Glory will be like God world s kingdoms The Appeal of Personal Gain you will not die not strike your foot THE TEMPTATION OF JESUS Luke 4:1-13 Temptation #1: The Relationship of God s Provision Temptation #2: The Relationship of God s Plan TEMPTATION NUMBER 3 3:9-13 The Record 3:9-11 A man led by the Spirit is not likely to be misled by the devil Satan can persuade, but he cannot push. He creates the temptation; we create the transgression. If misquotation was the issue, Jesus could have easily corrected Misapplication of text is Satan at his best 2 Timothy 2:15 The devil knows Scripture; the devil perverts Scripture Spurgeon: God will help us in our ways if we keep in His ways. The Response 4:12, 13 The prerequisite for properly interpreting Scripture is a living relationship with God Testing God is not trusting God We test God by presuming upon His will. We rationalize until our rationalizations become rational lies We presume something to be His will simply because it is something we want The devil just waited for the next opportunity All about convincing Jesus that man was not worth dying for. COMPARISON OF GENESIS 3 AND LUKE 4 The Appeal of Physical Appetite The Appeal to Power or Glory The Appeal of Personal Gain Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(30).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/18/2022 5:04:50 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message OUR EXAMPLE IS HE LUKE 4:14 And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee What does it mean to walk in the Spirit? Why has Luke regularly inserted the Holy Spirit into his gospel? How is this to influence our daily life? Many commentaries give little attention to the statement returned in power Yet, the statement has rattled in my brain all week. We must explore the statement s significance to believers and the local church Is this only an historical account or something for the local church to imitate? Is it too bold to suggest that the lack of exploring this statement has led the church to come up with all sorts of programs to counteract the lack of understanding? A.W. Tozer is quoted to have said, If God were to take the Holy Spirit out of this world, most of what the churches are doing would go right on and nobody would know the difference. Do we know the difference? How do you know you are walking in the Spirit of God? Can you give an example of walking in the Spirit? Is this church led by the Spirit or do we make it up as we go along? Is the condition of this ministry because it is Spirit led or the lack thereof? Luke 4:14 has been cited as key for effective ministry To ignore this key will result in ministry of wood, hay, straw I Cor. 3:9-15 The context of which is to pastors, elders, teachers John 15:5 explains why we must abide in Him Question: do we minister in our natural or supernatural power? My old fleshly power or the Holy Spirit s power? Luke and the Holy Spirit 1:35, 3:22, 4:1 We know Luke s favorite title for Jesus: Son of Man We believe that Jesus was in all ways fully God and fully man R.C. Sproul: truly God and truly man because fully leaves no room for anything else We believe Philippians 2: Jesus yielded the independent exercise of His divine attributes So, He lived on earth as truly man Temptations: He refused to turn stones into bread because man lives by the Word of God His human nature was sinlessly perfect, BUT it did have supernatural power. Christ performed miraculous deeds through the Spirit s power. So much so that to attribute His works to Satan is to blaspheme the Holy Spirit Matthew 12:31 32 The success of His ministry is attributed to the Spirit; not His essential deity. Thomas Constable: The Spirit empowered and enabled Jesus in His words and deeds. Luke would stress His teaching ministry. MacArthur: The Spirit s power was essential, since in His deity Christ would not have done the miraculous deeds He did, or say the divine words He spoke The Jesus Way Luke s emphasis: Jesus continually depended on the Holy Spirit for supernatural power Consequently He was a model that all believers can and should copy. JESUS BELIEVERS Luke 3:22 anointed by the Spirit 1 John 2:20, 2:27- Luke 4:1 Controlled by the Spirit Eph. 5:18 Luke 4:14 Empowered by the Spirit Acts 1:8; Eph. 3:16 It is all the same Spirit Underscores every believer s desperate need to rely on the same Source of power Fruitful believers absolutely must: 1 John 2:6- walk in the same manner as He walked 1 Peter 2:21- follow in His steps 1 Cor. 11:1- "Be (present imperative ) imitators of me, just as I also am of Christ Adrian Rogers Jesus went to face the devil under the leadership of the Spirit. Jesus defeated the devil and came out of that temptation experience in the power of the Holy Spirit.....If Jesus overcame the devil as God, then He's no example to me. But if Jesus overcame the devil as a man filled with the Holy Spirit, the same Holy Spirit that was available to Jesus is available to me. Jesus full of the Holy Ghost overcame Satan. You're no match for Satan, but dear friend, the Holy Spirit of God who is resident in you is. And unless there is that principle of sonship, there can never be that principle of submission. And unless there's that principle of submission, there can never be that principle of spirit-filledness. Because God does not give His Holy Spirit in full power to rebels. French Arrington Just as the third Gospel relates the life and ministry of Jesus, who executes His mission in the power of the Holy Spirit, the sequel to Luke's Gospel, the Acts of the Apostles, records the life and ministry of the early church as it is empowered by the Holy Spirit to bear witness to the saving works of Jesus from Jerusalem to Rome. That is, Jesus and His Spirit-filled ministry is the model for the Church; the Spirit-anointed ministry of Christ foreshadows and prepares for the worldwide mission of the Church CONCLUSION Warren Wiersbe - Because we have trusted Jesus Christ and received salvation, we have the Holy Spirit dwelling within us (Rom. 8:9 ), but does the Holy Spirit have us? When evangelist D. L. Moody was ministering in Great Britain, a critical minister asked, Does Mr. Moody have a monopoly on the Holy Spirit? A friend replied, No, but the Holy Spirit has a monopoly on D. L. Moody. The power of the Holy Spirit is released to each one of us on the basis of our obedience to the Spirit of the living God. sonship, submission, controlling What would take place in our churches if the Holy Spirit were completely removed? How much of our ministry would still remain? The church needs to have a renewed desire to be desperately dependent on the Spirit for everything. Foundational teaching on the Holy Spirit is better than a marriage seminar. Better than a child rearing conference. Better than a reaching your community seminar. Desiring God Ministries: We join Jesus in destroying the devil s works not mainly by casting out demons, or working miracles, or engaging in power encounters, but by teaching the truth. OUR EXAMPLE IS HE LUKE 4:14 key for effective ministry LUKE AND THE HOLY SPIRIT 1:35, 3:22, 4:1 truly God and truly man Philippians 2 His human nature was sinlessly perfect, BUT it did have supernatural power. Matthew 12:31 32 THE JESUS WAY JESUS BELIEVERS Luke 3:22 Anointed by the Spirit 1 John 2:20, 2:27 Luke 4:1 Controlled by the Spirit Ephesians 5:18 Luke 4:14 Empowered by the Spirit Acts 1:8; Eph. 3:16 Fruitful believers absolutely must: 1 John 2:6 Walk 1 Peter 2:21 Follow 1 Cor. 11:1 Be Adrian Rogers principle of sonship principle of submission principle of spirit-filledness Because God does not give His Holy Spirit in full power to rebels The church needs to have a renewed desire to be desperately dependent on the Spirit Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(31).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/25/2022 7:59:00 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message RIGHT HERE, RIGHT NOW! Luke 4:14-21 INTRODUCTION The Context of Luke 4:14 Luke jumps from the temptations to ministry in Galilee Yet some considerable time had intervened It is interesting to note that some refer to this time as a year of obscurity Though not mentioned in the synoptic gospels, John does record some events Remember: John 21:25 there are not enough books to record all that Jesus did and said John s gospel fills us in: John the Baptist was arrested The first disciples are identified: Andrew, Simon, Philip, Nathaniel The first miracle is performed in Cana at the wedding feast Jesus goes to Passover; turns over the tables Jesus has His encounter with Nicodemus Then Jesus returns to Galilee in the power of the Spirit On His journey speaks with the woman at the well in Samaria Word of His miracle and ministry had reached back to Galilee Almost one year after His baptism, Jesus returns to Galilee Matthew and Mark record Galilean ministry beginning at the arrest of John the baptizer Luke placed John s arrest in 3:19:20; but we stated his work is theological and chronological Galilee: Josephus tells us The area was 50 miles N to S; 25 miles E to W Included over 200 villages; each with a population of 15,000 very fertile: a saying that it was easier to raise a legion of olives in Galilee than it was to bring up one child in Judea Galilee comes from a word meaning circle Galilee was surrounded by Gentiles: Phoenicians, Syrians, Samaritans Open to new ideas Regarding roads: it is said the traffic of the world passed through Galilee Geographically: original tribal homes of Naphtali, Asher, Zebulun From the 8th to 2nd century overrun by Gentiles Daily Study Bible: The most amazing thing of all is that in 104 B.C. Aristobulus reconquered Galilee for the Jewish nation, and proceeded forcibly to circumcise the inhabitants of Galilee, and thus to make them Jews whether they liked it or not. History had compelled Galilee to open its doors to new strains of blood and to new ideas and to new influences. The natural characteristics of the Galileans, and the preparation of history had made Galilee the one place in all Palestine where a new teacher with a new message had any real chance of being heard, and it was there that Jesus began his mission and first announced his message. John MacArthur gives an excellent alliterative summary of Luke 4:14-15 * We find the place of His ministry: Galilee. * We find the power of His ministry: the Holy Spirit. * We find the popularity of His ministry: spreading throughout all the surrounding district. * We find the priority of His ministry; teaching in the synagogues. * And we find the praise of His ministry, as people responded to Him. A word about His popularity Location of two early miracles: Jn. 2: water to wine in Cana; Jn. 4: healing of the Nobleman s son So when Jesus showed up at the synagogue, you can be sure by the time He arrived in Nazareth, the place of meeting was full of people HIS PLATFORM Synagogue To lead together; literally describes a bringing together or congregating in one place Originating during the Babylonian captivity; a place of worship and instruction Continued their existence after the temple was rebuilt by Zerubbabel Required at least 10 men to establish Therefore, many towns and villages had more than one synagogue Some locations had over 100 synagogues organized by families or even vocations If no specific location or less than 10 men, would meet by a river; Acts 16 Synagogues also provided schools for children The man in charge of the scrolls was often the school administrator Each location had one man in charge and an assistant Synagogues were built so that the teacher was facing Jerusalem They did not necessarily have a priest There was no altar, no sacrifices, nothing that was uniquely the temple The reader stood for the reading of the Law and sat down to teach e.g. the elders before Ezekiel: (Ezekiel 8:1 ; Ezekiel 11:15-16 ; Ezekiel 14:1 ; Ezekiel 20:1 ) Services generally included singing, reading the Law, reciting Deut. 6:4; several benedictions, reading the prophets, instruction, and final benediction The operation of a synagogue is very similar to what a church does today. Should note: God used the Babylonian captivity to establish system of instruction that was used while in captivity and became part of the fabric of Judaism in the land of Israel. These provided ready made places for Jesus to teach and ready make places for the early apostle to present the gospel. HIS PURPOSE 4:15 He Taught All done in the power of the HS Miracles accompanied the teaching: Matt. 4:23-25 Taught: Gk didaskolos; Eng. Didiactic; the passing on of information He was continually teaching Over 200 villages, many with multiple synogogues Plenty of opportunities to teach Public response: Luke 4:15 , 22 , 28 , 32 , 36 , 37 A T Robertson adds General admiration of Jesus everywhere. He was the wonder teacher of his time. Even the rabbis had not yet learned how to ridicule and oppose Jesus. Attendance: as was His custom You knew where Jesus was going to be on the Sabbath Two other things He did regularly: He prayed: Luke 22:39-46 He taught: Mark 10:1 stood up the pattern of reading; probably at the invitation of the leader Providence: present reading in Isaiah; Jesus did not choose; it was handed to Him by the attendant: v. 20 under-rower (1 Cor. 4:1) Entirety of book could be in one scroll Opened to passage: whether assigned or chosen? HIS PROPOSAL 4:18-21 Isaiah 61 anointed Messianic passage recognized by the people announce good news to make whole To preach restoration of sight The benefactors: The poor The broken hearted The captives The blind Sin, suffering, broken, oppressed 4:19 to preach the acceptable year of the Lord What the people heard: the year of Jubilee Leviticus. 25:10 the year of the cancellation of all debt, the return of all property, slaves were set free Became of figure of spiritual, divine forgiveness Jubilee Michael Card The Lord provided for a time for the slaves to be set free For the debts to all be canceled so His chosen ones could see His deep desire was for forgiveness, He longed to see their liberty And His yearning was embodied in the Year of Jubilee At the Lord's appointed time His deep desire became a man The heart of all true jubilation and with joy we understand In his voice we hear a trumpet sound that tells us we are free He is the incarnation Of the year of Jubilee Chorus: Jubilee, Jubilee Jesus is our Jubilee Debts forgiven Slaves set free Jesus is our Jubilee V. 21 This day, this Scripture is fulfilled He began to say there was a lot more to the message Luke gives us the summary of the message Today. Now. This moment Jesus declares He is the fulfillment of the passage The promised new age of deliverance has arrived because the Deliverer has arrived This is the dawning of the era of Messianic salvation The real year of Jubilee has come RIGHT HERE, RIGHT NOW! Luke 4:14-21 Almost one year after His baptism, Jesus returns to Galilee Galilee: The area was 50 miles N to S; 25 miles E to W Included over 200 villages Galilee was surrounded by Gentiles Location of two early miracles HIS PLATFORM Synagogue bringing together Originating during the Babylonian captivity Required at least 10 men to establish Synagogues also provided schools for children Synagogues were built so that the teacher was facing Jerusalem HIS PURPOSE 4:15 He Taught Didiactic; the passing on of information Public response: Luke 4:15 , 22 , 28 , 32 , 36 , 37 You knew where Jesus was going to be on the Sabbath HIS PROPOSAL 4:18-21 anointed Messianic passage recognized by the people What the people heard: the year of Jubilee Became of figure of spiritual, divine forgiveness Luke gives us the summary of the message Today. Now. This moment This is the dawning of the era of Messianic salvation Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(32).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/2/2022 12:12:35 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE WELCOMED YEAR Luke 4:18 The Trinity A gospel of healing, liberty, illumination, and comfort for all, beginning with the lowest of the people. Pulpit Commentary on He has anointed Me to preach the Gospel - The common interpretation referred this passage to the state of the people on the return from the Captivity. Nothing, however, that the people had yet experienced in any way satisfied the brilliant picture painted in the great prophecy.
