A remnant certainly had returned several centuries back from their distant exile, but the large majority of the chosen people were scattered abroad; their own land was crushed under what seemed a hopeless servitude; poverty, ignorance, universal discontent, reigned alike in Jerusalem, garrisoned with Roman legionaries, and in the most distant of the poor upland villages of Galilee. Only could deliverance come and a golden age of prosperity return with the promised Messiah. This was the interpretation which the choicest spirits in Israel applied to the great Isaiah prophecy read that sabbath day in the little synagogue of Nazareth. This was the meaning which Jesus at once gave to it, only he startled his hearers by telling them that in him they saw the promised long-looked-for Deliverer. We only possess, it is evident, the very barest abstract of the words of the Teacher Jesus on this occasion. They must have been singularly eloquent, winning, and powerful to have extorted the wonder and admiration alluded to in the twenty-second verse. Anointed: consecrated to spiritual service Acts 4:27; 10:38 Jesus' purpose was to proclaim the truth of the eternal life giving Good News to souls dead in their trespasses and sins. In reading Isaiah 61, Jesus quoted the LXX The last phrase: to set free the oppressed is from Isa. 58:6 William MacDonald comments on what Jesus read in Isaiah - Notice the revolutionary implications of the Messiah s mission. He came to deal with the enormous problems that have afflicted mankind throughout history: * Poverty. To preach the gospel to the poor. * Sorrow. To heal the brokenhearted. (If one accepts the KJV rendering) * Bondage. To proclaim liberty to the captives. * Suffering. And recovery of sight to the blind. * Oppression. To set at liberty those who are oppressed. YLT TO PROCLAIM GOOD NEWS TO THE POOR To preach the gospel to the poor To tell glad tidings unto the destitute Preach the gospel; good news Poor: to crouch, beggar; pauper Matthew 5:3 blessed are the poor Luke 7:22 part of the report to John; Jesus was doing exactly what the OT said although not in the manner of what was expected Another common word for poor: Luke 21:2, 3 poor to toil for daily subsistence Message to individuals who were dependent on others for their daily needs The crouched and extended on hand with a cup and hid their face with the other A person completely dependent The word includes moral and physical poverty; A soul who is worldly rich is less likely to see his spiritual poverty (Laodicea 3:17) It is the person who has been marginalized by the world for their faith that are most open to the gospel YLT TO HEAL THE BROKEN IN HEART To heal the broken hearted To heal the crushed in heart The contrite of heart Heal: to cure, make whole; (28x) Matthew 8:8; 1 Peter 2:24 To crush completely; to shatter; broken to shivers (8x) Matt. 12:20; Rev. 2:27 Part of the Isaiah text but left out of many translations "He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted" in Isaiah 61:1 which is included only in the King James Version (and Young's Literal Version) which are based on the Greek Textus Receptus. sent one Hebrews 3:1 To send off with a commission as one s personal representative Sent by God: He belongs to God He is commissioned to be sent out He possesses all the authority and power of God Sent: perfect indicative; sent with a commission which remains upon Him John 4:34; 17:4; YLT TO PROCLAIM TO CAPTIVES DELIVERANCE To preach deliverance to the captives To announce to prisoners You are free To proclaim a pardon Proclaim release to Proclaim: herald (as a public crier) Captive: prisoner of war; (1x); from 259 (1x); 2 Peter 2:12 to be taken One who is taken captive by spear point Here is a soul in moral and spiritual bondage to the world, the flesh, the devil Isaiah 42:7 Isaiah 5:13 go into exile go into bondage (LXX) lack of knowledge we must be saturated with the Word of God 2 Timothy 2:25, 26 the power of knowledge Deliverance: (aphesis); freedom, forgiveness, liberty, remission (16x) Matt. 26:28; Heb. 10:18 The deliverance is not a half-way thing; Jesus work is full and complete A once and for all separation; put apart; leave behind Remission of sins is release from the penalty, the power, and ultimate the presence Aphesis is one aspect of redemption: Eph 1:7 Redemption: to buy back, pay a price; ransomed Purchased not by silver or gold but by the precious blood of Jesus Aphesis: to free from obligation; liberating a soul from that which confines them Year of Jubilee: Leviticus 25 LXX translates it Year of Release Setting free of indebted servants We have been released from our sin debt There are many forms of spiritual bondage Bondage to money Luke 19:1-10 Bondage to Satan Luke 8:26-39 Bondage to guilt Luke 7:41-50 Bondage to sensuality; hatred He breaks the power of cancelled sin , He sets the prisoner free; His blood can make the foulest clean; His blood availed for me. Charles Wesley (1739) YLT AND TO BLIND RECEIVING OF SIGHT And recovering of sight to the blind To the blind, You will see again Blind: opaque; blind, (physically, mentally) (48x) Matt. 9:27; Rev. 3:17 Receiving of sight: restoration, recovery of sight; (1x) from anablepo: to look up; recover sight (24x) Matt. 11:5; Acts 22:13 Within the context of the proclamation of the gospel, Jesus is speaking of opening the eyes of one s heart to see spiritual truth 1 Corinthians 2:14 Acts 26:16-18 Blindness is a problem of those with religion but no relationship to Jesus Scribes and Pharisees of Jesus day; John 9:39-41 Revelation 3:17, 18 2 Corinthians 4:18 a description of believers YLT TO SEND AWAY THE BRUISED WITH DELIVERANCE To set at liberty them that are bruised To dispatch the oppressed with a pardon To release the oppressed To set free the downstrodden To let the broken victims go free To set: to set apart, to send out; apostello; (130x); Matt. 2:16; Rev. 22:6 Bruised: to crush (1x) Deliverance: aphesis; (same as deliverance to captives) Isaiah 58:6 uses the words let go and Jesus says set free Jesus will do what the nation failed to do Jesus just doesn t proclaim a message, He brings the deliverance Macolm Muggeridge - All other freedoms, once won, soon turn into new servitude. Christ is the only Liberator whose liberation lasts forever. Oppressed: crushed, to shatter in pieces like pottery Here is a person broken in spirit by oppressive circumstances Downtrodden, overwhelmed with trouble; people who see no way of escape 24x in LXX; most speaking of literal crushing power Exodus 15:6 CONCLUSION He came for people dehumanized by sin and suffering, by brokenness and sorrow. For those who sin and those who suffer. For those who suffer because of sin. For those who sin to alleviate suffering. Lord, have mercy on us. Robert Gelinas, The Mercy Prayer THE WELCOMED YEAR Luke 4:18 TO PROCLAIM GOOD NEWS TO THE POOR Poor: to crouch, beggar Matthew 5:3 Luke 21:2, 3 The word includes moral and physical poverty; TO HEAL THE BROKEN IN HEART sent one Hebrews 3:1 He belongs to God He is commissioned to be sent out He possesses all the authority and power of God TO PROCLAIM TO CAPTIVES DELIVERANCE Captive: prisoner of war Isaiah 42:7 Isaiah 5:13 2 Timothy 2:25, 26 Deliverance: (aphesis); freedom Aphesis is one aspect of redemption: Eph 1:7 liberatng a soul AND TO BLIND RECEIVING OF SIGHT opening the eyes of one s heart to see spiritual truth 1 Corinthians 2:14 Acts 26:16-18 TO SEND AWAY THE BRUISED WITH DELIVERANCE Isaiah 58:6 uses the words let go and Jesus says set free Jesus just doesn t proclaim a message, He brings the deliverance For those who sin and those who suffer. For those who suffer because of sin. For those who sin to alleviate suffering. Lord, have mercy on us. * Robert Gelinas, The Mercy Prayer Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(33).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/9/2022 9:47:42 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE THE GIFT OF LIVING HOPE 1 Peter 1:3-5 INTRODUCTION The event of Vivian Wilson s passing into glory changed what I would like to do today. Our present text in Luke did not seem like the place to be on a day such as this. When such an event occupies so much of our prayer time and thoughts, I thought it appropriate to spend a few minutes talking about heaven. You probably have a favorite verse of thought about heaven which we will ask you to share in a few moments. Many years ago now, we had a bat in the house. It was not noticed until all were in bed and one of the kids thought it looked like a sock laying on the floor beside their bed. When they went to pick it up, it not only moved, it flew. It was alive. I have watched several videos of a live mannequin. Many times they have been painted up to blend in or sometimes they look like a bronze statue. Someone will come up to stand beside them or to sit beside them and the statue will move. The person is scared, sometimes screams, but n all occasions move swiftly away. It is alive. In each circumstance, something thought dead, or inanimate, was very much alive. Two thousand years ago Jesus died. Certified by the spear thrust in His side. Confirmed by the testimonies of experienced soldiers. Customarily placed in a tomb. He was dead. Three days later, He began to show Himself to a few individuals, then more than one at a time, then to a few hundred at one time. Soon the echo was heard throughout the land: He Is Risen Jesus was alive. I think it D. L. Moody who made these remarks. Someday you will hear that D. L. Moody has died. Don t you believe it. For at that time I will be more alive than I have ever been. We don t know a lot about heaven. Some things have been revealed to us. But many of you have a favorite verse about heaven. As prompted earlier, what verse or thought about heaven would you share with us this morning? For believers, the concept of heaven is one that brings great joy, blessing, confidence, hope. Many other folks you encounter have thoughts about heaven many of which are the products of wild imaginations or a warped view of Scripture. Verses like 1 Corinthians 2:9 But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him. What is warped about using that verse? Because they neglect the next verse: 2:10 which says: But to us, God revealed them through the Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God. 1 Peter 1:3-5 C. H. Spurgeon titled his message from this text: A String of Pearls John MacArthur used this title: Your Best Life Later Of course, his message was back when Joel Osteen s book Your Best Life Now was being heavily promoted in Christian circles. And just to be clear for any who may watch this video on YouTube, Joel Osteen s book is very much a poor representation of what the Bible says as is most of the rest of his ministry. It is not God s intention, purpose or design for you to guarantee you health and wealth. In fact, the Bible states just the opposite. As a believer in the Lord Jesus Christ you are to expect tribulation, trials, temptations, hardships. Once you received Christ as your Savior, you were immediately involved in a spiritual war; you were immediately the enemy of the devil when just a few moments before you were solidly on his side. Now to be sure, this is indeed the best life many people will ever experience. They may gain the whole world , live luxuriously, rule nations, have millions of followers on Twitter, but once they enter into death their soul is lost, they will be cast into the Lake of Fire where they will experience dying for all eternity. Peter was writing to early believers who were finding life difficult They were suffering just for being a Christian Rome was burning and they received the blame They were the problem of the culture; the trouble makers; the ones to blame for the troubles of the world. They were facing the hostilities and rejection of the world. Peter s overriding passion is for his readers to know the true nature of salvation God the Father planned it; God the Son achieved it; God the Holy Spirit applies it 1:3-5 is praise to the God of salvation 1:1, 2 described their unique relationship as the elect of God 3-5 is a wonderful doxology of praise; then sings my soul Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ To speak well of, to praise, to eulogize Blessing God for our salvation is the heart of worship His message: Hope Is Alive! THE GIFT: LIVING HOPE The world only has dead hope. Their hopes and dreams die when they die. If in this world only I have hope, I am most miserable. (1 Cor. 15:9) Rev. 3:17 Because thou sayest, I am rich, and increased with goods, and have need of nothing; and knowest not that thou art wretched, and miserable, and poor, and blind, and naked: We typically point to the homeless, serious ill, less fortunate financially as the hopeless But the happy, fulfilled suburban pagan is just as lost, just as hopeless He simply has accumulated more toys to shield him from the real condition of his soul Living hope is a real hope Paul: to die is gain Foundation of the Gift the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead As believers, we are in Christ; Christ is in us Therefore, when He died, we died; when He rose, we rose Christ s resurrection is absolutely huge for a Christian 1 Corinthians 15 should be a favorite of every Christian Resource of the Gift the abundant mercies of God Describes the activity of someone to an undeserving party It is not about a reward for performance mercies assumes two things: the need on the part of the recipient An adequate supply from the giver We are in a pitiful condition, desperate, wretched, miserable Lamentations 3:22 It is the Lord s mercies that we are not consumed abundant mercies much mercy Not crying out, it s not fair! if God was fair we would be dead It is by His mercy that we have anything at all It is by His mercy we have a new birth: begotten again caused us to be born again The new birth is encased in the great mercy of God Titus 3:5 According to His great mercy Ephesians 2:4 But God, who is rich in mercy, for His great love wherewith He loved us God s love is clothed in mercy Was our need that great? 2:5 we were dead God looked upon your dead soul, had compassion God had the mercy and capacity to do something about it Result of the Gift An Inheritance Not earned but received Ultimately, our inheritance is not a possession, but a Person It is not about some mansion, condo, apartment I m satisfied with a cottage below, A little silver, and a little gold. But in that city, where the ransomed will shine, I want a gold one, that s silver lined Our inheritance is Jesus Jesus, the very thought of Thee, with sweetness fills my breast; But sweeter far Thy face to see, and in Thy presence rest Jesus, our only joy be Thou, as Thou our prize wilt be; Jesus, by Thou our glory now and through eternity. Substance of Inheritance: Incorruptible Not decay, spoil, shrivel Purity of Inheritance: Undefiled Not sullied, not tainted Even inheritances in this world are polluted, stained Beauty of Inheritance: Fades not Away Not decline in beauty Security of Inheritance: Reserved To guard from loss or injury; to keep an eye on Result of Inheritance: Kept To guard as a sentinel Which answers two troubling thoughts: I m not good enough to become a Christian: 1:3 It is His mercy How do I know I will persevere: 1:5 God keep us; preservation of the saints There is protection; God s power is the garrison of our protection Timing of the Gift: Ready You remember when you couldn t wait to open gifts at Christmas or birthdays Or sometimes what was ordered was late in arriving The gift of the Living Hope is ready to revealed Ready to have the cover taken off It is all finished, completed, done All that remains is for the curtain to be lifted CONCLUSION Is your hope alive? And well? Life on this earth may not go as planned; So much of it may cause major disappointment Your earthly inheritance may go up in smoke, commandeered by the government, rust, decay, stolen By faith have you been born again? If so, your heavenly inheritance is guaranteed Blessing God for our salvation is the heart of worship. THE GIFT OF LIVING HOPE 1 Peter 1:3-5 Blessing God for our salvation is the heart of worship Foundation of the Gift the resurrection of Jesus Christ Resource of the Gift the abundant mercies of God Titus 3:5 Ephesians 2:4 Result of the Gift An Inheritance our inheritance is not a possession, but a Person Substance of Inheritance: Incorruptible Purity of Inheritance: Undefiled Beauty of Inheritance: Fades not Away Security of Inheritance: Reserved Result of Inheritance: Kept Timing of the Gift: Ready Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(34).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/30/2022 6:18:19 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHAT BROUGHT THAT ON? Luke 4:22-30 INTRODUCTION It seems to me a growing phenomena is taking place in public venues where people no longer give respect to a speaker but are often seen turning on them. At first, the assembly is sitting quietly, but something is said and the mood changes. This has been often observed if you watch a session of Parliament It is a wonder that anything gets accomplished with all the yelling taking place. Political speeches in our own country are often met with some chorus of boos but sometimes much worse. If there was ever an occasion of turning against a public messenger it is in Luke 4. INITIAL RECEPTION 4:22 His message was pleasing and comforting They kept repeating (present imperfect) what a wonderful words He spoke marveled - wondered, admiration, astonishment The Perfect Man gave a perfect message No man ever spoke like Jesus spoke Luke 2:46, 47 John 7:45 the officers of the temple Never did a man speak the way this man speaks His words were gracious sweetness, delight Like yours are suppose to be: Col. 4:6 Let your speech be aways with grace . . They were winning words fascinating, charming The poor have been waiting for such a message Freeing prisoners, the blind seeing, the oppressed released The year of jubilee arriving; the year of favor and grace That sounds good. And it is the boy from Nazareth that will bring this about Jesus spoke smoothly and impressively. He made a wonderful declaration, but could a local carpenter's son make such an offer? That was their real question." One writer: "despite being so impressed and perplexed by Jesus' remarks that they marvel, resolve the situation with the realization that this is a carpenter's son. In other words, someone with this common a background cannot be who he claims to be." The witness and wonder gave way to doubt and skepticism Their skepticism was obvious on Jesus second visit to Nazareth Matthew 13:54-57a His message was scandalous A rapid and radical transformation came over the listeners IRRITATING REACTION 4:23-27 Jesus seems to make a jump in His message Two things to remember v. 21 He began to say we basically have none of His message He had much more to say John 2:23-25 many believed when they say the signs But Jesus did not commit Himself to them, because He knew all men, And had no need that anyone should testify of man, for He knew what was in man. So Jesus knew what they were thinking We often get irritated when others think they know what we are thinking And perhaps even more irritated when they are right Jesus did know and called them on it Jesus understood they did not believe in His suggested position as the Anointed One The people proposed raising the level of anticipation through miracles What is the evidence of your claim? If you have such a calling from God, prove it! Put up or shut up! Proverb = parable, saying, allegories, metaphors Luke 23:35 Greek literature: A physician for others, but himself teeming with sores They had heard about Capernaum; John 4:46-54, healing nobleman s son they had heard about Cana; activities in Jerusalem Capernaum did become headquarters for Jesus A sizeable town; Matthew collected taxes there How did Jesus presence profit them? 10:15 Being a hometown boy was a detriment Did not point out any sin, but saw Him as common; a carpenter What gives Him the idea He is something special? Jesus then presents two illustrations Two prophets who did not score points at home ; Elijah, Elisha Very low points in Israel s history Elijah: 1 Kings 17:1 No introduction, just shows up on the scene Ahab, wicked, married to Jezebel who turned his heart to Baal worship; 16:31 Jesus takes the people back to a time when the nation of Israel was most obstinate The prediction of famine led to many deaths, including men leaving behind countless widows Elijah ministers to one; in Zarephath of Sidon; a Gentile, the homeland of Jezebel The nation of Israel was so bad that no miracles could be performed there so Elijah was sent out of the country Elijah found more faith outside Israel than within the country Elisha: 2 Kings 5 many lepers but the only one saved was a Gentile; another Syrian Naaman s faith was rewarded through his obedience to what seemed to him a silly request Emphasis: none of them were saved no, not even absolutely one Message is simple: God will do as He pleases with His grace and mercy Rom. 9:15 Nazareth is not going to force Jesus to do God's work when they have no faith in him. The people understood that they had no claim on Jesus and that He was going to do His greatest work outside of town. This is what caused the Irritation; the angst; the wrath of the people They were hearing things they did want to hear They had no intention of admitting they were poor, blind, lame, oppressed Far easier to run the preacher out of town than to submit oneself to such rhetoric And if I have not been guilty of being a source of irritation with messages, I have failed. Naaman s pride was almost his undoing and it was that of the people of Nazareth The problem of Jesus hearers is still the problem of many today: unbelief Things have not changed Sinners refuse to acknowledge they are sinners in need of a Savior They are spiritually bankrupt, spiritually blind, lamed, and crushed What is their response? ULTIMATE REJECTION 4:28, 29 Jesus is controlled by the Spirit; the people are controlled by rage all the people all means all in the synagogue where devotion to God is supreme Those possessing religion but lacking a relationship It was not Jesus position as the Anointed One what bothered them as much as the fact that God practices sovereignty; God would save Gentiles before His own people Compare: Acts 13:46-48; Acts 22:21, 22; Acts 26:23, 24 Religious people reject Jesus Religious people can seethe with anger God does not owe salvation to anyone Romans 9:15 Jesus was thrust out of ; thrown out of town ekballo Same word Scripture uses to cast out demons; Matt. 7:22; 8:16, 31; 9:34 What is missed from the scene: they were clamoring for a miracle Jesus gave them one though not recorded as such. He passed through their midst They were so caught up in their rage, they misplaced Jesus. Can you imagine them getting to the edge of town, about to throw Him down the clift, and No Jesus. CONCLUSION How is that a crowd of people can hear a message of hope and turn so quickly from delight to disgust? From hope to hate? From reception to rage? Unbelief, pride, arrogance Jesus was speaking to a group of the entitled, God s chosen ones Folks, fret not when those who seem to be religious refuse your message, your position of separating from the world, your standards for living for Christ WHAT BROUGHT THAT ON? Luke 4:22-30 INITIAL RECEPTION 4:22 marveled - wondered, admiration, astonishment The Perfect Man gave a perfect message Luke 2:46, 47 John 7:45 The witness and wonder gave way to doubt and skepticism Matthew 13:54-57a IRRITATING REACTION 4:23-27 Jesus knew what they were thinking Proverb = parable Elijah: 1 Kings 17:1 Elijah found more faith outside Israel than within the country Elisha: 2 Kings 5 Naaman s faith was rewarded through his obedience God will do as He pleases with His grace and mercy Romans 9:15 They had no intention of admitting they were poor, blind, lame, oppressed The problem of Jesus hearers is still the problem of many today: unbelief Sinners refuse to acknowledge they are sinners in need of a Savior ULTIMATE REJECTION 4:28, 29 Those possessing religion but lacking a relationship Acts 13:46-48; Acts 22:21, 22; Acts 26:23, 24 God does not owe salvation to anyone Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(35).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 8/6/2022 11:03:24 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE AUTHORITY OF JESUS DEALING WITH DEMONS AND OTHER DISEASES Luke 4:31-37 Cf. Mark 1:21-28 INTRODUCTION Capernaum: 20 miles north and west of Nazareth; the new home for Jesus, His mother and family Serious drop in elevation: 1,200 above to 680 below sea level Rejected by Nazareth Matt. 9:1 His own city Capernaum was a major trade and economic center in the North Galilean region. Nothing but ruins today; Luke 10:15 A perfect illustration for all who reject Jesus The second record of Sabbath day teaching More opposition Preach the Word and duck! I think we have 3 responsibilities in the teaching and preaching ministry Discipleship: grow in grace and knowledge 2 Peter 3:18 Equipping for Ministry Alerting to false teachers and doctrines Heresy flourishes where sound Christian teaching lags. That is why we do expositional preaching. We want to know about Jesus. John 12:21 Greeks to Philip: We would see Jesus teaching to instruct by word of mouth; generally in groups Teach in such a way that the will of the student becomes conformed to what is taught Doctrine determines our direction of behavior It is information that leads to transformation Present tense: Jesus was always teaching and so are you It was a busy day in Capernaum Chronology of the events of a Day in the Life of Jesus in Capernaum - based on the Synoptic Gospels: 1. Healing a leper (Only in Mt 8:1-4 ) 2. Healing centurion's son (Only in Mt 8:5-13 ) 3. Preaching the Word with Authority in Synagogue (Mk 1:21-22 , Lk 4:31-32 , not in Matthew) 4. Casting out unclean spirit/demon (Mk 1:23-27 , Lk 4:33-36 , not in Matthew) 5. Spread of the report/news about Jesus (Mk 1:28 , Lk 4:37 , not in Matthew) 6. Entering Peter's home and healing his Mother-in-Law (Mk 1:29-31 , Lk 4:38-39 , Mt 8:14-15 ) 7. Healings and casting out demons in evening (Mk 1:32-34 , Lk 4:40-42 , Mt 8:16-17 -adds quote from Isa 53:4 ) Don t forget the message preached in Nazareth Proclaim good news to the poor Heal the brokenhearted Deliverance to the captives Sight to the blind Deliverance to the crushed He is about to deliver on that message Up until now the gospel of Luke has been about the Person of Jesus; who He is Beginning with a message from an angel, then angels, shepherds, Simeon, Anna, John the Baptist, God the Father Now, Luke will settle on the Power of Jesus POWER OF THE MESSAGE 4:32 As in Nazareth, the people had never heard a speaker like Jesus And we haven t either astonished amazed 4:22 wondered = admired 15x in NT; often associated with teaching In Luke: 2:48; 9:43 Our English word astonished comes from the Latin to leave someone thunderstruck To stun, strike senseless; sudden great wonder Here in the imperfect tense repeated amazed Jesus would say something amazing and then He would immediately say something else amazing and so on The oohs and aahs; overwhelmed spoke with power authority Jesus had the right and might Mark 1:22 explains: and not as the scribes (cf. Matthew 7:29) Customary for rabbis to cite other authorities for their source They were expounders of traditions But here was a speaker who got His power direct from God Jesus stands apart from the old group These people were struck out of their senses; their mouth was open in amazement I will often quote from a commentary or message from Spurgeon, MacArthur, Luther, whoever Hopefully, it is always done for illustration and not authority The Bible is its own authority. It needs not the authentication from others Your presence says volumes about your commitment to the Scriptures Most preachers have no idea where their people stand because of all the programs and blowout Sundays they promote. I was admonished early on that if you want the church to grow, put it on a diet of the Word of God. Authority and power come from the Scriptures POWER OVER DEMONS 4:33- Standing among the crowd: a man which had a spirit of an unclean demon What was a demon possessed man doing in the synagogue? Was he a regular attender? Had the people got use to his presence? unclean physically, morally or both? Someone has said, this is not the last time a demon possessed person sat in a pew Demon possession is a tough topic to cover Interesting to note: only 4x demon possession mentioned outside the gospels 1 Samuel 16:14 and Saul; 1 Kings 22:22, Ahab s prophets Acts 16:16 Philippian servant girl; Acts 19:13, sons of Sceva Luke refers to demons 23x As Jesus began His public ministry the powers of hell were unleashed Here is spiritual opposition to the spiritual ministry of Jesus Here is supporting evidence to Jesus claim the acceptable year of the LORD How were the people to know Jesus had the power to deliver? Luke generally just uses demon ; here it is unclean demon As if there are clean ones Let s get this end before we finish for today 3 things: A believer can never be demon possessed 1 John 4:4 Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world. A believer need not fear demons James 2:19 (WEB) You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder. Note who is afraid: demons There is a big difference between possession and influence Demons are influencing believers every day; they are quite good at it That is their limit screamed with a mega phone Had possibly sat in this synagogue many times and never said a word throw a rock into a pack of wolves and the one who yelps is the one who got hit babies cry when you change their formula The presence, power and preaching of the Person of Jesus disturbed this demon's spiritual world I am not seeker sensitive. There is no reason for an unbeliever to sit in our services and be comfortable. Further, there is no reason for a believer out of fellowship with the Lord Jesus to be comfortable. We don t expect someone to yell out, but at least not find the service acceptable. THE AUTHORITY OF JESUS DEALING WITH DEMONS AND OTHER DISEASES Luke 4:31-37 Heresy flourishes where sound Christian teaching lags. Doctrine determines our direction of behavior POWER OF THE MESSAGE 4:32 astonished amazed to stun spoke with power authority Customary for rabbis to cite other authorities for their source The Bible is its own authority POWER OVER DEMONS 4:33- unclean physically, morally or both? only 4x demon possession mentioned outside the gospels 1 Samuel 16:14; 1 Kings 22:22; Acts 16:16; Acts 19:13 Here is spiritual opposition to the spiritual ministry of Jesus A believer can never be demon possessed 1 John 4:4 A believer need not fear demons James 2:19 There is a big difference between possession and influence screamed with a mega phone The presence, power and preaching of the Person of Jesus disturbed this demon's spiritual world Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(36).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 8/20/2022 4:37:47 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message SETTING THE CAPTIVE FREE Luke 4:34-41 Why did Jesus come? IDIOM 4:34-37 I want to begin by looking at the words from the demon During the time of Jesus speaking, the demon breaks the quiet Screams out: mega phone Great disruption revealing great anxiety Several translations: Ha! Leave us alone! Go away! (YLT) Away! What to us and to thee Jesus, O Nazarene! An idiom; also used 8:28; Mark 5:7; (parallel passages) a group of words in a fixed order that has a particular meaning that is different from the meanings of each word on its own: e.g. to bite off more than you can chew something which is too difficult for you to shoot yourself in the foot to do something which hurts yourself Ah away; an expression of anger, surprise, displeasure Only 4x in Scripture; Job 15:16; 19:5; 25:6; 9; (parallel Mark 1:24) Here it is a diabolical screech what I have to you What do we have in common? Do not meddle with me! In the OT; can imply hostility or merely disengagement when one person was unjustly bothering another Judges 11:12 2 Chronicles 35:21 1 Kings 17:18 When someone was asked to get involved in a matter he felt was no business of His 2 Kings 3:13 Hosea 14:8 Paul asked rhetorically: 2 Corinthians 6:14, 15 Outside of the fact the demon prior to his disobedience was created by Jesus Rev.12:4 1/3 of angels followed Satan in rebellion What does light have in common with darkness? Christ with Belial? Righteousness with lawlessness? A believer and an unbeliever? That is the question we must ask Are we guilty of moving in arenas where we should not be? A word about destroy utter ruin but not cease to exist Not the loss of being but the loss of well-being For the demon it will be utter ruin and eternal uselessness Revelation 20:1-3; 7-10 For us: Luke 9:24 whoever seeks to save his life shall lose (destroy) it; but whoever shall lose (destroy) his life for my sake, shall find it ILLNESS 4;38, 39 Upon leaving the Synagogue; enter the house From Synoptics: Simon and Andrew s house; Simon s wife and her mother James and John are along; Luke does not mention them yet Probable that Simon s wife accompanied him on his journeys: 1 Cor. 9:5 afflicted to hold together, compress; constrain, lie sick of, be in a strait Luke 8:37 they were taken ; with great fear; Luke 8:45, throng ; Luke 12:50 straightened; Luke 19:43 keep thee in ; Luke 22:63 held Mark: lay prostrate burning with fever; Matthew: stretched out with a fever Luke as doctor more technical term; suffering with a mega fever rebuked to censure, admonish, forbid, charge 4:35, 41 Not always demons 8:24 rebuked the wind and the raging water 9:55 rebuked them (James and John); 17:3 rebuke the brother who sins against you 18:15; 18:39; 19:39 23:40 one thief rebuked the other thief on the cross Only 2x outside the synoptics; 2 Timothy 4:2 Preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with all patience and teaching. Jude 9 (WEB) But Michael, the archangel, when contending with the devil and arguing about the body of Moses, dared not bring against him an abusive condemnation, but said, "May the Lord rebuke you!" Matthew and Mark state Jesus only touched her Luke s use of rebuke does not suggest the fever was demon induced But the disease is treated like a power that needs to be stopped Similar to Jesus rebuke of the storm (8:24) Jesus is the kind, sympathetic physician The scene is a demonstration of Jesus power over every force of nature The healing was miraculous and immediate; It was not an exorcism No need for a time of recuperation Immediately speed and totality TRANSITIONS: DOES JESUS HAVE THE PROPOSED AUTHORITY TO DELIVER THOSE WHO ARE CAPTIVE? THE PEOPLE WANTED SIGNS; LUKE PROVIDES A RECORD ILLUSTRATIONS 4:40, 41 The sun was setting; when evening come Why is that important? Sabbath day; limitation of travel; time s up The news had spread of synagogue happenings A steady stream of needs were brought to Jesus Picture is you will, Jesus standing at the door (Mark 1:33) and to receive the constant flow of people as they passed Matthew 4:24 The news about Him spread throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all who were ill, those suffering with various diseases and pains, demoniacs, epileptics, paralytics; and He healed them. Luke: divers diseases; various So a long day continues 1. Healing a leper (Only in Mt 8:1-4 ) 2. Healing centurion's son (Only in Mt 8:5-13 ) 3. Preaching the Word with Authority in Synagogue (Mk 1:21-22 , Lk 4:31-32 , not in Matthew) 4. Casting out unclean spirit/demon (Mk 1:23-27 , Lk 4:33-36 , not in Matthew) 5. Spread of the report/news about Jesus (Mk 1:28 , Lk 4:37 , not in Matthew) 6. Entering Peter's home and healing his Mother-in-Law (Mk 1:29-31 , Lk 4:38-39 , Mt 8:14-15 ) Healings and casting out demons in evening (Mk 1:32-34 , Lk 4:40-42 , Mt 8:16-17 -adds quote from Isa 53:4 ) all those who came no mention of faith, repentance More power on display. Power over demons who could not persuade the people not to come. Did not they know they were about to see Jesus? Here is instantaneous healing of everyone; not pre-chosen candidates those who had any bringing relatives; immediate family No one was too unclean for Jesus that is still true Life Application Bible: the touch of Jesus when He could have spoken a word over all Human touch is so important Jesus is still the great Physician; We still need His touch But any physical healing is only temporary; all these people died The healing of the soul is permanent; Psalm 103 bless the Lord o my soul 4:40 healed them - therapy; also 4:23, Jesus used on Himself To care for; to minister to; to take care of the sick SETTING THE CAPTIVE FREE Luke 4:34-41 IDIOM 4:34-37 Great disruption revealing great anxiety Ah away; an expression of anger, surprise, displeasure What do we have in common? 2 Corinthians 6:14, 15 destroy utter ruin but not cease to exist Revelation 20:1-3; 7-10 Luke 9:24 ILLNESS 4;38, 39 afflicted to hold together, compress; constrain suffering with a mega fever rebuked to censure, admonish, forbid, charge does not suggest the fever was demon induced The healing was miraculous and immediate ILLUSTRATIONS 4:40, 41 Matthew 4:24 no mention of faith, repentance No one was too unclean for Jesus Jesus is still the great Physician The healing of the soul is permanent; Psalm 103 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(37).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/3/2022 11:05:43 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE SEARCH Luke 4:42-44 THE SEARCH FOR SECLUSION 4:42a Mark 1:35 while it was still dark somewhere between 3 and 6 a.m. This is the day after Sabbath When day came Jesus left Knowing His time was short; redeeming every moment Seeking rest in the presence of the Father after a busy day of ministry Jesus humanity: showing us dependence on His Father Willingness to submit to His will Psalm 90:12 so teach us to number our days that we may apply our hearts to wisdom James 4:14 your life is like a vapor; it appears for a little while and then vanishes away Ephesians 5:15-16 (WEB) Therefore watch carefully how you walk, not as unwise, but as wise; redeeming the time, because the days are evil. How are we using our allotment of time? Are there changes we should make to redeem the hours of the day? Secluded place: desert: wilderness; abandoned 1:80 3:2, 4 4:1 5:16 wilderness and prayed Mark 6:31, 32 Jesus instructed the disciples to go to a similar place The word is also used in a metaphorical sense: Matthew 23:38 behold, your house is being left to you desolate Probably referring to the coming destruction of the temple but speaking to their spiritual condition as they reject their Messiah Amos 8:11 (WEB) Behold, the days come," says the Lord Yahweh, "That I will send a famine in the land, Not a famine of bread, Nor a thirst for water, But of hearing the words of Yahweh. That is spiritual desolation Another interesting usage of the word wilderness: Pr 21:19 it is said to be better to live in a desert (eremos) than with a contentious woman! Mark 1:35 and was praying there imperfect tense; praying through this time It is difficult to pray in earnest while life is busy around you Rest after work is important to the body Including prayer is important to the soul Luke 8:46 gives us some insight into Jesus power went out of Him It cost Him life to restore life to others Christ spent Himself in the service of others Remember: Jesus lived on earth as a man; dependent on His relationship with His Father John 9:4 "We must work the works of Him that sent Me while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work" And side by side with this, we have the picture of Jesus practicing His devotions There is an intimate connection between the two In the midst of all your activities, don t forget you need a power room. A place of quiet for restoration to return to the work at hand. As has been said by others, come apart before you come apart Matthew 6:6 it is ok to have a closet but the emphasis is not on having a closet but condemning the show of religious piety THE SEARCH FOR SIGNS 4:42b Search: imperfect tense As Jesus was continually praying; the people were continually searching Mark 1:36 Simon, Andrew, James and John were leading the crowd Search: eagerly hunting; pursuit in hot haste stayed (KJV) Hold down; hold on to Trying to hang on to Him so He would not leave the area And why not? They had just observed His authority over the supernatural and natural This became the common occurrence with people 5:1, 19; 6:19; 8:19; 12:1 One large crowd after another Their primary purpose: Jesus could heal; deliver; put on quite a show But even though their motives may have been selfish; it was still better than Nazareth While the people s search for Jesus was commendable it was for all the wrong reasons They totally missed the significance of His miracles The miracles signs were not to an end in themselves but pointing to something else If you want to hold fast to something, try these Luke 8:15 (WEB) That in the good ground, these are such as in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, hold it tightly, and bring forth fruit with patience. 1 Corinthians 15:2 (WEB) by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you--unless you believed in vain. There are times we fail to see beyond the immediate Government decisions; employer moves; family matters All come under the sovereignty of God: a sovereignty to be worshipped, not questioned We often try to hold on to what is transient; it was not ours to hold on to in the first place; it was intended all along to be temporary Like these folks of Capernaum, we often fail to see beyond what brings immediate satisfaction There is another verse about holding down: 2 Thessalonians 2:6-7 (WEB) Now you know what is restraining him, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season. For the mystery of lawlessness already works. Only there is one who restrains now, until he is taken out of the way. In the mean time: THE SEARCH FOR SUBJECTS 4:43, 44 Must: obligation, necessity Jesus says this is His divine commission: to preach There is a catchy saying that I have heard and when I was a younger believer bought into, but it is not Biblical. What is the saying? "Preach the Gospel with your life and if necessary use words." The bottom line for Jesus is it is always necessary to use words. Jesus preached with His miracles but His ultimate purpose was to reach sinners with the gospel His purpose was to provide a way for man to detour from the eternal consequences of sin That requires faith and belief in the gospel of the Kingdom for God has chosen you to salvation through the sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth 2 Thessalonians 2:13 Romans 10:17 faith comes by hearing and hearing by the Word God Those who believe become part of the Kingdom of God There is a kingdom, there is a throne, there is a king Two aspects: Luke 17:21 in your midst present, internal The King reigns in the heart of those who have accepted Jesus as the Anointed One The day will come when it is known who served the kingdom of self and who served the kingdom of the Savior There is a future Kingdom; the Messianic Age which we will talk about in detail later The context of Luke 4 is the kingdom presented by the good news The kingdom based on Jesus authority to defeat the forces of evil It is the arrival of forgiveness and the presence of the Spirit 4:44 so He kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea (Galilee) A simple summary of weeks or months of ministry The Proof of His Kingship: the miracles The Power of His Kingship: Prayer life The Priority of His Kingship: Preach the gospel 19:10 He came to seek and save the lost No better way than the preaching of the gospel THE SEARCH Luke 4:42-44 THE SEARCH FOR SECLUSION 4:42a Jesus humanity: showing us dependence on His Father Psalm 90:12 James 4:14 Ephesians 5:15-16 Mark 1:35 prayer is important to the soul Luke 8:46 Christ spent Himself in the service of others In the midst of all your activities, don t forget you need a power room. THE SEARCH FOR SIGNS 4:42b Mark 1:36 Trying to hang on to Him so He would not leave the area They totally missed the significance of His miracles Luke 8:15 1 Corinthians 15:2 We often fail to see beyond what brings immediate satisfaction THE SEARCH FOR SUBJECTS 4:43, 44 Must: obligation, necessity 2 Thessalonians 2:13 Romans 10:17 Those who believe become part of the Kingdom of God Luke 17:21 There is a future Kingdom The context of Luke 4 is the kingdom is presented by the good news The Proof of His Kingship: the miracles The Power of His Kingship: Prayer life The Priority of His Kingship: Preach the gospel Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(38).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/10/2022 2:59:54 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message LUKE 5 As we begin a new chapter, let s make sure we still see the big picture. Using Jenson s Survey of the NT Remember: it is John who records the first year of Jesus ministry which took place in the southern part of the country, generally called Judea John 1-4:42 Also note, that in that time Jesus had contact with Andrew and Simon John 1:40-42 They were not committed as disciples yet; official designation not until Luke 6:12 But it does give some understanding of Luke 4 and Jesus at Simon s house Now, I don t want to get to involved in details but I think these next thoughts are important to set the stage of where we are Matthew 4:18-22 and Mark 1:16-20 are considered parallel passages to Luke 5:1-11 There are some strong similarities but also some contrasts I think they are separate events so let me give you a possible timeline Jesus was baptized by John in the Jordan River and then faced Satan in the wilderness Jn 1:35-51 Upon His return, John points him out Andrew gets Simon and they follow Their following is spontaneous out of interest in what Jesus did and said Jn. 2:1-11 Jesus and disciples are at wedding in Cana Jn. 2:12 disciples with Jesus at Capernaum Jn. 2:1-22 disciples observed temple cleansing Jn. 3:22-26 disciples with Jesus in Judea Jn. 4:4-42 with Jesus in Samaria At this time, Jesus went to Nazareth and disciples possibly returned to their homes around Capernaum. Bethsaida bayth-sahee-dah' (home of Andrew, Simon, Philip) was near Jesus comes down to Capernaum and we have Matthew 4:18-22 and Mark 1;16-20 Then we have Luke 4:31 to where we are in chapter 5 Luke 5:1 NKJV After this So it was KJV And it came to pass ASV Now it came to pass NIV One day ESV On one occasion WEB Now it happened All simply to say, an indefinite time has taken place The fellows had returned to their day jobs That is all about to change What will we find in Luke 5 The Call of Simon (Andrew, James and John) The Cleansing of the Leper The Cure of the Paralytic The Call of Levi Jesus continues to provide evidence of who He is He performs a miracle and gives us evidence of His Sovererignty WEB Now it happened the word means to become, cause to be You may have said from time to time: it just so happened The timing was just perfect Why? Because it was a God thing Everything that happened in Jesus' life was by PROVIDENCE and with PURPOSE! We know that nothing just happens Our Father is in control -- superintending the seemingly random events in our life with His providence and for His purpose Luke 5 is PROVIDENCE AT WORK Luke 5 PROVIDENCE IN COMMUNICATION 5:1-3 PROVIDENCE IN CASTING NETS 5:4-9 PROVIDENCE IN CATCHING MEN 5:10, 11 5:10 turns the miracle of catching fish into a picture of mission Here is a merging of even and symbol The event signifies not only what disciples are called to do but who disciples are as they do it Simon is brought to the place where he acknowledges he is a sinner and not worthy to benefit from God s power and presence Jesus makes it clear that such a humble approach is exactly what God will use The call of Jesus is to catch men and not finned water dwellers It is possible that 7 of the 12 disicples were fishermen John 21:1-3 WWW makes the following comments about the qualities that fishermen have It takes courage and daring, patience and determination to work on the seas it also takes a great deal of faith Fishermen must be willing to work together and help one another. They must develop the skills necessary to get the job done quickly and efficiently 5:10 note the word catch means to take alive In fishing you catch a finned water dweller and it dies In evangelism, you catch that which is dead and it becomes alive Ephesians 2:1 and you He made alive, who were dead in trespasses and sins I am not a fisherman and can t say I have been good at catching men So our passage here, I trust will be of help to all of us. The story shows us how Jesus transforms everyday people (even sinful people, like Peter) into His servants, involved in His great cause of catching people for God. It teaches us that the greatest purpose we can have in life is to follow Jesus in catching men for Him. PROVIDENCE IN COMMUNICATION 5:1-3 The Mass the multitiude pressed about Him Rest upon; impose In other words they were all over Him We spoke last week of the crowd that came to the house for healing Josephus estimated the population of Galilee at 3 million Plenty of people to make a crowd Mark 3:9; 4:1 Does not mean the crowd was responsive, just curious They were pressing against Him, forcing Jesus into the water 4:22 they marveled at the gracious words 4:36 what a word this is 23:23 the people pressed upon Pilate to crucify Him The Message to hear the Word of God We wonder what they really thought they were hearing Doubtful, but they knew the words were gracious, authoritative, powerful They knew that no one has ever spoken like that Jesus was preaching the gospel of the Kingdom of God (4:43) He did spend His time quoting other rabbis; He spoke with authority He spoke Isaiah 61 (4:18, 19) John 7:16, 17; 8 31, 42; 12:49, 50; 14:31 word of God - meaning the word that comes from God; God is the source Jesus is speaking that which comes directly from God 2 Tiimothy 4:1-4 (Word of God in Luke) 8:11, 21; 11:28 Jesus opened His mouth and God spoke He spoke Good News: preach to the poor; healed the brokenhearted, liberty to captives, sight to the blind, liberty to the crushed Jesus is the source for truth: John 14:6 Gabriel told Mary your baby will be the Son of the Most High God He wll be called the Son of God; He will be one in essence Here is evidence of His Deity; Providence at work PROVIDENCE AT WORK Luke 5 Jesus had contact with Andrew and Simon John 1:40-42 Luke 5:1 an indefinite time has taken place Everything that happened in Jesus' life was by PROVIDENCE and with PURPOSE! 5:10 turns the miracle of catching fish into a picture of mission Jesus makes it clear that such a humble approach is exactly what God will use 5:10 note the word catch means to take alive Ephesians 2:1 PROVIDENCE IN COMMUNICATION 5:1-3 The Masses Josephus estimated the population of Galilee at 3 million They were forcing Jesus into the water The Message Jesus was preaching the gospel of the Kingdom of God (4:43) John 7:16, 17; 8 31, 42; 12:49, 50; 14:31 word of God - God is the source 2 Tiimothy 4:1-4 Jesus opened His mouth and God spoke Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(39).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/17/2022 3:13:41 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message Luke 5 is PROVIDENCE AT WORK Luke 5 PROVIDENCE IN COMMUNICATION 5:1-3 The Masses The Message We spent some time establishing the relationship Jesus was developing with Simon From the initial meeting after Jesus baptism, to possible identification as a disciple, to Jesus presence in his home and healing his mother-in-law In our present text as Jesus is teaching the mulititude and being presssed into the water, refuge is sought in Peter s boat while Peter himself is tending to his nets One point made last week is that everything about the life of Jesus was appointed by God s Providence and Purpose Jesus was clear is stating He only did and spoke what was ordained by His Father Thus, His message could rightly be described as the word of God 5:1 There are two more items for our outline PROVIDENCE IN CASTING NETS 5:4-9 PROVIDENCE IN CATCHING MEN 5:10, 11 Jesus is still in the Idenfication stage of His ministry Establishing that He is God in the flesh PROVIDENCE IN CASTING NETS 5:4-9 Teaching time is over Simon is in the boat Important to note: at this time Simon is more focused on his work than listening to Jesus It had been a bad night of fishing, so probably occupied with their thoughts Grumbling ?? One other thought about Jesus choosing uneducated fishermen as disciples God selects people who know how to work; not the lazy Cleaning Nets; a good net will pick up debris (Mattoon) The fumes of decay: dead fish, seaweed, mud Will rot the net and stink the boat if not cleaned Rats would eat the debris and break the net The flimsiness of the chord Net will rot and become unrealiable and useless The frustration of the fishermen The net is intended to slide easily through the water The net needs to be nearly invisible A dirty net makes the hard work harder The fish will be frightened Fish can see a dirty net Our lives are like those nets They must be clean to be effective Sinful habits cause a stink; our faith becomes weak and will break We create more frustation and stress for ourselves by the complications created by sin Others will be repulsed by what they see Choosing the Right Boat Why does Luke note that there were two boats? One was Simon s and Andrew s The other Zebedee s; with James and John Not an accident that Jesus was in Simon s The Boat: these are larger vessels which could hold a crew of a dozen men and able to carry a large number of fish Most translations use the word boat but some use ship Could be easily 20 30 feet in length Jesus is honing in on Simon who was to become the leader of the disciples Simon was now going to have a front row seat for the teaching Jesus is not just moving into Simon s boat but into his life Though Jesus would return to the synagogue, more and more of His teaching was outside the norm; the lakeside, the open air The initial request was to put out from the shore The first command is harder for Simon Teaching time is over; now time for a life-changing illustration Jesus was asking for overtime at the wrong time and wrong place Not the time to fish in the deep Here is a carpenter telling a fisherman how to fish The second command was to let down the nets Could not have obeyed the second if not the first; would have missed the miracle I wonder what we have missed because we were in a state of spiritual disobedience Or perhaps Jesus wants you to set sail; leave the place of security and trust Him Simon did give a little protest He could have made many excuses You stick to religion; I will do the fishing Here is great reward for ready compliance The path of duty may sometimes be hard and disagreeable We are meant to learn the blessing of immediate unhesitating obedience to every plain command of Christ. PROVIDENCE IN CATCHING MAN 5:9, 10 The result of obedience was a huge haul Here a pattern God often uses in dealing with men to do what seems foolish with surprising results How did Jesus know where the fish were? Or did Jesus supernaturally bring the fish to Simon? The miracle did not take place withouth human agency They did the work; Jesus did the miracle Simon s reaction: another command depart Simon had witnessed other demonstrations of Jesus power Jesus had shown His power over that part of nature that Simon knew best He fell down, utterly astonished at what had happened The new revelation of Christ s power and glory gave Simon a great sense of his own sinfulness Overwhelmed with a sense of unworthiness Calls him Lord earlier has used the title master Simon is now recognizing Jesus as deity, more than a man, and therefore one with more than human authority 5:9 literally: astonishment seized him and all the ones with him Simon knew if Jesus could see the depths of the sea He could see the depths of Simon s heart It was when we get close to God that we truly see the state of our own soul Simon s eyes are open; knows he has no place in Jesus presence Neither do we, but Jesus did not leave Simon and neither will he leave us Simon s response of fear and penitence is common in Scripture Job 42:5,6 Judges 13:22 Exodus 20:18, 19 Isaiah 6:5 Revelation 1:7 5:10 An often repeated refraim: Fear not Stop being afraid A most proper reaction when confronted with deity Simon wanted separation; Jesus wanted reconciliation There will be a change in vocation; from catching what is alive to catching what is dead Think of the first time Simon Peter through out the gospel net; Acts 2; a great catch 2 Timothy 2:26 taking back those captured by Satan 5:11 they left their livelihood, possessions, and family They did not leave their families destitute John 19:25-27; 1 Corinthians 9:5; 1 Timothy 5:8 But they left everything; leaving all Left: send off, forsake; (forgive); total detachment from their earthly source of security 5:28 the call of Levi; Luke 14:33 Illustration: When the Spanish explorer Hernando Cortez landed at Vera Cruz, Mexico in 1519 he was intent on conquest. To assure the devotion of his men, Cortez set fire to his fleet of eleven ships! With no means of retreat Cortez s army had only one direction to move, into the Mexican interior. Cortez understood the price of commitment and he paid it. Philippians 3:7-11 Luke 18:28-30 CONCLUSION Here is the formal and permanent call to disicpleship Having just made the biggest catch of the careers Jesus calls us to be fishers of men, not catchers (Matthew 4:19 ). My job is to go where the fish are, walk among them, study their habitat, and learn their ways. And then to toss out a line and see if one rises to the surface. How clean is your net? Will fish be attracted by or repulsed by? Have you create excuses for not fishing for men? Training; job for professionals What are you hanging on to that prevents you from leaving the safety of the shore? PROVIDENCE AT WORK Luke 5 PROVIDENCE IN COMMUNICATION 5:1-3 The Masses The Message PROVIDENCE IN CASTING NETS 5:4-9 God selects people who know how to work Cleaning Nets A good net will pick up debris A dirty net makes the hard work harder Our lives our like those nets Choosing the Right Boat Jesus is not just moving into Simon s boat but into his life Jesus was asking for overtime at the wrong time and wrong place The path of duty may sometimes be hard and disagreeable PROVIDENCE IN CATCHING MAN 5:9, 10 The miracle did not take place withouth human agency Overwhelmed with a sense of unworthiness Simon knew if Jesus could see the depths of the sea He could see the depths of Simon s heart Simon wanted separation; Jesus wanted reconciliation Left: send off, forsake Philippians 3:7-11 Luke 18:28-30 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(40).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/24/2022 10:53:15 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FOLLOW ME Luke 5:11 INTRODUCTION One of the first games children learn to play is Follow the Leader Whether your recall of the game brings fun memories or horror thoughts, it was a game Since then we have done a lot of following: We followed in a straight line to the lunch room We followed home and classroom instructions We followed the rules for some sporting event We followed the rules of the road We followed the commands of a military instructor We followed the dictates of a boss We have followed someone or something all of our lives ILLUSTRATION S. I. McMillen, in his book None of These Diseases, tells a story of a young woman who wanted to go to college, but her heart sank when she read the question on the application blank that asked, "Are you a leader?" Being both honest and conscientious, she wrote, "No," and returned the application, expecting the worst. To her surprise, she received this letter from the college: "Dear Applicant: A study of the application forms reveals that this year our college will have 1,452 new leaders. We are accepting you because we feel it is imperative that they have at least one follower." Follow: to walk on the same road Simply put: are you willing to walk on the same road as Jesus? The metaphorical extension of this idea connotes imitating the thoughts, beliefs, actions, or lifestyle of another. Similarly, we might say of a boy adopting his father s occupation, He s following in the footsteps of his father. The Greek word came to mean to imitate or to follow someone s example The word is used 88 times in the NT; only 12 times outside of the gospels When Jesus issued the call to follow Me it was always in the present imperative Imperative: command; present: was to be a life-long walk Not only are we to start to follow Jesus but to continue following as a habit of life The big difference between the mulititude of people following Jesus and that of the disciples was the disciples left everything, the people left nothing Practicing the Presence of Christ As used by Jesus: A Command Luke 5:27 Matthew; John 1:43 Philip; John 21:19, 22 Peter, final command Proper Response: Luke 5:11 aorist indicative; immediately, right now Follow: involves a total commitment and in an exclusive relation to one who is recognized as not just a teacher but the Messiah The disciple expresses that basic commitment by Daily choices of obedience to the Lord. 5 Reasons folks followed Jesus: 1. the disciples followed Jesus because of the sheer compelling nature of His call 2. The crowds followed Jesus because they desired the things which He alone could give them. Mark 2:15 3. The sinners followed Jesus because they felt that He alone could enable them to mend their broken lives and to begin again. 4. The blind men followed Jesus that they might receive their sight. They Desired to experience his wonder-working power. Matthew 9:27 5. The blind men whose eyes were opened followed Jesus in sheer gratitude for what He had done for them. Matthew 20:34; Luke 18:43; Mark 10:52 6 Spiritual Applications from the common usage of the day: 1. The Christian is in the Position of the soldier who follows Jesus Christ, and who must immediately obey his eader s command. 2. The Christian is in the Position of the slave, who must obey as soon as his master speaks. 3. The Christian must Ask for the advice and for the ruling of Jesus Christ and must have the humility to follow it, whatever it may be. 4. The Christian is the man who desires citizenship of the Kingdom of Heaven, and, if he is to receive it, he must Agree to live according to the Laws of the Kingdom 5. The Christian is the learner and the listener who must Listen to the words of Jesus, and who must follow their thread, so that day by day he may learn more of the wisdom which Jesus is ever wishing to teach him. 6. The Christian is always in the position of one who needs and desires the favour and the grace and the help which Jesus Christ can give to him, and who follows Christ because In Christ alone he finds his need supplied. What does following Jesus involve? (William Barclay) Involves a Cost Luke 9:59, 61 Jesus seemed to discourage followers until they were quite sure of what they were doing. Jesus wanted no followers with false pretences. There was no easy believeism in Jesus call. LXX: Ruth 1:14 clung Involves Sacrifice We repeatedly are reminded what people left to follow Jesus Luke 5:11; 14:33 forsake all that he has Following Jesus is a full-time job The difference for us is that we follow Jesus in our work, not leaving it We minister where we are 1 Kings 19:20 Elisha burned the old life; no intention of returning Involves a Cross Luke 9:23 (Matt. 16:24; Mark 8:34) Following Jesus is an act of surrender It may well mean the sacrifice of pleasures, habits, aims, ambitions It is not about my schedule because His schedule is not my schedule What does following Jesus give? Gives the priviledge to walk in the light, not darknes John 8:12 A walk in darkness is a walk of uncertainty and sin A walk in the light is a walk of security and safety Gives the certainty of glory John 12:26 Sacrifice and the cross are the price of eternal glory The road to glory is rough and narrow and will be forgotten There is a stairway to heaven and a highway to hell which says something about the expected traffic. What is an inadequate way of following Jesus? Not to be condemned; better than nothing; but less than desirable To follow afar off Luke 22:54 Maybe following closer would have prevented the denials It was the eventual look of Jesus that crushed Peter To follow afraid Mark 10:32 They did not understand; they feared the worst; but they followed What about refusing to follow Jesus? Luke 18:22, 23 he went away sorrowful Refusal is always sorrowful Jesus never called for half-hearted followers It was to leave everything, come out from among them, separate yourselves Be willing to sever all ties to follow A close word to follow is obey When Jesus called His disciples it was more than an invitation; it was a command Wuest in his word studies makes this point: the command was not merely, Follow Me. It was Follow with Me. The pronoun is in the associative-instrumental case. . . . Our Lord did not therefore merely command Levi to become His follower. He welcomed him to a participation in His companionship. And this with Me companionship, was not one of an Indian-file nature, one following after another. It was a side by side walk down the same road. And this blessed fellowship is for every believer in the Lord Jesus. What is The Culmination of Following: Revelation 19:14 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(41).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/1/2022 9:03:54 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE WILLING TOUCH OF JESUS LUKE 5:12-15 How did you do this week traveling the same road as Jesus? Does the concept of trespassing have a little more meaning? INTRODUCTION Bruce: men more easily believe in miraculous power than miraculous love The leper was convinced of Jesus ability; what he did not know was His willingness Mark 1:41 includes moved with compassion Splagchnon: deep compassion; to take pity in a certain city probably on the edge as lepers were denied entrance But it is possible he ignored prodigal and came into town to see Jesus Dr. Luke includes the word full ; the body was totally covered What a contrast: Jesus, who is full of purity; a leper, who is full of disease PHYSICAL DEFORMITY The disease acts as an anesthetic, bringing numbness to the extremities as well as to the ears, eyes, and nose. The body s warning system of pain is destroyed A variety of skin disorders: symptoms ranged from white patches on the skin to running sores to the loss of digits on the fingers and toes. What is important to note is that leprosy, or Hansen's disease as it is better known today (after the man who diagnosed its cause), is not a rotting infection as is commonly thought, nor are its horrible outward physical deformities imposed by the disease The devastation that follows comes from such incidents as reaching one's hand into a charcoal fire to retrieve a dropped potato, or washing one's face with scalding water, or gripping a tool so tightly that the hands become traumatized and eventually stumplike. Here is a man who had not been able to feel for years and his body is mutilated from head to foot. Rotten, stinking, repulsive covered full, plethora Leviticus 13:45, 46 identified by raw flesh, torn clothes and verbal announcement Lived in isolation As unclean: Considered unfit to worship God Jesus does not chastise him for approaching Nor failing to cry out unclean Jesus saw the man s heart, not his scaly flesh Jesus looks at us the same way Custom of the Jews: walk at least 6 feet away If the wind is blowing from the leper toward you, it was 150 The only thing more defiling was to touch a dead body A leprous person was considered dead while yet living What a picture of sin! Our sin before a holy God is like a case of "advanced leprosy" and needs a miraculous cure and cleansing by the precious blood the Lamb! Praise the Name of Jesus that the price for our cleansing has been paid PERSONAL PLEA fell on his face bowed down Sister word to 5:8 and Peter The heart s position before Jesus LXX: Psalm 51:16, 17 contrite low, not rising far from the ground James 4:6 God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble The leper was in perfect position for healing A question of willingness: this man had lived in isolation; all others shrank from him The Lord of glory not only loved this man and us but He was and is able to do something about his and our miserable condition for God so loved the world He is able to deliver thee Here is the perfect marriage of ability and will I would love to impart to each of you a million dollars; but there is no ability The touch of Jesus illustrates the deep mystery of Jesus identifying with our sin 2 Corinthians 5:21 Dr. A. B. MacDonald, who was in charge of a leper colony in Itu: The leper is sick in mind as well as body. For some reason there is an attitude to leprosy different from the attitude to any other disfiguring disease. It is associated with shame and horror, and carries, in some mysterious way, a sense of guilt shunned and despised, frequently do lepers consider taking their own lives and some do. Behold a man with leprosy shows up in the crowd Violated all established policy Behold is a word of wonder; intended to excite admiration; a divine highlighter implored to ask for something with the sense of pleading; a sense of urgency All hope of returning to a normal life was wrapped up in Jesus can ability, power, dunamis clean catharsis; cleanse from filth, impurities This is the first mention of a Jew being healed of leprosy He was a Jew because of order to go to the priest After the giving of the Law there is no record of anyone healed of the disease Miriam was before the Law was given Naaman was Syrian, not Jewish PHYSICAL TOUCH Stretch and Touch touched wonder how long it had been since the man had physical touch The ceremonially unclean was not clean 2 Cor. 5:21 We are never too far or too defiled for His healing touch Jesus is willing to save 1 Tim. 2:4; 2 Peter 3:9 He is able to save Hebrews 7:25 He can do it now 2 Cor. 6:2 touch not just a little pinky touch; but it means to attach oneself to; to cling to Firmly touched him; possibly an embrace He touched the filthy leper, cleansed him and gave him purity Leprosy was seen culturally as the result of sin; a physical disease with spiritual roots I am willing present tense; always ready and willing be cleansed command did the leper have that power within him? Passive: a power was to work outside of him; it was a work done on his behalf Redemption, like creation, has its word of might Jesus spoke and it is done He loves, He looks, He touches. We live The leprosy left the leper and the leper left a changed man In the country of India there are some 300 million who are considered untouchables Placed in the lowest strata of society, they have no hope of escape They are told where they can live, work, and worship They are the ones most receptive to the message of Christianity. They felt the love and care of missionaries We truly need to see ourselves from His eyes Go and Show Imagine the shock of the priests when some fellow shows up cleansed of leprosy They would have to get the book out to see what they were to do next The next time Jesus would send 10 healed lepers The entire procedure of restoration to public life lasted 8 days Lev. 14:10 MacArthur: "If he obeyed and went to recount to the priests how Jesus had healed him, it would be a powerful testimony to them that Jesus was indeed the Messiah and Son of God. This testimony would be either convincing to the priests so that they would acknowledge the claims of Christ, or if they rejected Him self-indicting, since they had personally examined the miraculously healed leper." Here is a testimony to the religious community Keeping it a secret was probably 4-fold To avoid being considered just a miracle worker To avoid undue publicity which would hinder His movement To avoid any mistaken notion as to what type of Messiah He came to be To avoid premature death His popularity might bring The Law was unable to cleanse; it could only determine a cleansing had taken place Romans 3:20 Proper use of the Law would point to Christ Galatians 3:23, 24 The crowds grew; but look at the text They came to hear and be healed What a great church ministry: hear the Word to be healed Cannot Jesus still heal broken hearts THE WILLING TOUCH OF JESUS LUKE 5:12-15 PHYSICAL DEFORMITY The body s warning system of pain is destroyed Leviticus 13:45, 46 Considered unfit to worship God Jesus saw the man s heart, not his scaly flesh A leprous person was considered dead while yet living PERSONAL PLEA Psalm 51:16, 17 contrite James 4:6 The leper was in perfect position for healing 2 Corinthians 5:21 Behold is a word of wonder implored sense of urgency can power clean catharsis This is the first mention of a Jew being healed of leprosy PHYSICAL TOUCH Stretch and Touch We are never too far or too defiled for His healing touch Jesus is willing to save 1 Tim. 2:4; 2 Peter 3:9 He is able to save Hebrews 7:25 He can do it now 2 Cor. 6:2 touch to attach oneself to Go and Show The Law was unable to cleanse; it could only determine a cleansing had taken place Romans 3:20 Proper use of the Law would point to Christ Galatians 3:23, 24 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(42).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/8/2022 6:54:22 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message JUST THE FACTS Luke 5:17-26 INTRODUCTION Something is going on here but not sure what it might be FACT DECIPHERED The Pharisees were the Fact Checkers of the day Which also tells you how much they could be trusted; things have not changed Pharisee means separated one; they separated themselves from all ordinary life in order to keep every detail of the law of the scribes The scribes worked out the details of the law; the Pharisees swore to uphold them What details? They recognized the first five books of Scripture as the perfect and complete Word of God You were not to add or subtract one word of it; in not explicitly then implicitly So they set out to extract from the great principles of the law an infinite number of rules and regulations to govern every conceivable situation in life. In other words they changed the law of the great principles into the legalism of by-laws and regulations. Some examples: Sabbath: was to be holy; no work must be done by man or animal Problem: what is work? It must be defined The Mishnah is the codified scribal law. It has 24 chapters defining work To tie a knot on the Sabbath was work. But what was a knot? Suppose a man wished to let down a bucket into a well to draw water on the Sabbath day. He could not tie a rope to it, for a knot on a rope was illegal on the Sabbath; but he could tie it to a woman s girdle and let it down, for a knot in a girdle was quite legal. To heal on the Sabbath was to work. It was laid down that only if life was in actual danger could healing be done; and then steps could be taken only to keep the sufferer from getting worse, not to improve his condition. A plain bandage could be put on a wound, but no Neosporin on the bandaid; plain wadding could be put into a sore ear, but not medicated There were about 6,000 Pharisees at this time; small in number; great in influence They were the dominant force in Judaism A bunch of them from all over are gathered to check out Jesus Accompanied by the doctors of the law ; the scribes, special interpreters of the Law Not all Pharisees were scribes but most scribes were Pharisees A major battle is brewing A challenge is issued by Jesus An attack is made by the opponents A victory is won A triumph is celebrated it came to pass it happened 4:36; 5:12 Mark 2:1 back in Capernaum; His new residence or Peter s house? He was teaching emphasis; Jesus was teaching the teachers They were hearing the gracious words; they were hearing the Word of God from God Jesus had disrupted their business affairs in Jerusalem; upsetting the tables The Pharisees had watched as the popularity of Jesus grew; jealousy surfaced Power for healing was on display; Jesus yielded full to the Holy Spirit They have a healed leper in which to contend Now looking for some way to indict, discredit From their own teachings only the Messiah could heal a leper The Pharisees are fulfilling two responsibilities Observation This was the first step in identifying the Messiah Observe: what is being said, what is being done, what is being taught They are not to ask questions or raise objections They were then to return to Jerusalem with their findings and a determination made as to the significance of what they saw If seen to be important, then step 2 Interrogation Talk to the individual; then then the followers Attempt to decide whether the claims should be accepted or rejected All the leaders are gathered here for step 1 FACT DISPLAYED 5:18-24 Another person needing healed from a condition seen as caused by some sin problem Common cultural position and the fact that Jesus pronounced such Carried by 4 (Mark 2) friends bring friends to Jesus Here is an individual with a permanent condition of paralysis As is common in that day, the house has a flat roof with stairs going up the side The 4 worked their way through the composition of the roof to gain an opening You could imagine the dust and debris falling into the house and on the Pharisees gathered 5:20 your sins are forgiven you Jesus identifies the faith of their the four carriers and the man on the cot They believed Jesus could heal if their friend could only get there Vicarious faith performed as a benefit or advantage of another Vicarious sacrifice Here it is faith: your faith cannot get another to heaven The faith of the four performed for their friend Today, we speak of intercessory prayer; God hears prayers offered up for others Their faith was persistent A weak faith would have stopped at the crowd A wavering faith would have stopped at the roof Once their friend was on the stretcher there was no stopping them Their faith was creative There was probably someone in the crowd who said, why didn t I think of that!
