Faith will find a way Their faith was sacrificial Someone was going to have to repair the roof Time, labor, expense A faith that brings Christ s power to the world must always be willing to pay the price A faith seen in the palsied man as his sins were forgiven John 9 reminds us that not every disease is a consequence of sin Jesus displayed concern for the whole man Your sins are forgiven: once and for all time God s forgiveness is complete Psalm 103:12 Isaiah 1:18 Isaiah 55:6, 7 Micah 7:18, 19 Jeremiah 31:34 Acts 3:19, 20 forgiven send away; perfect tense: an abiding state of forgiveness; permanent Once forgiven, forever forgiven. Eternal security; Once saved, always saved. Passive voice: the power to forgive the sins of the paralytic came from without, ultimately from God Forgiveness is man s greatest need and God s most important gift That is our standard: Ephesians 4:32 FACT ILLUSTRATED 5:25, 26 The fact is Jesus is the Messiah The fact is Jesus is God The Fact Checkers missed out on both accounts They had the right thought: v. 21 Who can forgive sins? They either failed to make the connection; or they refused to make the connection Jesus healed a leper Jesus read their minds A paralytic picked up his cot and walked; immediate healing CONCLUSION Something is going on here Jesus just demonstrated in no uncertain terms who He is to the most influential, the most spiritual group in Israel I readily acknowledge we often miss out on what God is doing He owes us no explanation The Pharisees had their own agenda; self-preservation They had to discredit the competition Nothing good come out of Nazareth Jesus was conceived out of wedlock Jesus operated under the power of Beelzebub JUST THE FACTS Luke 5:17-26 FACT DECIPHERED The Pharisees were the Fact Checkers of the day They changed the law of the great principles into the legalism of by-laws and regulations They were the dominant force in Judaism Observation Interrogation FACT DISPLAYED 5:18-24 Vicarious faith performed as a benefit or advantage of another Their faith was persistent Their faith was creative Their faith was sacrificial Jesus displayed concern for the whole man forgiven send away Forgiveness is man s greatest need and God s most important gift FACT DEMONSTRATED 5:25, 26 The fact is Jesus is the Messiah The fact is Jesus is God Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(43).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/15/2022 7:47:30 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE SORRY FOR THE LATENESS. THERE IS NO WAY I WILL GET THROUGH ALL OF THIS SO IF YOU LEAVE OFF THE LAST TWO POINTS, WE ARE GOOD. JESUS, WHAT AT FRIEND FOR SINNERS? IF STILL LOOKING CALLING THE HORRIBLE DEPLORABLES Luke 5:27-32 INTRODUCTION I am assuming everyone here remembers that prior to the last election Hillary Clinton called Donald Trump supporters a basket of deplorables And we have often heard the term horrible deplorables ever since Personally, I prefer the title Adorable Deplorable but that is just me A basket of deplorables is an accurate title to describe the disciples of Jesus at least as identified by the people of the day It would be hard to find a more dysfunctional group; a greater group of misfits Uneducated, despised, low-lifes THE SETTING After the episode of healing the paralytic After providing convincing proof of His Deity Mark 2 locates Jesus by the sea shore; still at Capernaum People kept coming; Jesus kept teaching Note who Luke has presented to Jesus Demon possessed guy in the temple The masses of sick and possessed at the house The paralytic at the house Now: a tax collector Levi, son of Alphaeus; a collector of toll for Herod; hated by Jews A profession making a profit by adding to the total for personal gain Tax collecting was not a crime; the crime came from extortion and defrauding Luke 3:13 John the Baptist message to tax collectors Also known to employ thugs for intimidation purposes Zacchaeus was a chief tax collector ; one who someone like Levi would work for Jesus saw not a momentary glance, but to look closely at, a contemplative gaze Probably a gaze that got the attention of Levi To gaze at as in a theater; where we get the English word It was a gaze that penetrated Levi It was a gaze into the heart; 1 Samuel 16:7 Matthew is the name used after becoming a disciple Publican a man who did public duty Tax collectors had other names extortioners Hucksters Shepherds of fornication Flatterers All unflattering; all indicating the low opinion of the populace Ranked right along side prostitutes Working in his toll house; collectors booth Probably located along a trade route; taxing produce and other goods THE CALLING Command follow Me a clear call to become a disciple Present active imperative: a continuous command to follow The challenge was sudden and sharp Follow: as before; to walk the same road The call of Jesus was upon the whole life; not just when it was convenient An old Pony Express ad read: DUTY IS HAZARDOUS. ORPHAN PREFERRED Jesus wanted people to understand what they were getting into It is hard to find followers when everyone wants to lead In 2002 the Oakland Athletics built a winning baseball team in an unorthodox way. They had lost three top players after 2001, and the team didn t have money to sign any stars. So Oakland s general manager, Billy Beane, used some often-neglected statistics to assemble a group of lesser-known players either past their prime or seen by other teams as not skilled enough. That ragtag team ran off a 20-game winning streak on the way to winning their division and 103 games. Jesus picked some less desirables 1 Corinthians 1:27 Sometimes we seek out the familiar, the influential, and the rich. And we tend to ignore people with less status or those with physical limitations. Joe Stowell, former president of Moody Bible Institute, wrote in Following Christ: Many of us live out our faith as though [Christ] exists to follow us. We come to believe that Christ exists to satisfy our demands. . . . This disguised form of self-serving religion sets Christ up as just one more commodity in life that will enhance and empower our dreams. Spurgeon looks at the calling of Levi as parallel to healing the paralytic One was physical paralysis; the other moral paralysis Response and he left behind everything Levi made the choice of a lifetime, to leave a profitable temporal occupation to enter into the eternally profitable service of the Lord Jesus Christ Peter had a little more to go on when he left it all; the record catch of fish We are not told Levi had much, if anything, other than what he probably had heard And Levi had a cozy little occupation compared to the toil of fishing It is always interesting to hear how missionaries or preachers experience a call to their ministries. Some have experienced great financial success; some leave behind some pretty nice stuff And there are plenty of stories of those who could not give up what they have to live on less Luke 14:33; Mark 8:34-37 Luke 18:18-30 note vs. 29, 30 Compare Luke 9:57-62 two guys who wanted it, but could not pull the trigger Levi followed v. 28 Following is the essence of Christianity A close friend asked Gandhi, If you admire Christ so much, why don t you become a Christian? It is said that he replied, When I meet a Christian who is a follower of Christ, I may consider it. Interesting word order: left all, rose up Already yielded in his mind to the call; then physically made the move We get weighed down with the things we think we need when Jesus wants to set us free Matthew was now marching to the drum beat of a different drummer. He no longer listens to the drum beat of Rome or to the crescendo of coins in a bag. No, Matthew abandoned his lucrative job, his power, his authority, his prestige among other tax collectors and Roman officials, to march to the drum beat of Jesus Christ. Marching to the drum beat of a different drummer involves the identification of the proper value of material possessions compared to spiritual matters. Matthew saw the greater value in following Christ than making more money. Can a person make lots of money today and still follow Christ? The answer is "Yes," but it is very difficult in juggling the time and the pull of possessions. If a person sees finances as a tool to serve Christ and help others, then he will be able to keep his focus on the Lord and his funds in proper perspective. If he sees money as a tool for grasping power or living in wickedness, his funds will eventually ruin him and his family. Most people in America today do not realize just how rich they are and what God has given to them, even though they don't have a mansion or have millions of dollars in the bank. This happens when we fail to count our blessings. Paul Brand, in his book Fearfully and Wonderfully Made, recorded this letter to the Lord, which reminds us of the truth of how much we truly have. He said, "Dear Lord, I have been re-reading the record of the Rich Young Ruler and his obviously wrong choice, but it has set me thinking. No matter how much wealth he had, he could not ride in a car, have any surgery, turn on a light, buy penicillin, hear a pipe organ, watch TV, wash dishes in running water, type a letter, mow a lawn, fly in an airplane, sleep on an inner-spring mattress, or talk on the phone. If he was rich, then what am I?" The second principle involved in marching to the drum beat of a different drummer involves the immediate response or taking advantages of present opportunities. It involves not making excuses for inactivity, but seeing the possibilities in your situation. It like the story in the March, 1988, issue of the Rotarian. It tells about a bounty of $5,000 offered for each wolf captured alive. This bounty turned two country boys, Sam and Jed, into fortune hunters. Day and night they scoured the mountains and forests looking for their valuable prey. Exhausted one night, they fell asleep dreaming of their potential fortune. Suddenly, Sam awoke to see that they were surrounded by about fifty wolves with flaming eyes and bared ivory teeth, dripping with saliva. He nudged his buddy and said, "Jed, wake up! Wake up!......We're rich! We're rich!" Sam saw an opportunity! Matthew saw the opportunity presented to him. He immediately followed the Lord. The opportunity presented itself and he took advantage of it. People who stand out, people who tend to accomplish great things, are people who work hard and discern the importance of today's opportunities. They know that present opportunities can be gone tomorrow. There are folks who claim that you can serve the Lord anytime. If you decide to not live for Him today or take advantage of present opportunities, you can always serve the Lord some other time when you are older. Hold the phone! This is not true. It may sound good, but it is not true. Your opportunities to follow Christ may be gone tomorrow. Why? 1. Disability: You may become disabled or sick where you are hindered from or limited in your service to Christ. 2. Desire: You may become spiritually cold and hard and have no desire to live for Christ. 3. Death: You could die. 4. Difficulty: Others that need to be reached for Christ may become harder toward the gospel, they may move out of reach, or they may die without the Lord. 5. Deceptive Conclusions The drum beat of Jesus Christ beats the cadence of TODAY and living for the Lord NOW! Today! Today! Today! Now! Now! Now! (Mattoon - Treasures from the Scriptures ) THE GATHERING 5:29 Another indication of what Levi is leaving behind Levi is now sharing the blessedness of what he found John Wesley once said, No man ever went to Heaven alone; he must either find friends or make them. Levi responded to his call by calling others Used his resources to minister to others Matthew s version of an evangelistic outreach Jesus attended a variety of meals Luke 7:36 ; 9:12 ; 10:38 ; 11:37 ; 14:1 ; 19:7 ; 22:14 ; 24:30 ; 24:41 Here a tax collector introduces other tax collectors to Jesus Worked within his sphere of influence Is your home a place of influence? Jon Courson makes an interesting observation; Three places Jesus frequented: Open places: a place for preaching Quiet places: where He prayed to the Father Festive places: where He could celebrate with people Matthew 9:10 describes the others as sinners The very ones Jesus came to seek: 19:10 sat down reclining at the table Suggests a lengthy celebration; time for extended conversation The GRUMBLING 5:30 An after-party encounter; Pharisees would not have attended The Separatists Would not even let their robes touch the likes of Levi Jesus was interested bringing change in the lives of sinners Jesus did not pray the Father to take us out of the world John 17:15 But to keep us from the power of the evil one We are to be in the world but not of the world A boat is made for water but when water gets in the boat, there is a problem We are insulated but not isolated We are to be lights. Lights shine best in darkness The disciples were learning about opposition John 15:18-21 Matthew 10:25 Luke 10:16 The Pharisees missed Micah 5:8 Grumbling is the first word in the sentence for emphasis The question was not sincere but a rebuke for the activity It was an offense for a Jew to eat with a Gentile; ceremonial uncleanness Peter in Acts 10 Table fellowship was taken seriously in Jesus time Implies deeper unity than what we give it Jesus! What a Friend for sinners! Jesus! Lover of my soul! Friends may fail me, foes assail me, He, my Savior, makes me whole. THE CORRECTING 5:31, 32 Matthew 9:12, 13 adds I desire compassion and not sacrifice The perfect answer: only sick people need a doctor The Pharisees assumed they were spiritually well if you think you are good enough for heaven, then you are not A sick person knows he is sick For too many people their religion acts like a vaccine: it keeps them from the real thing Far too many people are filled with religion but not a relationship They think they are well The Great Physician can do nothing for those who think they are not sick. If you are self-sufficient, if you recognize no personal needs, and if you go in a rebellious, self-chosen pathway, your choice will lead you to destruction. Those who recognize their sinfulness, however, find the gospel very appealing. The grumbling led to Jesus stating His purpose in coming to earth Perfect tense: Jesus invaded earth for one purpose and that purpose continues Jesus is not endorsing the self-righteous Pharisees He will blister them later for their hypocrisy Luke 19:10 is the theme of the book The smug self-righteous Pharisees shunned contact with sinners Jesus wrapped His arms around them The Pharisees even sought to use it against Jesus 7:34 used as a means of condemnation The call of Jesus is to Repentance Nothing less than a complete transformation; a reversal Two elements: sorrow for sin and a turning to God in faith The call to repentance is how Jesus began His public ministry; Matthew 4:17 Luke 13:5 Still the call today Acts 3:19, 26 2 Timothy 2:25, 26 2 Peter 3:9 Metanoia: means however much more than merely a change of one's mind but also includes a complete change of heart, attitude, interest, and direction. Luke uses the word 14x in the gospel; 11x in Acts The related word, epistrophe, meaning conversion is also frequented by Luke 1 Thessalonians 1:9 is the perhaps the best illustration CONCLUSION Are sinners welcome in our service? I mean hard core, well-known, in the gutter sinners? We already have plenty of the good-looking kind Let me introduce you to Tammy and Trudy CALLING THE HORRIBLE DEPLORABLES Luke 5:27-32 THE SETTING Mark 2 locates Jesus by the sea shore; still at Capernaum Luke 3:13 John the Baptist message to tax collectors Jesus saw a contemplative gaze Publican a man who did public duty THE CALLING Command a clear call to become a disciple The call of Jesus was upon the whole life 1 Corinthians 1:27 Response - the choice of a lifetime Luke 14:33; Mark 8:34-37 THE GATHERING 5:29 Levi is now sharing the blessedness of what he found Is your home a place of influence? Three places Jesus frequented: Open places: a place for preaching Quiet places: where He prayed to the Father Festive places: where He could celebrate with people THE GRUMBLING 5:30 The Separatists Lights shine best in darkness The question was not sincere but a rebuke for the activity Table fellowship was taken seriously in Jesus time THE CORRECTING 5:31, 32 if you think you are good enough for heaven, then you are not Far too many people are filled with religion but not a relationship Jesus invaded earth for one purpose and that purpose continues Luke 19:10 is the theme of the book Repentance: sorrow for sin and a turning to God in faith 1 Thessalonians 1:9 is the perhaps the best illustration Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(44).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/29/2022 7:27:48 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message EXCLUSIVE Luke 5:33-39 INTRODUCTION It has been the position of this church that salvation is exclusive. That does not mean that this church has a corner on the truth or that Baptists are the only ones going to heaven. I had a Methodist lady one day accuse me of such because I declined participating in something they were doing. Our definition of exclusive is directly related to our understanding of the gospel of Jesus Christ. Paul wrote in Galatians 1:6-8 I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different "good news"; and there isn't another "good news." Only there are some who trouble you, and want to pervert the Good News of Christ. But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any "good news" other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed. Acts 4:12 There is salvation in none other, for neither is there any other name under heaven, that is given among men, by which we must be saved!" This is Reformation Sunday. The battle of Martin Luther was about the roll of the Romans Catholic Church in salvation; specifically buying your way into heaven. The salve of indulgences really got his attention leading to the posting of the 95 Thesis on the local town bulletin board in an effort to stimulate conversation. The battle ultimately led to his excommunication from the church. The effort went something like this: When Tetzel came near Wittenberg, St Peter s profits soared, I wrote a little notice for the All Saints Bull tin board: You cannot purchase merits, for we re justified by grace! Here s 95 more reasons, Brother Tetzel, in your face! Oh, Papal bulls, indulgences, and transubstantiation Speak your mind against them and face excommunication! Nail your theses to the door, Let s start a Reformation! Papal bulls, indulgences, and transubstantiation! To the Roman church and many churches today, you are saved by Jesus Christ and It was the and that includes a boat load of stuff Church tradition, church councils, papal authority, the mass, last rights Good works, church attendance, baptism, marriage, Mary, indulgences, purgatory The result of the work of Luther and others is what we know today as the 5 Solas: Sola Scriptura, Sola Gratia, Sola Fide, Solus Christus, Soli Deo Gloria Sola means alone Scripture Alone, Grace Alone, Faith Alone, Christ Alone, to the Glory of God Alone All of this study is to answer the question: How does a guilty man become acceptable to God? The Philippian jailor asked it another way (16:30) Sirs, what must I do to be saved? The only answer, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ and you shall be saved The message of salvation is exclusive. There is no other way. Jesus said, I am the Way, the Truth, and the Life. No one comes to the Father but by Me John 14:6 Salvation is not Jesus and Our text in Luke 5 deals with the same question How does a guilty man become acceptable to God? 3 things to examine from verses 33-39 The Inquiry, The Instruction, The Illustrations THE INQUIRY 5:33 The question comes as a challenge of religious practices Another conflict incident Matthew and Mark include the disciples of John in the questioning Fasting; 2 Samuel 1:12 expressing grief 1 Kings 21:27 expressing repentance Jonah 3:7 expressing humility Leviticus 16:29 the only commanded public fast afflict your souls Otherwise it was a voluntary observance In Jesus day, it had become a common sign of piety Luke 18:12 the Pharisee who fasted twice a week (Monday and Thursday) Pharisees had made fasting a significant ritual; seeking he applause of man Lack of fasting would be seen as undisciplined, impious Disciples of John combined with Pharisees is not a strange alliance Not all of John s disciples followed Jesus; but remained strict in their religious practices THE INSTRUCTION 5:34, 35 Replied with a rhetorical question During a joyous celebration such as a wedding it would be absurd to expect the guests to fast instead of feast Jesus presence was to inspire joy It would be totally inappropriate to fast in mourning But the rejoicing will not last forever Here is a hint at His coming death At that time, food will no longer be desired because of grief Post resurrection: 24:41 Jesus asked for something to eat There may not have been food on the table Acts 13:2,3; 14:23 fasting after the ascension Fasting today: We have the presence of the Bridegroom no such command but voluntary; grief, repentance, humility Personal time of seeking God Isaiah 58 God is more interested in an empty wallet than an empty stomach THE ILLUSTRATIONS 5:36-39 Two short illustrations revealing a tremendous change taking place Note the use of old and new Jesus is preparing them for a radical change Old and New cloth: the stronger, unshrunk cloth would contract and make the hole larger Old and New wine skins: old skins could not withstand the fermentation of new wine, the pressure would burst them, spilling the contents The old and new as used by Jesus is not primarily addressing the Old Covenant or the Old Testament. Psalm 19:7 the law of the Lord is perfect, converting the soul Matthew 5:17 "Don't think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn't come to destroy, but to fulfill. Galatians 3:24 Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith. The main issue was not with the Old Testament Jesus is telling the Pharisees and disciples of John to not try and patch the gospel into their old works oriented fasting religious system The gospel is not designed to blend with the old A new container is needed: believers become the new container for the Holy Spirit 5:39 states why I don t think Jesus is primarily speaking of the OT The old is better the old is preferable The Pharisees had become drunk in the old wine of their traditions and rituals They would not accept the new wine of the gospel In their self-sufficiency and pride they believed they needed nothing new It was their form of Judaism that they hung on to If they really followed Old Testament Judaism, they would have believed in Jesus Luke 24:27 Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself. CONCLUSION The question how does a guilty man become acceptable to God? Over 500 years ago it was the 95 Thesis of Martin Luther which helped redirect the church to an orthodox theology. Luther dealt with as soon as the coin in the coffer rings, a soul from purgatory springs. It was the works oriented salvation of the Roman Catholic church. Many churches continue to add something to the message of salvation. Jesus is not viewed as exclusive. Too many want to add something to the message. Man has become convinced that it is up to him to make himself acceptable to God. Jesus is exclusive. The gospel message is exclusive. There is no other name given among men whereby we must be saved. EXCLUSIVE Luke 5:33-39 Galatians 1:6-8 Acts 4:12 Scripture Alone, Grace Alone, Faith Alone, Christ Alone, to the Glory of God Alone How does a guilty man become acceptable to God? John 14:6 THE INQUIRY 5:33 Fasting; 2 Samuel 1:12 expressing grief 1 Kings 21:27 expressing repentance Jonah 3:7 expressing humility Leviticus 16:29 the only commanded public fast afflict your souls THE INSTRUCTION 5:34, 35 During a joyous celebration such as a wedding it would be absurd to expect the guests to fast instead of feast We have the presence of the Bridegroom THE ILLUSTRATIONS 5:36-39 Jesus is preparing them for a radical change Psalm 19:7 Matthew 5:17 Galatians 3:24 The main issue was not with the Old Testament The gospel is not designed to blend with the old The Pharisees had become drunk in the old wine of their traditions and rituals Luke 24:27 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(45).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/5/2022 9:39:42 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message SABBATH CONFRONTATIONS Luke 6:1-11 INTRODUCTION How would you like to live in a world where someone is constantly spying on you? Someone besides Google Not only spying but seeking to use whatever information they may find to bring about your destruction. Aka Politicians Luke 6:7 were watching - were watching closely; extreme close observation of the legal codes Their intent is clear: they might find an accusation against Him A malicious intent The Pharisees were the king of rule makers We live in a country that has books and books of laws trying to improve on the 10 Commandments The Pharisees were no different. Exodus 20:8-11 Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy And then it goes on to say In it you shall do no work . . . But the Jews went on to ask, What is work? They went on to define it under thirty-nine different heads which they called Fathers of Work. Even that was not enough. Each of these heads was greatly sub-divided. Jeremiah 17:21 Take heed to yourselves, and bear no burden on the Sabbath day What is a burden you ask? A burden is food equal in weight to a dried fig, enough wine for mixing in a goblet, milk enough for one swallow, oil enough to anoint a small member, water enough to moisten an eye-salve, paper enough to write a custom-house notice upon, ink enough to write two letters, reed enough to make a pen. And the Pharisees were well-aware of the thousands of rules and now they are watching closely to identify violators. So they set out to extract from the great principles of the law an infinite number of rules and regulations to govern every conceivable situation in life. Our location in Luke is part of the first wave of opposition Jesus and the disciples were to face from the religious leaders: Scribes, Pharisees, disciples of John the Baptist, Herodians First: the authority to forgive sins; Luke 5:17-26, healing a paralytic Second: He associated with sinners; Luke 5:27-32, the party hosted by Levi Third: He did not observe the many religious fasts; Luke 5:33-39 Jesus in Jerusalem (Harmony of the Gospels) Fourth: gospel of John 5; Jesus healed a man of the Sabbath by the pool of Bethesda and had him carry his bed through the temple area He had been sick for 38 years (5:5) 5:16 For this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath Fifth: also in John 5; Jesus spoke of God as His Father and claimed diving prerogatives 5:18 For this cause therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the Sabbath, but also called God his own Father, making himself equal with God. That brings us to Luke 6 SETTING THE STAGE Opposition #6 Jesus defends His disciples for plucking off heads of grain on the Sabbath Matthews s account in 12:1-8 the accusation is against the disciples; 12:2 Luke says Jesus is included Opposition #7; Luke 6:6-11; Jesus heals a man with a withered hand (Matt. 12:9-14; Mark 3:1-6) Both occasions occur on a Sabbath day Defaming the Sabbath was a capital offense; Exodus 35:2; Num. 15:32-36 The religious leaders were desperate to get something to stick to remove Jesus through death Luke records 7 Sabbath scenes 4:22 after initial reception gracious words , Jesus mentions the healing of Naaman, a Gentile and the crowd moved to throw Him off the cliff 4:31 in Capernaum; cast out a demon 6:1-2 in the grain field 6:6-9 healing of withered hand 13:10-16 healing of woman ill 18 years 14:1-5 healing a man with dropsy in home of a Pharisee 23:54, 55 resurrection morning Jesus was addressing one of the four major tenets of Judaism Sabbath, circumcision, eating clean and unclean, worship in Jerusalem Rabbit trail #1 Sunday is not the Sabbath Warren Wiersbe says "The Sabbath is a reminder of the completion of "the old Creation," while the Lord's Day is a reminder of our Lord's finished work in "the new Creation" The Sabbath speaks of rest after work and relates to the Law, while the Lord's Day speaks of rest before work and relates to grace." Rabbit Trail #2 It is easy to fall into legalism THE ACCUSATIONS Jesus healed a bedridden man on the Sabbath, told him to take up his bed and walk John 5 Jesus permitted His disciples to pluck heads of grain to pull the heads of grain from the stalks, rub the kernals, blow off the chaff and eat the grains This was permitted on weekdays, so no complaints about damage to the field OT clarification: Deuteronomy 23:24, 25 In the esteemed opinion of the Law interpreters, this was work. Pulling off the heads of grain was harvesting Rubbing out the kernels was threshing Blowing the chaff was winnowing Chewing the raw grain was grinding They were not breaking the Law at all; only the added rules Jesus healed a man with the withered hand Jesus did not apply a remedy or even touch the man so outwardly He had not broken any law. This fact probably only brought more anger THE DEFENSE An appeal to the example of God John 5:17 My Father has been working until now, and I have been working. His Father works at sustaining the universe from creation until now Jesus appealed to an act of David 1 Samuel 21:6 6:3 Have you not read? sarcastic, taunting? We cannot miss the slam of Jesus here How dare He question our devotion to the Scriptures? Doesn t He know who we are? Jesus appeals to Scripture. What does God have to say about this? 1 Samuel 21:6 David on the run from Saul (after meeting Jonathan in the field) David was not a priest; Lev. 24:5-9; ate the show-bread to meet his needs David was never condemned for his action; men were hungry "some aspects of the Sabbath restrictions are not inviolable moral absolutes, but rather precepts pertaining to the ceremonial features of the law" - MacArthur It was not a violation of Sabbath law but another law just as strict Pharisees had read the account, but their reading failed to notice the deeper implications Jesus appealed to the actions of the priests Matthew 12:5 On the Sabbath prepare animals for sacrifice and offer them Matthew 12:5 12:7 Jesus quotes Hosea 6:6 For I desire mercy, and not sacrifice; And the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings. (WEB) relieving suffering or hunger is more pleasing to God than the sacrifices in the temple Mark 3:5 Jesus was grieved with their heartless response Jesus stated the purpose of the Sabbath Jesus stated The Sabbath was made for a blessing to man God did not create mankind just to be a race of Sabbath-keepers Mark 2:27 By the addition of all their minute rules and regulations the Jewish "sages" had in effect made the Sabbath the "master" over men, and a heavy burden hardly conducive to giving one rest. The Jews instead of enjoying the Sabbath as a day of rest, now had to work even harder than ever to make sure they did not break any of the many Sabbath regulations. Jesus declared His own Messianic authority included jurisdiction even over the Sabbath day This meant that His interpretation of what constitutes a violation of the Sabbath law was more authoritative than that of the learned scribes of His day Luke 6:9 Jesus is declaring His Anointed position; revealing Himself as Lord Jesus is saying He had the right to break the Law since He was the Lord of the Sabbath. (Matthew 5:17) a rather staggering claim He was the One who made the Sabbath in the beginning and the authoritative Interpreter of the purpose of the day Jesus maintained that doing good to one s fellow man is right any time 6:9 Relieving suffering is lawful even on the Sabbath Here you have a compassionate Lord in contrast to the heartless opponents Jesus appealed to their own habits in caring for valuable animals on the Sabbath Matthew 12:11, 12 Jesus honored the Sabbath It was a day of worship and a day of rest and mercy It was the foolish and unmerciful demands of the rabbinical interpretations that aroused His anger and grief Mark 3:5 To Jesus Sabbath was for rest When occasion arose to bring someone rest from suffering and affliction and humiliation, it was both permissible and proper to give it THE TAKE-HOME Our text today is about authority. There was natural conflict: the authority of Christ vs. the authority of the Pharisees. This is battle of our day; who is in charge; who is calling the shots; who is the authority Romans 7:23-25 Our text today is about mercy SABBATH CONFRONTATIONS Luke 6:1-11 Exodus 20:8-11 Jeremiah 17:21 SETTING THE STAGE Defaming the Sabbath was a capital offense; Exodus 35:2 Jesus was addressing one of the four major tenets of Judaism Rabbit trail #1 Sunday is not the Sabbath Rabbit Trail #2 It is easy to fall into legalism THE ACCUSATIONS Jesus healed a bedridden man John 5 Jesus permitted His disciples to pluck heads of grain Jesus healed a man with the withered hand THE DEFENSE An appeal to the example of God John 5:17 An appeal to an act of David 1 Samuel 21:6 David was never condemned for his action An appeal to the actions of the priests Matthew 12:5 Jesus stated the purpose of the Sabbath The Sabbath was made for a blessing to man Jesus declared His own Messianic authority included jurisdiction even over the Sabbath day THE TAKE-HOME Our text today is about authority Our text today is about mercy Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(46).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/11/2022 5:15:14 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DESIGNATING DEITY S DOZEN Part 1 Luke 6:12-16 INTRODUCTION Henry Drummond , the Scottish author, evangelist and author was once invited to speak to an exclusive men's club in London. He began his talk with this analogy: "Gentlemen, the entrance fee into the kingdom of heaven is nothing; however, the annual subscription is everything." Because Jesus Christ paid the total price for salvation, it costs nothing to become His disciple. But to follow Him as a faithful disciple costs everything we have. We are not only saved by Christ's blood but are bought with it and therefore belong totally to Him (1 Cor. 6:19-20 ; 7:23 ) Our study of the life of Jesus in the gospel of Luke brings us to the selection process of the 12 men out of the total disciples to be in a close relationship with Him. 12 men who would receive training to be catalyst in bringing the gospel of Jesus to the world. PREPARATION What? Prayer: a word meaning to speak out before the one who prays seeking God s face convey the sense of being immediately before Him and hence the ideas of adoration, devotion, and worship. in the NT always used of prayer before God 37x in the NT; note the use in Acts and the early church Luke's uses of proseuche - Lk. 6:12 ; Lk. 19:46 ; Lk. 22:45 ; Acts 1:14 ; Acts 2:42 ; Acts 3:1 ; Acts 6:4 ; Acts 10:4 ; Acts 10:31 ; Acts 12:5 ; Acts 16:13 ; Acts 16:16 the imperfect tense emphasizes all-night; He prayed and prayed and prayed over and over reinforced in the words: continued all night Pray is in the middle voice which the subject is intentionally, voluntarily acting in His own interest, and He receives the benefit of the action. Prayer benefits the one who is praying When? in those days Selection of the men who will carry on the ministry after His death, burial, resurrection and ascension. Days of ministry; days of opposition The opposition particularly give us insight of the character of those selected The selection would influence the future of the world By praying Jesus is recognizing His dependence of the faithfulness of His Father, but His selection would recognize His dependence of the faithfulness of His friends The decisions He was about to make called for great spiritual discernment. The character, capacity, and commitment of each disciple had to be weighed. Luke frequently shows Jesus in the "secret place" praying to His Father in Heaven and particularly before major events in His ministry. Lk3:21 ; 5:16 ; 9:18 , 28 , 29 ; 11:1 ; 22:32 , 40-46 . Where? the mountain A specific known mountain not known to us Provided a place of seclusion; the height of a mountain would give the sense of nearness to God A fixed law of Jesus was to never act independently of His Father The original sin in the Garden of Eden was acting in independence of God D. L. Moody: "I'd rather be able to pray than be a great preacher; Jesus Christ never taught his disciples how to preach, but only how to pray." Thoughts about prayer (which may be of help) (Rod Mattoon) 1. Be simple and direct the grace of simplicity vs. offense of formality 2. Pray audibly utterance helps in defining the request 3. Be honest do not express a want you do not feel; e.g. confession 4. Pray earnestly desire should be intense but no need to shout 5. Do not mock God Do not beg Him to come to you 6. Do not ask God to do for you what He has given you to do Illustration: "What is the best position to pray?" The story about three preachers answers this question. Three preachers were talking about prayer in general and the appropriate and effective positions for prayer. As they were talking, a telephone repairman was working on the phone system in the background. One minister shared that he felt the key was in the hands. He always held his hands together and pointed them upward as a form of symbolic worship. The second suggested that real prayer was conducted on your knees. The third suggested that they both had it wrong. The only position worth its salt was to pray while stretched out flat on your face. By this time the phone man couldn't stay out of the conversation any longer. He interjected: "I found that the most powerful prayer I ever made was while I was dangling upside down by my heels from a power pole, suspended forty feet above the ground!" Beloved, don't worry about your position, just pray! Spurgeon: While others found rest in sleep, Jesus refreshed Himself in prayer The time he chose was admirable, it was the hour of silence, when the crowd would not disturb him; the time of inaction, when all but himself had ceased to labour; and the season when slumber made men forget their woes, and cease their applications to him for relief. Andrew Murray: Prayer is the one hand with which we grasp the invisible. Fasting is the other hand, the one with which we let go of the visible. Fasting helps to express, to deepen, and to confirm the resolution that we are ready to sacrifice anything, even ourselves, to attain the Kingdom of God. We generally are guilty of talking much about prayer but pray too little PURPOSE Chosen: to select out, picking out; chosen of Himself and for Himself John 15:16 You did not choose Me, but I chose you Chosen by the sovereign will of God for the sovereign purpose of God Even one who would betray Disciple: a learner; we learn by listening and by watching We are not told how many total disciples followed Jesus v. 17 We know He sent out 70 one time We know the 12 were soon to be called apostles sent ones 12: no doubt relating to the 12 tribes of Israel The importance of the number is evident from Acts 1:23-26; adding Matthias Luke 22:29, 30 clear connection of 12 The number is not arbitrary or accidental Mark and Matthew add to the text: Mark 3:14, 15 "appointed twelve, so that they would be with Him and that He could send them out to preach, 15 and to have authority (exousia = the right and the might!) to cast out the demons." Matthew 10:1 "gave them authority (exousia = the right and the might!) over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal (therapeuo ) every kind of disease and every kind of sickness." Harmony: Jesus chose them in Luke 6 and commissioned them later (9:1) Mark also adds 3:14 so that they would be with Him 2 reasons stated for calling of disciples: So that they would be with Him So that He could send them out MacArthur: "Much can be learned from the classroom, from good books, and from personal experience. But spiritual growth comes best from close contact with a holy example If there was ever a group of men who lived Romans 12:1, it was the disciples Herbert Lockyer "All the apostles of the Bible" "Some time ago, The Reader's Digest carried a most illuminating article by Ernest Hauser on The Miracle of the Twelve, which bore the subtitle, Hand-picked by Jesus, they were the missionaries of a message that changed the world." Who were these men whom Jesus chose, in whose honor cathedrals have been built, around whom legend has spun golden webs, and whose names have been used naming multitudes since? How was it that principally through the evangelistic efforts within three and a half centuries, proud imperial Rome succumbed to the glad tidings of the Redeemer from the East? A fact we cannot deny is that no body of men, few or many, has ever exercised so vast an influence on the world as the small circle of ordinary men Jesus called, trained, commissioned, and empowered to further His cause. surveyed from the human angle, the twelve had meager equipment for the great tasks before them. They are referred to as being "unlettered and ignorant men," which means they were unprofessional men absolutely outside the current schools of philosophical, political and religious thought. To all appearances no mission seemed more hopeless than theirs. Were they not facing and enterprise doomed to failure, seeing they had no social status no organization behind them, no wealth of their own or wealthy backers? How could they then expect success in the world wide campaign awaiting them? They became companions of a Man who was born in poverty, spent most of His years in obscurity, never had any position in life, never wrote a book, was gibbeted as a felon between two thieves, and who lived for only 33 years. How could they expect to bring the impact of their witness to bear upon the world as followers of One who was despised and rejected of men? Yet forward they went to summon the world to His feet, and the world came - and is still coming, for in spite of the godlessness of our age, there are millions today in the world who love this Man of Galilee... ...they were men with like passions as ourselves. With all their noble qualities, they had their imperfections and limitations...He called them to follow Him, not because of what they were in themselves when He met them for the first time, but because of what under His tuition, and by His power they would become... ...It is not what we are, in ourselves, when we hear and respond to Christ's call, but what by His grace and power He can make us as we company with Him. When Jesus called those first disciples, He said,"Follow me, and I will make you" - and there are no self-made Christians in His service;... ...those whom the Lord wants and wins are those He is able to fashion into human books to tell the story of His love and grace. As did those He gathered around Him in the days of His flesh, disciples today begin their apprenticeship by following Christ, daily learning of, and from Him (Matt. 11:29 ). Then as they grow in grace and knowledge, they become more fitted to represent the Savior in a world of sin and need.. ...To us, the apostles were not exceptional men, strangers to our weakness, temptations and difficulties. Jesus chose twelve typical representative men...He chose those who were poor and teachable as the clay out of which to fashion spiritual greatness... ...The twelve Christ chose, then, were twelve men, distinguished by marked degrees of difference and varieties of temper and disposition... ...These twelve types were selected as a representation of a narrow circle of the whole human race, in which there is a great variety of types... ...It has been suggested that in the choice of the twelve, Christ was experimenting on the entire human race. If He could bring these twelve under His power and subdue them to His qualities, it would be proof that all men might be made to yield to His sway. It was a prophetic demonstration that His salvation was adapted to all classes with varieties of disposition and qualities. The selection was also proof that Jesus was both willing and able to employ all manner of gifts and all manner of natures in His service. Doubtless if the choice had been ours we should have left several of the twelve out, and chosen others.... The names of the twelve appear on the foundation stones of the New Jerusalem which their devotion and dedication helped to build"... ...Twelve is the number establishing governmental perfection...it is a clear illustration to the tribes of a new people. It happily expresses that Jesus was the Divine Messianic King DESIGNATING DEITY S DOZEN Part 2 Luke 6:12-16 DESIGNATING DEITY S DOZEN Part 1 Luke 6:12-16 PREPARATION What? Prayer: seeking God s face When? in those days Days of ministry; days of opposition Where? the mountain 1. Thoughts about prayerBe simple and direct 2. Pray audibly 3. Be honest 4. Pray earnestly 5. Do not mock God 6. Do not ask God to do for you what He has given you to do We generally are guilty of talking much about prayer but pray too little PURPOSE Chosen: to select out John 15:16 Disciple: a learner Luke 22:29, 30 Mark 3:14, 15 Matthew 10:12 Reasons stated for calling of disciples: So that they would be with Him So that He could send them out Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(47).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/19/2022 6:53:15 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: RE: MESSAGE I am going to put the whole outline on paper without blanks. Dwain A Hill From: Jonathan Hill Sent: Saturday, November 19, 2022 5:48 PM To: pastordhill813@gmail.com Subject: Re: MESSAGE Like the personality traits and whatnot? Get Outlook for Android From: pastordhill813@gmail.com Sent: Saturday, November 19, 2022 5:36:21 PM To: Jonathan Hill Subject: MESSAGE I think I will put all the disciple info on a paper so don t worry about getting it all on the screen. That is way too much typing and I might lose some people in the process. Thanks. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(48).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/19/2022 5:36:28 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE I think I will put all the disciple info on a paper so don t worry about getting it all on the screen. That is way too much typing and I might lose some people in the process. Thanks. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(49).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/19/2022 5:34:35 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE DESIGNATING DEITY S DOZEN Part 2 Luke 6:12 16 INTRODUCTION Preparation Purpose PARTICULARS Four Scriptures citing the 12: Matthew 10: Mark 3; Luke 6; Acts 1 Always listed in a similar order List contains 3 groups of four names The first name in each subgroup is always the same; Peter, Philip, James (Alphaeus) Judas Iscariot is always last The order is in decreasing intimacy with Jesus The first four: Peter, Andrew, James and John The first four called: John 1:35-42 The most inner circle ; those who are the most known We know a little about group 2 but almost nothing about group 3 Jesus knew their character; their positive and negative attributes James and John: sons of thunder ; displayed in calling down fire doubting Thomas, devoted John, diligent Matthew, devious Judas, dependable Andrew, daring Peter, discerning Nathaniel. He knew what they would become Two sets of brothers; four fishermen; one tax collector Nothing know about occupation of the rest Vance Havner: Not that our Lord went around "seeing the good in everybody," the latent possibilities, calling out "hidden powers." "He knew what was in man." He saw within Simon nothing that Simon could make of himself, but rather what God would make of him. To educate the old Adam is to make him doubly dangerous. To polish him is to render him far more deceptive. To make him more religious is to leave him tenfold more the child of hell. When Jesus saw Simon, he saw Peter, not by reformation but by transformation. "Thou art.. thou shalt be." PRINCIPLES Person: Simon; Peter (rock) foundation Personality: Impulsive Matthew 14:28 Cowardly Matthew 14:30; 26:69-74 Hot Tempered John 18:10 Tenderhearted Matthew 26:75 Insightful Matthew 16:16 Dense Matthew 16:21-23 Courageous Acts 5:27-30 Person: Andrew; brother of Peter Personality: Enthusiastic about Christ John 1:35-42 Inquisitive John 1:35-38 Resourceful John 6:8, 9 Person: James, son of Zebedee Personality: Vengeful and fiery Luke 9:54 Selfish and conceited Mark 10:35-37 Committed to Christ Acts 12:2 Person: John, brother of James Personality: Vengeful and fiery Luke 9:54 Judgmental Mark 9:38 Selfish Mark 10:35-37 Loving, compassionate Acts 4:13; 1 John 4 Person: Philip; not Philip the evangelist Personality: Practical John 6:7 Helpful John 12:20-21 Literal, confused John 14:8 Person: Bartholomew; Nathaniel Personality: Skeptical John 1:46 Honest John 1:47 Faithful John 1:49 Person: Matthew, Levi Personality: Penitent Matthew 9:9; 10:2 Hospitable Matthew 9:10 Person: Thomas, Didymus (twin) Personality: Inquisitive John 14:5 Doubtful John 20:24, 25 Courageous John 11:16 Faithful John 20:24-29 Person: James, son of Alphaeus Personality: Unknown Person: Simon, the Zealot Personality: Patriotic Matthew 10:4 Loyal Mark 3:18 Passionate Luke 6:15 Sacrificial Acts 1:13 Person: Judas of James, (son or brother); Thaddeus; Jude, Lebbaeus (leb-bah'-yos) Personality: Inquisitive John 14:22 Confused John 14:22 Person: Judas Iscariot Personality: Greedy Matthew 26:14-16 Deceitful Matthew 26:25 Treacherous Matthew 26:47-50 Remorseful Matthew 27:3-5 PRACTICAL: Being a Disciple Count the Cost: deny yourself; lose your life Fellowship: not forsake the assembling Serving: the greatest is servant of all Failure and Forgiveness: Jesus forgave and restored Power of the Spirit: one Spirit; different gifts It is ok to work and never be noticed: James, son of Alphaeus CONCLUSION: From the list of names it is evident Jesus used a variety of personalities, temperaments, backgrounds But Jesus knew what they could become We have testimonies of changed lives From cowardly to bold before national leaders From vengeful to the disciple of love Scripture records only one death: James Tradition states each died of martyrdom except John who is thought to have died of old age Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(50).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/26/2022 3:28:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message MESSAGE PREPARATION Luke 6:17-19 INTRODUCTION In our study of the Lord Jesus Christ in the gospel of Luke, we have come to 6:17-19. These three verses reveal no quotes from Jesus, no personal interactions of Jesus, no names are mentioned, nothing new to report. We could easily read them and immediately jump into the Sermon on the Mount in verse 20. But I am compelled to spend today speaking from this text . All Scripture is given by inspiration of God These verses are still telling us about Jesus, and we want to know about Jesus. Out of all his research, Luke found this material significant enough to include in his message to Theophilus. Our job is to examine the text and reap the benefits of study. It is a little different than sermon preparation today. We are not outside on a mountain, no miraculous healing will take place, no one has traveled 100 miles, we are not a mixed group of Jews and Gentiles. But the text does say the people came to hear. So with that in common, we can begin. THE LOCATION 6:17 v. 12 Jesus had gone further into the mountain to pray all night He has chosen out 12 disciples v. 17 He descended to a level place stood in plain this could have been at the foot of the mountain, but I don t think so. Matthew s account in chapter 5: And seeing the multitudes, He went up into a mountain Putting the text together: I believe Jesus came down from His praying location to a level place on the mountain where the people came up to meet Him. There are suggested places should you tour the area The Horns of Hattim; twin peaks by the road between Tiberias and Nazareth Another: a hill 3 miles south of Capernaum THE CONGREGATION 6:17 Their Identity We can identify 3 groups of people The 12 disciples A crowd of disciples: a bigger group of learners who traveled with Jesus Some were believers; others more casual followers A great multitude: from Judea, Jerusalem; Tyre and Sidon This is the crowd for the Sermon on the Mount Primarily presented to the 12 but in the hearing of all Jews from all over Israel Gentiles, suggested by their local some were true disciples, but many were there for the show Jesus had a way of weeding them out through His teaching Calling of discipleship; forsaking all, take up a cross Giving wealth away Or simply stopping the show Jesus will someday separate sheep from goats, wheat from tares Jesus will identify His true followers Those who have come to the saving knowledge of the Lord Jesus True believers: those who characterize the qualities of the Sermon on the Mount The presence of the people suggests the popularity of Jesus His fame has gone out throughout the land Mark 3:8 they had heard what Jesus had done Their Expectations to hear and to be healed 4:22 gracious words 4:32 and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority. 4:36 authority and power 5:1 heard the Word of God We must never change our approach to our Bible It is God s Holy Word It is inspired; it is sharper than a two-edged sword; it is not of private interpretation; it will never pass away ; it is the light to your way, the foot-lamp to every step; forever settled in heaven People, churches, seminaries, denominations continue to turn away from Scripture to an interpretation of their own making. Some try to use Scripture to justify their own sinful lifestyles They deny the Bible s authority for life The depravity of man is becoming more bold in their attacks How and why do they do that? Because Christians are for the most part of failing to trust and obey If we are careless in our approach to elevating the Scripture as God s Holy Word and the final authority for faith and practice, why should the ungodly have the first hint of fear that it is true? Judgement must begin with the house of God (1 Peter 4:17) To quote another, How shall we then live? THE POWER 6:17, 18 Present life suggests there are a number of ways to gather a crowd Self-esteem messages of Joel Olsteen pack a basketball arena Suppose healing miracles bring them out in bunches Church platforms have been given over to motorcycles, zoo animals, smoke machines, entertainers of all stripes It is all about counting noses and collecting money Jesus: Power went out from Him and healed all No wire in His ear to call out someone with a back pain He healed diseases He healed those tormented by demons He healed all; body, soul, spirit In spite of the priority of the Word, Jesus was still compassionate He knew if they were to receive thus saith the Lord , they needed to have other needs met Later He is going to feed those attending so that none feint on their return home Jesus is the channel of God s power as He reached out The power dispensed was given by the Holy Spirit Acts 10:38 As Jesus dispensed God s power, God gave Him more power to dispense Those present were prepared for the teaching which would follow If they are willing to accept Jesus power, they must hear His teaching These are the signs of the Kingdom Luke 4:18, 19 "The Spirit of the Lord is on me, Because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim release to the captives, Recovering of sight to the blind, To deliver those who are crushed, And to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord." R. C. Sproul: I think the greatest weakness in the church today is that almost no one believes that God invests His power in the Bible. Everyone is looking for power in a program, in a technique, in anything and everything except where God has placed it: His Word. CONCLUSION Preparing for the Message Jesus prayed all night He then selected His disciples Power prepared the people for the Sermon Power was through the Holy Spirit of God and Prayer We talk about prayer; we talk about the value of prayer But do we pray What power would be ours if we prayed? What would a praying church look like? MESSAGE PREPARATION Luke 6:17-19 THE LOCATION 6:17 He descended to a level place THE CONGREGATION 6:17 Their Identity 3 groups of people The 12 disciples A crowd of disciples A great multitude Jesus will someday separate sheep from goats True believers: those who characterize the qualities of the Sermon on the Mount Their Expectations to hear and to be healed We must never change our approach to our Bible The depravity of man is becoming more bold in their attacks THE POWER 6:17, 18 He healed diseases He healed those tormented by demons He healed all; body, soul, spirit In spite of the priority of the Word, Jesus was still compassionate As Jesus dispensed God s power, God gave Him more power to dispense These are the signs of the Kingdom Luke 4:18, 19 Power was through the Holy Spirit of God and Prayer Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(51).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/10/2022 6:42:43 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DROPPING A BOMBSHELL Luke 6:20-23 INTRODUCTION The ordination sermon for the disciples We will see four radical statements describing four paradoxical blessings to four groups: the poor, hungry, weeping, detested. The natural man reads them from the world's perspective as referring to literal poverty, hunger, etc. However, Jesus is looking at four spiritual conditions: poor in spirit, hungry for Jesus, mourning over sin, and persecuted for following Jesus Jesus is saying His followers will experience spiritual blessings in this life and in the life to come. One pastor titled this section: How to Live Life Happily Ever After The contrast will be true spiritual values verse the false values of the Pharisees The first three deal with how the sinner sees himself as poor, hungry and sorrowful. The fourth one is how the world sees the sinner. This is what is commonly called The Sermon on the Mount Some prefer to call Luke s account: The Sermon on the Plain Matthew has a much more extensive account in his gospel; chapters 5-7 Luke is much more abbreviated leading some to say they are different sermons Both accounts begin and end the same Both accounts are immediately followed by the healing of the centurion s servant What do we know? You can read Luke s sermon in just a few minutes; even 3-4 You can read Matthew s in a few more; maybe 10 I am convinced Jesus preached more than 10 minutes Neither Matthew or Luke records the full message Jesus could hold an audience Remember, there was a time when the attention span of people was much longer than it is today. And in the message, He no doubt reworded things, repeated things, gave other examples, expounded upon other things It is also easy to assume the message was repeated throughout the earthly ministry of Jesus John 21 tells us Jesus did a whole lot more than whas is recorded in Scripture What do we know? Jesus spoke the sermon in Aramaic; not Greek As translated, Matthew and Luke may use different words Luke places some other aspects of the message in other parts of his gospel Each writer wrote under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit Each writer wrote for an intended audience; Matthew to Jews; Luke to Theophilus Matthew included items of special interest to Jews: 5:17-42 Luke includes them elsewhere: 11:1-4; 12:22-31 Luke includes lines Matthew does not: 24-26; 38-40 What do we know? Jesus spoke words described as gracious, with authority, with power 5:1 the word of God How powerful? How authoritative? John 1:2 He was in the beginning with God. All things were made by Him and without Him nothing was made that was made How did He create? He spoke. He said let there be light He commanded demons to flee; illnesses to depart; the lame to walk That is powerful speech; that is every word of Jesus That is speaking with authority That is the Sermon on the Mount; that is the Word of God Man does live by bread alone, but every word that proceeds from the mouth of God What do we know? This is Jesus definitive explanation of what a follower of His looks like He is specifically addressing the 12 disciples; other disciples are present Still many making up the larger crowd of observers who have come from the entire region to hear and be healed v. 17 This is what a Christian looks like This is the measuring stick to which we look; This is the plumb line This is the determining factor between heaven and hell Wiersbe breaks it down this way: Being vs. 20-26 Loving vs. 27-36 Forgiving vs. 37-45 Obeying vs. 46-49 Description of Persons 20-26 Proclamation of Principles 27-45 Evaluation for Judgment 46-49 CHARACTERISTICS OF THOSE IN THE KNGDOM 20-26 Beatitudes and Woes PRACTICE OF THOSE IN THE KINGDOM 27-45 Loving and Forgiving EVALUATION OF THOSE IN THE KINGDOM 46-49 Illustration The message Jesus presents is something totally unexpected, surprising, startling and shocking. 6:20 Jesus is clearly directing His attention to the disciples This is their ordination message; their training officially begins now His gaze is firmly placed upon the 12 The crowd at large is at various levels of commitment and understanding The Sermon is not a statement of Christian ethics but a message of salvation CHARACTERISTICS OF THOSE IN THE KNGDOM 6:20-26 Beatitudes and Woes Beatitude #1 A paradox is a seemingly absurd or self-contradictory statement or proposition that when investigated or explained may prove to be well founded or true. Jesus places two things here in contrast: blessings and woes The entire population of the world fits into one of these categories As someone said, this life is the best the lost will experience and the worst that the blessed/saved will experience. Happy the poor--because yours is the reign of God. (YLT) Poor bankrupt, poor as a church mouse, dirt poor Matthew 5:3 poor in spirit ; nothing to do with finances Luke 4:18 preach the gospel to the poor same word Those who recognize they are bankrupt in spirit see their need for the gospel They are spiritually impoverished Spiritually bankruptcy puts one in position for spiritual blessing The blessing is a gift of grace; not the recognition of spiritual poverty One can recognize their spiritual poverty but never come to Christ Some might use the word happy for blessed Might be ok, but limited in its scope Happy depends too much on happenings; it fluctuates blessed makarpios; describes one who is fully satisfied regardless of circumstances or feelings blessed is a believer s steady state or condition Blessed is the person who sees his greatest need is spiritual and not material A financially poor person may be more aware of his spiritual needs Does not say a financially rich person cannot be a follower of Jesus Luke 8:1-3 Jesus had some financial backing This is not a blanket statement equating one s financial position to his spiritual condition Scripture is clear that finances can be a hindrance Jesus is not blessing poverty; it very well can be a curse There are many poor people who don t love Jesus Lloyd-Jones: poor is first because it is obviously the key to all that follows WWW: The truly blessed life comes not from getting, or from doing, but from being Wiersbe: Imagine how surprised they were when they heard Jesus describe happiness in terms just the opposite of what they expected! They discovered that what they needed most was not a change in circumstances but a change in their relationship to God and in their outlook on life. Jesus was not teaching that poverty, hunger, persecution, and tears were blessings in themselves. If that were true, He would never have done all He did to alleviate the sufferings of others. Rather, Jesus was describing the inner attitudes we must have if we are to experience the blessedness of the Christian life No amount of things can substitute for a personal relationship to God Jesus was calling for a brokenness of heart that confesses spiritual poverty within The humble person is the only kind of person Jesus can save Isaiah 57:15; 66:2; 1 Peter 5:6 The opposite of being poor in spirit is having a spirit full of self Having said all that, here is the summary: Poor in Spirit: To acknowledge our spiritual poverty before God; utter helplessness To acknowledge the real blessings of life and eternity come only from a right relationship with God to acknowledge our utter deadness before God we are no better, no richer, no more superior than the next person we are not proud nor haughty the poor in spirit approaches life in humility and appreciation Spirt full of Self: the opposite We are righteous in ourselves Yours is the kingdom of God Those who recognize their spiritual poverty enter the kingdom of God Having formerly been in the kingdom of darkness; the kingdom of Satan John 3:3, 5 Jesus to Nicodemus; Unless one is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God The rich cannot purchase it; but it is imperishable, undefiled, will not fade away, reserved in heaven for you those who admit their spiritual poverty. Kingdom is present and future is present tense; right now, now a future realization Presently: decidedly to live under the lordship of Jesus Christ, obeying His commands, living with the aim of pleasing Him Future: a coming day when Jesus will rule with a rod of iron, righteousness will dwell There are only two ways to live You can live for the pleasures of this present world, which will perish Or, you can submit yourself to Jesus and live for His present and coming Kingdom Leifeld: the poor can rejoice even in the midst of their destitution because they are already able to partake of some of the kingdom blessings NET: the kingdom of God belongs even now to people like you Why? The word kingdom suggests there is a King Those who admit their spiritual poverty experience the reign of the King Being blessed does not mean it will be an easy life It is a state of well-being that does not depend upon physical, temporary circumstances Jesus was born a King Are you a child of the King? DROPPING A BOMBSHELL Luke 6:20-23 Four radical statements describing four paradoxical blessings Neither Matthew or Luke records the full message Jesus spoke the sermon in Aramaic This is what a Christian looks like CHARACTERISTICS OF THOSE IN THE KNGDOM 6:20-26 Beatitude #1 Poor bankrupt They are spiritually impoverished blessed makarpios; describes one who is fully satisfied regardless of circumstances or feelings Jesus is not blessing poverty The humble person is the only kind of person Jesus can save Isaiah 57:15; 66:2; 1 Peter 5:6 Poor in Spirit: To acknowledge our spiritual poverty before God; To acknowledge the real blessings of life and eternity come only from a right relationship with God to acknowledge our utter deadness before God Yours is the kingdom of God Kingdom is present and future The word kingdom suggests there is a King Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(52).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/17/2022 3:43:18 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DROPPING A BOMBSHELL Part 2 Luke 6:20-26 INTRODUCTION We are looking at Luke s account of Jesus Sermon on the Mount Luke s 29 verses is much abbreviated from Matthew s 111 verses The sermon is not about Christian Ethics but about the qualities of discipleship. There are 3 groups of attendees: 12 disciples called apostles in v. 13 A greater number of disciples of various levels A large number of Jew and Gentile observers Jesus is describing what a follower of His looks like Question: would you rather be poor, hungry, crying, and scorned OR wealthy, satisfied, happy and popular? You will say that is a trick question because Jesus is speaking in spiritual terms But take that out of the equation; put yourself on this mountain plateau What would be your initial reaction to the presentation? Why would anyone in their right mind choose poverty over riches; hunger over satisfaction, crying over happiness and scorn over popularity? Apparently, Jesus does not understand what it means to be seeker sensitive Apparently, Jesus is not interested in having the biggest crowd, the largest building, His own podcast, or multimedia production company He was not concerned about being censored, although the threat was real His coming was to call sinners to repentance, to announce the coming kingdom of God, to preach the gospel and set the captives free He came to pay the sacrifice for man s sin His message is not pie-in-the-sky fluff His message was direct, confrontational, gut wrenching, convicting Note the response: Matthew 7:28, 29 (WEB) It happened, when Jesus had finished saying these things, that the multitudes were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them with authority, and not like the scribes. So the question on the table: which person would you rather be? The answer also describes why most people never set foot in a Bible teaching church Why subject yourself to that when across the street you can hear that God loves you and has a wonderful plan for your life? Last week: Blessed are the beggarly poor; those who know they are spiritually bankrupt, have nothing to offer God, and can only plead for His mercy What do they benefit? Theirs is the kingdom of God That is how you come to Christ, that is where salvation begins Blessed are you who hunger now, For you shall be filled 6:21 Speaking in the spiritual realm Matthew includes: hunger and thirst after righteousness Physical hunger is not a blessing. It is often sad and tragic Here it means to have a starving spirit that craves righteousness An impoverished, starving spiritual beggar and the only hope is found in Christ s righteousness Hunger is present tense. The hunger is continual. How is your spiritual hunger? Psalm 41:1, 2 As the deer pants for the water brooks, So my soul pants for You, O God. My soul thirsts for God, for the living God; When shall I come and appear before God? Psalm 63:1 O God, You are my God; I shall seek You earnestly; My soul thirsts for You, my flesh yearns for You, In a dry and weary land where there is no water. Luke 18:13 But the tax collector, standing some distance away, was even unwilling to lift up his eyes to heaven, but was beating his breast, saying, God, be merciful to me, the sinner! For they shall be filled satisfied, gorged Jesus is the bread of life; Jesus is fountain of living water Your cistern is broken (Jer. 2:13); come to Jesus Blessed are you who weep now, For you shall be laugh. Unique to Luke; Matthew uses the word mourn Present tense: a continual weeping over sin, spiritual bankruptcy and lack of righteousness The sign of a broken heart; the external expression of grief The person who knows he is unworthy before God Laughing comes from seeing the end of sin and shame Laughing comes from being comforted The comfort is present: assurance of forgiveness and assurance The comfort is eternal: passing from death to life, wiping away all Tears. John 5:24; Rev. 21:4 Blessed are you when men shall hate you, and when they shall exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man's sake. 6:22 This beatitude is different as it is something from outside of you The first three is a reflection of your heart This one indicates that the first three have taken hold This is the evidence that Christ is real in your life Key: for the Son of Man s sake From the world's perspective hatred, ostracism, insults and scorn are hardly a good recipe for blessing. True Christ followers are hated by the world because the world rejected Him John 5:18, 19 If the world hates you, you know that it has hated Me before it hated you. If you were of the world, the world would love its own; but because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, because of this the world hates you. Your treatment is compared to the Godly prophets; 2 Chron. 36:15, 16 The LORD, the God of their fathers, sent word to them again and again by His messengers, because He had compassion on His people and on His dwelling place; but they continually mocked the messengers of God, despised His words and scoffed at His prophets, until the wrath of the LORD arose against His people, until there was no remedy. Hated: a strong dislike, detest Exclude: exclude, to set off by boundary Revile: defame, reproach Cast you out: eject, throw you out as they persecute you they persecute Jesus: Acts 9:4-6 "you will be hated by all because of My name." (Luke 21:17 ) You will even be brought before governors and kings for My sake, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles. (Mt 10:18 ) He who has found his life will lose it, and he who has lost his life for My sake will find it. (Mt 10:39 ) "I (JESUS) will show him (Saul of Tarsus) how much he must suffer for My name s sake. (Acts 9:16 ) There is no more intimate fellowship with Jesus than the fellowship of His sufferings: Philippians 3:10 Christianophobia: hates or despises Christians or what they stand for As the world increasingly turns away from God, the hatred of God s people will increase exponentially. We are on the opposing side of the social issues of the day: abortion, homosexuality, transgenderism, marriage, child training, home schooling By standing for biblical truth in these areas, you will be accused of Hating homosexuals, hating women, intolerance, brainwashing It is all Satanically driven. Satan hates God. He is the god of this world Rejoice in that day and leap for joy Acts 5:41 So they went on their way from the presence of the Council, rejoicing that they had been considered worthy to suffer shame for His Name. Gundry: The reward is signed and sealed and not yet delivered Reward: pay which is due for labor performed Used again in v. 35 Payday Someday Estimated R. G. Lee preached the message over 1,200 times Not just for the unsaved which that message was directed God will reward His children also DROPPING A BOMBSHELL Part 2 Luke 6:20-26 His message was direct, confrontational, gut wrenching, convicting Blessed are you who hunger now, For you shall be filled 6:21 starving spirit that craves righteousness Psalm 41:1, 2 Psalm 63:1 Luke 18:13 For they shall be filled satisfied, gorged Blessed are you who weep now, For you shall be laugh. The sign of a broken heart The person who knows he is unworthy before God Laughing comes from seeing the end of sin and shame Blessed are you when men shall hate you, and when they shall exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man's sake. 6:22 This is the evidence that Christ is real in your life John 5:18, 19 Hated: a strong dislike, detest Exclude: exclude, to set off by boundary Revile: defame, reproach Cast you out: eject There is no more intimate fellowship with Jesus than the fellowship of His sufferings: Philippians 3:10 Rejoice in that day and leap for joy Acts 5:41 Gundry: The reward is signed and sealed and not yet delivered Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(53).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 12/31/2022 11:12:21 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DROPPING A BOMBSHELL Luke 6:20-40 CHARACTERISTICS OF THOSE IN THE KNGDOM 20-26 Beatitudes and Woes PRACTICE OF THOSE IN THE KINGDOM 27-45 Loving and Forgiving EVALUATION OF THOSE IN THE KINGDOM 46-49 Illustration But alla; establishing a major contrast He had just described their enemy in vs. 22, 23 Wiersbe: Jesus assumes that anybody who lived for eternal values would get into trouble with the world s crowd. 6:22 they will reject or avoid us 6:28 insult 6:29 physically abuse 6:30 sue Matthew quotes the former concept: Love thy neighbor, and hate thine enemy (5:43) Jesus is bringing to the world a new dimension of love The call to unconditional love is a hard one We must be convinced that there will come a day when God will make all things right Must not the judge of all the earth do right (Genesis 18:25) Vengeance is mine. I will repay, says the Lord (Romans 12:19) J. C. Ryle: Love is the grand characteristic of the gospel Love is the bond of perfectness (Col. 3:14) Love: without which a man is nothing in God s sight They were to give up much, and endure much, for the sake of showing kindness and avoiding strife. They were to forego even their rights, and submit to wrong, rather than awaken angry passions and create quarrels. In this they were to be like their Master, long-suffering, meek, and lowly of heart. unto you who hear Not just those who heard the voice of Jesus Also those who have spiritual ears to hear spiritual truth to you who are willing to listen hear carries with it the thought of obeying what is heard True disciples of Jesus will act on what is presented to them 6:47, 48 Jesus is about to present 9 commands to obey v. 27 Love, do v. 28 Bless, pray v. 29 offer v. 30 give, not ask back v. 31 do v. 35 Love, do good, lend v. 36 Be Commands which run counter to way we would typically respond There must be dependence on a supernatural source Romans 12:14-21 PRACTICE OF LOVE 6:27-36 A present tense command; always to be practiced Goes against every fiber of our fallen flesh Humanly IMpossible but supernaturally HIMpossible Love s Recipient Enemy: a deep seated animosity or hatred, which may be open or concealed or a deep-rooted hatred One who is antagonistic, seeking to injure, overthrown, or confound the opponent Love s Requirement It is one thing to say I love my enemy But Jesus follows it up with Do good It is not truly love until it acts A believer may refrain from hating his enemy, but that is not enough You are not required to admire or even like them; but you are to love them Romans 5:5 Galatians 5:22, 23 To love a friend is natural; to love an enemy is Christlike Agape: neither negative nor neutral; it is positive It is a self-sacrificing, self-denying love Unselfish, uninfluenced by any hope of return There is no greater testimony to a life changed by the grace of God than when someone does something good for someone who hates them or has done them harm.
