9:41 faithless and perverse generation 11:29 evil generation 32 Jesus characterized their opinions as childish and immature The games of children are often patterned after the activity of adults Play grown-up The children wanted to play a wedding game but others refused to dance It s a party but no one wanted to play They offered to play a funeral game but others refused to weep They could not agree on what games they wanted to play The games presented a great contrast Jesus said these religious leaders were playing games with their eternal souls they refused to accept God s message, no matter which messenger, John or Jesus, presented it John MacArthur calls this the The Parable of the Brats: Style Versus Substance "in this parable He identifies His generation as brats, impossible to please, impossible to satisfy, belligerent." There is a major difference between being childish and childlike. Here Jesus was dealing with ill-tempered malcontents They want to take their ball and go home 33. John the Baptist came with all the marks of the OT Nazarite s holy devotion to God (Num. 6:1-8; Judges 16:17; 1 Sam. 1:11-18; Luke 1:13, 15) John was your prototypical hellfire and brimstone preacher! He preached judgment. He denounced hypocritical religious leaders 3:7 The Pharisees criticized him when they should have admired him They should have sought him out as man of character and conviction They lessoned their own guilty feelings by declaring John had a demon spirit within him Their reasoning was that one would have to be demon possessed to live like a hermit. A person will go to great lengths to ignore a divine diagnosis. You can t call me a sinner and get away with it We want to discredit anyone who would expose us as frauds 34 Jesus came with a sympathetic heart that shared the common life of the people and showed the love of God through it Jesus came like a normal person. He attended weddings and funerals He associated with people like the Pharisees and lawyers (vs. 36-50) He fellowshipped with the despised publicans Unlike John the Baptist, Jesus entered into the social life of the people The strong contrasts between John and Jesus underscore the people s unjust judgment They were fickle The children refused to play either game; the Pharisees refused either messenger Because John was an eccentric hermit who shunned social integration, he was labeled a madman Because Jesus socialized with the outcasts, He was called a glutton and drunkard They claim they are rejecting the lifestyles when in truth they are rejecting the message The critics just did not want to believe MacArthur: The outward style of ministry is never the issue, but rather the truth of the message Alexander Maclaren sums up Lk 7:31-35 - The point is clear enough. If the message is unwelcome, nothing that the messenger can say or do will be right. Spurgeon: It is easy to find fault when you want to do so. This man s style is much too elaborate; he has a superabundance of the flowers of oratory. That other man is much too dull; there is nothing interesting about his discourses. This man is too coarse; he is so rough as even to be vulgar. That other man is too refined, and uses language which shoots over people s heads. It is well documented that the church today is trying to become all things to all people. Seeker Sensitive. The church for people who don t like church. You may be discouraged that no matter how you live, you are always thought wrong. J. C. Ryle: You could withdrawal from the world and you are thought exclusive, narrow-minded, sour-spirited, and righteous overmuch If, on the other hand, you go much into society, and endeavor as far as you can to take interest in your neighbor s pursuits, the remark is soon made that you are no better than other people, and have no more real religion than those who make no profession at all. The plain truth is that the natural heart of man hates God. The carnal mind is enmity against God. It dislikes His law, His Gospel, and His people. It will always find some excuse for not believing and obeying. The doctrine of repentance is too strict for it! The doctrine of faith and grace is too easy for it! John the Baptist goes too much out of the world! Jesus Christ goes too much into the world! And so the heart of man excuses itself for sitting still in its sins. All this must not surprise us. We must make up our minds to find unconverted people as perverse, unreasonable, and hard to please as the Jews of our Lord s time. We must give up the vain idea of trying to please everybody. The thing is impossible, and the attempt is mere waste of time. We must be content to walk in Christ s steps, and let the world say what it likes. Do what we will, we shall never satisfy it, or silence its ill-natured remarks. It first found fault with John the Baptist, and then with his blessed Master. And it will go on and finding fault with that Master s disciples, so long as one of them is left upon earth. (Luke 7 ) Concluding Principle 35 Yet a term of contrast A contrast of who rejected John and Jesus vs. those who receive the message Wisdom is personified; as a mother with children The point is that wisdom is vindicated (shown to be right or correct, proven to be true) by its consequences, it's "fruit," or by what it produces, in this case by the righteous fruit of the "offspring" or the followers of John and Jesus. Jesus' point is that not all who hear the Gospel message will reject it. Spurgeon: when the wisdom of God shall be all unfolded, it will be seen that the roughness of John and the gentleness and lovingkindness of Jesus were both right in their proper place. If fish are not caught in the gospel fishery, it may sometimes be the fisherman s fault, but more often, it is the fault of the fish themselves. Here we have two very different kinds of fishermen, yet neither of them attracts all, though each of them draws some. The truth of what Jesus proclaimed will become obvious to all Jesus is called the Wisdom of God (1 Cor. 1:24) We are not justified (made righteous in the sight of God) by our own wisdom or works but only by faith in the Lord Jesus A person s life and testimony will prove whether or not he had accepted Christ as Savior and Lord the wisdom of God s ways is always recognized and acknowledged by those who are wise-hearted. The multitudes saw no wisdom in the ministry of John or Jesus but there were a chosen few who did They are the ones who say God is right Wisdom is not proven by argument but over time The result of a practice proves its validity, not how loudly it is promoted. Do you deserve to be called a child of wisdom? Have the eyes of your understanding been opened? Let us not be ashamed to confess our Savior before men Let us stand on the promises of the Word of God The world may call us fools. But as the old preacher said, I may be a fool for Christ but whose fool are you? What matters in the end is not the recognition and gratitude we receive for the work we ve done, but the souls of those gentle folk in whom we ve sown the seeds of righteousness. GETTING IT RIGHT Luke 7:24-35 RIGHT BY JOHN Luke 7:24-30 RIGHT BY JESUS Luke 7:31-35 Childish Behavior Jesus characterized their opinions as childish and immature Jesus said these religious leaders were playing games with their eternal souls There is a major difference between being childish and childlike. John was your prototypical hellfire and brimstone preacher! A person will go to great lengths to ignore a divine diagnosis. Jesus came like a normal person. They claim they are rejecting the lifestyles when in truth they are rejecting the message MacArthur: The outward style of ministry is never the issue, but rather the truth of the message The plain truth is that the natural heart of man hates God Concluding Principle A contrast of who rejected John and Jesus vs. those who receive the message The point is that wisdom is vindicated by its consequences The truth of what Jesus proclaimed will become obvious to all The result of a practice proves its validity, not how loudly it is promoted. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(64).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 3/17/2023 12:34:25 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE LOVE OF A SINNER Luke 7:36-50 SIMON 7:36 Identified as such in v. 40 For whatever reason, Jesus was invited and accepted We are not told the timing; could have been after a synagogue service Customary to invite the speaker to lunch; hint, hint Could have been an evening meal and already dark Simon was a separatist Pharisee would have declined her presence Jesus ministry was to Simon; He loved him; calling him to repentance Luke 19:10 SINNER 7:37-39 a woman in the city, which was a sinner emphasis in the original A person of bad character and reputation; probably a prostitute A woman of notoriety Not invited: Simon knew who she was and was repulsed by her presence The custom of the day for such an occasion to leave the gate open so that others could benefit from the teacher s presence People would slip in; stand around the wall; listen to the conversation; at times join in; poorer folks may seek left overs She knew Jesus was there for the meal Jesus reclined at the table Indicates this is not a quick snack but a full meal Rest an elbow on a pillow with feet away from the table brought an alabaster box of ointment Carrying her essential oils Alabaster: type of marble out of Egypt Containing oil perfume used by all women but especially for her business I read where some contained as much as 8 oz Expensive to have; she was good at her business 7:38 Standing behind Jesus, she wept profusely; continuous sobbing Weeping to the extent her tears could be described as washing His feet Wesley: watered His feet with a shower of tears wiped them with her hair An act of disgrace for a woman to let her hair down in public A cause of divorce for a married woman Here it is an act of love and appreciation Kissing His feet: a demonstration of deep reverence 7:39 Simon s reaction reveals his invitation was not of genuine interest in Jesus We learned back in chapter 5 the growing hatred toward Jesus They were looking for occasions to bolster their claims against Him Simon s invitation was for the purpose to build a case against Him Now he had a situation to report For him: there is no way a true prophet would have permitted such an act A prophet would have know what kind of person she was A true man would not even talk with a woman (John 4; woman at the well) Now Simon had proof, Jesus was not a prophet Gotcha Jesus knew who the woman was And Jesus knew what was in Simon s heart SAVIOR 7:40-50 Jesus demonstrates He is indeed a prophet Jesus answered the reasoning within himself v. 39 Jesus proved a greater discernment than knowing the sins of the woman by knowing even the thoughts of Simon himself Master referring to Jesus as a teacher; nothing else 7:41, 42 a story lesson Denarii: a days wages. So one owed a year and a half the other two months Neither able to pay but in love the creditor forgave both which of them will love him the most? 7:43 Only one correct answer; the one whose debt was greater I suppose two possible reasons for response 1. Sarcasm; duh why ask a question so beneath me? 2. Reservedly: Trick question; something is up 7:44 see this woman the first time Jesus even had looked at her Of course, Simon saw her; but he really did not see her Here was a living lesson before him She was the sinner who sought and found forgiveness She was the forgiven sinner who returned to give thanks and lavish tears, kisses, and ointment on the feet of Jesus Simon was blind in the sense he had not really seen this woman standing before him Jesus is showing Simon who he really is Great love comes from great forgiveness. I entered your house emphasis on I You are bound by the obligations of a host: water, kiss, oil 7:45, 46 Simon had thought he provided a gotcha for Jesus Jesus turned the tables and had a gotcha for Simon Simon should have been a gracious host but was negligent No water, no kiss, no ointment Jesus purpose in being there was not to judge manners but to forgive sin Her gestures were not mere social courtesies but acts of repentance and worship 7:47 The greater her relief from sin, the more abundantly she loved Simon was still a debtor The woman had repented of all her sins Simon still had many sins to confess to the Lord his God 7:48 forgiven perfect passive; already had been forgiven by the creditor Her notorious past life; all those present knew her Everyone there now knew Jesus forgives even the vilest of sinners 7:49, 50 the third gotcha Jesus was making claims no prophet before Him would dare make your faith has saved you perfect active: now and forever Her faith saved her and she was still saved Her act here was an exercise of that faith Continued faith and obedience would cause her to go in peace Having been justified by faith we have peace with God (Rom. 5:1) CONCLUSION Simon simply had no category for a former prostitute whose life has been radically changed by Jesus Christ. Vance Havner: There is much shallow repentance today because men have such a shallow sense of sin. Better to be like the repentant prostitute than to be like super-religious Simon. Jesus was here because He was after Simon s heart The woman was a testimony to the power of a redeemed life Ryle: There never will be more done for Christ till there is more hearty love to Christ Himself. . . . Once let that mighty principle get hold of a man, and you will see his whole life changed. We love Him because He first loved us Your love for the Lord is directly related to your estimate of how greatly you have sinned and how much he has forgiven you. It s not how much you sin, but how deeply you feel it that matters. What we need to remember that the creditor was now assuming responsibility for the debt. The cost was eliminated but transferred When God forgave you sin, He then incurred the debt and Jesus Christ died to pay it. The debt does not go away; it still needs to be payed. THE LOVE OF A SINNER Luke 7:36-50 SIMON 7:36 Simon was a separatist Pharisee Luke 19:10 SINNER 7:37-39 A woman of notoriety Carrying her essential oils Wesley: watered his feet with a shower of tears An act of disgrace for a woman to let her hair down in public Simon s invitation was for the purpose to build a case against Him SAVIOR 7:40-50 Jesus proved a greater discernment than knowing the sins of the woman by knowing even the thoughts of Simon himself I suppose Sarcasm Reservedly Jesus is showing Simon who he really is Great love comes from great forgiveness. Jesus purpose in being there was not to judge manners but to forgive sin forgiven already had been forgiven your faith has saved you now and forever Her act here was an exercise of that faith It s not how much you sin, but how deeply you feel it that matters. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(65).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 3/25/2023 3:36:18 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE MODEL FOR MINISTRY Luke 8:1-3 INTRODUCTION 8:1-3 a general summary of Jesus ministry Jesus is making a second tour of Galilee 4:14 is where it begins; 4:31 He centers in Capernaum We are about 1 years from 4:14 Appears to be a more itinerant ministry: Capernaum no longer a focus Though opposition is growing, He is still quite popular 8:4 great multitude 8:19 crowd 8:40 multitude We are shortly after the events of Chapter 7; now it came to pass This final phase of Galilean ministry goes to 9:51 8:1-3 is a little behind the scenes look Kinda like opening the blind for a quick look The verses may not be of significant doctrinal importance but don t think for a moment there is nothing here to learn THE MINISTRY Location: every city and town here, there, everywhere Every place became His pulpit a continually wandering ministry rather than a journey from one point to another Specific locations have been noted: 5:1, 12; 7:1, 11 4:43, 44 originally description of His ministry Why Galilee? Jerusalem is the theological center, cultural center, the home of the movers and shakers Galilee was the home of the backward, rednecks, the poor This an area only spoken of with scorn If you only had a short time to get your message out, this is not where you would consecrate your efforts. The gospel came to the Jew first Jesus sent the disciples to the lost sheep of the house of Israel Mt. 10:5,6 Jesus did not travel on a whim, He was always doing the will of the Father Lecture: preaching and evangelizing; present tense, continual activity A single idea connected by and Convey a single truth that the King was on the scene Preaching: the public herald, the town crier; hear ye, hear ye to give a message bringing the glad tidings preaching the gospel, evangelizing Proclaiming the good news Lesson: The Kingdom of God That was the sum total of His message He did not get involved in politics, the headlines of the day There are at least Three aspects to the Kingdom of God Broad sense: Psalm 103:19 Internal: within you Luke 17:21 the kingdom of God is a spiritual rule over the hearts and lives of those who willingly submit to God s authority Those who defy God s authority and refuse to submit to Him are not part of the kingdom of God John 3:5-7 External: revealed Luke 17:30 The heart of every person will be revealed as to whether they served the kingdom of self or the kingdom of the Savior this future phase, also called the Messianic Age, Millennial Kingdom What is the difference between the Kingdom of God and the Kingdom of Heaven? Matthew 19:23-25 Jesus used them interchangeably The disciples question: Who then can be saved? They were entering the same place When Jesus preached the Kingdom of God, He was preaching salvation The Kingdom is where the King rules Acts 1:3 the same message after the resurrection Entrance into the kingdom is based on repentance and believing the gospel Mark 1:14, 15 THE MINISTERS the twelve Ordinary men: disappointed Him, doubted Him, deserted, denied But transformed into world changers We know one who had all the exposure yet never surrendered his life This is Discipleship 101 The mention of their presence suggests they were not always with Him Jesus was not about numbers, how big the ministry was Jesus was about the depth of His followers He focused on a few knowing multiplication would come 2 Timothy 2:2 instructions from Paul Jesus was developing a tax collector and one who was trained to kill tax collectors He had fishermen, not trained business men He collected them from the seashore; picked off some of John s followers For the most part it was a ragtime bunch of misfits That should give us great encouragement Not many wise, not many mighty, not many noble are called 1 Cor. 1:26 also women 8:2, 3 Mentioned by Mark 15:40, 41; Matthew 27:55 This is ground breaking for the culture; revolutionary This was a patriarchal world Rabbi stated 3 blessings every day: Thank you, you did not make me a Gentile, a woman, or an unrefined person Rabbis refused to teach women; had their own place in the temple; Luke has mentioned several: Elizabeth, Mary, Anna, sinful woman More to come: Martha and Mary, crippled woman, woman with lost coin, Woman and the judge These women healed of evil spirits and illnesses Dr. Luke: had been healed was healed and continue to be healed These women all had personal grounds of gratitude to Jesus 7:41-43 those forgiven much, love much; the same for healed from much Evil spirits is synonymous with demons Evil: active evil, evil with an intent to do harm; Satan is called the evil one Interesting to note: there is no record of a woman in Scripture being hostile to Jesus Jesus was content to be supported this way it is more blessed to give than receive These women are walking examples of Eph. 2:10 created unto good works Mary who was called Magdalene; (from Magdala) Previous indwelled by 7 demons; whether the number of complete possession We can only imagine what she looked like in such a condition She was at the cross, saw where Jesus was buried, first to know of the resurrection and tell the disciples She is often associated with harlotry but there is no mention of that in Scripture She has been linked to the woman taken in adultery; John 8 Some linked her to being married to Jesus; no basis There is no hint that the relationship of Jesus with these women was anything but pure and holy. Joanna the wife of Chuza (khood-zas ) Chuza: steward of Herod; possible over his finances, household manager A very trusted and important official No mention of his spiritual condition, but must have approved of his wife s activity Joanna is mentioned at the resurrection 24:10 Susanna: totally unknown on earth but not in heaven O Susanna many others These women were loyal to the end Luke 23:49 , 55-56 ; Lk 24:1 ; cf. Acts 1:14 There is a place for everyone who is willing to be used by God There was something for the 12 to do; something for the women to do One had been demon possessed; one had access to wealth Spurgeon: In that last great day when the rewards are distributed, there will be as much for Joanna as for John, and as much for Mary Magdalene as for Simon Peter! They ministered of their substance True love will count it a pleasure to give anything to the object loved. False love will often talk and profess much, but do and give nothing at all THE MODEL FOR MINISTRY Luke 8:1-3 THE MINISTRY Location: every city and town This an area only spoken of with scorn Lecture: preaching and evangelizing Lesson: The Kingdom of God Three aspects to the Kingdom of God Broad sense: Psalm 103:19 Internal: within you Luke 17:21 External: revealed Luke 17:30 Matthew 19:23-25 The Kingdom is where the King rules THE MINISTERS The Twelve Discipleship 101 Jesus was about the depth of His followers also women 8:2, 3 This was a patriarchal world These women all had personal grounds of gratitude to Jesus There is no hint that the relationship of Jesus with these women was anything but pure and holy. These women were loyal to the end Luke 23:49 , 55-56 ; 24:1 ; Acts 1:14 There is a place for everyone who is willing to be used by God True love will count it a pleasure to give anything to the object loved. False love will often talk and profess much, but do and give nothing at all. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(66).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/1/2023 12:52:16 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE RESPONSE TO REVELATION Luke 4:4-21 PARABLE OF THE SOILS Luke is developing a theology of the Word of God and a response to it Luke 7:18-35 is the revelation of who Jesus is 8:4-15 Various reactions to Jesus message 8:16-18 The message is compared to light 8:19-21 Commendation to a positive response It all is a Call to Faith Cf. Mark 4:1; Matthew 13:1 location by the sea; teaches from a boat The crowd was continuing to grow in size Parable: placing one thing beside another for the purpose of comparison Luke has cited others: Lk 5:36 39 ; Lk 6:39 , 41 44 , 47 49 ; Lk 7:41 42 . Parable in Luke - Lk. 4:23 ; Lk. 5:36 ; Lk. 6:39 ; Lk. 8:4 ; Lk. 8:9 ; Lk. 8:10 ; Lk. 8:11 ; Lk. 12:16 ; Lk. 12:41 ; Lk. 13:6 ; Lk. 14:7 ; Lk. 15:3 ; Lk. 18:1 ; Lk. 18:9 ; Lk. 19:11 ; Lk. 20:9 ; Lk. 20:19 ; Lk. 21:29 ; Parables are simple stories to teach spiritual truth A true-to-life story to teach one main lesson There may be many parts and characters but each is intended to teach a single idea Truth is taught through comparison or contrast The picture is something that is readily recognized by the hearer Told in such a way that invites the hearer to inquire more Steve Cole: Parables ..reveal truth to those who are spiritually responsive; and they conceal truth from those who are spiritually superficial or scoffing. this marks a turning point in Jesus ministry Matthew 13:34 "From this time on He spoke to them only in parables." 1 Corinthians 2:14 What Jesus did was put a story alongside a spiritual truth to make it better understood. Our parable in Luke 8:4-15 is commonly known as the Parable of the Sower We learned it early on in Sunday School But as we see today, it is more accurate to label it the Parable of the Soils The parable puts the responsibility on the hearer Jesus is attempting to gain the attention of those followers who have consistently been on the fringe. They have hung around, fascinated by what they hear and especially see, but will not make a commitment They could also be part of the crowd who Jesus addressed back in 7:18-35 Those who criticized the style Or those who attended Simon s meal and criticized Jesus who had forgiven the sin s of harlot Note the statement in 8:8 he who has ears to hear, let him hear His call to those who have faith to hear Repeated: (Lk 8:8 , 10 , 12 , 13 , 14 , 15 , 21 , 50 ) Now, remember Jesus had at one time spoken very plainly 4:16-30; speaking in the synagogue of Nazareth They knew plainly His claim to be the Messiah and sought to throw Him off the cliff at the edge of town THE FOUR SOILS A different approach to sowing: broadcasting Broadcast: to send something out; whether seed or the airways The manner of sowing landed seeds of different kinds of soil Wayside: a narrow pathway separated different fields or farms Earth was packed hard by constant foot traffic This seed had little chance for growth Birds ate the seeds before they could even sprout It was on such a path Jesus and disciples walked plucking grain on a Sabbath; Luke 6:1 Rocky ground: a thin layer of soil covering a ledge of rock Seeds could sprout quickly, wither and die Thorns: soil containing weeds or roots Weeds grew with the good seeds but were stronger and overcame the delicate young plants Good, fertile ground: soil which would receive and nourish the seeds These responses are not about any given moment but a lifetime of exposure Only over time do the pleasures of life erode the seed s effectiveness Jesus is interested in more than the physical act of hearing He desires those who hear with their heart Those who have not closed their spiritual ears would be the ones to enter the kingdom of God THE FOUR RESPONSES 8:9-15 Deciphering for the Disciples The disciples know this was not a lesson for the FFA or 4 H Mark 4:10 when He was alone as soon as the crowd dispersed It is easy for the disciples or us to get discouraged, thinking our efforts to share the gospel are in vain This parable tells us not to become disheartened 1 Corinthians 3:6-8 Parables gave all Jesus hearers an external acquaintance with His teaching But not all would fully understand the spiritual truths He presented He said His disciples would know the mysteries of the kingdom of God The facts or truths revealed only to the initiated Jesus quotes Isaiah 6:9, 10 God foresaw Israel s rejection of Isaiah s message Spurgeon calls this a time of judicial visitation Since they would not hear, they cannot hear This is a judicial act; a judgment on Israel The reward of virtue is the capacity for higher virtue The effect of vice is the tendency to yet greater vice Purpose of Parables: Matthew 13:11, 12 Romans 1 uses the phrase: God gave them over 2 Thessalonians 2:9-12 Parables clarify nothing for those who choose to reject truth They just do not have the spiritual power to grasp the truth presented to them Unless a person allows the Holy Spirit to anoint his understanding, the things of God are foolishness to him 1 Corinthians 2:7-14 Luke s account emphasizes the seed and the soils; not the sower The seed is the word of God; it is good Whether or not it bears fruit depends on the type of soil into which it falls THE RESPONSE TO REVELATION Luke 4:4-21 Call to Faith PARABLE OF THE SOILS Parable: placing one thing beside another for the purpose of comparison A true-to-life story to teach one main lesson Matthew 13:34 THE FOUR SOILS Wayside: a narrow pathway separated different fields Rocky ground: a thin layer of soil covering a ledge of rock Thorns: soil containing weeds or roots Good, fertile ground THE FOUR RESPONSES 8:9-15 1 Corinthians 3:6-8 Isaiah 6:9, 10 This is a judicial act Purpose of Parables: Matthew 13:11, 12 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(67).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/8/2023 1:15:32 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message Could you include the chart as a slide? I can make it a separate email if needed A PROMISE AND PLEDGE 1 Corinthians 15:20 INTRODUCTION If you think back through human history there are events that we would mark as monumental. OT: Israel s deliverance from Egypt, the Davidic dynasty, the Babylonian captivity and restoration to the land NT: the Jerusalem council in Acts 15, salvation is by grace not observing the Law The Council of Nicaea, the affirmation of the deity of Christ; Very God The Reformation: Constantine made Christianity the religion of the Holy Roman Empire Every one signed up and Christianity became swallowed up in Roman Catholicism The Reformation came: the just shall life by faith C.F. Evans, a contemporary writer, has said, To a greater extent than it is anything else, Christianity is a religion of the resurrection. Opposition to the physical resurrection of the body of Jesus has always been the case We know the guarding soldiers were paid to lie: Matthew 28:11-15 Acts 17; Paul at Mars Hill; 17:18, 32 Body resurrection was not part of the Greek philosophical religious system They did believe in the immortality of the soul Plato: the human body is a prison and man should long to escape that prison Celsus in 220 AD said, To believe in a physical resurrection is to have the hope of worms. It is important for us to know the theological arguments for the resurrection Why? Because The doctrine of the resurrection impacts other areas of your system of theology Erich Sauer, a great theologian, said this, quote, The present age is Easter time. It begins with the resurrection of the Redeemer and ends with the resurrection of the redeemed. Between lies the spiritual resurrection of those called into life through Christ. So, we live between two Easters. And in the power of the first Easter, we go to meet the last Easter. From verses 12-19, Paul had been looking at life if there was no resurrection He goes from if to but now He goes from a position of denying the resurrection to one of reality The look now is some positive truths associated with the resurrection but now a change from argument against a preposterous idea to triumphant vindication of facts." (Vine) Christ has been raised - He rose triumphant and He remains triumphant first fruits the first of a great harvest In ancient Israel, the first fruits referred to the first installment of the crop which foreshadowed and in effect pledged the ultimate offering of the whole harvest to Yahweh. History of First Fruits: Leviticus 23:10,11 Utley says "The first fruits in the OT were ripened sheaves of the barley harvest waved before the Lord in the Temple the day after the High Holy Sabbath of Passover Week, which would be Resurrection Sunday. They were given to show God s ownership of the entire crop. Should you look at a calendar of Jewish feasts, this is what you find In the first month of the Jewish calendar, Nisan (nee-sawn') you find three celebrations Passover, Unleavened Bread, First Fruits Passover was on a Friday; Unleavened Bread began on the next day, Sabbath The First Fruits Offering was the day after Sabbath; our Sunday So it would be perfectly proper to refer to this day as First Fruits Sunday Happy Feast of First Fruits would be our greeting That would elevate the conversation instead of coloring eggs and eating the ears off of chocolate bunnies This is an OT type for the promise of the resurrection of ALL Christ followers!" It is not that Jesus would precede that of His people, but as the first sheaf of the harvest presented to God as a thank-offering, was the pledge and assurance of the ingathering of the whole harvest, so the resurrection of Christ is a pledge and proof of the resurrection of his people. What a shift from the gloomy outcome of the previous verses: If Christ was not raised preaching is vain, your faith is vain, ye are still in your sins, the dead in Christ have perished, we are more miserable than other men. Romans 8:23 The first fruits is the first in a long series; there will be more Jesus was not raised in isolation Thomas Watson: We are more sure to arise out of our graves than out of our beds. All that are united to Christ by faith, are by His resurrection assured of their own The whole system of Christianity rests on this doctrine: Christ rose from the dead If Christ were not raised, it would be proper to question His deity Romans 1:4 If Christ were not raised, it would be proper to question His sovereignty Romans 14:9 Our justification is linked to His resurrection Romans 4:25 Our living hope is linked to His resurrection 1 Peter 1:3 Our resurrection is dependent on His Romans 8:11 Spurgeon: the silver thread of resurrection runs through all the believer s blessings, from his regeneration onwards to his eternal glory, and binds them together. those who are asleep all believers in Jesus Christ who have died In a Christian context sleep means death; a beautiful picture for the Christian Not soul sleep but the picture of the body in the ground sleeping until awakened Asleep: to lie outstretched Cemetery: sleeping chamber They sleep with the anticipation of waking up Death for a Christian is holy triumphant: 2 Corinthians 5:8- Paul has given us a wonderful picture here in the illustration of First Fruits Jesus, as the first fruits of the resurrection, guarantees the resurrection of all believers The first of the harvest is the pledge that there will be more of a harvest Jesus was raised; continues to be raised, so all believers share in that hope They also will have new, glorious, eternal resurrected bodies Acts 24:14, 15 Paul s testimony before Felix and Festus Two Questions But what about . . . How can Christ be considered the first fruits what about others have been raised before Him? Luke 7, young man from Nain; Luke 8; Jairus daughter; John 11, Lazarus Simple answer: these were raised to die again; continue life on earth Christ was the first to rise in power and glory; Jesus will never die again Firstborn from the dead Colossians 1:18; James 1:18; Revelation 1:5 Not first in number but first as the best, the greatest Matthew Henry: Would we triumph in that solemn and important season, we must now submit to his rule, accept his salvation, and live to his glory. Then shall we rejoice in the completion of his undertaking, that God may receive the whole glory of our salvation, that we may forever serve him, and enjoy his favor. This is the gospel: 15:3, 4 On this Resurrection Sunday as you sit here this morning, can you say with confidence based on the authority of the Scriptures that you will be raised with Jesus to live with Him for all eternity? Are you confident that your sins have been A PROMISE AND PLEDGE 1 Corinthians 15:20 To a greater extent than it is anything else, Christianity is a religion of the resurrection. The doctrine of the resurrection impacts other areas of your system of theology. - C. F. Evans Christ has been raised - He rose triumphant and He remains triumphant History of First Fruits: Leviticus 23:10,11 Passover Friday Unleavened Bread the Sabbath First Fruits Offering day after Sabbath The resurrection of Christ is a pledge and proof of the resurrection of his people Romans 8:23 Thomas Watson: We are more sure to arise out of our graves than out of our beds. The whole system of Christianity rests on this doctrine His deity Romans 1:4 His Sovereignty Romans 14:9 Our Justification Romans 4:25 Living Hope 1 Peter 1:3 Our resurrection Romans 8:11 Sleep means death Asleep: to lie outstretched Cemetery: sleeping chamber Two Questions What about others have been raised before Him? these were raised to die again What about the phraise firstborn from the dead? Colossians 1:18; James 1:18; Revelation 1:5 Not first in number but first as the best Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(68).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/11/2023 7:37:14 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE THE FOUR RESPONSES 8:9-15 Deciphering for the Disciples The disciples know this was not a lesson for the FFA or 4 H Mark 4:10 when He was alone as soon as the crowd dispersed It is easy for the disciples or us to get discouraged, thinking our efforts to share the gospel are in vain This parable tells us not to become disheartened 1 Corinthians 3:6-8 Parables gave all Jesus hearers an external acquaintance with His teaching But not all would fully understand the spiritual truths He presented He said His disciples would know the mysteries of the kingdom of God The facts or truths revealed only to the initiated Jesus quotes Isaiah 6:9, 10 God foresaw Israel s rejection of Isaiah s message Spurgeon calls this a time of judicial visitation Since they would not hear, they cannot hear This is a judicial act; a judgment on Israel The reward of virtue is the capacity for higher virtue The effect of vice is the tendency to yet greater vice Purpose of Parables: Matthew 13:11, 12 Romans 1 uses the phrase: God gave them over 2 Thessalonians 2:9-12 Parables clarify nothing for those who choose to reject truth They just do not have the spiritual power to grasp the truth presented to them Unless a person allows the Holy Spirit to anoint his understanding, the things of God are foolishness to him 1 Corinthians 2:7-14 Luke s account emphasizes the seed and the soils; not the sower The seed is the word of God; it is good Whether or not it bears fruit depends on the type of soil into which it falls Person on the Path The message is heard: The message is seemingly wasted Their heart is like concrete The devil comes and takes the Word out of their hearts We must always remember spiritual warfare for the soul of man Note: the devil does not interfere the hearing; only the believing And although the devil certainly has a role in picking off the seed, the real problem is still man s heart. Once the seed penetrates the soil, it is much harder to pick it out. A hard heart does the devil s work for him The greatest difficulty in conversion, is to win the heart to God; and the greatest difficulty after conversion, is to keep the heart with God. How is the seed snatched? The fear of man, pride, doubt, stubbornness, love of sin Or simply get caught up in the mundane things of life and deep thoughts about God disappear. The problem is not the sower or the seed, but the heart Hebrews 3:13 exhort one another daily . . lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin And that is talking to believers Sin sears the conscience and hardens the heart so one cannot and will not listen Each one is accountable to God for the truth he hears; Romans 14:12 Each one must give an account of himself to God Each person must cultivate his own heart Person with Rocky Soil Rocky soil produces quick, shallow faith; not permanent An emotional response (hear with joy); not one of conviction Awakened but not regenerated The response from high pressure evangelism Phillips: enough soil to give promise but not enough to give performance A plant quickly rises, but quickly dies off It is not that there rocks in the field, they could be removed, but that there was a thin area of soil on top of a layer of limestone who believe for a while intellectual, superficial faith; not saving faith Those who are forgiven much, love much. These folks never got there Persecution or seductive doctrine turns people away Such people live on their feelings rather than on their convictions The promise that life will be good never materialized Person on Thorny Ground Trying to place seed in soil already occupied by weeds The weeds would use up all the moisture and nutrients The farmer may have tried to pull out the weeds but some roots remained As explained: the cares, riches, and pleasures of this life that he cannot be totally committed to Christ Life gets in the way. Distractions, worries choke out God s Word Pleasures hedonism; wrong priorities He is a double-minded man; pre-occupied unwilling to separate from the world s system Mark 4:19 the worries of the world; the deceitfulness of riches; the desires of other things They profess Christ, but it is business as usual There is the appearance of fruit, but it does not mature TRANSITION The listener is responsible Hard-packed soil can be plowed and made ready for planting Rocks can be removed from stony soil Thornes and weeds can be pulled out by the roots Person on Good Soil makes full use of the seed of God s Word The parallel passages amplify the meaning Matthew 13:23 says hears and understands He digs into the Word; meditates on it, finds its true meaning The good hearer receives the Word and it becomes a part of him Mark 4:20 bears fruit, some 30 fold, 60 fold, 90 fold Luke characterizes the good-ground person as one who has an honest and good heart Honest: beauty Good: nobility or unrightness Brings forth fruit with patience: endurance No plant grows to maturity overnight; neither does a Christian The proof of salvation is in the fruit; Luke 6:43-49 SUMMARY Even Jesus had superficial responses to His message Jn 8:30 , 43 , 45 , 58 ,59 all the same group of Jews The parable does not provide percentages: as in only 1 in 4 of the people you share the gospel with will accept the message It does tell us that the number will be few There will be those who make a profession of faith but are not saved I do believe in the perseverance of the saints THE RESPONSE TO REVELATION Luke 8:4-21 THE FOUR SOILS Wayside: a narrow pathway separated different fields Rocky ground: a thin layer of soil covering a ledge of rock Thorns: soil containing weeds or roots Good, fertile ground THE FOUR RESPONSES 8:9-15 1 Corinthians 3:6-8 Isaiah 6:9, 10 This is a judicial act Purpose of Parables: Matthew 13:11, 12 Person on the Path Their heart is like concrete The fear of man, pride, doubt, stubbornness, love of sin Hebrews 3:13 Person with Rocky Soil Awakened but not regenerated Person on Thorny Ground Pleasures hedonism Mark 4:19 They profess Christ, but it is business as usual Person on Good Soil Matthew 13:23 hears and understands Mark 4:20 No plant grows to maturity overnight The parable does not provide percentages Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(69).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/22/2023 10:45:42 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message GOT YOUR EARS ON? Luke 8:16-18 Cf .Matt. 5:15, 16; Mark 4:21-25 INTRODUCTION How good of a listener are you? You re the engineer of a train. There are 36 people on board. At the first stop, 10 get off and 2 get on. At the next stop, no one gets off, but 5 get on. At the third stop, 4 get off and 2 get on. Now for the question: What is the name of the engineer? One husband dropped his newspaper, looked directly into his wife s eyes, and gave her his full attention while she was speaking. Stop it, she snapped. You re deliberately listening just to confuse me. Years ago truckers would ask: heh, good buddy, you got your ears on? I m all ears Listen up Lend me your ears I m talking to you! LISTEN AND SEE Not all lamps are created equal. In this case, we need to be careful in our interpretation of Scripture to take the concept of a lamp and understand all Bible usages as the same. In other words, how consistent are we to be? So, some caution is in order: we must allow the context to determine our interpretation. Who is Jesus speaking to? The passage began by Him speaking to the multitudes; 8:4 8:9 Jesus is addressing the disciples; Mark 4, a separation had happened Among the disciples were the 12 and others who came under the definition as a learner Some were beginning to believer; others were followers 8:16 is a duh! self-evident The light here is a bowl of oil with a wick Candles were not yet invented You don t take a light and put it under something to prevent the light from shining You place the light where it can be most useful A simple picture: 11:33; Matt. 5:16; Mark 4:21 What does the light represent? Light reveals what we cannot otherwise see The Word of God reveals truth about God we could not otherwise see The Gospel reveals the way into the kingdom we could not see The Word of God brings to light what is truly in men s hearts When a disciple hears the Word of God, he does not hide it from others nor does he seek to hide from it himself It is to be proclaimed to those in darkness It requires a response to the hidden areas of a man s soul Why speak of it now in the context? Although Jesus was speaking in parables, it does not mean the message was only for the elites or should be kept secret The message should still be displayed for the benefit of others Psalm 119:105 This gives us more light to what Jesus said earlier: 8:10 The parables were to reveal truth to the spiritually responsive and to conceal truth from the spiritually superficial The main purpose was not to conceal truth The main purpose of His message was to illumine truth, not hide it Light serves two functions: illumine and expose Jesus teaching not only brought light through the truth but also exposes the evil that lurks in the dark corners of a man s heart 8:17 Nothing will be concealed on the day of judgment 12:2, 3 (Ecc. 12:14; 1 Cor. 4:5) The revelation of a man s heart will be revealed by the lack of fruit and the unwillingness to come to the light Men loved darkness rather than light John 3:19, 20 God desires all to be saved. The Seed is sown to all; all are without excuse But that same Seed will be used to expose all that is hidden in the human heart And that warning is not just for the ungodly Aside: all sins of a believer have been exposed at the cross of Jesus But while living on this earth: There are saved people who will only take God so far and protect the dark corners of their life Rather than deal with the foolishness exposed, they turn off the light The Puritan pastor, Thomas Watson, said concerning the Scriptures, Take every word as spoken to yourselves. When the word thunders against sin, think thus: God means my sins; when it presses any duty, God intends me in this. Many put off Scripture from themselves, as if it only concerned those who lived in the time when it was written; but if you intend to profit by the word, bring it home to yourselves: a medicine will do no good, unless it be applied Sooner or later all will be exposed Even that person who made the profession of faith received with joy bring no fruit to maturity The reality of his heart will be known. Was his salvation experience genuine? Where is the fruit? Where is the light? So, a self-examination is in order LISTEN AND LEARN 8:18 therefore Be careful how you listen (present tense; to be a lifestyle) There was a day where listening was the means of learning Printing not yet invented and reading was for a few Your hearing is just as important as my speaking Criticize the preaching if you want, but have you ever criticized your hearing? The New Living Translation paraphrases it in a way we can better see what Jesus is saying - "So pay attention to how you hear. To those who listen to My teaching, more understanding will be given. But for those who are not listening, even what they think they understand will be taken away from them." You have heard the phrase: Use it or lose it A positive response; a learning response; a growing response; an obedient response Luke 6:46-49 We don t shrink back because it exposes some sin in our life If we respond to the light, we receive more light If we shrink back, what light we have will be taken away The truth is revealed in Jesus We must listen very carefully to that truth because that very truth will someday be used to judge us I hear and now I am accountable So, first Jesus compares sowing the seed of the Word and now uses light as synonymous with that same Word In one, the seed is the Word; the other, the seed is the light 1:78, 79; Jesus ministry is compared to light Next week, we will see the duty in response to the revelation But here, the light we receive must shine J. C. Ryle: The highest form of selfishness is that of the man who is content to go to heaven alone. The truest charity is to endeavor to share with others every spark of religious light we possess ourselves Spurgeon - True religion and true doctrine are not intended to be concealed, they are meant to be seen, and if any of you are hiding these blessed things away, I pray you to do so no longer. Bring out your candle, and put it on the candlestick, that they which enter in may see the light. LISTEN AND GROW How do you know if you are hearing the Word of God the way you should? Evangelistically: have you received Jesus as your Lord and Savior? That is where it begins All teaching is of an evangelistic purpose until one is saved Authentically: the fallow ground of your heart has been tilled to receive and nurture the Word of God. The soil of your heart is not hard, shallow, or worldly. Fruitfully: the evidence of salvation is clear as mature fruit is produced in your life. Fruit is two things: Attitudes and Actions Attitudes: the fruit of the Spirit; Galatians 5 Actions: your light shines before men Ryle: The Gospel which we possess was not given us only to be admired, talked of, and professed, but to be practiced. Are you listening? From the earlier parable: all four soils heard Listen with close attention: it is God s Word not man s Listen with deep seriousness: it speaks to important subjects Listen with prayer: Holy Spirit inspired; Holy Spirit illuminated Listen with faith: Faith comes by hearing Listen for yourself: as if you were the only one to hear Listen like the Bereans: search it out Heed the warning: Today, if you hear His voice, harden not your heart GOT YOUR EARS ON? Luke 8:16-18 LISTEN AND SEE Light reveals what we cannot otherwise see Light serves two functions: illumine and expose Nothing will be concealed on the day of judgment LISTEN AND LEARN Be careful how you listen Luke 6:46-49 I hear and now I am accountable LISTEN AND GROW Evangelistically Authentically Fruitfully Listen with close attention Listen with deep seriousness Listen with prayer Listen with faith Listen for yourself Listen like the Bereans Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(70).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 4/28/2023 2:42:28 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message ARE YOU A REALITVE OF JESUS? Luke 8:19-21 INTRODUCTION Section began at 8:4; titled Response the Revelation Parable of the Four Soils; Seed is the Word of God; soil is the heart Hard Shallow Worldly Good Each type of soil heard Parable of the Light Revelation was not intended to be kept hidden Jesus had judicial prerogative to withhold ability to understand Therefore, be careful how you hear Possible to lose what a person once had What now turn to what you do after you hear 8:19-21 Hear Obediently FAMILY CONCERNED 8:19 Matthew 12:46-50 and Mark 3:31-35 both have this encounter before the Parable of the Soils Luke places it hear because it fits his purpose The family had a growing concern over what they had been hearing Mark 3:20-22 The friction with the Jewish leaders was increasing He did not take time to eat He is demon possessed His family had come to the conclusion He was mad, crazy Luke 8:19 His mother and brothers: Mark 6:3 we have names: James, Joseph, Judas, Simon Matthew 13:56 sisters are mentioned but not named Such a comment should put to rest any questions about Jesus family Mary was not a perpetual virgin; there were other children The brothers are not from a previous marriage of Joseph Can you imagine how old Joseph would have been when he married Mary if he had all these other children? The additional children are not mentioned in the travel to Bethlehem, to Egypt, or to Jerusalem when Jesus was 12 The word for brother is not cousin; as some suppose The Greek has a word for cousin (1:36); this is not it The assumption is Joseph is dead; most certainly by the time of the cross When common sense makes perfect sense, don t look for some other sense Matthew 10:36 Jesus said that your enemy will come from your own household John 7:5 not even His brothers believed on Him FAMILY CORRECTED 8:20 The physical family of Jesus arrives Believing they walked from Nazareth to Capernaum; 30 miles Mark 3:21 to lay hold of Him to seize Him; to use strength Used to take one by the hand, to arrest someone Rev 20:2 He seized the dragon, the old serpent, which is the devil and Satan, who deceives the whole inhabited earth, and bound him for a thousand years, In other words, they were here to do business Were they interested in Jesus or protecting the family name? Was Mary really part of this or coerced to come along? You know the circumstances of the Jesus birth were still part of her Mary had already professed her need of a Savior These are my family Spiritual ties supersede physical ties Luke 2:48-50 His call was to be in His Father s house FAMILY COMMISSIONED 8:21 hear and do Be careful how you hear Hearing must be linked to obedience Jesus came unto His own and His own received Him not But most of them were not of relation as they were disobedient A hearer that does not do has not really heard This is the mark of a true disciple They hear the call to repent and be converted and they do it He puts off the old man and puts on the new man 1 Peter 1:14, 15 as obedient children Galatians 5:16 Hebrews 12:1 Obedience takes precedence over natural relationships Allegiance to God always trumps allegiance to one s family Hearing well leads to increased intimacy with Christ John 14:21 manifest disclose, reveal, make myself known Luke 9:59-61; 14:26 Luke 11:27, 28 Doing does not save us; doing is proof we are saved Acts 1:14 His mother and brothers are with the disciples Galatians 119 James is cited as Jesus brother Hearing and doing Luke 6:47-49 build on a solid foundation James 1:22-25 jumps all over hearing and doing This should be warning to the comfortable and complacent And an encouragement to the dejected Is your good impression a reflection of a good profession? No matter the family, the spiritual tie takes precedence over the physical I audited on class in college; sat in, took notes, but no test Many people audit church. They sit, take up space, take notes, ask questions, and seemed engaged but fail to ever do what God requires Jesus is not denying His physical family, but there is something deeper, higher, and more permanent than any blood relationship. Watch what we hear What how we hear We obey because we can, not because we must We obey because of grace, not law Has God impressed on you the need to Seek forgiveness of someone To confess your own sin to God or someone You should apologize To speak the truth no matter the consequences To discontinue a certain practice You should make a gift You should witness to someone You are to step out and trust God s call upon your life Romans 8:13-17 Are you a relative of Jesus? ARE YOU A REALITVE OF JESUS? Luke 8:19-21 FAMILY CONCERNED 8:19 Mark 3:20-22 Mark 6:3 Matthew 13:56 Mary was not a perpetual virgin The brothers are not from a previous marriage of Joseph The word for brother is not cousin John 7:5 FAMILY CORRECTED 8:20 Mark 3:21 to lay hold of Him Spiritual ties supersede physical ties FAMILY COMMISSIONED 8:21 Hearing must be linked to obedience 1 Peter 1:14, 15 Galatians 5:16 Hebrews 12:1 Hearing well leads to increased intimacy with Christ John 14:21 Watch what we hear What how we hear We obey because we can, not because we must We obey because of grace, not law Romans 8:13-17 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(71).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 5/6/2023 10:06:41 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 INTRODUCTION What an exciting place we are in our study of the gospel of Luke. His intent was to provide Theophilus an eyewitness account of the earthly life of the Lord Jesus; to provide an accurate account of His teachings and miracles. Luke s intent was not to stick to a strict chronological rendering but to weave together a story to provide a full picture of who Jesus really is. The text has been divided up for us into chapters and we are transitioning in chapter to 8 to properly understanding what we hear to what we see. Beginning in verse 4, we are told be careful how you hear the revelation of the Word of God. There are four types of hearts in people as they respond. The hearts are compared to soil: some as hard as concrete, some very shallow, others wrapped up the things of this world, and others will recipients. We have also been exhorted to not hide the revelation but to let it shine forth. And lastly, we saw that if you desire to be in a relationship with Jesus, you must be a doer of the Word and not a hearer only. Now, a change is coming. From what you hear to what you see. There are 4 miracles in the remaining verses of the chapter. Calming the storm, dealing with demons, disease and death We move from the power of the words of Jesus to the power of His person. The people will now be challenged to understand the authority Jesus has over various elements of this world; power over nature, the spirit world, illness, and death itself. The reader is to arrive at the conclusion that as only God can do these things, Jesus is God. Please note the reactions: vs. 25, 37, 47, 56 That should be the reaction when in the presence of God The reaction that you are not worthy to be in such a presence The same as Peter when fishing with Jesus: 5:8 As we see the power of Jesus in these miracles, keep in mind the presence of the disciples. Not all who call themselves disciples are believers; be definition they are simply learners. Only a smaller number are in a relationship with Jesus. We can expect their education be something like ours. Once a certain amount of material is presented in a classroom, what typically comes next? TEST Any student needs to know what he knows; here s your sign For the disciples, there is one more T that needs to be crossed: TRUST They have received excellent teaching; the best Here is their TEST to expose the debt of their learning Do they really TRUST their Teacher. THREE THINGS TO KNOW ABOUT STORMS Storms can hit suddenly and without warning We can easily say to another: See you later I ll be back What starts out as just another day can become a huge hearbreak in a matter of moments Storms hit believers Christians are not exempt Mark 4 tells us there were other boats; those who believe in whiffle dust would say that the other boats were tossed to and fro but the boat Jesus was in maintained calm sailing. Storms hit obedient believers actively serving Christ These disciples were in the center of God s will They were led straight into a major storm. SAILING WITH THE MASTER 8:22-26 The Moment of the Storm Could not be Predicted The Sea of Galilee, we are told, is like a bowl some 700 feet below sea Level surrounded by hills. Cold air coming down mixes with the warm air from the water and violent storms can suddenly spring up A calm beginning does not guarantee a smooth trip One moment you can be enjoying the blue skies of life and the next moment find yourself in the biggest storm of you life No Partiality There was no favoritism to the disciples just because Jesus was in their boat Any thought we might have of smooth sailing because of our personal, intimate, growing relationship with Christ is a myth We are not exempt Jesus could have kept them from the storm; rather He took them right into it Don t fall for the lie that every major storm in your life was sent straight from hell, as if God has not part in it. God does not need to be taken off the hook. We are prone to say, God did not cause this trial, He only allowed it A trial does not mean you are disobedient and need to brought back into line. It was obedience that put the disciples in the storm Isaiah 45:6, 7 I am the Lord, and there is no other, the One forming light and creating darkness, causing well-being and creating calamity; I am the Lord who does all these God is Sovereign. He schedules the storms Psalm 119:67, 71, 75, 92 The Misery of the Storm Matthew 8:24 great storm Mark 4:37 fierce gale of wind The Storm was Severe It was a mega storm; a whirlwind from the 4 corners of the world Ferocious, violent Exceeded all past experiences of former fishermen The person who says, I didn t know it could be this bad I didn t know I could hurt this much The Struggle in the Storm Filling up with water; in serious danger; they were swamped This was not just another storm They were about to learn what they are to do when they have no where else to turn Their resources are no longer sufficient The hope of survival was quickly departing Fear, frustration, failure; we have been there Where do you turn? Supplication in the Storm They came to Him Storms have a way of driving us back to God They were fearful because they depended on their own resources Folks, you are either in a storm or headed for a storm God should not be your last resort, but the first place you go Master over the Storm Mark 4:38 Jesus was in the hinder part of the ship That is where the captain stayed He was there all the time Does Jesus care? Of yes, He cares. I know He cares Note: it was not the storm that woke Jesus, but the cry of His followers Power over the Storm Mark 4:39 peace, be still literally be muzzled There was an instant calming; supernatural Storm do not subside like that; they settle into a calm state Dr. Jerry Vines, The winds fell as if they were shot with a tranquilizing dart. The sea turned into a mirror of glass. Presence in the Storm Whether He is standing or sleeping, He is present in your storm The Promise in the Storm Let s go to the other side They arrived The boat could not sink because Jesus was on board The storm will no last forever Folks, you are going over, no under Safety is not found in the absence of a storm, but in the presence of the Savior Sometimes Jesus will tell the storm Be Still Other times, He will tell the saint, Be Still Sometimes, Jesus will change the situation Other times, Jesus will change the saint Sometimes, Jesus will move the mountain Other times, Jesus will help you walk over the mountain WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 SAILING WITH THE MASTER 8:22-26 THREE THINGS TO KNOW ABOUT STORMS Storms can hit suddenly and without warning Storms hit believers Storms hit obedient believers actively serving Christ THE MOMENT OF THE STORM Could not be Predicted No Partiality Psalm 119:67, 71, 75, 92 THE MISERY OF THE STORM The Storm was Severe The Struggle in the Storm Supplication in the Storm THE MASTER OVER THE STORM Power over the Storm Mark 4:39 peace, be still literally be muzzled Presence in the Storm The Promise in the Storm The boat could not sink because Jesus was on board The storm will not last forever Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(72).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 5/13/2023 12:33:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 INTRODUCTION We are presently looking in Luke 8 at a series of miracles answering the question asked in v. 25. NKJV reads who can this be? other texts: who is this? If you remember from last week, each miracle had the same result: fear. v. 25, 37, 47, 56 it is the result of knowing you are in the presence of deity; 5:8 Miracle one demonstrated Jesus authority, power over the storm Jesus demonstrated His authority over the natural elements of His creation His command brought about an instant calming of a storm that was about to swamp the boats making their way across the Sea of Galilee. Experienced fishermen believed they were doomed; v. 24 Last week s message: Sailing with the Master This week: Speaking for the Master MacArthur: The Maniac Who Became A Missionary Jesus focus is no longer on the physical world but the supernatural one He has calmed a tempestuous sea and now will heal a tormented soul It is one thing to tame a wild sea; what about a wild man Luke has already recorded Jesus victory over Satan; Luke 4 Providing for us the assurance that we can trust Jesus to save us from the realm of darkness Since we are called upon to trust Jesus for salvation, how do we know He will be able to deliver? Luke 4 tells us. And furthermore, Luke 4:31-37 describes an encounter with a demon during a synagogue service. power also demonstrated 4:41 The stakes are raised here a bit when Jesus encounters a legion 8:30 Legion is of no certain number; a large number; 3,000 6,000 No wonder this man was tormented Where did demons originate? Created angels who followed Lucifer in his rebellion Some are already bound; 2 Peter 2:4; Jude 6 But there are still plenty of demons around to do the bidding of their master Jesus was able to provide peace in a storm, can He give peace to a soul? We go from a wild sea to a wild man. A sea and man untamable On the one hand there is a howling storm and hysterical disciples, On the other the screaming demoniac, demons, pigs, and a frightened populace COUNTRY 8:26 Gadara; the region Gadarenes; the country Gerasa; the small village Eastern shore of the Sea of Galilee; Gentile country Matthew mentions two men; no contradiction, Luke does not say only one So violent no one could pass that way 288: The field of swine was a good way off 8:30 Demons ask: have you come here to torment us before the time? Mark 5: the man could not be bound by chains; screaming day and night; cutting himself with stones CLEANSED 8:27-34 Reluctant Condition Why is Jesus here? He came unto His own 19:11 He came to seek and save the lost Gentile region; area of swine and tombs; both unclean to a Jew had demons literally: having demons possessed by; the man had demons and the demons had him no story of his background how he came to be possessed idolatry? Jesus and the others are greeted by this scene two naked men running down the hill screaming at them Bodies covered with scars, caked on blood, with fresh wounds Uncut hair, untrimmed beard matted and tangled A wild look their eyes, odor beyond imagination I would be headed back to the boat wore no clothes Kent Hughes: "Typically, those under the sway of demons descend to filthy living, both physically and morally. It is not at all incidental that the rise of occultism and Satanism in recent years has been accompanied by increasing drug abuse, pornography, and obscenity." It is alarming to know that nakedness which should cause shame (Garden of Eden) is glorified today. Lived in tombs; more at home with the dead than the living Rock bands: Megadeath, Anthrax, Slayer: thrash metal Here are men dehumanized, animalized, marginalized A Word about demons: C. S. Lewis wrote: There are two equal and opposite errors into which our race can fall about the devils. One is to disbelieve in their existence. The other is to believe, and to feel an excessive and unhealthy interest in them. They themselves are equally pleased by both errors and hail a materialist and magician with the same delight. We have little record of demons in the OT Saul had an evil spirit from the LORD We have no instruction on demon possession in the epistles But there was this explosion of activity in the gospels I agree with MacArthur: demons much prefer to operate in the background but it was the very presence of Jesus that precipitated their exposure Luke 4:33 Jesus was in the middle of a message Luke 8 Jesus had just arrived Power over the demons was given specifically to the apostles Note Acts 19:11-16 Demonic activity is real; all around us; their influence is tremendous 1 John 4:4 But like these men, Satan is out to destroy Has a particular interest in God s image bearer and his destruction Self-dishonoring, self-destructive habits; goad people from vice to vice Plunges people into the depths of depravity The wreckage of body and mind with drugs (even legal ones) Our battle today is not a power struggle but a truth struggle We must deal with the deceptions and fiery darts The warfare is real. The battle is for the mind. We have the equipment for the job. Reluctant Submission 8:28 Falling before Jesus: submission, fear; not worship Idiom: what to me and to you used John 4 to His mother When one person was unjustly bothered by another When asked to get involved in something that does not concern you Here: leave me alone! Jesus, thou Son of the Most High Demons were fundamentalist; they know exactly who Jesus is Said with a loud voice all could hear; apostles, disciples and observers The demons are answering the question: who is this? James 2:19, 20 do not torment me Matthew 8:29 have you come to torment me before the time? The tormentor begging not to be tormented Time: Kairos; an appointed time in the future it s not time yet Demons are not only fundamentalists they are premillennialist When Jesus comes to earth the second time, Satan and his minions will be bound for 1,000 years v. 31 the abyss; bottomless pit Revelation 9:1-3; 29:1-3 a prison from which one can be released Not the same as the final lake of fire The demon knows that is the second coming so says, Before the time Demons know their time of judgment is coming. They know their time of deception will come to a close. They know that after the 1,000 year period, their release will be short before the final judgment in the lake of fire reserved for them Reluctant Request 8:29-33 The destructive nature at work; could not have man so took to pigs Brian Bell "They (demons) desire a host. They want contact with the material realm. Satan will take a pig or a man; if he gets a man he will do his best to turn him into a pig! were imploring more than once Mark 5:13 some 2,000 pigs Why such a show? There is a great crowd of bystanders observing The drowning of the pigs proved the demons were submissive to Jesus The demons had left the man Deviled ham A great swine dive Soo-ee cide CLOTHED 8:35-37 Conversion of the Man In one moment, a complete change has taken place A wonderful change in one who was once the slave of Satan Old things have passed away; all has become new And no matter how marvelous, miraculous this is; it is not more miraculous than your own conversion For you were delivered from the kingdom of darkness No man is in his right mind until he is converted No man is in his right place until he is sitting at the feet of Jesus No man is rightly clothed until clothed until he has put on Christ Miraculous release, miraculous deliverance, restoration Conviction of the People At this stage, we tend to get caught up in the destruction of the pigs The natural conclusion is the wanted Jesus to leave because of material loss They were willing to choose pigs over a maniac And, no doubt, a lot of bacon went down the drain 8:35 the people were not angry at the loss, they were frightened Phobos: fearful Same response as the disciples, but different results The disciples followed; the people sought to dismiss Jesus One leading to heaven; one leading to hell A crazy man beside himself A present danger too. Guard, chain, and shackle warned That it was surely true. A demon legion rampaged through The caverns of his mind. Naked, raving midst the tombs, More beast than humankind. Jesus sent a question To pierce his trembling frame. The first step on his road back home: Man, tell me, what s your name? Legion, was the stark reply, For demons had control. "Give us pigs as habitat, If you plan to make him whole." Pigs it was, and down they ran To leap into the sea. While clothed, serene, and at His feet The man sat demon-free. But irony of ironies In what the people do. They join the demon legion By choosing swineherd too! Go away, they shouted. Pig killer, leave this place. They had no thought for their own kind, That one so touched by grace. Things haven t really changed today, The question s just as big For those bound by possessions: Will it be Christ, or pig? Elwood McQuaid 8:37 is one of the saddest verses in the Bible Spurgeon comments that "Here was a whole city at a prayer meeting, praying against their own blessing. Horrible was their prayer; but it was heard, and Jesus departed out of their coasts!". One would have thought they would receive a man of such power Surely there were others in the city who would benefit from healing Should a man tell Jesus to leave him alone, Jesus is gentlemanly enough to do just that. And that man will come to the place where the opportunity for repentance will be gone Here is the illustration of sin s power over the lost; blinds them to truth They saw God at work and rejected COMMISSIONED 8:38, 39 The third use of begged 1. The demons begged him, not the pit, but the pigs! (Lk 8:31 , 32 ) 2. The people begged him, leave our country! (Lk 8:37 ) (Contrast Jairus, the synagogue official, begging in Lk 8:41 )! 3. The former demoniac begged him, let me follow you! (Lk 8:38 ) This is a strange twist. Jesus honored the request of demons to enter the pigs; Jesus honored the request of ungodly people to leave the area; but denied the request of the one who wanted to follow. Seems most backward Jesus knew that although the people as a whole pleaded for His departure, there would be some who would benefit from a salvation testimony. Thus the maniac became a missionary. The lesson is simple: those who have experienced the transforming power of Christ in their lives should proclaim it What is there in your life that can only be explained because of God? To what extent am I proclaiming the transforming power of Christ? Jesus went out of His way to cross the Sea of Galilee We have to go out among the pagan The hardest place to go is to the people who know you There is a time to sit at the feet of Jesus; another to rise and tell others Mark 5:20 he went to Decapolis; ten cities; the people were amazed Here is the poster child of the new birth Soul-winning is generally not accomplished by argument but testimony WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 DELIVERED BY THE MASTER COUNTRY 8:26 Eastern shore of the Sea of Galilee; Gentile country CLEANSED 8:27-34 Reluctant Condition the man had demons and the demons had him more at home with the dead than the living dehumanized, animalized, marginalized the very presence of Jesus precipitated their exposure Acts 19:11-161 John 4:4 Reluctant Submission 8:28 Falling before Jesus: submission, fear; not worship Demons were fundamentalist James 2:19, 20 Demons are premillennialist the abyss; bottomless pit Revelation 9:1-3; 29:1-3 Reluctant Request 8:29-33 Mark 5:13 some 2,000 pigs The drowning of the pigs proved the demons were submissive to Jesus CLOTHED 8:35-37 Conversion of the Man No man is in his right mind until he is converted No man is in his right place until he is sitting at the feet of Jesus No man is rightly clothed until clothed until he has put on Christ Conviction of the People frightened Phobos: fearful 8:37 is one of the saddest verses in the Bible COMMISSIONED 8:38, 39 The maniac became a missionary Those who have experienced the transforming power of Christ in their lives should proclaim it Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(73).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/3/2023 3:11:40 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 Mark 5:21-43 SAILING WITH THE MASTER 8:22-25 SPEAKING FOR THE MASTER 8:26-39 (maniac becomes a missionary) Jesus is soon to commission the 12 to preach the kingdom 9:1, 2 Chapter 8 is getting them ready The Parable of the Soils placed an emphasis on their hearing and doing The Parable of the Light (16-18) sharing what they hear and see The arrival of family (19-21) places hearing and doing to relationship status The apostles and disciples were then treated to power over the elements They further witnessed the power over the spiritual world Important points from two weeks ago: Jesus gave the apostles authority: Luke 9:1; Acts 19:11-16 The epistles refer to demonic activity but is silent about casting out demons We are told about the equipment for spiritual warfare Ephesians 6 We are told to resist the devil James 4:7 resist, he will flee Be careful of the devil 1 Peter 5:8 roaring lion We are told not to give him a place in our lives Ephesians 4:17 The manner of breaking the binding of Satan is to be saved Ephesians 2:1-4 Put off ungodly habits Ephesians 4:17-24 Renewing our mind 4:23 We are in a section of miracles with each bringing the same response: fear 8:26; 37; 47; 56 We are ready to look at the second miracle SYMPATHY OF THE MASTER 8:40-48 MAN IN PERIL Expectation 8:40 Jesus returns to Capernaum Left a single convert as a testimony to 10 cities and a people who insisted they wanted no part of Jesus Isaiah 6; Ezekiel 3:9 they were all waiting Anticipation, expectation, looking for Did they know when He was returning? No, but they were looking Present tense: lifestyle Waiting in Luke: 1:21 People waiting for Zecharias 3:15 people wondering if John was the Anointed One 7:19 the disciples of John s question Jesus; do we wait for another? 12:46 the master came when the servant was not looking Luke in Acts 28:6 the people looking for Paul to swell and die after viper bite Instruction for us 2 Peter 3:12-14 Are we looking for Jesus like the folks of Capernaum? Did we wake this am thinking Jesus could come today? Not just a few but a multitude Mark 5:21 a great multitude so much that Jesus remained by the water A wonderful reception rather than the incredible rejection on the other side Over the holiday, reports surfaced everywhere about Wilhelmina Lancaster, a nun who died in 2019 at the age of 95 in Missouri. When her body was exhumed to be moved into a chapel, she was found to be "incorrupt" - a Catholic term for a corpse (and in this case, not embalmed) that shows no signs of decay. Multiple outlets ran stories over the weekend about people traveling to the rural monastery to view Lancaster's body. The reports, including a a video segment on CNN, said in some cases people were inspired to even touch and kiss her remains. Invitation 8:41 Behold look at this! It s a word of wonder There is something here worthy of observation The wonder is that a man in so high an ecclesiastical position should come to Christ Usually they were the despisers of Jesus Spurgeon: Sometimes the camel really does go through the eye of an needle Synagogue Chief: Jairus Had heard and seen; now Interceeding No doubt heard the report of 4:31-41; Jairus synagogue? 7:17 This was not a private humbling; the masses were gathered Quite literally sticking out his neck; subject to ridicule, scorn, Loss of position. Quite a scene for a synagogue official to bow before Jesus begged beseech, desire; comfort, pray Implore with a sense of urgency Parakaleo: to call alongside, to urge someone to action Mark 5:23 includes please come and lay your hands on her What a parent will do for his child.
