Willing to cross what would have been previously considered out-of-bounds What have people done for health reasons: across the country, Mexico, Canada; CBD s His daughter is dying; her first year of womanhood in Jewish culture Mark 5:23 at the point of death the multitudes thronged Him to strangle completely; to choke Pressing in so hard one could hardly breathe It was amazing Jairus even got to Jesus But one woman pushed her way through the crowd Interruption 8:41-48 Note the contrast of individuals A Man A Woman Wealthy Poor Respected Rejected Ruler Nobody Honored Ashamed Leader in Synagogue Shut out of the synagogue Came submissively Came secretly 12 year old child 12 year old illness 12 years of delight 12 years of misery Needs met at the feet Needs met at the feet WOMAN IN PERIL 8:43-48 Faith Concealed 43, 44a Interruptions were not new to Jesus None ever through Him off, but became a ministry tool 12 years of misery; all money spent to no avail Mark 5:26 suffered many things from physicians The condition declared her unclean to herself and all who touched her She was not able to enter a synagogue She was an outcast to family, friends, and culture Her disease was slowly killing her, draining her energy, and her life-blood Matthew 9:1; Mark 5:28 she was saying repeatedly to herself If I just touch His garments . . premeditated touch the border the fringe, one of four tassels at the corners 4 attached to the garment worn over the robe; i.e. poncho, shawl identify them as a Jew and a reminder of the commandments Numbers 15:38,39; (Deuteronomy 22:12) some consider the word meaning hem which meant she was ground level Some consider this to be weak faith; others great faith Spurgeon: Great fear kept her from facing Him: great faith led her to believe that a touch of his robe behind him would cure her. She was ignorant enough to think that healing went from him unconsciously; but yet her faith lived despite her ignorance, and triumphed despite her bashfulness With the multitudes brushing up against Jesus, you can picture His robe even dragging behind Him farther than usual touch to grab in order to manipulate It was not just a mere tap but a grab The touch brought instant healing Faith Rewarded 44b Her flow of blood stopped ESV: her discharge of blood ceased suggests she was actively bleeding up to that time which is the only way she would have known healing took place stopped literally stood ; almost like her hermorrage was damned up Faith Revealed 45-48 Jesus perceiving power had gone out of Him Objection 8:45 Still learning; here is a difference in touching out of curiosity and faith Still learning about power Perception who? Jesus knew It was not about gaining information, but obtain her confession and clarification For the benefit of the people; she would no longer be considered unclean There is no magic here; it was not the touch that healed her but her faith Her only hope was Jesus Just like He is our only hope; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Acts 4:12 Just as this woman was instantly cured, so all who come to Christ in faith are instantly cleansed of their sin." The power of Jesus is not impersonal, but personal for explanation; not because He felt some tug on His garment An incredible thought that Jesus feels the flow of power We can sense the infusion of spiritual power At salvation At sanctification At glorification Power left: dunamis, inherent power residing withing by virtue of His person 4:14; 5:17; Acts 1:8 Identification 8:47 Mark 5:32 Jesus was looking around; probably made eye contact Why expose? She was hoping for secrecy What would be of value to have her identified? To give an official declaration of healing to her To announce healing for the benefit of those who knew her So she would know it was her faith that healed; not the touch To display His power before Jairus To pronounce a blessing with the special designation: daughter She was the third person in the chapter to fall at Jesus feet; vs. 28, 41 Affection 8:48 Daughter: the only time used in the gospels to address a woman She is now in the family of God; a word of relationship Spoken in tenderness and love; assurance, comfort Whatever your problem, you can come to Jesus Jesus can cleanse every dirty wound He has adequate power to cleanse all sin A personal response to personal faith Some commentaries question or do not speak in salvation terms whole well sodzo save; same word used in 7:50 She was made whole; physically and spiritually Public profession; public commendation; public pronouncement It is one thing to be around Jesus and another thing to touch Him by faith You can be part of the crowd but never receive the blessing of being near Jesus. WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 SYMPATHY OF THE MASTER 8:40-48 MAN IN PERIL 8:40-42 Expectation 8:40 2 Peter 3:12-14 Invitation 8:41 The wonder is that a man so high in an ecclesiastical position should come to Christ Interruption 8:41, 42 WOMAN IN PERIL 8:43-48 Faith Concealed 43, 44a She was an outcast to family, friends, and culture touch the border the fringe Numbers 15:38,39 touch to grab in order to manipulate Faith Rewarded 44b stopped stood Faith Revealed 45-48 Objection 8:45 Perception The power of Jesus is personal Identification 8:47 To give an official declaration of healing to her To announce healing for the benefit of those who knew her So she would know it was her faith that healed; not the touch To display His power before Jairus To pronounce a blessing with the special designation Affection 8:48 Daughter: a word of relationship A personal response to personal faith She was made whole; physically and spiritually Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(74).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/10/2023 1:43:21 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A WORD ABOUT FAITH (Steve Cole) Luke 8: 43-48 INTRODUCTION We must lay hold of Jesus by faith FAITH IS ACTIVE, NOT PASSIVE For 12 years this woman tried every doctor, every possible remedy Who knows what she put herself through except all her money If there was a quack of a doctor, she tried it She would not quit until she found a cure She pushed her way through the crowd She knew everyone she touched would be unclean She elbowed her way through She did not care that Jesus was on mission to help another She had a goal and was out to obtain it Many are oblivious to the death raging through their body They are either ignorant or ignoring their coming death They shut it out or hope for the best The faith that saves actively seeks the Lord Isaiah 55:6, 7 FAITH WORKS BEST WHEN HUMAN EFFORTS FAIL The best thing that happened to this woman was she ran out of money Her only hope was to come to the One who heals without money. If we think human effort will do the job, we will continue working. But when we realize that Jesus is the only way for cleansing, we will rely on Him alone. It is Peter crying out, Save me, Lord, I perish! FAITH ACCEPTS THE SIMPLICITY OF THE GOSPEL The woman had tried some difficult, bitter and expensive solutions. But this one was easy and free. No bitter potions to drink, no medicinal application 3 times a day No one collecting a fee. She touched and she was instantly healed Many stumble over the gospel because it is too simple, too free. If they were required to crawl on broken glass, they would do it. If they thought good works would qualify them for heaven, they would give, serve, and expend themselves. Problem: after doing all of that the question still lingers, was it enough? But they find the message, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ and thou shalt be saved too simple. Faith becomes a stumbling block to their pride. There is no human glory. see what I have done no boasting God s way is simple and free. But what about the person who wants to believe that message but does not have that much faith? FAITH NEED NOT BE PERFECT TO BE EFFECTIVE Somehow this woman thought just touching Jesus garment would heal. She was expecting some kind of magic. She came only looking for a cure and would have walked away wholly content to find it. Her faith was ignorant. She didn t know much theology. Jesus took her weak faith and strengthened it. How much faith does it take to be forgiven of your sins? Not much Salvation depends more on the strength of the Savior than us. Jesus said, Your faith (however weak) has saved you. But her faith was the channel through which God worked. It is the object of our faith, Christ, that saves. Your faith is really not the issue. FAITH MUST BE CONFESSED This woman was in stealth mode; coming from behind. Jesus singles her out; Jesus would let her get away and remain anonymous Jesus does not like undercover believers She would have missed the opportunity for worship: fall down She would have missed testimony time: He healed me She would have missed His words of blessing She would not have know her place in the family. makes her confess to what had happened. Romans 10:9-13 Once love is understood as the path for deliverance, we obey the commands Salvation from sin requires water baptism; (Acts 10:48) symbolizing the complete cleansing of a soul. CONCLUSION We have all been unclean; all defiled by sin. Only Jesus can cleanse us through His death on the cross. We must lay hold of Him. Don t be put off by those around you. Don t worry that your weak faith is not enough. Don t think of anything but you are a sinner and Jesus is a Savior. Is there something in you life for which you need to reach out and grab Jesus? Healing: physical, emotional, spiritual, mental (feel like you are losing it) You have tried other things, no ready to come to Jesus Or you could be like those who don t mind being part of the crowd; like to be in His presence but not so close as to benefit from His power A WORD ABOUT FAITH Luke 8: 43-48 FAITH IS ACTIVE, NOT PASSIVE She would not quit until she found a cure The faith that saves actively seeks the Lord Isaiah 55:6, 7 FAITH WORKS BEST WHEN HUMAN EFFORTS FAIL The best thing that happened to this woman was she ran out of money FAITH ACCEPTS THE SIMPLICITY OF THE GOSPEL She touched and she was instantly healed Faith becomes a stumbling block to their pride. God s way is simple and free. FAITH NEED NOT BE PERFECT TO BE EFFECTIVE She was expecting some kind of magic. She didn t know much theology Salvation depends more on the strength of the Savior than us It is the object of our faith, Christ, that saves. FAITH MUST BE CONFESSED This woman was in stealth mode Jesus does not like undercover believers She would have missed the opportunity for worship: fall down She would have missed testimony time: He healed me She would have missed His words of blessing She would not have know her place in the family. Romans 10:9-13 Once love is understood as the path for deliverance, we obey the commands Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(75).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/17/2023 10:24:59 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 INTRODUCTION Jesus is about to send out the 12 apostles; chapter 9 No doubt, they have learned much from him the past 1 1/2 years We have witnessed much in our study. But here is chapter 8, I do believe there specific events for the benefit of the disciples. We began if you remember regarding the Parable of the Four Soils Then we are told that the message is one to be shared And who is qualified to be a member of God s family: hearers and doers Beginning in verse 22; the apostles are witnesses to miracles Each having the same result: vs. 25, 37, 47, 56 What did they see? Sailing with the Master 8:22-25 Speaking for the Master 8:26-39 Sympathy from the Master 8:40-56 If you remember we have looked at A Man in Peril; A Woman in Peril Jairus brought his plea for his daughter A Woman interrupted Jesus walk with her own desperate plea Last week was a message on faith. "Faith is not an instinct. It certainly is not a feeling feelings don t help much when you re in the lions den or hanging on a wooden Cross. Faith is not inferred from the happy way things work. It is an act of will, a choice, based on the unbreakable Word of God who cannot lie, and who showed us what love and obedience and sacrifice mean, in the person of Jesus Christ." -- Elisabeth Elliot We now return to Jairus: 8:49-56 A Word of Encouragement 8:49, 50 A WORD OF ENCOURAGEMENT 8:49, 50 Have you ever had to change plans at the last minute? To cancel something you were really looking for to because of an emergency? Jairus had to deal with the divine delay Psalm 130:5,6 ; (Habakkuk 2:3 ). We, too, have to learn to wait on the fulfillment of God's Word. Fear sets in during the delay period between the promise given and the promise fulfilled. Jesus told Jairus that He would heal his daughter, and nothing was going to change that fact. (disicples were told they were going over v. 22) Note other Biblical examples of those who waited on God in faith during periods of delay: Abraham waited 10 years for Isaac to be born, then was tested by God at Mount Moriah. Joseph waited 12 years in prison before his dream was fulfilled. Joshua and Caleb waited 40 years in the desert because of the unbelief of their generation, but they lived to possess the Promised Land. David waited for 10 years, being hounded by King Saul in the desert, before he was crowned king. As a fugitive on the run, he wrote: "The Lord is my light and my salvation whom shall I fear?" (Psalm 27:1 ). The disciples waited 10 days in the upper room for the Spirit, who came with power on the Day of Pentecost. Remember the adage: "Fear knocked at the door. Faith answered, and there was no one there Hope to Hopeless 8:49 Perhaps the most devastating message a parent can receive Do not trouble the Teacher Tantamount to saying: Jesus can t do anything with a person who died The messengers (Mark 5:35 more than one) must not have heard reports This takes the miracle from healing to resurrection Ephesians 2:12- many in this world have no hope 1 Timothy 1:1 the Personification of Hope Matthew 9:18 gives us insight to Jairus v. 50 2 commands stop being afraid start believing. Do it now Seeking to encourage and reassure In spite of all that Jairus has heard and maybe seen, this is new territory for him. And this is not some member of his congregation, but his own daughter. believe only It is one thing to believe in Jesus when things are going well, but quite another when things get dark; hope turns into hopelessness. God s people have always had times of fear and challenged to believe Isaiah 43:2 Genesis 22:2 the sacrifice of Isaac Exodus 14:10 Israel hemmed in by Pharaoh s army 2 Chronicles 20:1, 2 Jehoshaphat For the God on the mountain, is the God in the valley. When things go wrong, He'll make them right. And the God of the good times is still God in the bad times. The God of the day is still God in the night. - Gaither A WORD OF REVELATION 8:51-53 The funeral was already under way No embalming; temperatures required a quick burial The local mourners were probably on stand-by and ready to go Matthew 9:23 flute players and noisy disorder quite a commotion; Controlled chaos Jairus position would have brought out the best of the professionals What a difference from the way funeral are conducted today The first record of the inner circle Jesus does not always make a show of His miracles v. 52 if you want to cause a fuss tell people they are not seeing what they are seeing Jesus redefines death; sleeping, temporary v. 53 laughing: ridiculing, jeering, deriding, laugh to scorn (3x NT; all this occasion) all the noise now directed to Jesus MacArthur: "That their weeping could so quickly turn to laughter, even mocking laughter, betrayed the fact that their mourning was a paid act and did not reflect genuine sorrow. Adrian Rogers: Christians are going to be laughed at by the world You will be the butt of jokes; be open to ridicule; Fleas come with the dog put them all out Perfect case of not casting pearls before swine Spurgeon: Many things have to give place when Jesus comes on the scene A WORD OF LOVE AND POWER 8:54-55a Matthew 9:18 Jairus had asked for Jesus to lay His hand on her That is what He does; touch of life Jesus spoke in Aramaic; language of the child; Mark has original; Luke translates into Greek child little girl damsel maiden arise rise; stand up; awaken; wake up from the dead Romans 4:24 Matthew 9:6 get up Immediate Results 55 There is no delay in Christ s miracles. Spurgeon: If there is a distressed soul here now, your salvation need not take months and years; it may be done today, and in a moment thou mayest be able to rejoice that thy sins are forgiven, and that thou art a child of God. Commanded food: proof she was alive; had a real body This is the power Jesus will one day use to raise all peoples John 5:28, 29 A WORD OF TENDER CONCERN 8:55b-56 The 4th time in Luke 8 where we encounter astonishment. Parents amazed: literally out of their minds Mark: completely astounded Why tell no one? In just a few moments everyone is going to know anyway Matthew 9:26 Curiosity seekers can hinder His work; Mark 1:40-45 Crowds may make Him King by force; John 6:14, 15 Act of judgment to hide the truth; Luke 9:21 Contrasts 8:39 Perhaps the real reason: Mark 8:30, 31 There is no story about Jesus without the cross and resurrection. He did not come to be known as a miracle worker. These last two miracles tell us about the sympathy and compassion of Jesus. Jesus ministers to hurting people. Matthew 11:28-30 He offers rest John 14:27 He provides peace Luke 19:10 He offers salvation to the sinner Jesus can tell the waves to be muzzled Jesus can command demons to leave He elevates human faith: the woman and child In fearful situations we must overcome hindrances to faith and put our trust in Jesus. 1. Does faith require that we believe Jesus is going to answer our prayers affirmatively or is it enough to believe Him whatever the answer? 2. Why does God sometimes delay the answers to our prayers? 3. What would you say to a critic who taunted, How can you trust a God who allows a child to die? 4. Is fear sin? Is it ever okay? If so, when? WHO IS THIS? Luke 8:22-56 Sailing with the Master 8:22-25 Speaking for the Master 8:26-39 Sympathy from the Master 8:40-56 A WORD OF ENCOURAGEMENT 8:49, 50 Psalm 130:5,6 Ephesians 2:12 1 Timothy 1:1 stop being afraid start believing. Do it now God s people have always had times of fear and challenged to believe A WORD OF REVELATION 8:51-53 Matthew 9:23 Jesus redefines death; sleeping, temporary laughing: ridiculing, jeering Perfect case of not casting pearls before swine A WORD OF LOVE AND POWER 8:54-55a Jesus spoke in Aramaic There is no delay in Christ s miracles. John 5:28, 29 A WORD OF TENDER CONCERN 8:55b-56 Curiosity seekers can hinder His work; Mark 1:40-45 Crowds may make Him King by force; John 6:14, 15 Act of judgment to hide the truth; Luke 9:21 Perhaps the real reason: Mark 8:30, 31 Matthew 11:28-30 John 14:27 Luke 19:10 In fearful situations we must overcome hindrances to faith and put our trust in Jesus. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(76).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 6/24/2023 9:57:55 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message LAST CHANCE Luke 9 INTRODUCTION Luke 9 is a transitional chapter. The public ministry of Jesus has been a little over 1 years Outside of John 1-4, His ministry has been in Galilee With the exception of His early months, Jesus has been the only preacher of the Kingdom of God in the land of Israel. His cousin, John the Baptist, was the forerunner. He has been arrested and by 9:9 has been martyred. Jesus is the sole individual speaking the message of Jehovah. The reality of that had never crossed my mind. That is about to change as He is about to send out twelve disciples. In this chapter, we will see Herod s interest in Jesus; the feeding of at least 5,000; Peter s confession; Jesus predicting His coming death; the Transfiguration; more healing; more teaching. By the end of the chapter, we see Jesus is moving south to Jerusalem; 9:51. The chapter begins by giving Galilee one final opportunity to repent. Nazareth ran Jesus out of town; 4:28, 29 His new hometown, Capernaum, fared no better; 10:15 But Jesus is going to expand His ministry by sending out more proclaimers of the message accompanied by power to deal with disease, demons, and death. The disciples have been observers; learners by definition. They are about to fulfill their role as apostles; sent ones with an assigned message. And another significant part of Chapter 9 is Jesus laying out in no uncertain terms what it means to follow Him. 9:23 death to self; take up a cross 9:62 there is no looking back This is riveting stuff; exciting material; challenging, serious, gut wrenching. Our hearts will be exposed; we will have to reckon with our personal walk with Jesus; we no longer sit on the sidelines taking in the miraculous, beholding the beauty. This chapter is as much transitional for us as it is to the life and ministry of Jesus. EMPOWERING THE TWELVE Luke 9:1-6 (Matthew 10:1-15; Mark 6:7-13) Divine Summons 9:1a called to call together Called out of a larger group of hundreds Called the 12 even though one would be a betrayer This is perhaps how the conversation went: Robert Morgan paraphrases Jesus' instructions to the disciples in Luke 9 - "Now, you ve been sitting around listening to me long enough. I want you to go out knocking on some door and trying your hand at preaching. Yes, I know it s a little threatening. Yes, I know it s outside your comfort zone. No, I m not going to give you a lot of training in public speaking. I just want you to travel light don t take too many clothes with you because you ll have a hard time lugging around that suitcase. Just show up and say a word for me and if they listen to you, that s great. And if you fall flat on your face, just dust yourself off and go to the next town and try again. Here, they are getting their feet wet; some exposure Luke 10; they are sent out again with many others; total of 70 There are given a little more instruction Here is their exposure to the process of ministry Jesus wants to begin to cultivate in them a sense of faithfulness This is our lesson: We don t have to do big things for God; We just need to do little things faithfully. That means we do it whether we like the task or not; whether it is convenient or not; whether it seems to be doing any good or not. Divine Privilege 9:1 power and authority Privilege brings responsibility How did Jesus begin His public ministry: 4:14 in the power of the Spirit The Holy Spirit is not mentioned in Luke 9, but He is here None of us should try to minister without reliance on the power of the Holy Spirit. You say, I don t have that type of power 2 Peter 1:3, 4 Are we being good stewards of the gifts God has granted to each of us 1 Peter 4:10, 11 We have the power to live a supernatural life: " Not by might nor by power, but by My Spirit, says the LORD of hosts." Zechariah 4:6 Whatever God has called you to do for the Kingdom, He will give the power of the King to accomplish it. The corollary is that apart from Him we can do nothing (John 15:5, 15, 16) The Holy Spirit is not decreased in His ability to empower. The same Holy Spirit that came on the disciples (24:49) is ours today The same Holy Spirit that empowered the disciples to turn the world upside down is ours every day. Are we living up to our potential? No, we are not going to raise the dead, lay healing hands on the diseased, etc. That authority died off with the apostles. But we are stilled called upon to present our bodies as a living sacrifice ; to not be conformed to this world ; to not think of ourselves more highly than we should; to serve one another in love ; to bless those who persecute you [sacrifice, separation, sober assessment, serving one another in love, supernatural response] power dunamis; dynamite, dynamo authority exousia; heal therapeuro Luke distinguishes between authority over demons and diseases Not all diseases are related to demonic activity Power is a force to be able to do something Authority is the right to use that power Matthew 28:18, 19 All authority has been given unto Me in heaven and on earth. Go, therefore, and make disciples of all nations Acts 1:8 you shall receive power after the Holy Spirit has come upon you The Gospel is the dunamis of God for salvation; Romans 1:16 It has the power to save sinners Adrian Rogers: That's power that can make a crooked man straight, make an impure woman pure, can make a drunkard sober, that can give a hope that is steadfast and sure. It is not just the power to transform a life but the power to energize and work through you. (dynamite and dynamo) Not so much power put forth as power possessed It is now ability and capability. That is what the disciples and you have been given. The power for victory over the world, the flesh, and the devil authority the right to use the power of the gospel for salvation John 1:12 But as many as received Him to them He gave authority Demons: as said before, during the days of Jesus demonic activity was exposed; no longer hidden Demons are just as active today Behind every idol man worships is a deceiving spirit; 1 Corinthians 10:20, 21 Seducing spirits: 1 Timothy 4:1 Divine Proclamation 9:2 Send out: official or authoritative representative Matthew adds: 10:5, 6 Mark adds sent them out in pairs (6:7) Proclamation is key The message is not to be adulterated, altered Matthew 10:7 the kingdom of heaven is at hand has come and will remain The message of the kingdom is the gospel: 9:6 It is only through hearing and believing the gospel that one has the authority to enter the kingdom of God (John 3:3) Luke 4:18 It has been well said that The only kingdom that will prevail in this world is the kingdom that is not of this world! A quick summary of the Kingdom of God Invisible, internal in your midst (17:21) The King lives in the heart of one who has believed, trusted in the Him Proclaiming the gospel message explains how one can enter the kingdom 17:30 the day of revelation: revealing who has received the message Millennial Kingdom: Messianic Age; Jesus ruling on earth from Jerusalem His preaching and that of His disciples was to explain how one could enter the invisible aspect of the Kingdom of God in the present and in that way they would be assured of experiencing the visible Kingdom of God in the future. Divine Performance A quick summary of healing and miracles Seen throughout the Bible but mainly in clusters: Exodus, Elijah and Elisha, Jesus and the apostles The purpose was to authenticate the message at a critical time in God s working with man Once the purpose of the miracles was accomplished , the miracles decreased Hebrews 2:3, 4 The writer no longer pointed to present experience EMPOWERING THE TWELVE Luke 9:1-6 Divine Summons 9:1a Jesus wants to begin to cultivate in them a sense of faithfulness We don t have to do big things for God; We just need to do little things faithfully Divine Privilege 9:1 None of us should try to minister without reliance on the power of the Holy Spirit. 2 Peter 1:3, 4 1 Peter 4:10, 11 We have the power to live a supernatural life Zechariah 4:6 Power is a force to be able to do something Authority is the right to use that power It is not just the power to transform a life but the power to energize and work through you Divine Proclamation 9:2 Send out: official or authoritative representative It is only through hearing and believing the gospel that one has the authority to enter the kingdom of God (John 3:3) The only kingdom that will prevail in this world is the kingdom that is not of this world Invisible, internal in your midst (17:21) Millennial Kingdom: Messianic Age Divine Performance The purpose was to authenticate the message Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(77).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/1/2023 4:14:19 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE LORD S SUPPER 1Corinthians 11:23-26 INTRODUCTION This is a special weekend in the history of our country. July 4 is more than just a holiday, but a day to celebrate the beginning of the United States of America. I am so grateful for the men who possessed the insight and fortitude to separate from Great Britain and become an independent nation. I am convinced that God had His omnipotent hand in the formation of the laws which brought out land into existence. Holidays such as this bring about annual activities. Parades, picnics, parties of various shapes and sizes. Perhaps you have some activity which has become an annual event. I do believe it is a day to let freedom ring! It is unfortunate that many in school today cannot accurately tell you about George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Benjamin Franklin, James Madison, Benjamin Rush, and so many others who were instrumental in writing the Declaration of Independence and other documents laying the foundation for a new country. They would not recognize how so many other brilliant minds have so poorly supported their work. I have no interest in such a discussion this morning. What I do want to do is focus on a more significant event in the life of the church. An event that is even more historic, more life changing, an event of eternal consequences. Countries of the world come and go. Leaders come and go. This event will endure to the end of the ages. We interrupt Luke 9 to spend the entirety of the time focusing on the Lord s table, the Lord s Supper, Communion. It has been a while since the whole service was used and I thought this weekend would be a good one to do so. A. A MEMORIAL 1 Corinthians 11:23-26 While no one can be exact on the chronological order of NT writings, it is quite probable that this is the first written account of the Lord s Table. In remembrance of me. Paul was not with the disciples at the Last Supper. He spoke from special revelation of the occasion. The remembrance is so significant that Paul was given special revelation to that effect. This memorial keeps the death of Christ before the people. 1. INFEDILITY ANTICIPATED. We forget so quickly. There were so many problems in Corinth that required Paul s attention. 1 Corinthians 15:3, 4 was his message. The message was the substitutionary death of Christ. In a day of false teaching and apostasy, we keep the gospel message alive through this memorial. 2. SUITABILITY FOR THIS AGE. Jesus knew the tendency of man to attach sacred significance to objects rather than to Him. You are familiar with the golden serpent. Numbers 21:8 A wonderful provision that became a problem 2 Kings 18:4 Hezekiah destroyed it for the idolatry attached to it. Think of the cross today. Predominantly an emblem of shame; has become of object of decoration and even idolatry to many. Jesus did not choose a crucifix for the memorial. Jesus chose bread and juice; objects that quickly pass away. 1 Cor. 10:21 the Lord s table We partake to remember His death. It is a supper prepared by the Lord Himself. 3. THE EXCLUSIVENESS OF THE ORDINANCE. Who may partake? What do the elements represent? Body Psalm 40:6 a body hast Thou prepared for Me. Hebrews 10:10 we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all Cup blood Hebrews 9:22 without the shedding of blood there is no remission. 1 Peter 1:18, 19 redeemed with the precious blood Think back to that night in Egypt. Families gathered around the table to eat the lamb, bread , herbs. Their gathering did not ensure their safety from the destroying angel. It was the blood upon the doorposts. The blood of the lamb alone sheltered them. Only those who have trusted the One who bore our sins, who have appropriated by faith the cleansing blood of the Savior, may come to the Lord s Table. You do not come to the table to get saved, to obtain grace. This do in remembrance of Me. You cannot remember One whom you have never known. 4. THE PURPOSE OF THE SUPPER. This ordinance is intended to exhibit the Lord s death. Not His life, though wonderful it was. Not His teachings, though no man spoke like this Man. We are to remember His death. 1 Cor. 11:26 In Egypt, a spotless lamb was slain and its blood applied. Only the blood protected them from judgment. when I see the blood, I will pass over you. That was the central fact that they were to remember. 1 Corinthians 5:7 Christ, our Passover, is sacrificed for us. We remember His death. We proclaim not our worthiness, but His saving grace. The table is for those who have new life in Christ, who have accepted by faith the death He endured for their sins. B. COMMUNION REGULATIONS. In Corinth, the Lord s Supper had been downgraded to a position even lower than the common meal. The love feast which preceded the observance of the Lord s Table was to a meal of fellowship. It has become a time of gluttony and drunkenness. Rather than a bond of fellowship, it had become a source of division. Rather than establishing intimate relationships, it had become a time for revelry. It dishonored the sacred picture the death of Christ represented. We may not do the same activity today. Today, we dishonor the table by empty formality, presumption, and carelessness, if not outright unbelief. For some, there is an attitude of indifference. For others, a time of superstition regarding the elements. 1. THE APOSTLE S CONDEMNATION. In Corinth, carelessness about the meaning resulted in dishonoring Christ at His table. There was division rather than unity. one bread and one body Paul condemned such behavior. 1 Corinthians 10:16, 17 The very word communion implies joint participation. We share as partners, we have a common bond. We have a common relationship to Christ. We have common enjoyments from Him. The Lord s Table is not the place for bitterness or division. You dishonor the Lord by coming with that attitude. Peter, after speaking of the blood, admonished his readers - 1 Peter 1:22. love one another fervently with a pure heart 2. THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TABLE. 1 Corinthians 11:23-26 We have the tendency to get distracted by other things. Let us call afresh the scenes of Calvary. The bread is to remind us of Christ s body. The fruit of the vine speaks of His blood. This do in remembrance of Me. It is the Lord Jesus we are honoring. Lord is His name of deity. Jesus is His human name. We remember the death of one who became the bridge between God and Man. He placed one hand on the throne of holiness and the other upon a lost race. When we as believers remember Him, the scenes of His agony, suffering, and death are pressed upon our hearts. We partake with the same spirit of joy that Jesus manifested. Heb. 12:2 The table is not for fear and trembling, but of reverence, gladness, and joy. We are reminded through the emblems that He has lifted the weakest, poorest, most unworthy believer into a heavenly standing. 3. THE MANNER OF PARTICIPATION. A warning. 1 Cor. 11:27, 29 unworthy has nothing to do with the person who comes, but the manner of coming. The Corinthians had turned the memorial of Christ s death into a worldly festival. Some were hungry, others were drunk. There were not treating the table with respect. What do you see at the Lord s Table Liitle pieces of broken wafers; some grape juice in a cup. Or are you discerning the Lord s body as broken for you ? Thomas Watson: If He had anything to part with of more worth, He would have bestowed it upon us. Was Christ's body broken? Then we may behold sin odious in the red looking-glass of Christ's sufferings. It is true, sin is to be abominated since it turned Adam out of paradise and threw the angels down to hell. Sin is the peace-breaker. It is like an incendiary in the family that sets husband and wife at variance. It makes God fall out with us. Sin is the birthplace of our sorrows and the grave of our comforts. But that which may most of all disfigure the face of sin and make it appear abominable is this It crucified our Lord Jesus! It made Christ veil His glory and lose His blood. If a woman saw the sword which killed her dear husband how hateful would the sight of it be to her! Do we count that sin light which made Christ's soul heavy unto death? Can that be our joy which made the Lord Jesus a man of sorrows? Did He cry out, "My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?" And shall not those sins be forsaken by us which made Christ Himself forsaken? O let us look upon sin with indignation! When a temptation to sin comes, let us say, "Is not this the sin which poured out Christ's blood!" THE LORD S SUPPER 1Corinthians 11:23-26 A. A MEMORIAL 1 Corinthians 11:23-26 1. INFEDILITY ANTICIPATED. 2. SUITABILITY FOR THIS AGE. Numbers 21:8 2 Kings 18:4 Jesus did not choose a crucifix for the memorial 3. THE EXCLUSIVENESS OF THE ORDINANCE. Body Psalm 40:6 Hebrews 10:10 Cup Hebrews 9:22 1 Peter 1:18, 19 You do not come to the table to get saved You cannot remember One whom you have never known 4. THE PURPOSE OF THE SUPPER. This ordinance is intended to exhibit the Lord s death. 1 Corinthians 5:7 B. COMMUNION REGULATIONS. 1. THE APOSTLE S CONDEMNATION. 1 Corinthians 10:16, 17 The Lord s Table is not the place for bitterness or division. 2. THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TABLE. He placed one hand on the throne of holiness and the other upon a lost race. The table is not for fear and trembling, but of reverence, gladness, and joy. 3. THE MANNER OF PARTICIPATION. unworthy has nothing to do with the person who comes, but the manner of coming. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(78).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/15/2023 11:53:05 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message SOJOURNING WITNESSES Luke 9:2-6 INTRODUCTION TRAVELING LIGHT Luke 9:2, 3 Take nothing command Present tense: continuous; this was to be their lifestyle Their evangelistic crusade was not to become a show or business enterprise go lean and mean ; taking no nonessentials Dependence was to be on God s supernatural resources Nothing wrong with possessions but there is still truth in saying: Don t let your possessions possess you Possessions often lead to worry, anxiety, bondage Philippians 3:20, 21 this world is not our home; we will leave it all behind And your kids don t want it Matthew 9:9, 10 adds to the account when God calls, God provides Jesus is not issuing a call to poverty, but urgency Travel light and be quick about proclaiming the kingdom John 9:4 (WEB) I must work the works of him who sent me, while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work. No staff: (KJV stave) stick, cane, rod No bag: (KJV scrip) wallet, leather pouch for food the bag was also used by beggars to hold out like a tin cup so no begging (Luke 10:4; 22:35, 36) The disciples were to learn Jesus would care for them even when He was not present It is a challenge to learn dependency on God We tend to think that our resources are adequate 2 Corinthians 3:5-6 (WEB) not that we are sufficient of ourselves, to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God; who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant; not of the letter, but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life. It is one thing to pray give us this day our daily bread and quite another to live that way If you read Mark s gospel, he records Jesus said to take a staff and wear sandals 6:8 Possible solution: don t take any extras The emphasis is simply to travel light; travel simply Folks, we generally overpack for two days away from home What about if you were called away for an extended time? So, a little discrepancy over a staff or sandals is really not a big deal. Are we willing to live lean or is our comfort and a successful appearance of ultimate importance? Is the keeping of appearance about us or others? Do we ask God about our purchases? Are our things beneficial to ministry or a hamstring to ministry? 2 Corinthians 5:9-10 (WEB) Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or absent, to be well pleasing to him. For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ; that each one may receive the things in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad. Herbert Lockyer - There is here, obviously, no emphasis on asceticism , no glorification of austerity. But there is laid down an abiding principle that Christ's gospelers are to keep themselves as free as possible from worldly entanglements and encumbrances. John Piper - Pilgrims travel light. If we have a great inheritance just around the corner we do not quibble over treasures here. Matthew 6:33 SLEEP TIGHT 9:4 Hospitality came from fellow Jews No VRBO; no trip tics; no reservations Matthew 10:11-12 (WEB) Into whatever city or village you enter, find out who in it is worthy; and stay there until you go on. As you enter into the household, greet it. worthy spiritual worth, good reputations A house that would be receptive to the message Matthew 10:5-8 go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel; no Gentiles; no Samaritans; preach, heal, cleanse, raise the dead, cast out demons Why stay in one house? False teachers went from house to house seeking money Blessing of peace: conveyed a deeper meaning of total well-being and wholeness of body, mind, and spirit. SHAKE RIGHT 9:5 (Discernment) To the house or houses, or town who refuse to receive the message In effect, they were rejecting any possibility of peace with God The offer of peace with God is officially withdrawn MacArthur: the greatest blessing of God is worthless for a person who will not accept it. God's Gospel is offered to all the world, and it has power to save all the world (Ro 1:16 ), but it is powerless to save or help even a single person who will not have Jesus Christ as Lord and Savior. Matthew 7:6 (WEB) Don't give that which is holy to the dogs, neither throw your pearls before the pigs, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces. Acts 13:46 (WEB) Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, and said, "It was necessary that God's word should be spoken to you first. Since indeed you thrust it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles. Acts 13:51 But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came to Iconium. Acts 18:6 (WEB) When they opposed him and blasphemed, he shook out his clothing and said to them, "Your blood be on your own heads! I am clean. From now on, I will go to the Gentiles!" Jesus had already told them many will not receive the message Parable of Soils Matthew 7:13, 14 the gate to life is narrow; broad is the way to destruction Shaking the dust: The Jews had a practice when leaving a Gentile area to shake the dust off their feet less they bring contaminated dust to their homeland For the disciples to practice such is saying these Jews are no better than the Gentiles whom the Jews considered beyond the reach of God. (Acts 13:51) This is not to say that we give up too easily they had their chance How many of us came to Christ at the first gospel presentation? God is patient, longsuffering 2 Peter 3:9 not willing that any should perish But there is a difference between being slow to believe and obstinate refusal When we are convinced that a person is resolute in their refusal to believe, we look for others. The word shake is only used twice: Acts 28:5 Paul shaking a serpent off his arm; brush off Matthew 10:14 is a different word: to shake violently (Mark 6:11; Acts 13:51; 18:6) Shaking the feet is equivalent to washing my hands of it Our responsibility is to proclaim the gospel No where does it say that it must be done perfectly Power is in the gospel, not the presentation Where the message is rejected, our responsibility is over There are times we surrender the people to God and emotionally let go the retribution will be in proportion to the light received Matthew 11:21 Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes. Matthew 11:23 You, Capernaum, who are exalted to Heaven, you will go down to Hades. For if the mighty works had been done in Sodom which were done in you, it would have remained until this day. Rejecting the gospel is a more serious sin than the gross sin of Sodom and Gomorrah. (Matthew 10:15) The Pilgrim Life Travel light Travel Headaches: 1 Peter 5:8, the devil is a roaring lion Travel Through: Our conversation is in heaven Phil 3:20 Travel Ready: 1 Corinthians 15:52 All will change in the twinkling of an eye at the sound of the trumpet SOJOURNING WITNESSES Luke 9:2-6 TRAVELING LIGHT 9:2, 3 Take nothing command; this was to be their lifestyle Dependence was to be on God s supernatural resources Philippians 3:20, 21 Jesus is not issuing a call to poverty, but urgency John 9:4 2 Corinthians 3:5-6 2 Corinthians 5:9-10 Matthew 6:33 SLEEP TIGHT 9:4 worthy spiritual worth, good reputations False teachers went from house to house seeking money Blessing of peace: wholeness of body, mind, and spirit SHAKE RIGHT 9:5 The offer of peace with God is officially withdrawn This is not to say that we give up too easily When we are convinced that a person is resolute in their refusal to believe, we look for others Power is in the gospel, not the presentation Rejecting the gospel is a more serious sin than the gross sin of Sodom and Gomorrah Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(79).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 7/22/2023 6:06:15 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE GUILT AT WORK Luke 9:7-9 INTRODUCTION The activity of a guilty conscience Reading through the passage, it seems like an odd location for this account. In the middle of sending out the 12 and their report back, Luke includes this story of Herod. But it does make sense with the question Herod proposes: who is this? Luke is all about explaining who Jesus is The context is revealing Jesus Luke s account is greatly simplified. Matthew and Mark are more extensive Matthew 14:1-12 Mark 6:14-29 HEROD S CONTROL Son of Herod the Great who died in 4 B.C. The territory was divided and ruled by 4 Tetrarch: governor over a fourth part of a larger province (The others: Archelaus, Philip, Lysanias) Herod Antipas ruled over Galilee from 4 B.C. to 39 A.D. The entirety of the life of Christ What has he heard? Was continuing (imperfect) to hear all that was happening Ministry of Jesus; now also the 12 Galilee is an area only about 50 x 30 miles About the size of Madison, Clark, Champaign counties 204 towns and villages; Josephus lots of people there The other gospel writers spend more time on the martyrdom of John the Baptist Luke s focus is more on the question: who is this? For people to believe in Jesus they must understand who He is Sets the stage for the rest of the chapter: Vs. 18-20; vs. 28-36 HEROD S CONFUSION Greatly perplexed, acute anxiety, bewildered, disturbed A perplexity amounting to despair Imperfect tense: continual confusion His mind is spinning, his conscience in condemning; dazed and confused Herod does not know which way to turn It is one who goes through a whole list of possible ways and finds no way out Luke 24:4; Acts 2:12; 5:24; 10:17 Is it Elijah? Elijah boldly rebuked a wicked ruler: 1 Kings 17:1 Elijah s life was sought by a wicked woman There was widespread expectation in Judaism that Elijah would return Remember: he did not die 2 Kings 2:11 Some compared Elijah to Jesus Jesus: Luke 1:17; Mark 9:11-13 Malachi 4:5, 6 Elijah will come At a Jewish Passover, an empty chair is at the table for Elijah Matthew 17:10-13 His appearance would mean the end times had come Elijah and John Luke 1:17 prediction their work will be similar 2 Kings 1:8; Matt. 3:4 they were similar in their dress Matthew 3:1 they both preached in the wilderness Both preached a message of repentance 1 Kings 18:17; Matt. 14:3 both withstood wicked kings John 1:21 why did John deny he was Elijah? Countering the idea he was the Elijah who never died John s humble view of himself HEROD S CONFESSION 9:9 At least he accepts the blame Responding to the thought Jesus was John raised Herod himself believed such: Mark 6:16 If he was afraid of John before (Mark 6:29), what about now? Who is this man? The most important question ever asked or answered The response determines a persons eternal destiny Often repeated in Luke: 5:21 ; 7:20 , 49 ; 8:25 ; 22:67 , 70 ; 23:3 The question is amazing because the Jews even heard the demons testify Mark 1:24; 3:11 Peter 9:20; centurion, Mark 15:39; Thomas, John 20:28 Only Luke records Herod s desire to see Jesus Kept trying imperfect tense Is this John arisen or another coming on the scene? Luke 23:6-12 Herod finally gets his chance He wanted to see a miracle; not interested in who Jesus really is His evil had silenced the voice of God to him People are still miracle seekers, but it is not about miracles It is about hearing the Word of God Hebrews 3:7, 15, 4:7 In the NT, several searched for Jesus with varying results Luke 2:26-30 Simeon Luke 8:20 family of Jesus Luke 19:3,4 Zacchaeus John 12:21 Greeks Herod sought; kept trying; deliberately pursing Acts 17:27 man s quest for God Luke 12:48 what God requires or expects from man John 16:19 making inquiry or investigation Matthew 6:33 man s effort to obtain something pursue 1 Cor. 10:24 man s desire to seek, wish for Luke 19:10 seeking those who are lost GUILT AT WORK Luke 9:7-9 HEROD S CONTROL Tetrarch: governor over a fourth part of a larger province Galilee is an area only about 50 x 30 miles For people to believe in Jesus they must understand who He is HEROD S CONFUSION A perplexity amounting to despair There was widespread expectation in Judaism that Elijah would return Malachi 4:5, 6 Elijah will come Matthew 17:10-13 Elijah and John Luke 1:17; 2 Kings 1:8; Matthew 3:1; 1 Kings 18:17; John 1:21 Countering the idea he was the Elijah who never died John s humble view of himself HEROD S CONFESSION 9:9 Herod himself believed such: Mark 6:16 The most important question ever asked or answered Luke 5:21 ; 7:20 , 49 ; 8:25 ; 22:67 , 70 ; 23:3 Only Luke records Herod s desire to see Jesus Luke 23:6-12 His evil had silenced the voice of God to him Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(80).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 8/5/2023 3:03:21 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE FEEDING THE FIVE THOUSAND Luke 9:10-17 INTRODUCTION The context here is that which we have been looking at: Who is this? First asked in 8:25 in the stilling of the storm Still the question in healing the demon possessed or healing the bleeding woman or restoring life to a little girl. It was even asked by the local tetrarch in 9:9 Luke is carefully weaving the story together as he explains in Acts 2:22 a man accredited by God to you by miracles and wonders and signs, which God did among you through him A shortage of food is no more a problem for Jesus than a storm at sea Which leads one to believe the miracle was done primarily for the disciples Who are about to be asked, Who am I? v. 20 Only John records the peoples reaction: 6:14 When the people saw the sign which He had performed, they said, This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world. THE INTERRUPTION OF PLANS You can imagine the excitement of the disciples in their detailed accounts You can also understand the possibility of needing a retreat Another possible reason is the chronology in Matthew Jesus has just received the report of the death of John the Baptist They headed just outside of Galilee It was not easy to withdraw from people very long, especially in a country as small as Galilee Mark 6:31 people kept coming and restricted even the time to eat Bethsaida: northeast corner of the Sea of Galilee (Mark 6:45; another Bethsaida farther west) Seeking to provide a place for spiritual, physical, mental rest A boat trip of about 9 miles The area is described as a desert but probably meaning secluded Mark 6:39 they sat on green grass 9:11 the crowds followed Mark 6:33 ran on foot and got there ahead of them John 6:2 they followed because they saw the signs which He was performing They were not interested in the message but the miracles People are no different today. Everybody loves a circus These people were so excited John 6:15 Jesus perceived they were going to take Him by force and make Him king People love a show; thrill seekers When really they are superficial and shallow Such a mentality drew a rebuke from Jesus; John 6:26, 27 Even with plans interrupted, Jesus showed compassion Mark 6:34 they were sheep without a shepherd Jesus welcomed them: He spoke and healed Repeated the message of the kingdom: the message did not change You must come as a little child (18:17) It is hard for the wealthy to enter the kingdom (18:24) Mark 1:15 repent and believe the gospel Jesus demonstrated why He came: 19:10 Jesus redeemed the time; Ephesians 5:16 He knew His time in Galilee was coming to an end He has been here for 18 months and mostly found rejection TRANSITION Plans get interrupted; even vacations It is still about people The only kingdom that will prevail in this world is the kingdom that is not of this world! Every week in Sunday School is your opportunity to give a report of what God did, what you learned, what you experienced. THE LIMITATION OF RESOURCES John s gospel: Jesus initiated the discussion by asking Philip, Where can we buy bread so these people can eat? John 6 the timing is at Passover so many folks on the road Philip does some quick calculating: we don t have that in the treasury Would take enough money 7-8 months wages (200 denarii) It has been some months since the wedding in Cana Possible chronology: The question could have been asked earlier in the day and gave Philip time to discuss with the other apostles They are discussing the dilemma while Jesus is teaching Now the day is drawing to a close to wear away to slant or slope; to bow down; to decline 12 we see the practical side of the apostles Considering the time (evening), the place (desert) Here was a problem beyond their ability Wiersbe: Jesus had just sent them out to meet the needs of the people and now the people have come to them and the disciples want to send them out. The disciples were actually commanding Jesus to send them away They are not yet people oriented; this was a major inconvenience To Jesus they are sheep without a shepherd; to the apostles they are a nuisance John 6: Jesus knew what He was going to do The disciples never looked beyond their own resources With the exception of the Resurrection, this is the only miracle the Holy Spirit recorded in all four gospels. MacArthur calls it the climax of the Lord s ministry Man s inadequacy; Christ s adequacy God wants to give me His sufficiency for my insufficiency to meet the needs of the world How many of us would have requested to turn the boat around when upon arriving the crowd was already there? How about Jesus request: you give them something to eat v. 13 We are talking about feeding the whole community of London. At their disposal is five loaves; two fishes Jesus could have called down manna from heaven Jesus could have spoken and a loave of bread could have appeared with each person. Much more efficient than distributing. Angels could have been used to give the food out Jesus chose people to meet the needs of people What kind of people does God use? Inadequate, tired, spent Also evident in their request: send them away Steven Cole: Jesus doesn t work through people who choose to serve. He works through His servants. Servants don t volunteer to serve. . . . Servants serve when they re tired, emotionally drained, busy, and lacking in adequate resources. Servants serve because they re under obligation to their master. It is not I ll serve Jesus someday when I get my affairs in order Or I ll give someday when I get my finances in order Or I ll serve but I am just too stressed right now We serve by yielding our inadequacies to God. What is the key? Place our inadequacies in the hands of God ____ Miracle haters: looking for some logical explanation Suggest the miracle took place in people s hearts Once the food started to distribute, they produced what had been hidden the meal was ceremonial; their participation was in a symbolic way satisfying their spiritual hunger what you learn from the haters is that it takes more faith to believe the lies _____ TRANSITION: INTERRUPTION OF PLANS; LIMITATION OF RESOURCES PROVISION OF UNDERSTANDING Mark 6:40 Jesus had the people sit in an orderly manner Disciples did not balk; did not ask why? What s the point? 9:15 no insight given as to what the disciples expected Jesus to do 9:16 Jewish grace: Blessed are Thou, Jehovah our God, King of the universe, who bringest forth bread from the earth. He blessed, broke aorist He gave present; continued to give to the disciples The miraculous multiplication of bread in His hands Jesus does not demand what we do not possess. He only requires the disciples He can work through He manufactures; we distribute 17 miracle accomplished: They did eat and were all filled Little is much when God is in it Moses stick. David s rock. Elijah s mantle/cloak. Widows jar/oil. Samson s donkey jawbone. A lil lad s lunch... Your money in the offering. Your prayer. Your individual time. Here was a direct challenge to their faith They had just completed a challenging task of preaching about the kingdom, healing the sick, casting out demons Now it is feeding the crowds Disciples learned they were still thinking naturally, not supernaturally They saw 5 loaves, 2 fishes physically, not with the eyes of faith We walk by faith, not by sight God s provision always begins with what we have Requires wisdom on our part in how we use it WWW: "The first step is not to measure our resources, but to determine God s will and trust Him to meet the need." Jesus commanded You feed them Jeremiah 32:17 Disciples learned Jesus can provide Disciples learned the wisdom of economy; gathering of fragments Each disciple went home with a doggy bag *We learn that in giving what we have, there will not only be enough for those to whom we minister, but something left over for us. Perhaps even more than when we started FEEDING THE FIVE THOUSAND Luke 9:10-17 THE INTERRUPTION OF PLANS 9:11 the crowds followed John 6:2 They were not interested in the message but the miracles Mark 6:34 they were sheep without a shepherd It is still about people THE LIMITATION OF RESOURCES Here was a problem beyond their ability To Jesus they are sheep without a shepherd; to the apostles they are a nuisance The disciples never looked beyond their own resources Man s inadequacy; Christ s adequacy God wants to give me His sufficiency for my insufficiency to meet the needs of the world Jesus chose people to meet the needs of people We serve by yielding our inadequacies to God. PROVISION OF UNDERSTANDING The miraculous multiplication of bread in His hands Jesus does not demand what we do not possess. Disciples learned they were still thinking naturally, not supernaturally God s provision always begins with what we have Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(81).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 8/19/2023 7:37:38 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FILL IN THE BLANK Luke 9:18-22 INTRODUCTION One of the most scarry greetings a teacher of Junior High, High School, or college can use is take out a piece of paper and put your name and date at the top. This is a pop quiz. You knew the quiz would cover reading material you did not read. OR a home work you did not do. OR yesterday s lecture that you missed. The ONLY time such an introduction to class did not bother me was in college, the last day of the quarter, when 80% of the class was absent. Those of us attending received some kind of extra credit just be turning in an autographed paper. And then for some of us, any type of exam, whether true/false; multiple choice; blue book; fill in the blank was a great cause of concern. Jesus is going to give a test. The lessons have been taught; evidence has been presented; the experiments have examined. Now it is time to discover how well the students, the learners, have received the material. Have they grasped the gravity of the lessons? Do they comprehend the significance of their observations? In short, have they really been paying attention? Luke has taken on the task of presenting to Theophilus an accurate account of the life of Jesus of Nazareth. His gospel provides incredible testimony as to Jesus identity. The disciples, the learners, have been immediate recipients of the testimony both by word and works. But have they really learned anything. So, Jesus gives them a fill-in-the-blank exam. FILL IN THE HISTORY Cf. Matthew 16; Mark 8 Luke leaves out a lot of material leading up to the question I think a quick survey will add to the weightiness of the exam. Matthew 14:22-16:12 and Mark 6:45-8:26 Jesus sent the disciple by boat back to Capernaum while He dismissed the crowds Jesus went up mountain to pray (Matt. 14:22, 23) A storm hits the disciples; Jesus walks on water; Peter walks on water (Matt. 14:24-33) John 6:32-66 reveal the events of the day after feeding 5,000 plus 6:26, 27 which we read last week He then teaches that He is the bread of life which brings a strong reaction resulting in a major withdrawal of the followers: 6:66 People were following Him but not coming to Him Criticized for not following tradition of washing hands (Mark 7:1-23) Went out of Jewish territory and healed daughter of Syrophoenician woman (Matt. 15:21-28) Went south and east to Decapolis; healed one deaf and dumb (15:29-31) Matt. 15:32-39a; feeding 4,000 Warned against the leaven of the Pharisses; (16:4-12) Healed a blind man; (Mark 8:22-26) And now, we find ourselves at Caesarea Philippi Four things occurred: Encouraged a formal confession of His person Makes known His coming humiliation and death Announces for the first time His second coming Glorified in His transfiguration FILL IN BY THE MASSES 9:19 Human opinion Question: Who do people say that I am? By far the most important question anyone will ever answer We have to ask and answer the right questions The right question can be crucial for your well-being When the temperature soared to 120 degrees, a missionary in South America was tempted to cool off with a swim in the local river, but he was leery because of the man-eating fish. The locals assured him, though, that piranhas only bite people while the fish are swimming in schools, which they never did in that part of the river. So each afternoon for the rest of the summer, the missionary enjoyed cooling off in the river. Months later he heard reports that a local fisherman had fallen out of his boat and had not been found. Alarmed, he asked his neighbors if perhaps the man had been eaten by piranhas. Oh, no, they assured him. Only while swimming in schools do piranhas bite people, and they never swim in schools around here. But why not around here? the missionary asked. Oh, the neighbor casually replied, they never swim in schools where there are alligators. (Adapted from Reader s Digest [7/96], p. 48.) You got to ask the right questions Acts 15: Must a man be circumcised to be saved? Acts 16: what must I do to be saved? believe in Jesus The wrong answer to either question is the difference between heaven and hell Presenting the wrong answer is preaching another gospel and worthy of condemnation. There are thousands of people who believe the Bible is God s Word They seek to obey its moral standards They believe in Jesus virgin birth They believe He sacrificed His life for their sins (Watchtower Society, 1996) Yet these people deny the deity of Jesus of headed to hell Luke has repeatedly emphasized the identity of Jesus 2:11 the announcement of His birth 2:25, 36 the announcements of Simeon and Anna 3:15-17 John the Baptist pointed people to Jesus 4:1-13 Satan knew who He was 4:34, 41 the demons announced who He was 5:21 the healing of the paralytic; who can forgive sins but God 7:49 the same question is asked 8:25 the disciples ask who is this? 9:9 Herod asks the same question We have to ask and answer the right question And none more important than this one Who do you say that I am? It is crucial for each person to answer correctly Theologically, this is the most important question and answer in the Scriptures There are several ramifications to consider: The question has one right answer It is not Jesus is however you conceive Him to be Jesus is always accepting and loving Jesus is not whatever you want Him to be How you feel about Jesus does not change who He is The answer is not based on feelings or personal opinions The answer is based on objective revealed truth In our postmodern world, truth is based on our own experiences Therefore, it is not verifiable If it s true for you, it s true If my experience is different than yours, one is right and the other wrong They can both be true according to popular thought So, to say you are born again can mean a variety of things (Pastor James Singleton) Some people believe they are born again in the waters of baptism Some are born again at their confirmation To others, it is every time they attend mass It can mean whatever you want it to mean who do you say Jesus is? There is one correct answer and a multitude of incorrect answers It is not a matter of personal truth, but objective revelation The Question Divides People Peter and the disciples went against public opinion. The opinion of the Roman government They did not care as long as you also affirmed Caesar as Lord Bumper sticker: If you don t believe in abortion, don t have one Meaning: find for you, but don t preach to me; don t judge me The opinion of the Jewish crowd Their notions might have been flattering but inadequate Much of the Christian crowd today is all about tolerance They use Jesus to endorse their sins Jesus Gets Us The opinion of the Religious Leaders Most formidable opposition Disciples were not educated; not public influencers Today you are standing against scholars who have taken upon themselves to determine what Jesus really said. TRANSITION: the answer is one of objective truth and will divide people The Question s Answer will Grow on You There will be a deepening level of correct understanding Peter was not exactly on the same page Jesus was To Peter: You are the One to sit on the throne of David ruling with a rod of iron. Totally correct according to Peter s understanding To Jesus: (9:22) the Anointed One of God to be the sin-bearer for the sins of the world He would not die a helpless victim He was in sovereign control Peter later grasped this: Acts 2:23, 24 The joy of the Christian life is growing in the grace and knowledge of the Lord Jesus Christ. FILL IN BY THE STUDENTS 9:20, 21 You is emphatic: But you, who do you say that I am? 2nd person plural; He is talking to all of them Every one must answer the question Peter is again the spokes-person Acknowledging Jesus as the Anointed One; Christos; the Messiah of God Their answer was true, but does not imply they understood in what sense He was the Messiah We already spoke of how the answer will grow on them. The Question Requires a Personal Response of Faith and Obedience 22 Warning not to tell Judgment on the people Nothing to thwart His reason for coming Jesus answer was not what the disciples expected or even wanted to hear They were thinking power and dominion; not suffering and death Jesus was sent to deal with man s sin issue not to make everyone happy The crowed was ready to make Him king; John 6:15 God s sovereign plan was the cross; not only for Jesus but us (v. 23) A. B. Bruce: For the whole aim of Satanic policy is to get self-interest recognized as the chief aim of man The self-interest of Jesus would be to avoid the cross Satan would have triumphed But Jesus came to do the will of the Father (9:22) All centers on the word must The necessity of obedience to the Father s will His coming death is necessary and part of God s plan We who follow Him must also walk in the way of the cross Trusting and obeying Conclusion Who do you say Jesus is? FILL IN THE BLANK Luke 9:18-22 FILL IN THE HISTORY Matthew 14:22-16:12 and Mark 6:45-8:26 People were following Him but not coming to Him FILL IN BY THE MASSES 9:19 The most important question anyone will ever answer Presenting the wrong answer is preaching another gospel and worthy of condemnation. Luke has repeatedly emphasized the identity of Jesus The Question has One Right Answer Jesus is not whatever you want Him to be The answer is based on objective revealed truth The Question Divides People The opinion of the Roman government The opinion of the Jewish crowd The opinion of the Religious Leaders The Question s Answer will Grow on You The joy of the Christian life is growing in the grace and knowledge of the Lord Jesus Christ. FILL IN BY THE STUDENTS 9:20, 21 Their answer was true, but does not imply they understood in what sense He was the Messiah The Question Requires a Personal Response of Faith and Obedience 22 Warning not to tell Judgment on the people Nothing to thwart His reason for coming God s sovereign plan was the cross The necessity of obedience to the Father s will Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(82).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/2/2023 12:30:37 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A STAGGERING ANNOUNCEMENT Luke 9:22-26 Cf. Matthew 16:21-26; Mark 8:34-38 INTRODUCTION A major change is about to take place Matt. 16:21 from that time Turning point; new emphasis this is what to expect Definition: deeply shocking, astonishing It is the doctor saying you need to make some life changing decisions or your life will be cut drastically short. The auto technician saying it would probably be cheaper to buy another car. You get a call about the sudden death of someone. From Thursday s news: Gene Hamilton, America First Legal Vice President and General Counsel. The evidence accumulating against the Biden family s malfeasance is staggering. It s astonishing how the place of power controls people Why won t Diane Feinstein and Mitch McConnell go home? Other things can be staggering The number of shoes in your wife s closet The number of tools in his workshop Inflation, house prices, car prices, college education The diamond engagement ring. But you love her so got to have it Diamonds are a girl s best friend. A rare, special jewel. A man s best friend is a dog. Some plentiful we have them neutered Staggering news: have you ever been told you d better sit down for this? This news was so staggering that Peter took Jesus aside and rebuked Him. (Matt. 16:22) STANDARD 9:22 No mention of the specifics of the mode of His death elders, chief priests, scribes make up of Sanhedrin Rejection by this court meant trial for a capital offense and a formal condemnation to death This suggests the gravest king of justice: judicial murder Jesus left the disciples no doubt that being the Messiah meant the Cross This is the first Passion Prediction in Luke (Luke 9:43-45; 18:31-34) Jesus includes speaking of His cross as the standard for discipleship He is about to present to His true followers The title Son of Man was often used by Jesus and frequently in the context of suffering. 84x in 80 verses; mostly by Jesus Matthew records Jesus used it in framing the question, Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am? Must present tense, a continual necessity; an obligation, a non-optional absolute necessity Definition: comes from a word meaning to bind A binding obligation upon someone coming from a decree of God Must do the Father s will 2:49 Must preach 4:43 Appointment with tax collector 19:5 Scriptures must be fulfilled 24:44 Remain in heaven until appointed time Acts 3:21 Believers suffer for the kingdom s sake Acts 9:16:14:22 Must believe in Jesus for salvation Acts 4:12; 16:30, 31 Jesus to Nicodemus: you must be born again This has been God s plan all along Peter; Acts 2:23 the predetermined plan and foreknowledge of God The fulfillment of Isaiah 53:4-10 Elements of His suffering include the hatred of the Jewish leaders, the agony in Gethsemane, the betrayal by Judas, mockery, a brutal whipping, the crown of thorns. rejected a technical term including a careful legal scrutiny It implies the hierarchy would consider Jesus claims but decide against Him This Living Stone in the Person of God the Son became incarnate, lived for thirty-three years in the midst of Israel, offered Himself as its Messiah, was examined by official Israel for the purpose of approving Him as its Messiah, and then repudiated because He was not what official Israel wanted in a Messiah. Rejected by the Sanhedrin but by Luke 23 all the crowd joined in What a contrast to the scene John records in heaven Revelation 5:11, 12 We must be reminded that every time we willfully sin, we are deciding against Him What a commentary on a totally depraved human heart and be killed By the hands of Roman soldiers By the hatred of the Jewish leaders Our own personal sins But Jesus included a note about the resurrection; the rest of the story The Father s Amen to the Son s It is Finished be raised up a divine passive with a futuristic intent; simply meaning His resurrection is so certain that it could be spoken of as if it was already accomplished This is the gospel: the death, burial, resurrection of Jesus SOBERING 9:23 The new Standard now required a response For The starting point for discipleship (MacArthur) The necessary components of saving faith (MacArthur) Must Jesus bear the cross alone? He said to all Mark 8:34 in brought in the crowd with His disciples If any man will come desire, wish, choose, prefer Present tense, active voice A desire which is energized by the Holy Spirit because no one on his own seeks after God A desire planted by God which must be acted on by the human will The interaction of God s sovereignty and man s responsibility which is a mystery The crowd is together; all men are to hear Jesus just explained His mission He begins to explain what it means to follow Him Jesus had to die but so must His followers What He goes on to explain in verses 23-27 is not physical death He describes a figurative death, a death to selfish tendency in every human heart, a selfishness prompted by sin It would be far easier to die physically to sin than to kill the sin principle which is in me We are called to daily cast off self-reliance This is not a secret teaching: all are to hear But it is a teaching that is foreign to the church in the US This is not an elective class but mandatory And not just a one-time message from Jesus; it is repeated We will see it again in Luke CHS: There are no crown-wearers in heaven who were not cross-bearers here below Vance Havner -- What our Lord said about cross-bearing and obedience is not in fine type. It is in bold print on the face of the contract. A W Tozer: The old cross slew men; the new cross entertains them. The old cross condemned; the new cross amuses. The old cross destroyed confidence in the flesh; the new cross encourages it. A STAGGERING ANNOUNCEMENT Luke 9:22-26 STANDARD 9:22 This is the first Passion Prediction in Luke Must absolute necessity rejected a technical term including a careful legal scrutiny We must be reminded that every time we willfully sin, we are deciding against Him SOBERING 9:23 The starting point for discipleship A desire which is energized by the Holy Spirit because no one on his own seeks after God A desire planted by God which must be acted on by the human will Jesus had to die but so must His followers We are called to daily cast off self-reliance This is not an elective class but mandatory A W Tozer: The old cross slew men; the new cross entertains them. The old cross condemned; the new cross amuses. The old cross destroyed confidence in the flesh; the new cross encourages it. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(83).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/8/2023 11:00:45 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A STAGGERING ANNOUNCMENT Luke 9:22-26 Cf. Matthew16:21-26; Mark 8:34-38 INTRODUCTION In 1982, Rev. Robert Schuller wrote a book entitled, Self Esteem-The New Reformation . Schuller was heavily influenced by Norman Vincent Peale s (1898-1993) book, The Power of Positive Thinking and Dale Carnegie s book, How to Win Friends and Influence People . He came to believe his own approach to ministry was overly theological and uninspiring. He determined he would adopt the spirit, style, strategy, and substance of a therapist in the pulpit. So he established a ministry based on what people would accept in a church. Thus began what has come to be know as the seeker friendly movement. Bill Hybels and Rick Warren are two of Schuller s disciples. Other names of note are Peter Drucker and Stephen Furtick. Growing a church became more about marketing and managing from a corporation style. The result was mega churches and establishing a network of over 1,600 hundreds churches who have sought to copy the same strategies. Rather than preaching the whole counsel of God , messages were down to 20 minutes of how to s. How to have a better marriage, how to improve your finances, how to get along with your teenager, even how to have better sex. And how can you argue when these churches are growing? So what if the message is watered down? So what if they do not see God as transcendent, holy, sovereign, righteous, jealous, wrathful? There will be time for that; we just have to get them in the door. In the meantime we will dazzle them with our psycho-babble. Who doesn t want to feel good about themselves. The contemporary evangelical gospel is a gospel of self-fulfillment, self-help. Jesus wants you well, wants you free from debt, wants you happy. Schuller wrote: It is precisely at this point that classical theology has erred in its insistence that theology be God-centered and not man-centered. This master plan of God is designed around the deepest needs of human beings. And what are those needs? Self-dignity, self-respect, self-worth, self-esteem. The pearl of great price is genuine self-respect and self-esteem. He further writes, If we follow God s plan as faithfully as we can, we will feel good about ourselves. It would be to our detriment to fail to see how this has influenced Bible believing churches today. In music, singing off the wall, powerpoint, dress, lack of expository preaching, and emphasis on numbers, we can get lazy and settle for less than God s best. All having their roots in a man-centered theology. We must ask ourselves the question, how have we allowed the world to squeeze us into its mold? And before you throw out your favorite verses in support, am I proof texting or keeping in context? That is why I said last week, Luke 9:23 does not preach well in churches in the US today. John Stott: large numbers of people have covered themselves with a decent, but thin, veneer of Christianity. They have allowed themselves to become somewhat involved; enough to be respectable but not enough to be uncomfortable. Their religion is a great, soft cushion. It protects them from the hard unpleasantness of life, while changing its place and shape to suit their convenience.
