No wonder the cynics speak of hypocrites in the church and dismiss religion as escapism. The Purpose Statement of Grace Baptist Church The purpose of Grace Baptist Church is to develop believers in their relationship to the Lord Jesus Christ. To that end, we are committed to honor the Lord through the proclamation of Biblical truth as the standard for obedience, the Christian life, Biblical worship [God focused, not man pleasing; Christ-centered, not experience oriented; Holy Spirit empowered, not emotionally driven], and service to others. Looking at the result of a generation of seeker sensitive theology, what do we have? People in the pew, I mean chairs, that approve overwhelmingly abortion, recreational marijuana, homosexual marriage, cohabitation before marriage. They know a God of love, grace, and mercy but no acceptance of a God who is angry with the wicked every day ; a God of righteousness, justice, and wrath. They know Jesus loves me, but not he who does not believe is condemned already. A W Tozer: The old cross slew men; the new cross entertains them. The old cross condemned; the new cross amuses. The old cross destroyed confidence in the flesh; the new cross encourages it. THE NEW STANDARD: death to live THE SOBERING REALITY: required of me It is the starting point of discipleship. Three things required of disciples: Deny Himself; Take Up His Cross Daily, Follow Jesus The death here is figurative; a death to selfish tendency It would be one thing if this was a one-time teaching moment But it will be repeated First: Deny Himself The message is to anyone; more than the 12 are listening (Mark 8) Wishes: desires; energized by the Holy Spirit Anyone who comes is acting on the power of the Holy Spirit Deny to turn someone away, to refuse association; to disown him Here the one who is to be denied is self. I disown you Aorist Imperative: do it now, don t delay, urgent It may mean giving up certain earthly joys and selfish gratification Titus 2:12 e.g. Moses; Hebrews 11:24 reputation; refused to be called son of Pharaoh s daughter In Luther s 95 thesis, this is number 4 hatred of self which he says is essential to entrance into the kingdom of heaven MacArthur: "once you ve gone through self-denial, or self-hate, or self-loathing because you no longer want to associate with the sinner you are, you re that desperate. Then you are in position of honoring Jesus as Lord and Savior What is the secret to denying self? How? Titus 2:12 grace This is not some little slap on the wrist; It is an alteration of one s life Adrian Rogers: the Lord doesn t want you to dedicate yourself to Him; He wants you to deny yourself. If you don t deny yourself, you are going to deny Jesus. e.g. Peter; filled with self-confidence Illustration: some of you will remember the story of Mohammad Ali While seated on an airplane the flight attendant told him he would have to fasten his seatbelt. He replied, Superman don t need no seatbelt. She answered, Superman don t need to airplane either. This is the crucifixion of self; Galatians 2:20 Leaving behind temporal pursuits; live for eternal pleasure Yielding over the right to self-determination; live as Christ directs Put to death the idol of I walk by the Spirit and you will not fulfill the lust of the flesh Galatians 5:16 This is the basics of discipleship which if practiced today looks radical. Some descriptions of denying self John MacArthur Self denial becomes a life pattern. Now what does it mean as a Christian? Here are some practical things. 1. When you are not forgiven, or neglected, or purposely set aside, and you sting and hurt with the insult or oversight, but your heart is happy and you re content to be counted worthy to suffer for Christ, that s dying to self. 2. When your good is evil spoken of, when your wishes are crossed, your advice is disregarded, your opinions are ridiculed, and you refuse to let anger rise in your heart or even defend yourself, but take it all in patient, loyal silence, that is dying to self. 3. When you lovingly and patiently bear any disorder, any irregularity, or any annoyance, when you can stand face-to-face with foolishness, extravagance, spiritual insensitivity and endure it as Jesus endured it, that is dying to self. 4. When you re content with any food, any offering, any clothes, any climate, any society, any solitude, any interruption by the will of God, that is dying to self. 5. When you never care to refer to yourself, or to record your own good works, or seek commendation, when you can truly love to be unknown, that is dying to self. 6. When you see another brother prosper and have his needs met, and can honestly rejoice with him in spirit and feel no envy, nor even question God while your own needs are far greater and in desperate circumstances, that is dying to self. 7. When you can receive correction and reproof from one of less stature than yourself, and can humbly submit inwardly as well as outwardly, finding no rebellion or resentment rising up within your heart, that is dying to self. A Christian cannot afford to be self-indulgent It is following Jesus all the time in joyous self-denial How did the Sermon on the Mount begin? Blessed are the poor in spirit; blessed are the spiritually bankrupt Blessed are they that mourn over their spiritual bankruptcy Blessed are they who hunger and thirst after the things they cannot find in themselves which is true righteousness. Denying self; honoring Jesus in obedience Sometimes as simple as evaluating for what am I willing to miss church or better question; what am I willing to miss to attend church? Time, money A new study from Lifeway Research shows that an increasing share of American Protestant churchgoers believe that financial prosperity is part of God's plan for them and that giving more money to their church and charities will lead to more blessings from God. The study found that 52% of churchgoers say their church teaches that God will bless them if they donate more, marking a significant increase from 38% in 2017. It bears repeating: the cost of discipleship does not preach well in the US. But this is where true worship begins. Do you know when the first occasion of the word worship is in the Bible? Genesis 22; the sacrifice of Isaac Worship for Abraham involved the sacrifice of his son of promise Everything was wrapped up in Isaac. Do you now think the cost of discipleship is too high? Is this the God who saved you, redeemed you, sustains you? It was said two weeks ago that claiming Jesus as the Anointed One will grow on you. Peter came to understand, so can we. May God help us. A STAGGERING ANNOUNCMENT Luke 9:22-26 A W Tozer: The old cross slew men; the new cross entertains them. The old cross condemned; the new cross amuses. The old cross destroyed confidence in the flesh; the new cross encourages it. Death to selfish tendency Deny Himself The message is to anyone Deny to refuse association Titus 2:12 What is the secret to denying self? grace It is an alteration of one s life If you don t deny yourself, you are going to deny Jesus. This is the crucifixion of self; Galatians 2:20 Galatians 5:16 This is the basics of discipleship which if practiced today looks radical. A Christian cannot afford to be self-indulgent How did the Sermon on the Mount begin? Denying self - honoring Jesus in obedience This is where true worship begins Genesis 22 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(84).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/16/2023 7:21:57 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A STAGGERING ANNOUNCMENT Luke 9:22-26 A W Tozer: The old cross slew men; the new cross entertains them. The old cross condemned; the new cross amuses. The old cross destroyed confidence in the flesh; the new cross encourages it. THE NEW STANDARD 9:22 death to live THE SOBERING REALITY 9:23 required of me It is the starting point of discipleship. Deny Yourself : give up the right to self-determination; live as Christ directs Treasure and value Jesus more than yourself, your comforts, your aspirations Put to death the Idol of I Philippians 2 Second: Take Up Your Cross Die. Daily activity Self wants to lead daily. Die We are not instructed to try (to do better) but die Not normal, not natural, but necessary It is a slow, painful death The command is again one of urgency; do it now, don t delay The condemned man was required to carry his own cross to the place of execution Pick it up and Walk the narrow road of renunciation of the old self It is not easy The old Adam is screaming out for the pleasures of this world Self cannot and will not follow Jesus The disciples knew about crucifixions; they had seen plenty The cross was a horrible means of capital punishment No Roman citizen could be crucified reserved for their enemies At times, the roads around Jerusalem were lined with hundreds of crosses bearing dead and dying men, their bodies bloated in the sun, surrounded by flies, covered with maggots. Estimates say up to 30,000 Jews were crucified during the time of Jesus What are our crosses? Many people think when they experience difficulties, that is tantamount to the "cross" but that is not necessarily the case Our crosses come from and are proportionate to our dedication to Christ. Difficulties are not an indication of cross-bearing Kent Hughes: "What are our crosses? They are not simply trials or hardships. Some think of a nutty boss or an unfair teacher or a bossy mother-in-law as a "cross." But they are not. Neither can we properly call an illness or a handicap a cross. A cross results from specifically walking in Christ's steps, embracing his life. It comes from bearing disdain because we are following the narrow way of Jesus Christ, "the way and the truth and the life" (John 14:6 )....Our crosses come from and are proportionate to our dedication to Christ. Difficulties do not indicate cross-bearing, though difficulties for Christ's sake do. The question is this: Do we have any difficulties because we are closely following Christ? Why daily? Why can t it be a one-time forever decision? Because I face a new challenge every morning I wake. Fallen flesh is still depraved flesh, but not dead Every day I am prompted to live life my way I have to choose to do it Jesus way There is never a day off; no furlough, no vacation, no break from battle Galatians 5:16 walk by the Spirit and you will not fulfill the desire of the flesh It does not say there we will not have the desire 5:17 For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one other, that you may not do the things that you desire. There are Two characteristics to a Christian s cross It is a voluntary commitment a new vision of self It is an act of obedience a new vision of the world s needs Will always include suffering, always leads to rejection by the world, always involve obedience to God We forget the shock of His words Jesus was calling men to come and be willing to die in His service He was calling his followers to heroic effort in the face of certain opposition, suffering, pain, and death. John Piper: In other words, treasure Me more than your own comfort and your own safety. The opposite of self-denial is the idol of self-gratification, and the opposite of cross-bearing is the idol of self-preservation.....if you would save your life, you must lose it, and if you would follow Jesus, you must take up your cross daily. The great tragedy of much contemporary Christianity is that the cross is safely relegated to the distant past. And practically what it means is that Jesus was soaked in blood so that I can soak in a Jacuzzi. And the bigger the tub, the more we honor the cross so goes the prosperity gospel... To take up a cross is the ultimate in self-denial; the utmost in self-sacrfice; the death to all forms of self-seeking AW Tozer: what crucified with Christ means The one who is crucified is only facing one direction He has said his good-byes, he is not going back He has no further plans of his own Third: Follow Me the most common commandment of Jesus To follow is a long and continuous action A lifestyle, habitual practice of life A constant obedience in thought, word, and action This is the working definition of a disciple of Jesus How well does it describe your life? 19x in the gospels; all red letters This is simple enough and can be understood by anyone who was a child Did you ever play follow the leader ? You are to do everything the leader does. No one must shrink from going where the leader has gone May require a thankless job, becoming involved in a difficult relationship, or just choosing a hard place Anything less is superficial lip service This is not what some refer to as second level Christians What is that? Those who consider commands as in verse 23 for those who are already saved. This is growing to another level in your life with Christ. It separates salvation from obedience; Jesus as Savior and Lord You are saved. (unbiblical phrases like: ask Jesus into your heart, receive Jesus) That is not the way Jesus gave an invitation, by the way Now it is time to get serious; time to obey; time to self-deny Jesus is not some deeper life teacher Salvation and discipleship are not two different things SUPERIOR CALLING 9:24-26 For three verses of explanation and clarification you think the Standard is too high death then life you think the Sober Reality is more than you can handle Deny self, take up cross daily, follow Me The disciples had two choices: they could choose a temporary gain, but the loss would be irreparable they could choose minor loss, but the gain would be eternal It is the choice between what is the maximum I can do or what is the minimum I can do It is the willingness to pay a high price for something of great value What is my value system? How would others evaluate my value system? How important is this present life to you? Speaking of eternal values The time may come when you can save your life by abandoning your faith The end result of denying Him is to be cast away The language of eternal punishment The question is not what is the safe thing to do? but what is the right thing to do SUMMARY A verse on the final judgment Jesus has been speaking of loyalty Mark 8: ashamed of Me and My words in the adulterous and sinful generation If we are true to Him now, He will be true to us in eternity. If, by our lives, we disown Him, the day will come when He will disown us. It is possible to gain all we set our hearts upon, only to awaken one morning to find that we have missed the most important things of all. Nothing material can compensate for the loss of eternal life As a born again believer in the Lord Jesus Christ You are not your own; you have been redeemed; purchased; bought CONCLUSION There are no cheap seats when you follow Jesus It is easy to say the Jesus died for us so that we might not die A more complete statement is this: Jesus died that we might die. Jesus died for sin so that we might die to sin. Following Jesus is to be a passionate pursuit To follow Jesus is a worshiper who worships at any cost, above the cost of personal relationships, above the cost of personal reputation, above the cost of personal realization. LUKE 9:23 SEE PRINCIPLES OF RECKONING (download files folder) A STAGGERING ANNOUNCMENT Luke 9:22-26 THE NEW STANDARD 9:22 THE SOBERING REALITY 9:23 Deny Yourself Take Up Your Cross We are not instructed to try but die Walk the narrow road of renunciation Our crosses come from and are proportionate to our dedication to Christ. Do we have any difficulties because we are closely following Christ? Every day I am prompted to live life my way Two characteristics to a Christian s cross It is a voluntary commitment It is an act of obedience The one who is crucified is only facing one direction He has said his good-byes, he is not going back He has no further plans of his own Follow Me A constant obedience in thought, word, and action This is not what some refer to as second level Christians Salvation and discipleship are not two different things SUPERIOR CALLING 9:24-26 The disciples had two choices: they could choose a temporary gain, but the loss would be irreparable they could choose minor loss, but the gain would be eternal What is my value system? Nothing material can compensate for the loss of eternal life Jesus died for sin so that we might die to sin. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(85).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/23/2023 11:44:06 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A STAGGERING EVENT Luke 9:27-36 INTRODUCTION The glory of God is manifested in the summation of all His attributes Exodus 33:18 Moses show me your glory Moses only got a glimpse of the backside. the manifestation of God s glory was accompanied by supernatural fire, thick clouds, and a great quaking of the earth. Exodus 19:18 the giving of Law on Mt. Sinai; a glory so magnificent Moses himself shone The cloud protected wandering Israel in the wilderness during the day The pillar of fire guided them at night 1 Kings 8 the dedication of the temple Ezekiel 1:26-28 high above on the throne was a figure like that of a man. I saw that from what appeared to be his waist up he looked like glowing metal, as if full of fire, and that from there down he looked like fire; and brilliant light surrounded him. Like the appearance of a rainbow in the clouds on a rainy day, so was the radiance around him. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the Lord Revelation 4, 5 a look into the throne room of God Revelation 21:23 The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light, and the Lamb is its lamp The past weeks had us looking at the Staggering Announcement from Jesus Not only predicting His own death but the death of His followers His death was literal; ours is figurative but might become literal The Confession is followed by The Announcement is followed by The Event The high points; the pivotal points; perhaps the most significant points of Jesus earthly ministry aside from the cross itself PREDICTION STATED 9:27 Numerous interpretations: The liberals state is was a Mistaken Prediction stupid ramblings of irreverent men Destruction of Jerusalem How could that represent a coming kingdom? Establishment of the Church kingdom more spectacular than that Resurrection and Ascension the kingdom is about His return, not His departure But I tell you the truth speaking of a contrast; but a contrast to what v. 26 those ashamed when He comes in His glory a reference to His Second Coming in power and glory as He described in Mt 24:30 when all "will see the SON OF MAN COMING ON THE CLOUDS OF THE SKY with power and great glory." shall not double negative; shall in no way; absolutely will not taste of death colloquial expression for dying; full expression of death Hebrews 2:9 "by the grace of God He (Jesus) might taste death for everyone." Context is key in interpretation Matthew 16:28 "till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom" Mark 9:1 "till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power" Each statement just before the Transfiguration Kingdom properly defined: royalty; sovereign; royal splendor Jesus is making a prediction like a prophet How as a prophet identified in the OT? His prophecy must be true Many times their predictions were far off in the future But they also predicted things to come soon which did happen Since the early predictions were true, the latter predictions were also certain to take place The disciples had already experienced aspects of the kingdom Had witnessed and even participated in casting out demons, healings, feedings But nothing rises the level of coming in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels. The second coming of Jesus is yet to come. How do we know it will really happen? We have the Transfiguration How do we know Jesus is who He says He is? The Transfiguration THE PRINCIPLE SITE 9:28 Climbing The exact location is not certain. Some possibilities Mt. Tabor: not exactly high at 1,843 and also has a fortified town occupying the top Mt. Hermon: Gentile territory north of the sea of Galilee Mt. Meron: 12 miles NW of Capernaum; 4,000 peak, highest in Galilee The thing to learn here is the location is not important or God would have identified it for us Timing: a quick note about differences in the synoptics Luke says 8 days ; Matthew and Mark say 6 Luke includes the day Jesus made the prediction and it taking place Matthew and Mark only include the days between Just different ways of a keeping time Praying In light of the pronouncement of death, did Jesus need some strength and encouragement from above?? Observing Why Peter, James, and John A select group from the 12 All three had their own weaknesses Peter had protested violently against the Cross James, John were throne seekers; Mark 10:37 Their faith needed strengthening for the troubled times ahead All three were to have positions of leadership Peter spoke on behalf of the 12; led in opening the gospel to Jews and Gentiles John was the disciple who Jesus loved John 13:23; received the Revelation James early church leader in Jerusalem; would be the first martyr Acts 12:20 The only three present at the raising of Jairus daughter; 8:51- The only three present in the garden; Matthew 26 WWW: These three occasions remind me of Philippians 3:10 , "That I may know Him [the Transfiguration], and the power of His resurrection [raising the girl], and the fellowship of His sufferings [in the Garden]." The three fulfilled an OT requirement Deuteronomy 19:15 A single witness shall not rise up against a man on account of any iniquity or any sin which he has committed; on the evidence of two or three witnesses a matter shall be confirmed. Their testimonies John 1:14 (ASV) And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us (and we beheld his glory, glory as of the only begotten from the Father), full of grace and truth. 2 Peter 1:16-17 (WEB) For we did not follow cunningly devised fables, when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty. For he received from God the Father honor and glory, when the voice came to him from the Majestic Glory, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased." A STAGGERING EVENT Luke 9:27-36 The Confession is followed by The Announcement is followed by The Event PREDICTION STATED 9:27 Mistaken Prediction Destruction of Jerusalem Establishment of the Church Resurrection and Ascension shall not double negative taste of death colloquial expression for dying Context is key in interpretation THE PRINCIPLE SITE 9:28 Climbing Timing Praying Observing All three had their own weaknesses All three had positions of leadership Philippians 3:10 All three fulfilled an OT requirement Deuteronomy 19:15 John 1:14 2 Peter 1:16-17 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(86).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 9/30/2023 11:07:03 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message A STAGGERING EVENT Luke 9:26-36 INTRODUCTION The Great Confession of the Person of Jesus by Peter is followed by The Staggering Announcement of Jesus coming death and the grounds for discipleship is followed by This Staggering Event of the Transfiguration. Jesus took three out of His 12 disciples who we recognize as the Inner Circle up the mountain with Him. Last week we identified the character flaws each of them possessed but also identified the significant roles each had in the early church. It appears that Jesus wanted to give them a greater understanding of who He is in preparation of those roles. They had only known Him in His physical body, they now could see Him in His exalted state, though they did not fully understand all they were seeing. John and Peter both gave written testimony as to their experience. John 1:14; 2 Peter 1:16-18 Unfortunately, we live in a day when people are more interested in which mountain Jesus is on than what took place there. You have heard it said that some people are so heavenly minded they are no earthly good. But the real truth is most people are so earthly minded they are no heavenly good. Colossians 3:1, 2 We are going to see here that Peter is still resisting the prediction of Jesus coming death. Like Peter, John, and James, we must come down off the mountain to deal with difficult situations, but we will deal with them more effectively if we have seen the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. Philippians 2; Hebrews 1 PREVIEW SCENE 29 Change in Countenance in the act of praying the appearing of His face altered Prayer changes things It will change you. Let me suggest that prayer will change your countenance, too. The power of prayer can replace confusion with peace, fear with faith, consternation with confidence and courage, discouragement with dedication and determination, and hopelessness with hope. We pray for our enemies. We pray for help to forgive. Communion with God is essential for a transformed life. Jesus took on human flesh and lived in total dependence on the Father to show us how He willingly accepted the limitations of humanity Was He praying for His upcoming suffering? Was He praying that God would permit the transformation for the benefit of Peter, John, and James? His countenance was altered His face became different A quick lesson in Greek. The Greek has two words that can be translated different or another One meaning different of the same kind; the other different of a different kind We see it especially in 1 Cor. 15 and Galatians 1:6, 7 The sun, moon, and stars are different but still of the same primary stuff 1 Corinthians 15:40 There are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another. A glory of a different king Galatians 1:6, 7 a different (eteros) gospel which is not another (allos) another of the same king Jesus face was different. It was a face but not of a human body. The disciples knew His human face; this was different The veil of His flesh became transparent and His deity shone through His face shone like the sun (Matthew 17:2) Matthew 17:2; Mark 9:2 eta ??f?? metamorphis, transfiguration describes a change in appearance that comes from within. In biology it is the changing of a caterpillar into a butterfly Weust: The translation expanded, thus reads, The manner of His outward expression was changed before them, that outward expression coming from and being truly representative of His inner nature. What the world saw was a peasant from Galilee, the son of a carpenter from Nazareth. That outward expression was changed. Out from within the inmost being of the Son of God, there shone that dazzling glory of the essence of Deity which He possesses co-eternally with God the Father and God the Spirit. It shone right through the clay walls of His humanity and through the clothing He wore. It was that same dazzling radiance which the angels saw in His preincarnate state (Phil. 2:6 ) The same word is used of us Romans 12:2 transformed by the renewing of your mind 2 Corinthians 3:18 changed into the same image from glory to glory Transfiguration is the goal of humanity. 1 Thessalonians 4:16, 17 1 Corinthians 15:51-57 The theological word for this is sanctification The process of becoming more like Jesus Prayer is a key factor in a transformed life. The glory that was always in the depth of Christ is allowed to shine through Not only a glance back (Phil. 2; John 17:5) but a look into the future [a good question is why Luke avoided using metamorphis; probably because of his intended audience and pagan religious myths] Remember v. 26 this is a preview of His second coming his clothing became white and dazzling His clothing whiter than anyone on earth can make it; Matthew, Mark white the color representative of what is beyond Hair - Revelation 1:14; horse Rev. 6:2; cloud Rev. 14:14; throne Rev. 20:11 The color of purity, divinity, holiness Isa. 1:18 though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be white as snow glistering flashing as the brilliance of lightening; 1x A form of the word is used of the angel rolling the stone away (Matt. 28); in the LXX of the lightnings on Mt. Sinai; Change in Company 9:30 Behold now look at this! A word like a divine highlighter Three quick things we learn: there is life after death; people are recognizable; communication We also remember the incorrect guesses as to Jesus identity vs. 18-20 Jesus had asked for man s opinion of who He was; now God expresses His opinion The appearance of Moses and Elijah Both men had previously conversed with God on a mountain top Moses: Exodus 31:18; Elijah: 1 Kings 19:8- Both had been shown God s glory Both had unusual departures from this earth Moses: died on Mt. Neob, a grave known only to God Elijah: taken up alive in a whirlwind Both were expected to return Moses: Deut. 18:15, 18 raise up a prophet like me Elijah: Luke 1:17; 9:8, 19 Moses was the law giver; Elijah was a prophet Moses dead over 1,400 years; Elijah about 900 years Conversation 9:31 Conversation was about Jesus coming death; decease ; exodus As the Exodus delivered Israel from the bondage of Egypt, Jesus, by His Exodus , would deliver His people from something far worse the bondage of sin. Here is talk of an exodus for the entire world An exodus that will only come after suffering On this mountain the Law of God and the grace of God converge Luke specifically mentions Jerusalem It is through there the redemptive acts of God take place Luke 1 the announcement in the temple to Zechariah Luke 2 the dedication of Jesus Luke 2 the yearly pilgrimage to the city Luke 9 Jesus final journey 9:51, 53; 13:33; 17:11; 18:31 Aside: it is interesting that Matthew only mentions Jerusalem 1x; Mark and John 0x PETER S SPEECH 9:32 And guess who was sleeping In their defense: were oppressed with sleep perfect passive Heavy, weighed down, overcome They were overcome; long climb up the mountain; no doubt night time Not an active choice on their part, but overwhelmed with sleepiness Before you jump on these men, how many blessings have you missed? No different than some of you on a Sunday morning Maybe no physical snoozing but spiritually Our spiritual drowsiness results in missed opportunities and defeats. The admonition to us: Romans 13:11; Eph. 5:14; 1 Thess. 5:6-8 The glorious scene before them must have left them dumbfounded in the departing Moses and Elijah were moving away it is good for us to be here and goes down hill from there His immediate thought is KINGDOM The idea of a tabernacle goes back to the OT of temporary settlement Feast of Booths: being presently celebrated; 6 months before Passover Will be celebrated during the Milenial Kingdom: Zechariah 14:16-21 He knew Elijah was associated with the kingdom Tabernacle is never used to describe common dwelling places of men such as tents of patriarchs or the people of Israel Tabernacle is a more poetic word and this characteristic led to is specific religious use Peter desired to contain the sacred atmosphere not knowing what he said we say foot in the mouth Foolish talking; Peter often spoke first and thought last Peter was placing Jesus on the same level as Moses and Elijah He wanted to maintain the spiritual high His desire to stay on the mountain is understandable But Peter is also bypassing the cross But the mountain experiences are only given to us for strength to minister daily to the needy world in the plains below. SOVEREIGN REBUTTAL 9:34 When God draws near to man, it is absolutely necessary that His glory should be veiled. No man can see His face and live The Jews regularly connected clouds with the presence of God Ex. 13:21,22; 14:19, 20; 40:35, 38; 1 Kings 8:10, 11 Moses met God in a cloud God came to the tabernacle in a cloud Solomon s temple dedication there was a cloud While Peter was still talking putting a stop to his stupidity You have been interrupted before or interrupted others Imagine being interrupted by God This is the cloud that passed by Moses as God hid him in the cleft of the rock The cloud that guided Israel out of Egypt The cloud that rested on the mercy seat in the Most Holy Place The last mention was over 600 years before: Ezekiel 11:23 Jesus will return in a cloud; 1 Thess. 4 overshadowed them partially blocking out the light afraid mild uneasiness to stark terror could refer to all six or a group of 3 Peter, James and John or Jesus, Moses, Elijah God spoke to the disciples out of the cloud This suggests the disciples were outside the cloud 9:35 God demanded that they hear only Jesus His words encouraged the disciples to accept all the teachings of Christ, including His predictions of His shameful death on Calvary 9:31 Jesus was to accomplish; fulfill the Sovereignty of God was at work start to finish Jesus is the Chosen One Luke 3:22 at His baptism Here is reaffirmation that Jesus is the Son of God The command is to listen to Him He far greater than Moses and Elijah Listen now and keep on listening Peter did not forget; 2 Peter 1:16-19 What kind of a listener are you? Hebrews 11:1-4 How does the glory of God influence your life? 1. When all my labors and trials are o er, And I am safe on that beautiful shore, Just to be near the dear Lord I adore, Will through the ages be glory for me. 2. When, by the gift of His infinite grace, I am accorded in heaven a place, Just to be there and to look on His face, Will through the ages be glory for me. * Refrain: Oh, that will be glory for me, Glory for me, glory for me, When by His grace I shall look on His face, That will be glory, be glory for me. A STAGGERING EVENT Luke 9:26-36 PREVIEW SCENE 29 Change in Countenance His face became different Metamorphous: describes a change in appearance that comes from within Rom. 12:2 2 Cor. 3:18 1 Thess. 4:16, 17 1 Cor. 15:51-57 The theological word for this is sanctification Prayer is a key factor in a transformed life. glistering flashing as the brilliance of lightening Change in Company 9:30 Behold divine highlighter Moses and Elijah Both men had previously conversed with God Both had been shown God s glory Both had unusual departures from this earth Both were expected to return Conversation 9:31 Jesus coming death exodus On this mountain the Law of God and the grace of God converge PETER S SPEECH 9:32 were oppressed with sleep overcome His immediate thought is KINGDOM Peter was placing Jesus on the same level as Moses and Elijah SOVEREIGN REBUTTAL 9:34 The Jews regularly connected clouds with the presence of God overshadowed them partially blocking out the light God demanded that they hear only Jesus Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(87).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/6/2023 11:37:24 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FAITH DISTINCTIVES LUKE 9:37-43a Cf. Matthew 17:14-21; Mark 9:14-29 INTRODUCTION On the 50th anniversary of Sir Edmund Hillary s historic ascent of Mt. Everest, a television newsman said that much of Hillary s status as a hero in Nepal was not about what he did when he stood on top of the world, but what he did when he came back down. After conquering the world s highest mountain with his climbing companion Tenzing Norgay in 1953, Edmund spent the next five decades helping to build schools, hospitals, and bridges for the Sherpa community. Great experiences must bring us back to everyday life. They must be related to the heartbreaks of people. Mountaintops don t mean anything without the valleys. Wiersbe: We dare not stay on the glorious mountain top when there are battles to fight in the valley below. There could be many directions to take with this passage, but I think Jesus gives us the best theme to pursue. 9:41 faithless and perverse generation I can picture the statement accompanied by a big sigh. Perhaps a shaking of the head, a groan, downcast eyes A countenance totally different than that seen on the Mount These disciples were described as faithless. Their failure was in delivering this boy from a demon. How is that faithless? That is a tall order. They are not Jesus. Jesus has demonstrated His authority over the demonic world. Chapter 4 His own temptation in the wilderness 4:33 the demon in the synagogue 8:27 the Gadarene demoniac How does that make the disciples faithless? 9:1 Jesus gave them authority 9:10 they reported back all that they had done No report of failure; no discussion of it not working They are faithless because somewhere along the way, they neglected to believe. What is faith? Hebrews 11:1 Faith is believing what God said. Some of you will remember a spiritual song late 60 s God said it, I believe it, and that settles it for me to be biblically accurate you have to say, God said it, that settles it; whether I believe it or not. But one stanza says this: Do you believe that God created you Just like He told you in His word? Do you believe that every world in space Came to place by His own power? Do you believe that God can change your life And give you freedom from your sin? He'll open every door that blocks the way Though some may doubt that His word is true I've chosen to believe it, now how about you? The entire chapter then describes the faith of God s followers They believed what they were told and acted upon it Maybe the disciples began to believe in their own power. Maybe they began to read their own press; accept the praises of the people Not told; Jesus only describes them as faithless and perverse. I want to highlight 3 types of faith that we will see or experience DISTORTED FAITH Mark 9:14 the next day descending the lowlands of reality What a difference a day makes! One day you are single, the next married. One day free, the next hooked; One day on your own, the next responsible for another; one day at a camp, the next on a cruise; Luke speaks of the next day to highlight the difference The future glorious kingdom and the fallen world For the disciples it was not only a change in elevation but elation MacArthur notes the striking contrast between the mountain top experience and that in the valley - The contrasts between the two incidents are striking. One happened on a mountain, the other in a valley. There was glory on the mountain, tragedy in the valley. On the mountain Jesus Christ displayed His glorious majesty, while in the valley Satan displayed his ugly, cruel violence. Two sons are in view, one God-possessed, the other demon-possessed; one in whom His Father was well pleased, the other whose father was tortured with displeasure over his condition. One Son fulfilled the glorious plan of the ages, confirmed by both Old Testament and New Testament saints; the other son was disassociated, disconnected, demented, and chaotic, without purpose or value to anyone. One Son was the destroyer of demons; the other son was destroyed by demons. Both sons were given back to their fathers. The demon-possessed son was delivered and returned to his father; the Son of God was killed, rose from the dead, and ascended back to His Father. Met by a crowd Mark 9 mentions the scribes argument with the disciples Probably over their failure to drive out the demon Probably minimizing the power and authority of Jesus It is just like the fallen world to mock any failures by Christians They are ready to pounce, ridicule, attack, harass Their attacks justify their own ungodliness in their eyes Their attacks provide opportunities to question God Even David, in his moral failure, was accused by the prophet Nathan of giving the enemy an occasion to blaspheme God. 2 Samuel 12:14 The enemy loves to magnify our lack of faith; failure to live according the biblical standards Bad philosophy isn t hiding around the corner. It s heralding its various gospels in the public square, and every person with Twitter or TikTok is a town crier. In fact, the world many times has a higher expectation of the Christian life than most believers Two reasons: at times, their expectation is unbiblical At times, the low-life Christian is the problem Where is your God now? is a common question asked. Five answers Psalm 115:2, 3 in heaven doing as He pleases; always wise and good Job 35:9, 10 Elihu: the same place when evil comes as He is when He gives songs in the night Psalm 42:3-6 God will come at the perfect time; in life or death Psalm 79:10 preparing vengeance on those who taunt His loved ones v. 12 John 8:18, 19 right there in front of them Where is God when it hurts? Do we still believe that He will never leave us or forsake us? Do we still believe that nothing can separate me from His love? Do we still believe His mercies are new every morning? Do we still believe all things work together for good? Do we still believe His grace is sufficient? The question is not so much where is God when it hurts but how is my relationship to God when it hurts? Can I forgive as God forgives me? Can I pray for my enemies and those who despitefully use me? Can I still trust His plan when I cannot see His hand? Joni Eareckson Tada: God sometimes permits what He hates to accomplish what He loves TRANSITION: We will run into those who have a Distorted Faith A lack of faith leading to being critical of Bible Believers DISTRESSED FAITH 9:38 Plea of a Father Matthew 17:14, 15 describes the son as an epileptic Literally: moonstruck; from the belief that lunacy fluctuated with the phases of the moon. Definition of lunatic: someone affected with a severely disordered state of mind; is also listed as now offensive Luke gets out his divine highlighter again behold Luke and Mark- Teacher Matt. - Lord possibly a reference to deity because of his request It is clearly an expression of faith, however little It is the valleys of life where our faith is tested and often becomes weak Wave after wave of discouragement and disappointment There was little to no lull of the torment in this family Here is a father crying out a high volume seeking assistance implore beseech, beg; real urgency used earlier 5:12 Look epiblepo; look intently, pay close attention to Only son the only one of its kind; unique, one and only 7:12 the only son of the widow at Nain 8:12 Jairus only daughter Pattern of Illness 9:39 Combining the gospel writers: very ill, mute, suddenly cries out, spirit seizes him, tears him, slams him to the ground, often falls into the fire or water, foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, stiffens out, leaves him bruised and broken Somewhat surprising that Dr. Luke does not use the term epileptic Luke is more concerned with the demon A demon was in control or possession of his physical faculties Jesus had demonstrated His authority over the demonic Luke 4:1-13 His own temptation in the wilderness from Satan Luke 4:31-36 possessed man in the synagogue Luke 8:26-39 legion of demons sent into herd of swine We previously stated that demons prefer to remain quiet, behind the scenes Demons are still very active today. Still possession but especially influence Issue 1 is pure evil; the killing of babies Much of Washington D.C. is demonic If the light of the body is the eye, the souls of many politicians is very dark we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, spiritual forces in high places Those with a Distressed Faith still come to Jesus I believe Those with a Distressed Faith know their faith is lacking help my unbelief Faith is a gift from God; Ephesians 2:8, 9 Life Application Bible: No matter how much faith we have, we never reach the point of being self-sufficient. Faith is not stored away like money in the bank. Growing in faith is a constant process of daily renewing our trust in Jesus. There is no direct verse that says grow in faith 2 Peter 3:18 grow in grace and knowledge But from where does faith come? The Scriptures Romans 10:13 Faith comes by hearing the Word of God The more we study, meditate, memorize, the Scriptures, the more we grow in faith. You do that work and God will provide the training ground. TRANSITION: WE WILL FIND PEOPLE WITH A DISTORTED, CRITICAL RESPONSE TO FAITH WE WILL FIND PEOPLE, MAYBE OURSELVES, WHOSE FAITH IS DISTRESSED DISAPPOINTING FAITH 9:41 O faithless and perverse generation Lacking faith and distorted, twisted Jesus is probably speaking to the crowd as a whole But I think He is speaking directly to the disciples How could He come down on them? Demon possession is way up there on the spiritual warfare scale 9:1 given power; no qualifications 9:6 they had acted on that power; they saw it work Jesus is grieved over the disciples failure They continued to display a lack of faith And somehow distorted the message given to them Did they begin to think the power resided in them? Did they begin to think they could act independently? Mark 9:29 The disciplines of the Christian life What was their problem? Look at the rest of the chapter. No wonder Jesus was disappointed 9:37-42 unbelief and failure 9:43-45 dealing with spiritual blindness 9:46-48 dealing with pride 9:49-56 dealing with the lack of love 9:57-62 dealing with a lack of dedication CONCLUSION Jesus delivers; the boy is restored in health and to his parents People are amazed Faith: Distorted, Distressed, Disappointing The Distorted Faith is that of an unbeliever; critical, mocking The Distressed and Disappointing Faith is from a believer Are you asking Where is God? Why is he distant when I hurt so much? Have you given Jesus reason to be disappointed in your faith? Have you been guilty of twisting the Scripture to justify your behavior? Have you been depending on your own wisdom, leaning on your own understanding? Has your faith come to a standstill because of failure to spend time in the Scriptures? Oh, may we grow to limit the number of times Jesus has reason to be disappointed in our faith. When God says it; believe it. FAITH DISTINCTIVES LUKE 9:37-43a Hebrews 11:1 Faith is believing what God said DISTORTED FAITH Mark 9:14 It is just like the fallen world to mock any failures by Christians The enemy loves to magnify our lack of faith Where is your God now? Psalm 115:2, 3 Job 35:9, 10 Psalm 42:3-6 Psalm 79:10 John 8:18, 19 DISTRESSED FAITH 9:38 Plea of a Father Matthew 17:14, 15 epileptic = moonstruck It is the valleys of life where our faith is tested and often becomes weak Pattern of Illness 9:39 Luke is more concerned with the demon Distressed Faith still comes to Jesus Distressed Faith knows their faith is lacking Faith is a gift from God Ephesians 2:8, 9 Romans 10:13 DISAPPOINTING FAITH 9:41 Lacking faith and distorted Mark 9:29 When God says it; Believe it. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(88).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 10/27/2023 4:55:22 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message CHALLENGING CHURCH UNITY Luke 9:43-62 INTRODUCTION Jesus has just expressed His disappointment with the apostles Their inability to deliver a young boy from demon possession received this rebuke: O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Faithless and distorted What was their problem? They failed to believe the words of Jesus After being personally selected and intimately associated with Jesus for over 2 years, after listening to His preaching and preaching, after observing numerous miracles, after receiving authority to fulfill their own miraculous ministries, the twelve still did not get it. After all of that, the report card says they have failed. They might receive participation ribbons, but they don t know Jesus They don t believe Jesus They are faithless, they continue to distort the message Now, some of us have sat in class and had a difficult time grasping the material. Sometimes it was our problem and sometimes a teacher is the problem. The student and teacher are not on the same page. There was no better teacher than Jesus; He is the divine logos. He is called rabbi, teacher. 4:22 So all bore witness to Him and marveled at the gracious words which proceeded out of His mouth. 4:36 Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, "What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!" They heard straight forward teaching; they heard Beatitudes Hebrews 1:1, 2a God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways, has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son . . . All of that and Jesus expresses His disappointment. What was their problem? Look at the rest of the chapter. No wonder Jesus was disappointed 9:43-45 dealing with spiritual blindness 9:46-48 dealing with pride 9:49-50 dealing with competition 9:51-56 dealing with jealousy If you want to bring disunity to a family, church, place of employment, any organization, here is a short list to avoid 4 testimonies of distorting truth; 4 problems bringing great disappointment COMPREHENSION 9:43-45 Miracle is over; a young boy is restored to health Look at the response: Amazed to strike with astonishment Majesty mighty power; glory, splendor, magnificence, superbness Marveled wonder, admire The response is right and proper but also a potential issue That is the response where people will take Jesus and make Him King Mark 9:30 states Jesus and the disciples departed after the miracle Why? The marvel of the miracle was not to mislead the disciples They were not to be influenced by the fickle crowd So, Jesus repeats the message of the cross Put this once and for all into your ears let these words sink down into your ears Take My words to heart; listen carefully to what I say; mark diligently I am on My way to die Jesus was always moving toward the cross First prediction of the cross 9:22 Here is a shortened version A veiled reference is in 12:50 17:25 18:31-33 a clear and extended prediction The disciples understood none of the death predictions They were surprised and puzzled. And what s more, they outright rejected the message. Peter s response? 9:45 Why didn t they ask? They may have been afraid to know; afraid of another rebuke They did not want it to be true it was hid from them, that they perceived it not Worded to mean that an outside force veiled it to them so they might not grasp it. We know 1 Cor. 2; the natural man cannot understand the things of the spirit of God; foolishness to him. Not the case here. It could be a Judicial blindness: Jesus kept them from knowing; kept their minds blinded to the direct message. We know that unbelief begets unbelief. The city of Nazareth ran Jesus out of town so no more miracles or teaching was done for them. In 9:53 a Samaritan town refused His entrance. 9:13-16 In the OT, we read that God hardened Pharaoh s heart. I really think the problem here is they were Just not ready to receive it. The information was there; Jesus withheld nothing; even rebuked their lack of faithfulness and distortion of truth They just were not ready. The disciples are still earthly minded. In the next paragraph, we see they are still thinking earthly kingdom and what roles they will have. That is why Jesus took them away from the crowd of v. 43 He was concerned about that influence, so He reminded them about the cross Because they had forgotten where discipleship begins: 9:23 A disregard for self; lose sight of and forget himself and his own interests It took the cross, burial and resurrection to complete the message 24:6, 7, 25, 27 24:44, 45 He opened their understanding, that they might comprehend the Scriptures Nothing new here. Have you ever heard someone say, Now, I understand. The light comes on. Now I know what they were talking about. I struggled big time in high school with Algebra 1 and breezed through Algebra 2. I was 3-4 years playing the trombone before it clicked. I was in tech school for auto technician and standing on a wet floor and touch the motor of the tire balancer. Now I know what an electric shock is like. Many things you have been told, but the message was not received because you had no experience, it was beyond your level of expertise. The disciples were slow to come around, but come around they did. All with maybe the exception of John died for what they believed. It can be a problem in a local church. Those of us who have been around the block a time or two or three, have learned a few things to pass on to others. We have to be patient with those who are coming behind. And those who make up the younger crowd need not be frustrated or antagonistic when they do not understand or appreciate another view. That is the beauty of a local church. We are not all on the same level, the same page; but we can appreciate and love one another and grow when iron sharpens iron. And unlike these disciples, ASK Students, you are coming into the years where you will realize your parents were not as dumb as you thought they were. Where once you reacted to their council, you will soon be seeking it out. And it will be ok, if you one day tell them, you were right TRANSITION The lack of COMPREHENSION often reflects the lack of spiritual maturity. The disciples did not get it; they had much learning to be relearned. They had a view of the kingdom that needed to be totally reworked. The different levels of COMPREHESION can be a blessing or major roadblock in the life of a local church. So, we when ask the question, what was their problem , we have one answer with three more to pursue. COMPARISON 9:46-48 COMPETITION 9:49, 50 CONDEMNATION 9:51-56 CHALLENGING CHURCH UNITY Luke 9:43-62 COMPREHENSION 9:43-45 The marvel of the miracle was not to mislead the disciples Jesus was always moving toward the cross First prediction of the cross 9:22 The disciples understood none of the death predictions They did not want it to be true An outside force veiled it to them so they might not grasp it Judicial blindness Just not ready The disciples are still earthly minded. They had forgotten where discipleship begins: 9:23 It took the cross, burial and resurrection to complete the message 24:6, 7, 25, 27 24:44, 45 We have to be patient with those who are coming behind. The lack of COMPREHENSION often reflects the lack of spiritual maturity. Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(89).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/4/2023 7:29:21 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WELCOME HOME WHO IS THE G.O.A.T.? Luke 9:46-48 Cf. Matthew 18:1-5; Mark 9:33-37 INTRODUCTION The section of Luke s gospel we are presently studying from the life of Jesus is the supporting evidence that Luke includes based on Jesus rebuke of His apostles in 9:41. O faithless and perverse generation: how long shall I put up with you. The statement was issued as a result of their failure to deliver a young lad from demon possession. They failed in spite of the fact they were given the authority of Jesus to accomplish that very task; 9:1. They lost sight of their dependence on God through the lack of the personal discipline of prayer and fasting (Matthew 17:21). A reminder to us that we have no power apart from our relationship to Jesus. Luke then highlights for us at least 4 areas of the lives of the apostles that needed work. And if any of these areas are found in our lives, you can just plan on bringing disunity to a family, church, place of employment. We see it happening in the world around us every day. Last week in 9:43-45 we looked at the lack of COMPREHENSION. Whether their lack of understanding was because of divine blinding of the eyes or simply an unwillingness to accept the message, the message of Jesus coming suffering was ignored. Looking at Luke 24:25 suggests to us it was indeed a problem with the apostles. slow of heart to believe The same blockages to understanding are still very prevalent today. Some are unwilling to receive the message but for many a judicial blindness as come as described in Romans 1 with the phrase: God gave them over . Read Romans 1:20-32 If all the polling in the state of Ohio is accurate, that judicial blindness will be on display once again. The failure does not lie with the media or the State House or even Washington. They are contributing factors, but the blame lies in the pulpits of churches with weak kneed and spineless preachers telling people what they want to hear. As we highlighted at the beginning of the service, we are not here to entertain or provide an emotional high. Our dependence is on the Holy Spirit of God taking the Word of God to move you, not singing 50 stanzas of Just As I Am. TRANSITION: a lack of COMPREHENSION can bring great disunity. COMPARISON 9:46-48 This is a big one. Jesus has been talking about His personal suffering, the disciples are arguing about personal glory. Context: the miracle is over; Jesus departs with His disciples before they get caught up in the frenzy of the crowd: v. 43 astonished, majesty, marveling They are traveling through Galilee to Capernaum (Mk. 9:30, 33) The disciples got into a discussion among themselves who would be the greatest in the kingdom 9:46 dispute - reasoning, discussion, deliberate, argument They had much to unlearn; still very earthly minded; kingdom focus Prevented them from comprehending the message of the cross Now it leads to an argument over who is the greatest People are passionate about their athletes; for these guys it was not about somebody else, it was about them. You know how this took place Say, when you were out there on your preaching assignment, how many did you heal? You don t ask the question unless you think you can top it. There are preachers who have scheduled special meetings at their church shortly before attending a conference. And they are sure to bring it up when around their buddies. How many walked forward in your service last week? Now you know why we don t pass the offering plate. I can say we had 10 people walk forward last Sunday. So, they can compare their preaching assignments. What else happened in this chapter? Transfiguration Bragging rights for Peter, James, and John Who better to brag than someone who had been in the presence of the Lawgiver and arguably the greatest OT prophet? So, they can first talk about the accomplishments. What comes next? The attacks. Pride makes sure you knock down the next guy. Peter would be an easy target. Speaks first, think later. Heh, Peter! Remember when Jesus called you a tool of Satan. They had forgotten their lack of faith in the storm; 8:25 They had forgotten their lack of faith in feeding 5,000 plus; 9:13 They have already conveniently forgotten their failure to deliver v. 41 Look at Mark 10:32 Mark s record of the third prediction of the cross Now: 10:35 still pushing for position William Barclay suggests Three Wrong Motives for service in his Daily Study Bible Series Prestige: seeking honor from men instead of God Position: a call to service is not an appointment to office Prominence: others know of our labor and sacrifice Pride brings Disunity Pride destroys relationships Pride is Revealed 9:47 Jesus having seen the reasoning of their heart Reasoning the same word as disputing in v. 46 Jesus knew they were arguing when they thought they were out of earshot. He knew of the deliberations that occupied their minds Mark 9:33-34 (WEB) He came to Capernaum, and when he was in the house he asked them, "What were you arguing among yourselves on the way?" But they were silent, for they had disputed one with another on the way about who was the greatest. Pride is easy to see in other people but so hard to recognize in ourselves. My child is an Honor Student at London Elementary Moderately proud parent of an average C student Proud parent of an Unsocialized Homeschool Child Proud Parent of child whose self-esteem does not depend on minor scholastic achievements being displayed on a bumper sticker Matthew 20:20-24 Jesus, can you place my boys at your right and left hand in glory. May God help us for our lack of Comprehension May God help us for making Comparisons 2 Corinthians 10:12 For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among themselves, are not wise. 2 Corinthians 12:7 (WEB) By reason of the exceeding greatness of the revelations, that I should not be exalted excessively, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, a messenger of Satan to torment me, that I should not be exalted excessively. God can keep you in check. What did He do for the disciples? Here are 12 guys full of themselves long before social media 9:47, 48 Jesus is in a house in Capernaum; takes a little child in His arms A child, who previously had occupied no place of honor was now next to Jesus Jesus says, this is greatness Humble, dependent, trusting For he who is least among you all will be great Jesus is to depend on these ragmuffins to build the church They were going need each other; not compete against each other Sober assessment of themselves and serving one another in love And don t miss this v. 48 Jesus loves the little children of the world Receive a little child; receive Me; receive the Father When you look at a child, see Jesus This account is also in Matthew 18:1-5 which includes this instruction in v. 6 Matthew 18:6 (WEB) but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him that a huge millstone should be hung around his neck, and that he should be sunk in the depths of the sea. Unfortunately, this is not the last time of discussing who is the greatest Luke 22:24-30 at the Last Supper WHO IS THE G.O.A.T.? Luke 9:46-48 COMPREHENSION 9:43-45 COMPARISON 9:46-48 Jesus has been talking about His personal suffering, the disciples are arguing about personal glory. 9:46 dispute - reasoning, discussion, argument They had forgotten their lack of faith in the storm; 8:25 They had forgotten their lack of faith in feeding 5,000 plus; 9:13 They have already conveniently forgotten their failure to deliver v. 41 Three Wrong Motives - William Barclay Prestige: seeking honor from men instead of God Position: a call to service is not an appointment to office Prominence: others know of our labor and sacrifice Pride brings Disunity Pride destroys relationships Pride is Revealed 9:47 Pride is easy to see in other people but so hard to recognize in ourselves. Matthew 20:20-24 2 Corinthians 10:12 2 Corinthians 12:7 A child - no place of honor, humble, dependent, trusting When you look at a child, see Jesus Matthew 18:6 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(90).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/11/2023 5:21:40 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message MY CHURCH IS BETTER THAN YOUR CHURCH! Luke 9:49, 50 INTRODUCTION After the rebuke of the disciples in verse 41, we are looking at 4 situations that are directly related to unbelief and distorting the truth of Jesus Each of these areas not only impacted the disciples but can directly influence a church to its detriment. COMPREHENSION: judicial blindness or just not ready to understand Because of the hardness of some men s hearts, God withholds the ability to understand even basic spiritual truths. And some people are like the disciples who needed to unlearn what they thought they knew in order to receive new material. The excuse that s the way we have always done it is not always the best response. A reminder of original of lack of comprehension: The person is not saved. Evident once again on the past election Those in favor of killing babies used supposed preachers to support their position. Those in a position of prestige who are spiritually blind COMPARISON: destroys relationships, brings disunity Pride leads to the discussion of who is the greatest Rather, we are to be like a little child in humility, dependence, trust Romans 12; we must have a sober assessment of ourselves Galatians 6:3 For if anyone thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself. TRANSITION: That brings us to our third problem area the disciples had because of their lack of faith. Not believing the words of Jesus but living by sight. Sight which cannot understand spiritual things; sight with promotes the exaltation of self over others. Sight which leads to: COMPETITION Or you may use a word like sectarianism; partisanship excessive attachment to a particular sect or party, especially in religion. To be narrow-minded, biased, one-sided, unjust, blind Often used in the political world but not uncommon in religion Discussion 9:49 In verse 49 the wording is such that it is part of the previous discussion It is not a new subject; still comparing but now to an outsider Instead of arguing within the group, they go outside the circle John is bringing up something that may have occurred on their preaching tour They saw a man casting out demons in Jesus name. They try to stop him because he did not belong to their group. Did not carry the company name; does not hang with us; wrong brand He had the wrong color of uniform Could be a hint of jealousy because of their recent failure What this man was doing was remarkable. He had grasped the truth of what could be done in the name of Jesus He had not been called or directly employed by Jesus He attained this knowledge and power by his own faith in the Teacher In the present discussion, John could have made the connection of the teaching and his wrong attitude. If it was true that to receive a little child was to receive Jesus, to receive God, then how much more should he have received a man who was casting out demons in Jesus name. The issue here is competition; partisanship John, and whoever else in the we , had the mistaken idea that every follower of Jesus should have the same stripe. Correction 9:50 Another rebuke We should not hinder others just because they promote the gospel in a different way. Two questions to ask: Are they really accomplishing good? Are they doing the work in the name of Jesus? If so forbid them not. There is no room for jealousy in the spiritual warfare of Christ s kingdom Philippians 1:16-18 Paul: some are preaching the gospel from selfish ambition thinking to add to my afflictions Totally wrong motive but Paul said if the gospel is being preached I rejoice MacArthur: it is ironic that this comes from the mouth of John; he who became to known as the apostle of love Jesus says there is no middle ground: either for me or against me There is no neutrality Two tests of discipleship are given to us in this chapter: Outward test: here is a man performing work in Jesus name Inward test: 9:23 deny himself, take up cross and follow How can we test another person s ministry? Is it just as long as they call themselves Christians good enough? Testimony: When I got saved, my immediate environment was all about separation. Even to the point of not having fellowship with that person because they have fellowship with liberals e.g. Billy Graham So, I have had to unlearn some things. Unfortunately, as so often happens, the pendulum has swung to far the other way that no one ever talks about separation anymore. Where do we draw a line? We have to ask questions? Are they doing good; are they ministering in the name of Jesus? That is a great starting point. That does not mean you will agree on all points or that you will join their church or have rich fellowship with them. It does mean that you will not be critical of their ministry. There are other churches in our area where the gospel is taught. They are not our enemy even though we may not do things the way they do. Each one of could be running 1k in attendance with still plenty of people left over. We have had this discussion with several students as the school year began. They will often attend a church to be with their friends. There is little evaluation of the ministry or asking the question is this where I should be for growing and serving others ? Is there I would be if I was married and raising a family? What are my non-negotiables when it comes to church? What things can I tolerate though not my favorite? The same partisan disciple wrote an epistle that provides some areas for evaluating a ministry. Evaluation The Test of Doctrine 1 John 4:1-3 2 John 7-11 The Test of Fruit 1 John 2:4-6 1 John 2:29 1 John 3:4-12 1 John 4:5, 20, 21 Cf. Matthew 7:16 You will know them by their fruits By the way: a verse about judging others from the same chapter people use to not judge others MY CHURCH IS BETTER THAN YOUR CHURCH! Luke 9:49, 50 COMPREHENSION COMPARISON COMPETITION narrow-minded, biased, one-sided Discussion 9:49 He had the wrong color of uniform He had not been called or directly employed by Jesus John had the mistaken idea that every follower of Jesus should have the same stripe Correction 9:50 There is no room for jealousy in the spiritual warfare of Christ s kingdom Philippians 1:16-18 Outward test: performing work in Jesus name Inward test: 9:23 Evaluation The Test of Doctrine 1 John 4:1-3 2 John 7-11 The Test of Fruit 1 John 2:4-6 1 John 2:29 1 John 3:4-12 1 John 4:5, 20, 21 Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(91).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/18/2023 1:04:34 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message CALLING DOWN FIRE Luke 9:51-56 INTRODUCTION This is lesson #4 from what started in v. 41 faithless and perverse generation The inability of the disciples to deliver a boy from demon possession Initially, a rebuke from Jesus Then Luke gives us four accounts demonstrating to us what faithlessness looks like It began with a lack of COMPREHENSION Then a COMPARISON AND COMPETITION. Today it is CONDEMNATION Any of these four can spell disaster to a family, church, any group Look at the progression of these COMPREHENSION: that is a personal, individual problem COMPETITION: That takes place within your little circle COMPETITION: we find that by looking at those outside of our circle but people who are of similar persuasion CONDEMNATION: we are now looking beyond even those of similar persuasion So, faithlessness within will influence how I view myself among my piers, my larger associations, and to those without It begins with me, then my close friends, then to the church, then to the unbeliever A little reminder of Romans 12 My relationship to God, to the world, myself, other believers, the unsaved Sacrifice, separation, sober assessment, serving in love, supernatural service CALLING DOWN FIRE CONDEMNATION His Sight 9:51 set His face Jesus ministry was drawing to a close Ahead was the Cross, Agony, and Passion He never lost sight of His destination set His face implies determination, fixed purpose, purposeful precision it came to pass, when the time had come a time lapse from v. 50 His vision was so clear He looked beyond the Cross Hebrews 12:2 He moved without hesitation Why? Hebrews 2:9, 10 He should taste dead for every man and bring many sons to glory The synoptic gospels have moved along pretty consistently to this point with slight variations in the record Luke omits much of the material recorded in Mark 9:42 10:12; and does not return to Mark s chronology until Luke 18:15 About 60% of Luke 9:51 through 18:14 is unique to this gospel; with a little in Matthew Luke records 18 parables unique to his gospel; 16 in this section We are now looking at the last 6 months of ministry Until now, Luke has spoken of Christ s coming; now about His going Luke has recorded His birth, temptation, Transfiguration As we said in the early part of our study, Luke was more interested in the themes of Jesus life than the timing Luke emphasized the finished work of Jesus when He is taken up Received: mission accomplished; crucifixion, burial, resurrection, seated in glory OR received up could also be a direct reference to the cross so must the Son of Man be lifted up (John 3:14) Jesus is going to receive growing opposition The disciples are still thinking kingdom would have been excited to go to Jerusalem. Where else would a King be crowned? When the real question is, where else would a sacrifice be offered? What Luke does record here is the resolute purpose of Jesus Isaiah 50:7 therefore I have set My face like a flint Jesus did not run from what is ahead; He willingly headed to the place of sacrifice There was no turning back Has there been a time in your life when you had clear direction from God, you started out well and have since lost your way? Daniel 1:8 (WEB) But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king's dainties, nor with the wine which he drank: therefore he requested of the prince of the eunuchs that he might not defile himself. Missionaries called to difficult places; you called to give a testimony Ezekiel 6:2 ; 13:17 ; 14:8 ; 15:7 . Jesus is about to respond to those with a lack of purpose 9:62 Maybe now you are seeking God s will in some area and wonder why the message in still so foggy? If we have not responded to past light, why should God provide more? Am I daily setting my face toward the cross? 9:23 1 Peter 2:21 (WEB) For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you an example, that you should follow his steps, His Scouts 9:52, 53 The Advance Team Not checking out the security of a location and working out a map of travel Jesus sent messengers before Him to ask for accommodations There were a number of people with Him besides the 12 (8:2, 3) It was a courtesy to send a message ahead A large group would strain the resources of a small village if they arrived unexpectantly. The first village would not receive Him; what a missed blessing There was no welcome mat for Jesus The reason: Jesus was going to Jerusalem Seems like a silly reason to us Culturally: still a great controversy between Jews and Samaritans Especially related to the temple The Samaritans believed it should be on Mount Gerizim; they had built a temple there The Jews argued for the temple in Jerusalem The feud was so bitter that the Samaritans would not help anyone traveling to Jerusalem Josephus, historian, tells how Jewish pilgrims were often mistreated and sometimes murdered by the Samaritans Many Jews traveled around Samaria while moving north or south The Samaritans were a mixed race people placed there by the king of Assyria They practiced a cultic form of Judaism with a separate temple During the days of Antiochus Epiphanes, they denied all relation to Israel and dedicated their temple to Jupiter. The Maccabees eventually took and destroyed the Samaritan temple John 4 Jesus chose to travel through and had the encounter with the woman at the well Now, He is not only traveling through but seeking lodging Jesus was extending the hand of friendship; the offer was refused We have no record of Jesus reaction to the refusal But we sure do from James and John His Surprise 9:54-56 Indignant Perhaps where the nickname sons of thunder originated Several rebukes have been handed out recently Lack of faith, lack of comprehension, comparisons, competitions Jesus had just said v. 50 no neutrality; either for Me or against Me These two disciples were seeking justice on those against Not told if James and John were part of the advance party or heard the report For them, the insult was too great to go unpunished We learn about zeal and their lack of understanding What had Jesus previously said about being unwelcomed: 9:5 But fire is much more spectacular and they had Scriptural support It is one of the stories you can remember from early days of Sunday School Elijah: 2 Kings 1:10, 12 servants of the wicked king of northern kingdom In so doing they missed other lessons from the Scriptures Exodus 23:4, 5; 2 Kings 6:21-23; Matthew 5:44 Here comes the Surprise v. 55 not in the verb rebuke it came while turning passive participle the effect of their suggestion cause Jesus to turn suddenly as though their words surprised Him The turning is viewed as a dramatic act His rebuke: there is too much pride, passion, and personal revenge underneath your pretended zeal for me There is a place of righteous indignation There is no place for intolerance or discrimination It is not for us to administer vengeance; Romans 12:19 In Noah s day, God waited 120 years while the ark was being built He is still waiting; when judgment comes, it will be in God s time Jesus calls for a Supernatural Response: Romans 12:17-21 We all want the fire; retaliation, vengeance Matthew Henry: the sons of thunder want to add lightening to their repertoire What might be right in one case could be totally wrong in another Zeal without knowledge is like an army without a general 9:56 Luke 19:10 our theme for the gospel Christ determined to set up a Kingdom ruled by love and compassion Not one ruled by fire and the sword He sought to win people by miracles of healing, not plagues of destruction His disciples were not to drive men into the Kingdom But to draw them by the cords of love the weapons of our warfare are not carnal and they went to another village The true spirit of Christ Not only did He refuse to punish for their lack of hospitality, He quietly went to another village Matthew 28; Great Commission; Judea, Samaria, uttermost parts Acts 8:25 preaching in the villages of Samaria Because the people were not burned by two hot heads The focus of Jesus was not on feuding but finishing CONCLUSION Comprehension, Comparison, Competition, Condemnation CALLING DOWN FIRE Luke 9:51-56 COMPREHENSION COMPARISON COMPETITION CONDEMNATION His Sight 9:51 set His face implies determination Hebrews 12:2 Hebrews 2:9, 10 60% of Luke 9:51 through 18:14 is unique to this gospel Received: mission accomplished a direct reference to the cross Isaiah 50:7 Daniel 1:8 Am I daily setting my face toward the cross? 9:23 1 Peter 2:21 His Scouts 9:52, 53 It was a courtesy to send a message ahead The feud was so bitter that the Samaritans would not help anyone traveling to Jerusalem His Surprise 9:54-56 the insult was too great to go unpunished Elijah: 2 Kings 1:10, 12 the effect of their suggestion caused Jesus to turn suddenly as though their words surprised Him There is a place of righteous indignation There is no place for intolerance or discrimination Matthew Henry: the sons of thunder want to add lightening to their repertoire Luke 19:10 Christ determined to set up a Kingdom ruled by love and compassion Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(92).txt ============================================= From: Sent: 11/25/2023 11:41:58 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message AN HONEST RECRUITER Luke 9:57-62 INTRODUCTION We will see 3 wanna-be disciples; 3 would-be followers Standard for following Jesus is set: 9:23 A hungering and thirsting after God so there is no clinging to material things, material comforts, material possessions Deny: refuse to associate with who you used to be A level of desperation where I come to the end of myself I can truly sing, Christ Is All I Need The face of Christ is set for Jerusalem; the arrest, agony, death Christ is the face of all He requires of us Key Thought: John 2:23-25 is an important text for our study The Key Word: Follow To walk the same road Are we willing to walk the same road as Jesus?
