We see here the call for wholehearted commitment as they went in the way down the road Same word to describe Christians; followers of the way Jesus is looking for followers of the Way on the way Character #1: SCRIBE COMFORT SEEKER Matthew identifies him in 8:19 Matthew 8 gives us more of the setting Jesus had been performing miracles The centurion who said just say the words and healing will happen Commendation: so great faith in Israel Peter s MIL is healed Caught up in the excitement but also the teaching Matt. 8:19 calls Jesus teacher Was willing to become His pupil; recognized no greater teacher The man s promise is a good one; no doubt a sincere plea Should be the sentiment of any follower: wherever you go This man was charmed by Jesus but not changed Would been a nice feather in Jesus cap to recruit one from the opposition The scribe had great intentions Here is a fellow willing to follow whatever the cost; wherever you go We will see here that a wholehearted commitment is different than the road to hell is paved with good intentions The response of Jesus reveals the decision to follow Him cannot be done flippantly, frivolously Jesus calls no one under false pretences Jesus set the standards high from the beginning and never lowered them From Leadership magazine; summer 1983 as the church growth movement was riding high: The LITE CHURCH: 24% fewer commitments, home of the 7.5% tithe, 15 minute sermons, 45 minute worship service; we have only 8 commandments your choice. We use just 3 spiritual laws and have an 800 year millennium. Everything you ve wanted in a church and less! To be upbeat so everyone feels loved and accepted There are millions who call themselves Christian but not fully committed to Jesus Nothing about their Christianity influences the way they live A Lutheran pastor said, Ninety percent of our parishes across the country require less commitment than the local Kiwanis club It is not always that they preach something wrong, they just don t say anything of substance. 9:58 No luxury suites; you can expect self-denial, sacrifice, service, suffering The man was willing but Jesus saw his weak spot Jesus presented privation; the man was looking for promotion Jesus possessed less creaturely comforts than His creation Here is a man with a rocky soil; received the message with gladness, but no deep root and soon to wither away This is interesting: look at 10:2 the harvest is plentiful but laborers are few In today s world, you would just hirer a marketing consultant Their first report: Well, Jesus, you just lost three candidates because your standards are too high. You have to be a bit more realistic! Following Jesus is pass/fail. No grading on the curve. The focus here is not on the response of the candidates The focus must be on us; my heart Am I a casual follower or a committed follower? This thing about following Christ is to step outside your comfort zone The natural inclination is to rely on ourselves I am no longer seeking to save myself but depending on another; surrendering myself completely to God And in my day-to-day life, I find myself called upon to love the unlovely, pray for enemies, talking salvation to sometimes an unreceptive crowd This call of Jesus is not about those seeking full-time service This is the average Joe; the man in the pew The apostles sacrificed everything to follow Jesus; Matthew 19:27 They face arrest and persecution; Acts Their biases were erased; they went to Samaritans, Gentiles They were in uncomfortable situations We need not seek out undesirable situations to be spiritual Just be willing to be used when called The call by Jesus is not to sell your house and deny all creaturely comforts To follow Jesus requires a life of self-denial and not of self-indulgence You home can be comfortable without being a showcase How many times have you heard, You never see a UHaul truck in a funeral procession. I told Ruth I think I will make that part of my service arrangements, so you can say, actually I did. But to complete the illustration, the truck will be empty. He would have no man enlisted on false pretences. He would have it distinctly understood that there is a battle to be fought, and a race to be run, a work to be done, and many hard things to be endured, if we propose to follow Him. Salvation He is ready to bestow, without money and without price. Grace by the way, and glory in the end, shall be given to every sinner who comes to Him. But He would not have us ignorant that we shall have deadly enemies, the world, the flesh, and the devil, and that many will hate us, slander us, and persecute us, if we become His disciples. He does not wish to discourage us, but He does wish us to know the truth. J. C. Ryle Counting the Cost 1. Counting the Cost with Self Yielding over prestige, power, popularity, positions Willing to follow wherever the Lord leads James 1:8 a warning to a double-minded man Philippians 2:5-8 2. Counting the Cost with Society Society can make life difficult; make life miserable 1 Corinthians 4:13 (WEB) Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now. 1 Corinthians 15:58 (WEB) Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord's work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord. 3. Counting the Cost of Security and Sacrifices We are not called to seek earthly riches: Matthew 6:19 (WEB) "Don't lay up treasures for yourselves on the earth, where moth and rust consume, and where thieves break through and steal; We are not to worry about our future: Luke 12:22 (WEB) He said to his disciples, "Therefore I tell you, don't be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear. We are to live sacrificially for the good of others: Matthew 22:39 2 Corinthians 8:9 (WEB) For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich. Most recruiters will only highlight the positive stuff. After graduation high school and contemplating tech school, a fellow came to the house and spoke of all the advantages of their school for mechanics and where their graduates have been hired. Many of you have heard the same pitch Jeff had the job to recruit. Jesus laid out all the negatives; no false promises It was all about counting the cost (He will hit it again in chapter 14) "You must give up everything for the Son of Man, but you must not give up the Son of Man for anything." This is God s will for your life Saved: 2 Peter 3:9 Sanctified: 1 Thessalonians 4:3 Spirit-filled: Ephesians 5:17, 18 Submissive: 1 Peter 2:13-15 Suffering: 1 Peter 3:17, 4:49 Satisfied: 1 Thessalonians 5:18 The call of Jesus was not to bring someone to an emotional experience and then pray this prayer From Jesus, we learn you can draw men without wooing them AN HONEST RECRUITER Luke 9:57-62 Key Thought: John 2:23-25 Key Word: Follow Character #1: COMFORT SEEKER This man was charmed by Jesus but not changed Jesus calls no one under false pretences Jesus presented privation; the man was looking for promotion Following Jesus is pass/fail This thing about following Christ is to step outside your comfort zone This call of Jesus is not about those seeking full-time service We need not seek out undesirable situations to be spiritual Counting the Cost 1. Counting the Cost with Self 2. Counting the Cost with Society 3. Counting the Cost of Security and Sacrifices "You must give up everything for the Son of Man, but you must not give up the Son of Man for anything." Dwain A Hill ============================================= === SERMON: message(93).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/9/2023 2:35:26 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE RUBBER NECKER Luke 9:61, 62 INTRODUCTION Luke chapter 9 has been a long chapter. I did not go back and count the messages but I do know I now have 120 pages of notes. Jesus sends out the 12; feeds the 5,000; Peter s confession; prediction of suffering and death; Standard of discipleship; the Transfiguration; rebuke of disciples for lack of faith through their lack of Comprehension, Comparisons, Competition, and Condemnation; and finishing with 3 excuses for refusing to follow Jesus. We have looked at two excuses: the COMFORT SEEKER (9:57, 58) and the PROCRASTINATOR (6:59, 60) We now meet a man who is looking backward even as he is volunteering to move forward We meet the RUBBER KNECKER It does not seem to be simply a matter of saying good-bye Even Elijah permitted such: 1 Kings 19:19-21 Elisha did not dilly-dally; within a day made a fire, sacrificed the oxen, and followed From this man we once again hear the words but first He steps up and makes a great declaration But he wants to serve God on his own terms I will serve you, Jesus, but only if I can still have the comforts of life I still want to drive a nice car Live in a nice neighborhood Please don t make me shop at Target, Walmart, Aldis Let Allow Permit release from restraint in order to have freedom of choice Translated: suffer, leave, leave your gift at the altar The idea of departure, sending away forgive us this day Same word in 8:32; permitting swine to enter the pigs It is seeking the freedom to carry out a preferred activity The first place I want you to send me is back home Putting parameters on our serving good bye - to set apart, to leave, to appoint the man desiring to return home, not merely to take formal leave, but also to give his final instructions to his friends and servants. Acts 18:18 Paul leaving instructions upon his departure We see a man s good motive; Jesus saw a divided heart Like Lot s wife, he found it hard to sever ties with the old life He wasn t willing to make a clean break 9:62 the admonition is clear You can t keep one foot in the world just in case things don t work out in the kingdom We are told it is always nice to have a backup plan Let s look at plowing a field in Jesus day The farmer had one hand on the plow and with the other controlled the animal or animals. The plows of the day were light so not only both hands were occupied but he also needed to place his weight on the plow to turn the soil. In no way can he look behind him. He is totally occupied with the job. Looking back would not only yield a crooked furrow, there would be no furrow at all. We have a picture of being dedicated to the task You get your line of direction and stick to it your focus is ahead, even well beyond the immediate task It calls for a reorder of all other duties Philippians 3:13-14 (WEB) Brothers, I don't regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before, I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus. 1 Kings 18:21 How long will you halt between two opinions? The verse is not condemning those who start out in so-called full-time service for the Lord and then turns to secular work Pastors, missionaries, evangelists Jesus is not talking about how one earns a living but rather to a basic focus in life. The focus is on Jesus and His cause Matthew 6:33 seek ye first the kingdom of God Whether you are digging ditches or preaching behind the sacred desk, your consuming purpose is to know Jesus and to make Him known. There is no sacred and secular is serving Jesus Colossians 3:4 Christ (who is) our life The challenge of being a living sacrifice is not just for missionaries It is not just important to follow Jesus, it is the most important thing in life Everything revolves around His purpose, His kingdom My career, my family life, my spare time Jesus does not say every spare minute must be spent is some service We need rest, recreation There is no reason for guilt for taking time off But the reason for time off is refreshment so we might serve the Lord better I hope to be teaching someplace as long as I effectively communicate It will all be in the voice. I can sit and teach Commitment to Christ cannot be based on some emotional decision Our first man may well have been caught up in the miracles and the crowds. He wanted a piece of the action He had not considered the cost, the hardship, the rejection, the persecution His glamorous view would quickly fade when difficulty came Profession is easy; practice is hard Commitment to Christ as Savior cannot be based on good vibes. (S. Cole) Commitment to Christ is not a casual, whenever-I-find-the-time matter. Jesus would not allow the man to postpone his commitment until it was more convenient. Heaven and hell are real; physical death is a daily potentiality The message of the kingdom is urgent Everyone we meet has a story and everyone we meet is a heartbeat from heaven or hell Commitment to Christ is not about exploring your options It is not some phase you put behind you some day Luke 17:32,33 Remember Lot's wife! Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it. The Christian life is about yielding more and more to Jesus and not I ll try it for a while to see if it works out. The only direction is forward. There is no turning back 2 Timothy 2:3-4 (WEB) You therefore must endure hardship, as a good soldier of Christ Jesus. No soldier on service entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier. Am I following Jesus totally or am I holding back? It is not I ll follow you, Lord, but first . . . It is easy to hear a message such as this and respond with a I will serve Jesus with no reservations. But have you counted the cost? How do we implement total commitment to Jesus on a daily basis? Someone observed that we think giving our all to the Lord is like taking a $1,000 bill and laying it on the table. Here s my life, Lord. I m giving it all. But the reality for most of us is that He sends us to the bank and has us cash in the $1,000 for quarters. We go through life putting out 25 cents here and 50 cents there. We listen to the neighbor kid s troubles instead of saying, Get lost. We go to a committee meeting. We give a cup of water to a shaky old man in a nursing home. Usually giving our life to Christ isn t glorious. It s done in all those little acts of denying self for Jesus sake, 25 cents at a time. It would be easy to go out in a flash of glory; it s harder to live the Christian life little by little over the long haul (Fred Craddock, in Leadership [Fall, 1984], p. 47). This broadens our understanding of the basics of discipleship 9:23 We can sing I Surrender All but do we really? What level of participation would we have if the admonition was given don t sing unless you mean it. There are three recorded events in history: Cortes, Julius Caesar, Alexander the Great When Julius Caesar landed on the shores of Britain with his Roman legions, he took a bold and decisive step to ensure the success of his military venture. Ordering his men to halt on the edge of the Cliffs of Dover, he commanded them to look down at the water below. To their amazement, they saw every ship in which they had crossed the channel engulfed in flames. Caesar had deliberately cut off any possibility of retreat! Now that his soldiers were unable to return to the continent, there was nothing left for them to do but to advance and conquer! In Switzerland, in a church graveyard near the base of a great mountain, there is a special tombstone. It is the tombstone of a man who died on a mountain climb. These words are upon it: "He Died Climbing." Let us die climbing. May that be our spiritual epitaph. THE RUBBER NECKER Luke 9:61, 62 COMFORT SEEKER (9:57, 58) PROCRASTINATOR (6:59, 60) RUBBER NECKER Elijah 1 Kings 19:19-21 but first he wants to serve God on his own terms Let Allow Permit release from restraint in order to have freedom of choice We see a man s good motive; Jesus saw a divided heart We have a picture of being dedicated to the task Philippians 3:13-14 Jesus is not talking about how one earns a living but rather to a basic focus in life. Matthew 6:33 Colossians 3:4 Commitment to Christ cannot be based on some emotional decision Commitment to Christ is not a casual matter. Commitment to Christ is not about exploring your options Luke 17:32,33 2 Timothy 2:3-4 Let us die climbing ============================================= === SERMON: message(94).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/16/2023 12:33:43 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE VIRGIN BIRTH ISAIAH 7:1-14 INTRODUCTION There have been many babies born who have received the title miraculous . Abraham and Sara: old, well beyond child bearing age Manoah and his wife: barren and have borne no children (Judges 13:3) Hannah: not told her age, but the Lord had closed her womb (1 Sam. 1:5) Shunammite woman: her husband is old (2 Kings 4:14) 12/23/2013; NBC printed a story 6 Miracle Birth Stories Beyond Jesus At that time a US survey said about 75% of Americans believed in the virgin birth Their article has all kinds of wild stories mostly from mythology And there are many virgin birth stories from a variety of religions The best title I saw was by a Richard Carrier, Virgin Birth: It s Pagan Guys. Get Over It From 12-1-23 New York Post: 70 year old Ugandan woman gives birth to twins Donor s egg and partner s sperm; doctor called it an extraordinary achievement 8-8-82 New York Times: in a time span of 10 days two women gave birth after abdominal pregnancies On December 9, a report of a present abdominal pregnancy was reported of a 37 year old woman on vacation. She was 23 weeks along; placed in hospital care for 6 weeks and surgery to deliver the baby was performed. The first American quintuplets were born 1896 1970 the first surviving American quintuplets conceived through fertility medication September, 1866, the first surviving sextuplets born in Chicago; four lived into adulthood May, 1985, the first septuplets born in CA; only two boys and one girl survived November, 1997, the McCaughey septuplets born. The first set to reach age of maturity Octuplets: first set born March, 1967, in Mexico; all died within 13 hours January , 2009, a set born became the world s first set to survive infancy A set of nonuplets was born in May, 2021, and all reported in good health on their first birthday All to say, there have been some amazing births Unfortunately, some scientist are playing around with petri dishes as they desperately try to diminish the work of God This month we once again celebrate the Incarnation; God taking on human flesh His name is Immanuel: God with us. Galatians 4:4 (WEB) But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law, Philippians 2:5-8 (WEB) Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus, who, existing in the form of God, didn't consider it robbery to be equal with God, but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men. And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, yes, the death of the cross. John 1:14 (WEB) The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth. I want to focus on the subject of The Virgin Birth Many, if not all religions, have tried to copy it and make it their own Many others, like Richard Carrier, have desperately tried to dismiss it, dispute it Many desperately want to believe it did not happen to appease their conscience For if indeed God became man, then the rest of the story is also true and they are facing judgment and the eternal lake of fire Today, I want to focus on the familiar passage in Isaiah 7; put it in its historical context We will add to our study next Sunday and be looking at the first mention of the virgin birth and see it s important place in Scripture. Augustine: He was created of a mother whom He created. He was carried by hands that He formed. He cried in the manger in wordless infancy, He the Word, without whom all human eloquence is mute." Isaiah ministered from about 740 B.C. to 690 B.C. He was a contemporary of Micah and Hosea and with Micah was a prophet primarily to Judah, the southern kingdom. Hosea was to the northern kingdom, also called Israel, Ephraim, Samaria The Kings of Judah he served were Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah Also remember the northern kingdom fell to Assyria in 721 Isaiah is considered the greatest of the prophets. His writing is one of the longest, is quoted more frequently than any other OT prophet and more than any other tells of the coming Messiah. John Phillips writes, One moment his book is black with the thunder and the darkness of the storm. The next, the rainbow shines through, and he sweeps his readers on to the Golden Age that still lies ahead for the world. JUDAH WILL BE DELIVERED FROM HER ENEMIES Jotham: yo-thawm' Rezin: , rets-een' Pekah: peh'-kakh Uzziah became king at 16 years old and was a great king and served well until he violated temple But when he was strong, his heart was lifted up, so that he did corruptly, and he trespassed against Yahweh his God; for he went into the temple of Yahweh to burn incense on the altar of incense. (2 Chronicles 26:16) He became a leper until the day of his death Jotham (2 Chron. 27) is also recorded as a good king and Isaiah was given no record of prophecy to record Jotham dies and Ahaz, his son, becomes King; and not a good one Summary of his life: 2 Chron 28:5-8 HISTORICAL CONTEXT The Situation 7:1, 2 It came to pass under the sovereign control of God Assyria was growing stronger and was becoming a major threat Israel and Syria partnered to create strong opposition and invited Judah to join What they did not know was that King Ahaz was secretly bribing Assyria to protect him; 2 Kings 16:1-9 Syria and Israel are about to attack and the people are scared Their king was depending on the strong arm of the flesh Why was Ahaz so scared? Because of an earlier defeat 2 Chronicles 28:1-4 It should be noted in v. 2 House of David and not that of Ahaz Another reminder of Satan s desire to destroy the Davidic dynasty from which would come the Savior God s covenant with David is immutable: 2 Samuel 7:12-16 Important question: what do we depend upon in a time of crisis? Is God the first one we turn too? 2 Chronicles 16:12 And Asa in the thirty and ninth year of his reign was diseased in his feet, until his disease was exceeding great: yet in his disease he sought not to the LORD, but to the physicians. The Promise 7:3-9 God sent Isaiah and his son who was a walking sermon (sheh-awr'yaw-shoob'I a remnant shall return) to meet Ahaz while he was inspecting the water supply The message presented was full of hope and confidence. Isaiah: the salvation of the Lord; his son: a remnant shall return don t be afraid. Keep calm and don t lose your head. Guard yourself and be quiet. Within 65 years those oppressing will be destroyed Syria; 732 B.C.; Israel (Ephraim, Samaria), 721 B.C.; both by Assyria v. 4 two tales of smoking firebrands about to flame out more pride than power; more bark than bite Now, nothing wrong with making sure of needed provisions like water especially when a siege is expected. But not at the expense of ignoring the spiritual side We often fail to see things as God sees them. It shall not stand, nor come to pass 7:7 Double promise Spurgeon on the link between God s promises and man s trust A PROMISE from God may very instructively be compared to a check payable to order. It is given to the believer with the view of bestowing upon him some good thing. It is not meant that he should read it over comfortably, and then have done with it. No, he is to treat the promise as a reality, as a man treats a check. He is to take the promise, and endorse it with his own name by personally receiving it as true. He is by faith to accept it as his own. He sets to his seal that God is true and true as to this particular word of promise. He goes further, and believes that he has the blessing in having the sure promise of it, and therefore he puts his name to it to testify to the receipt of the blessing. This done, he must believingly present the promise to the Lord, as a man presents a check at the counter of the bank. He must plead it by prayer, expecting to have it fulfilled. If he has come to heaven s bank at the right date, he will receive the promised amount at once. If the date should happen to be further on, he must patiently wait till its arrival; but meanwhile he may count the promise as money, for the bank is sure to pay when the due time arrives. In 65 years, the northern kingdom saw a great transfer of occupants. Many of Israel were carried away; many foreigners were brought in v. 9 If you will not believe, you will not be established; NIV: If you do not stand firm in your faith, you will not stand at all. A play on words in Hebrew; put into English If there is no belief, you will find no relief. If you will not be sure; you will be not secure Hebrews 11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please God What are we saying when we like Richard Carrier refuse to believe God? 1 John 5:10 The one who believes in the Son of God has the witness in himself; the one who does not believe God has made Him a liar, because he has not believed in the witness that God has borne concerning His Son. You either live by faith or you will not live at all. Ahaz s refusal to believe did not effect the outcome as it was already determined by God But his refusal did impact the remaining time of his position as king THE DEFINITIVE TEST The Sign 7:10-13 God speaks; Isaiah speaks Paul: We preach not ourselves but Christ Ahaz put to the test; this is not a test but a command; a command that calls for immediate action The test is will he receive or refuse the promise? A sign points to something; it could be the dairy isle, the tool section, the next town Here is a sign to authenticate the promise of God A rainbow, circumcision, blood on the door, plagues in Egypt, stones in the Jordan Used in Isaiah in chapters 7, 8, 19, 20, 37, 38, 44, 55, 66 v. 11 there is no limit the potential of world shaking importance; all men would know Ahaz acted very pious by refusing to seek a sign from God; a hardened heart reflecting pride Contrast with Isaiah s response: 6:1-5 An indication he did not believe the promise Someone might quote Deuteronomy 6:16 you shall not put the Lord your God to the test That is true except when commanded by God to do so Malachi 3:10 (WEB)Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house, and test me now in this," says Yahweh of Armies, "if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough for. Contrast this king s refusal to the widow of Zarephath No bread for Elijah yet she laid hold of the promise resulting in an inexhaustible barrel Ortland: Ahaz proves here that faith can be refused by the will, no matter how strong the evidences. If we don t want God, we can find a way to make our unbelief sound plausible, even pious. Oswalt: Evidence does not create faith, it can only confirm it. Evidence is unwelcome where there is no faith. When we cannot trust God, it suddenly makes good sense to trust our worst enemy. Wesley: If a man will not believe God, he will believe anything. You read salvation verses to someone and they respond: I don t believe God would do that for me. Beloved, when you read God s promises, endorse the check and cash it. 7:13 "Look, isn't it enough that you make me tired without making God tired as well?" God commanded, Ahaz refused. What an insult to God! House of David magnifying his lack of faith; God has already promised You is now plural; not just a king problem but a people problem So, God turned to the entire nation, v. 13 ============================================= === SERMON: message(95).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/23/2023 7:31:34 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE VIRGIN BIRTH ISAIAH 7:1-14 INTRODUCTION In Matthew 16:13, Jesus asks a question of His disicples, whom do men say that I the Son of Man am? They provided the popular answers of the day. There are still a lot of popular responses today which fall far short of what is true; responses which deny the supernatural. Some Bible translations are guilt of denying or minimizing the miraculous. We are looking at the virgin birth of Christ. John Walvoord: The virgin birth of Jesus Christ is the central fact of Christianity. All other aspects hang on it. Dr. Hugh Schonfield published a book in the 70 s entitled The Passover Plot He goes to great lengths to refute Christ s vicarious atonement. To do so, it was needful for him to deny any occurrence of a virgin birth. He refers to faith as fairy dust and a desire to believe in the impossible. Man cannot deny the historical Jesus, but how He came to this world is the subject of much debate. Was the offspring of an illicit love affair? That would explain His kind dealings with the Samaritan woman and the woman taken in adultery. Was he totally human but the closest to God possible? He had his God-like moments; set out to fulfill Scripture as best He could through His own scheming and the plotting of His own death. Was He just another man? So says the once popular rock opera Jesus Christ, Superstar Like many things in the biblical realm, man says it makes no matter but spends an inordinate amount of time and energy seeking to discredit it. Last week: HISTORICAL CONTEXT The Situation 7:1, 2 Assyria was growing stronger and was becoming a major threat Syria and Israel are about to attack and the people are scared 2 Chronicles 28:1-4 The Promise 7:3-9 The message presented was full of hope and confidence. Double promise If there is no belief, you will find no relief Hebrews 11:6 1 John 5:10 THE DEFINITIVE TEST 7:10-13 A sign points to something Here is a sign to authenticate the promise of God Ortland: Ahaz proves that faith can be refused by the will Oswalt: Evidence is unwelcome where there is no faith Wesley: If a man will not believe God, he will believe anything. THE DEFINITIVE TEXT Many call verses 14-16 one of the most difficult verses to interpret Spurgeon: One of the most difficult in all the Word of God. It may be so; I certainly did not think it was until I saw what the commentators had to say about it, and I rose up from reading them perfectly confused. Only controversial because many go to great lengths to deny the virgin birth and work the language to say it says no such thing These verses are of incredible importance to every member of the human family All Christianity rests on the fulfillment of this passage Textual Substance therefore a term of conclusion; because of Ahaz s act of unbelief and failure to ask for a sign Behold - look at this, this demands your attention; get out your highlighter listen up, all you who would be wise in the way of Jehovah There is a lot of beholding in Isaiah; uses the word over 60x In a way, we can be glad that Ahaz refused to ask for a sign God says, I will give you a sign This greatest sign has also become the most controversial The sign was ultimately fulfilled in the birth of Jesus Christ; Matthew 1:23 Born to the virgin Mary, conceived by the Holy Spirit; Luke 1:31-35 Immanuel with us God Isa. 8:8, 10 Virgin: the very word is a source of much disagreement, controversy Definition can change according to context; 7x in the OT Genesis 24:43 a young woman of marital age This is the same girl mentioned in v. 16 young woman = bethulah A more familiar word for virgin Comes from word meaning secluded Bethulah: a maiden who has been kept from intercourse with men; to seclude, private, keep in privacy Almah: a woman kept in private out of sight, veiled and withheld from intimacy with a man but who is approaching readiness to engage in marriage So the perfect word for Isaiah 7:14 the Holy Spirit was exact in the word usage The use of both words is emphatically conclusive Further emphasis in Genesis 24:16 no man had known her Exodus 2:8 the maiden Miriam some guessing she between the ages of 10-15 also 1 Chronicles 15:20; Psalm 46:1; 68:25; Proverbs 30:19; Song of Solomon 1:3; 6:8 Hebrew - almah describes not only a young woman of marriageable age but one who undoubtedly is a virgin. 'Almah is never employed of a married woman. So yes, it can be defined as a young woman as some English translations state But by using young woman in Isaiah 7:14 says more about the translators and their denial of the virgin birth; noted because they relegate virgin to the footnotes We believe you need to let Scripture interpret Scripture The Jews have a Greek translation of the OT called the LXX Translated by Jewish scholars about 150 B.C. For Isaiah 7:14 they chose the Greek word Parthenos which by definition means virgin Matthew uses Parthenos; 1:23 Luke uses Parthenos; 1:26, 27 Niessen: The evidence supports both the traditional translation of virgin and the modern translation of young woman, but each must be qualified. The English term virgin does not suggest age limitations while the English phrase young woman does not suggest virginity. The word ??????? ('almah) demands both, and so a more accurate translation would be young virgin. (Bibliotheca Sacra 137:546, April, 1980 ) The NT clears any possible misunderstanding of almah Ray Stedman perhaps has the best summary: It is not wrong to translate "a virgin" as "a young woman." The Hebrew allows for that. But to be a "sign" it would have to be a young unmarried woman who had never known a man -- a virgin, in other words. Young women have sons all the time, but it would only be a sign if a woman who never knew a man conceived and bore a son. That is what the prophet said would happen. It was a sign to the whole House of David. Note the verbal expression: English; the virgin shall conceive and bear a son literally it is not future at all but present Behold the virgin, presently pregnant, and bringing forth a son It is not that a pregnancy will take place at a future time, but the almah is now pregnant, about to bear a son and still a virgin. Fulfillment: possible immediately as a sign to Ahaz and the nation Not a miraculous virgin birth but it does suggest that a Jewish virgin wed and within a year gave birth to a child Before this child reached the Jewish legal age of 12 , the enemy nations were defeated It is possible the promised child was born to Isaiah s wife; 8:8 We know Isaiah already had a son. Maybe his wife died and he remarried? Some prefer to point to Hezekiah as the fulfillment 2 Kings 23:25 Others say the prophecy had no fulfillment in Ahaz s day, but why give Ahaz a sign he could have recognized? The important point to remember is that while the interpretation of the near fulfillment is disputed, there is no controversy (at least among conservative commentators who accept predictive prophecy) on the interpretation of the future and final fulfillment in the birth of Jesus, Immanuel, to the virgin Mary. The child s name: Immanuel Matthew leaves no doubt as to who Isaiah 7:14 was pointing to by quoting in his gospel; 1:23 Jesus was God walking in sandals on this earth The name captures the awe and wonder of the incarnation: With Us God The name was a rebuke to the unbelieving Ahaz; If God is with us, why fear the enemy? Adam Clarke asks "In what sense then, is Christ God with us? Jesus is called Immanuel, or God with us, in His incarnation; God with us, by the influences of His Holy Spirit, in the holy sacrament, in the preaching of His word, in private prayer. And God with us, through every action of our life, that we begin, continue, and end in His name. He is God with us, to comfort, enlighten, protect, and defend us, in every time of temptation and trial, in the hour of death, in the day of judgment; and God with us and in us, and we with and in Him, to all eternity." Textual Support the biblical support for the virgin birth is overwhelming but I am only going to look at two biblical accounts Genesis 3:15 But the thought of a virgin birth did not originate here in Isaiah Time does not allow us to explore the entire story which I trust you are most familiar with the events of sin entering this world God is the One who declares war: I will put Satan may have assumed he had won the allegiance of the woman Borrowing an outline from Adrian Rogers A Battle Prophesied: enmity means warfare A battle where women still hate snakes today A battle between offspring, descendents The first evangelistic message is right here A Birth Prophesied: her seed The seed of the Serpent would include all the followers of Evil A family we all belonged to before being rescued by Jesus The word seed is used over 100x in the Bible and always refers to the offspring of the male Here it is the seed of the woman ; the only time used this way Henry Morris; Defender s Bible: The "seed of the woman" can only be an allusion to a future descendant of Eve who would have no human father. Biologically, a woman produces no seed, and except in this case Biblical usage always speaks only of the seed of men. This promised Seed would, therefore, have to be miraculously implanted in the womb. In this way, He would not inherit the sin nature which would disqualify every son of Adam from becoming a Savior from sin. This prophecy thus clearly anticipates the future virgin birth of Christ. Jeremiah 22:24-30 Critics love to jump on the supposed problems in Christ s genealogy as examined in Matthew and Luke In Matthew 1: Abraham to Jesus through David s son Solomon The typical A begat B begat C It changes in v. 16 Jacob begot Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus who is called Christ. of whom feminine singular gender; only refer to Mary Luke 3 uses the same names from Abraham to David Differences then arise with Matthew s account Matthew traces through Solomon; Luke through David s son, Nathan Matthew records women (5) and Luke records none Matthew records kings; Luke none Matthew has 27 names between David and Jesus; Luke records 41 Two different fathers are listed for Joseph Why such a difference? Here are a couple First view: the physical and legal rights to the throne came through Mary Heli (3:23) had no sons and according to the law of inheritance Mary had to marry within the tribe of Judah to keep the inheritance. That would be the purpose of Matthew s genealogy. Second view: the physical right comes through Mary, the legal right comes through Joseph. The physical right continues through Solomon but because of disobedience stops at Jehoiakin Jeremiah 22:30 none of his descendants shall prosper, sitting on the throne of David Some suggest there is no indication that the legal right stopped also So for Immanuel to be born required God s direct intervention in the form of the virgin birth. CONCLUSION Adrian Rogers: Now, let me come to a conclusion tonight and tell you two things: number one, you can trust the Word of God; secondly, you can trust the God of that Word. I mean, if you trust the Word of God, you certainly ought to trust the God of that Word. Now, the Old Testament says that somebody is coming. The New Testament says that somebody has come. And, the book of the Revelation says that somebody is coming again. Now, the New Testament tells you how Jesus, who is coming, gets you ready for His Second Coming, how you can know the Lord Jesus Christ as your Savior and Lord. HIS FIRST CRY TORE THE SILENCE HIS SECOND CRY TORE THE VEIL HIS FINAL CRY WILL TEAR OPEN THE SKY THE VIRGIN BIRTH ISAIAH 7:1-14 HISTORICAL CONTEXT The Situation 7:1, 2 The Promise 7:3-9 THE DEFINITIVE TEST 7:10-13 THE DEFINITIVE TEXT All Christianity rests on the fulfillment of this passage Textual Substance This greatest sign has also become the most controversial Matthew 1:23 Luke 1:31-35 Immanuel with us God Virgin: Genesis 24:43 Bethulah: a maiden who has been kept from intercourse with men; to seclude Almah: a woman kept in private out of sight, veiled and withheld from intimacy with a man but who is approaching readiness to engage in marriage Exodus 2:8 Miriam Isaiah 7:14 Greek word Parthenos which by definition means virgin Matthew uses Parthenos; 1:23 Luke uses Parthenos; 1:26, 27 To be a "sign" it would have to be a young unmarried woman who had never known a man Behold the virgin, presently pregnant, and bringing forth a son Fulfillment: Possible the promised child was born to Isaiah s wife; 8:8 Some prefer to point to Hezekiah as the fulfillment 2 Kings 23:25 Textual Support Genesis 3:15 A Battle Prophesied: enmity means warfare A Birth Prophesied: her seed the seed of the woman ; the only time used this way Jeremiah 22:24-30 First view: the physical and legal rights to the throne came through Mary Second view: the physical right comes through Mary, the legal right comes through Joseph. ============================================= === SERMON: message(96).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/29/2023 7:55:16 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message TO THE VIRGIN BIRTH DENIERS 1 Johnn 4:1-3 INTRODUCTION It is easy to sucked into things we see and hear. The razzle, dazzle, phenomena can quickly overcome any level of discernment. And there is something to be said for taking things at face value. After all, love believes all things. (1 Cor. 13:7) Well, it is one thing to trust folks in personal relationships, because the truth will eventually be known, and quite another to trust those who claim to speak for God. It is easy for someone to gather a crowd claiming they are speaking for God. Throw in a little entertainment and the crowd soon becomes a following. There are many false teachers and voices out there today clamoring for attention. Christianity is saturated with liberal theology, Unitarianism, neoorthodoxy, universalism, situation ethics, secularized religion, and idolatry. Much of their false teaching is a direct reflection of denying who Jesus is. John wrote his first epistle to assist the children of God in the confirmation of their faith. Chapter 5, verse 13. In chapter 4, he begins a new strain of thought, arguing that a true relationship to Jesus can be demonstrated by adherence to truth, particularly the truth about the person of Christ. John opens with a command to stop putting faith in every spirit , referring to people claiming to get their message from a supernatural source. They need to test any prophet who claims to speak as a mouthpiece for the Spirit of God. There are many false prophets already in circulation, promoting their teachings around the world. Phillips writes discover where they came from. Wuest says to test for the purpose of approving them if they are, and finding that they meet the specifications laid down. 4:2 here is a practical way to determine the truth: examine the message Test them in this simple way: what do they say about Jesus Every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God Some teachers asserted that the divine Christ was separate from the man Jesus and merely indwelt Him temporarily. The test: Christ came in flesh emphasizing the kind of nature He assumed. is come is not a verb but a perfect participle; this was more than just a temporary arrangement. Properly translated: that Jesus Christ has come in the sphere of the flesh and still remains incarnate. Literally: Presently confesses Jesus Christ in flesh having come is from God We spoke last week of some Bible translations and how they translate Isaiah 7:14 and the word virgin ; almah Is she a true virgin or just a young maiden? We sought to establish without any doubt she was a virgin, kept from any physical relations with a man, but one who was contemplating marriage. Mary was a virgin and remained a virgin until the birth of Jesus. She is not a perpetual virgin as she did give birth to other children. How a verse is translated says much about the heart of the translators. Test the spirits. 1 Thessalonians 5:21 Test all things; hold fast what is good Apostates are here to stay. The spirit of antichrist is at work. To deny Jesus has come in the flesh is to deny the supernatural and the grace which it reveals. 1 Timothy 3:16 (WEB)Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh, Justified in the spirit, Seen by angels, Preached among the nations, Believed on in the world, And received up in glory. 1 Timothy 1:15 (WEB)The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief. What are the dangers in denying the truth that Christ has come in the flesh? We are looking at 7. 1. It is to deny the fact that God has given an everlasting revelation of Himself in bodily form. There has been a long desire of man to see God Exodus 33:18 show me your glory John 14:8 Lord, show us the Father, and we shall be satisfied John 1:18 No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him. has declared Him declared = to lead out To lead out into clear view and comprehension To give a full and detailed exposition and explanation John 12:44-45 (WEB) Jesus cried out and said, "Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me. He who sees me sees him who sent me. To deny that Christ has come in the flesh is to deny God has ever made Himself known. It is to deny that there is a personal, living God in heaven who has not forsaken men in their sin and degradation. 2. It is to deny that the Lord Jesus Christ came into the world to reveal the ideal humanity. Man lost the ideal when sin entered the world and it is impossible of him to reach that goal by himself. Romans 1 reveals to us the depth of human depravity. It is only in Christ we will reach the ideal We are not there yet. If Jesus was the natural product of two parents, there is no such thing as an ideal humanity; there will be no society of the redeemed; no hope of glory 1 John 3:2 (WEB) Beloved, now we are children of God, and it is not yet revealed what we will be. But we know that, when he is revealed, we will be like him; for we will see him just as he is.. The first Adam lost it; the second Adam will restore it. 3. It is to deny the supreme purpose for which Christ came into the world. Matthew 20:28 even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many." 1 Peter 3:18 Because Christ also suffered for sins once, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring you to God; being put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit; Galatians 4:4 5 But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law, to redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption as sons 1 Timothy 1:15 The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief. If Jesus was not virgin born, He had a sin nature and would not have been an acceptable sacrifice for you and me. Looking at the dangers of denying Jesus has come in the flesh 4. It is to deny that the works of Satan have been overcome. 1 John 3:8 He who sins is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. To this end the Son of God was revealed, that he might destroy the works of the devil. Hebrews 2:14 Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in like manner partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil, Jesus was manifested to overthrow what Satan has done and is doing Don t think of destroy as meaning annihilation or total destruction It is a breaking up something that was compactly built together, depriving it of authority and effectiveness Destroy means to loose those who were bound by it The devil is still active walking about, seeking whom he may devour If the Son of God had not taken on human flesh Colossians 2 would never have happened Colossians 2:13-15 (WEB) You were dead through your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh. He made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses, wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us; and he has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross; having stripped the principalities and the powers, he made a show of them openly, triumphing over them in it. 5. It is to deny the present great high priestly ministry of the Lord Jesus Christ Hebrews 2:17, 18 Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people. For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted. Hebrews 4:14-16 Having then a great high priest, who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold tightly to our confession. For we don't have a high priest who can't be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin. Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and may find grace for help in time of need. We have a high priest who knows our weaknesses from His own experiences; He can be sympathetic with us in our infirmities and trials 6. It is to deny the possibility of real communion between God and man. Jesus in His human life demonstrated that sin is not an essential quality of the physical life and that physical life did not contaminate and corrupt His purity. He condemned sin in the flesh Romans 8:3 1 Timothy 2:5, 6 For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself as a ransom for all; the testimony in its own times; The coming of Christ in the flesh establishes the fact that there is no unbridgeable gap between God and human nature. The problem is between God and fallen, sinful human nature 1 John 5:20 We know that the Son of God has come, and has given us an understanding, that we know him who is true, and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life. 7. It is to deny that Christ is the final and impartial judge of all men. The deniers of the doctrine of Christ will one day face the very One whose supernatural person and power they have denied. John 5:22, 27 For the Father judges no one, but he has given all judgment to the Son, He also gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man. Contrary to all the false teachings and Satanic deceptions which characterize the liberal theology of our day, all men will not be saved. But all unsaved will stand in the presence of the great Judge of all the world in the final day of reckoning. Death is not annihilation. All is not over when men die. Hebrews 9:27 Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment, CONCLUSION I again quote from last week: John Walvoord: The virgin birth of Jesus Christ is the central fact of Christianity. All other aspects hang on it. We have looked at 7 theological truths that necessitate a virgin birth. The whole of our salvation rests on this doctrine. The resurrection depends on the virgin birth of Christ. Everything we believe hangs on this truth. We must not yield to the liberals in their speech or translations this vital doctrine. If Jesus is not who the Scriptures say He is, we have no salvation. He was born of a woman through the power of the Holy Spirit. No husband was involved. We come to God on no other basis. Do you know the Jesus of the Scriptures as your Lord and Savior? TO THE VIRGIN BIRTH DENIERS 1 Johnn 4:1-3 There are many false prophets already in circulation The test: Christ came in flesh It is to deny the fact that God has given an everlasting revelation of Himself in bodily form. John 1:18 John 12:44-45 It is to deny that the Lord Jesus Christ came into the world to reveal the ideal humanity. 1 John 3:2 It is to deny the supreme purpose for which Christ came into the world. 1 Peter 3:18 It is to deny that the works of Satan have been overcome. 1 John 3:8 Hebrews 2:14 Destroy means to loose those who were bound by it It is to deny the present great high priestly ministry of the Lord Jesus Christ Hebrews 2:17, 18 Hebrews 4:14-16 It is to deny the possibility of real communion between God and man. Romans 8:3 It is to deny that Christ is the final and impartial judge of all men. John 5:22, 27 Hebrews 9:27 ============================================= === SERMON: message(97).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/6/2024 11:22:06 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: MESSAGE GREAT CALVARY CHAPTER HUMILIATION: THE SORROWING SERVANT 53:1-3 Who is speaking and when are they speaking? We know the NT uses these verse to speak of Jesus Acts 8:27-38 John 12:38 Romans 10:16 Examine the pronouns vs. 1-6: the pronouns - first person plural We interpret the passage as future; looking to Jesus Look at the verbs: the verbs - past tense these passages are more than a prophecy looking forward to the first coming of the Messiah. They are also a lament of those Jews who are saved in the future and who look back on this prophecy that was missed by most of their Jewish ancestors. And so this section begins with a rhetorical question (Isa 53:1 ) that expects a negative answer - "Who believed..." The answer is that most of the Jews did NOT believe. The author is transporting himself into the future and looking back and describing their reaction to the suffering Servant when He appeared the first time The author is speaking for a group of redeemed Jews Zechariah 12:10-14 The pronouns speak of Israel: we, us, our Israel s griefs and sorrows 1 John 2:2 propitiation for the sins of the whole world This is Israel finally recognizing the long-rejected Messiah Presently estimated 15.7 million Jews in the world; a remnant will be saved Who has believed what we just heard? 52:13-15 The answer is hardly anyone. Arm of the Lord strength, power 51:9, 10; 52:10 John 12:37-40 They refused to believe and then were spiritually blinded The arm came and they failed to recognize it The arm of the Lord was the arm of the carpenter Elizabeth Barrett Browning, in her Aurora Leigh, has written a stanza that has always impressed me. Earth s crammed with heaven, and every common bush a fire with God, and only he who sees takes off his shoes. The rest sit round and pick blackberries. That s what so many in the world are doing Reasons for Rejecting the Servant 53:2, 3 Constable - Instead of appearing as a mighty oak or a flourishing fruit tree, the Servant would grow up before the Lord as a sucker, a normally unwanted shoot that sprouts up from a root." Brian Bell: Rejected His Words: Report Rejected His Works: Arm Rejected His Person Tender shoot: a way of expressing His upbringing was irrelevant Unimportant, insignificant sucker ; little bush Insignificant family, insignificant town, no royalty, no status Root out of a dry ground: A wilderness of dry ground Physically and spiritually No comeliness: splendor, honor Nothing about His appearance to make Him attractive Past tense had There is nothing in the gospels about His physical appearance Won't it be incredible that one day we shall look on Him on Whom men would not care to look at His first coming! 1 John 3:2 Psalm 45:3 And John 1:14 we beheld his glory though there was nothing about His physical appearance that was attractive, there were times the curtain was pulled back and glory was seen. Jesus is the Creator of the entire world, the Creator of everything beautiful and magnificent and also the Sustainer of it all (Col 1:17 , Heb 1:3 ), and yet He Himself came to earth with an unremarkable appearance, certainly not one that would draw the attention of others! He Who was the most special of all mankind, simply did not look "special." Oswalt had an excellent summary - The real issue is not whether this person was good-looking, but that the way in which he set about delivering his people was just as shocking and as off-putting as it would be to have the ugliest man in a group chosen best-looking. David Guzik has an interesting application is this description of Messiah - This means that when we try to attract people to Jesus through form or comeliness, or beauty, we are using methods that run counter to the nature of Jesus. These days it appears that we must dress up the gospel to make it attractive. We have to use the methods of technique which must be smart, well-presented, streamlined. There must be something about the presentation of the gospel that will appeal to people . . . to what is called the modern mind. I wonder if we stop to think that in our efforts to make the gospel message attractive we are drawing a curtain across the face of Jesus in His humiliation? The only one who can make Him attractive is the Holy Spirit. (Redpath) 53:3 He was despised, and rejected by men; a man of suffering, and acquainted with disease: and as one from whom men hide their face he was despised; and we didn't respect him. The aversion and hostility of the nation s ignorance manifested toward Jesus Despised: of little worth, hold in contempt, insignificant and worthless Gen. 25:34 Psalm 22:6 The humiliation of Christ Rejected; forsaken; abandoned Man of suffering: The NET Bible renders it "one who experienced pain and was acquainted with illness." Intimate knowledge with grief and suffering Heart sorrow; grief of soul Suffering and grief affected Him greatly Hid their face: their fixed position They turned their face from Jesus and remained that way Spurgeon s testimony of salvation: Isaiah 45:22 It is one thing to not look at someone and another to purposefully hide from them Despised: same word Not esteemed: accounting word; a reckoning of value Ordinary, unfortunate; estimated at nothing; counted a zero Not give a second thought Psalm 118:22 Remember: chapter 53 is Israel looking back at how they treated Jesus They once had a very low estimate of who Him whom they now adore Do we estimate Jesus at His true worth? Are we prepared to forsake all and follow Him outside the camp? Hebrews 13:22-25 Before Communion: from Ira Sankey HALLELUJAH WHAT A SAVIOUR! - Written in 1876, shortly before his death, this was the last hymn I heard Mr. Bliss sing. It was at a meeting in Farwell Hall in Chicago, conducted by Henry Moorehouse. A few weeks before his death Mr. Bliss visited the State prison at Jackson, Michigan, where, after a very touching address on The Man of Sorrows, he sang this hymn with great effect. Many of the prisoners dated their conversion from that day. When Mr. Moody and I were in Paris, holding meetings in the old church which Napoleon had granted to the Evangelicals, I frequently sang this hymn as a solo, asking the congregation to join in the single phrase, Hallelujah, what a Saviour, which they did with splendid effect. It is said that the word Hallelujah is the same in all languages. It seems as though God had prepared it for the great jubilee of heaven, when all his children shall have been gathered home to sing Hallelujah to the Lamb! GREAT CALVARY CHAPTER ISAIAH 53 HUMILIATION: THE SORROWING SERVANT 53:1-3 Pronouns first person plural verbs - past tense Zechariah 12:10-14 Who has believed what we just heard? 52:13-15 Arm of the Lord strength, power John 12:37-40 Reasons for Rejecting the Servant 53:2, 3 Tender shoot: Unimportant, insignificant Root out of a dry ground: Physically and spiritually No comeliness: splendor, honor 1 John 3:2 Psalm 45:3 Despised: of little worth Rejected; forsaken; abandoned Man of suffering: Intimate knowledge with grief and suffering Hid their face: their fixed position Not esteemed: estimated at nothing ============================================= === SERMON: message(98).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/13/2024 3:53:24 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message BEING A BRICK FOR JESUS Luke 10:1, 2 INTRODUCTION One of my favorite people for simplifying the message of Scripture is Warren Wiersbe; former pastor of the Moody Church in Chicago and director of Back to the Bible radio ministry. He approaches Luke 10 this way: What in the world does a Christian do? Ambassadors: sent to represent Him in this world Neighbors: looking to show mercy in the name of Christ Worshippers: taking time to listen to His word From Halley s Bible Handbook: The period between Jesus final departure from Galilee and His last week is usually spoken of as the later Judean and Perean ministry; it took place partly in Perea and partly in Judea. Perea was east of the Jordan (see map: Perean and Later Judean Ministry), in Herod s jurisdiction; Judea, west of Jordan, was under Pilate s jurisdiction. Most of Luke 10-18 is unique to Luke THE ADVANCED MEN 10:1-12 Now after these things after closing out His ministry in Galilee (chapter 9 highlights) 9:51 He set His face for Jerusalem Jesus further highlighting the conditions for discipleship Here are 70 people who no doubt heard the requirements for discipleship and responded to the call They were appointed ; not volunteers, but not slaggers either These were the advanced team to places Jesus was headed They were sent and they went It is great to have the desire, but also must have His appointment I wonder how many of the others were willing Someone has said, If we go and are not sent, then woe to our go Ephesians 2:8-10 we are His workmanship; created for good works We need spiritual eyes to see the works God has lined up for each one of us so we may walk in them. Someone has compared the 70 to the thin edge of a wedge Knowing Jesus was coming behind to accomplish the greater work Why pairs? Also used in chapter 9 Mutual encouragement and help Ecclesiastes 4:9, 10 two are better than one because they have a good return for their labor, for if either of them falls, the one will lift up his companion. But woe to the one who falls when there is not another to lift him up." Supporting witness: Deuteronomy 19:15 "On the evidence of two or three witnesses a matter shall be confirmed. Seen in the NT: Barnabas and Saul; Judas and Silas; Barnabas and Mark, Paul and Silas, Timothy and Erastus Lone ranger Christians are sitting ducks for Satan s minions They were sent by two to preach, to fetch the colt (Matt. 21:11); to prepare the Passover room (Mark 14:13) Even in the book of Revelation: two witnesses Moses & Aaron; Joshua & Caleb; David & Jonathan; Peter & John; Paul & Barnabas; Luther & Melanchthon; John & Charles Wesley; Moody & Sankey; Graham & Shea; ILLUSTRATION There once was a popular expression, "He was a brick!" The term implied all that is brave and loyal. Plutarch, in writing about the king of Sparta, tells how the phrase was coined. - An ambassador on a diplomatic mission visited the famous city. Knowing that its strength was acclaimed throughout all of Greece, he had expected to see massive fortresses surrounding the town, but he found nothing of the kind. Surprised, he exclaimed to the ruler, "Sir, you have no fortifications for defense. Why is this?" "Ah, but we are well protected," he replied. "Come with me tomorrow and I will show you the walls of Sparta." The next day he led his guest to the plain where the army was drawn up in full battle array. Pointing proudly to his soldiers who stood fearlessly in place, he said, "Behold the walls of Sparta -- 10,000 men and every man 'a brick'!" Each believer should be a brave & loyal brick as He constructs His Kingdom. Now, there are 70 others that obeyed Him, and would help to reap the harvest. We have no record of names of the 70 though, of course, many speculate It seems to be a good reminder to us; our names and our contributions are not important When our focus moves to ourselves and how God is using us, we are headed in the wrong direction. The lesson in verse 20 is important for us It doesn't take great people to do great things just unselfish ones. All glory goes to Jesus ADDITIONAL MEN 10:2 Motivation: Compassion Luke 7:12 ( Mt 14:14 , Mt 15:32 , Mt 18:27 , Mt 20:34 ) The word is sp??????? spl nchnon; meaning inner bowels Jesus has an overwhelming compassion for the lost souls The picture presented is the distress of a farmer when he sees his fields golden for harvest and there are no servants to gather it in. We have had several occasions locally where a farmer took ill or experienced some other hardship and many others gathered to do the work. Here, Jesus is telling the laborers to pray for more laborers. Consider the "5P's" - Pause to Ponder the Passage then Practice it in the Power of the Spirit. Why are there so few willing to labor for souls? 9:57-62 Jesus has already spoken to the issue; He just turned down 3 So what do we do? therefore, beseech Command expressing urgency It is the Lord s harvest; we have the privilege of participating send out to eject, drive out The word suggests a measure of compulsion for an overmastering impulse is always needed to overcome human reluctance and laziness. (McClaren) No man has ever done large service for God who has not felt that, like the prophet, he was laid hold of by the Spirit, and borne away, whether he would or no. I must speak, is felt by every true messenger of God Where is our measure of compulsion? Has the salvation of a lost person even crossed your mind lately? Where do we begin? The Lord s Prayer: Our Father Who is in heaven, Hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done, On earth as it is in heaven." It shows Our priority for living: His kingdom, not ours Only after that do we pray for personal needs; our daily bread If we are not praying first and foremost for God s glory to be increased through the spreading of His kingdom, we are not praying rightly. This is God s work; God s ministry; God s harvest We begin by praying; the people who pray wind up going; the people who don t pray, never go. It is the laborers, not the spectators, who pray for more laborers. BEING A BRICK FOR JESUS Luke 10:1, 2 ADVANCED MEN 10:1-12 They were sent and they went Why pairs? Mutual encouragement and help Ecclesiastes 4:9, 10 Supporting witness: Deuteronomy 19:15 Lone ranger Christians are sitting ducks for Satan s minions It doesn't take great people to do great things just unselfish ones. ADDITIONAL MEN 10:2 Motivation: Compassion Jesus has an overwhelming compassion for the lost souls Jesus has an overwhelming compassion for the lost souls "5P's" - Pause to Ponder the Passage then Practice it in the Power of the Spirit therefore, beseech Command expressing urgency send out to eject, drive out Our priority for living: His kingdom, not ours It is the laborers, not the spectators, who pray for more laborers. ============================================= === SERMON: message(99).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/20/2024 11:30:30 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY LUKE 10:3-12 THE NATURE OF THE MISSION 10:3 v. 1 sent imperative v. 3 Go away Present imperative; go your ways Get on with it there comes a time when the believer must put into action his deepest beliefs speech, morality, thoughts, obedience there are those out there whose doctrine is solid but their actions are inconsistent with what they claim to believe The command is to move Note: what is called the Great Commission; Matthew 28:18, 19 The command is not to go but to disciple the nations Go is a present participle; while you are going disciple all nations Jesus assumes movement on your part Move with a purpose; calling others to a relationship with Jesus We get wrapped up in our kingdom, we forget about God s. The 70 here are commanded to get moving then Jesus describes the Nature of their Mission behold middle voice: you yourself look and pay attention Listen up and pay attention to what I am going to say (highlighter) Do not be na ve; your work is not going to be easy The job is dangerous; prepackaged with persecution Be like a lamb but don t be na ve Going out among the wolves midst surrounded expect fierce opposition to the message of the Kingdom of God shining into the kingdom of darkness some opposition is not as noticeable: editing out praise to Jesus Other opposition will be violent; you are not facing one wolf but many wolves the wolves were not among them (Acts 20:29), but they among the wolves Matt. 7:15 false prophets sheep s clothing but ravening wolves (Matt. 10:16 adds be wise as serpents and harmless as doves ) The call is for continued trust and dependence on protection of the Sender It is hard to remain noncombatant when the enemy begins to show his teeth Vance Havner said, most church members do not give Satan enough trouble to arouse his opposition. We all should have a sense of mission from God We have a job to do according to our giftedness and will be responsible to the Lord of the harvest to how we fulfill our duty. And Acts 20 states the day is coming when the wolves will be among the sheep; 20:28-31 2 Timothy 3:12 (ASV) . . . all that would live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution. THE CONDUCT OF THE MISSION 10:4-8 Light Travel v. 4 Bare necessities; Jesus is not only their sender but their provider The call of urgency is reflected in their travel attire; don t carry extras No purse, wallet, scrip: knapsack or traveler s bag No extra pair of shoes; sandals They were to be living pictures of dependence on Jesus It challenges us on if we really trust God We can talk a good game, but the execution is far different Philippians 4:11-13 No dilly-dallying: greet no one on the way Jewish custom of elaborate, time-consuming salutations when meeting people The intention is not to be rude, but don t get caught up in unnecessary delays Stay on target; stay on focus Each pair were given a specific destination; get there without delay Whole intent is to express the urgency of the command This was not about when you get the time or when convenient Like the harvesting of crops; time is of the essence Be careful of getting caught up in lesser things We must avoid the clutter of this material life William Barclay: It is easy to get entangled in the things of this life. Once Dr. Johnson, after seeing through a great castle and its policies, remarked grimly, "These are the things which make it difficult to die." Earth must never blot out heaven. A verse summary would say this: No Hoarding No Excess Baggage Don t Waste Time Discriminating Travel v. 5-8 Finding a receptive home Peace be to this house - willing to receive the divine message It was not about going from house to house looking for the best arrangement Peace: common greeting Inner rest, well-being; ultimate peace is reconciliation with God Find a house sympathetic to the mission; stay there and get to work We must stay focused on the mission We are to pray for laborers for the harvest, not hobbyists. Workers work. A man was fishing without success when he noticed a woman nearby who was reeling in one after another. Frustrated, he finally asked the woman her secret. Are you fishing for supper or for sport? she asked. I m fishing for sport, he answered. Well, there s your problem, the woman stated. I m fishing for supper. (Reader s Digest [11/96].) Workers are intent on the job because they are working for their supper Have you prayed, Lord, what s my job? What s my mission? v. 6 if the household did not receive the peace and the message which the pair offered, it would be discerned soon. Any further greeting would then cease. Some would choose to remain at enmity with God; in essence choosing judgment son of peace a Hebrew idiom meaning a person characterized by whatever they are a son of; a character of wholeness; a peaceful man Cf. Sons of Thunder; sons of Belial, sons of disobedience Your peace the message from God announcing peace with Him Limited Travel v. 7, 8 the Seventy would need to establish themselves in the community in order to lend credibility to their message Stay put: stability of the message and quicker response Room and food would not be charity but deserved compensation Some of these homes may have been non-Jewish and not follow Jewish dietary restrictions Willingly eat what was set before them; not be distracted by their meals 1 Cor. 9:14 receivers of the gospel provide for the evangelist 3 John 5-8 Cf. 1 Cor. 9:14; Galatians 6:6; 1 Tim. 6:17, 18 1 Timothy 5:18 Paul uses the exact phrase laborer is worthy the only place where a New Testament writer quotes another New Testament passage as "Scripture. In the Didache (The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles, circa 100 A.D. Reference is made to wandering prophets abusing the teaching of Jesus. In this book of religious order it clearly states if a prophet wished to stay more than 3 days in one place without working, he was a false prophet. If he asked for money or a meal, he was a false prophet. Receive what is given without complaining The suspicion that a Christian worker is fond of good living and sensuous delights robs his work of power. " Their value system was to revolve around people not things Any breech of hospitality would have resulted in greater resistance when Jesus came to town THE MESSAGE OF THE MISSION 10:9 Heal bodies and souls Also minister to the physical and emotional: heal therapy the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you When He arrived in their villages and towns it would be their greatest opportunity to give God s Kingdom first place. Jesus the King was coming, God s Kingdom was so very near. Kingdom: is a realm, sphere, or territory ruled by an absolute monarch who functions with absolute authority. The first phase is invisible, internal; where Jesus is accepted as Savior, Lord To proclaim the kingdom is to preach the gospel; how one enters the Kingdom When the King is revealed it will be known who sought the kingdom of self and who the Kingdom of the Savior Then the Millennial Kingdom The Jews were looking for this future Kingdom; the preaching of the 70 was about the need to enter the invisible Kingdom first has come near to you perfect tense, has come and remains From this concept the kingdom of God is comprehended as breaking forth around as well as in us. James 5:8 (ASV) Be ye also patient; establish your hearts: for the coming of the Lord is at hand. THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION 10:10-12 Sad reality: some will reject; refuse to set out the welcome mat Public object lesson; note the instructions to the 12 (9:5) Go out into the street public declaration; a message of judgment When the people reject, walk away Shake the dust off: cleansing themselves of the town and the spiritual uncleanness of the inhabitants. The message is to serve as a warning Revelation refused receives retribution A Rabbinical idea when leaving Gentile territory Declares that Jews who rejected the Kingdom were no better than Gentiles. Acts 13:50, 51 Paul and Barnabas The NLT has a good paraphrase "shake its dust from your feet as you leave to show that you have abandoned those people to their fate." Repeating the message: the Kingdom of God has come to you The kingdom of God is the rule of God in the hearts of men. Receivers of the message not only enter the kingdom of God but the Kingdom enters them. v. 12 the people who hear His message and reject it are more guilty than those who populated Sodom. The Kingdom is here and they slammed the door in God s face Whenever we share a message of judgment, we do so with tears; sometimes in our eyes, but always in our hearts Before God passes judgment, He always extends mercy. The history of Israel is full of accounts of prophetic warnings and peoples refusal to heed the message. It is clear that people will be judged according to the degree of light they rejected. There will be degrees of punishment in hell. Sodom will still face eternal punishment on judgment day But these cities will receive greater judgment for the privilege of having personal representatives sent to them with an urgent appeal but reject their golden opportunity. A reminder to us the be careful how we evaluate sin. Jesus here envisions the judgment of God as being as actual event, not a philosophical speculation. Thousands today have heard the gospel and have turned their backs on the invitation to accept God s rule in their lives. We are to pray Thy kingdom come We are also commanded to be ambassadors for Christ. There is still a harvest; the need of laborers remain. Jude 20-24 (ASV) But ye, beloved, building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Spirit, keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life. And on some have mercy, who are in doubt; and some save, snatching them out of the fire; and on some have mercy with fear; hating even the garment spotted by the flesh. Now unto him that is able to guard you from stumbling, and to set you before the presence of his glory without blemish in exceeding joy, Next Sunday, we will be given a report of what is happening in a small part of Zambia. May it move our hearts to action here at home. THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY LUKE 10:3-12 THE NATURE OF THE MISSION 10:3 The command is to move Move with a purpose Be like a lamb but don t be na ve THE CONDUCT OF THE MISSION 10:4-8 Light Travel v. 4 They were to be living pictures of dependence on Jesus Whole intent is to express the urgency of the command Discriminating Travel v. 5-8 Find a house sympathetic to the mission We are to pray for laborers for the harvest, not hobbyists. Some would choose to remain at enmity with God son of peace a person characterized by whatever they are a son of Limited Travel v. 7, 8 Stay put: stability of the message and quicker response 1 Timothy 5:18 the only place where a New Testament writer quotes another New Testament passage as "Scripture. Their value system was to revolve around people not things THE MESSAGE OF THE MISSION 10:9 Heal bodies and souls James 5:8 THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION 10:10-12 When the people reject, walk away Revelation refused receives retribution The kingdom of God is the rule of God in the hearts of men. It is clear that people will be judged according to the degree of light they rejected. ============================================= === SERMON: message(100).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 2/3/2024 10:09:09 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION LUKE 10:10-16 Public Object Lesson 10:10, 11 Revelation refused receives retribution The Woe of Exposure 10:12-16 Woes Pronounced on Impenitent Cities Before God passes judgment, He always extends mercy OT history illustrates: many times prophets told of pending judgment with the intent of bringing the people back to God. If they returned to God; judgment passed by The warning here is of consequences to those who reject Him. Judgment is an actual event; not some speculation. It is never tossed out there as a far-fetched concept Many living today will one day stand before the Great White Throne where their choice of rejecting God will be judged. There are also many living today as believers in Christ, who although will never stand for that devastating judgment, are still enjoying sin, playing around with sin, entertaining sin, and refusing separate themselves from it. A wide variety of judgments may be experienced presently; the most aggravating being living with a guilty conscience.
