Every time the Lord s Supper is shared, the same sin issue arises, the same half-hearted confession is made, and no change takes place. The judgments are explained away and even God gets blamed. It is much too painful and humiliating to confess. So before sharing the elements of the Lord s Supper, let s talk for a few minutes about Mortifying the Flesh. The sinful flesh is not going away; not matter how old you are. I need the reminders as much as anyone else; so don t sit there and wonder what I heard or who am I going after. MORTIFICATION OF THE FLESH Romans 6 The process of deadening; to drain the life out of Illustration: driving a stick shift A stick shift is more than just an antitheft device You have probably seen videos of a thief jumping into a car to steal it, only to find out they can t drive it. To stop a vehicle with a stick shift you not only must apply the brakes, but you must disengage the engine from the transmission. Push the clutch pedal. You release the driving force from the power source You cut off the supply Romans 8:13 (WEB) For if you live after the flesh, you must die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live. Colossians 3:1-5 (WEB) If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God. Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth. For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God. When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you will also be revealed with him in glory. Put to death therefore your members which are on the earth: sexual immorality, uncleanness, depraved passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry; Romans 13:14 (WEB) But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, for its lusts. John Owen: the primary task of mortification is to weaken this habit of sin so that its power to express itself in violence, frequency, tumult, provocation, and unrest is quelled. We kill the influence of the flesh, not its existence The flesh and its resulting deeds, must be mortified Self and its ungodly lusts must be denied Luke 9:23; Titus 2:12 The deeds of the old man must be put off Eph. 4:22; Colossians 3:9 The indwelling sin nature must not be served Romans 6:6,12,13,16-19 First things First: Salvation If you are continuing to struggle with a sin habit: examine your heart Is Jesus there? Not did you pray a prayer, because there is no such prayer. Salvation is believing in your heart the gospel Christ died for our sins, was buried, and rose again. Isaiah 53 the vicarious, substitutionary sacrifice of Jesus Second things Second: Confession 1 John 1:9 If we confess our sins . . What does it mean to confess? To say the same thing about sin that God says about it: He hates it. If you do not hate your sin, you cannot confess it. You can only say I am sorry for the consequences sin brings into my life You can t even say you are sorry for something you like, love, entertain. Until you find no pleasure in a fleshly pursuit, you cannot truly confess it as sin. ROMANS 6 KNOW, RECKON, YIELD The pull of indwelling sin can be broken Before salvation, we had no choice but to obey the sinful pulls within Baptism: no water baptism, but a spiritual baptism The placing of the soul into the body of Christ by the Holy Spirit This happens the moment we are saved I am immersed in Christ and receive all benefits of His death, burial, and resurrection Meaning: when He died, I died. When He was buried, I was buried. When He rose, I rose. That is what the word vicarious means 6:6 know The control of indwelling sin over us has been destroyed or nullified our flesh itself is not destroyed in our co-crucifixion with Christ, but just as a dead corpse is powerless to respond to anyone s will, so our flesh s absolute power over us has been broken. (Berg) Dead people don t respond. I now have a choice. I am free from sin s absolute rule; it no longer has dominion. I am free to respond to the leading of the Holy Spirit 6:4 I now possess newness of life ILLUSTRATION Let s say you have been renting a home from Jim Young. He has been coming the first of each month to collect the rent. He sells the house to Mr. Evans. What would be your response should Mr. Young show up the next month to receive the rent payment? His power or authority to collect has been broken because the house in now under a new owner. You are now required to pay the new landlord. I no longer need to obey the flesh. Beginning with salvation, my life in under new management know resisting the indwelling power of sin begins here The power to the engine must be broken RECKON: The implication of what I know 6:11 personal application: now I consider such knowledge to be true for me I don t have to obey sin s urges But: I don t feel free I feel like I have to give in I need to know better: reckon yourself to be dead indeed unto sin I can either live be feelings or I need to know better I can live by feeling or facts James 1:6-8 a double-minded man is unstable in all his ways YIELD 6:13-19 We are all experts at yielding; we have been doing it for years Only to the wrong master Are we going to deny God or the flesh? It is not a matter of being defeated, but being disobedient. Just two choices on the shelf pleasing God or pleasing self. (K. Collier) The Christian life is not an easy life because of this warring sinfulness that dwells within us. In this thing before me, am I going to please God or please myself? 6:17, 18 A matter of obedience Paul does not prescribe some long, convoluted series of therapy sessions. Paul is saying, You got yourself into this mess by obeying your flesh and denying God, and the only way out is to start denying the flesh and obeying God. The flesh can be denied and must be denied. What we often call a lack of self-discipline is actually a lack of obedience to God. If you wish to restrain the flesh as God commands, you are being foolish to feed it. 1 Peter 2:11 (WEB) Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul; Galatians 6:7-8 (WEB) Don't be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap. For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life. We have to exercise self-denial by saying no to the promptings of the flesh We also have to say no to any pull to feed the flesh, thus making it stronger. Every time we feed it in one area of life, we make it harder to say no to it in any area. Exposure is critical Romans 13:14 What are the contaminating elements around me? What are my safe-guards? Just like a seat belt. A seat belt is always needed because the danger is always present. Self-denial protects us from the danger of giving in to the urges of the flesh. Luke 9:23 Self-denial is always needed because the danger is always present. Spirituality is not measured by what we do not do. Restraining the flesh by imposing restrictions on ourselves or others doesn t make us holy. The actual production of spiritual fruit is the work of God alone. Safeguards and restrictions are necessary. What would you think of a surgeon who did not wear gloves? We cannot underestimate the destructive nature of the flesh. Indulging the flesh is not something you grow out of Early detection and early treatment are the surest remedies. CONCLUSION The cities in Luke 10 will face judgment because they refused to have Jesus rule over them. You have probably heard your life is like a tea bag in hot water The hot water only reveals the flavor contents of the bag. Mark 7:21-23 We need a changed heart; specifically a change in the desires of the heart. Do you know Jesus as your Lord and Savior? Is there an area of sin you need to starve; refuse to feed it? Can you confess or say the same thing about that sin that God does? KNOW: as a believer you are dead in Christ RECKON: live by the fact of what you know YIELD: starve the flesh; feed the Spirit THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION LUKE 10:10-16 MORTIFICATION OF THE FLESH Romans 6 The process of deadening; to drain the life out of Romans 8:13 Colossians 3:1-5 Romans 13:14 We kill the influence of the flesh, not its existence First things First: Salvation Salvation is believing in your heart the gospel Second: Confession 1 John 1:9 If you do not hate your sin, you cannot confess it. ROMANS 6 KNOW, RECKON, YIELD The pull of indwelling sin can be broken Baptism: The placing of the soul into the body of Christ by the Holy Spirit know The control of indwelling sin over us has been destroyed Dead people don t respond I no longer need to obey the flesh. RECKON: The implication of what I know 6:11 personal application: I don t have to obey sin s urges YIELD 6:13-19 It is not a matter of being defeated, but being disobedient. The flesh can be denied and must be denied. 1 Peter 2:11 Galatians 6:7-8 Every time we feed it in one area of life, we make it harder to say no to it in any area. What are the contaminating elements around me? Spirituality is not measured by what we do not do. The actual production of spiritual fruit is the work of God alone. Indulging the flesh is not something you grow out of Mark 7:21-23 ============================================= === SERMON: message(101).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 2/10/2024 12:44:10 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION LUKE 10:10-16 Public Object Lesson 10:10, 11 Revelation refused receives retribution Mortification of the Flesh: Know, Reckon, Yield THE ACCOUNTABILITY OF OPPORTUNITY 10:12-16 The Woe of Exposure Rejection of the message is a real possibility Jesus provides instruction on how to respond wipe your feet Jesus is announcing the reality of hell; the message of damnation J. I. Packer: To announce the reality of hell is a testing and grueling task. The compassion and fellow-feeling that should mark all Christian communicators require us to do it, not with gloating and contempt, but with tears, if not in our eyes, then in our hearts. It is not to be done in a flippant manner R. W. Dale once said that D. L. Moody had a right to preach about hell because he so clearly did so from a weeping heart. Jesus makes in clear in v. 12 that judgment will come according to the degree of light that was rejected. Sodom and Gomorrah will be judged for the horrendous sin But a city that rejected the select representatives of the Messiah will suffer more. These cities heard the message, witnessed the miracles There is accountability in opportunity. Opportunity Warning 10:13 Woe is not a call for vengeance, but an expression of deep regret Chorazin and Bethsaida were both close to Jesus home base The cities would not just face temporal ruin but eternal Mind-boggling warning Jesus is saying He knows those cities would have responded had they been given the opportunity. Yet, they will still be judged for their wickedness and unbelief. Chorazin and Bethsaida Here is a warning for us Chorazin and Bethsaida were religious cities. Bethsaida was the town of Peter, Andrew, and Philip Luke 9 mentioned in feeding the 5k There is no record of teaching or miracles in Chorazin They were familiar with the message and miracles. Sodom, Tyre, and Sidon were pagan Gentile cities. The warning is that those who sit in church and yet remain unmoved by the offer of peace with God through Christ, those who are familiar with spiritual truths, but who refuse to submit to Christ as Lord-these religious people will be judged far more harshly than raw pagans who are ignorant of the gospel J. C. Ryle puts it this way, "We need not run into any excess of riot. We need not openly oppose true religion. We have only to remain cold, careless, indifferent, unmoved, and unaffected, and our end will be in hell. sackcloth and ashes signs of deep mourning, voluntary humiliation Opportunity Response repentance change one s mind in respect to God, sin, and self It is a change of mind that results in a change of behavior Sorrow for sin may lead to repentance but in itself is not repentance Repentance is a reorientation of perspective; a fresh point of view There can be no genuine conversion without genuine repentance. Four Factors to Prompt Repentance * The knowledge of God s truth Luke 16:30, 31 One can have truth without repentance Matt. 11:21-24 * Sorrow can lead to repentance 2 Corinthians 7:9, 10 Still not a sure thing; Judas felt sorry for betraying Jesus * God s kindness prompts repentance Romans 2:4 * Fear of final judgment Acts 17:30, 31 Repentance is not an act separate from faith, but saving faith includes and implies the true change of mind which is called repentance Repentance is not just an act at conversion, but a lifestyle We are daily in need of God s gracious gift of repentance. Albert Barnes wisely said "False repentance dreads the consequences of sin; true repentance dreads sin itself." Woe of Destination Opportunity Destination Hades instead of Heaven 10:15 Capernaum: Jesus second hometown We have no record of their rejection like that of Nazareth But it is clear here of their rejection of an incredible opportunity Here is another clear example that miracles do not save writing in the sky return from the dead hear God speak To hear the Scriptures is to hear God; reject the Bible and reject God Many of those who do profess Christ show by their disobedient and self-centered lives that they are not subject to His lordship. NT Illustration: 16:31 rich man and Lazarus OT Illustration - Numbers 14:11 self-centered complainers Hades: transliteration of Greek word; literally translated not seen, unseen In the OT, it is the location of all departed souls In the NT, the abode of only unbelievers; also referred to as hell It is only a holding location as all inhabitants will be finally condemned to the lake of fire Revelation 20:14 Hades is thrown into the Lake of fire Opportunity Reaction 10:16 Here are Your Options 10:16 All verbs in present tense; continuous activity Jesus charge to the 70: As Jesus represented the Father, so the 70 represent Him Rejection of their message is a rejection of Jesus and the Father Listen to hear with the intention to follow through on what is presented To not listen is to reject the gospel Luke 9:35 the Father s message at the Transfiguration Listen to Him No one can say they believe in God but not Jesus People will reject your presentation of the gospel Your only responsibility is to present it as clearly and personally as you can Any rejection on their part, is not a rejection of you, but God. Hear from Jeremiah 6:10 To whom shall I speak and testify, that they may hear? behold, their ear is uncircumcised, and they can't listen: behold, the word of Yahweh is become to them a reproach; they have no delight in it. Heed the message of 2 Corinthians 6:2 Today is the day of salvation The opportunity to reach someone is today No one knows what a day may bring forth. People get hardhearted, apathetic, senile or die. We must have a sense of urgency. We can t grab people by the lapel and scream repent or perish. Neither is our message just try Jesus and you ll feel better and have a happier life But with sensitivity we present the gospel and a message of warning. There is a judgment to come. We pray for openings. We pray for laborers for the harvest. The suddenness and certainty of death should motivate us to seek the salvation of others. During the 1981 Byron Nelson Golf Tournament in Dallas, a massive tree limb broke off and fell on a spectator who was killed instantly. It happened near the third hole where Charles Coody was playing at the time. Shortly after the accident, he was interviewed on the radio and he said, After running over and seeing the accident, I tried to play golf, yet I had no desire to play after that. All of a sudden those three foot putts didn t seem all that important. It is C. S. Lewis who said, "Christianity, if false, is of no importance, and if true, of infinite importance. The only thing it cannot be is moderately important." REASONS FOR OPPORTUNITIES REJECTED 1. Pessimism of Unbelief John 3:36; Matt. 13:57, 58 The doubt that salvation only comes through Jesus 2. Perverse Lifestyle Matthew 24:12; Hebrews 3:13 The love of sinful living. Mistakenly assume their sin will make them happy 3. Pride Proverbs 16:18; Nehemiah 9:16 Too proud to admit their need for Christ 4. Prejudice against Christ Luke 9:51-53 Bigotry toward Christians; church is full of hypocrites You can close the shutters of your mind, but you will only be in a dark place. Prejudice is knowing the answers without having all the facts. 5. Preoccupied with Self Mark 4:18, 19 Distracted and preoccupied with matters of this life 6. Power of God is Forgotten Markk 6:52 Think Numbers 14 7. Perspective of Ungratefulness Gratefulness is expressing to God and others in what way they have benefitted your life. People are ungrateful because of a lack of humility. Realizing that God and others are responsible for the achievements in my life. Don t grumble because you don t get what you think you deserve. Be grateful you do not receive what you do deserve. 8. Personal Independence No need of God; they can do it on their own Isaiah 47:8-11 Proverbs 14:12 He who says he is free from God is a prisoner of Satan 9. Procrastination James 4:14 Proverbs 27:1 THE REJECTION OF THE MISSION LUKE 10:10-16 THE ACCOUNTABILITY OF OPPORTUNITY 10:12-16 Opportunity Warning 10:13 Woe is an expression of deep regret Jesus is saying He knows those cities would have responded had they been given the opportunity. Chorazin and Bethsaida were religious cities. Opportunity Response repentance change one s mind in respect to God, sin, and self There can be no genuine conversion without genuine repentance. Four Factors to Prompt Repentance * The knowledge of God s truth Luke 16:30, 31 * Sorrow can lead to repentance 2 Corinthians 7:9, 10 * God s kindness prompts repentance Romans 2:4 * Fear of final judgment Acts 17:30, 31 Repentance is not just an act at conversion, but a lifestyle Opportunity Destination Here is another clear example that miracles do not save In the OT, it is the location of all departed souls In the NT, the abode of only unbelievers Opportunity Reaction 10:16 Listen to hear with the intention to follow through We must have a sense of urgency ============================================= === SERMON: message(102).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 2/16/2024 1:03:40 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message DISCIPLESHIP JOY Luke 10:17-20 INTRODUCTION It is important to look at this passage in the whole of the context. 9:18-20 Peter speaks for the disciples by properly identifying Jesus thou art the Christ of God Now that they have identified Jesus and declare their role as learners, Jesus lets them know what is required The Standard of Discipleship 9:23 There is to be the total rejection of self, selfish ambitions and be willing to pay the ultimate price in cross bearing, namely death. This is a direct assault against self-love, self-esteem; seeker sensitive kick a Bible through the goal post like the church near Cincinnati It is the abandonment of self; it is self-denying obedience My relationship to Jesus is so precious I would die if called upon. This is the hard call because it's against the grain of one's own self-preservation, self-exaltation, and self-fulfillment. Dietrich Bonhoeffer was a Lutheran pastor in Nazi Germany. He wrote, "When Christ calls a man, He bids him, 'Come and die.'" He is also the one who coined the term cheap grace and wrote "The only man who has the right to say he is justified by grace alone is the man who has left all to follow Christ." 9:57 the subject is again discipleship: those who are unwilling to pay the price A Comfort Seeker, don t plan on having a nice place to sleep a Procrastinator, waiting until a father dies so an inheritance can be received A Rubber Necker, it s good to touch the green, green grass of home The exact reasons in John 6 where many walked away and followed no more. (v. 66) 10:1 the Seventy are sent out They are told to Travel Light, Limit their Travel, and know Rejection will come To this point, there has really been nothing in their lessons or activity to suggest that they could expect anything but travail, labor, and hardships. Who in their right mind would sign up for such a task? The verses have been challenge after challenge The lack of personal acknowledgement of who Jesus proper identity; thinking Jesus is just a prophet The prediction of Jesus coming death The rebuke: O faithless and perverse generation 9:41 The disciples lack of Comprehension, the sin of Comparison, Competition, and Condemnation The warning to unrepentant cities Outside of the Transfiguration, not much to lighten the spirit, and that was only for James, John, and Peter That is about to change. 10:17-20 we see rejoicing 1x v. 17; 2x v. 20 What is there in which to rejoice? What we need to know is that the reasons laid out for the 70 followers in Luke 10 are still reasons for a disciple of Jesus to rejoice today. DIVINE POWER 10:17, 18 v. 17 the seventy return; 35 pairs accompanied by joy with implies companionship or fellowship; What is your walk like when accompanied by joy? their steps are lighter saying - continually expressing their joy even the demons not expressly stated in commission: 10:9 Was a promise to the 12 in 9:1, 2 The demons were subordinate to the name of Jesus Another strike against the disciples failure 9:37-40 Demons responded to the ministry of healing and the preaching of the message Remember Luke 4:31-37; Jesus preaching in the synagogue; a demon, who preferred to remain working the background, cried out with a loud voice What brings you joy? Winning the Publisher Clearing House grand price? Or Congratulations! You have just been used by God to deliver a soul from the kingdom of darkness into the kingdom of God s Son! Whatever makes us the happiest reveals our true values. Do we get more excited about temporal blessings or eternal ones? Do we get more excited about a new car or a new brother or sister in Christ? Do we get more worked up over an official s call during a football game or blasphemous theology used for a commercial of Jesus Gets Us ? When we talk of God s attributes, what typically comes to mind? Holiness, righteousness, sovereignty, immutability, omniscience, omnipresence, omnipotence, grace, love . . How about joy? 1 Chronicles 16:27 strength and joy are in His place Zephaniah 3:17 Jehovah thy God is in the midst of thee, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over thee with joy; he will rest in his love; he will joy over thee with singing. God only acts in what brings Him joy The dominant reality of heaven is unmixed joy forever - MacArthur Matthew 25:21 enter into the joy of your Lord Jude 24 Now unto him that is able to guard you from stumbling, and to set you before the presence of his glory without blemish in exceeding joy, Joy is a promise to those who abide in Christ; John 15:11 These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be made full. Joy is a depth of assurance and confidence that ignites a cheerful heart. It is a cheerful heart that leads to cheerful behavior. Joy is contentment and satisfaction with God and His dealings. True joy is evident of the presence of God s Spirit; Galatians 6:22, 23a But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. v. 18 is more about Power over the enemy I was watching Satan falling like lightening Many interpretations of the verse: Jesus observing Satan s original fall; Isaiah 14:12 Jesus noting Satan s failure at His temptation Jesus looking ahead to the cross Jesus looking ahead to Satan s final judgment keep the context in mind: the testimony of the 70 this is Jesus commentary on what they had just reported Jesus addresses their assault on the kingdom of darkness as having an impact against the head of that kingdom, Satan himself. watching imperfect tense; continuous action in the past Jesus is the spectator looking on as over and over another demon was subjected to the 70 in His name. Bell: Jesus was not speaking of Satan being cast out at that precise moment, but that his power had been broken and that he was subject to Jesus' authority." Ryrie - The power of Satan was broken, and the success of the 70 over demons was proof of it MacArthur: The picture here is of Jesus rejoicing as He observed Satan s kingdom being destroyed one rescued soul at a time through the seventy s evangelistic ministry. There is joy in the believer as power over evil is evident 1 John 4:4 Ye are of God, little children, and have overcome them: because greater is he that is in you, than he that is in the world. DIVINE PROTECTION 10:19 Behold, look at this! Jesus has the right to dispense all authority: Matt. 28:16 Jesus knew that "the enemy will not give up! Satan would certainly attack Christ s servants and seek to destroy them. That is why our Lord added the words of encouragement in v.19 . He assured them that their authority was not gone now that the preaching mission had ended, and that they could safely tread on the old serpent without fear (Gen. 3:15 ; Rev. 12:9 ) given perfect tense; will always be operating serpents and scorpions figurative descriptions of Satan s kingdom of darkness Henry Morris - "Serpents and scorpions have always symbolized the devil, and God has promised that Christ will ultimately crush Satan (Ge 3:15 ). Psalm 91:13 Revelation 9:1-4 demonic forces described as scorpions and nothing will injure you nothing will in any way Nothing; no no; triple negative Church history is full of occasions of great suffering and martyrdom The promise here is of ultimate protection 1 Peter 1:3-5 1 John 5:18 Romans 8:38, 39 DIVINE PRESERVATION 10:20 Cause of true rejoicing the 70 are overwhelmed with the success of their ministry Jesus seeks to prevent the joy turning to pride Here is a better joy Your names are recorded A caution of becoming enamored with sensational spiritual events Attention should be focused on salvation The power of the gospel is best understood as delivering from the kingdom of darkness, as giving life to that which is dead. It is being born again, is being quickened They were excited with how God used them in defeating Satan s forces Jesus is not telling them to not rejoice is such victories Put it in perspective Our greatest joy is not in how God might use us, but in having our own names recorded in heaven. If not careful, we can be envious of the miraculous works of the early disciples. They had special and temporary privileges given for a particular purpose. In 1 Corinthians 13, Paul tells the Corinthians they like the showy gifts. Nothing compares to possessing eternal salvation You have personally experienced the greatest miracle of all Rejoice a continual habitual practice Continual because the gift of salvation is eternal Acts 13:38, 39; 48 Your name recorded in the Lamb s Book of Life is not written in pencil It is written perfect tense; past completed action with ongoing results In a word: permanent It stands written William Barclay - It will always remain true that a man's greatest glory is not what he has done but what God has done for him DISCIPLESHIP JOY Luke 10:17-20 The Standard of Discipleship 9:23 DIVINE POWER 10:17, 18 The demons were subordinate to the name of Jesus 1 Chronicles 16:27 Zephaniah 3:17 God only acts in what brings Him joy Matthew 25:21 v. 18 this is Jesus commentary on what they had just reported Ryrie - The power of Satan was broken, and the success of the 70 over demons was proof of it 1 John 4:4 DIVINE PROTECTION 10:19 Jesus has the right to dispense all authority: Matt. 28:16 Psalm 91:13 Revelation 9:1-4 Nothing; no no; triple negative 1 Peter 1:3-5 1 John 5:18 Romans 8:38, 39 DIVINE PRESERVATION 10:20 Jesus seeks to prevent the joy turning to pride Jesus is not telling them to not rejoice is such victories Our greatest joy is not in how God might use us, but in having our own names recorded in heaven Nothing compares to possessing eternal salvation written: permanent ============================================= === SERMON: message(103).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/2/2024 10:37:33 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message WHEN JESUS REJOICED Luke 10:21-24 INTRODUCTION It is sometimes amusing but always fascinating what gets folks excited. We had one Christmas many years ago when a child tore the paper off the package and exclaimed, A box. Unfortunately for the parents, such level of excitement quickly dissipated and it took more than a box to bring similar joy. There are times we understand what stimulates a person s joy and other times we are amazed what floats their boat. There are things that gets your heart pumping that other folks look at with dismay. Personally, I am amazed how far on can travel in 1 hours by plane. I really like Corvairs and many of you have never had the honor of riding in such a fine automobile. We all have those experiences of trying to explain to others what we saw or did and they try their best to look interested but inwardly wishing the conversation would quickly come to an end. Much like sermons, heh? The 70 have returned rejoicing because of what took place through them as they fulfilled their ministry assignments. There has not been much rejoicing in Luke s gospel. It has been teaching, correcting, rebuking. A breath of fresh air was evident at the Mt. of Transfiguration, but that was only seen by a few. A couple of weeks ago we saw rejoicing over: The Divine Power; Divine Protection, Divine Preservation Don t get excited because God worked through you in healing and delivering folks from demon possession; rejoice because your name is written in heaven, never to be erased. We move from the joy of the disciples to the joy of Jesus. Just two points to explore before sharing the Lord s Table. What led to rejoicing in the heart of Jesus? THE PLAN OF GOD 10:21, 22 10:21 Wuest At that very hour He rejoiced exceedingly, this rejoicing being energized by the Holy Spirit, and said, I give praise to you, openly and from the heart, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you hid these things from the wise and the learned, and uncovered them to the untaught. Yes, Father, because it was your good pleasure and you willed it so. Here is a verse of praise and adoration in the form of a prayer It is an expression of joy and passion Supernatural joy energized by the Holy Spirit The only time in Scripture Jesus is described as rejoicing in the Spirit (2x Jesus speaks of His own joy; John 15:11; 17:13) The Holy Spirit is our source for supernatural joy independent of circumstances; whether good or bad Joy: to jump for joy; skip and jump with happy excitement A joy that would make you leap even if you don t. Here is joy in the work of His followers; their victories over Satan s dominion It is victorious joy; a joy from the Spirit; it cannot be worked up Rejoicing in God s sovereign grace resulting in the salvation of souls He is Lord of heaven and earth These things refers to things relating to the Gospel of the Lord Jesus Christ and in context includes what the 70 disciples had reported. The revelation of everything concerning Jesus; Life, ministry, mission The idea of Hidden is it was truth that could not be known except by divine revelation, revelation by the Spirit of God. Wise and intelligent speak of the evaluation of people according to the standards of this fallen, God hating world It is a sarcastic swipe at the scribes and Pharisees They assumed their knowledge and practice of the Law put them in favored status. From the Life Application Bible: Jesus thanked God that spiritual truth was for everyone, not just the elite. Many of life s rewards seem to go to the intelligent, the rich, the good-looking, or the powerful, but the Kingdom of God is equally available to all, regardless of position or abilities. We come to Jesus not through strength, or brains, but through childlike trust. Jesus is not opposed to engaging in scholarly pursuits, He is opposed to spiritual pride. 1 Corinthians 1:26-29; Luke 9:47, 48 MacArthur: The contrast is not between being educated and being uneducated, but between being able and unable. Matthew 11 is the parallel passage. There the invitation is given in vs. 28-30 But the preface to the invitation of intimacy with this statement God only reveals Himself to the humble. Babies are helpless, dependent. The invitation is to those who recognize their need. You can imagine the ridicule the 70 received by the refined of Jewish religious society. There has always been Rob Reiner s. There are always men in the church, intelligent, wise, and even good, to whom popular religious movements are distasteful. The noise, the excitement, the extravagances, the delusions, the misdirection of zeal, the rudeness of the agents, the instability of the converts all these things offend them." (A. B Bruce) 10:22 Wuest: All things to me were delivered by my Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son, and he to whom the Son desires to reveal Him. All things without exception By Context: the outworking of the plan of redemption handed over (Matt. 28:18) pointing back to a moment in eternity no one nobody, no exceptions knows not head knowledge but personally, experientially. The reason one does not know is because the Son has not willed to take the blinders off. My Father seems like an innocent statement but know what it meant to the listeners: John 5:18; equality with God anyone to whom the Son wills many have strong reactions to that A simple statement of a spiritual truth; John 6:37, 44, 65 The Son has all that the sinner needs We are dependent on God to reveal His salvation to us because we are spiritually blind by nature. Philosophy begins with man and tries to reason to God. Can t be done anymore than a blind man seeing the sunset. We are dependent on God to reveal His salvation to us because we are under the domain and power of Satan. 2 Cor. 4:4; Col. 1:13 Luke s primary audience, the Greeks, taught that it was impossible to know God. The focus was on man s intellect. We are dependent on God to reveal His salvation to us because there is nothing in us that obligates God to reveal Himself to us. v. 22 to whomever the Son wills God s sovereign pleasure He chose Abraham and not his brothers He chose Isaac not Ishmael; Jacob and not Esau Totally on God s mercy not man s merit The reason people react to this doctrine is because they have too high a view of man and too low a view of God A too high a view of man makes God obligated to show mercy to everyone equally. There is no understanding of absolute holiness. God could have left us in our condemnation. In His holiness, God would be perfectly just to condemn the entire human race to hell. As we look around us, the evidence is overwhelming as to the depths of depravity man will go. Abortion, Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, the destruction of marriage, the family, sexes . . . The Plan of God brings delight to the spirit of Jesus. THE PRIVILEGE FROM GOD 10:23, 24 10:23 Blessed with Supernatural Vision Spiritually prosperous are the eyes that are seeing the things which you are seeing, (Wuest) A private conversation with the disciples; probably the 70 Blessed satisfaction from God not from favorable circumstances Beatitude of privilege - what they just saw; vs. 17-20; eternal truth in Jesus Jesus told the disciples they were blessed, happy, or even fortunate that they were witnesses of the divine workings. Spurgeon: Do we think enough of our privilege in being permitted to live in an age when salvation is fully revealed and plainly preached in all our streets? It is to be feared that the commonness of the blessing has made it cheap in our esteem. 10:24 The Consummation of all History 1 Peter 1:10-12 for explaining why they were blessed Jesus is saying, "I am the One to Whom all the prophets and the saints and the kings looked forward and for Whom they longed." The faith of the OT prophet; declaring what had been given to him The faith of the OT saint; believing in the message they heard Faith is the essence of things unseen; the substance of things hoped for. Great and noble leaders looked for Jesus. But it was to simple fishermen, a tax collector, and other common men that He ultimately came. With great privilege comes great responsibility. The Plan of God and the Privilege from God are what excited Jesus. On the night of His betrayal He said, Luke 22:15 with desire I have desired I have earnestly desired WHEN JESUS REJOICED Luke 10:21-24 THE PLAN OF GOD 10:21, 22 The Holy Spirit is our source for supernatural joy Rejoicing in God s sovereign grace Hidden: truth that could not be known except by divine revelation 1 Corinthians 1:26-29 The invitation is to those who recognize their need. Context: the outworking of the plan of redemption My Father John 5:18 We are dependent on God to reveal His salvation to us because we are spiritually blind by nature. because we are under the domain and power of Satan. because there is nothing in us that obligates God to reveal Himself to us The reason people react to this doctrine is because they have too high a view of man and too low a view of God THE PRIVILEGE FROM GOD 10:23, 24 Blessed with Supernatural Vision Beatitude of privilege - what they just saw The Consummation of all History 1 Peter 1:10-12 ============================================= === SERMON: message(104).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/8/2024 6:47:09 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message WHAT MUST I DO? Luke 10:25-37 INTRODUCTION We live in a world with thousands of resources available as to how to witness. There seems to be as many resources as there are churches. I don t believe we can say there is only one right way. I do believe that like the proliferation of Bible translations, it is not an issue of doing things better but another angle on making money. People seek for what is new or up to date; they want to be on the cutting edge. If you looking for something new to try in your work of evangelism, look to Jesus. He is presented with a straightforward question: What must I do to inherit eternal life? What separates his question from someone like the Philippian jailor (Acts 16:33) is the motive behind the inquiry. The jailor was desperate, the lawyer was deceptive. The Perfect Question One of the most valuable tools available to us is the ability to ask questions. It is not always a means of gaining information but providing another person the opportunity to explore their own thought processes. They come to you confused and lay out the situation in their life. But instead of providing a quick response you help them by asking pertinent questions to help them think and come to a conclusion. There are many questions which have great implications on one s life. What kind of work should I do? Should I go to college and where? Who should I marry? Should I change jobs? Where should I live? What kind of a church do I want or need? What about children? Do I have my children vaccinated? How can I save money for emergencies? Who should I vote for? Should I even participate in politics? How many questions is he going to ask? Ordinarily, it would be wonderful to have someone be as straightforward as this lawyer. Some folks refuse to entertain such a discussion For some, they deny the existence of God even though God s Law is written on their hearts. Romans 2:15 in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience testifying with them, and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them For others, while acknowledging the possibility of God, deny there is anything after death. Ecclesiastes 3:11 set eternity in their hearts They suppress the truth in unrighteousness Romans 1:18 The question is not if we live forever but where we live forever. Who is this fellow? Lawyer: a Jewish expert in the Mosaic Law; this is the fellow who would have PhD or MDiv after his name He is asking a good question with a bad motive tempting testing Same word used in 4:12 in the temptation of Jesus He was not seeking truth but setting a trap. His question revealed his understanding; one receives eternal life by virtue of self-righteous works What shall I do to inherit eternal life? Literally By doing what, shall I have eternal life? emphasis on doing His interest was in finding something to discredit Jesus Family Life Study Bible: "Among ancient Jewish legal experts, there was an ongoing attempt to prioritize the commandments. Their debates considered which laws were light and which were weighty. The main interest in contemporary evangelism is how to make things better in this life. Whatever your felt needs Jesus can make it better. The reality is nowhere in Scripture does it say your life will be better after salvation. In fact, it is likely to get worse. The Perfect Answer Jesus has the perfect response to the lawyer while ignoring his reason for asking. 2 questions: What is written in the Law? How does it read to you? You are an expert in the Law, what is written? Stand written; permanent Phillips comments "If something has to be done to acquire eternal life, then surely the Law is the place to go." Jesus is using the Law for the purpose it was given The Law is a schoolmaster to bring us to Christ Galatians 3:24 The purpose of the Law and what this lawyer had to learn is that no one is capable of perfectly keeping the Law. They need to plead their inability to God and seek His forgiveness. Galatians 3:24 fully reads: (WEB) So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith. The Lawyer properly quotes: Deuteronomy 6:5 and Leviticus 19:18 No doubt proudly quotes the verses Key word is love - describes a supernatural, self-less, sacrificial love that is only possible as one is energized by the Holy Spirit A total commitment of selfless love: agape Includes heart, soul, strength, mind The two commands summarize the 10 commandments Loving God; Loving others Heart: the center of our being; the seat of our affections Soul: life in our individual existence Strength: the quality required to do something Mind: moral understanding In a word: the totality of your being; total devotion Love your neighbor as yourself: loving ourselves is the perfect measure to use to love others no man ever hated his own flesh (Eph. 5:29) The two commandments go together; you can t do one without the other We must first be right with God or we can never be right with man. 1 John 4:19-21 (WEB) We love Him, because he first loved us. If a man says, "I love God," and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who doesn't love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen? This commandment we have from him, that he who loves God should also love his brother. Do this and you shall live Only one problem with the lawyer s answer: nobody can do it To slip once is to fail James 2:10 what shall I do? do this Do: present imperative command; constant, habitual practice Do it perfectly without one slip up At once the lawyer should have recognized the command was beyond his ability to perform That is when you drop to your knees and cry out for mercy Instead the lawyer seeks to justify himself; to show himself to be righteous He answered correctly ; right the word is orthodox But he failed to grasp the full meaning The purpose of the law was to produce the knowledge of sin Romans 3:20 by the works of the law shall no one be justified Jesus knew the man could not do it you got to get someone lost before they can be saved The Perfect Application Who is my neighbor? Justify: a lawyer using courtroom terminology The legally binding verdict of a judge He sought in a sense to "pronounce a verdict" that he was in full accordance with requirements of God's law to love God and to love his neighbor as himself. The lawyer wanted to vindicate himself, to clear himself from guilt, accusation, blame, by evidence or argument. Notice he assumed he was in good standing with God Also, interesting that no one accused him of anything, but yet he was seeking to declare himself innocent of all charges. Was there a sense of fault in his soul? The Good Samaritan An injured Jew beaten and robbed Traveling a road that was called The Red or Bloody Way A road 17 miles long with a drop of 3,600 feet in altitude Narrow, ravines, cliffs, caverns, sudden turns; perfect place for ambush The Samaritan stopped, provided care, took him to a facility and paid for services. No bond of personal relationship between the two Opposite sides of the track; traditionally hostile Simply two human beings; one in need and the other able to meet the need The description of a neighbor is clear Our neighbor is anyone we come into contact with who has a need. And to love one's neighbor means to be moved by compassion to reach out and seek to meet that need This is loving your neighbor as yourself -- agape love is giving out to another regardless of whether you feel like they deserve it and with the aim to achieve their highest good. This is not humanly possible but only as we are led by the Spirit. The story is not the point of the account; it is important to help the needy Not show indifference or just a look of curiosity 1 John 3:17, 18 The original question is about inheriting eternal life The point is for the man to look into his ugly heart, recognize the lack of love and plead for mercy. The lawyer could not bring himself to use the word Samaritan That is the revelation of his heart He was not able to Go and do the same It was not within him to Love Mercy He assumed he was right with God because he was a member of the covenant race, he studied and was diligent to keep the Law, never missed a Jewish feast or sacrifice, he observed Jewish traditions, careful to avoid ceremonial defilement, the tithed, and observed the Sabbath. He thought he had all the bases covered. If this lawyer would have evaluated himself correctly by the standard of God s law, he would have seen how woefully short he came to the required standard. He would have asked, how can I know God as I ought? 10:22 When confronted with God s standard, we can do one of two things? Plead for mercy for our failure Seek to lower the standard to fit our lifestyle You cannot be saved by doing good works, but those who are saved will do good works. By good works, I am not trying to gain eternal life; I am demonstrating I have eternal life. And the fact is I will not do it perfectly; I will pass on the other side, I will express passing curiosity; reveals to my heart I need Jesus. The Law of the Lord is perfect for its intended purpose: to demonstrate sin in man s heart. Our text does not tell us the lawyer repented when confronted with his own lack. You can today. Do you love God with all your heart, soul, mind, strength? Do you love your neighbor as yourself? If you are trying to work your way to heaven, thinking you can do enough good works to be accepted by Jesus, give it up. Your works will not justify you You can only be justified faith; faith that Jesus did live a perfect life; faith that His death on the cross was accepted by God; faith that He faced separation from God so you do not have to. WHAT MUST I DO? Luke 10:25-37 The Perfect Question Romans 2:15 Ecclesiastes 3:11 Romans 1:18 The question is not if we live forever but where we live forever. Lawyer: a Jewish expert in the Mosaic Law He was not seeking truth but setting a trap. His interest was in finding something to discredit Jesus The Perfect Answer Jesus is using the Law for the purpose it was given Galatians 3:24 Deuteronomy 6:5 and Leviticus 19:18 Key word is love - supernatural, self-less, sacrificial The two commands summarize the 10 commandments 1 John 4:19-21 To slip once is to fail James 2:10 The Perfect Application Justify: to clear himself from guilt The Red or Bloody Way 17 miles long with a drop of 3,600 feet in altitude Our neighbor is anyone we come into contact with who has a need. The story is not the point of the account 1 John 3:17, 18 The point is for the man to look into his ugly heart ============================================= === SERMON: message(105).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/15/2024 1:27:23 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message BETTER OR BEST Luke 10:38-42 INTRODUCTION What is the one activity that you know if you did superbly well and consistently would have significant positive results in your walk with God? Then, If you know this would make such a significant difference, why did you not do it this past week? If we only had the story of the Good Samaritan, we might allow service for God to take precedence over devotion to God. But the story of Mary shows us that devotion to God must be the basis of all our service for Him. Every time we find Mary of Bethany in the gospels, she is at the feet of Jesus. John 11:32; John 12:3 It is also worthy to note that Jesus spends time with women. Goes against the cultural norm; He considers them of value. Most of us would admit that sitting at the feet of Jesus should is the most important thing we can do, but how well did we do that this past week? Jesus Himself says one thing is needful necessary, required Luke 10 is about 6 months from the crucifixion Barclay calls this text the clash of temperaments Martha is identified as the homeowner Suggesting she is the oldest in the family Bethany is located about 2 miles SE of Jerusalem; on the eastern slope of the Mt. of Olives Jesus was welcomed into the home by Martha; initiated by her, personally involved herself with His care We are not told but easy to assume at least the 12 disciples were with Him Before we get into our text, let s make sure we know about Martha John 11:19 she loved her brother John 11:21 Her faith in Jesus John 11:24 Her belief in the resurrection Spurgeon: They were both women of a choice spirit our Savior s selection of their house as a frequent resort proved that they were an unusually gracious family; they are persons representative of different forms of excellence, and I think it altogether wrong to treat Martha as some have done, as if she had no love for good things, and was nothing better than a mere worldling. It was not so; Martha was a most estimable and earnest woman, a true believer, and an ardent follower of Jesus whose joy it was to entertain Jesus at the house of which she was the mistress 39 Mary sat at His feet listening and listening and listening She was more concerned with the things of God than the societal role placed on her. Her attention was to the Living Word speaking the Words of Life She is focused on His face, His eyes, His lips People have different temperaments Some are active; some are contemplative One is not wrong; the other right Duty and devotion; work and worship are both needful Martha believed Jesus needed food; Mary believed Jesus wanted fellowship Each of the sisters responded with kindness 40 here is a distracted, working woman Martha was busy and really overburdened Distracted Literally: dragging all around; drawn every which way had her hands full of the work of the house got worried about much housework going around in circles Hospitality was highly valued in Jewish society and in fact the honor and even reputation of a woman was associated with her hospitality. Getting frustrated and angry The harder Martha worked, the more worked up she became. If you are one who likes to serve others, it is easy to become bitter to those who sit on the sidelines. Proverbs contains a dozen verses warning about slothfulness Paul laid down the rule, if you don t work, you don t eat. Martha was so obsessed preparing a physical meal, she missed out a spiritual one. If we are serving the Savior instead of sitting before the Savior, we have our priorities inverted. Sit before you serve WWW: The most important part of the Christian life is the part that only God sees are we weak and heavy laden; cumbered with a load of care? Psalm 16:11 in thy presence is fullness of joy JOY is still Jesus first, others second, yourself last If you believe Jesus has called you to a particular task, don t worry what others are doing. Martha finally vents; even to telling Jesus what to do tell her to help came to Him literally: exploded, assaulted Ray Pritchard: When Martha says, Lord, don t you care? she really means to say, Lord, you don t care at all because if you did, you would have told Mary to go to the kitchen and help me. ...She is both criticizing Jesus and blaming her sister. Underneath this are her real problems: unrealistic expectations, misplaced priorities, and misdirected anger. To say it another way, Martha s problem is that she doesn t think she has a problem. She thinks everyone else has a problem but her. Martha was busy but not blessed Spurgeon calls it the Martha spirit It is satisfied with mere activity It censures those who are more concerned with orthodoxy It is more concerned with what is manifest than what is secret. As long as the work is done, we re good. But Jesus did not come to be fed but to feed Redeeming the time; His time was short 41 My dear Martha sensitive and tender Did not rebuke her for her work; Hospitality was important in that culture Worried and troubled too much on your plate Matthew 6:25, 34; Luke 12:22; Phil. 4:6 worry : to strangle or choke; exactly what worry does to joy Webster is "to harass by tearing, biting, or snapping especially at the throat", and then "to subject to persistent or nagging attention or effort" and "to afflict with mental distress or agitation = make anxious" Troubled about many things: the meat, the vegetables, the fruit, dessert Table decorations, room temperature, lighting, seating Full disclosure: the word also has the connotation of care, concern There is a level of anxiety that is legitimate; 1 Cor. 7:32, 33, 34 Martha learned: John 12:1-3 still serving without the agitated spirit 42 one thing not the many J. Dwight Pentecost said To be occupied with Christ is more important than to be occupied for Christ. Mary had chosen something which had eternal value Martha was overconcerned how to care for her honored Guest It is possible to demonstrate one s love for the Lord in different ways The choice is not always between what is good or evil Often the choice is between what is good and what is better. A.B. Simpson: the better often is the foe that keeps us from the best In studying this text, you will often find the word priority used. It means that something comes before something else Priority is status established in order of importance or urgency. Martha s priority was supper; Mary s priority was the Savior The Lord did not reprimand Martha for what she was doing; He said there was something better. Our busyness may sometimes detract from the important business that Mary was engaged in. Nothing is more important than communing with Christ and letting Him speak to us Jesus said, it shall not be taken away for her The time with Jesus will be of value long after the meal is over. Deuteronomy 8:3 Man does live by bread alone If we don t get the big rock in first, there will never be a place for it We get busy with urgent demands and forget what is most important. An important word: chose You will never end up at Jesus feet by accident One writer notes that something eternal is cooking in this home in Bethany, but it s not in the kitchen. That meal will soon be consumed and forgotten. But the meal Jesus is serving in the living room will last forever. Don t miss the one while you are preparing the other.....I love the final phrase of the text where Jesus declares that what Mary has gained will not be taken from her. What she gained, she would have forever. Meals come and go. Sometimes mothers complain along these lines: I work for hours to prepare a nice meal and then it s gone in ten minutes. But time with Jesus is yours forever. I m sure Mary never forgot that wonderful day when she sat at Jesus feet drinking in every word. Most believers find it easier to be a Martha than to be a Mary It is good to serve but service must be kept in its proper place. Martha s actions were acceptable, Mary s were better A Practical How-to Don t blame others: Martha could have taken the correction and gone off to sulk, nobody understands me! Nobody else even cares We can t blame others for why we don t spend time with the Lord Start simply and work up from there Don t begin with a goal of an hour a day; seven days a week If you are new to this discipline; start with the NT or even just the gospel of John Maybe do Psalms and Proverbs; 5 Psalms, 1 Proverb Sitting at the feet of Jesus is something Mary chose to do There was plenty of other things she could have been doing Something you have to choose and keep on choosing So when the world says, don t just sit there, do something Know that sitting might be the best thing to do. Psalm 27:4 One thing I have asked from the LORD, that I shall seek: That I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, To behold the beauty of the LORD and to meditate in His temple. FOR SUNDAY SCHOOL? 1. What is your biggest enemy in spending consistent time alone with God? How can you overcome it? 2. Someone says, I tried doing that, but I didn t get anything out of it. Your reply? 3. Must a person be a reader to grow spiritually? Is it significant that God communicated to us in written form? How so? 4. How can we be consistent in the Word without falling into either a dry routine or legalism? BETTER OR BEST Luke 10:38-42 Every time we find Mary of Bethany in the gospels she is at the feet of Jesus John 11:32; John 12:3 v. 39 Her attention was to the Living Word speaking the Words of Life Duty and devotion; work and worship are both needful v. 40 The harder Martha worked, the more worked up she became. came to Him literally: exploded, assaulted Martha was busy but not blessed v. 41 Martha was not rebuked her for her work worry : to strangle or choke Martha learned: John 12:1-3 v. 42 J. Dwight Pentecost: To be occupied with Christ is more important than to be occupied for Christ. Often the choice is between what is good and what is better. Priority is status established in order of importance or urgency. Deuteronomy 8:3 You will never end up at Jesus feet by accident A Practical How-to Don t blame others Start simply and work up from there Psalm 27:4 ============================================= === SERMON: message(106).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/22/2024 6:15:31 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message PRAYING DISIPLES Luke 11:1-13 INTRODUCTION We began last week asking the question what is the one spiritual discipline you know would improve your walk with Christ? The second question was why aren t we doing it? Our subject last week revolved around the reading of God s Word. Man does not live by bread alone, but every word that comes from the mouth of the Father. We saw where Martha was busy but not blessed. You will never end up at Jesus feet by accident. Intimacy with Jesus requires listening to Him speak. Mary was at His feet; not just listening but close enough to see His eyes and mouth. Luke 11 moves to another key aspect of spiritual disciplines; prayer Jesus disciples had seen Him pray many times. They must have made the connection between Jesus intense prayer life and the power He showed in every aspect of life. Finally, one disciple asked Jesus to teach them how to pray. Jesu gave them a simple, yet powerful, prayer that has come to known as the Lord s Prayer The last time we studied this prayer we used an outline from D. Jeremiah PRAISE PRIORITIES PROVISIONS PERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS PROTECTION PROMISES Here is another way of looking at the prayer: The number of Relationships addressed Our Father: Parent / child Hallowed be Thy name: Deity / worshipper Thy kingdom come: Sovereign / subject Thy will be done: Master / servant Give us this day: Benefactor / beneficiary Forgive us our debts: Savior / sinner Lead us not: Guide / pilgrim Thine is the kingdom . . Creator / creature Or another: The Emphasis on God Praise Priorities Emphasis on Us Provision Personal Relationships Protection Emphasis on God Promise Let me encourage you with 3 habits and 1 caution Anticipation: His arms are full of gifts; the Lord is there. He has come to spend time with you. You have His undivided attention. Acknowledge: God is present. Just as close as the nearby chair Acclimate: Be comfortable, but not too comfortable. Whether you kneel, bow, stand, lay down. Be in a posture that requires little thought Warning: Matthew 6:5 Don t be a hypocrite Those who timed their prayers according to their location Just happened to be in the market place at prayer time Our motivation is not to be known by others as a praying person Our only motivation is know our Father who is in heaven Know you have an audience with God Long to communicate with your own words No husband arrives home with this greeting: O, thou dear, most wonderful wife. How thou art a blessing to me this day! Not out to impress people; there are no special words We celebrate being in His presence 11:1-13 We will be talking prayer for a couple of weeks THE MODEL PRAYER; THE MINISTRY OF PRAYER; THE METHOD OF PRAYER The model prayer reveals God s attributes and includes seven petitions. Seven Petitions: The Lord s Prayer Our Father in Heaven Fatherly love God is a loving compassionate Father who gives life, provides for, and protects those who trust Him. 1. Hallowed be your name Holiness Hallow: to make holy. To hallow God s name means to honor it as holy and sacred In the prayer, we are setting God apart in our minds to be praised and Adored Calvin: That God s name should be hallowed is to say that God should have His own honor of which He is so worthy, so that men should never think or speak of Him without the greatest veneration. We acknowledge His worth. We ascribe His holiness. We glorify His name. No religion ever rises higher than its concept of God It matters the manner in which we pray, sing, speak, give money We yield control; we honor His presence In prayer we are talking with Our Father Imagine the conversation as if Jesus was sitting beside you 2. Your Kingdom come Sovereignty God has supreme power and authority over everything in heaven and earth. Priorities in Living I want your kingdom principles and purposes to be lived out with me. Andrew Murray: The children of the Father are here in the enemy s territory, where the kingdom, which is in heaven, is not yet fully manifested. What is more natural than that when they learn to hallow the Father-name, they should long and cry with deep enthusiasm: Thy kingdom come . 3. Your will be done Authority God s perfect will is always being done in heaven. But on earth, human free will results in evil. We ask that God s will would take place on earth. What will that look like? Micah 4:3 (ASV) and he will judge between many peoples, and will decide concerning strong nations afar off: and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning-hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more. This is a dangerous prayer Your will be done Can you pray, whatever, whenever, wherever, forever? Oswald Chambers (something we continue to hear) it is not so true that prayer changes things as that prayer changes me, and then I change things; consequently we must not ask God to do what He has created us to do. Prayer alters a man on the inside, alters his mind and his attitude to things. What a man needs to do is to get hold of God s order in the kingdom on the inside, and then he will begin to see how to handle the riddle of the universe on the outside. TRANSITION: Praying for God s HOLINESS, SOVEREIGNTY, AUTHORITY 4. Give us this day Providence God is able to provide for all our needs. If for one minute God closes His hand, we are in trouble Psalm 104:10-21; 27-29 Matthew 4:4 Matthew means that bread itself would have no value to us unless its value had been supplied by the Creator If we have bread, it s because He gave it to us It is daily bread, because He knows we often forget He provides We do not operate on a week-to-week plan How many times is the word daily used in the NT? 2x; Matt. 6 and Luke 11:3 I can talk to Him about tomorrow tomorrow Better never trouble trouble til trouble troubles you, for you only make your trouble double trouble when you do Our daily bread Lord, as you meet my needs, please meet the needs of others Daily prayer is an antidote for pride, materialism, and worry 5. Forgive us our debts Mercy We ask God to forgive the wrong we have done. God will forgive us only as much as we forgive those who have injured us. God is merciful and He expects us to be also. The only part of the prayer with immediate commentary Matthew 6:14, 15 Lewis Smedes, It is the hardest trick in the whole bag of personal relationships. Forgiveness is hard, it is difficult; it is gut-wrenching We do not have the luxury to have an unforgiving heart In a companion text of forgiveness in Matthew 18, we come to this conclusion: While we cannot say that a lack of forgiveness will resort in a person losing his salvation, is it beyond the realm of possibility to say the unforgiving person does not have Jesus in his heart? If unforgiveness fills our heart, the Holy Spirit does not Romans 8:9 Forgiveness sets the prisoner free and that prisoner is you. Jesus says that those who live by God s forgiveness must imitate it Forgiveness is not natural; it is supernatural Remember the woman in Luke 7 She loved much because she had been forgiven much And don t buy the saying; forgive and forget How does God forgive? Isaiah 43:25 I will remember their sins no more I promise not to bring it up to others, to you, to God, or myself 6. Lead us not into temptation Protection Believer's Study Bible - This is a request for the intervention of God in life's moments of trial and temptation in such a manner that the "way of escape" is made clear (cf. 1 Cor. 10:13 ). The petition gives full recognition to the incredible deception and power of temptation and affirms that deliverance from the grasp of evil can come only from the Lord. We pray for protection from all the things that trip us up and undo us. The Holy Spirit gives us strength to withstand temptation. Temptation is a difficult word to understand We generally associate it with an inducement to do evil The word itself is morally neutral; pe??as ?? peirasm s describes putting one to the test and then refers to the actual tests. Tests (trials) come (sent or allowed by God) in order to discover a person s nature or the quality of something. Think of yourself as a tube of "spiritual toothpaste". Pressure (tests ~ temptations) simply bring out what's really on the inside (your character)! Every day we are exposed continually to see how we will respond There is a wrong alternative to every call to do right Every call to duty, call to sacrifice, call to resist sin This prayer: MacArthur: It is an appeal to God to place a watch over our eyes, our ears, our mouth, our feet, and our hands-that in whatever we see, hear, or say, and in any place we go and in anything we do, He will protect us from sin We can pray that God protects us from temptation, but many of us are guilty of setting temptation before our own eyes, of leading ourselves into temptation. J. I. Packer: life is a spiritual minefield amid such dangers we dare not trust ourselves How might we set ourselves up for trouble? Romans 6 Know Reckon Yield Forget our running shoes: fail to flee circumstances where we have previously fallen; we yield to unrighteousness We make provision for the flesh Joseph ran from Potiphar s wife Genesis 39:12 Paul: flee youthful lusts 2 Timothy 2:22 We fail to think: 2 Corinthians 10:5 bring every thought to the obedience of Christ We entertain vain thoughts; we delight in thoughts of sin When we do sin, we remain where we are allowing sin to gain in strength 7. Deliver us from the evil one Deliverance God s deliverance from enemies is a legitimate concern. We can be confident in our prayers for deliverance because we are more than conquerors through Christ. deliver from evil - keep some space between us Thomas Watson: The thing expressed is that we may be kept from evil. The thing further intended is that we may make progress in piety. "Denying ungodliness and worldly lusts;" there is being delivered from evil; "we should live soberly, righteously, and godly;" there is progress in piety. Titus 2:12 TRANSITION: A PRAYER HONORING GOD S ATTRIBUTES HOLINESS, SOVEREIGNTY, AUTHORITY, PROVIDENCE, MERCY, DELIVERANCE The prayer concludes as it began: PRAISE Hallowed be Thy name Thine is the glory Thy Kingdom come Thine is the kingdom Thy will be done Thine is the power On earth as it is in heaven Forever and ever CONCLUSION OF THE DISCIPLES PRAYER Can you say our if your religion has not room for others and their needs? Can you say, Father if you have not been born again and adopted as His child? Can you say, Who art in heaven if all your interests and pursuits are on earth? Can you say hallowed be Thy name if the One who is called by that name is not holy to you? Can you say Thy kingdom come if you are unwilling to give up your independence and accept the righteous reign of God? Can you say Thy will be done if you re unwilling or resentful of having to relinquish your own agenda? Can you say on earth as it is in heaven if you re not truly ready to give yourself to His service here and now? Can you say give us this day our daily bread without expending honest effort for it, or by ignoring the needs of your fellow man? Can you say forgive us our debts as we forgive our debtors if you continue to hold a grudge against someone? Can you say, lead us not into temptation? if you deliberately choose to remain in a situation where you re going to be tempted? Can you say deliver us from evil if you re not prepared to fight in the spiritual realm with the weapon of prayer? Can you say Thine is the kingdom if you don t give the King the disciplined obedience of a loyal subject? Can you say Thine is the glory if you re seeking your own glory first? Can you say forever if you re full of anxiety about the here and now Can you say Amen if you don t honestly mean Cost what it may, this is my prayer. * M. W. Gass PRAYING DISIPLES Luke 11:1-13 Acknowledge: God is present Anticipation: You have His undivided attention Acclimate: Be in a posture that requires little thought Warning: Matthew 6:5 Our only motivation is to know our Father who is in heaven Seven Petitions 8. Hallowed be your name Holiness Hallow: to make holy No religion ever rises higher than its concept of God 9. Your Kingdom come Sovereignty I want your kingdom principles and purposes to be lived out with me. 10. Your will be done Authority Micah 4:3 Can you pray, whatever, whenever, wherever, forever? 11. Give us this day Providence If for one minute God closes His hand, we are in trouble Psalm 104:10-21; 27-29 12. Forgive us our debts Mercy Matthew 6:14, 15 If unforgiveness fills our heart, the Holy Spirit does not Isaiah 43:25 13. Lead us not into temptation Protection Temptation: putting one to the test There is a wrong alternative to every call to do right How might we set ourselves up for trouble? Forget our running shoes Genesis 39:12; 2 Timothy 2:22 We fail to think: 2 Corinthians 10:5 14. Deliver us from the evil one Deliverance deliver from evil - keep some space between us Titus 2:12 ============================================= === SERMON: message(107).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/29/2024 11:12:07 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE PERFECT BODY AT LAST 1 Corinthians 15:35-49 INTRODUCTION We received the news Monday morning of the passing of Ed Spencer. He was on a short list of favorite professors during his time at Cedarville and was a friend of this ministry. It has been my thought for some time if I could choose the season of my departure from this earth, it would be in the spring, especially around Easter. I had never thought of it until I officiated the service for my grandmother. It was this time of year and has since been a welcoming thought. Resurrection 1 Corinthians 15 is the chapter when it comes to the resurrection of Christ Jesus Here is the most extensive treatment of the subject The gospels present to us the historical account, but the Apostle Paul presents to us the doctrinal and practical application. Such a thorough presentation that his conclusion in verse 58 is our daily motivation. Steadfast immovable working The greatest gift we have been given is Jesus The greatest truth we have is the gospel: 15:3 4 I am not here to convince you of the resurrection Neither do I feel the need to preach an evangelistic message because of those folks who only bloom twice a year: Christmas and Easter But with that being said, please note 15:2 unless you have believed in vain There may be someone here with a shallow, non-saving faith It is not a problem believing the gospel is true, but you have never fully trusted Christ as Savior. You have placed limits on Christ s control of your life Savior is fine, but LORD is something you choose to reserve for yourself To you I read John 5:28, 29 John 5:28-29 Don't marvel at this, for the hour comes, in which all that are in the tombs will hear his voice, and will come out; those who have done good, to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of judgment. The issue is not resurrection but which resurrection will be yours? Paul brings out several questions v. 12 resurrection is truth, why are some saying otherwise? v. 29 why baptize for the dead if there is no resurrection? Cannot be done; baptism has no part in a person s salvation A person who was baptized because of the influence of someone who had passed - MacArthur v. 30 why put my life on the line? He has discussed the impact of no resurrection Christ is not risen, preaching is useless, your faith is worthless, we are liars of God, you are still in your sins; we should be pitied To summarize: v. 32 eat, drink, for tomorrow we die! I want to begin with the question in v. 35 how are the dead raised up? With what body do they come? To answer, Paul provides us with An Illustration, A Description, A Contrast, A Reminder AN ILLUSTRATION 15:36-38 Now at face value, the thought of a dead body buried 6 feet under ground coming back to life is truly hard to believe. Paul was dealing with those who denied the resurrection; some who believe in the immortality of the soul, but no resurrection To Paul, it is foolish to have one without the other So the natural question is what does that body look like? We know the body goes from dust to dust, some have been buried at sea, some are cremated; some donate their body to science How about John Wycliffe: buried in the ground for 12 years and they were still so mad at him they exhumed the body, burned it, and scattered the ashes over a river which took them to the North Sea? And remember Job 19:26 in my flesh I shall see God? Paul says it is a stupid question; mindless, ignorant He says there is amble evidence around you in nature itself Death is but a sowing The body is sown; Paul does not used the word buried It is sown because we expect results This has become my thought I shared a few weeks ago on a Wednesday When the discussion of burial or cremation comes up. People of different opinions as the Bible is somewhat silent My approach: burying my body will my last testimony to the gospel of Christ My last testimony that I believe in the resurrection. I will be planted awaiting the change that is coming. It is springtime here and within a few days, you can expect to see some tractors in the field planting new crops of corn and beans. Yes, the seed dies when planted but death is not the end of the seed. Death is not an obstacle, but is essential to resurrection The new living organism is not the old one reconstructed; although there is a close connection to it Death is disorganization so it can be reorganized Death is a necessary part of the process; there is no resurrection without death Don t fret when you put my body in the ground. (no rejoicing either) v. 38 answers Paul s second question: what body do they come? The product will be determined by the will of God You cannot infer from looking at a seed what the plant is to be; it is very foolish, therefore, to attempt to determine from our present bodies what is to be the nature of our bodies hereafter. - Hodge An Illustration, A Description, A Contrast, A Reminder A DESCRIPTION 15:39-41 From botany to zoology; a death blow to evolution Each type of flesh is distinctive Each designed for particular function: fly, swim, hibernate God is able to design a body for whatever purpose He desires. Celestial and Terrestrial bodies There are differences in fleshly bodies on earth so also with heavenly The basic idea of glory is manifestation Differing degrees in beauty and splendor This is not the text to support a differences between the saints in glory but to establish a difference between the earthly body and a heavenly one A CONTRAST 15:42:45 Paul gets to a direct comparison From botany, to zoology, to astronomy Sown in Corruption: perishable, dissolution brought about by internal decay Dishonorable, weak, natural The five Bs of middle age: Baldness, Bifocals, Bridges, Bulges, and Bunions. Pritchard: Nothing works right. Our bodies wear out, slow down, decay, sag, groan, and even begin to smell bad. We do our best to slow down the process but we cannot outrun death As Spurgeon says " The righteous are put into their graves all weary and worn; but as such they will not rise. They go there with the furrowed brow, the hollowed cheek, the wrinkled skin; they shall wake up in beauty and glory. Death accelerates the decaying process. Embalming might slow it down but does not eliminate it. There is nothing more unlovely than a dead body but it will be raised without defects and deformities. Raised in incorruption A state not subject to decay or death; not capable of corruption I won t look like much by the time I die, but look at what is coming! Even I will look good. 43 Dishonor: without dignity; that which was created to bring honor to God is sown as a reproach, vile (Romans 1:26) The indignity of dust to dust Raised in glory: dignity, honor Philippians 3:20, 21 Man who was crowned with glory and honor at his creation now committed to the grave weakness feebleness, frailty A corpse in the grave is the epitome of weakness power : dunamis; dynamite; no longer subject to limitations 44 Natural: suitable for a short stay upon this earth; natural, sensual Subject to the laws and conditions of physical life; soulish Spiritual: suited for the world to come When we think of spiritual, flesh and bones does not come to mind The truth is that this resurrected body is to be a very real body, but a body that is no longer under the sway of the fleshly desires or impulses that drive the natural body.
