The spiritual body will be dominated by the Spirit Luke 24:39 "See My hands and My feet, that it is I Myself; touch Me and see, for a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have." The resurrected body has flesh, bones 45 Adam-like: tending downward toward sinfulness and deterioration A living soul Christ-like: tending upward toward righteousness and holiness Adam gave us one kind of body; made from dust Jesus will give another A REMINDER 15:46-49 The natural comes first, dies and then the spiritual comes The Mormons are wrong in saying we were spiritual beings who came to earth to become men; the spiritual was not first Here is general law: the lower precedes the higher; the imperfect the perfect The earthly state is preparatory to the heavenly state The first birth gives us that which is natural, but the second birth gives us that which is spiritual God rejected the first birth and said ye must be born again (WWW) At one time we looked, acted, talked, and thought like Adam As believers, we are presently being conformed to the image of Jesus and will one day fully attain that image. (2 Cor. 3:18; 1 John 3:2) That is the difference in a transformed life Out of the millions who bear the image of Adam we are told no two are alike Don t you think Jesus has the power to make us after His spiritual image and still be distinguished for who we are? MacArthur From Jesus post-resurrection appearances we get some idea of the greatness, power, and wonder of what our own resurrection bodies will be like. Jesus appeared and disappeared at will, reappearing again at another place far distant. He could go through walls or closed doors, and yet also could eat, drink, sit, talk, and be seen by those who He wanted to see Him. He was remarkably the same, yet even more remarkably different. After His ascension, the angel told the amazed disciples, This Jesus, who has been taken up from you into heaven, will come in just the same way as you have watched Him go into heaven (Acts 1:11 + ). The body the disciples saw after Jesus resurrection is the same body that will be seen when He returns again. Just as with our Lord, our bodies, which are now perishable, dishonored, weak, and natural, will be raised into bodies that are imperishable, glorious, powerful, and spiritual. That which hindered our service and manifestation of God will now be the marvelous channel of fulfillment. We will have His own power in which to serve and praise Him, and His own glory by which to manifest and magnify Him. Then the righteous will shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father (Matt. 13:43 + ). In heaven we will radiate like the sun, in the blazing and magnificent glory which the Lord will graciously share with those who are His. Christ will transform the body of our humble state into conformity with the body of His glory, by the exertion of the power that He has even to subject all things to Himself (Phil. 3:21 + ). We cannot imagine exactly what that will be like. Even our present spiritual eyes cannot envision our future spiritual bodies. Beloved, now we are children of God, and it has not appeared as yet what we shall be. We know that, when He appears, we shall be like Him, because we shall see Him just as He is (1 John 3:2 + ). We will not see our own resurrected bodies, or even have our own resurrected bodies, until we first see Christ s. I'm gonna let the glory roll when the roll is called in glory I'm gonna get beside of myself when I get beside the King that day I'm gonna have the time of my life when the time of my life is over I'm gonna get carried away when I get carried away THE PERFECT BODY AT LAST 1 Corinthians 15:35-49 John 5:28-29 AN ILLUSTRATION 15:36-38 Death is sowing Death is disorganization so it can be reorganized The product will be determined by the will of God A DESCRIPTION 15:39-41 Each type of flesh is distinctive The basic idea of glory is manifestation A CONTRAST 15:42:45 Corruption: perishable, internal decay Death accelerates the decaying process Dishonor: without dignity Philippians 3:20, 21 A corpse in the grave is the epitome of weakness Natural: Subject to the laws and conditions of physical life The spiritual body will be dominated by the Spirit Adam-like: sinfulness and deterioration Christ-like: righteousness and holiness A REMINDER 15:46-49 The earthly state is preparatory to the heavenly state The first birth gives us that which is natural, but the second birth gives us that which is spiritual THE PERFECT BODY AT LAST 1 Corinthians 15:35-49 John __________ AN ILLUSTRATION 15:36-38 Death is ____________ Death is disorganization so it can be ______________ The product will be determined by the ___________ of God A DESCRIPTION 15:39-41 Each type of flesh is ______________ The basic idea of glory is _______________ A CONTRAST 15:42:45 Corruption: perishable, internal ____________ ____________ accelerates the decaying process Dishonor: without ____________ Philippians __________ A corpse in the grave is the ____________ of weakness Natural: Subject to the ___________ and conditions of physical life The spiritual body will be _______________ by the Spirit ____________-like: sinfulness and deterioration ____________-like: righteousness and holiness A REMINDER 15:46-49 The earthly state is _____________ to the heavenly state The first birth gives us that which is ____________, but the second birth gives us that which is ____________ ============================================= === SERMON: message(108).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/5/2024 12:20:11 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE PRAYER LESSON Luke 11:5-13 INTRODUCTION We resume our study in Luke 11 and the teaching ministry of Jesus to His disciples about prayer. One of them brought it up after watching Jesus practice His alone time with the Father. Whether this is the same occasion as recorded in Matthew 6 or an additional teaching moment, is hard to determine. Nevertheless, the same model prayer is presented. We arrive at verse 5 and also find that this story, account, parable is unique to Luke. An unpleasant knock on the door at midnight. Then when we get to verses 9-13, we have Matthew s account of the same teaching. How is your prayer life? Or how are you and God getting along? You can tell a lot about a person when you look at their friend list. You learn much by watching who they often engage in conversation and who they avoid. We speak with those whom we like, share similar interests, going through the same struggles. We will talk with those who can be of benefit to us; a doctor, story clerk, law enforcement. We talk to just about anybody but God the Father. We communicate with those we love: spouses, children, grandchildren, friends, the brethren and sistern. So how are you and God getting along? Do you realize if just half of us take the teachings of Jesus about prayer and commit ourselves to a greater prayer life, how much difference it would make? I am not saying that all of a sudden we would see a great harvest of souls, or have 30k to repave the parking lot. That is not what prayer is about. It is not primarily about getting things. We believe in the sovereignty of God. God has a plan and He will accomplish His plan. Job 42:2 I know that thou canst do all things, And that no purpose of thine can be restrained. Ephesians 1:11 the purpose of Him who works all things after the counsel of His own will Ephesians 3:11 eternal purpose Psalm 33:11 The counsel of Jehovah standeth fast for ever, The thoughts of his heart to all generations. Isaiah 46:10 declaring the end from the beginning, and from ancient times things that are not yet done; saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will do all my pleasure; But such a belief leads many people to ask why pray? God is going to do what God is going to do. Why bother? So if you are ready and even if you are not, let s look at our text. Because next week, I will repeat the question, how are you and God getting along? THE PRAYER INTRUSION 11:5-8 Jesus has a particular point He wants to make about prayer He presents a story; many translations begin with suppose How often are you interrupted at midnight? A knock on the door, phone call? The only one I remember was a neighbor who came knocking. I was either in high school or just out; I was home alone. Upon answering, the neighbor lady needed to use our phone because the sheriff just reported her son had been shot. A midnight knock is seldom good. There was another time when a boy was throwing rocks at my sister s window to wake her up. He had recently left and his motorcycle broke down a couple miles from home. He was trying to avoid waking the folks. For you college folks, this was back in the 1900 s before cell phones. We have to put ourselves in the culture of Jesus Traveling at night was not uncommon because of the temperatures But for the purpose of the lesson; consider prayer to be an inconvenience Hospitality was highly valued; failure to do so would bring great ridicule Friend or at least he use to be; one kindly disposed Not expecting a visitor, the man s supply of bread was consumed for the day 11:3 daily bread 3 loaves; more like flat bread, indicates a real hunger The man is responding to the tyranny of the urgent Our Key Word is in v. 8 KJV: importunity NKJV, WEB: persistence; ESV: impudence; DARBY: shamelessness It is the negative of bashfulness, modesty, awe Persistence is not the lesson here: we will get to that in chapter 18 The neighbor was ashamed NOT to ask for help; to violate a social code Prayer overcomes our insensitivity; not God s The problem is not that man has to overcome the reluctance of God to hear and answer his prayers. To the contrary, the problem is located in the one who prays. If God does not respond immediately and on his terms, then the individual is apt to lose faith, either in God s existence or in his character as a loving Father. Persistence in prayer is an act of faith, a testimony to our belief in a loving personal God The story is not about the reluctance of God to answer; provide God is not the grouchy neighbor The account is about man s reluctance to ask Importunity: without shame; insensitive to what is proper; carelessness about the opinion of others The man has no shame in making an unreasonable request in the middle of the night. Jesus point is that if even a human being will respond to his neighbor in that way, then Christians should go boldly before God with any need they face, for God is more gracious and caring than any human neighbor. ON the flip side of this; some believe the shamelessness is on the part of the neighbor as a giver; knowing his reputation was at stake in providing for another. Note that he does not just give 3 loaves, but will give as many as he needs. Prayer is based on sonship; Father Prayer is based on the Father s kingdom, will, glory Necessity drives bold prayer There was a need and no resources We see our desperate need and ask for help If you want to see boldness in approaching God, read the Psalms Psalm 4:1 Here me when I call, O God of my righteousness Psalm 13:3 after asking how long will you hide? Consider and hear me Psalm 27:7 Hear, O Lord, when I cry with my voice Psalm 30:8 I cried out to you v. 10 hear, O Lord and have mercy Psalm 55:1, 2 Give ear to my prayer Attend to me and hear me Psalm 69:13, 16, 17 hear me Hear me speedily Psalm 147:7 answer me speedily THE PRAYER ACROSTIC Ask Seek Knock Present tense: keep on Asking, keep on Seeking, keep on Knocking Here is a promise but it is not a blank check Here is persistent intensity in praying Barry Bonds had 762 homeruns; but he struck out 1539 times. That is almost 3 seasons worth of at bats. God wants us to be constantly praying; even when it looks like we are striking out We continually bring needs (ours and others) before God It is not about receiving what we ask but spending time alone with God Did you ever think that God wakes you up in the middle of the night because that is the only time you spend with Him? Shamelessness is important; persistence in important We are not out to convince a reluctant God, but it reveals our interest and concern 1 John 5:14, 15 This is the confidence which we have before Him, that, if we ask (present tense - continually) anything according to His will, He hears us. And if we know that He hears us in whatever we ask (present tense - continually), we know that we have the requests which we have asked (perfect tense ) from Him. Neither one's many words nor his repeated prayers will motivate the Father to give that which is not in one's best interest. The best thing believers get in prayer is a growing relationship and dependence on God. Ask, Seek Knock; boldly, aggressively; progressively more intense Does not mean hammering away until God opens It is not about overcoming God s unwillingness to act Brian Bell: Asking = Implies humility & an awareness of need! Seeking = Is Asking + Acting! Knocking = Is Asking + Acting + Persevering! It is easier to A. S. K. when dealing with physical needs, financial needs, salvation of others But what about our own spiritual growth; the willingness to forgive; for a pure mind; to remove an angry or critical spirit? How persistent are we for those needs? Puritan Thomas Manton wrote that "If we don t receive by asking, then let us seek; if we don t receive by seeking, then let us knock." You know how annoying it was to have one of your kids continually knocking on the bathroom door? Or your grandkids at your front door? The knocking is not to annoy God but to express your own heart Spurgeon wrote that "There was a nailhead for the knocker to drop on, and people used to smite it so heavily that some remarked that such blows on the head were killing. Hence arose the mirthful proverb, "as dead as a doornail." It betokens a hearty kind of knocking, which I would have you imitate in prayer. Knock at heaven's gate as ear nestly as people knocked at doors in the olden time. Asking is verbal; Christians are to use their mouths and petition God for their needs and desires. Seek with your mind this is more than asking; it is a setting of priorities and a focusing of the heart. Knock involves physical movement, one in which the Christian takes action. Stephen Cole: God doesn t always answer according to our timetable. He knows when our faith has been sufficiently tried and our submission to His will is sufficiently complete so that the time is right for Him to grant our requests. I agree with Martyn Lloyd-Jones who writes (The Sermon on the Mount [Eerdmans], 2:201), "The importance of this element of persistence cannot be exaggerated. You find it not only in biblical teaching, but also in the lives of all the saints. The most fatal thing in the Christian life is to be content with passing desires. If we really want to be men of God, if we really want to know Him, and walk with Him, and experience those boundless blessings which He has to offer us, we must persist in asking Him for them day by day. We have to feel this hunger and thirst for righteousness, and then we shall be filled. And that does not mean that we are filled once and for ever. We go on hungering and thirsting." THE PRAYER PRINICPLE 11:11-13 Cf. Matt. 7:9, 10 The argument is from the lesser to the greater Why do children ask for things? They have confidence in their parents to provide. The passage moves from asking a friend to a father The obvious answers to the questions is NO Stone, serpent, scorpion: not uncommon for the area Children often ask for foolish things But would a father deliver a serpent? (depends on the day of the week) Father knows best, even if the answer is no Spurgeon - The point is, not only that God gives, but that He knows how to give. If He were always to give according to our prayers, it might be very injurious to us. He might give us that with which we could do hurt, as when a father should put a stone into a boy's hand; or he might give us that which might do us hurt, as if a father were to give his child a serpent. He will do neither of these things; but He will answer us in discretion, and with prudence will He fulfill our desires. You know how to give to your children; How much more shall your infinitely-wise Father, Who from heaven sees all the surroundings of men, give good things to them that ask Him? v. 13 how much more sinful parents do their best; how much more imperfect parents respond to cries; how much more while no one who asks for bread will receive a stone, no one who asks for a stone will receive a stone It might have looked like bread to our deluded vision; but to our Father it is a mere stone and will leave us starving. The comparison is between our intrinsic depravity and God s holiness Any good we might do is evidence of our creation in the image of God however marred it may have become. Brian Bell - When You Pray, Remember * The Love of God that wants the best for us. * The Wisdom of God that knows what is best for us. * The Power of God that can accomplish it. "We tend to live with what is good for us as our deepest concern. We want our friendships to be happy, our investments to have a good return, our health to be constantly good, our families to be united, and our futures to be secure. Now there is nothing wrong with wanting these things, but if that is all you want, your world is narrower than it was ever meant to be. "You see, God isn t entering into your miniscule little kingdom to do everything he can to make it successful. Instead, he has welcomed you out of your personal kingdom allegiance into the history-spanning expansiveness of his kingdom, to be part of what he planned not only before your first breath, but also literally before the foundations of the world were laid in place. "You were never created to live with your goals and needs as your deepest concerns. You and I were created to know, serve, love, and worship him. This means desiring that his kingdom flourish and his glory to be displayed. "Practically, this means being motivated more by the glory of God than by what you think would make you comfortable and happy." -- Paul David Tripp, "Lost in the Middle" THE PRAYER LESSON Luke 11:5-13 THE PRAYER INTRUSION 11:5-8 The man is responding to the tyranny of the urgent Key Word (v.8) KJV: importunity; NKJV, WEB: persistence; ESV: impudence; DARBY: shamelessness Prayer overcomes our insensitivity The account is about man s reluctance to ask The man has no shame in making an unreasonable request in the middle of the night. Necessity drives bold prayer Psalm 4:1 Psalm 13:3 Psalm 27:7 Psalm 30:8, 10 Psalm 55:1, 2 Psalm 69:13, 16, 17 Psalm 147:7 THE PRAYER ACROSTIC Here is a promise but it is not a blank check Here is persistent intensity in praying We are not out to convince a reluctant God 1 John 5:14, 15 The best thing believers get in prayer is a growing relationship and dependence on God. Asking is verbal Seek with your mind Knock involves physical movement THE PRAYER PRINICPLE 11:11-13 The argument is from the lesser to the greater Father knows best, even if the answer is no Brian Bell - When You Pray, Remember * The Love of God that wants the best for us. * The Wisdom of God that knows what is best for us. * The Power of God that can accomplish it. ============================================= === SERMON: message(109).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/20/2024 2:58:26 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message WHOSE TEAM ARE YOU ON? Luke 11:14-26 INTRODUCTION The opposition to Jesus is growing and was seeking to belittle, discredit and destroy His message. When people respond in anger, it is not usual for them to speak of the most outrageous things. The mind shuts off but the mouth continues to spout off. The result is things that make no sense, but pride refuses to admit how dumb they really are. We see it just about everyday when some aspect of media plays a video of members of Congress questioning some bloke sitting before them. Either one side or the other with opens their mouth without engaging their brain. We need reminded that the enemy is real. There is a roaring lion out to devour. There are rulers, power, principalities of darkness, spiritual forces of wickedness working against us; against which we wrestle. (Eph. 6) 1 John 3:8 (ASV) he that doeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning. To this end was the Son of God manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil. The miracles of Jesus established His legitimate authority as the Messiah. AMAZEMENT 11:14 Delivery from demon possession is a big deal Miracles are hard to argue against The result here is amazement ; the people wondered, marveled The demon keeps the man silent; now he has a lot to say Here is a one-verse miracle. No details. cast out (ekballow) - throw out, eject The focus is on the response. What does the miracle mean? Question: who is this ? what is the source of His power? Even evil spirits submit to His commands Even others were credited with casting out demons (v. 19) but did so with incantations, magical formulas Jesus possessed such authority He only had to speak and spirits obeyed This is not the first time for this type of reaction 4:36; 9:37-43 Satan and his followers are out to destroy the human race. The human race is created in the image of God; designed to bring Him glory. Satan hates you and has a terrible plan for your life. ACCUSATION 11:15, 16 Matt. 12; Mark 3 has the same scenario; though taking place in Galilee the Pharisees are behind many of the accusations Pharisees are plainly present: 11:37 , 38 , 39 , 42 , 43 , 53 Some of them were spouting forth the party line When you can t deny the miracle, you attack the source Beelzebub In Syria, it denoted the Lord of the Dung; Lord of Flies In Palestine, it was a euphemism for the prince of darkness Jesus equates to Satan, v. 18 (Matt. 12:26) These people were ascribing to Satan the gracious works of God. Mark 3:22 they clearly stated Jesus was possessed They were so concerned with legalistic details of religious life they were blind and insensitive to the working of God. v. 16 looking for a sign something to be used against Him had just witnessed ejecting a demon; want something better? this is more of a test to trip Him up sign from heaven: realign the stars, an eclipse, turn the moon to blood? Seeking some outward compelling truth of divine authority, but they can t recognize a sign when they see it their view Jesus was either a charlatan, or a false teacher the request: v. 29; also 1 Cor. 1:22 the Jews require a sign predisposed to such an attitude: escape from Egypt, journey in the wilderness, dry river bed crossings, Elijah calling down fire from heaven Signs will return someday; book of Revelation ARGUMENTS 11:17-26 Advantage of knowing their thoughts Knew the motives behind the questions asked They want a sign? Jesus evidently did not hear their remarks They could have been in the back row of the crowd How about the sign of knowing what they are thinking? Or how about a sign of opening the earth to swallow the antagonists? Abandon to eternal doom; yet He responds with mercy Jesus replies to their accusations with 6 arguments First: (17, 18) the charge His miracles had a demonic sour Such divisiveness in a kingdom brings its own destruction Desolation: laid waste, leveling a city Destroyed from within; no population left Perhaps a challenge to recall their own history Abraham Lincoln used this line believing the US would have to choose either freedom or slavery; it could not survive with both It is the picture of complete ruin The listeners were familiar with the tactics of the Roman army Their custom of utterly destroying conquered cities Satan is not going to destroy himself; self-destruct Why would Satan perform a miracle that would result in his losing his power over his victims? Become his own worst enemy Jesus presumed the presence of an evil kingdom which was opposed to God s rule Clearly asserted a being called Satan, who has his own kingdom He opposes God and all that He stands for Second: (19) A little takes one to know one turn Turn the accusation against them They had naturally assumed their own cast out demons by the power of God The implication is that these others who cast out demons must also be from demonic sources if His source was. Differing style: Jesus spoke; they went through their rituals and magic formulas Acts 19; the 7 sons of Sceva, a Jewish high priest Jesus, I know, and Paul, I know; but who are you? It s got to be one or the other They could not attribute Jesus work to the power of God and they would not speak of their own exorcisms as the work of the devil. Third: (20) If the miracle was not performed by dark powers, it must have its source in the power of God. The conclusion: the kingdom of God is among them The message of the 70 (10:9) 7:22 testimony given to John if is not a condition of doubt but since the finger of God occurs throughout Scripture referring to the power of God at work. Psalm 8:3 the heavens were the work of (His) fingers Exodus 8:19 Egyptian magicians This is the finger of God Exodus 31:18 tablets written with the finger of God True source of power; In Matthew account, demons cast out by the Spirit of God showing the source of the power The casting out of demons shows another kingdom has arrived Demonstrating victory over the forces of evil, a victory consummated at the cross, vindicated by the resurrection, firmly established at the return and defeat of the antichrist, Satan in prison, 1000 year reign on throne, Lake of Fire. Satan is still powerful but His power is limited, his doom is sealed, and his time is short. Fourth: (21, 22) The strong man guarding his house Whoever attacks the strong man must be stronger Satan is the strong man; Jesus is stronger The immediate application is that as Jesus cast out demons, Satan was bound. When the kingdom of God increases, the kingdom of darkness decreases 1 John 4:4 The strong man indwells His followers The picture is of complete domination When we were born again, we were set free from Satan s power and control. We were rescued from the domain (might of the strong man) of darkness and transferred to the kingdom of the Son. Colossians 1:13, 14 1 John 5:18 Fifth: (23) No neutrality Each person volitionally takes a stance as to which kingdom he belongs Every person is in one kingdom or the other; right now, not just at death The mission of the Lord s followers is to gather; picture of netting fish 9:50 is a test of outward conduct; here is test of inward conduct Contrast: those not with Jesus are scattering No gain but expected coming destruction You do not have the luxury of sitting on the fence; there is no such thing To be undecided is to be decided The history to the song I Have Decided to Follow Jesus states the words were recited by a convert in India as he and his family were being killed. Neutrality toward Jesus is offensive to God; it is also dangerous Neutrality in religion is always cowardice. God turns the cowardice of a desired neutrality into terror. --Oswald Chambers Sixth: (24-26) A short explanation of the actions of evil spirits The wilderness or desert areas were considered to be the place where evil spirits lived. Jesus describes the nature of evil spirits: restless, wandering beings seeking a body in which to live (demoniac of Gadara; Luke 8) Here is the fellow who tries to be neutral He has reformed; turned over a new leaf Experienced a Jewish exorcism The Pharisees were all about reformation, making bad people good and good people better. They had a list of religious rules a mile long all designed to curb the excesses of human behavior. Even their exorcisms were designed to help a person clean up his act and live a normal life Any reformation is incomplete unless Christ comes in to live What are we to learn from this illustration? J C Ryle says it well: Let us observe ... how dangerous it is to be content with any change in religion short of thorough conversion to God . Jesus words are a solemn warning to us, never to be satisfied with religious reformation without heart conversion (p. 26). As Martyn Lloyd-Jones points out, we must always remember that there are other powers, beside that of Christ, which can give results. ... It is possible for men and women to get relief from many of their ills and troubles apart altogether from the gospel ( It is wise for us to be aware of the plans and strategy of Satan A person who has been cleansed from evil and delivered from Satan s power must ever be alert because Satan is ever lurking The secret of defeating Satan s plans to return are to become so filled with God s Word and His Spirit, Satan will find no opportunity to reinvade a life. Exorcism is not the end goal. Its purpose is to drive out evil so that truth and righteousness may take its place. And empty house just invites squatters. The man is no longer a drunk, but does He know Christ? The lady is no longer a prostitute, but does she know Jesus? The teenager had given up drugs, but what will fill the emptiness? there is a very real sense in which a reformed person without God can actually be worse off than an unreformed person because at least the latter knows he s messed up and can t save himself, while the former is often proud of the progress he has made on his own. John MacArthur writes cogently: Jesus had little trouble reaching prostitutes, thieves, extortioners, murderers, and the outcasts of society. But he had an almost impossible time reaching religious and moral people who were under the delusion that outward propriety made them acceptable to God. The fact is, there has probably never been a group more committed to a demanding religious and moral code than the Pharisees, and there has never been a group so far from God. v. 26 is the warning to those in the crowd don t take lightly the strength and determination of evil spirits protection only comes from being a part of the kingdom of God and living a life in the Spirit WHOSE TEAM ARE YOU ON? Luke 11:14-26 1 John 3:8 AMAZEMENT 11:14 amazement ; the people wondered, marveled Jesus possessed such authority He only had to speak and spirits obeyed ACCUSATION 11:15, 16 When you can t deny the miracle, you attack the source Beelzebub: Lord of the Dung; Lord of Flies These people were ascribing to Satan the gracious works of God. This is more of a test to trip Him up ARGUMENTS 11:17-26 First: Such divisiveness in a kingdom brings its own destruction 11:17, 18 Second: Turn the accusation against them 11:19 Third: the kingdom of God is among them 11:20 Satan is still powerful but His power is limited, his doom is sealed, and his time is short. Fourth: Satan is the strong man; Jesus is stronger 11:21, 22 1 John 4:4 Fifth: No neutrality 11:23 Sixth: the nature of evil spirits 11:24-26 Any reformation is incomplete unless Christ comes in to live And empty house just invites squatters. ============================================= === SERMON: message(110).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/27/2024 9:22:10 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message A BLESSED CLARIFICATION Luke 11:27, 28 INTRODUCTION We finished the passage from last week with the Sixth Argument Jesus gave in response to the Accusation presented that delivering a person from demon possession was accomplished through the authority of Satan. We spoke of the nature of evil spirits and their desire to inhabit a body. Luke 11:24-26 was describing a person who had gone through some type of reformation but fell short of a personal relationship with Jesus. Jesus was about to go on in and direct some thoughts to the request for a sign; v. 16 An interruption takes place THE BLESSING PRESENTED 11:27 This account is unique to Luke; maybe received from one of the women who had accompanied Jesus; 8:1, 2 Here is a woman caught up in the moment Raised her voice with earnestness She has witnessed a miracle; witnessed the teaching ministry of Jesus She heard His response to the accusation Jesus is working by the finger of God; not even the strong right arm She is one of the crowd described in amazement , marveling Your mother must be so proud She must be thrilled to have given birth to such a successful outspring You are such a gift to her So she honors Mary; probably did not know her Though it is possible Mary could have been one of the number Here is one mother honoring another Here is a good fellow so we honor the parents I can remember my parents be congratulated because of their kid And, of course, the parents received the blame if the kid was a problem these people should not be allowed to reproduce You cannot deny that Mary received a high honor Every Jewish mother desired to be the mother of the Messiah The wonder is not that this woman gave forth praise to Mary So as not to fall into the idolatry category, we probably fail to honor Mary as we should. It was said in Genesis the seed of the woman would crushed the head of the serpent We must give honor to whom it is due. A young woman, engaged but not married, pregnant in a town with a lousy reputation. Blessings on her! THE BLESSING PREFERRED 11:28 Jesus did not deny the elevated position that Mary possessed He did not rebuke the lady s praise or the blessed place Mary had Mary herself knew: 1:48; the angel said so: 1:28 To hear the Word of God and keep it This is the highest blessing and it is within our reach Even Mary stated, Be it done to me according to thy word It was her faith that made her blessed Even the happiness of Mary consisted of her being a true believer in Christ than in her being His mother This is your delight Blessed are they that are hearing the Word of God and keeping it The implication is this: the Bible is a book to live by You have a Bible, but is it an open Bible? It should come to you as a major warning if you lose your appetite for the Word of God The Bible is not to be read for literature sake but a means to an end. The end is a personal knowledge of and personal union with the Lord Jesus. The lady in v. 27 was caught up in the moment, but no record of a personal relationship with Jesus. She would fall into the context of the previous verses; one who was reformed but not regenerated. One who looked good on the outside; one who misdirected her praise Moralism, living by a high ethical code, has led many to hell. They are clean but empty; Matthew 12:44 In that day, there was no one more clean than the Pharisees 11:39 They were the moralists of the day They adhered to the strictest code of living Testimony of Paul: Acts 22:19; 26:4, 5 Jesus uses the interjection of the woman to establish the true basis of blessing; The Word of God hear and keep we are to always hear the Word of God with the intent to obey the Word of God The Word will bring faith: Romans 10:17 A prejudiced and wicked heart will never see the light shining Jesus had just performed a miracle They had just attributed the gracious work of God to Satan Matthew 12:31, 32 Jesus speaks of the unpardonable sin Keeping the Word of God is a far greater blessing than giving birth to the Messiah. We live in a world where we often misinterpret the blessing of God. If a person has many material goods, he is especially blessed by God A large church ministry is more blessed than a smaller one The book of Proverbs does not use the word bless but better Evaluate your thinking with this list: Proverbs 3:14 (KJV) For the merchandise of it (WISDOM) is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold. Proverbs 8:11 (KJV) For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it. Proverbs 8:19 (KJV) My (WISDOM) fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver. Proverbs 12:9 (KJV) He that is despised, and hath a servant, is better than he that honoureth himself, and lacketh bread. Proverbs 15:16 (KJV) Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith. Proverbs 15:17 (KJV) Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith. Proverbs 16:8 (KJV) Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right. Proverbs 16:16 (KJV) How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver! Proverbs 16:19 (KJV) Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud. Proverbs 16:32 (KJV) He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city. Proverbs 17:1 (KJV) Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife. Proverbs 19:1 (KJV) Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool. Proverbs 19:22 (KJV) The desire of a man is his kindness: and a poor man is better than a liar. Proverbs 21:9 (KJV) It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house. Proverbs 21:19 (KJV) It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman. Proverbs 25:24 (KJV) It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house. Proverbs 27:5 (KJV) Open rebuke is better than secret love. Proverbs 28:6 (KJV) Better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness, than he that is perverse in his ways, though he be rich. The testimony of Sir Walter Scott: Men of profound intellect, men who have risen to the very top of literary success, like Sir Walter Scott, have lived by the Bible. It was the one thing he cared for when he was a dying man. He said, Read me the book, and they said to him, What book? and he said, There is only one book. Read the Book. What does a blessed man do? Psalm 1 delights, meditates, walks He does not pattern his life contrary to the Word of God He does not become accustomed to the philosophies ungodly Four Views of Scripture A Delightful Story Book: human document, full of myth, legend A Divine Story Book: some parts are inspired Volume 1 of the Truth Vol. 2 is reason; Vol. 3 is experience Each volume is equal but Vol. 2 and 3 carry more weight Authoritative Counsel of God Word is sufficient in dealing with problems Does not need shored up with psychological insight Ps. 119:89 Forever, O LORD, Your word is settled in heaven. 2 Peter 1:3 2 Peter 1:16- 2 Cor. 3:5 2 Cor. 9:8-10 John 17:17 Micah 2:7 Mark 12:24 Hebrews 4:12 Acts 20:20, 27 Col. 2:3, 4, 8 1 Thess. 2:13 1 Cor. 5:8 You are going to go through stages Medicine: Psalm 119:25 My soul clings to the dust You want to read in the am? My soul clings to the sheets Dry Cereal: Psalm 119:81 Take it or leave it Peaches and Cream: Psalm 119:97 O how I love Thy law Have a Plan Mark It: Psalm 119:9 Memorize It: Psalm 119:11 Meditate on It: Psalm 119:15 Master It: Psalm 119:18 Voltaire made the prophecy that in one hundred years the Bible would have passed away, yet in the very room in which he was, the Geneva Bible Society, not long after, started its operations, and the very press which Voltaire had used for the propagation of his atheistic opinions was used for the printing of the Scriptures; and under that very roof, within twenty years, there were above twenty tons of Bibles. When the Word of God, faithfully taught by the people of God, and empowered by the Spirit of God, falls down, people become different. Lusting people become pure, fearful people become courageous, thieves become givers, demanding people become servants, angry people become peacemakers, complainers become thankful, and idolaters come to joyfully worship the one true God. The ultimate purpose of the word of God is not theological information, but heart and life transformation. -Paul David Tripp That man is blessed of God John 17:17 A BLESSED CLARIFICATION Luke 11:27, 28 THE BLESSING PRESENTED 11:27 Your mother must be so proud You cannot deny that Mary received a high honor THE BLESSING PREFERRED 11:28 Jesus did not deny the elevated position that Mary possessed It was her faith that made her blessed The Bible is a book to live by It should come to you as a major warning if you lose your appetite for the Word of God There was no one more clean than the Pharisees 11:39 Jesus uses the interjection of the woman to establish the true basis of blessing We are to always hear the Word of God with the intent to obey the Word of God A prejudiced and wicked heart will never see the light shining We live in a world where we often misinterpret the blessing of God. Read the Book. Psalm 1 Ps. 119:89 2 Peter 1:3 John 17:17 A BLESSED CLARIFICATION Luke 11:27, 28 THE BLESSING PRESENTED 11:27 Your mother must be so proud You cannot deny that Mary received a high honor THE BLESSING PREFERRED 11:28 Jesus did not deny the elevated position that Mary possessed It was her faith that made her blessed The Bible is a book to live by It should come to you as a major warning if you lose your appetite for the Word of God There was no one more clean than the Pharisees 11:39 Jesus uses the interjection of the woman to establish the true basis of blessing We are to always hear the Word of God with the intent to obey the Word of God A prejudiced and wicked heart will never see the light shining We live in a world where we often misinterpret the blessing of God Read the Book. Psalm 1 Ps. 119:89 2 Peter 1:3 John 17:17 ============================================= === SERMON: message(111).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/11/2024 9:33:01 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message Let the Lower Lights Be Burning Open My Eyes Open Our Eyes, Lord Be Thou My Vision This Little Light of Mine O Love That Will Not Let Me Go All for Jesus Now I Belong to Jesus Heaven Came Down EYE EXAM Luke 11:33-36 INTRODUCTION There has probably been a time in your life, as it has in mine, when you finally understood that our eyes sometimes need help. It started with the need for assistance in either distance or close up. Adjustments continued to be made to strengthen visual aids. Or it could be a situation where there was a struggle with identifying colors. We then graduated to things called cataracts, macular degeneration, glaucoma. Prescription changes were no longer going to help; surgery was required. And if you are like me after cataract surgery, you did not realize the gradual decline of a grey haze covering the eyes and changing the color of the sky. Suddenly, the world was brighter and the sky bluer. The downside was that lights at night were also brighter. That explains vision in the physical realm. But eyes, vision, light are used to describe spiritual or metaphorical ideas. A variety of phrases have been used to describe finding clarity. The light came on; Now I see; There is a light at the end of the tunnel; or negatively, All that was said has left me in the dark. John 3:19-21 The Prerequisite for Sight 11:33 These verses are a commentary upon the previous situations The slanderous accusations The evil generation had refused to believe in His teachings; refused to accept the miracles Jesus addressed them concerning their poor spiritual eyesight Jesus is greater than Solomon; greater than Jonah Jesus is a greater light Jesus is talking about Himself Man s unbelief and rebellion are inexcusable Jesus had just spoken about the sign of Jonah; the sign of the resurrection He is saying that the light of that sign will not be so obscure or secret that no man will see it No man can claim ignorance Light exists in order for us to see How many of you have been in a cavern or cave when they turn out the lights you cannot see the hand in front of your face? Some see light and are dazzled by it, but fail to see the significance of the light Others misinterpret the light: v. 27; praise the wrong thing The Pharisees saw nothing of the light The light shines brightly today but many refuse to acknowledge it In the context they are saying, it is your fault we don t believe you. Jesus says, you got it wrong. The shoe is on the other foot. The light is shining, the problem is your vision You are taking the light and hiding it It was never an issue of light. It was always an issue of sight. The use of light and lamps was a favorite device used by our Lord Matthew 5:14-16 is a similar analogy; focus on light of disciples Matthew 6:22, 23 the darkness of an unsound eye; Luke 11:34 Matthew 15:3; 23:33; Jews were hiding their light behind the traditions of the elders The statutes of men had become the secret place of v. 33 Where men were led to and kept in darkness We know from John 1:9 that He is the light that came to enlighten every person in the world; yet, His own not only refused to accept His light, they sought to put it out. They hated the message. They hated the indictment of their sin and hypocrisy. They hated the idea that He called on them to acknowledge themselves as poor, prisoners, blind and oppressed. 4:18 And that was in His hometown. Men still hate the light in the last days 2 Timothy 3:1-9 having a form of godliness never come to the knowledge of the truth We have already seen the heavy charge against many in the last day 10:13-15 Light exists in order for us to see. The Principles of Sight 11:34 the eye is the organ that brings light into the body the light rays are translated by the mind into information that makes things clear; (at least most of the time) the eye is both a receiver and reflector The eye is quite sensitive; the smallest spec is an irritant body soma; connotation of the entire person Jesus was making a comparison of the realm of the spirit single single, simple, sincere The focus was upon that one thing , that is eternal and certain: God s kingdom Jesus was referring to spiritual vision that is sensitive to the things of God Also meaning clear, healthy ; generous It is able to see widely, clearly. It indicates not only straightforward vision that is centered upon God, but also peripheral vision that sees God s work and will. If you cannot see clearly, it impacts the way the whole body works You become wavering, unstable full of light fully lit Points out the God-ness orientation of the spiritual eye that in centered upon Christ evil - In a figurative sense it refers to that which is evil, vicious, degenerate Ignore the light long enough and you go over to the dark side We know physically there are things that cloud our vision; cataracts, macular degeneration, detached retina The problem is not with the light but the eyes Fortner: describes things that blind the eyes of men - The Darkness of Our Nature. The Cataracts (blinding scales) of Tradition and Heresy. The Glaucoma (haziness) of Self-righteousness. The Myopia (shortsightedness) of Worldliness [[Mammon repays its worshippers with blindness.]] Spiritual sight: Romans 11:8-10; 2 Cor. 4:4; 2 Thess. 2:9-12 There is still plenty of evidence that demands a verdict But many love darkness rather than light because their deeds are evil Reject truth for preference of sin and evil (John 3:19) The nation of Israel was in darkness; they refused the light Bring condemnation upon themselves J. C. Ryle: The single heart is a heart which is not only changed, converted, and renewed; but thoroughly, powerfully, and habitually under the influence of the Holy Ghost. It is a heart which abhors all compromises, all luke-warmness, all halting between two opinions in religion. It sees one mighty object, the love of Christ dying for sinners. It has one mighty aim, to glorify God and do His will. It has one mighty desire, to please God and be commended by Him. Psalm 27:4 One thing I desire; one thing I do; one thing I live for 10:42 one thing is needed, and Mary has chosen the good part May God help us to have a single focus. Premonition of Sight 11:35 (strong warning) take heed watch out Many in the audience thought they had light; they could see clearly They had spiritual insight; understood spiritual truth John 12:35-38 some of Jesus last words to the nation Jesus has been talking to the massive crowd v. 35 you is singular; now speaking to the individual Has the light of the gospel changed your life? There is a darkness that comes by ignorance and a darkness that comes by stubbornness. The latter is more dangerous These Jewish leaders thought they had the light. But it was darkness. The cults think what they have is light but it is darkness 2 Corinthians 11:13-14 (ASV) For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, fashioning themselves into apostles of Christ. And no marvel; for even Satan fashioneth himself into an angel of light. Romans 1:21-22 (ASV) because that, knowing God, they glorified him not as God, neither gave thanks; but became vain in their reasonings, and their senseless heart was darkened. Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools, Take care that what light we have does not cease and become darkness to ourselves and others Look at the US. The light has been rejected. Many of the same institutions which were started by Congregationalists, Calvinists, Baptists have been turned over to the tent-dwelling, Jew hating morons of today. Jesus' teaching presents a vital principle in our modern age where visual stimuli are everywhere and sadly (and seductively) much of what our eyes are exposed to is spiritual darkness. The implication is clear --- when we take in the spiritual darkness that is so pervasive in our immoral, godless society, we are at definite risk of making our body full of darkness! Psalm 101:3 I will set nothing wicked before my eyes You are what you see Hughes: The condition of the eyes determines the quality of the light that enters the body. Not just physically but spiritually How can light in a person become darkness? - Fortner Men turn light into darkness when * They turn the grace of God into lasciviousness. * They pervert the ordinances of the gospel into sacraments. * They make God s holy law a means of holiness. (works salvation) * They make freedom from the law a license to sin. * They make the graces of the Spirit conditions of grace. * They make the doctrine of Christ salvation. (no personal relationship) * They make divine sovereignty an excuse for irresponsibility. * They make character and conduct meaningless. * They make character and conduct a basis of hope and assurance. The Preciousness of Sight 11:36 at first, it seems to be a mere repetition of the previous verses but a further look shows that it intensifies the meaning when light comes in, it shines when the light comes in, you know it the focus on whole body full of light no part dark bright shining Jesus was calling for a complete commitment to His lordship in every are of life. Only in this fashion can there be a bright shining witness to Him It is not possible to pass from darkness to light without knowing it The shining light dispels darkness The shining light exposes what is in us What we are The light shining in us shines out of us to others John 8:12 Again therefore Jesus spake unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in the darkness, but shall have the light of life. Ephesians 5:8 For ye were once darkness, but are now light in the Lord: walk as children of light 1 John 1:6, 7 If we say that we have fellowship with him and walk in the darkness, we lie, and do not the truth: but if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanseth us from all sin. 1 Peter 2:9 But ye are a elect race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God's own possession, that ye may show forth the excellencies of him who called you out of darkness into his marvellous light: There will come a time when God will turn the light off We might fool others with our moral walk, but we cannot fool God If you are having trouble seeing the spiritual light, ask God for help EYE EXAM Luke 11:33-36 John 3:19-21 The Prerequisite for Sight 11:33 Jesus addressed them concerning their poor spiritual eyesight It was never an issue of light. It was always an issue of sight. Matthew 5:14-16 Matthew 6:22, 23 Matthew 15:3; 23:33 The Principles of Sight 11:34 single simple, sincere Jesus was referring to spiritual vision that is sensitive to the things of God evil - go over to the dark side Spiritual sight: Romans 11:8-10; 2 Cor. 4:4; 2 Thess. 2:9-12 Premonition of Sight 11:35 John 12:35-38 some of Jesus last words to the nation v. 35 you is singular There is a darkness that comes by ignorance and a darkness that comes by stubbornness The condition of the eyes determines the quality of the light that enters the body The Preciousness of Sight 11:36 Jesus was calling for a complete commitment to His lordship in every area of life. It is not possible to pass from darkness to light without knowing it John 8:12 Ephesians 5:8 1 John 1:6, 7 1 Peter 2:9 ============================================= === SERMON: message(112).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/18/2024 1:21:15 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message DINING OUT LUKE 11:37-44 INTRODUCTION A popular occupation today is that of an influencer. I admit I don t understand it for two reasons: one is why someone becomes so dependent on the opinion of somebody you don t know; and two, how in the world did some nobody decide that their role in life was to give direction to the masses. Influencers have always been around. We use to call them teachers, coaches, governors, and presidents. We found them in the military and on the athletic field. The best influencers we called mom and dad. Hootsuite bills itself as the all-in-one social media tool has a list of the top influencers of 2024 to be found on Instagram. These people earn thousands and millions of dollars sharing branded content with their audience. The top person in the article I read had 80 million followers as a comic. I can t promise his name. The second person on the list with 52 million followers was Huda Kattan for her hudabeauty brand account. I am sure there are women who don t leave the house without consulting that latest video. I am not against looking nice. As the old evangelist said, if the barn needs painting, paint it. Who is your influencer? Who do you depend on for direction? Who helps you with your wardrobe, makeup, hairstyle, automobile, restaurants, and the list goes on and on and on and on. These are not self-help videos, but helping people navigate life. I read someplace the other day what a genius Taylor Swift is. She is dating a football player which will popularize that sport, which will lead to more head injuries, which will make more Taylor Swift fans. Genius! Full disclosure: I do research on the best products to buy, sometimes online or asking those whose opinions I respect. But I don t believe I am to the place where I need to log in to see what I should wear today. And you say, obviously . Our text in Luke 11 brings us to a dinner meeting Jesus has with an influencer of His day. Pharisees had that role with the nation of Israel. They, along with the scribes who show up next, literally dictated every aspect of life. Not just in the religious world, but what you eat, how to wear clothes, what role each person has. It was through their elevated position in society that established the traditions practiced in Jesus day. Like Jesus, they were interested in the will of God, but approached it from a different point of view. We are about to learn the fallacies of their approach. Things to keep in mind for context Know the party line: attributing miracles to Satan; Matt. 12 Hiding the light behind traditions; 11:33 Bright shining becoming complete darkness; 11:35 The problem is not light but sight What the people need to know that such thinking will result in judgment This section will reveal the consequences of their continued hostility. DINNER COMPANION 11:37 Know nothing of motive of Pharisee invitation Many times we learn a question is asked to test Him; no such record here What we do know, he is the typical Pharisee; legalistic and little tolerance for differing understandings of God s will We do know this fellow is part of the wicked generation Jesus spoke about We do know that Jesus is still reaching out to this crowd He is still interested in their conversion, but He will not sugarcoat it. We tend to push away religious hypocrites; Jesus says we can still dialogue with them. Let us also remember His boldness in speaking the things of God J.C. Ryle: If we feel that we dare not imitate Christ in the company which we are invited to join, we may be sure that we had better stay at home." Jesus comes in and flops Himself down to recline at the table MacArthur makes a good point about religionist in order to live out your religion and put on a convincing show, you elaborate the external, so you expand the emblems; you expand the functions; you expand the ceremonies; you expand the rituals; you proliferate the prescriptions. That s exactly what the Jews had done way beyond the Old Testament. That s what the Roman Catholics, the Greek Orthodox have done way beyond what the New Testament teaches, adding almost endless rituals, routines, ceremonies, regulations, because there s nothing on the inside but you can create a bigger illusion that way. DINNER CONFUSION 11:38 Marveling that Jesus did not ceremonially wash His hands All about ceremony; nothing to do with getting the dirt off The issue is all about ceremony; tradition of Pharisees A word about legalism Legalist seem to be concerned with holiness, but Jesus had more problems with legalist than any other group. It was legalist that put Jesus on the cross; not the adulterers and robbers Steven Cole: When you study the life of Christ, it is noteworthy how He deliberately did things to provoke the legalists. He could have healed people on any other day of the week, but He often did it on the Sabbath. He could have been more discreet in violating the Pharisees rules, but He did it openly. When a Pharisee invited Jesus to dinner, He could have gone along with their elaborate hand-washing custom, but He deliberately ignored it. When they questioned Him about it, He could have been more polite, but He blasted them for their hypocrisy. When a lawyer pointed out that Jesus had offended them as well, He didn t say, I m sorry! I didn t mean to offend you good folks. He said, Woe to you lawyers as well! Jesus confronted legalism as sin. What is legalism? It is not an emphasis on obedience. Every book of the Bible tells us to obey. It is not establishing man-make rules. E.g. curfew by parents, a dress code for church or work Legalism is an attempt to gain favor with God or to impress our fellow man by doing certain things (or avoiding other things), without regard to the condition of our hearts before God. At the root of legalism is the sin of pride, because the legalist thinks that he is able to commend himself to God by his own good deeds. Salvation is about a transformed heart; life flows from the heart. Pharisees saw themselves as good people because they kept the Law But they never applied it to the heart level. Jesus is about to explain that if they were as concerned about cleaning the inside as they are the outside, they would be in good standing with God. 11:38 Jesus response; definitely knew his thoughts; 11:17 Possibly overheard mumbling There was no Mosaic law about washing before eating That comes from your mother Edersheim, the great Jewish scholar, writes, "Water jars were kept ready to be used before every meal. The minimum amount of water to be used was a quarter of a log, which is defined as enough to fill one and a half eggshells. The water was first poured on both hands, held with the fingers pointed upward. It must run down the arm as far as the wrist and drop off from the wrist, for the water itself was now unclean having touched the unclean hands. If it ran down the fingers again, it would render them unclean. The process was repeated with the hands held in the downward direction, the fingers pointing down, and finally, each hand was cleansed with being rubbed with the fist of the other, and a strict Jew would do this before every meal and between every course in every meal." DINNER CONVERSATION 11:39-44 Direct indictment that contained as much sorrow for the pitiful situation as it did denouncement. You can picture Jesus picking up a plate or cup off of the table You wouldn t think about putting out dishes which were not clean on the outside. But when you eat, it is not the outside that is most important. Get to the bottom of the food and find a dead fly stuck to the plate. External cleanliness is fine, but internal cleanliness is essential. This is a rather brutal assessment of the Pharisee s heart Remember, Jesus is a guest in this house This was a different era; I would be careful with this approach Colossians 4:5, 6 salt you can and should be provocative grace sensitive You lack spiritual substance; your life is all a charade It hides the sinful condition of the heart full : their continued condition ravening (KJV) contains a strong indictment of their actions Translated greed, extortion, plunder Carry off someone s belongings by robbery wickedness general tenor of their lives while greed reflected specific instances Jesus called their self-righteous legalism wickedness Externally, they looked very healthy Story of Typhoid Mary In the early twentieth century, typhoid fever was a major epidemic in the United States. In 1907, Mary Mallon became the first person to be identified as a typhoid carrier. She was an Irish immigrant and a cook in New York City. Many of the families she cooked for became infected with typhoid fever and some of them died. In 1906 one of those families hired a typhoid researcher to investigate the cause of the fever. He believed it all traced back to Mary Mallon. But Mary was adamant that she was not a carrier. You see, from all external appearances, Mary was in perfect health. She showed no outward symptoms of typhoid. She was what doctor s call asymptomatic. So she secretly kept moving around from family to family, cooking and spreading her disease. Eventually, she was busted and put into prison, then quarantined. While in prison, they did tests and determined that she was a typhoid carrier. She was responsible for spreading typhoid to at least fifty-three people and the death of at least three people. Some believe that she is responsible for the death of up to fifty people. 11:40 fools one that does not use his rational powers You will never see this approach in the How to Evangelize brochure Charged them with superficiality because of their determination to spell out God s will for everyone. They might make the outside of their lives look great, but inside there were wrong motives Some student of Scripture will cite Matt. 5:22, don t call anyone a fool Different context (name calling); different word (moron) Eph. 5:17 don t be a fool, know the will of God He who made you is concerned with the outside and the inside Psalm 51:16, 17 11:41 Difficult verse Beck: Just give your in heart in helping the poor and you ll find everything clean Williams: But dedicate once for all your inner self The giving of alms was a reference to helping the poor Give with correct motive Give freely from a heart of love and compassion It was an outward expression of mercy (root word) If you can give out of mercy, it will reflect your heart condition By default will be a clean heart give alms of such things as you have Dedicate once for all your inner self such things as you have = the things which are within What we do should come from the heart, or it is meaningless To the Jew, almsgiving was first on the list; the most sacred of all religious duties To give alms and to be righteous were one and the same behold pay attention to what follows Don t give with great fanfare; a great indication of giving from the heart CONCLUSION It is so easy for any of us to fall into some aspect of legalism We perform not because we love God or others, but it is expected of us. Or we complain that after reading our Bibles and praying regularly God is not working on our behalf or answering our prayers. He owes us. And may we not fall into the trap that God is only interested on the inside. That is another carryover from the seeker sensitive, church growth crowd. He made the inside and the outside, the disciple of Jesus will honor both. The Pharisees had a problem. The light within them became dark and they spread that darkness to all who followed them. They were instruments of tremendous influence. How is your heart? DINING OUT LUKE 11:37-44 DINNER COMPANION 11:37 Jesus is still reaching out to this crowd Let us also remember His boldness in speaking the things of God DINNER CONFUSION 11:38 Jesus had more problems with legalists than any other group It is not an emphasis on obedience It is not establishing man-made rules At the root of legalism is the sin of pride Legalism is an attempt to gain favor with God or to impress our fellow man by doing certain things (or avoiding other things), without regard to the condition of our hearts before God. There was no Mosaic law about washing before eating DINNER CONVERSATION 11:39 fools one that does not use his rational powers Charged them with superficiality The giving of alms was a reference to helping the poor Give freely from a heart of love and compassion Dedicate once for all your inner self ============================================= === SERMON: message(113).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/25/2024 11:01:54 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: MESSAGE DINING OUT Part 2 Luke 11:37-54 INTRODUCTION Jesus has accepted the invitation for a meal at the home of a Pharisee. Last week we looked at DINNER COMPANION, DINNER CONFUSION, DINNER CONVERSATION. Pharisees meet the definition of a legalist: seeking to earn the favor of God or man through outward means without giving any consideration to the heart. Their standards may be the same as yours. Where is your heart? It is not about obedience, not about man-made rules; it is all about why you doing what you are doing. To the legalist, it is to look good; for the Christian it is to love good. Jesus continues to reach out to those who have refused to receive the Light that has been presented to them. But the Confusion of the Pharisee over the lack of ceremonially washing ones hands before eating has turned into a Conversation of exposing the heart. Fools Jesus says in v. 40 you are mindless, stupid, ignorant, egotistical You make the outward show but refuse to give your inner self. Jesus took the evangelism Conversation to a Confrontational level of Denouncing their spiritual lives through the presentation of 6 Indictments. 3 expressly given to the Pharisees and 3 more including the scribes. We concluded last week stating that is easy for any of us to adopt a spirit of Phariseeism. So we need to pay attention. We may deny the title but yet be exposed by the illustrations. It is having the symbol but no substance. INDICTMENT OF SUBSTITUION 11:42 but here is a contrast from v. 41 of giving with from a heart that is clean Jesus is going to expose an evil heart; spiritually bankrupt heart This had been called majoring on the minors; meticulous on the minutiae; and punctilious regarding the petty Woe used by Jesus to convey a warning of impending judgment and disaster They had their man-made regulations on tithing Jesus referred to their tithing mint and rue and all manner of herbs No such rule was ever given There is a tithe of the land; Leviticus 27:30-33 They had imposed a legalistic system of tithing on everything at the expense of judgment (justice, making decisions) and the love of God Micah 6:8 (WEB) He has shown you, O man, what is good. What does Yahweh require of you, but to act justly, To love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God? Zechariah 7:8-10 (WEB) The word of Yahweh came to Zechariah, saying, "Thus has Yahweh of Armies spoken, saying, 'Execute true judgment, and show kindness and compassion every man to his brother. Don't oppress the widow, nor the fatherless, the foreigner, nor the poor; and let none of you devise evil against his brother in your heart.' The Pharisees had become so engrossed with their tithing system that they had neglected the important issues of life and justice Cf. Matthew 23:23 Jesus did not condemn them for the tithing of mint; practice away But don t forget more important stuff What did Jonathan Edwards, Charles Spurgeon, G. Campbell Morgan, Martyn Lloyd-Jones, and C. S. Lewis all had in common? At one time they all smoked. Too often the substance of a ritual can be lost; why we do what we do Your morning devotions can become an empty ritual OT speaks to such distortions: Isaiah 1:10-17; Amos 5:21-24 INDICTMENT OF ELEVATION 11:43 Berkeley: for you cherish the prominent pews Campbell: conspicuous seats Alba: special honors in the streets Weymouth: and you like to be bowed to in the places of public resort Denouncement of Loving the Spotlight Pride and pompous attitudes; subtle, can creep up on us Liked the best seats to be seen Sitting in a semi-circle around the pulpit you love the publicity, the prestige Pride is the heart of legalism We don t compare ourselves with others and come out looking good; we compare ourselves with God and realize no good thing dwells in me As long as you are on the throne, Jesus can t be greeting in the market place not a pleasant hello but an elaborate verbal recognition Status, recognition, reverence, admiration Yet religious hypocrites Psalm 139:23, 24 Search me, O God INDICTMENT OF SEPARATION 11:44 Beck: You re like the unmarked graves . . .without knowing what they are Their influence on others Denouncement of Separation Third woe was a strong denunciation from Jesus Comparing Pharisees to unmarked graves During festival times the Jews would whitewash the graves so visitors to Jerusalem would not step on them and thereby become ceremonially unclean Contact with the dead required cleansing; Number 19:1-11 Ashes of a red heifer and ceremonial washing Jesus rebuked the Pharisees themselves as being the very ones who created ceremonial uncleanness The Pharisees were guilty of defiling the Jewish nation through their own spiritual death Those who follow them become unclean like those who walk on graves Not aware of becoming defiled Anyone who follows the Pharisaic system is separated from God The people are unaware they are spiritually contaminated Legalism, like carbon dioxide, kills slowly The Pharisees were not bringing people to God; their system would not promote holiness Their system was spiritually dead and would separate people from God Snowflake Interruption 11:45 Scribes (lawyers) were concerned with understanding the legal side of the Law is it related to God s will. Many scribes were also Pharisees A lawyer was a member of a learned profession; a Pharisee a member of a religious party. Luke 10:25; the lawyer who sought to justify himself: Good Samaritan story He caught on that Jesus was not just going after Pharisees but scribes also He felt the cutting edge of Jesus words. You are continually insulting us as well. reproach treat in an arrogant manner, insult, scoff at This guy is offended to be treated in such a manner He should have kept his mouth shut. Jesus boldly turns His attention to him INDICTMENT OF RESTRICTIONS 11:46 The Mosaic Law, given by God, was meant to enhance life in Israel, not to restrict By the time of Jesus, the traditions of the elders had accumulated until they were oppressive. They were managing the smallest detail of family and social life, as well as religious ritual. These restrictions had become burdensome Burden is the same word in Matt. 11:28 You crush people with impossible religious demands (NLT) You are overloading the laity like they were a beast of burden You overload the donkey and they get upset when it moves slowly Quick to tell others what to do, but provide no resources to accomplish. Leon Morris: on the Sabbath, they taught, a man may not carry a burden in his right hand or in his left hand, in his bosom, or on his shoulder. But he may carry it on the back of his hand, or with his foot or with his mouth or with his elbow, or in his ear or in his hair or in his wallet (carried) mouth downwards, or between his wallet and his shirt, or in the hem of his shirt, or in his shoe or in his sandal But there were plenty of loopholes to allow the Pharisees to do what they wanted. Experts in evasion. The present indicative touch with the negative ou indicates they were right now living in that fashion INDICTIMENT OF COMMEMORATION 11:47, 48 The Pharisees honored the ancient prophets who were slain by their father by renovating their tombs. True honor of these prophets should have included obedience and repentance Don t just honor their burial tomb; honor the message they proclaimed. They pretend outwardly to honor but avoid the personal call to holiness. Jesus, you hate the prophets just like your fathers did By their actions, the Pharisees implied their approval of the actions of previous Jews in killing the prophets. They also implied that they would also be willing to follow in that tradition and kill any prophet who spoke rightly of God In the Kidron valley even today can be seen the tombs of the prophets Some say these date back to the time of Jesus Jesus by your actions you are consenting to and approving what your ancestors did you testify you approve you acquiesce you are consenting full approval Campbell accessories There is a prophet standing in their midst, but He is attacking their hypocrisy just like the prophets of old. They, like their fathers, hate the message and seek to kill the messenger. INDICTMENT OF OPPOSITION 11:49-53 therefore on account of this lays the groundwork for last indictment wisdom of God unusual expression of Jesus Summarizing and personifying all of God s wisdom Seems to refer to the wisdom of God in allowing His prophets to be killed and yet continuing the power of His message In other words, the work of death against the prophets had not nullified the ultimate plan of God. Evil will not frustrate God s plan. He will have the final word. 50 Each generation is given an opportunity to repent and change the course of evil If no radical repentance is presented, this present generation will suffer just like previous Responsibility increases through the years. If you do not learn from the previous generation, you are adding to your guilt and the severity of punishment. Catch the weight of Jesus words MacArthur: The Lord s shocking declaration that the blood of all the prophets, shed since the foundation of the world, would be charged against this generation reveals just how much worse the generation alive at that time was than those who killed the prophets. 51 The death of God s prophets had been the trademark of those who opposed God s will. Abel was the first in the OT to die; Zechariah the last as 2 Chronicles is the final book in the Hebrew Bible The murder of Abel too place over the issue of acceptable offerings to God The murder was over a religious issue The same for Zechariah; 2 Chronicles 24:20-22 He had spoken against the idolatry of Joash the king; another religious issue Abel was killed after a sacrifice; Zechariah was killed at the place of sacrifice. (between the altar and the house of God) Jesus reminded His listeners that the blood of the prophets would be required of any generation that heard the call to repentance but refused to acknowledge its evil way and turn to God. INDICTMENT OF OBSTRUCTION 11:52 The Pharisees were placing blocks in the way of salvation for all the people v. 52 clarifies the real effect of their self-righteous systems by their legalism the Pharisees locked the door of knowledge of salvation that could set people free. Their traditions hid the truth; the OT was made into a riddle needing the experts to clarify. key used 4x in Revelation and Matthew with regard to releasing the power of the kingdom Matt. 16:19; Rev. 1:18; 3:7; 9:1; 20:1 The tragedy is that the door could have been opened so easily by faith in Christ The Pharisees opted for legalism rather than the faith of Abraham Not only did they isolate themselves from knowledge, but they also hindered those who desired to enter for themselves hindered forbid, prevent Instead of opening the word, they kept it hidden The Pharisees refused to go through the door and locked it up so no one else could enter. John 17:3 INDICTMENT REACTION 11:53, 54 The indictments caused a severe reaction urge him vehemently fearfully, terribly, act in a hostile manner To be extremely angry, fiercely opposed, violently enraged Indicates a radical break in the relationship of Jesus to the Pharisees He was now religious enemy number 1 provoke question closely, interrogate A picture of determined harassment Instead of evaluating the message and repenting of their sins, They plotted verbal ambushes for Him Determined to find a way to end His witness They were exposed; their empty hearts, their empty system Not hard to do today: just say there are two sexes; the greatest glory of a woman is in the home; playing girl sports is not a substitute for failing in boys sports Guzik - The religious leaders responded the way many do when they are faced with the correction and the truth of God. Instead of humbly receiving the correction, they responded with outraged accusations. Proverbs tells us what those who refuse correction do. First, they hate those who correct them (Pr 9:8 , Pr 15:12 ). Second, they do not listen to the one correcting them (Pr 13:1 ). Third, they despise their own soul (Pr 15:32 ).. Proverbs also tells us the character of those who refuse correction. They are stupid (Pr 12:1 ) and they are foolish (Pr 15:5 ). When you can t argue with the message, you seek to eliminate the messenger. CONCLUSION The Pharisees were the influencers of the day. You have an influence. Do any of the indictments leveled by Jesus speak to our own hearts? Substitution: Forgetting the more important stuff Elevation: Desire to be recognized Separation: Creating unnecessary barriers to God Restrictions: Precise stipulations of burdens Commemoration: a lot of show but no substance Obstruction: your life is an obstacle to others to find Jesus DINING OUT Part 2 Luke 11:37-54 DINNER COMPANION, DINNER CONFUSION, DINNER CONVERSATION. INDICTMENT OF SUBSTITUION 11:42 Woe warning of impending judgment Micah 6:8; Zechariah 7:8-10 The Pharisees had become so engrossed with their tithing system that they had neglected the important issues of life and justice INDICTMENT OF ELEVATION 11:43 Loving the Spotlight Pride is the heart of legalism INDICTMENT OF SEPARATION 11:44 Jesus rebuked the Pharisees as being the very ones who created ceremonial uncleanness Anyone who follows the Pharisaic system is separated from God INDICTMENT OF RESTRICTIONS 11:46 You crush people with impossible religious demands (NLT) You overload the donkey and then get upset when it moves slowly INDICTIMENT OF COMMEMORATION 11:47, 48 True honor of these prophets should have included obedience and repentance They hate the message and seek to kill the messenger. INDICTMENT OF OPPOSITION 11:49-53 the work of death against the prophets had not nullified the ultimate plan of God. If you do not learn from the previous generation, you are adding to your guilt and the severity of punishment.
