INDICTMENT OF OBSTRUCTION 11:52 By their legalism the Pharisees locked the door of knowledge of salvation that could set people free. By their legalism the Pharisees locked the door of knowledge of salvation Instead of opening the word, they kept it hidden INDICTMENT REACTION 11:53, 54 act in a hostile manner violently enraged DINING OUT Part 2 Luke 11:37-54 INDICTMENT OF ____________ 11:42 Woe warning of impending ____________ Micah 6:8; Zechariah 7:8-10 The Pharisees had become so engrossed with their tithing system that they had ____________ the important issues of life and justice INDICTMENT OF ____________ 11:43 Loving the ____________ ____________ is the heart of legalism INDICTMENT OF _____________ 11:44 Jesus rebuked the Pharisees as being the very ones who ____________ ceremonial uncleanness Anyone who follows the Pharisaic system is ____________ from God INDICTMENT OF _____________ 11:46 You ____________ people with impossible religious demands (NLT) You ____________ the donkey and then get upset when it moves slowly INDICTIMENT OF _____________ 11:47, 48 True honor of these prophets should have ____________ obedience and repentance They ___________ the message and seek to kill the messenger INDICTMENT OF ______________ 11:49-53 The work of death against the prophets had not ____________ the ultimate plan of God. If you do not learn from the previous generation, you are ____________ to your guilt and the severity of punishment. INDICTMENT OF _____________ 11:52 By their legalism the Pharisees ________ the door of knowledge of salvation Instead of opening the word, they kept it ___________ INDICTMENT REACTION 11:53, 54 act in a hostile manner; ____________ enraged ============================================= === SERMON: message(114).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/8/2024 9:41:51 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 WARNING 12:1-3 WORRY 12:4-7 WITNESS 12:8-12 INTRODUCTION We are in an extended section of Luke that is unique to his gospel. I think it goes from chapters 11-18. We will see a few things from other gospels, but not much. Furthermore, if you have a red-letter Bible edition, you will see much of what we will be reading are the words of Jesus. I can t think of a better activity for a follower of Jesus than to immerse yourself in the words of Jesus. Luke 12 through 13:9 is one long sermon with the occasional question. The gospel of Luke has some long chapters. I concluded chapter 11 with 68 pages of notes and chapter 12 looks to be about the same. We concluded chapter 11 with Jesus leveling a series of indictments at the Pharisees and scribes for the negative influence they have on the people. The religious system they have developed does not and cannot bring people to a relationship with God. Jesus continues His rebuke on hypocrisy but to a different audience in chapter 12. The meal with the Pharisee is over. We are not told that food was ever served. What was served was a 6-course meal of condemnations. WARNING 12:1 cf. Matthew 16; Mark 8 The chapter has an interesting beginning In the meantime; under these circumstances; meanwhile; at which time The crowd is gathering. For a variety of reasons For some his message touched a chord in their common experience They were willing to hear Him out J. B. Phillips paraphrase: Meanwhile, the crowds had gathered in thousands, so that they were actually treading on each other s toes. Literally many ten thousands there were a multiple of 10 thousands My understanding the largest number available; no word for millions Used in Rev. 5:11 I saw, and I heard something like a voice of many angels around the throne, the living creatures, and the elders; and the number of them was ten thousands of ten thousands, and thousands of thousands; The point is it was a huge crowd Jesus turns His attention to the disciples; not just the 12 Remember, He had sent out 70 in chapter 10 Many others technically fall under the same category Out of this collection of disciples there were also those who never came to saving faith. John 6; after what was described as a hard saying; who can understand it? in v. 60; verse 66 says from that time many of His disciples went back and walked with Him no more. Most of those who are given the title of disciples in Luke 12, will call for His death in just a few months. This is indeed a mixed multitude The text and context does not tell us how to interpret the crowd demeanor Easy to assume there are folks there on both sides His warning was clearly related to Luke 11 and the attack on the Pharisees and scribes Matthew 16:6, 12 and Mark 8:16 record the command to beware of the leaven but only Luke identifies the leaven as hypocrisy. 6:12 does speak of the teaching of the Pharisees Leaven: causes things to rise; an expanding agent; its nature is to grow The leaven of the Pharisees was based on hypocrisy and led to spiritual immaturity and destruction of true spiritual vitality. Leaven was many times a symbol of corruption A hypocrite is one who has sacrificed truth to appearance; Whether consciously or unconsciously. He is so busy living up to his reputation that he has no time to be himself. He must always be justifying himself to others, to himself, or to God. (Caird) Look at the word first It is not so much that He was talking to the disciples first as this was the first thing the disciples should be aware of The first thing is to pay attention to this command "Be on guard against the pervasive, gradual, imperceptible spread of hypocrisy, which had corrupted the Pharisees." (Swindoll) Take heed to yourselves; watch out; take personal responsibility to protect yourself; also in 12:15 take heed It was a nautical term meaning to steer a ship in a certain direction or to a particular destination. Be continually steering clear of hypocrites Don t fall into a trap of pursuing popularity with the crowds or avoiding trouble with the Pharisees Leaven is that which influences and any of us can be subject to it; good or bad Galatians 2:13 Peter and Barnabas for the bad Luke 13:20, 21 for the good The leaven of the Pharisees will only corrupt, swell; avoid at all costs The OT Jew was to remove any leaven from the home before Passover Exodus 12:13 This was symbolic of leaving behind the pagan influences of Egypt 1 Corinthians 5:6-8 our old influences must be discarded Mark 8:15 the leaven of Herod; Matthew uses Sadducees of which Herod was one; the leaven of liberalism, denying the supernatural or its about the corrupting influence of being politically engaged; seeing no problem with submitting to a foreign ruler because of monetary enrichment the love of money is still the root of all evils Hypocrisy is play-acting; for the Pharisees play-acting at religion That is a dangerous role to play because when the play is over the payment is eternal separation from hell. The Pharisees posed as paragons of virtue but actually they were masters of masquerade. -- William MacDonald From the Life Application Bible: What are the signs of hypocrisy? If Peter and Barnabas could be guilty, we all can 1. Hypocrisy is knowing the truth but not obeying it. People can say they follow Jesus, but not be obedient to his Word. 2. Hypocrisy is living a self-serving life. People may desire leadership only because they love position and control, not because they want to serve others. 3. Hypocrisy reduces faith to rigid rules. People can end up worshiping their own rules and regulations about what they think God wants instead of worshiping God himself. 4. Hypocrisy is outward conformity without inner reality. People can obey the details but still be disobedient in general behavior. We often here that people don t go to church because it is full of hypocrites. Well, there is always room for one more. We are not perfect. But I hope we are striving for that; individually and collectively. And I hope that when confronted with hypocrisy, we seek forgiveness and move on. We can be easily influenced by what others say 1 Corinthians 15:33 Bad company (lit, communications) corrupts good morals bad worthless, depraved, evil Used in LXX: Proverbs 7:21 with her persuasive words she entices him Who we expose ourselves to is of tremendous importance. The Warning from Jesus of first importance: hypocrisy 12:2, 3 Hypocrisy will become known Living Bible - It will become as evident as yeast in dough God will rip off the masks Numbers 32:23 ye have sinned against Jehovah; and be sure your sin will find you out. Abraham Lincoln put it this way, "You can fool some of the people all of the time and all of the people some of the time, but you can't fool all of the people all of the time." May we also add, "You cannot fool God any of the time!" Ecclesiastes 12:14 (ASV) For God will bring every work into judgment, with every hidden thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil. This does not mean that forgiven sins will be exposed * Forgive them all (1 Jn. 1:9 ). * Cast them into the sea (Mic. 7:19 ). * Take them away (Isa. 6:7 ). * Cover them up (Ps. 32:1 ). * Blot them out (Acts 3:19 ). * Put them away (Heb. 9:26 ). * Remember them no more (Heb. 8:12 ). It means that we can never truly cover them by lying and hiding them As a believer, confess and forsake Everything will be brought to light: 1 Corinthians 4:5 No matter how fast a lie may travel, the truth will overtake it at last. 12:3 Jesus is still directing His attention to the hypocrites Based on the fact that everything will be exposed as in v.2 Future exposure should impact our thoughts, words, conduct The most private of acts and utterances become the most public The focus was on the power of spoken words; even whispering Psalm 139:12 and Romans 2:16 testify to the power of God over darkness The closets refers to the inner room of a house where treasures were kept for safe keeping. In other words, even when we think we are safe to whisper our mischief, it will ultimately be found out and will seem like something shouted from the housetop. The roofs were flat and often used for announcements If you would be ashamed to have it known; be ashamed to even do it Arthur Conan Doyle of Sherlock Holmes fame used to playfully tell a bogus tale about how he sent a telegram to each of twelve friends, all men of great virtue, reputation, and considerable position in society. The message simply said: Fly at once, all is discovered. Within twenty-four hours, Doyle says, all twelve had left the country Early in 1996, the body of the former ambassador to Switzerland (M. Larry Lawrence) was buried in Arlington Cemetery, America's graveyard of war heroes. His granite tombstone read, "S1C [Seaman First Class] U.S. Merchant Marine." But according to Don Van Natta Jr. and Elaine Sciolino in the New York Times, on December 11, 1997, cemetery workers hauled that tombstone away, and they exhumed the casket. (READ THE ORIGINAL ARTICLE ) The reason: the man had lied. For years he told others he had served on the Coast Guard ship Horace Bushnell during World War II. He said the Germans torpedoed the ship, and he had been thrown overboard, sustaining a head injury. In fact, records showed that at the time he said he was serving in the Merchant Marine he was actually attending Wilbur Wright College in Chicago. The Coast Guard had no record of his serving in the Merchant Marine, and of course he had never earned the rank of seaman first class. Somehow his lie was not discovered when the State Department investigated his background, and he was approved as an ambassador. Somehow his body was permitted to be buried in Arlington Cemetery with a tombstone engraved with a lie, but to no surprise, the truth eventually came out. The truth always will. AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 WARNING 12:1 John 6 60; 66 Matthew 16:6, 12 and Mark 8:16 A hypocrite is one who has sacrificed truth to appearance - Caird The first thing is to pay attention to this command Leaven is that which influences Galatians 2:13 Luke 13:20, 21 Exodus 12:13 leaving behind the pagan influences of Egypt 1 Corinthians 5:6-8 our old influences must be discarded Hypocrisy is play-acting Hypocrisy is knowing the truth but not obeying it Hypocrisy is living a self-serving life Hypocrisy reduces faith to rigid rules Hypocrisy is outward conformity without inner reality * Life Application Bible 1 Corinthians 15:33 Hypocrisy will become known Everything will be brought to light: 1 Corinthians 4:5 Future exposure should impact our thoughts, words, conduct Psalm 139:12 and Romans 2:16 testify to the power of God over darkness The closets refers to the inner room of a house where treasures were kept AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 WARNING 12:1 John 6:_________ Cross References: Matthew 16:6, 12 and Mark 8:16 A hypocrite is one who has sacrificed truth to _______________ - Caird The first thing is to pay attention to ___________ command Leaven is that which ______________ ____________ 2:13 ____________ 13:20, 21 Exodus 12:13 leaving behind the ____________ influences of Egypt 1 Corinthians 5:6-8 __________ old influences must be discarded Hypocrisy is _______________ Hypocrisy is knowing the truth but ________ obeying it Hypocrisy is living a _______-serving life Hypocrisy reduces faith to rigid ____________ Hypocrisy is outward conformity ____________ inner reality * Life Application Bible 1 Corinthians 15:______ Hypocrisy _________ become known _______________ will be brought to light: 1 Corinthians 4:5 Future ______________ should impact our thoughts, words, conduct Psalm 139:12; Romans 2:16 testify to the power over God over __________ Closets refers to the inner room of a house where ______________ were kept. ============================================= === SERMON: message(115).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/15/2024 11:42:10 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 Part 2 INTRODUCTION In Luke 11 comes before Luke 12. And you say, I got up this morning to be told that? Luke 11 sets the stage for Luke 12. Luke 12 is in response to what transpired in Luke 11. Jesus is in Judea, the southern portion of the land. 9:51 told us he set His face to go up to Jerusalem. He sent our 70 ambassadors to prepare the way He was to go (10:1). He has given to us the account of the Good Samaritan and Luke tells us of the visit to Bethany and the home of Lazarus, Martha, and Mary. He gave the disciples instructions on the Pattern, the Principle, and the Practice of Praying. At 11:14 the attack came; leveling the outrageous accusation that the miraculous deliverance from demon possession was accomplished through the power of Satan. And then at 11:37, Jesus accepts the dinner invitation which results 6 Indictments leveled at the Pharisees, Sadducees, and Scribes. These were the influencers of the day. In chapter 12, Jesus directs His attention to those following Him. As we mentioned, not all of them are true disciples yet fall under that broad title. He will carefully lay out the dangerous consequences of following the influencers of the day. Not only did the religious elite fail to have a relationship with God, they prevented others from knowing God. Their religious system included a lot of show, but no personal knowledge. The Indictments resulted in greater hatred toward Jesus. By the time we get to verse 51, we find that following Jesus will put us at odds with many others including members of our own family. It is no wonder that many of those called disciples in 12:1 called for His crucifixion in just a few months. WARNING 12:1-3 A command of first importance: Steer your way cleanly through the hazards of hypocrisy. The word beware is a nautical term. Avoid the rocks, the sand bars, and other obstacles often unseen. It means to pay attention. Our oldest son, Jesse, went fishing in northern WI when much younger. Ruth s cousin, Jerry, was driving the boat up a river and Jesse spoke of how he meandered back and forth. His familiarity with the river provided the safety in reaching the fishing destination. We are admonished to beware of Substitutions and Obstructions in our walk with Jesus. Anything that would create a barrier of unnecessary burdens that are all about show and no substance. Folks, we don t have time for show. We don t have time to tip-toe around the issues. We must address through Biblical teaching the answers to man s need. It is not that man must be saved or he is going to hell; it is he is going to hell and he needs a Savior. The only contribution that man makes to the whole plan of salvation is the sin that makes salvation necessary. Retired NFL tight end, Ben Watson, made quite a statement recently. Ben has been an outspoken believer in Jesus. He cited a Pew Research Survey from 2021 that said 87% of abortions were performed on unwed mothers. He was stressing the importance of the family, of a dad in the home. A properly functioning family would take care of a lot of what ails our culture. The SBC failed this week to pass an amendment to prohibit woman from the office of the lead pastor. Only failed by 5% but there was a lot of misinformation purposely shared to muddy the issue for those who voted. But not only is there a command to steer clear of hypocrites Jesus also gives instruction on responding to them. WORRY 12:4-7 Jesus is going to name off two consequences of opposing hypocrites The first is addressing The Reality of Fear Hypocrites will come after the opposition Bodily harm is well within the realm of possibility Of course, it will be hired out to keep the hands of the hypocrites clean I know, I have watched too many NCIS shows But is that not what they did? What did they do with the 30 pieces of silver Judas hurled at them? Matthew 27:5-7 They could not put it back in the treasury as it was blood money So, they bought a field. Hypocrites hello, friends Jesus can identify true followers 12:32 little flock addressed out of the multitude The world knows who theirs is and reject the rest All the speeches about tolerance only extends to those whose heads are full of mush Those who will acquiesce to the correct position Jesus and friends: John 15:13-15 Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends. Ye are my friends, if ye do the things which I command you. No longer do I call you servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I heard from my Father, I have made known unto you. Are you a friend of Jesus? Jesus is going to draw a line; either follow the hypocrisy of the Pharisees or follow Me. A choice must be made in the face of certain hardships including potential persecution and death. Straddling the line will bring ultimate final judgment The call of Jesus is to be understood in relation to the first three verses The tragedy of hypocrisy v. 4; Jesus takes issue with the weakness of hypocrisy to accomplish its goals. Only those weak in courage and moral fortitude turn to a life of falseness. This falseness is related to fear. Throughout the Bible fear is seen as one of our greatest spiritual enemies. Proverbs 29:25 the fear of man brings a snare Are you a man-pleaser or God-pleaser? By the fear of the Lord men depart from evil; By the fear of man, they run themselves into evil. - John Flavel Why is this so important? The context explains Jesus is going to address some challenges His followers should expect The challenge from the opposition 12:4-12 The challenge of the lure of possessions 12:13-21 The challenge from worry and anxiety 12:22-34 The challenge of laziness 12:35-48 The superficial nature of hypocrisy will not provide the depth of soul necessary to endure Spurgeon: You may rest assured that, if it had been best for us to be taken away to heaven directly when we were converted, the Lord would have done it, and that, as he has not done so, there are wise reasons why he keeps his people here for a while. The gold must go through the fire ere it has its place in the king s crown, and the wheat must be exposed to the winnowing fan ere it can be taken into the heavenly garner. Nothing about this is new to the believer: Jeremiah Ezekiel 2:6 And thou, son of man, be not afraid of them, neither be afraid of their words, though briers and thorns are with thee, and thou dost dwell among scorpions: be not afraid of their words, nor be dismayed at their looks, though they are a rebellious house. Philippians 1:28 and in nothing affrighted by the adversaries: which is for them an evident token of perdition, but of your salvation, and that from God; 1 Peter 3:14 But even if ye should suffer for righteousness' sake, blessed are ye: and fear not their fear, neither be troubled; Revelation 2:10 Fear not the things which thou art about to suffer: behold, the devil is about to cast some of you into prison, that ye may be tried; and ye shall have tribulation ten days. Be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee the crown of life. Our present context reveals those who would kill are the religious leaders Death is the best they can do. They cannot do permanent harm They can kill you but can t do real damage! Death for a believer is liberation: for me to live is Christ, to die is gain (Phil. 1:21) Yet death is probably the greatest fear that most people face Death generally involves pain and there is the mystery of the unknown What does it mean to walk through the valley of the shadow of death ? Whereas at one time the Shepherd directed the sheep to green pastures and still waters, it has come time for the sheep to walk through the darkest valley they have ever experienced. The surroundings are complete with dangerous creatures, rocky edges, low hanging branches. Sheep don t have a concept of death, but they do know dark valleys. Your darkest valley may not be death. The point is that you have every reason to be afraid, but you need not fear. You have a companion (Shepherd) and you are going through the valley. Real fear should be directed toward He who can send you to hell He has the authority, He has the right Hell: Gehenna; a deep narrow ravine south of Jerusalem once associated with the pagan god Moloch and the disgusting rite of infant sacrifices the valley where the filth and dead animals of the city were cast out and burned and where there were trash fires and perpetually burning rubbish a place where fire, smoke and stench never ceased The Cure of Fear First: Overcomer John 16:33 be of good cheer, I have overcome the world The victory of Jesus of Jesus should inspire us to face our fears and rebuke them in the power of His name Rebuke how? By quoting Scripture Second: Love 1 John 4:18 there is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear; because fear has torment. Love means that we are accepted by God in Christ and there is nothing that can separate us from His love. (Rom. 8:39) Steadfast, unfailing John 15:14, 15 His new relationship to His followers: friends Fear is not an element of friendship The government sells fear so they can be your savior. Christ says, fear not because He is your Savior. Third: Assurance Fear and reverence before God results in His constant care and attention Luke 12:6 5 sparrows for 2 cents You may see the word farthing or pence ; The word is assaria The point is that the sparrows are throw away birds and insignificant, an extra is thrown in for the price of 4 (2 cents)(Matt. 10:29) which even more forcibly proves how insignificant was the value of the sparrows and yet even that unvalued sparrow thrown in for 2 cents was not forgotten before God. Nothing escapes His notice And speaking of insignificant: how about the hairs on your head? v. 7 Numbered: perfect tense; your hairs are permanently numbered even if they don t remain permanently on your head! The average number is 100,00 hairs on your head If God has that much care about the most insignificant things, how much more care has He for the whole person? Folks, my assurance is not that I know Jesus, but that He knows me Everything about me Never yield to the temptation to believe God does not care or that He has forgotten about you Luke 21:18 repeats; I ll protect you Worry is real. It is really easy for us to lose sleep anxious for things we have no control over. Fear is real. Especially when our lives are at stake. We have not yet suffered to the sacrifice of life, but rest assured there are those out there who have little regard for your life. They are the hypocrites and your very life condemns them. The Cure is Real We may be called upon some day to stand for Biblical truth at the cost of our life. Fear God; not man. AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 Part 2 WARNING 12:1-3 Steer your way cleanly through the hazards of hypocrisy WORRY 12:4-7 Hypocrites will come after the opposition Jesus can identify true followers 12:32 A choice must be made in the face of certain hardships Proverbs 29:25 Ezekiel 2:6 Philippians 1:28 1 Peter 3:14 Revelation 2:10 Death is the best they can do They can kill you but can t do real damage! Your darkest valley may not be death. Hell: Gehenna; a deep narrow ravine south of Jerusalem A place where fire, smoke and stench never ceased The Cure of Fear Overcomer John 16:33 Love 1 John 4:18 Assurance Luke 12:6 Sparrows are throw-away birds Your hairs are permanently numbered How much more care has He for the whole person? ============================================= === SERMON: message(116).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/21/2024 1:25:44 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 Part 3 Jesus has condemned the hypocrisy of the Pharisees, Sadducees and scribes In chapter 12:1-3, He presents a Warning to those called disciples to avoid the show of religion and seek the substance of a relationship with Christ The principle established in verses 4 and 5 still hold true here Will we live by the fear of man or the fear of God? Which arena do you Worry about the most? The confidence gained from trusting the Lord and being liberated from the bondage of fear enables the believer to become an effective witness for Christ. WITNESS 12:8-12 The second consequence Jesus speaks to being called on the carpet You are the whistle blower; you have exposed the hypocrites for what they are; they have retaliated You are being threatened; called before the tribunal The jury is fellow hypocrites; the possibility of a fair hearing is remote What are you going to do? Plead out? Daniel 3:16, 17 Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego answered the king, Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter. If it be [so], our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace; and he will deliver us out of your hand, O king. Jeremiah 26:14, 15 But as for me, behold, I am in your hand: do with me as is good and right in your eyes. Only know ye for certain that, if ye put me to death, ye will bring innocent blood upon yourselves, and upon this city, and upon the inhabitants thereof; for of a truth Jehovah hath sent me unto you to speak all these words in your ears. Value of Your Testimony Everyone: no exceptions; meaning this is not just for the disciples The message is for everyone; not a special class Every believer is a disciple confess to say the same thing; active acknowledgment It is not just recognizing a truth, but to live in it Williams: everyone who owns me before men Acknowledge, declare openly and freely Romans 10:9 confessing Jesus as Lord John 9:27 confessing Jesus as Messiah 1 John 4:2 confessing Jesus has come in the flesh Titus 1:16 same word used; different results; they counter their confession with a continual denial of Him by their deeds That is the hypocrite living a double life Our confession, our testimony, our witness of Christ before men It is meant to be definitive, resolute, without apology; willingness to be bold You confess before men; Jesus confesses in heaven Jesus puts our relationship to Him in eternal perspective Here is another lesson in courageous Christianity It is to be open and honest about a total life-commitment to Jesus It is verbal confession demonstrated in a life of godly living At home, at work, at play I confess regardless of the opposition I may face We stand for Jesus; Jesus will stand for us This verse is not about loss of reward, but loss of eternal life I must fear the judgment of God more than the judgment of men Galatians 1:10 For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn't be a servant of Christ. If you are going to faithfully present the gospel of Christ you will step on toes; people will be offended So you must fear God even in the face of opposition and death Psalm 116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of His saints Thomas Cranmer served faithfully as Archbishop of Canterbury under kings Henry VIII and Edward VI in England. But when Bloody Mary came to the throne, he was condemned to death for treason and heresy. He was forced to watch as Hugh Latimer and Nicolas Ridley were burned at the stake in Oxford. The fear of such a painful death caused Cranmer to recant his Protestant views and to sign a paper that he agreed with the Roman Catholic view of transubstantiation. But even though he recanted, the Catholics planned to burn him anyway. On the eve of his execution, he was brought before the church where he was expected to acknowledge publicly his shift toward Rome. But he shocked his enemies when he suddenly renounced his recantation, declared the Pope to be antichrist, and rejected transubstantiation. Then, with a light heart and a clear conscience, he allowed himself to be hurried to the stake amidst the outcries of his disappointed enemies. As the flames curled around him, he boldly held out his right hand into the fire, the hand that had signed his recantation, and said, This unworthy right hand, while he held his left hand up toward heaven as he perished in the flames (J. C. Ryle) In the summer of 2004, Warren Beamer, a missionary from San Antonio, Texas, visited an orphanage in Nigeria. Beamer was startled when one of the children at the orphanage spoke to him with a southern accent. The girl quickly shared that she was from Houston, Texas. To convince the missionary that this was true, she recited her social security number. Then the girl led Beamer to six other children in the orphanage, whom she described as her brothers and sisters. The children, who ranged from eight to sixteen years-of-age, had been sent to a Nigerian boarding school by their adoptive American mother. When the woman stopped making tuition payments to the school, the children were sent to the orphanage, living in terrible, squalid conditions. Gradually the children gave up hope of ever returning home to the great state of Texas. When the children saw Warren Beamer, they began singing the "Star-Spangled Banner" in an effort to convince him of the truth of their claim that they were Americans. They boldly proclaimed their citizenship and the place of their true home. With the assistance of Beamer's pastor and a U.S. congressman, the children were back in the United States of America within eight days. - Rod Mattoon The animated story A Charlie Brown Christmas airs on one of the major television networks seasonally. The two producers who worked closely with Charlie Brown creator Charles Schultz remembered their desperate efforts to convince a television network to show the special originally. All the major networks were hesitant. Finally, one agreed, and the great cartoonist got to work. A memorable and moving part of A Charlie Brown Christmas occurs when the cartoon character Linus, strolls to center stage and reads the biblical account of the meaning of Christmas. The two producers working with Schultz cautioned him about putting something like that in the cartoon special, because they were convinced it wouldn't go over well. Charles Schultz faced both of the producers and said, "If not us, then who's going to do it?" The Value of Your Testimony The Refusal of Your Testimony 12:9, 10 Operating by the fear of man; The hook has been placed in the mouth To repudiate, disown Denying Christ: Jude 4 1 John 2:25 2 Peter 2:1 ESV Study Bible - The eternal consequences for those who deny Christ, in fact, will be far worse than the persecution that they sought to avoid. This is not about the occasional failure to speak up but a firm refusal If it only takes one time, Peter would not have made it v. 10 Jesus moves from denying Him to denying the Holy Spirit We must take great pains not to stray form the intention of the text Mark 3:22-30; Luke 11:15; John 7:20; 8:48, 52; 10:20 Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit is committed as one ascribes the works of Jesus to the work of the devil The Unpardonable Sin The line drawn in the sand To be sure, anyone dying without confessing Christ will never be pardoned after death; so in a sense he committed the Unpardonable Sin There are many who hold that interpretation. I think the context takes a different bent A person who believes that Jesus is demon-possessed is completely outside the sphere of grace, and the Holy Spirit is not in him. There are many who have falsely believed they have committed this sin Persons who are concerned that they might have committed such a sin are already within the reach of grace since their hearts are broken and contrite before the Lord. I have come to believe that Jesus is referring back to the last chapter and the accusation leveled against Him. These people, namely the hypocrites, personally witnessed the Holy Spirit working through Jesus and still falsely identified the work accomplished through the power of Satan. I don t think this sin can be committed today, because Jesus is not here performing miraculous healings. But again, in a very real sense, if a person dies without seeking forgiveness of their sins, they will never be pardoned. They lived with a conscious, persistent rejection of the Spirit s work. A person has continually hardened his heart to the Holy Spirit There will come a time when he will have crossed the line where God will confirm the hardness and the person will be unable to seek God ESV Study Bible - The eternal consequences for those who deny Christ, in fact, will be far worse than the persecution that they sought to avoid. Voltaire is reported to have said "If a miracle occurred in the market place of Paris and in the presence of two thousand men, I would rather disbelieve my own eyes than the two thousand." Voltaire, on his deathbed, addressed his apparently Christian physician Trochim..."I am abandoned by God and man. I will give you half of what I am worth, if you will give me six months life." The doctor replied "Sir, you cannot live six weeks." Voltaire replied "Then I shall go to hell, and you will go with me." And so, as almost his last utterance, he cried out, "I am abandoned by God and man! I shall go to hell! O Christ! O Jesus Christ!" The Christian physician Trochim who attended Voltaire during the last illness gave the following testimony in a letter to a friend "When I compare the death of a righteous man, which is like the close of a beautiful day, with that of Voltaire, I see the difference between bright, serene weather and a black thunderstorm. It was my lot that this man should die under my hands. Often did I tell him the truth. Yes, my friend, he would often say to me, you are the only one who has given me good advice. Had I but followed it I would not be in the horrible condition in which I now am. I have swallowed nothing but smoke. I have intoxicated myself with the incense that turned my head. You can do nothing for me. Send me a mad doctor! Have compassion on me-I am mad! Trochim went on to say "I cannot think of it without shuddering. As soon as he saw that all the means he had employed to increase his strength had just the opposite effect, death was constantly before his eyes. From this moment, madness took possession of his soul. He expired under the torments of the furies. The Assurance of Your Testimony 12:11, 12 Key word: when not if Jesus was speaking to those who would be brought before the very ones who had hardened their hearts to the most glorious testimonies. They had just been put on notice that they will never be forgiven Persecution will come from the religionist Synagogues were the court room of the day Do not worry be not anxious; take no thought Look around and be distressed. Look inside and be depressed. Look at Jesus and be at rest. Corrie Ten Boom Acts 4; Acts 7 and Stephen When he was persecuted the Holy Spirit gave him a vision of the Son of Man standing at the right hand f God 7:55 Note the pattern that is brought together in this verse Vs. 8 and 9 refer to confession or denial of Christ Confession leads to acceptance in heaven, but denial leads to denial by Christ v. 10 focuses on the power of the Holy Spirit and how He can be dismissed from the life Vs. 11 and 12 take these two themes; confession and Spirit, and unite them in the life of the believer who stands before the world bearing witness to the lordship of Christ It is not beyond the realm of possibilities that believers will be called upon to stand for the faith once delivered to the saints. God will not only teach you what to say, but will teach when to remain silent The Holy Spirit is faithful to teach the believer at the moment of crisis Not early, not late, always on time While 12:10 refers to blasphemy against the Holy Spirit in relation to Jesus 12:11, 12 refer to the power of the Holy Spirit in the life of the person who is willing to acknowledge Jesus publicly. John 14:26 But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, He will teach you all things, and bring to your remembrance all that I said to you. Exemplified: Acts 4:8, 31; 6:10, 16 Barclay: It was not the humiliation which early Christians dreaded, not even the cruel pain and the agony. But many of them feared that their own unskillfulness in words and defense might injure rather than commend the truth. It is the promise of God that when a man is on trial for his faith, the words will come to him. Leon Morris - Jesus is concerned with the duty that rests compellingly on believers even in such a time of danger. He is not telling them how to secure acquittal. He is telling them how they may best serve God in their trying situation. The Spirit will inspire them with such a defense that through it the gospel will be proclaimed and God s purposes be set forward. Spurgeon: there have been answers which have been deeply theological, and there have been some which have been wisely evasive and, also some full of weight, and others full of grace and truth, for the Holy Ghost has helped his saints, in time of persecution, to answer well those who have accused them. You remember when Martin Luther was given the chance to recant his own writings, he first asked for time to think it over saying, ' To say too little or too much would be dangerous. I beg you, give me time to think it over." The next day he came back with his bold statement that is known so well. CONCLUSION Hypocrites will be hypocrites. Fret not because of them and seek never to be numbered among them. Jesus has pronounced judgment upon them and given us a warning of their exposure, told us not to fear them but God, and has given us assurance should we be called to stand before them. The fact that of this assurance is not an excuse to be ignorant of the Scriptures. I am sure there have been preachers who stood in the pulpit simply believing God will tell them what to say. The promise is not an excuse for no preparation. Read your Bible, Study your Bible, Memorize your Bible Hebrews 13:13-15 (WEB) Let us therefore go forth to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach. For we don't have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come. Through him, then, let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips which make confession to his name. AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 Part 3 WITNESS 12:8-12 Daniel 3:16, 17 Jeremiah 26:14, 15 Value of Your Witness confess It is not just recognizing a truth, but to live in it Jesus puts our relationship to Him in eternal perspective It is verbal confession demonstrated in a life of godly living Galatians 1:10 Psalm 116:15 The Refusal of Your Witness 12:9, 10 Jude 4 1 John 2:25 2 Peter 2:1 Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit is committed as one ascribes the works of Jesus to the work of the devil A person who believes that Jesus is demon-possessed is completely outside the sphere of grace These people personally witnessed the Holy Spirit working through Jesus and still falsely identified the work accomplished through the power of Satan. The Assurance of Your Witness 12:11, 12 Persecution will come from the religionist God will not only teach you what to say, but will teach when to remain silent The Holy Spirit is faithful to teach the believer at the moment of crisis John 14:26 Acts 4:8, 31; 6:10, 16 AS FOR YOU Luke 12:1-12 Part 3 WITNESS 12:8-12 ____________ 3:16, 17 ____________ 26:14, 15 Value of Your Witness confess It is not just recognizing a truth, but to ___________ in it Jesus puts our relationship to Him in ____________ perspective It is verbal confession _____________ in a life of godly living Galatians 1:_____ Psalm 116:_____ The Refusal of Your Witness 12:9, 10 ________ 4 1 John 2:25 2 Peter ______ Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit is committed as one ascribes the works of Jesus to the work of the ____________ A person who believes that Jesus is demon-possessed is completely ____________ the sphere of grace These people personally witnessed the Holy Spirit working through Jesus and still ________ identified the work accomplished through the power of Satan. The Assurance of Your Witness 12:11, 12 Persecution __________ come from the religionist God will not only teach you _________ to say, but will teach __________ to remain silent The Holy Spirit is faithful to teach the believer at the ____________ of crisis John 14:______ _________ 4:8, 31; 6:10, 16 ============================================= === SERMON: message(117).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/29/2024 11:59:24 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message IT S JUST A SIMPLE QUESTION Luke 12:12-21 INTRODUCTION I did not watch the debate Thursday night. My exposure was limited to the commentaries I knew would follow which I found to be quite entertaining. The panel that caught my attention was from CNN when, while bemoaning how poorly President Biden did, one woman (not knowing her preferred pronouns), made the comment that Biden knew the question was coming and still flubbed it. Now, I don t know if he had advanced knowledge of the questions or not. We went through that with Hillary. Whether advanced knowledge was gained or not, to the best of my knowledge there were no got you questions. At the conclusion of our text this morning is another question. It is a simple question, but requires more than a quick response. It cannot be glossed over; it cannot be dismissed; it requires some serious soul searching. It demands an evaluation of our lives that may be uncomfortable. The question is from Jesus statement in verse 21; Am I one who is more concerned with treasures on earth than treasures in heaven? This will be followed by next week s challenge from v. 34; for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. These two weeks will be present a serious challenge to our attitude toward stuff; our manner of living; the way we use our money; our relationship with the world. Is my life all about my comfort and keeping up with whoever? It is more than having stuff, it is my attitude about stuff. THE PROBLEM 12:13 one from the crowd He had to be close enough to be heard among the multitude And if he was close enough to be heard, he had to have been close enough to hear what Jesus had just said. Jesus talked about caring about sparrows and the hairs on your head Jesus has already promised God s care over His people, but here is a guy concerned about his inheritance. Jesus has spoken of persecution; this guy in interested in personal treasures So, the disconnect is established God s promise and man s greed This will be a challenge for us Jesus had just taken worry off the table; this guy, like many of us, puts it right back Someone has said, When there is an inheritance 99% of us become wolves We might as well be saying, Yea, the eternal stuff is nice, but what about now? Everybody deserves to have nice stuff And assuming the other brother is older, the law stipulates that the older gets 2/3 and the younger gets 1/3. Deuteronomy 21:15-17 It appears this guy is looking for a 50/50 split. As we look at the text, keep in mind the question we need to answer Am I a person going to live life toward myself or toward God? THE PRIORITY 12:14 Jesus refused to get involved He has a mission; preaching the Kingdom; little concern with legal matters Why did the fellow even ask? He saw Jesus as a teacher of Old Testament law Rabbis often ruled on disputed sections of the Law Man is not a cordial response in this case; it is harsh, a rebuke Jesus sees behind the legal issues and addresses the motives of the heart Law may lay a restraint on sinful men, so as to make them law-abiding citizens, but it cannot make them good. (C. B. Caird) Jesus was not interested in legal issues but dealing with sin problems. The greedy man was not interested in dealing with deeper issues, he wanted his problem fixed. Even ordering Jesus to make the ruling. It is no fun dealing with our own hearts. Asking what is in my heart that caused that response? The heart of every problem is a problem of the heart. The other brother could have also been greedy. He probably was present. v. 14 you is plural The them in v. 15, 16 could have been to both brothers and the crowd Like too may people today, he wanted Jesus to serve him, not save him. v. 15 addesses the crowd There are two commands: Take heed and beware Take heed keep looking out; take it to heart; take inventory continually Am I a greedy person? Do I enjoy giving or do I have selfish tendencies? Am I wrapped up in material stuff? Mark Twain once defined "civilization" as "a limitless multiplication of unnecessary necessities. Many Christians are infected with covetousness and do not know it. 1 Timothy 6 does not just apply to the rich and famous Greed is constantly close by Eccesiastes 5:10 He who loves silver shall not be satisfied with silver; nor he who loves abundance, with increase: this also is vanity. Luke 8:14 The seed which fell among the thorns, these are the ones who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with worries and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to maturity. Romans 7:7, 8 Paul used the sin of covetousness to illustrate the battering effect of sin upon the life of the flesh Romans 7:7-8 What shall we say then? Is the law sin? May it never be! However, I wouldn't have known sin, except through the law. For I wouldn't have known coveting, unless the law had said, "You shall not covet." But sin, finding occasion through the commandment, produced in me all kinds of coveting. For apart from the law, sin is dead. Greed is the classic symptom of a life that has lost touch with spiritual realities. The Greek word for Greed has the sense of the thirst for having more It comes in all shapes and sizes. beware guard yourselves present tense; a continual warning We all are under attack Ephesians 5:5; Colossians 3:5 covetousness is linked to idolatry Idolatry is looking to things to provide what God said He will do A person is measured by who he is, not what he has Christina Onassis said, "Happiness is not based on money and the greatest proof of that is our family." a limitless multiplication of unnecessary necessities THE PARABLE 12:16-19 illustration through a parable: comparison, to lay beside Parable of the Rich Fool or The Doom of the Materialist - MacArthur Jesus was making a point of the foolishness of concern about material things in light of coming tribulation and the kingdom of God This is more than just a warning to the greedy fellow Jesus had just warned His disciples about fearing God more than man Here is another danger: excessive attachment to wealth It is A warning about living for time and forgetting eternity The man expected to control the fate of the crops Seems he was a good farmer; maybe he worked the land; maybe he had hired help The yield was good; the good God gave the good yield; bumper crop brought forth abundantly = English word euphoric - high spirits What would you do if you suddenly came into great wealth? Would that be a problem for you? We saw in Luke 8 that wealth can choke the Word of God Wealth can create all kinds of snares and temptations 1 Timothy 6 Would you build larger barns or ask God for a bigger heart? 19, 20 The epitome of secular humanism He is a man who feels self-sufficient 11x in vs. 17-19 we find the personal pronouns I or my He is concerned about himself He has no sense of his responsibility, no reality of death The people would have called him a successful farmer God called him a fool ; lacking sense; stupid fool Psalm 14:1 Psalm 39:4 Psalm 90:10 Psalm 103:15, 16 soul his inner self and mental faculties This was not unconscious action, but a clear decision on his part to eliminate God from his life It was never what God would want; what his family would want; what his synagogue might need His legacy was warehouses full of grain that would last longer than he would The future was not under his control James 4:13-15 Come now, you who say, "Today or tomorrow let's go into this city, and spend a year there, trade, and make a profit." Whereas you don't know what your life will be like tomorrow. For what is your life? For you are a vapor, that appears for a little time, and then vanishes away. For you ought to say, "If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that." On March 11, 1856, Henry David Thoreau wrote in his journal, That man is the richest whose pleasures are cheapest. THE PURPOSE 12:21 The greatest irony of this story is the rich fool s own words Eat, drink and be merry The rich man pronounced his own epitaph The parable does not condemn planning for the future or being wealthy Kent Hughes: this is the only place in the Bible where retirement is spoken of, and here it is in the context of disapproval. Of course, the Bible recognizes aging and slowing down. But retiring to a life of self-indulgence finds no favor with God. The disease: the essence of selfishness 1 Timothy 6:18, 19 the antidote 1 Timothy 6:18-19 (WEB) that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to communicate; laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life. Life, in spite of all its complexities, may be reduced to the very simple formula-question of v. 21 Is a person going to live life for himself or toward God? The preposition eis frequently carries the sense of direction and purpose Many times translated into A life lived eis theon is a life lived moving toward God It is a life that longs to follow after God and His precepts A life directed toward God is a life led by the Holy Spirit There is spiritual motivation This God-centered life is contrary to the life lived for self A life which panders after material wealth and possessions, and is in fact lived in bondage to mammon 16:13 This parable confronts us with the claim of exclusive loyalty either to God or to self. Revelation 3:14-17 "To the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: "The Amen, the Faithful and True Witness, the Head of God's creation, says these things: "I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot. So, because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I will vomit you out of my mouth. Because you say, 'I am rich, and have gotten riches, and have need of nothing;' and don't know that you are the wretched one, miserable, poor, blind, and naked; A Christian congregation is addressed concerning its dependence upon worldly riches rather than the riches of heaven found in Christ. Here is the ultimate investment strategy using the word Treasure ??sa????? thesaur zo, thay-sow-rid'-zo 8x NT Highest yield and ultimate hedge fund; Matthew 6:19, 20 Do not lay up an action already taking place; stop doing it But lay up make this a life habit Having possessions is not wrong, it is putting your security in them that is wrong. -- Hampton Keathley Jim Elliott: He is no fool who gives up what he cannot keep to gain what he cannot lose. Our money says In God we trust but do we trust God with our money D. L. Moody once said, It does not take long to tell where a man s treasure is. In fifteen minutes conversation with most men, you can tell whether their treasures are on earth or in heaven He who dies with the most toys still dies. Half the world is unhappy because it can't have the things that are making the other half unhappy." When you die will you be leaving your treasure behind or going to it? IT S JUST A SIMPLE QUESTION Luke 12:12-21 Am I one who is more concerned with treasures on earth than treasures in heaven? THE PROBLEM 12:13 God s promise and man s greed Deuteronomy 21:15-17 THE PRIORITY 12:14 Rabbis often ruled on disputed sections of the Law Jesus sees behind the legal issues and addresses the motives of the heart The heart of every problem is a problem of the heart. Mark Twain once defined "civilization" as "a limitless multiplication of unnecessary necessities. Ecclesiastes 5:10 Luke 8:14 Romans 7:7, 8 Greed: the thirst for having more A person is measured by who he is, not what he has THE PARABLE 12:16-19 A warning about living for time and forgetting eternity brought forth abundantly = euphoric This was a clear decision on his part to eliminate God from his life James 4:13-15 THE PURPOSE 12:21 The rich man pronounced his own epitaph 1 Timothy 6:18, 19 Is a person going to live life for himself or toward God? A life directed toward God is a life led by the Holy Spirit Revelation 3:14-17 When you die will you be leaving your treasure behind or going to it? IT S JUST A SIMPLE QUESTION Luke 12:12-21 Am I one who is more concerned with treasures on ____________ than treasures in ____________? THE PROBLEM 12:13 God s promise and man s ____________ Deuteronomy 21:15-17 THE PRIORITY 12:14 ____________ often ruled on disputed sections of the Law Jesus sees behind the legal issues and addresses the ________ of the heart The heart of every problem is a problem of the heart. Mark Twain once defined "civilization" as "a limitless multiplication of ____________ necessities. Ecclesiastes 5:10 Luke 8:14 Romans 7:7, 8 Greed: the ____________ for having more A person is ____________ by who he is, not what he has THE PARABLE 12:16-19 A warning about living for time and ____________ eternity brought forth abundantly = ____________ This was a clear decision on his part to ____________ God from his life ____________ 4:13-15 THE PURPOSE 12:21 The rich man pronounced his own ____________ 1 Timothy 6:18, 19 Is a person going to live life for himself or ____________ God? A life directed toward God is a life led by the Holy Spirit Revelation 3:_________ When you die will you be ____________ your treasure behind or____________ to it? ============================================= === SERMON: message(118).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/6/2024 11:03:38 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message CONFIDENT LIVING Luke 12:22-34 INTRODUCTION I realize our problems are first world problems. We talk of how prices increase, the election, crime, the temperature, education There was an article out this past week about the annual salary needed to live comfortably. It determined $186,000 was needed. Another article dated March, 2024, broke it down to particular cities. In the average city, an individual required $96k Two adults and two children require $235k And they highlighted 6 cities which required salaries over $300k to live In Ohio for two adults, two children Toledo: 208k; Cleveland: 217k; Cincinnati: 213k Toledo and Cleveland were highlighted as the most affordable. All that to say, how you doing? Money is one of the big items of the list of things in which people worry Health is another problem for the worry-wort A worrywart is a person who worries or frets excessively The expression originated in Amerca! There is not evidence that worry causes warts The old wives fable was that by picking up a frog you could get warts Even though it is not true, it was still a belief among some people who came to be known as worrywarts So, a worrywart is someone one worries needlessly, without justification What is interesting, at least to me, is that originally it did not mean that at all. The term started in the 1920 s as the name of a character drawn by J. R. Williams and appeared in Dell Comics. This tyke was not a worrier at all: He was a nuisance, an annoyance. He caused others to worry. You don t hear that name much anymore because working with worriers has become a major medical money maker. Anxiety disorders, panic attacks and the such are common fields of study and thousands, and probably millions, of people are taking some type of medication. The best that is offered is to help people deal with or manage their anxiety. I am not saying such a condition is not real. Some can get in such a state that to properly minister to them they may very well need some form of medication temporarily. For a Christian, our goal should not be managing our worry. It should be eliminating our worry. We could well title this section of Luke as How to Live Worry-free A quick Goggle search reveals that 85 99% of the things we worry about never happen. Psychology Today says that 91% of those things never happen and the other 9% proved to no big deal. We could highlight stats all morning, but the point is - worrying is sin. Look at out text: 12:22, 25, 26, 29 And to get to the heart of the matter, 12:28 o ye of little faith We had a question last week Am I more concerned about my treasures on earth than I am my treasures in heaven? A question for this week, Am I a worrywart? REVIEW: Last Week: The Problem, The Priority, The Parable Different recipients; same subject V.13 Jesus responds to the demand of one individual by Warning of Covetousness Am I going to live for myself or toward God? When I die will I be leaving my treasure behind or going to it? LIVING THE BASICS 12:22 Who is audience? The same group as highlighted in v.1; the casual learners? Or is Jesus speaking more directly to His true disciples; the 12, the 70 and many more? I think Jesus is speaking more directly to the few out of the multitude v. 32 little flock However, no reason to believe many others heard the message Here is a concrete application on the message of covetousness Take no thought do not continue to be anxious; stop being anxious Anxious about the necessities of life Direct application to food and clothing Can t get much more basic than that Clearly it is God s will for you not to worry; 3x; vs. 22, 25, 26 That lack of anxiety should characterize the people of God This is a liberating message: you can be confident God will provide v. 22 begins on account of this This is the lesson of the parable Beware of Greed, beware of selfishness, now beware of worry Greed can never have enough; worry is afraid it will not be enough Leon Morris Worry is the emotional reward for worrying about material things. K. Hughes Jesus is not encouraging a lazy who-cares attitude about money. God will provide for the birds, but He does not plop the worm in the mouth while the bird is sitting in the next. The bird has to go get the worm. Prov. 27:23, 24 speaks to the issue of money The warning is an inordinate, consuming worry. And we can easily masquerade our worry under the guise of being responsible Jesus temptation: man does live by bread alone Israel in the wilderness: help them to realize that their existence came not from food, but they should rely on the God to provide for their needs. We could easily spend the whole message on worry. Will limit it to this Worry is a sin for two reasons: (R. Pritchard) It displaces God in your life. When you worry you are living as if God does not exist You are living as if you alone can solve your problems It distracts you from things that really matter As long as you are worrying, you can t do anything else You are strangled by worry Worry is a thin stream of fear that trickles through the mind, which, if encouraged, will cut a channel so wide that all other thoughts will be drained out." LIBERATION FROM THE BASICS 12:23-28 Or An Antidote for Worry Explanation 12:23 for explaining why they did not need to worry life is more than meat to be overly concerned with them is to miss out on life s important concern a relationship to God Life can consist only in reliance on God; the material will only fade away. Your worrying only brings you down to the level of an animal whose only concern is physical needs You have eternal matters to pursue The core of life concerns the soul not the body (12:4, 5) Food and clothing should not be your main worry. Your eternal soul should be your main concern. If someone says, Yes, but I m going to starve to death, Jesus replies, But where will your soul spend eternity? But I ll freeze to death because I don t have proper clothing! Yes, but then you ll be too hot, if you re not rightly related to God! Illustrations 12:24 Illustration from nature 12:24 Ravens: only mention in the NT They are not farmers, they cannot plant crops They cannot store their crops for the future Have you ever seen one dying from starvation? What a contrast with the rich farmer. He wrongly was concerned with storing up for the future, but wrongly ignored God. The birds don t worry, but they do work Totally dependent on God Jesus is saying watch how I take care of them Illustration for extending life 12:25, 26 You cannot use anxiety to better your life Hendrickson: A man may worry himself to death; he cannot worry himself into a longer span of life. Stature maturity in years or size You cannot add an hour to your life (Hezekiah) There is a God-determined time You can however shorten your life by sinning; 1 Cor. 11:29,30; 1 John 5:16 Some view this as adding to your height; no amount of worry will grow you 18 inches; so most see greater worry about length of life than height of body Since you cannot do that (v. 26); why worry about it? This is not meant as Christian fatalism; rather, it is meant to provide the basis for living with confidence in a loving and trustworthy God. It is not about how many supplements you take, how much exercise you do You are not going to extend your life beyond the days established by God Yes, all things in moderation, gluttony is sin, you are a steward of your body Take proper care; be responsible But we all seen those who did everything wrong and lived to be in their 80 s and 90 s. Illustration from Lilies v. 27 Confident Living Lilies: can refer to many flowers on the hillsides in Israel Autumn crocus, Easter daisy, scarlet anemone, poppies None of these flowers achieve beauty on their own God gives them their beauty Consider notice, observe carefully, reflect upon, contemplate Not a casual glance; take a serious look at the created order Stop and smell the roses See how it magnifies the Lord and teaches us important aspects of God s richness and love toward us Do you see any flowers toiling and straining? Toil: exhibit great effort Spin: weave Solomon: the wisest and richest; yet flowers have better covering, colors He had the resources to seek out the best He pursued it all to the fullest extent; dressed for success Why do we worry ourselves to death about that which God gives to plants which cannot care? Illustration from Grass 12:28 even short-lived grasses are an illustration grass is not meant to last long grass was often used for cooking; hence thrown into the furnace Hendrickson: If God provides for the short-lived grass, He will surely provide for His children, destined for eternal glory. If God decks the wild flowers with such very beautiful garments, then He will certainly clothe His children with the ordinary garments which they need. CONFIDENT LIVING Luke 12:22-34 LIVING THE BASICS 12:22 Here is a concrete application on the message of covetousness Take no thought stop being anxious That lack of anxiety should characterize the people of God Worry is a sin for two reasons: (R. Pritchard) It displaces God in your life. It distracts you from things that really matter LIBERATION FROM THE BASICS 12:23-28 Life can consist only in reliance on God The core of life concerns the soul not the body (12:4, 5) Illustration from nature 12:24 Illustration for extending life 12:25, 26 Stature maturity in years or size There is a God-determined time Illustration from Lilies v. 27 None of these flowers achieve beauty on their own Consider take a serious look Toil: exhibit great effort Spin: weave Illustration from Grass 12:28 ============================================= === SERMON: message(119).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/10/2024 10:44:24 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: MESSAGE CONFIDENT LIVING LUKE 12:22-34 Alfred E. Newman (JON, PLEASE FIND A PICTURE) the fictitious mascot and cover boy of the American humor magazine Mad . The character's distinct smiling face, gap-toothed smile, freckles, red hair, protruding ears, and scrawny body dates back to late 19th-century advertisements for painless dentistry, also the origin of his "What, me worry?" motto. This was changed for one issue to "Yes, me worry!" after the Three Mile Island accident in 1979. Al Feldstein took over as Mad's editor in 1956: I decided that I wanted to have this visual logo as the image of Mad, the same way that corporations had the Jolly Green Giant and the dog barking at the gramophone for RCA . This kid was the perfect example of what I wanted. So I put an ad in The New York Times that said, "National magazine wants portrait artist for special project". In walked this little old guy in his sixties named Norman Mingo , and he said, "What national magazine is this?" I said "Mad," and he said, "Goodbye." I told him to wait, and I dragged out all these examples and postcards of this idiot kid, and I said, "I want a definitive portrait of this kid. I don't want him to look like an idiot I want him to be loveable and have an intelligence behind his eyes. But I want him to have this devil-may-care attitude, someone who can maintain a sense of humor while the world is collapsing around him." At the October 2008 Alfred E. Smith Memorial Foundation Dinner , then-presidential candidate Barack Obama joked, "It's often been said that I share the politics of Alfred E. Smith . And the ears of Alfred E. Neuman." During an interview on May 10, 2019, President Donald Trump said "Alfred E. Neuman cannot become president of the United States", in reference to presidential candidate Pete Buttigieg .[30] After Buttigieg said he didn't know who Neuman was, Mad subsequently referenced Pete Buttigieg on social media REVIEW: LIVNG THE BASICS, LIBERTY FROM THE BASICS LACKING THE BASICS 12:28-32 concludes with a statement about lack of faith O ye of little faith 1 word in the Greek Little trust; little faith; as an adjective meaning poverty of faith This was an indictment on the disciples for their failure to see the greatness of God even in the smallest items in the natural order We may not like the title, but it is often an accurate reflection of our soul Much care, worry; speaks of little faith Spurgeon: . Little faith is not a little fault; for it greatly wrongs the Lord, and sadly grieves the fretful mind. To think the Lord who clothes the lilies will leave his own children naked is shameful Little faith: we really don t believe God can take care of us All of our worry stems from a lack of faith in God Worry says What if ; Faith says, Even if Insurance companies deal with worry; Bible deals with faith We trust God for the care of our eternal soul, but fall into a state of unbelief when it comes to the matters of daily living We have little faith because we have not been in a position of lack We always have had food, clothes, the necessities of life. You have had a job, gas for the car, health has been pretty good But it is so easy for us to get all agitated when the smallest of issues arises We much too quickly resort to the why me ? We are altogether selfish. We are what Jeremiah wrote: following the dictates of our own evil heart; we love our own flesh; we look out for #1 Over the last 35 years, we have had folks experience some trying times Perhaps the biggest was Don being out of work for 26 months But you have heard their testimony of God s faithfulness during that time He provided everything needed. That is His promise. Philippians 4:19 and my God shall supply all your need, according to His riches in glory in Christ Jesus But, once again, the context is critical. Paul had learned to have and have not 4:11, 12 He had just said there were times he had a great need and the folks of Philippi provided for him. With that in mind, we can finish our text 12:29, 30 2 present imperatives with the negative; do not Stop seeking; stop doubting 12:29, 30 Jesus did not suggest we stop; He did not offer an alternative He commanded that we stop now those activities that are contrary to faith He does not say to stop working but to stop obsessing Faith is so powerful that we can trust God to be gracious toward us with all that He has to offer. Worry ties the hands of God when He really desires to show His ability to deliver Faith is truly the key that unlocks the storehouse of God s riches in glory. The word at the end of v. 29 is interesting; only use in the NT Translated: Doubtful mind, anxious mine, be not in suspense Definition: to raise in mid-air; the root word is meteor Suspended in air, not certain whether to hang or fall to the ground (Trapp) Don t be one who vacillates Eternal life is more important than what we eat Drinking fresh water in Israel was often hard to find doubtful do not be anxious, worried It his hovering between hope and fear The Consequences of Lack of Faith First, It limits the opportunities of the Kingdom in our life Second, It is a continuation of our commitment to the ways of the world Worldly standards are never to be the measurement for our faithfulness to the Word of God Haggai 1:1-11 v. 9 I blew it away v. 29 and 30 stop seeking; Start Seeking 12:31-34 The Kingdom of God the reality of the rule of God Jesus is saying, Get out of the way and put me first in your life. To solve your worries about money, You must seek for God s kingdom above your own needs. Once the rule of God becomes the priority of our life, then everything else takes its proper place. Don t be all consumed about food and clothes; they are not the main thing When we are consumed with making a living, we are mimicking the world Do we really need to imitate pagans? Hard work is a Christian virtue; worrying about money is not What does it mean to seek the Kingdom of God? We begin with the command. Seek In Matt. 6 it was seek first the kingdom of God It does not mean everyone is a missionary or in full-time Christian service God s kingdom is where He rules It gives direction to each day: I purpose to further His rule over my life He is to be the center of all we think, say, and do each day. He is Lord over every facet of our lives, including our money We live as stewards; seeking to glorify Him You honor God by loving your spouse, doing a honest day s work Exhibiting good character in all transactions In the context, not even our physical well-being is a worthy object to devote our lives to. Assurance: 12:31 all these things will be added Seek His kingdom; the Father will provide things that which the nations seek: food, clothing, material needs 12:32 don t be afraid little flock literally: stop being afraid A special theme of the Luke is the contrast between human fear and the power of God to overcome any obstacle of fear. 1:13 the angel to Zechariah: fear not 1:30 Do not be afraid, Mary 1:37 Gabriel: for with God nothing shall be impossible little flock a precious term to those called by God Places us directly under the rule and provision of the Great Shepherd of our faith; green pastures, still waters, through dark valleys The title, sheep, makes us vulnerable in a world full of wolves Application 12:33 v. 33 a call to not depend on the values of the world to determine our witnessing for Christ We are to free ourselves of those things that would ultimately hinder our obedience to His call. You may not have much here; but you will have the Kingdom Many others are like the rich farmer; they have now; their only enjoyment is now; they will leave it all behind Spurgeon - When Abraham had many sons, he gave to each one of them a portion and sent them away; but Isaac had the family heritage. It is the same with you. The Lord may give to others more than he bestows upon you in this life, but for you he reserves the kingdom. Are you not content with that, whatever else your Father gives you or withholds from you? May God plant our feet on Higher Ground Explanation 12:34 for where your treasure is there will your heart be also Cf. Matthew 6:19-21 for a word of explanation, but what is being explained? Our heart will always follow our treasure so where I lay my treasure that is where my heart is depend on God and the right kind of treasure the heart is considered the seat of the intellect, emotions, will Jesus is speaking of ultimate loyalties; if one s heart is really in search of heavenly treasure, then one will be utterly and wholeheartedly devoted to God and there will be no room in that person s life to pander after the things of this world. The passage speaks of how to deal with anxiety. Money and anxiety travel together. Your deposits are made to the First National Bank of Heaven or there will be no dividend when Christ returns. It will be there to greet you Is your treasure laid up in the right place? Where no thief can steal; rust corrupt; moth have for a snack If none of your treasure has gone to heaven, your heart is not there either We often get this backward; we think I will put my treasure where my heart is But Jesus says my heart will follow my treasure Follow the trail of a person s money and you will find their heart Remember, this message began with the demand about sharing an inheritance You will have a special interest in a missionary if you give them money It is easier to pray for them So, Jesus point is, if you want your heart in the things of God, put your treasure in the kingdom of God. Underlying our discussion is this: We don t own anything, we are stewards of what God has given to us We have the privilege of using some for our own needs, but must always be asking Lord, how do you want me to use my resources for your Kingdom ? Stewardship frees us from worry Pastor Steve Cole: Once when I was in the Coast Guard, we put out a fire on Frank Sinatra s yacht. I remember talking to the skipper and being surprised at how nonchalant he was about the great amount of damage done to the boat. He said, It s not my boat, it s Mr. Sinatra s boat. You have stewardship of a vehicle. The care and maintenance are your responsibility. But if someone puts a dent in it beyond your control, you can say, Lord, it s your car. What is your purpose for that ? The conclusion of the matter is back in v. 1 Believe in God; believe also in Me Do you really believe God when He speaks about your treasure? Where is your heart? Are you willing to examine the desires of your heart? The simple analysis is this: if the heart is right, everything else in life falls into its proper place. The person who is right with the Lord will be generous and happy in his giving to the Lord s work. By the same token, a person who is covetous, self-indulgent, and stingy has good reason to question his relationship with the Lord. MacArthur Henry Ward Beecher once said that "No man can tell whether he is rich or poor by turning to his ledger. It is the heart that makes a man rich. He is rich or poor according to what he is, not according to what he has." The late Chaplain of the United States Senate, Peter Marshall (1902-1949) - Let us give according to our incomes, lest God make our incomes match our gifts. "Make all you can, save all you can, give all you can" -- John Wesley The poorest person is one whose only wealth is money. CONFIDENT LIVING Luke 12:22-34 LIVNG THE BASICS; LIBERTY FROM THE BASICS LACKING THE BASICS 12:28-32 Little trust; little faith; poverty of faith Little faith: we really don t believe God can take care of us Worry says What if ; Faith says, Even if Philippians 4:19 Stop seeking; stop doubting 12:29, 30 He does not say to stop working but to stop obsessing Doubtful mind to raise in mid-air; the root word is meteor Eternal life is more important than what we eat Consequences of Lack of Faith It limits the opportunities of the Kingdom in our life It is a continuation of our commitment to the ways of the world Start Seeking 12:31-34 The Kingdom of God the reality of the rule of God You must seek for God s kingdom above your own needs Hard work is a Christian virtue; worrying about money is not He is Lord over every facet of our lives, including our money Assurance: 12:31 things that which the nations seek Application 12:33 We are to free ourselves of those things that would ultimately hinder our obedience to His call. Explanation 12:34 Our heart will always follow our treasure ============================================= === SERMON: message(120).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/19/2024 10:39:27 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message Do you need help picking songs? LIVING IN THE FUTURE TENSE Luke 12:35-48 INTRODUCTION The subject under discussion in Luke 12 originated with the demand of the fellow in verse 13 about dividing the inheritance. Not only did Jesus refuse to get involved, He exposed the heart of the man by presenting a message on covetousness. So for the past 3 weeks, we have examined the message by looking at LIVING THE BASICS, LIBERTY FROM THE BASICS AND LACKING THE BASICS. Covetousness is idolatry. Covetousness results in worry. Worry is not something to be managed, but a sin to be eradicated. It is destructive to a soul. It is an afront to God as we really don t believe when God said we are more important than birds and flowers. This culture is telling us our life is based on the things we possess. This is a serious issue. It will be reflected in our ability to help others: our contribution to the local food pantry, the readiness to assist a church family, bearing one another s burdens. It is an immediate indication of our priorities as we read in Haggai 1. It is an accurate reading of the temperature of our faith. Do we believe in God s ability to provide? Are we what if? or even if believers? Is our faith at the poverty level or strong? When I die will I be leaving my treasures behind or going to them? The next section may at first seem disjointed as if Jesus jumped to another subject. But there is a natural flow. Look at 12:34, for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. Jesus has spoken of our treasures in heaven and now focuses our attention on the return of our greatest treasure in heaven, Himself.
