The problem with focusing on worldly possessions is that we lose proper perspective of the parousia , the return of Christ. You cannot keep your eyes on God while they are focused on gold. Here is a reminder that earthly things are temporary, the coming of the Son of Man is certain. Wiersbe: "Jesus shifted the emphasis from being worried about the present to being watchful about the future. The themes in Luke 12 all go together, for one of the best ways to conquer hypocrisy, covetousness, and worry is to look for the Lord s return. When you are living in the future tense, it is difficult for the things of the world to ensnare you. A proper upward or vertical focus will provide the proper motivation and empowerment for our horizontal living. Who I am looking for will greatly impact who or what I am living for It has been estimated that 1 out of every 25 NT verses speaks to the return of Jesus. You cannot get very far without that reminder. Evidently, we need it. The subject of watchfulness and obedience is highlighted in 3 parables from verses 35-48 WAITING SERVANTS 12:35-38 These verses find their parallels Mark 13:33-37; Matt. 24:42 Verses related to the second coming of Christ Luke presents the same material with a different emphasis The Business of Being Prepared 12:35, 36 v. 35 be continually dressed in readiness in their world men would tuck their robes around a belt at the waist to facilitate work and speed The idea is a continual sense of readiness having tucked their robe Don t wait until the last moment to get prepared Within the context: be heavenly minded, not earthly minded In Jesus day, it was a long robe that could trip you up In the context: Dealing with hypocrisy: look up Dealing with covetousness: look up Dealing with worry: look up Girded loins is how the enslaved Jews ate their Passover; ready to march 1 Peter 1:13 Ephesians 6:14 NIV: Be dressed ready for service and keep your lamps burning The inner willingness and the illumination to serve well Poised for action; ready at all times Prepared Servants look at the future Illustration: one of the critiques of the present generation is once a paycheck or two is received they quit work; just don t show up. Only living for the present. Jesus has already given the parable of the rich fool; living for the immediate The wise man girds his loins and keeps his lamp burning During his 1960 presidential campaign, John F. Kennedy often closed his speeches with the story of Colonel Davenport, the Speaker of the Connecticut House of Representatives. One day in 1789, the sky of Hartford darkened ominously, and some of the representatives, glancing out the windows, feared the end was at hand. Quelling a clamor for immediate adjournment, Davenport rose and said, The Day of Judgment is either approaching or it is not. If it is not, there is no cause for adjournment. If it is, I choose to be found doing my duty. Therefore, I wish that candles be brought. Waiting takes a different twist in vs. 36-38 Here servants are waiting for the return of their master who is attending a wedding. Wedding celebrations may last a week or so; The return date and time is uncertain waiting continual mindset; ever expecting; ever looking How can I maintain a waiting mindset? Detached from possessions; 12:13-21 Detached from cares of daily life: 12:22-34 Their master could have been the bridegroom returning with his bride He wants to carry her over the threshold but can t stand there all night waiting for the door to open The point is continual vigilance. The Blessing of Being Prepared 12:37, 38 Here is a reversal of the servant image: the master will be so delighted that the servants are properly awaiting him that he serves them The blessing of faithful, obedient service Jesus came to earth to serve (Mark 10:45) and He will still be serving in Heaven His whole life was one of condescension, which was never more clearly manifested than it was when he, the Lord and Master of all, took the position of servant of all, and washed his disciples feet; yet he here tells us that, if he finds us watching when he comes again, he will once more take his place as our servitor. We may not think it strange of His servant mentality while upon earth But it remains the same in the day of His glory J C Ryle - "This is perhaps one of the most wonderful promises made to believers in the New Testament. It must probably be interpreted figuratively. It means that there is no limit to the honor and glory which the Lord Jesus will bestow on those who are ready to meet him at his second coming." WATCHING SERVANTS 12:39, 40 The parable in reverse Where we had the servant awaiting the arrival of his master Now there is an unwanted thief and a householder who might be asleep Jesus is not the thief here We are looking at the story as a whole; not the individual characters in it Thieves do not tell you when they are coming They come when least expected; quietly, swiftly You are like the householder who is to always on high alert Another reminder of a daily sense of readiness The Church must be awake for the coming of Christ It is awaking every day believing this could be the day Illustration: I was in the 5th or 6th grade. Our teacher was also the school principle as it was a small country school. He was called out of the classroom leaving instructions of the work we should do. He was a little longer than expected and one student close to his desk grabbed a prism laying there. It found its way to a fellow named Bobby. He is sitting there looking through it and talking when the teacher returns. The teacher next all students who had touched the prism lined up. I read of a person who learned as a young girl who grew up on CA to go to be each night with a flashlight and shoes under her bed. If an earthquake happened, she would be ready. Shoes to walk on any shattered glass and light to deal with lost electricity. This is fulfilling the command of v. 31 seek the kingdom Instead of worrying about material things, trust God s provisions WORKING SERVANTS 12:40-48 The Question 12:40 Peter - always the spokesman 12:22 the last record of an audience The answer would indicate it is to everyone listening as Jesus refers to faithful and unfaithful workers Not only are faithful servants to be watching, they are to working Like the parable of the soils, there is only one good category. The Faithful Worker 12:42 v. 42 he is faithful; v. 43 the same is described as a slave some are given specific authority: apostles, elders, teachers 1 Cor. 4:1 stewards are to be found faithful But speaks to all believers 1 Peter 4:10 giving rations feeding with pure milk (1 Pet. 2:2); solid food (Heb. 5:14) He is Rewarded 12:43, 44 Rewarded as a doer Rewarded when not if He returns; not known but a certainty, not a possibility Ephesians 2:10 The only good works are done in Christ; John 15:5; abiding in the vine 12:37, 38, 43 the word blessed is used A faithful worker: In 1864 during the Civil War General left 1,500 men in the Kennesaw Mountains at Altoona Pass to guard 1 million rations while he marched from Atlanta to the sea. General Hood with his 6,000 troops attacked the fort. Half of the defenders were either killed or wounded and the general in command was wounded 7 times. When they were about to raise the surrender flag, the signal corps on a nearby mountain this message, Hold out, relief is coming. The message reinforced the defenders of the fort and they held out for 3 more hours, keeping the fort from the enemy. P. P. Bliss changed the wording to 1. Ho, my comrades, see the signal, waving in the sky! Reinforcements now appearing, victory is nigh. * Refrain: Hold the fort, for I am coming, Jesus signals still; Wave the answer back to Heaven, By Thy grace we will. 2. See the mighty host advancing, Satan leading on; Mighty ones around us falling, courage almost gone! 3. See the glorious banner waving! Hear the trumpet blow! In our Leader s Name we triumph over every foe. 4. Fierce and long the battle rages, but our help is near; Onward comes our great Commander, cheer, my comrades, cheer! Be a faithful Worker The False Worker 12:45, 46 (Outline headings from Phillips) Some these this as believers vs. unbelievers; others view both as believers with one receive rewards and the other losing rewards If is introducing a hypothetical situation The slave abuses his authority; ignores the warning of v. 40 He has a heart problem; the issue of faithfulness or unfaithfulness Hardening of the heart arteries takes place slowly The man did not express doubt about his Lord s return but he has lost any sense of urgency Here is the principle: If you are looking for Jesus, you are living for Jesus 1 John 3:2, 3 What are the indicators of loss of urgency? V. 45 The mistreatment of others Given to worldly pleasures Given to intoxication Contrast to a prepared believer: love for others, spiritual focus, self-control v. 46 Servant s Surprise the horror of punishment: cut into two pieces punishment as bad as being literally dismembered Spurgeon: Truly, our Lord uses very strong words; the Saviour is not one of your effeminate preachers like those of modern times, who seem as if the very word hell would burn their lips, and who will not warn men to flee from the wrath to come. It is an unkind and heartless want of humanity which prevents their being faithful to the souls of men. It is hard to isolate the exact application but it seems to me to apply to unfaithful spiritual leaders who have used their office for their own advantage Perhaps another indictment of the hypocrites of chapter 11 Pastor Steven Cole: But before you all say, Whew, this doesn t apply to me, you need to realize that there is a secondary application to us all. If we know about the things of God, but we don t repent of our selfishness and abusiveness toward others, especially in our homes, are we not just like these unfaithful stewards? This especially applies to every husband and father who professes to be a Christian. If we do not repent of mistreating our wives and children, whom God has entrusted to our care, woe to us when the Master returns! The Forgetful Worker 12:47 Well-schooled in the things of God; he knows what is expected Wastes his time, talents, and ignores God s will for his life Fritters away his life; lays not up treasures in heaven James 4:17 beaten with many stripes maybe even in this life as God s chastisement Maybe judgment to be given out later as much of his life is burned as chaff The Feeble Worker 12:48 he knew not but did things worthy of stripes No excuses but allowance made for ignorance Ignorance is never absolute; Romans 1:20, 21; 2:14-16 To sin against greater light is greater punishment Scripture speaks of degrees of reward and punishment Will be found both in heaven and hell I appreciate the words of Henry Morris: We may not fully understand how this can be, but the righteous "Judge of all the earth" (Genesis 18:25 ) is well able to "render every man according to his deeds" I believe we have been entrusted with much. Much freedom to live for Christ, much financial resources to be used for Christ. Here is the antidote for hypocrisy, covetousness, worry Here is where we examine our hearts to see where are treasure truly is LIVING IN THE FUTURE TENSE Luke 12:35-48 You cannot keep your eyes on God while they are focused on gold WAITING SERVANTS 12:35-38 The Business of Being Prepared 12:35, 36 Don t wait until the last moment to get prepared 1 Peter 1:13 Ephesians 6:14 The inner willingness and the illumination to serve well How can I maintain a waiting mindset? Detached from possessions; 12:13-21 Detached from cares of daily life: 12:22-34 The Blessing of Being Prepared 12:37, 38 WATCHING SERVANTS 12:39, 40 We are looking at the story as a whole; not the individual characters It is awaking every day believing this could be the day WORKING SERVANTS 12:40-48 The Question 12:40 The Faithful Worker 12:42 The False Worker 12:45, 46 What are the indicators of loss of urgency? The mistreatment of others Given to worldly pleasures Given to intoxication The Forgetful Worker 12:47 Well-schooled in the things of God James 4:17 The Feeble Worker 12:48 Ignorance is never absolute Romans 1:20, 21; 2:14-16 ============================================= === SERMON: message(121).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/3/2024 11:49:59 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message CRISIS MANAGEMENT Luke 12:49-59 INTRODUCTION The Basic Themes of Luke 12: Steering clear of Hypocrisy; Covetousness, Worry; Serving in Confidence and Watchfulness Jesus is now going to lay out a call of decision; a crisis time in the lives of those listening. You have had several such times in your life. The first one may have been what are you going to do after high school? Another big one revolved around marriage: if, who, when, where? Several others have surfaced from time to time: job, housing, major purchases We make decisions every day, but we know that some are a whole lot more significant than others. Is this discomfort I m feeling enough to seek medical attention? Would this activity be the best use of my time? Is this cause worthy of my time and money? Spiritual decisions: What church should I attend? Do I just attend or join and get more involved? Is this a good time to share the gospel with that person? How best might I pray for that person? I heard their request, but is there something more important for which I should be praying? Don t forget, folks, that Paul prayed 3 times for the thorn in his own flesh to be removed. It remained, but God had a purpose. We can add the previous section of Luke 12 to the subject of Crisis Management: the Crisis of Jesus Return. To be a Waiting, Watching, Working Servant of Christ Jesus is now issuing an invitational call. It carries through chapter 13 concluding what will happen if they reject 13:34, 35 THE CRISIS OF DIVISION 12:49-53 Being a disciple of Jesus is not the road to health and wealth Do you remember the message on the Standard of Discipleship? Of course not Luke 9:23 Deny himself, take up cross, follow The road is described as narrow and rough It is a road that must be traveled in obedience Here it is spelled out more graphically I have come speaking of His incarnation but here a summary of His 3 year public ministry. Fire the first word in the sentence place for emphasis Fire I have come to bring calling attention to the metaphor Fire can be judgment or the Holy Spirit (Acts 2) The context speaks to fire of judgment 12:4, 5; 8-10; 20, 21; 35-48 You cannot be neutral about fire. Ignore and perish or get on the right side Jesus is forcing the issue to take sides To those who believe, it will purify; to those who reject, it will consume the judgment that will come upon the world in light of the death of Christ. Calvary brings men to a traumatic decision v. 50 baptism Jesus death at Calvary; immersion under divine judgment straitened pressed, deep anguish, strained, distressed He was not sprinkled with suffering; He was immersed in agony There was never a moment in His earthly life where He did not have the agony of the cross in His mind. 19:10 His purpose for coming distressed until it is accomplished Here is the fire of judgment. What did Jesus bear on the Cross? The sins of the world. And what did the world deserve from God? His righteous judgment. When Jesus was nailed to the Cross, He took our place (cf substitutionary atonement) and bore God's righteous wrath against our sins that had been place upon Him! That is when the fire of God's judgment was kindled. Notice the fire was kindled; only begun; The fire of division will continue until the end of time 51-53 The results of His ministry and death Two of the effects of His ministry will be discord and disruption At His birth the angels proclaimed, peace, good will toward men Simeon prophesied that Jesus was set for the fall and rising again of many in Israel and that a sword would pierce Mary soul The purpose of this division and this sword piercing was that many hearts may be revealed Jesus reveals what is in a person s heart. We don t like that any more than the scribes, Pharisees and secular rulers did. It causes division It is traumatic for a person to have his sinful heart exposed for what it is There was division on Mt. Calvary; an unbelieving thief on one side and a believing thief on the other There was a division of stewards in vs. 43-48 Jesus is the Prince of Peace; national and personal His first coming did not provide national peace but serious division Those divisions came in the world system and families Those who decide to follow Christ must recognize the possibility that even those who love them will turn away. We must not let this possibility deter us from total commitment to Christ. Wycliffe Bible Commentary - Judaism was a family religion, in which the people worshiped by households rather than as individuals. Jesus foresaw that his claims would cut across family life, and would necessitate individual decisions. A call for a decision is a call for division John 17:14-18 Luke 8:19-21 even experienced by Jesus Hebrews 2:17 He can truly say, I know what it feels like CRISIS OF DIVISION LORD S SUPPER CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT 12:54-56 Here is a warning in their decision-making process A warning to those who have thus far rejected Could be considered more of an indictment on their unbelief There are two Greek words for time: chronos and Kairos Chronos: chronology, the word for the regular flow of time Kairos: the moment of critical decision or utmost importance There is the chronos for planning for a wedding And then the hour will come when someone will say, It s kairos The same can be said for surgery, school, vacation The previous verses were to prepare the listeners for the Kairos of His coming; the certain event in an uncertain time The importance of being prepared for the time Practical Illustration: the weather They learned from experience Red sky at night sailors delight; Red sky at morning sailors take warning From the west: Mediterranean Sea and rain From the south: warm weather 56 As the Divine Logos, Jesus always spoke truth and used words properly He didn t call people hypocrites without good reason What would be the purpose here? They were impersonating being spiritual; they were fake, counterfeit Jesus was undergoing close scrutiny by the scribes and Pharisees They were now seeking a reason to bring Him before the Roman authorities 11:53, 54 They hated Him without a cause, but more than willing to manufacture one Jesus called them hypocrites because they applied an honest evaluating of circumstances in determining the weather, but they would not exercise that same kind of honest evaluation in determining who Jesus was and what His purpose might be. They could see miracles, good deeds, listen to His teaching, and yet fail to see He was the promised Messiah They could see natural signs but fail to see spiritual signs They failed to forecast the worst storm; the coming Day of the Lord They had Moses, Isaiah, Jeremiah and other OT writers All pointing to Jesus; all more reliable signs than those determining the next day s weather The problem was not the evidence, but willful rejection His displeasure was not directed toward those who were confused and had serious doubts His anger was directed to those who were in a position to know the truth, but who deliberately rejected that truth because of insincere motives It wasn't that they didn't want a kingdom; sure they wanted a kingdom. It wasn't that they didn't want salvation; they wanted salvation. It wasn't that they didn't want forgiveness and eternal life. They want all that. It wasn't the cure they hated; it was the diagnosis they resented. He attacked their religious system. He stomped on their self-righteousness. MacArthur The truth would force them to change their way of living, their selfish goals, the way they dealt with their fellowman Changes they refuse to make CRISIS OF DIVISION, CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT CRISIS OF DELIVERANCE 12:57-59 They had enough revelation; enough to do what is right Their failure to discern while they had a golden opportunity will bring serious consequences. An illustration to emphasize the importance of acknowledging Him as the Messiah The magistrate represents God; the adversary represents Jesus He is not the adversary by His choice but by choice of those who reject Him Anyone who can judge what is right can see the importance of getting right with God. If a person is taken to court, there is enough evidence for a conviction The wise thing to do is to make peace quickly; a plea deal If he rejects the opportunity to settle out of court, the court itself will find him guilty and sentence him to prison. There will be no deliverance until the debt is paid Caution: First: The teaching is only designed as an illustration of the importance of getting one s life right with Jesus while there is time Second: Matters which complete the temporal illustration are not to be used in attempting to disprove the Biblical teaching of the eternal separation from God that awaits the lost. There are no second chances Rejection of Jesus brings people into a position where the justice of God must be dealt out to them rather than His grace The warning is the nation and individuals The nation paid the price of rejection in 70 AD New Testament teaching emphasizes the importance of individual acceptance of Christ and that eternal judgment awaits those who reject Him. The only plea deal with God is to come to Him on His terms. Be ye reconciled to God. The invitation is urgent; do it now, on your way there Salvation is an out of court settlement Once you arrive in the courtroom of the Great White Throne, only a sentence of guilty will be pronounced. No reprieve; no release Anyone will do whatever is necessary to stay out of prison, but how many people will apply that same concern and diligence to stay out of hell? -- Warren Wiersbe CRISIS MANAGEMENT DIVISION, DISCERNMENT, DELIVERY CRISIS MANAGEMENT Luke 12:49-59 THE CRISIS OF DIVISION 12:49-53 Being a disciple of Jesus is not the road to health and wealth Fire I have come to bring Jesus is forcing the issue to take sides Calvary brings men to a traumatic decision baptism immersion under divine judgment The fire of division will continue until the end of time Two of the effects of His ministry will be discord and disruption Jesus reveals what is in a person s heart A call for a decision is a call for division CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT 12:54-56 A warning to those who have thus far rejected Chronos: the word for the regular flow of time Kairos: the moment of critical decision or utmost importance They could see natural signs but fail to see spiritual signs The problem was not the evidence, but willful rejection CRISIS OF DELIVERANCE 12:57-59 Anyone who can judge what is right can see the importance of getting right with God. Rejection of Jesus brings people into a position where the justice of God must be dealt out to them rather than His grace The only plea deal with God is to come to Him on His terms. Salvation is an out of court settlement CRISIS MANAGEMENT Luke 12:49-59 THE CRISIS OF DIVISION 12:49-53 Being a disciple of Jesus is _________ the road to health and wealth ____________ I have come to bring Jesus is forcing the issue to take ____________ ____________ brings men to a traumatic decision baptism ____________ under divine judgment The fire of division will continue until the __________ of time Two of the effects of His ministry will be __________ and ____________ Jesus reveals what is in a person s ____________ A call for a decision is a call for ____________ CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT 12:54-56 A warning to those who have thus far ____________ Chronos: the word for the ____________ flow of time Kairos: the ____________ of critical decision or utmost importance They could see ____________ signs but fail to see ____________ signs The problem was not the evidence, but ____________ rejection CRISIS OF DELIVERANCE 12:57-59 ____________ who can judge what is right can see the importance of getting right with God. Rejection of Jesus brings people into a position where the ____________ of God must be dealt out to them rather than His ____________ The only plea deal with God is to come to Him on _________ terms. Salvation is an ______ ____ __________ settlement ============================================= === SERMON: message(122).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/24/2024 8:34:12 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message CRISIS MANAGEMENT Luke 12:49-59 One quick announcement before I begin. The rumor of my passing is just that. It was brought to my attention Friday evening that at least one person here thought that I had died. The reason given was that when entering the building that seemed to be a somber, quiet spirit. The kicker was they found out there would be no lunch. This person has since expressed gratitude that, indeed, I had not died. I am still deciding whether or not Brooklyn gets lunch. Back to Luke and a quick review of Chapter 12 We have tried to keep an eye on the audience to which Jesus is speaking We know the chapter started with some 10,000 trampling on each other to hear and see 12:1 disciples not the disciples first but the first thing they should know 12:4 friends those followers who would face the wrath of the Scribes and Pharisees 12:22 disciples including those who were still in decision mode 12:54 multitudes those who have rejected the evidence presented Most of the chapter is directed to those in the balance; the undecided To those thinking about becoming a committed disciple, these are some things they should know There will be attacks from the pretenders You may be brought in to give an account You will have to deal with the heart issue of treasures: on earth or in heaven Worry: no amount of worry will add even one minute to your life; you are more important than birds and God cares for them Servanthood will require Faithfulness. Others will be discovered as False, Forgetful, or Feeble. The last subject of the chapter I entitled CRISIS MANAGEMENT Vs. 49-53 was the Crisis of Division Any disciple of Jesus can expect to find even those closest to them to reject them or even become hostile to their life as a Christian It is one thing to have the hypocrites mock your belief, but quite another to hear the same accusations from those you love. Now the Invitation is given. Not your typical altar call we sometimes see today. Jesus is particularly speaking to those who have rejected, and they are identified here as the multitudes . We concluded our last message from this text stating that the closer you get to the cross the smaller the crowd becomes. The multitudes; will reject the message. Many will take the broad road to destruction. This is a crisis moment for them It may very well be a crisis moment for someone here. CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT 12:54-56 Here is a warning in their decision-making process A warning to those who have thus far rejected Could be considered more of an indictment on their unbelief There are two Greek words for time: chronos and Kairos Chronos: chronology, the word for the regular flow of time Kairos: the moment of critical decision or utmost importance There is the chronos for planning for a wedding; all well documented the first date; the first kiss; highlights of courtship; engagement And then the hour will come when someone will say, It s kairos The same can be said for surgery, school, vacation The previous verses were to prepare the listeners for the Kairos of His coming; the certain event in an uncertain time The importance of being prepared for the time Practical Illustration: the weather They learned from experience Red sky at night sailors delight; Red sky at morning sailors take warning From the west: Mediterranean Sea and rain From the south: warm weather 56 As the Divine Logos, Jesus always spoke truth and used words properly He didn t call people hypocrites without good reason What would be the purpose here? They were impersonating being spiritual; they were fake, counterfeit Jesus was undergoing close scrutiny by the scribes and Pharisees They were now seeking a reason to bring Him before the Roman authorities 11:53, 54 They hated Him without a cause, but more than willing to manufacture one Jesus called them hypocrites because they applied an honest evaluating of circumstances in determining the weather, but they would not exercise that same kind of honest evaluation in determining who Jesus was and what His purpose might be. They could see miracles, good deeds, listen to His teaching, and yet fail to see He was the promised Messiah They could see natural signs but fail to see spiritual signs They failed to forecast the worst storm; the coming Day of the Lord They had Moses, Isaiah, Jeremiah and other OT writers All pointing to Jesus; all more reliable signs than those determining the next day s weather The problem was not the evidence, but willful rejection His displeasure was not directed toward those who were confused and had serious doubts His anger was directed to those who were in a position to know the truth, but who deliberately rejected that truth because of insincere motives It wasn't that they didn't want a kingdom; sure they wanted a kingdom. It wasn't that they didn't want salvation; they wanted salvation. It wasn't that they didn't want forgiveness and eternal life. They want all that. It wasn't the cure they hated; it was the diagnosis they resented. He attacked their religious system. He stomped on their self-righteousness. MacArthur Denying Jesus started early in His ministry Luke 4; after a making headlines in Judea, Jesus comes to His hometown He had the audacity to question their relationship to God by asserting that God would accept Gentiles over them. The accepted talking point was Jesus operated under the power of Satan 11:15 It was not about evidence. The material presented was so overwhelming that if had been done in Sodom and Gomorrah they would have repented. The truth would force them to change their way of living, their selfish goals, the way they dealt with their fellowman Changes they refuse to make It is not about the existence of God; the personal nature of God; the power of God; the wisdom of God The problem is the nature of man as selfish, prideful, wicked The problem is man stills believes the original lie that he could be like God We need to be wise in interpreting the time in which we live Earl Radamacher s statement still rings true: This is not a sign of the times because it is not the time for signs. But there are still things we can identify Isaiah 3:4, 5; 11, 12 2 Chronicles 28:19 Romans 1:18-32 We can grieve over our present situation here in the US. We can wring our hands over leaders, decisions, contrary to God I am greatly concerned with the world you students will experience I love the country I where I live I know God has done something special the past 200 years or so in this country by providing religious freedom. Christians in history have not experienced what we have here for as long as we have But this blip in church history is coming to a close Opposition to God, Jesus, the church, Bible-believing Christians is growing When you have a political party that boos God out of the platform, that cannot even define what a woman is, you are in trouble. When the other party becomes weak on statements on abortion, transgenderism, and ignores a statement on marriage and the family, you are in trouble. When political campaigns are all about hurling accusations to the other side and not talking issues facing the country, you are in trouble. We can predict the weather. Let s use the Scriptures to help us define our time. CRISIS OF DIVISION, CRISIS OF DISCERNMENT CRISIS OF DELIVERANCE 12:57-59 They had enough revelation; enough to do what is right Their failure to discern while they had a golden opportunity will bring serious consequences. An illustration to emphasize the importance of acknowledging Jesus as the Messiah The magistrate represents God; the adversary represents Jesus He is not the adversary by His choice but by choice of those who reject Him Anyone who can judge what is right can see the importance of getting right with God. If a person is taken to court, there is enough evidence for a conviction The wise thing to do is to make peace quickly; a plea deal If he rejects the opportunity to settle out of court, the court itself will find him guilty and sentence him to prison. There will be no deliverance until the debt is paid Caution: First: The teaching is only designed as an illustration of the importance of getting one s life right with Jesus while there is time Second: Don t use an illustration to define biblical doctrine There are no second chances. The lost will be separated from God forever. Rejection of Jesus brings people into a position where the justice of God must be dealt out to them rather than His grace The warning is to the nation and individuals The nation paid the price of rejection in 70 AD New Testament teaching emphasizes the importance of individual acceptance of Christ and that eternal judgment awaits those who reject Him. The only plea deal with God is to come to Him on His terms. Be ye reconciled to God. The invitation is urgent; do it now, on your way there Salvation is an out of court settlement Once you arrive in the courtroom of the Great White Throne, only a sentence of guilty will be pronounced. No reprieve; no release Anyone will do whatever is necessary to stay out of prison, but how many people will apply that same concern and diligence to stay out of hell? -- Warren Wiersbe CRISIS MANAGEMENT DIVISION, DISCERNMENT, DELIVERY ============================================= === SERMON: message(123).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/30/2024 1:59:01 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: MESSAGE COMMUNION AFTER THE MESSAGE DON T JUMP TO CONCLUSIONS LUKE 13:1-9 INTRODUCTION It always fascinates me when a text of Scripture lines up perfectly with a particular Sunday. I would love to say that when we started our study in Luke 2 years ago we would begin Luke 13 on the first Sunday in September, 2024. I m good, but not that good. And as the first Sunday of a month, this day we will include setting aside time to remember Christ s death through the Lord s Supper. It is my intent, though not always accomplished well, to make our Communion time more than just a add-on; something that needs to be done. It is so much more than that. 1 Corinthians 11 speaks of the proper spiritual understanding of what we will be doing. The Apostle Paul corrected the abuse which surfaced in the Corinthian church. There was nothing special about the Lord s Supper as it was being practiced. Little consideration as to the seriousness of the celebration resulted in the illness and even loss of life of some in the church. Our text this morning should help us in preparation. God calls for a time of self-evaluation; a time for proper introspection; a time to carefully consider one s participation so that they might partake in a worthy manner; that is, properly discerning the body and blood of the Lord Jesus. Luke 13:1-9 will help us. We tend to follow into the same trap as the Israelites. That we tend to quickly make summary judgments on events. We might use phrases like weren t they lucky or they had it coming or serves them right . We hear words like karma , whether good or bad. There is some force out there directing the events of a person s life. Have you ever heard someone say, God will get you for that! THE TEMPLE THE TOWER THE TRUTH THE TEMPERANCE THE TEMPLE 13:1 Present at the kaipos; moment in time This continues the invitation extended the end of chapter 12 The subject is judgment; settling your account with God before it is too late No other record mentions this event; not even Josephus though he writes of similar occurrences Pilate was notoriously cruel His motivation in murdering these Galileans is not known The murders took place while they were worshipping God Could well have been at a Passover when males were to go to Jerusalem The scene would be like a slaughter house with the number of animals slain Graphic picture of the blood mingling Take note that these Galileans were doing their religious duty There was no debauchery; nothing to suggest any wickedness on their part This would have been the major news in Jerusalem and naturally made its way to the ears of Jesus One would naturally expect Jesus to condemn the actions of Pilate He, however, turned into a challenge to the listeners; 12:54, multitudes Many people believe that sudden catastrophes occur because God is angry with the victims There have been times when it did happen Noah s Flood Destruction of Sodom Annihilation of the family of Ahab Acts 12:21-23 death of Herod There are built-in judgments for sinful behavior: Alcohol: cirrhosis of the liver Immorality: sexually transmitted diseases Murder: capital punishment The common Jewish mindset was that even individual suffering was because of some issue in their life Job 4:7; 8:20 There must be some sin in your life. You brought this on yourself John 9:2 re: blind man who sinned? He or his parents? We often do the same thing: must have done something wrong what did I do to deserve this? Or when something good happens: from Sound of Music nothing comes from nothing I must have done something good Jesus exposed their thought process - suppose The question was not if they were sinners, that is assumed, but greater sinners Answer: They were no worse than other Galileans Surprise: that went against their way of thinking Jesus had just reprimanded them for not accepting Him as the Messiah In order to take pressure off themselves they pointed to the Galileans as being especially deserving of condemnation No one thought well of them anyway THE TOWER 13:4 Jesus brings up another tragedy that happened We know nothing about what the tower was about: scaffolding? We do know: no worship involved; no wicked Galileans involved Just 18 people in the wrong place at the wrong time sinners idea of owing a debt Owing a debt you will never be able to repay: 12:59 Two tragedies: one due to human causes; one due to natural causes Neither case implied that the persons involved were worse than others who lived their lives free of such violence and died of old age in a comfortable bed Don t jump to conclusions; don t automatically attribute a tragedy to the vengeance of God Four Assumptions to Discard 1) Suffering is proportional to sinfulness. 2) Tragedy is a sure sign of God s judgment. 3) Bad things happen only to bad people. 4) We have the right to make such judgments. When you read of a tragedy, resist the urge to assign guilt It is not the time to assign blame THE TRUTH 13:3, 5 Jesus directs the listeners to not look out but look in Not the time to assign blame but for self-examination It is not time to find fault with others who seem to have a difficult time It is not time to compare them with you to elevate your spiritual condition I had my devotions today OR I am still alive so it s all good. Genuine conversion is necessary Repent: worded so it is an active personal choice; a lifestyle When was the last time you repented? God will not force you to repent; He does provide us with the gift of repentance Acts 5:31; 11:18 God enables us to make the choice to repent Repent: change of mind; a reorientation of perspective I change my mind about God He is God, not me; His law is perfect, not mine. He is holy and I need His help I change my mind about sin There is an intellectual apprehension of the character of sin Sin makes me guilty It is no longer the consequences of sin I dread, but sin itself I have read that in Russia today they do not refer to themselves as believers but as repenters. Repent: change of direction I have a duty to turn from sin Anyone who does not make that change of direction will be condemned No matter whether they are the best moral person or the worst reprobate Whether they are a worshipper or just walking around the pool The central issue is that repentance is an absolute must Each person must turn from sin to God I come to realize sin declares me guilty before a holy God and only from turning from that sin to God will I find a remedy. the tragedies and calamites of life are simple reminders of the uncertainty of our lives; life is fragile; the unexpected happens; towers still fall And the word perish is not just dying because you are going to die Perish is separation from God; perish is hell; perish is lake of fire THE TEMPERANCE 13:6-9 The parable is designed to Illustrate the need for repentance Two basic positions of interpretation Most common: the vineyard represents the world and the fig tree represents the Jewish nation God is the owner of the vineyard; He placed the fig tree The nation was not bearing the fruits of repentance Therefore, God determined to remove the unfruitful nation Lenski: the vineyard is the Jewish nation; the fig tree is Jerusalem Christ is the vinedresser Jerusalem has a special place with the nation of Israel The political capital; the spiritual hub Whether the three years refer to the time of the preaching of John and Jesus is intentional or coincidental is not the issue The point is that the people of Jerusalem had had numerous opportunities to hear, believe, and obey the truth Isaiah prophesied of Jerusalem that it was a well-cultivated vineyard but produced only wild grapes 5:1-7 That prophecy was fulfilled in 586 B.C Jesus seems to indicate that the prophecy is about to repeat itself with the difference in the analogy being the fig tree within the vineyard instead of the vineyard by itself. Jesus is teaching that God is patient He waits for people to repent If God had not been patient, man would not have left the Garden of Eden alive God showed patience in waiting 120 years through the preaching of Noah He was patient with Lot in Sodom He was patient with Israel when Moses interceded after the making of the golden calf However, His longsuffering does come to an end It was not just the fact that the fig tree bore no fruit; it also stood in the way of something else that would bear fruit Israel had been unfaithful much of the time Now once more manifesting that trait by rejecting Jesus as the Messiah God knew there were people in the world who would receive His Son Why let Jerusalem s snobbery stand in the way of Gentiles who would bear the fruit of repentance? If the message of Christ s death did not bring the necessary repentance, then nothing would produce the expected fruit. CONCLUSION Have you repented? Has your life taken on a new trajectory? It is not do you know God, but does God know you? Have you reconciled? Just because you are sitting here this morning does not mean all is right with you and God. It simply means that God continues to sustain you by His grace and your purpose on this earth has not come to a conclusion. You may be breathing because God is patient. He is waiting for you to yield to Him, but the longsuffering of God will come to an end. The Biblical principle is still true: you reap what you sow Reap more than you sow; reap in a different season The Bible does say, be sure your sin will find you out (Num. 32:23) And, secondly, this is the message we have to share We need not shy away from reminding people they will die and they have no control over when. 12:25 cannot add one minute to your life Corporate worship is designed to remind you that the most valuable thing in your life is something you could have never earned or deserved. It was and is a gift of diving grace. P. Tripp DON T JUMP TO CONCLUSIONS LUKE 13:1-9 THE TEMPLE 13:1 His motivation in murdering these Galileans is not known Galileans were doing their religious duty The common Jewish mindset was that even individual suffering was because of some issue in their life Jesus exposed their thought process THE TOWER 13:4 18 people in the wrong place at the wrong time Four Assumptions to Discard 1) Suffering is proportional to sinfulness. 2) Tragedy is a sure sign of God s judgment. 3) Bad things happen only to bad people. 4) We have the right to make such judgments. THE TRUTH 13:3, 5 Jesus directs the listeners to not look out but look in Repent: change of mind It is no longer the consequences of sin I dread, but sin itself Repent: change of direction Each person must turn from sin to God Perish is separation from God THE TEMPERANCE 13:6-9 Jesus is teaching that God is patient His longsuffering does come to an end It was not just the fact that the fig tree bore no fruit; it also stood in the way of something else that would bear fruit DON T JUMP TO CONCLUSIONS LUKE 13:1-9 THE TEMPLE 13:1 His motivation in murdering these Galileans is not known Galileans were doing their religious duty The common Jewish mindset was that even individual suffering was because of some issue in their life Jesus exposed their thought process THE TOWER 13:4 18 people in the wrong place at the wrong time Four Assumptions to Discard 1) Suffering is proportional to sinfulness. 2) Tragedy is a sure sign of God s judgment. 3) Bad things happen only to bad people. 4) We have the right to make such judgments. THE TRUTH 13:3, 5 Jesus directs the listeners to not look out but look in Repent: change of mind It is no longer the consequences of sin I dread, but sin itself Repent: change of direction Each person must turn from sin to God Perish is separation from God THE TEMPERANCE 13:6-9 Jesus is teaching that God is patient His longsuffering does come to an end It was not just the fact that the fig tree bore no fruit; it also stood in the way of something else that would bear fruit ============================================= === SERMON: message(124).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/6/2024 8:26:46 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message CREATING CONFLICT LUKE 13:10-21 INTRODUCTION Luke is now substantiating the reason why the tree must be cut down Will the fig tree bear fruit? It is amazing Jesus was even invited to speak by the head rabbi This is His last recorded appearance in a synagogue As Jesus attacked the religious system as practiced in Israel, one area of particular concern was the Sabbath. An early message in Nazareth (Luke 4:18, 19) is at the center of His ministry here Hard hearts will always resist the message of truth Mattoon - In this passage we find a wide variety of priorities and attitudes in people that are found in our own lives today. A sick woman, a sensitive Savior, the synagogue's ruler, and a smiling crowd all reveal their priorities by their attitudes, actions, and articulation as a crooked woman was straightened out by Jesus. The Sabbath was a special day for Israel It was a sign between the Lord and the nation of Israel the sign of the Old Covenant (the Law) that they might know He is the Lord Who sanctifies them, sets them apart. Israel was to observe the Sabbath because it was holy, set apart, for them. The one who profaned the Sabbath, did not treat it as holy, was put to death. Israel was to keep it throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant. Like many other things God has given to us, leave it up to man to mess it up. Likewise, the religious leaders could not leave well-enough alone and added countless burdensome rules and regulations so the NT observance of the Sabbath bared little resemblance to the OT Law. Today s text is a prime example. Jesus kept the Sabbath in the highest sense of the term. He observed every jot and tittle of the Mosaic Law in the freedom of the spirit. Several conflicts center around Jesus healing on the Sabbath: the healing of a man with a withered hand (Mt. 12:9-14 ; Mk. 3:1-6 ; Lk. 6:6-11 ), the disabled woman (Lk. 13:10-17 ), a man crippled for thirty-eight years (Jn. 5:1-9 , 16-17 ; 7:22 ), and the man born blind (Jn. 9 ). Jesus insisted that it was always lawful to do good on the Sabbath (Mt. 12:12 ). According to Jesus the Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath (Mk. 2:27-28 ). That is, in the creation story, God did not create a Sabbath and then create a human being to keep it; rather, he first created a human being, then knowing human beings would need rest, created the Sabbath for their benefit. The Lord wants man to enjoy the day of rest, not be burdened by it. The idea is not that of relaxation or refreshment, but cessation from activities of the work week. There are 7 Sabbath miracles: 13:10-16 Mark 1:21-28 casting out a demon Mark 1:29-31 healing Peter s mother-in-law John 5:1-18 healing a lame man Mark 3:1-6 healing of deformed hand Luke 14:1-6 healing of man with swollen arms and legs John 9:1-16 healing of man born blind THE CROOKED WOMAN 13:10, 11 Only Luke records this miracle and behold this is like a yellow highlighter; slow down and pay attention Of all the people present, this is the one deserving of special attention The most desolate person there Woman with a spinal condition causing her to stoop while walking Possibly a fusion of the spine She walked face down. Some writers describe it as looking for a grave and would have welcomed any provided. Any view of the platform would have come from looking sideways Jesus attributed her condition to Satan (16) Does not say she was possessed as no mention is made of demon departure She was definitely demon oppressed There is no evidence of any spiritual effects like ranting or raving or casting one s self down Further, there is no record of Jesus ever touching one who is demon possessed And knowing the Jews assumption of sin associated with illness and you can easily guess the conversation of this woman We must not conclude that all physical ailments are the direct result of the work of Satan The text from last week reminded us that we are not to think that spiritual issues cause all physical problems, but we are also foolish to think spiritual issues can never cause physical problems." Question: what excuses have you used for missing church? Would you be attending after 18 years of physical torment? Would you be attending knowing the conversation taking place around you? THE COMPASSIONATE CAREGIVER 13:12, 13 Some people have an eye to see what is wrong; others see what is right Jesus has an eye to see who is in need The woman made no direct attempt to gain Jesus favor It is unknown is she was a regular attender or came on this special occasion Jesus took the initiative in dealing with her condition She was probably in the back on the women s side His words are unique since what happens here is deliverance and a healing loosed means to set free ; used in Matthew 18:27 of a servant in great debt to his master 13 This passive verb indicates she was straightened up A power outside of herself The miracle was instantaneous, complete, and permanent The woman certainly recognized it was God s doing She praised Him for His goodness and mercy Not told what she said. Maybe nothing more than Hallelujah!! (Acts 3:8 walking, leaping, and praising God ) God is in the business of straightening out people s lives Some might question why Jesus called attention to her in front of everyone Proverbs: before honor comes humility Humility is not going to hurt you, but your pride will We need to be reminded that Coming to Christ may not bring an end to all your problems To be sure, you can be set free, but difficulties may continue for some time There are people who find immediate release from addictions, sin habits Others may struggle for a while. Does not mean salvation is not real There are other lessons to be learned. Does not mean you stop coming to church. You keep believing. You keep worshipping THE CALLOUSED CLERIC 13:14-17 The legalistic ruler of the synagogue was angry Adam Clarke says it seems as if the demon left the woman s body and entered the ruler Jesus main battles were not with raw pagans The main conflicts of Jesus were with the religious crowd He didn t even have the decency to speak to Jesus directly. He addressed his displeasure to the crowd to try to turn against Jesus The ruler paid more attention to what he considered wrong He had a critical mind with the saddest part of the whole thing being the ruler undoubtedly felt he was right in his criticism He was cold-blooded and heartless and spiritual blind Hear from Kent Hughes: "What a slab of ecclesiastical granite! He had no heart to pity the poor bent woman s plight, no eye for the beauty of Christ s compassion, no soul to rejoice with the woman s deliverance, no ear for the music of her praise. He was a chickenhearted religious snob. What we do learn is that miracles do not produce faith Saving faith is produced by the Holy Spirit 15 Luke accentuates Jesus authority by calling Him Lord Jesus did not respond to the man by speaking to the people He addressed the man face-to-face as an insincere individual His motives as well as his position were inconsistent with the spirit of the Law he claimed to uphold. The Law of Sabbath keeping was not contrary to the exercise of love Ten Commandments: Love God; Love neighbor The ruler s hypocrisy was exposed in that he did the same thing he accused Jesus of doing. Jesus did not tell him it was wrong to water his beasts on the Sabbath, because it was not wrong However, if it is right to do good for an ox or an ass on the Sabbath, it is also right to do good for a human being on the Sabbath The ruler had more concern for an animal than this lady It should be noted that we live in a society where priorities are out of balance. It is amazing how people today place more value on whales, birds, minnows, or wolves over the lives of unborn babies that are slaughtered in abortion clinics. 16 the Rebuttal It showed the ruler s complete disregard for the Jewish woman Jesus calls her a daughter of Abraham: a covenant connection many suggesting her salvation is real (Zaccheus; 19:9) interesting question: Who told Jesus she had been bound for 18 years? ought must The argument is so clear that the answer is YES 17 The evidence of the working of God was so overwhelming in this case that any honest observer would have praised Jesus rather than condemn Him The opponents being ashamed was good insofar as it went Humiliated; to shame down, disgraced; imperfect tense repeatedly ashamed all those who were hypocrites all those who witnessed a miracle; no correlation to salvation Repentance would have been a much more profitable response. CONFIDENT KINGDOM 18-21 Jesus expressed His confidence in the growth of the Kingdom by setting forth two parables Part of the lesson from the woman s healing Maybe directed to the disciples this is how the kingdom will grow one person at a time Little by little the Word of God will permeate the lives of people bringing them into the kingdom It will start small, but, oh, what a kingdom it will become CONCLUSION Here is a perfect example of a Personal Testimony She was: Crooked: Bowed together bent over Satan s business to bow the souls of men to the earth Sin bends our will and thoughts to the dominion of darkness Helpless: could in no wise lift herself up Utterly impossible for her to change her condition Her deformity was fixed; a crooked tree for 18 years She would have straightened herself out if she could Many people are morally bowed; drink, drugs, lust They have tried to lift themselves up but soon return to a position of deformity Invited: Jesus called her to Him There were many people in attendance. He singled her out Jesus message in Nazareth: 4:18 proclaim release to the captives This was His mission: to seek out those who are helpless Touched: He laid His hands on her Jesus comes into personal contact with every soul whom He saves He is ready to lay healing hands on all who respond to His invitation It is all of grace on His part His hands are the hands of unfailing, steadfast love and power Delivered: loosed from your infirmity (12) made straight (13) loosed from this bond (16) There was freedom from the binding power of Satan She was made straight for future life and walk Thankful: immediately she glorified God Psalm 40:2, 3 He brought me up also out of a horrible pit, out of the miry clay; And he set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings. And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: Many shall see it, and fear, And shall trust in the LORD Did you come here this morning, stooped over in your soul? Do you feel like your soul is bound? There is no reason to walk out as you came in You can leave here as that woman left the synagogue praising CREATING CONFLICT LUKE 13:10-21 THE CROOKED WOMAN 13:10, 11 Behold - a yellow highlighter Possibly a fusion of the spine Jesus attributed her condition to Satan We must not conclude that all physical ailments are the direct result of the work of Satan THE COMPASSIONATE CAREGIVER 13:12, 13 Jesus has an eye to see who is in need The miracle was instantaneous, complete, and permanent Humility is not going to hurt you, but your pride will Coming to Christ may not bring an end to all your problems THE CALLOUSED CLERIC 13:14-17 The main conflicts of Jesus were with the religious crowd The ruler paid more attention to what he considered wrong What we do learn is that miracles do not produce faith The Law of Sabbath keeping was not contrary to the exercise of love The ruler had more concern for an animal than this lady Jesus calls her a daughter of Abraham: a covenant connection Repentance would have been a much more profitable response. CONFIDENT KINGDOM 18-21 Little by little the Word of God will permeate the lives of people bringing them into the kingdom ============================================= === SERMON: message(125).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/13/2024 9:20:07 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE ROAD TO HELL Luke 13:22-30 INTRODUCTION Some commentaries place Luke 13:22-17:10 beyond the Jordan River in Perea which fits right after John 10 No specific location is given; only told Jesus was passing through And teaching no mention of synagogues but on the move Redeeming the precious opportunities to teach Jesus is not just teaching the multitudes, but time with the disciples passing on the truth about the Word of God, the God of the Word and the faith of the saints, with the goal of influencing their understanding Stimulating obedience to the truth The teacher teaches and the disciple hears and processes what is heard so that this truth affects his or her innermost being. Ultimately the purpose of didasko is to shape the will of the one taught. Finally, teaching of sound doctrine is vital to the growth and stabilization of one's faith Romans 10:17 We are passing through . We are aliens and strangers 1 Pet. 1:1; 2:11 We have a responsibility to be communicators of the good news We are His workmanship; created to good works Eph. 2 His direction of purpose will not change 9:51; 17:11; 18:31; 19:28, 41 QUESTION PRESENTED 13:22, 23 Jesus continues what He began in 9:51 v. 23 has the question which we will explore Are there few that be saved? why the question? Idle curiosity or great concern for people? Were the crowds noticeable smaller? Jesus has laid out some rather heavy things to consider 9:23-26; 12:4, 9, 11, 31, 34, 51 Maybe he heard Jesus say it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God. Matt. 19:24 Even the disciples choked on that one, Then who can be saved? Kent Hughes calls is a smug, self-complacent question as All Jews, except the very worst, assumed they would be saved QUESTION PROCESSED 13:24-30 Jesus did not answer the question Instead, He challenged the inquirer to confront his own personal spiritual condition Spurgeon: What business is that of ours? Our business is far more practical, to be saved ourselves, and to endeavor to be the means of saving others. There is far more value in asking the question, am I saved? Jesus did not just direct His response to the inquirer, but to all People have asked, what about those in Africa? Jesus answer, you should worry more about the person in your shoes. Some people are more interested in arguing points of doctrine than perform an inward inspection. Predestination, eschatology, spiritual gifts The stuff freshman Bible majors argue in the dorm. Pontificating off their limited knowledge There is a time for such studies. But get the core down first. The real question this morning, are you saved? Brian Bell: Salvation is not a theory to discuss. It is a miracle to experience. In our soft age we are more concerned with statistics than about spiritual power. It is not how many will be saved but am I one of them? Not will the saved be few? but will the saved be you? Reasons why people will miss the gate. The ROAD TO HELL is FOR THE LAZY 13:24 strive - agonize; put forth special effort to reach a goal Struggle, wrestle, exert ourselves, fight Hebrews 12:1 The way of the Kingdom is narrow Jesus is the only door; no one comes too God except through Jesus The soft-sell gospel says you only need to pray a prayer Accept Jesus We may react because strive smacks of self-effort; all up to me It is a narrow door, only entered by grace through faith in Christ alone strive present imperative; a life-long agonizing effort only ending when we enter glory Striving does not save us, but it proves we are saved Once you are in the narrow gate, don t take your foot off the gas pedal The battle is against the world, the flesh and the devil Spiritual forces in high places Jesus is not teaching a works-based salvation, but salvation has a cost deny yourself, take up your cross, and follow Why would anyone seeking to enter the Kingdom be ruled out by God? Reasons of Missing Out Some will want to enter on their own terms Religious leaders get God to conform to their idea of religion Some will try to enter by their good works Fail to know God describes good words as filthy rags Some will miss out because they have a false view of the nature of God God is love, love, love. His love is unconditional Why God s love is not unconditional Coined by German Erich Fromm in 1934 he associated it with a mother s love in contrast to a father s love Fromm was a socialist; not a Christian It was mainstreamed by Carl Rogers; not a Christian God never uses it to describe His love Everlasting Jeremiah 31:3 Unchanging Malachi 3:6 Great Ephesians 2:4 Deep Ephesians 3:17-19 Psalm 51:1 lovingkindness; unfailing, steadfast Definition: love without any moral judgment Popularized by the Seeker Sensitive Movement Result: My behavior, dress, sin does not matter because God loves me. Why agonize over something when a God of love will accept me anyway Puritan Thomas Watson: Strive as in an agony strive as for a matter of life and death. Though we must be men of peace yet, in matters of religion, we must be men of strife. It is a holy strife a blessed contention. Indeed, the Apostle said, "Let nothing be done through strife"; but, though strife does not do well among Christians yet it does well in a Christian. He must strive with his own heart or he will never get to heaven. A life-long agonizing effort The Road to Hell is FOR THE LATE 13:25 When people were invited to the home of in important host and the appointment was for a specific hour, they had better arrive on time A Near Eastern host had the right to bolt his gate at the given hour No one was permitted to enter after that The road to hell is paved with good intentions Matthew 25; the parable of the virgins Five foolish virgins were unprepared and went to buy oil 25:10 while they were gone, the gate was shut Their knock resulted in the reply, I don t know you The shut door connotes finality There was a time and opportunity when the door was open Once shut, no one can open The door named mercy will not be open forever Remember Noah s ark: once the door was shut all else excluded There are no second chances Late Excuses 13:26 Those who are lost express some of the reasons they feel they should be saved Some of the people listening to Jesus had eaten meals with Him They had followed Him in the streets to hear Him teach They felt their association with Him was assurance of friendship and therefore He would grant them salvation. Lord, Lord 6:46 the great disconnect Why do you call me Lord and not do what I say? Matt. 7:21-23 Many in churches today have no concept of a God who will shut the door The Bible says, today is the day of salvation George Barna in his research shows only 10% of American adults meet the theological criteria for what makes somebody an evangelical. That is about 1 out of every 4 presently sitting in churches this morning (25 million out of 100 million) From the American Worldview Inventory 2024: For nearly 50 years, Evangelicals have worked hard to look like the culture under the auspices of bringing unbelievers into the church to preach the gospel. In the process, they have succeeded in being shaped by the culture and losing their saltiness. What Barna calls the spiritual collapse of Christianity in our nation Luke 14:34, 35 Response to the Late-Comers 13:27 depart remove yourself, withdraw workers of iniquity They have never turned from their wicked ways Jesus knows the heart and motivation of each life They will remain outside where they will experience anguish The Road to Hell is FOR THE LAZY, FOR THE LATE FOR THE LOFTY 13:28-30 weeping and gnashing of teeth Used 6x in Matthew 8:12; 13:42, 50; 22:13; 24:51; 25:30 Specific terms: there is no weeping like this weeping The complete absence of happiness and the absolute presence of despair This despair, along with the rage of being consigned forever in a place of torment, produce a literal and continual gnashing of the teeth. Don t buy the lie that the weeping and gnashing is figurative for remorse and trauma There will be that. This is a horrible and frightening picture v. 28 not if you see but when you see It happens outside ; a place of utter darkness The torment is made worse by the ability to see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and the prophets enjoying the bliss of paradise. Here will be a painful vision for deceived professors These words of Jesus were a hard blow to the pride of the Jews They still felt the Messiah was for their glory alone Others will come from the Gentile world Acts 10, 11 What the Jews rejected as a nation has been given to individuals out of every nation, tongue, and people The number of the saved will be innumerable Rev. 7:9 The first and the last saying here is a pithy statement last could mean those who are far from the Kingdom first could mean those who are near the kingdom CONCLUSION There are several points made throughout this passage. * The door is narrow and not everyone enters (Lk 13:24 ). * The door is only open for a short time and then it will be shut (Lk 13:25 ). * The door will only be open to those who know Jesus and who are known by Him (Lk 13:26 ). * There is time for you to know Him today, but tomorrow may be too late. * The door will be opened to many rather than just a few (Lk 13:29 30 ). This was the original question. Now it is finally answered. But it is not answered in the way that the questioner anticipated. Agonizing (True Christianity) will cost a saved man His self-righteousness His sins His love of ease The favor of the world THE ROAD TO HELL Luke 13:22-30 QUESTION PRESENTED 13:22, 23 Idle curiosity or great concern for people? All Jews, except the very worst, assumed they would be saved QUESTION PROCESSED 13:24-30 He challenged the inquirer to confront his own personal spiritual condition Not will the saved be few? but will the saved be you? THE ROAD TO HELL is FOR THE LAZY 13:24 strive - agonize; a life-long agonizing effort only ending when we enter glory Striving does not save us, but it proves we are saved The battle is against the world, the flesh and the devil Reasons of Missing Out Some will want to enter on their own terms Some will try to enter by their good works Some will miss out because they have a false view of the nature of God FOR THE LATE 13:25 A Near Eastern host had the right to bolt his gate at the given hour The shut door connotes finality There are no second chances Late Excuses 13:26 They felt their association with Him was assurance of friendship Many in churches today have no concept of a God who will shut the door Response to the Late-Comers 13:27 depart remove yourself, withdraw FOR THE LOFTY 13:28-30 This is a horrible and frightening picture Here will be a painful vision for deceived professors They still felt the Messiah was for their glory alone The number of the saved will be innumerable Rev. 7:9 ============================================= === SERMON: message(126).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/28/2024 11:02:57 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message PUZZLING PLEA IN PERAEA LUKE 13:31-35 INTRODUCTION What do you want? DECEPTION Same day (TR) hour (WH) Same timing as the warning of entering at the narrow gate Warning that many Jews will be left outside Such a statement had them already gnashing their teeth That followed the woes in chapter 11 (Lk 11:42 , 43 , 44 , 46 , 47 , 52 ) 11:53 growing hostilities Highly unlikely the Pharisees arrived with the intention to help Jesus It is possible they were envoys of Herod Jesus was in Herod s jurisdiction; Galilee and Peraea This is the son of Herod the Great; killer of babies Herod Antipas killer of John the Baptist a tetrarch governor of Galilee and Pereae; not a king Herod lacked the courage to kill Jesus because of His popularity He had already killed John the Baptist and Jesus was even more popular Possible: Herod wanted Jesus out of his territory to avoid a problem with Roman officials due to the large crowds following Jesus and potential disturbances The Pharisees wanted Jesus to get on into Jerusalem where they could more easily lay hands on Him. Bottom Line: not a warning based on concern DEVOTION 13:32, 33 Jesus called Herod a fox; the Only time a derogatory term for a person was used by Jesus The Mosaic Law forbid name-calling a ruler; Ex. 22:28; Ecc. 10:20; Acts 23:5 There were exceptions: Isa. 1:23; Eze. 22:27; Hosea 7:3-7 Fox carries the idea of contempt; he is a varment Could be his sly nature in getting the Pharisees to do his bidding Fox also can mean an insignificant or worthless person and carries the idea of destructiveness Jesus was calling him both a crooked man and a small man Jesus response: tell him that Jesus would continue His ministry of spiritual and physical cleansing for a short time 3 days mentioned probably not to be taken literally Possible interpretation is a "triple time reference" as a Hebrew "idiom for a short indefinite time followed by an imminent and certain event Jesus is simply saying He will finish what He set out to do until finished No threats from religious or government leaders will alter the plan He will continue unhindered; His miraculous power will remain on display Whether over the natural world or the spiritual world Time will march according to God s timetable, not Herod s perfected completed; finished Same word in John 19:30 it is finished Jesus is saying I will reach my goal ; a glorious way of describing death His perfecting had to do with the completion of His earthly ministry Statement beginning with behold highlight it; mark it down The statement is true for us; you are immortal until your work is done Jesus had His own itinerary I must speaks of obligation; a divine necessity; not optional Any movement will be heavenly directed and not motivation to escape Jesus had no fear of anything Herod might try to do He knew it would not be Herod who would put Him to death He also knew it would occur in Jerusalem God had a predetermined plan; Acts 2:23 Jesus said He was going to walk the plan; journey; 9:51 go A plan set in place before the world began 2 Timothy 1:9; Titus 1:2; 1 Peter 1:20 DEJECTION 13:34, 35 (PASSIONATE PATHOS PETITION PRAYER 13:34) Jesus expressed His grief over the city of Jerusalem O Matt. 23:37-39; Luke 19:41- The sorrow was deep each time attention was given to the future The lament of a broken heart Repetition of Jerusalem indicates not just the city but the nation Jerusalem has a habit of rejecting the attempts of God to lead His people They kill and stone; present tense, still taking place those whom God had sent with His Word Matthew 21:33-41 a parable showing such treatment Stoning was most often the form of punishment in the OT Often carried out for blasphemy and apostasy ((Lev 24:14 , 16 , 23 ; Lev 20:2 ; Deut 13:11 ), Jesus is saying the nation treated God s prophets as blasphemers 2 Chronincles 24:20-22; Neh. 9:26; Acts 7 Jesus knew the troubles that would soon come upon Jerusalem The rending of the veil would be followed by the rending of the temple He knew they would not respond to Him any more than the prophets would not is emphatic; you would have none of it As opposed to the nature of God s heart to nurture, protect, care for What a contrast, I would but you absolutely would not Shows how they stubbornly resisted the pleading of God Samuel likened the stubbornness of Saul to idolatry; 1 Sam. 15:23 Stubbornness is a sin that sets self up as God People would rather perish than come under the protection of God s almighty wings Have you ever been rejected, spurned by another? It hurts, its painful That is how God feels; He who loved Israel dearly is rejected Isa 53:3 He was despised and rejected of men John 1:11 He came unto His own and His own received Him not The irony of it all is that they had warned Jesus that He was in trouble when in reality they were the ones who needed protection. 35 Behold highlight it; rejection will bring condemnation your house personal home; God s house; the nation, the city Jeremiah 22:5 But if you will not obey these words, I swear by Myself, declares the LORD, that this house will become a desolation. Jesus would visit the temple again for the purpose of cleansing it and teaching there. But once He left it for the last time before His crucifixion it would truly be desolate The purpose of the temple will have been forever fulfilled The futile worship that would go on there between 30 and 70 A.D. would go unrecognized by God The new covenant would find God dwelling through His Spirit in the hearts of believers since Christians are not the temple of the God 1 Corinthians 3:16; 6:19 Ye shall not see me judgment time beginning at 70 AD until happening up to a particular point in time, and then stopping Luke 22:15-18 another set of untils This until is the return of Jesus at His Second Coming to inaugurate a new age, the Messianic Age 1 Cor. 11:26 proclaim the Lord s death until He comes A time when you will say, 'BLESSED IS HE WHO COMES IN THE NAME OF THE LORD! Quoting Ps. 118:26 which was sung during the Passover Context vs. 21-29 The verse was quoted by the people at the Triumphal Entry when the leaders rebuked the people (Luke 19:37-39), Jesus then gave the prophecy, Matt. 23:38, 39 Luke 13:35 is Jesus looking beyond the Triumphal Entry to His second coming Hermeneutics says there will be a literal fulfillment of this promise Zechariah 12:10 speaks of a national repentance and salvation for the Jews they will see the one Whom they have piereced Rev. 1:7 God is not through with Israel Floyd Hamilton, an advocate of interpreting the prophecies regarding Israel non-literally acknowledges, Now we must frankly admit that a literal interpretation of the Old Testament prophecies gives us just such a picture of an earthly reign of the Messiah as the premillennialist pictures (The Basis of Millennial Faith [Grand Rapids: Eerdmans, 1942], 38). Loraine Boettner, who also advocated the non-literal view, agrees with Hamilton s assessment: It is generally agreed that if the prophecies are taken literally, they do foretell a restoration of the nation of Israel in the land of Palestine with the Jews having a prominent place in that kingdom and ruling over the other nations ( A Postmillennial Response [to Dispensational Premillennialism], in Robert G. Clouse, ed., The Meaning of the Millennium: Four Views [Downers Grove, Ill.: InterVarsity, 1977], 95) MacArthur: To adopt a hermeneutical method for interpreting prophecy different from that used to interpret the rest of Scripture is entirely arbitrary. Those who advocate such a method do so because the literal interpretation of the prophecies regarding Israel s future is inconsistent with their theological preferences. Theology, however, should be based on exegesis, not determine it. PUZZLING PLEA IN PERAEA LUKE 13:31-35 DECEPTION Highly unlikely the Pharisees arrived with the intention to help Jesus Herod Antipas governor of Galilee and Pereae DEVOTION 13:32, 33 Only time a derogatory term for a person was used by Jesus Jesus is simply saying He will finish what He set out to do until finished Time will march according to God s timetable His perfecting had to do with the completion of His earthly ministry I must obligation; not optional Jesus said He was going to walk the plan 2 Timothy 1:9; Titus 1:2; 1 Peter 1:20 DEJECTION 13:34, 35 The lament of a broken heart Jesus is saying the nation treated God s prophets as blasphemers 2 Chronicles. 24:20-22; Neh. 9:26; Acts 7 I would but you absolutely would not 1 Sam. 15:23 Stubbornness is a sin that sets self up as God People would rather perish than come under the protection of God s almighty wings John 1:11 The purpose of the temple will have been forever fulfilled 1 Corinthians 3:16; 6:19 This until is the return of Jesus at His Second Coming Quoting Ps. 118:26 which was sung during the Passover Hermeneutics says there will be a literal fulfillment of this promise God is not through with Israel ============================================= === SERMON: message(127).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/5/2024 9:48:14 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DEALING WITH THE LOFTY Luke 14:1-12 INTRODUCTION We have just read the words Jesus delivered to those who considered their place in the kingdom secure because they claim their position as a child of Abraham. 13:27-30 Jesus followed that describing their absolute unwillingness to come under His protective nurturing cover. 13:34, 35 He continues addressing the pride that exists within the spiritual leadership of Israel. Chapter 14 begins with another healing miracle again taking place on the Sabbath 14:1-6 Luke 14 has been labeled by Lowell Johnson as Table Talks of Our Lord Hypocrisy: 14:1-6 Humility 14:4-11 Hospitality 14:12-14 Hesitancy 14:15-24 SABBATH SETUP 14:1, 2 Now it happened it came to pass Nothing just happens in God s creation This is the last record of Sabbath activity for Jesus Jesus accepted the invitation of one who was likely a prominent Pharisee in a local synagogue in Perea Perfidious: deliberately deceitful, treacherous Not His first time in such an environment: 7:36; 11:37 Luke records 5 of the 7 Sabbath healings Luke 4:31-37; 4:38, 39; John 5:1-18; Luke 6:6-11; 13:10-17; Jn. 9:1-6 The Sabbath meal was prepared the day before; if a hot meal was kept warm but making sure no further cooking took place Those are the regulations the Pharisees considered religion It was a popular meal in the life of a Jewish family.
