Today our study leads us to 1 Timothy 6 where we find the first of phrases to study Our verses are 6:13-16 As we read them you will soon discover they do not speak of God the Son but of God the Father 6:13-16 DOXOLOGY 6:15, 16 This is not the first doxology in the letter: 1:17 Paul is stringing together some attributes of God for Timothy s encouragement He is not just fulfilling some word count for a paper These are for his motivation in ministry; all pointing to the Sovereignty of God Paul speaks of the manifestation of Jesus second coming which in His times He shall show That s God the Father Paul constantly spoke of His coming 1 Cor. 1:8; Phil. 2:15, 16; 1 Thess. 3:13; 5:23 Jesus said only the Father knew when He would return Mark 13:32 But of that day or that hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father. Acts 1:7 He said to them, "It isn't for you to know times or seasons which the Father has set within His own authority. The times are in the Father s hands; He will act in His own good time Blessed markarios; familiar word; used throughout the beatitudes It means happy; content; God is completely blessed in Himself Does not mean He displays no other emotion such as jealousy or wrath But it does mean that in heaven there is no anxiety, no worry, no problems only plans There are no surprises, no accidents; no I didn t see that coming Only Potentate dunamis; a power not delegated but inherent to God alone This power is unique to God And He is the only Potentate King of kings and Lord of lords Literally King of those being kings and Lord of those being lords the King of those kinging and Lord of those lording simply means all other kings and lords are subject to an ultimate Authority There is a chain of command; all other despots are there by design Only having immortality Only a unique kind of immortality; unending life The Christian will experience immortality (1 Cor. 15:53, 54) But your life had a beginning; God had no beginning and will have no end 1:17 dwelling in the light which no man can approach unapproachable light The Father is transcendent beyond or above the range of normal or merely physical human experience existing apart from and not subject to the limitations of the material universe The Father is invisible whom no man has seen or can see Paul repeats the word see God can see men but men cannot see God God is aware of all the men are doing but men are unaware of what God is doing God is spirit He will be the light of the new heaven and new earth (Rev. 22:5) to whom honor and might everlasting Honor: value, precious, esteem, dignity Might: dominion, power, strength Concludes pointing specifically to the Father s sovereignty DOCTRINE Why was this Doxology presented to Timothy Timothy was in a difficult ministry in Ephesus He had some unique challenges Temptation: flee youthful lusts Leadership: Let no one despise your youth The letter was written with a purpose: 3:15 A second letter was written endure hardship ; Avoid foolish disputes ; present yourself to God an approved workman preach because some will depart from the faith difficult days are coming Flee and Follow and Fight Pay attention to six virtues: Righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness Fight the good fight There are a lot of fights out there Make sure you fight the good one Because just as Jesus stood fast and presented a good testimony before Pilate knowing it would cost Him His life, so likewise should you The opposition will always be there Keep the charge given to you to fulfill all obligations and duties of the ministry without blame and irreproachable Guard all these things knowing whom you serve CONCLUSION This is the first NT occasion of reading the phrase King of kings and Lord of lords The other two we will examine are in the book of Revelation; both speaking of Jesus Remember the One you are serving; this also is our motivation A CHRISTMAS DOXOLOGY 1 Timothy 6:13-16 Matthew 2:2 John 12:13 Matthew 27:11 Deuteronomy 10:17 Psalm 136:3 Daniel 2:47 Daniel 11:36 DOXOLOGY 6:15, 16 These are for his motivation in ministry Jesus said only the Father knew when He would return Mark 13:32 Acts 1:7 Blessed happy; content Only Potentate this power is unique to God King of kings and Lord of lords there is a chain of command Only having immortality a unique kind of immortality dwelling in the light which no man can approach The Father is transcendent The Father is invisible to whom honor and might everlasting Honor: value, precious Might: dominion DOCTRINE Timothy was in a difficult ministry in Ephesus Flee and Follow and Fight Make sure you fight the good one Guard all these things knowing whom you serve A CHRISTMAS DOXOLOGY 1 Timothy 6:13-16 Matthew 2:2 John ________ Matthew 27:11 Deuteronomy 10:17 Psalm 136:3 Daniel 2:47 Daniel ________ DOXOLOGY 6:15, 16 These are for his ____________ in ministry Jesus said only the Father knew when He would return Mark ________ Acts ________ Blessed ____________; content Only Potentate this ____________ is unique to God King of kings and Lord of lords there is a ____________ of command Only having immortality a ____________ kind of immortality dwelling in the light which no man can approach The Father is ____________ The Father is ____________ to whom honor and might everlasting Honor: value, ____________ Might: ____________ DOCTRINE Timothy was in a ____________ ministry in Ephesus Flee and Follow and Fight Make sure you fight the ____________ one ____________ all these things knowing whom you serve ============================================= === SERMON: message(136).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/19/2024 3:05:31 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message BABYLON 2.0 JESUS: dba KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS Revelation 17 INTRODUCTION Our Christmas series this year is exploring the 3 occasions in the NT in which Scripture records the title: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS. In 1 Timothy 6:17, the name was included as part of Paul s doxology of motivation for ministry for Timothy in his work in Ephesus. In that instance, the words describe God the Father and His attribute of Providence. If you are commanded to FLEE, FOLLOW, AND FIGHT, the doxology is a great reminder of who you are serving. Such a reminder is the stimulus in FIGHTING THE GOOD FIGHT. Now we move to the book of Revelation for the other two occasions of this descriptive title. There are many titles for Jesus: Lamb, Light, the Way, the Truth, the Door, Shepherd, the Good Shepherd, the Great Shepherd. It would be a long series of messages just to explore those. During this annual celebration of God becoming flesh, the attention is on the virgin birth, God s love, peace on earth, wise men still seek Him. We are reminded Israel was under Roman rule, but the sacrificial system in the temple was most active. We are reminded of God s promises and fix our eyes on hope for the future. We know the world is coming to an end and God has described it for us. And just like God spelled out the arrival of Jesus to take on human form, He has not left us blind as to what we can expect when Jesus returns to earth. As the historical accuracy of the first coming has been witnessed, you can know that all future fulfilling of Scripture will be just as accurate. Like the OT Jew, we may fumble with perfect understanding of the events as laid out for us, but all events will take place as recorded in the Holy Scriptures. When the Lamb of God returns He will be doing business as the King of Kings and Lord of Lords. Revelation is the unveiling of Jesus Christ, the Lamb, as He reclaims the universe The process includes a series of judgments on the earth and its inhabitants: 6 seal, 6 trumpet and 6 bowl judgments. Not one area of the globe will be exempt except the location where God is protecting and providing for His own. By the time we arrive in Revelation 17, we are right at the end of the 7-year period called the Tribulation Period; the last 3 years of which is called the Great Tribulation. In chapter 19, Jesus will return and establish a 1,000-year physical reign upon the earth at the end of which a final rebellion will be squashed, the present earth and heavens will be burned up and a new earth will arrive where saints will forever be with the Lord. Do you know Jesus and does Jesus know you? There are two chapters in Revelation before the arrival of Jesus and His armies As John wrote this letter on the desolate isle of Patmos where He as assigned for the Word of God and testimony of Jesus Christ , he would lay out the events and then fill in some details. That is what he does here. These chapters do not describe the judgments, but who is being judged. We know the world at that time will be under the rule of Satan There will be a one-world government: politically, economically, and religiously. Chapter 17 is the judgment of the religious system and chapter 18 will be the judgment of the political system. What is fascinating is that the world things they are uniting to save the planet, when in truth they will bring the planet s final destruction. Human beings were created to worship Man has a God-made vacuum to be filled by Him who created it a man will find no rest until he rests in God Man is still a worshipper, but his attempt is twisted and perverted Romans 1:21-23 Satan has offered to fill the void He allows man to pursue his need for worship without being under the authority of God All over the world man is pursuing false deities to satisfy his distorted desire to worship. This statement came on the computer screen this week: With the emerging trend of devil worship occurring throughout many aspects of society, the natural progression of things would be to hang out with Satan for the holidays. * Patriot Post Like everything else about rebellion, false religion comes to its ultimate fulfillment in the last days. All false religions are founded in Satan. Note the beginning and end of our mankind: Genesis 3:4,5 and 2 Thessalonians 1-4 They have a variety of names, different rites, different ceremonies, but basically they are all the same. By this time in Revelation, we have identified the false trinity: Satan, the antichrist, the false prophet Their kingdom will be called Babylon; materialistic and spiritual The destruction of the materialistic side is in Chapter 18 and the spiritual side is chapter 17 IDENTITY OF THE HARLOT 17:5 MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH This may not be the name of the letterhead but it is the descriptive title 14:8 and 16:9, we were told Babylon would be destroyed We are not given the details Spiritual Babylon is described as a mother Described as great; not just a geographical location; something far-reaching THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS All spiritual harlotry is spawned from this Babylon Harlotry or prostitution is the standing symbol in Scripture for one thing idolatry Turning to other gods was considered prostitution; joining yourself to a harlot Jeremiah 3:6-9 Ezekiel 16:30-33 The final form of world religion will be the mother of all idolatry; the mother of all false religion Babylon once gave birth to all prostituted forms of religion and in the end they all come home to momma. Babylon is the source; the mother of them all. 13:4 they worshipped the dragon . . . they worshipped the beast Who is worshipping? 13:3 all the world Historical Babylon was built by Nimrod as monument to man Genesis 11:4 let us make for ourselves a name It is humanism; a false religious system Babylon mothered all false religions; all with a family resemblance In the end of time, they all come home to mother This is spiritual Babylon: all religions molded into one This is the spiritual side of Satan s kingdom it will be deceived into thinking it can win a battle against God at Armageddon the world will be seduced into losing particular religious distinctiveness and worship Satan THE ASSOCIATIONS OF THE HARLOT 17:2, 3 Kings of the earth Earth dwellers Woman and the Beast For a while, it looks like the harlot is in charge, riding Lasts for about 3 years but the beast wants all the focus 17:16 the beast will turn and devour the harlot; all worship directed to the antichrist THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE HARLOT The system is wicked, vile, obscene Parading her harlotry around without shame Drunk as a murderer A persecutor of the truth through the centuries It is amazing that as you look at the world today to realize that it will be unified someday around religion. The final religion has a name 17:8 more are in wonderment All unbelievers will be amazed 2 Thessalonians 2:7-10 received not the love of the truth People don t perish because they are not part of the elect People perish because they refuse the love of the truth so as to be saved The beast and the harlot work in unison until about the midpoint of the Tribulation Period The whole religious system is consumed and total worship is directed to the antichrist and the dragon THE WAR OF THE HARLOT 17:14 Make war with the Lamb the final physical conflict of the armies of the world as they attempt to thwart the installation of Messiah upon the throne of David in His Millennial Reign. Jesus is taking back what is rightfully His and restore everything the first Adam lost The Lamb will overcome; nike He is King of Kings; Lord of lords Theme of the Book: The Lamb will be Unveiled; The Lamb will be Revealed: The Lamb will Triumph He is the Supreme One 19:16 This is the ultimate fulfillment of Psalm 2:2, 3 It is ascribing deity to Jesus: Deuteronomy10:7 Psalm 110:1-7 they that are with Him Who are they? Can t be angels: might be chosen but they don t live by faith It has to be saints; it has to be describing believers 19:14 The saints, the redeemed, the glorified church Jude 14, 15 Joel 3:9-14 Zechariah 14:4, 5 Starts with election in eternity past Comes to the calling: a unique word to saints who at on time were not saints Saints returning with Jesus to participate in the Millennial Kingdom Manner of salvation: faith among those who are fallen The antichrist will have the greatest force ever to confront God yet will be destroyed The beast can t stand the competition of the harlot He wants it all He takes her followers; he takes her money eats her flesh totally consumes her It is all about him What is his purpose? 17:17 Fulfilling God s will; God is in charge, orchestrating the whole thing God is bringing the world together to destroy it God wants to totally remove sin from the earth Until the words of God shall be fulfilled This is the total destruction of every false religious system Founded in the hearts of those living at the time of scattering in Babylon King of Kings and Lord of Lords 1 Timothy 6:17 part of Paul s doxology for motivation in ministry Revelation 17:14 establishing Jesus as absolute over all nations "At the heart of the gospel message, and the message of Christmas, is the kingdom of God and the kingship of Christ. Again, every kingdom needs a king. Christ is the anointed king of God s kingdom. . . . .Christmas is a time when we mark his first advent and long for his return. A weary world aches for its king, and Christmas reminds us that as surely as he came the first time, he will return to fully establish his kingdom....." Christmas is the most wonderful time of year because it points us to the first and second advents of the King. It reminds us that peace on earth is not an empty slogan, but a purchased reality brought by the Shepherd King. We sing and rejoice because Christ came, he died, he rose again, he ascended to the Father, he reigns on high, and he is coming back. Everything must be seen in light of this reality the King has come and he is coming back. Levi Secord; christoverall.com BABYLON 2.0 Revelation 17 INTRODUCTION We know the world is coming to an end and God has described it for us. We know the world at that time will be under the rule of Satan Chapter 17 is the judgment of the religious system and chapter 18 will be the judgment of the political system. Human beings were created to worship Romans 1:21-23 All false religions are founded in Satan. IDENTITY OF THE HARLOT 17:5 Spiritual Babylon is described as a mother THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS Turning to other gods was considered prostitution Jeremiah 3:6-9 Ezekiel 16:30-33 Babylon mothered all false religions THE ASSOCIATIONS OF THE HARLOT 17:2, 3 THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE HARLOT Parading her harlotry around without shame 2 Thessalonians 2:7-10 People perish because they refuse the love of the truth THE WAR OF THE HARLOT 17:14 Jesus is taking back what is rightfully His they that are with Him Jude 14, 15 Joel 3:9-14 Zechariah 14:4, 5 Saints returning with Jesus to participate in the Millennial Kingdom God is bringing the world together to destroy it ============================================= === SERMON: message(137).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/3/2025 1:31:47 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message Communion after the message this week A CHORUS OF HALLELUJAHS REVELATION 19:1-10 INTRODUCTION The Christmas season provides some great musical selections More than Frost the Snowman and Grandma Got Run Over By a Reindeer We know even some of the church songs are not strong doctrinally And there are growing arguments about Mary, Did You Know? Put me on the list that Mary was not omniscient One of the greatest musical presentations is the Hallelujah Chorus from Handel s Messiah. I listened to several videos over the holidays including an instrumental version by the Canadian Brass. One public school has an annual tradition of gathering all who are interested; student, faculty and staff; and they sing together in the commons area of the school. You may be surprised to learn that if you look at the musical score of Handel s Messiah, the Hallelujah Chorus is towards the middle, but often presented at the conclusion. In the past weeks, we looked at the three occasion in the NT of the phrase King of Kings and Lord of Lords Scriptural references? 1 Timothy 6; Rev. 17, 19 In preparation for the Lord s Supper, I want to take a quick look at the 4 occasions the word hallelujah is used in the NT Each reference is in Revelation 19 Here is the chorus of Hallelujahs anthems of joy in heaven 4 Hallelujahs anticipating the return of Jesus to earth. Why is heaven rejoicing? chapters 17, 18 = deal with fall of the Babylonian world system chapter 19 = final defeat of the Antichrist; Christ s triumphal return Here are the two identifying characteristics of the chorus of Hallelujahs The deliverance of the saints The judgment of the ungodly We will quickly look at the first three and settle on number 4 1A. HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PERSON Hallelujah = Praise Jehovah, Praise the Lord He Judges v.1 recognizing God in a personal way; attributing to Him salvation, glory, honor and power. v. 2 praise is given to God for His judgments; always true, righteous, and just. judgment of harlot necessary to make room for the reign of Christ and His pure and spotless bride. God s judgment of the great harlot brought divine vengeance of its fornication or moral and spiritual prostitution, by which it corrupted the people on the earth. Babylon source of religious deception, confusion. He Avenges It also avenged the blood of all God s servants martyred by the anti-God world system. 6:11 The people in heaven rejoice that the divine judgment of Babylon had vindicated and avenged them with justice. Vengeance is mine, I will repay saith the Lord. Rom. 12:19 2A. HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PROMISE v. 3 Second hallelujah. from much people in heaven; more emphatic and dramatic prompted by the completeness of the just overthrow and judgment of the great Babylon world system. rejoicing called for in 14:11; 18:20 the smoke goes up for ever and ever. The effect of their praises: when the angels and saints cried Alleluia, her fire burned more fiercely and her smoke ascended for ever and ever, Edom = Isaiah 34: 2, 5, 6, 9, 10 3A. HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PRAISE v. 4 Third Hallelujah 24 elders and 4 living beings respond to much people in heaven They Fell They Worshipped They Feared honor God for His justice and righteousness of judgment Amen = positive response, truly, certainly, support response of faith that recognizes that what has been said is true. full approval of what God has done, certainty His judgment is just, right. v. 5 command that all servants of God are to keep on praising Him. servants those who fear Him 4A. HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS POSITION v. 6 Fourth Hallelujah His Majesty response to command from the throne to praise our God. Praise is deserved because He is the Lord, because He is omnipotent, Now will come about His will on earth as it is in heaven His throne in heaven will not be on earth Voice of angelic hosts?? or the same voices as v. 1 which would include believers?? God is fearful in praises. He is to be loved and feared (Ex. 15:11) There is no discord in heaven; the morning stars sing together; no jarring string, nor key untuned, but pure and perfect melody. fourth hallelujah is louder and more joyful like the sound of many waters, like the sound of mighty peals of thunder. He is worthy of praise for He is the Lord, He is our God in a personal way, He is mighty, omnipotent = all powerful His Marriage v. 7 marriage supper of the Lamb the image presented is a wedding; a familiar illustration in the Bible not every wedding illustration can be interpreted the same the bride in one may be the guests at another the combination of all is needed to develop a theology of wedding The Elements of a Bible Wedding Betrothal: engagement, espousal A legally binding contract usually arranged by parents Payment of dowry Choosing a family was viewed as more important than the individual Values, standards, morals The bridegroom was to make preparations; often adding on to his father s house Presentation After the preparations and sometimes with little warning, the bridegroom would fetch his bride and present her to the invited guests The time of festivities leading up to the actual wedding Could last a few days, a week, or several weeks The bridegroom was the center of the event Ceremony At the end of the festivities After meeting the bride and a lot of visiting There would be a final meal, exchanging of vows Best man would see that all the guests leave Biblical Imagery The Bride 2 Corinthians 11:1-3; Ephesians 5 Concern of presenting a bride as defiled The Groom Matthew 9:15; John 3:29 Hebrews 9:11, 12 Paid the dowry price John 14:2, 3 Prepared a place in the Father s house The Ceremony Rev. 19 bride is now the wife It will bring great honor and give great glory to God the Father as a culmination of His great plan of redemption. Marriage of the Lamb, not King or Lord. Lamb = title speaks of love, price paid. The wife has made herself ready v. 8 wife already clothed in fine linen, clean, and white. free from all impurity. linen = righteousnesses of saints, plural righteous deeds of saints. robes do not represent imputed righteousness of Christ in which we were justified, although it influences us to act righteously rather it is acts we do as the result of our relationship with Christ. They are the outworkings of the gift of salvation and the gifts of the Holy Spirit. rewards make up wedding gown. Dr. Lehman Strauss: has it ever occurred to you that at the marriage of the Bride to the Lamb, each of us will be wearing the wedding garment of our own making. this clothing of white linen reminds us of Christ s desire that His believers could come before Him not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing. Holiness, purity, bright; Glistening, shining, radiant You will look marvelous Bible Illustrations The wedding illustrations have different purposes Basic Biblical Hermeneutics: don t stretch to unintended purposes Matthew 22:2-13 Some invitees made light of the invite; unimaginable Even mistreat the messengers There is no mention of the bride; not the main message In this illustration the guests are the church 22:11-13 the emphasis is on the proper wedding garment Isa. 61:10 Proper attire is required to enter the kingdom Matthew 25:1-13 The message is preparedness No mention of the bride The redeemed are the 5 wise virgins; true believers are ready The marriage supper will celebrate the fact that the Church shall ever be with the Lord. 1 Thess 4:17 God s love that sent Jesus to die on the cross for a believer s sins made it possible for them to be saved from perishing in the lake of fire. it also made it possible for them to respond to that love and enter into and share the joy and triumph of the Lord. These promises of the Marriage Supper of the Lamb are the true sayings of God. CONCLUSION Are you wearing the right garment? Dressed in Christ s righteousness Are you looking for the coming of the groom? Are you preparing your wedding garment? A CHORUS OF HALLELUJAHS REVELATION 19:1-10 HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PERSON He Judges He Avenges HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PROMISE Isaiah 34 HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS PRAISE They Fell They Worshipped They Feared HALLELUJAH BECAUSE OF HIS POSITION His Majesty God is fearful in praises Exodus 15:11 His Marriage The Elements of a Bible Wedding Betrothal Presentation Ceremony Biblical Imagery The Bride 2 Corinthians 11:1-3; Ephesians 5 The Groom Matthew 9:15; John 3:29 The Ceremony Rev. 19 Marriage of the Lamb Bible Illustrations Matthew 22:2-13 Proper attire is required to enter the kingdom Matthew 25:1-13 The message is preparedness ============================================= === SERMON: message(138).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/10/2025 6:41:24 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message THE APPROACH OF THE UNTOUCHABLES LUKE 15:1, 2 INTRODUCTION Jesus had been emphasizing His ministry to the poor and outcast members of society. 4:18; 14:21 He now puts into practice what He had been saying by fellowshipping with the tax collectors and sinners; the outcasts of Jewish society He is spending time with them; He is sharing meals with them This chapter is a challenge to the cultural bigotry of this time The Pharisees and the Scribes were the in crowd There were perceived as the religiously upright; politically patriotic Tax collectors and sinners were the outs The implication is a disregard for the Law of Moses and traditions of the elders Publicans were doubly despised; seen as collaborators with Rome Luke 15 has 3 parables dealing with things lost and then found A lamb, a coin, a son Jesus is piling up parables to get to His Main Point: the attitude of the righteous elder brother toward his newly restored younger brother This chapter has been called God s Lost and Found Most writers speak of the Lost Sheep, the Lost Coin, the Lost Son I like what one writer does by adding one more, the Lost Sibling Many would have cut their losses One sheep out of 100; one coin out of 10 It would even be harder to go looking for a wandering son; could only pray But God could and does This chapter comes at the halfway point in Luke s gospel And could be considered the highpoint of the gospel; The purpose of His coming; Luke19:10 We are familiar with the stories in this chapter What we may not be ready for is the challenge presented here Do the things that bring you joy also the things that excite the heart of God? Or do you get excited about the things that brings great joy to God? 15:7, 10, 24 Let s approach it this way What is your picture of God? How do you see Him? Most we speak of His righteousness, holiness, grace, mercy, love,, justice Do you think of God as happy? We have no account of Jesus laughing We saw a little in chapter 10; we picture pleasure as Jesus welcomed children; we imagine a smile with some stories He told and as a demonstration of His compassion But typically, a jovial Jesus is not the picture we have What makes God happy? We looked at the Chorus of Hallelujahs last week in Rev. 19 The first 10x the word rejoice is used in the Scriptures is man rejoicing in God Deuteronomy 28:63 God rejoiced over His people Deuteronomy 30:9 Psalm 104:31 Isaiah 62:5 Jeremiah 32:41 Zephaniah 3:17 The point is we generally do not use the words happy and God together But if we desire to become godly in all things, how about what makes us happy? To what means will you extend yourself to reach the publicans and sinners? Are they welcome to gather with us on a Sunday morning? Would we feed them if it means we don t eat? How much Pharisaism is in us? I am weak in many areas and personal evangelism is definitely one There have been several lately I have offered to speak with, but no one has accepted the invitation. I can always use the line of showing my good works before them, but somewhere you have to sit down and use the Scriptures I am hoping someone will seek to speak of eternal things I am hoping to make this more of an emphasis for us this year I know many have never shared the gospel with another Many have never had the joy of seeing someone confess Christ and then live out that confession I have become a cynic too many times when I hear about salvation decisions Churches boast about their evangelism but yet they are adding few to the flock Were the decisions real, I have no clue; was the lack of discipleship the cause, I don t know The general rule seems to be it takes a year or two to determine the genuineness of one s salvation But neither are we going to water down Luke 9:23 I am not interested in a come as you are church where we will entertain you into the kingdom I have found nowhere in Scripture where God is satisfied with less than the best we can offer Our goal is not to fill seats but to transform lives But I also believe this chapter will challenge our hearts to how we look at the lost They are angry, hateful, vulgar, liars, immoral perverts They are also well-dressed, nice, moral, business people Some were just ordinary people who are indifferent to spiritual things Regardless, they all oppose God. Some from ignorance, some foolishness, some from a suppose offense Our challenge is this: if God rejoices with the restoration of the nation of Israel from exile and heaven rejoices over a sinner who repents, can we? Even if that person is a family member who has wasted an inheritance on wine, women, and feeding hogs? Chapter 14 finished with the challenge of discipleship and He who has ears let him hear 15:1 Now Then And all possible translations Notice who has ears to hear: the cultural rejects Remember the words of Jesus: 5:31, 32 They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick. I did not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance Where was he? In the house of a tax collector Someone considered worse than a heathen These were the spiritually untouchables, the outcasts Not only were they coming, but they were also continuing to come They were part of the great multitude of 14:25 They knew what discipleship meant and they were ready to hear It was not religion that attracted them but spiritual truth The problem the religious elite had was not so much that sinners were coming but This man receives and eats with them Jesus welcomed them; they were comfortable in His presence Eating together was a form of acceptance You have heard the slogan, you know a man by the company he keeps Well, if this is His company, so He must not be a good man It was the tax collectors and sinners who drew near v. 1 MacArthur: Drawing near to God is the essence of Christianity The Spirit enables but we must still choose James 4:8 Spurgeon - The Pharisees and scribes formed the outside ring of Christ s hearers, but the inner circle consisted of the guilty, the heavy-laden, and the lowly. They pressed as near to Christ as they could, that they might catch his every word; and besides, there was an attractiveness about his manner that drew them towards him. His mercy attracted their misery. They wanted him, and he desired them; they were thus well met. There will be an inner circle tonight when the gospel is preached, and it will not consist of the self-righteous. They that are full will not press to the table on which the gospel feast is spread, the hungry will be found nearest to the heavenly provision. The contrast is significant v. 2 The first word in the Greek text: grumbling place for emphasis Imperfect tense: grumbling over and over Visibly disturbed by this sea of sinners Chapter 14 began with their opposing healing a man retaining water The hostility continues, growing and will soon burst When we are discontented with ourselves, we grumble about others The Pharisees had a title for the people gathered around Jesus People of the Land From the Daily Study Bible: The Pharisaic regulations laid it down, "When a man is one of the People of the Land, entrust no money to him, take no testimony from him. trust him with no secret, do not appoint him guardian of an orphan, do not make him the custodian of charitable funds, do not accompany him on a journey." We should insert this here: Do you have the gift of Grumbling? Grumble in English dictionaries - a loud low dull continuous noise, a complaint uttered in a low and indistinct tone, to make a low, growling or rumbling noise, like a hungry stomach or certain animals, to make complaining remarks or noises under one's breath, to utter or emit low dull rumbling sounds, to utter (complaints) in a nagging or discontented way, to complain about something in a bad-tempered way. "At the beginning of a new year, a high school principal decided to post his teachers' new year's resolutions on the bulletin board. As the teachers gathered around the bulletin board, a great commotion started. One of the teachers was complaining. "Why weren't my resolutions posted?" She was throwing such a temper tantrum that the principal hurried to his office to see if he had overlooked her resolutions. Sure enough, he had mislaid them on his desk. As he read her resolutions he was astounded. This teacher's first resolution was not to let little things upset her in the new year!" The Pharisees grumbled, murmured Jesus associated with the riff raff; the low lives Jesus may have met them where they were, but called them to far better things Jesus may have received sinners, but He did not expect them to remain sinners There was never such a reception as this! This fact is still most sure this evening, he is still receiving sinners: would to God sinners would receive him. Spurgeon Conclusion How do you see yourself? Paul called himself the chief of sinners Are the one who would draw near or remain in the background grumbling? Have you received the sacrifice of Christ for sin as it relates to you? When it comes to witnessing to the lost can you sing: Change my heart, O God THE APPROACH OF THE UNTOUCHABLES LUKE 15:1, 2 Main Point: the attitude of the righteous elder brother The purpose of His coming; Luke19:10 What makes God happy? Deuteronomy 30:9 Psalm 104:31 Isaiah 62:5 Jeremiah 32:41 Zephaniah 3:17 How much Pharisaism is in us? Our goal is not to fill seats but to transform lives Notice who has ears to hear: the cultural rejects; the spiritual untouchables It was not religion that attracted them but spiritual truth Eating together was a form of acceptance MacArthur: Drawing near to God is the essence of Christianity James 4:8 There was never such a reception as this! This fact is still most sure this evening, he is still receiving sinners: would to God sinners would receive him. Spurgeon ============================================= === SERMON: message(139).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/17/2025 4:10:42 PM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message MORE HERE THAN MEETS THE EYE LUKE 15:3-32 INTRODUCTION Jesus is taking an interesting path to get to the heart of the Pharisees and their attitude toward sinners and publicans Their attitude toward those they think are beneath them; those upon whom they would place heaven burdens People of such low esteem there is no place for them in the kingdom So, He gives them a story with three parts Interesting to note: this parable Note how the three are connected: or (8) and (11) It is a single reply concerning God s attitude toward sinners It is a mirror to show the Pharisees what their own heart looks like There is a Single Theme: the joy of God over a sinner who repents The stories are used to expose hearts If finding a lost sheep and coin is considered important, how much more is the soul of a lost person? A parable is a true to life account to illustrate spiritual truth Parable by definition means to throw alongside You place the story along side the truth to illustrate the truth Parables were meant to encourage people to think Jesus did not start His public ministry teaching this way The disciples took note when He began using parables; Matthew 13:10 His answer: to reveal truth to those who want to know and hide the truth from those who are indifferent He who has ears to hear, let him hear May we not get caught up in what is lost in this parable but focus on a seeking shepherd, a searching woman, and a loving father All point to a loving heavenly Father How can we say we love God and not know what gives Him sorrow and what gives Him joy? The Father hurts for the lost and rejoices when they are found - CS Lewis, "The Magician's Nephew" "Up till then he had been looking at the Lion's great feet and the huge claws on them; now, in his despair, he looked up at its face. What he saw surprised him as much as anything in his whole life. For the tawny face was bent down near his own and (wonder of wonders) great shining tears stood in the Lion's eyes. They were such big, bright tears compared with Digory's own that for a moment he felt as if the Lion must really be sorrier about his Mother than he was himself." Psalm 119:136 Rivers of water run down from my eyes, Because men do not keep your law. Have you ever lost your phone? It is worse than losing a kid at the store You have more kids The Pharisees saw no sin in themselves and despised those who were sinners They concluded that Jesus could not be from God because God did not like sinners. The accusation is in verse 2 religious people don t do that The unspoken question is what is God s attitude toward sinners? Why welcome and eat with them? The answer is God s attitude toward sinners and self-righteous attitude of the Pharisees There is a common pattern in each account an item is lost, a search is made, the item is found, rejoicing follows Let s take a moment and look at another picture given to us here 3 Ways people tend to get away from God WANDERING like a wayward lamb People slowly drift away Isa. 53 all we like sheep have gone astray The song writer: Prone to wander, Lord, I feel it, Prone to leave the God I love The pull of sin is strong even for believers The Apostle Paul admitted so in Romans 7 as he battled against the flesh Can we point to a specific cause? The hymn writer also said, tune my heart to sing thy praise Think it not strange if you become out of tune with God Be sure to know, you are the one out of tune You need the tuning fork ringing in your ear to make the adjustments once again. There are many reasons a piano goes out of tune: regular use, stretching stings, temperature and humidity changes It always comes back to spiritual disciplines The Word of God is a lamp for your every step (119:105) neglect the lamp and darkness reigns 119:9 Mark It 119:11 Memorize It 119:14 Meditate on It 119:18 Master It One historical account reads this way: Robert Robinson In a spiritually backslidden condition, the author was traveling in a stage coach one day. His only companion was a young woman unknown to him. In the providence of God, and not realizing who it was she spoke with, the woman quoted Come, Thou Fount of Every Blessing, saying what an encouragement it had been to her. And try as he might, Robinson could not get her to change the subject. Finally, he said, with tears in his eyes, Madam, I am the poor unhappy man who composed that hymn, many years ago. And I would give a thousand worlds, if I had them, to enjoy the feelings I then had! Gently, she replied, Sir, the streams of mercy are still flowing. He was deeply touched by that. As a result of the encounter he repented. His fellowship with the Lord was restored through the ministry of his own hymn, and a Christian s willing witness. The same goes for your heart Psalm 119:10 With my whole heart have I sought thee: let me not wander from thy commandments. I found another stanza to Come Thou Fount O that day when freed from sinning, I shall see thy lovely face; Clothed then in blood washed linen How I ll sing Thy sovereign grace; Come my Lord, no longer tarry, Take my ransomed soul away; Send thine angels now to carry Me to realms of endless day. WAYWARD a coin suddenly falls A spur of the moment fleshly temptation; sensual desires ignited Drop into sin like a dropped coin A time of weakened resistance to temptation A time of broken fellowship with God Lead to a rapid fall into sin I hesitate to use the idea of falling into sin There are only 2x in the Bible where those two words are in the same verse But they don t go together Folks use that as an excuse: I was just going along my merry way, when sin jumped out and got me We speak as if the earth suddenly opened up like a sink hole and we had no choice Matthew 26:41 Watch and pray that you enter not into temptation Does not say fall but enter; the same word used as entering the kingdom Watch means to be vigilant, keep awake Pray the spirit is willing but the flesh is weak The flesh will walk right into sin Sensual desires get set on fire because we are in the wrong place We recently had another preacher favorite resign in disgrace because of adultery Al Mohler spoke to seminary students in Louisville and was very direct You cannot have sex with another woman if you not alone in a room with another woman. Yes, you have to be vigilant Ephesians 6; the spiritual armor is not just about destroying the fiery darts of the evil one, but we are also commanded to stand Don t cross the threshold The dog returns to its own vomit, and the sow returns to wallow in the mire (2 Peter 2:22; Prov. 26:11) 2 Peter 3:17 Ye therefore, beloved, knowing these things beforehand, beware lest, being carried away with the error of the wicked, ye fall from your own stedfastness. TRANSITION We can Wander; We can be Wayword There is a third picture presented here why people tend to get away from God WILLFUL Rebellion Our rebellious and hard heart can destroy us Lead us into all sorts of hardships and disgusting circumstances Scripture does distinguish between willful sinning and a sin of ignorance But both require repentance and forgiveness Willful Rebellion is to be proud and arrogant in one s defiance of God Psalm 19:13 Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous sins; Let them not have dominion over me: Then shall I be upright, And I shall be clear from great transgression. Verses about sinning in ignorance Ephesians 4:17-19 Acts 3:17-19 Acts 17:30, 31 People are not as ignorant as they may claim, however. No one can be utterly ignorant of God, and no one has an excuse to live in disobedience. The apostle Paul said, For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth. For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has shown it to them. For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world, in the things that have been made. So they are without excuse (Romans 1:18-20 ). Can we sin in ignorance? Sure 1 Timothy 1:13 Even though I was once a blasphemer and a persecutor and a violent man, I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and unbelief The warning is clear for willful and habitual sinners: if they have escaped the corruption of the world by knowing our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ and are again entangled in it and are overcome, they are worse off at the end than they were at the beginning. It would have been better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than to have known it and then to turn their backs on the sacred command that was passed on to them 2 Peter 2:20-21 . 1 John 3:8 Whoever makes a practice of sinning is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the works of the devil. (ESV) Hebrews 10:26 For if we go on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins, but a fearful expectation of judgment, (ESV) In other words: don t look for some other system or program to help You can only plead forgiveness through the blood of the Lord Jesus Acts 4:12 For there is no other name under heaven given among men whereby we must be saved Review: There is the Wandering Sinner, the Wayward Sinner; the Willful Sinner Apostle John referred to the same sinners this way: 1 John 2:15-16 (DBY) Love not the world, nor the things in the world. If any one love the world, the love of the Father is not in him; because all that [is] in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world. The Lust of the Flesh: the Wandering Sinner Just feeding on whatever the heart desires; always greener over there The Lust of the Eyes: the Wayward Sinner The Shiny Silver; all that glitters; what pleases the eye that would really look good in my driveway that is a fine looking woman That is a hunk of a man Psalm 119:37 Turn away my eyes from looking at worthless things Psalm 101:3 I will set nothing wicked before my eyes The Pride of Life: the Willful Sinner I know what I want and I want it now; Everybody deserves to have nice stuff It can t be wrong if it feels so right Let the devil take tomorrow, tonight I need a friend BENEDICTION JUDE 24 MORE HERE THAN MEETS THE EYE LUKE 15:3-32 Single Theme: the joy of God over a sinner who repents Parable by definition means to throw alongside The Father hurts for the lost and rejoices when they are found THREE Ways People Tend to Get Away from God WANDERING The pull of sin is strong even for believers It always comes back to spiritual disciplines Psalm 119:10 WAYWARD Drop into sin like a dropped coin Matthew 26:41 2 Peter 3:17 WILLFUL Rebellion Psalm 19:13 Can we sin in ignorance? 1 Timothy 1:13 2 Peter 2:20-21 1 John 3:8 Hebrews 10:26 1 John 2:15-16 ============================================= === SERMON: message(140).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/31/2025 1:09:11 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE PARABLE: STORY 1 AND 2 Luke 15:1-10 INTRODUCTION The emphasis in this chapter His ministry to the poor and outcast members of society. He now puts into practice what He had been saying by fellowshipping with the tax collectors and sinners The Pharisees and scribes were part of the in crowd Tax collectors and sinners were the outs They are viewed as having little regard for the Law of Moses and the traditions of Jewish elders Sinner was a general term used for those with supposed low sexual morals They were the religiously fallen; the politically traitorous Jesus presented a paradox: the Righteous One involved with the unrighteous 3 weeks ago the challenge was how we view the lost around us Luke 15 is one parable: 15:3 One parable with 3 stories: The Lost Sheep, the Lost Silver, the Lost Son 2 weeks ago, we looked at three reason we get away from God Wander, Wayward, Willful All three stories deal with things that are lost and then found Each part pointing to the main point: the attitude of the righteous elder brother toward his newly restored younger brother What do we learn about God? Most believe God would welcome the repentant sinner But what we many have a harder time with accepting is that God is a seeking God; a God who takes the initiative We must accept the Biblical teaching that If God did not seek us, we would still reject Him. Romans 3 There are none who seek after God If God did not choose to save, we would have no hope Many folks have a hard time with that truth Our pride says that we are not that bad; that we can decide; that we can choose; that given the right information we can make an intelligent decision on which God to serve. But who would think about coming to a God who says if you want to save your life, you must lose it? If you want to follow Me, you must first die to self? In fact, you must die daily Rejoice that the God of the Bible is a seeking God. Rejoice that the God of the Bible first loved you. STORY 1 THE LOST SHEEP 15:3-7 Please note the personal approach to the parable Which of you? Not like 10:30; 13:6 Take personal interest in what is of value to you So, right away the challenge is on. What self-respecting Pharisee would even consider the occupation of a shepherd? They were just a step above a tax collector. Uneducated, unskilled, dishonest, bottom of the social ladder It is well reported that sheep are dumb animals They will walk away from a flock; paying no attention Get themselves in all kinds of predicaments. There is a YouTube video of pulling a sheep out of a trench and it goes bounding off and falls right back into the trench All we like sheep fall back into the trench The biblical description of one who has no relationship with God is not unsaved but lost Ephesians 2:12 an accurate description; no hope A lost sheep in the wilderness is totally defenseless, no protection A person without Christ does not know he is lost; headed for destruction The use of a shepherd parable to illustrate His point had two particularly valuable aspects for Jesus and the listeners 1. It was a well-known and well-understood example 2. The relationship of a shepherd to his sheep ranks among the best illustrations of the love of God for mankind Ezekiel 34:6-16 the lost sheep of the house of Israel The story is presented in the form of a rhetorical question The Question: which of you would not look for a lost sheep? The implication is not that the shepherd took inventory and came up one short, but that the shepherd knew his flock intimately; as if having a name for each one The shepherd was given charge; may not be the owner There was personal responsibility A flock of 100 was a modest size flock If one was lost the shepherd must at least bring home the fleece as evidence of how it died Sometimes there was not a personal owner, but communal as in belonging to the entire village 2 or 3 shepherds in charge and might bring them in and report one shepherd was still out tracking down a lost one The whole village would then be on watch until the shepherd appeared with the lamb across his shoulders The search was rewarded as sheep was found Until he finds it Now this is important to note We have seen the picture but look beyond what is most evident The shepherd did not drive the sheep back to the flock The shepherd did not drag it back with a noose around its neck He lovingly placed the dear animal upon his shoulders You can picture the nose of the sheep nuzzling his ear His attitude was not one of reproach over what caused the separation No criticism of a wandering spirit He was rejoicing over the reunion and restoration A joy that needed to be shared by inviting others to rejoice with him 15:7 Application The lost sheep was identified with a sinner The 99 obedient sheep were intended to refer to the supposedly righteous scribes and Pharisees Jesus was not approving the piety of the 99 but desirous of every man seeing himself in the lost sheep, every person needs rescued by the Shepherd Jesus was extending the same love to those who considered themselves righteous, but they did not see themselves as lost The heart of the story is the passion for souls and the rejoicing when we see lives changed by the gospel to be your brother s keeper means to be your brother s seeker Adrian Rogers - A family put the following ad in the "Lost and Found" section of the paper: "Lost Dog: Crippled in front paw, blind in left eye, mange on back and neck, tail missing. Recently neutered. Answers to the name 'Lucky'" He truly was a lucky dog, because in spite of all that was wrong with him, somebody loved him enough to want him. Actually, we're all lucky dogs. Even better, we're blessed dogs. God loves us out of sheer grace. Marvel of marvels! Wonder of wonders! God loves us with an everlasting love. I was lost but Jesus found me Found the sheep that went astray, Threw His loving arms around me, Drew me back into His way. Rowley STORY 2 THE LOST SILVER 15:8-10 Jesus moves the illustration from a despised shepherd to a woman Another one not rightly respected by the Pharisees; low social standing The challenge is presented as to how you look at those who are lost Some writers believe the 10 coins were her wedding gift; often worn as a headdress across her forehead like a wedding ring worn today Correct understanding is unnecessary to the story What is important for us to know is this was something very precious to the woman Houses in Israel had little window area and were dark Lights a lamp, sweeps, searches: all present tense, continuous activity Left nothing to chance; no speck of dust overlooked Have you ever had to search through the garbage to find what was thrown away? The lost coin was a drachma; still used in modern Greece The drachma was considered equivalent to the Roman denarius which was a common daily wage for a laborer Ancient literature records that a drachma could buy a sheep and that 5 drachmas could be an ox. Until: up until a point in time The search was successful and a gathering of friends was called for rejoicing Another earthly story; part of the parable thrown along side the illustration revealing the rejoicing in heaven upon the restoration of a sinner to God through repentance The issue is not that God does not concern Himself with the righteous, but why don t you care about the sinners too? God and heaven rejoices The Pharisees would have agreed that the woman was right in her diligent search, but they could not make a connection with their attitude toward the lost They could get excited about a temporal item but not the eternal soul It was one sheep; one coin What is the worth of one soul? The woman was searching through the dust, cracks, and debris of what was probably a dirt or straw floor The coin was lifeless, inanimate; could do nothing on its own Jesus came all the way from heaven to earth to search for His lost ones, pursuing sinners into every dark corner, and then shining the light of the glorious Gospel - MacArthur On a cold night in England many years ago, a group of children slipped into a church to get warm. The preacher was speaking on Lu 15:2 , which in the King James Version reads, This man receiveth sinners and eateth with them. Afterwards, one of the children, a girl of 8, went up to the pastor and said, Pardon me, sir, but I didn t know that my name was in the Bible. He asked, What is your name? Edith, sir. No, he said, Edith is not in the Bible. Yes, it is, she replied. I heard you say, This man receiveth sinners, and Edith with them. ( Our Daily Bread. ) Even though that girl misunderstood the text, she had applied the truth personally to her own heart. If you know that there are sins in your heart that need God s merciful forgiveness, put your name in there. This man receives sinners, and ___________ with them. We are witnessing the release of hostages in Israel. We see the joy, hugs and tears accompanying the reuniting of families. How much greater should be joy when one is eternally set free from sin? Lord, lay some soul upon my heart, And love that soul through me; And may I nobly do my part To win that soul for Thee. Tucker THE PARABLE Luke 15:1-10 Each part pointing to the main point: the attitude of the righteous elder brother toward his newly restored younger brother God is a seeking God STORY 1 THE LOST SHEEP 15:3-7 The biblical description of one who has no relationship with God is not unsaved but lost Ephesians 2:12 Ezekiel 34:6-16 The Question: which of you would not look for a lost sheep? The shepherd did not drive the sheep back to the flock The shepherd did not drag it back with a noose around its neck Jesus was not approving the piety of the 99 The heart of the story is the passion for souls and the rejoicing when we see lives changed by the gospel STORY 2 THE LOST SILVER 15:8-10 What is important for us to know is this was something very precious to the woman The drachma was considered equivalent to the Roman denarius which was a common daily wage for a laborer The issue is not that God does not concern Himself with the righteous, but why don t I care about the sinners too? The woman was searching through the dust, cracks, and debris of what was probably a dirt or straw floor This man receives sinners, and ___________ with them. Lord, lay some soul upon my heart, And love that soul through me; And may I nobly do my part To win that soul for Thee. Tucker ============================================= === SERMON: message(141).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 2/7/2025 8:02:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE PARABLE Story 3 Luke 15:11-32 INTRODUCTION There is so much material in this chapter. As said a few weeks ago, many look at Luke 15 as the highlight chapter of this gospel. Knowing that Jesus came to seek and save those who are lost, Luke 15 features doctrinal, cultural, social, and practical lessons. We have been challenged in how we look at the lost We have been challenged in how we act like the lost: wander, wayward, willful We have been challenged in our response to the conversion of the lost This chapter helps us to fine tune our terminology The Bible does not use the word unsaved in describing those without a personal relationship to Christ. It is not that they simply need further information in order to make a proper decision. They are unable to make a decision unless the Spirit of God draws them. It seems to me that using biblical terms heightens our own awareness of their condition: lost, without hope, having their understanding darkened, alienated from the life of God, ignorant, blind, children of wrath, walking according to the prince of the power in the air, the spirit who now works in the sons of disobedience. Or we can use the word in Luke 15:32 dead Life is something to celebrate. We get all excited about a pregnancy and birth in the natural world; how much more should we be excited about new life in the spiritual world. Story #3 is often called The Prodigal Son The rich variety of applications to be gleaned from the story are seen in the many titles it has been given: The Lost Son, The Two Lost Sons, The Waiting Father, Parable of Divine Mercy, God s Love for the Lost, and the Lost Son and the Dutiful Son. It is easy to get lost ourselves in all the chapter contains and lose the main point Jesus main intent was to illustrate the folly of self-righteousness in contrast to the loving attitude of God. THE DEMAND 15:11, 12 Desiring an early share of his eventual inheritance The father made no attempt to stop him God does not force men to remain in fellowship with Him God will not always prevent you from stumbling He will provide warnings, maybe a roadblock or two, give you a troubled spirit Parents do the same thing. Provide counsel, give warnings, lay down the law All knowing you are going to fail Also praying that in your failing you do not sustain long lasting scars that will hinder you in the years to come Many adults carry the scars of their teenage years Yesterday, I officiated the funeral of a lady who made bad decisions most of her life. Of all Bible principles there is one that I find no one can find disagreement: Whatsoever a man sows, that shall he also reap. The son made the demand; the father complied And may I suggest to you with grief in his heart THE DEPRAVITY 15:12 The son drew away; the father remained James 4:8 The man squandered all his money in riotous living The older brother later identified this as consorting with prostitutes Jesus left no question but that the younger son Wasted his money in a generally immoral manner THE DESPERATION 15:14, 15 Famine in the land; not an unusual occurrence of Jesus day Sin always results in hunger but seeks satisfaction in ungodly ways A heathen citizen took pity on him and gave him something to do We now read the depths to which the son had sunk Moral desolation: tending pigs; no worse job for a Jew Swine were considered to be unclean animals; expressly forbidden in the Mosaic Law Leviticus 11:7; Deuteronomy 14:8 Contrast to the Greeks where we read the pig was most the most sacrificed animal THE DEGRADATION 15:16 Now occupied with the filthiness of jobs; slopping hogs So hungry that hog food was looking good to him Passable for livestock food but despicable human food Realization: detestable pigs now eating better than he is THE DISCOVERY 15:17-20a The First Step to true repentance: Hopelessness and futility came to himself literally: came into himself The tools at God s disposal to get our attention to realize the sinful life is hopeless and futile One must see that his own actions have led him away from the loving father Realizing that the seeming pleasures of the sinful life are easily lost Realizing that the seeming pleasures of the sinful life are unsatisfying He comes to realize that a life of sin offers only destruction He comes to realize that life with the father is full and rich Until a sinner can be convicted of the reality that he travels a dead-end road, true repentance cannot come to pass The Second Step to true repentance: Brokenness Realized his sinful life had lost the claim of sonship Take personal responsibility; no blame game His actions, his attitudes; his pride His only hope lies in the merciful, gracious nature of his father His father is under no obligation to receive him back Saved by the merciful grace of Almighty God Display of great humility What does that look like? Isaiah 57:15 I dwell . . .with him of a humble and contrite heart Isaiah 66:2 I will look . . . the contrite heart; trembles at My Word Psalm 34:18 God is near to the broken hearted Psalm 51:17 God will not despise the broken and contrite heart The Third Step to true repentance: Action Return to those who have been sinned against Where offense has been directed The people whose toes have been stepped on; their nose bloodied; their hearts broken; their spirits crushed; their love abused 2 Corinthians 7:9-11 Leave no overturned stone exposed THE DEVIATION 15:20b-21 The celebration altered the routine of the days The father is pictured as anxiously waiting for his willful child That is a major challenge for those who have been offended Because one side of us would welcome their continued misery, even death If there was ever a time we wished folks would reap what they sow, it is now It is hard to bring yourself to wish God s best for them The father did not reject the son; He did not accept him with punishment, denigration, scolding There was a welcome acceptance Demonstration of humility and conviction Divine offense Defying the Almighty who defined sin Personal offense Sin is rarely private and personal Sometimes we hurt the ones we love the most Accept Consequences Sin leaves us with no valid claims of any right to fellowship Any restoration is only through grace THE DEMONSTRATION 15:22-24 The father quickly reacted to the confession of his son Especially the thought of no longer being his son Did not even allow him to finish Fine robe, ring, sandals Signs of full restoration to the status as son A sign of trust and authority Fatted calf; one set aside for a special celebration The cause for rejoicing 15:24 A son, considered dead and lost, now is alive Here is a deeper reality than in the previous stories The condition of lostness is to be equated with death when it concerns fellowship with the Father (Eph. 2:1-6) Sinful man, alienated from God, are actually dead in God s eyes Sin and death are the gruesome companions of man s existence Christ s resurrection is more than a victory over the power of physical death; it squashes the power of sin, spiritual death 1 Cor. 15:21, 22 The last and greatest enemy to be defeated is death (15:26) The Christian who lives is a state of restoration with the Father can truly cry out, O death, where is they sting? O grave, where is they victory? (15:55) THE DECISION 15:25 Now, Jesus gets to the real point of the three stories The attitude and action of the elder son The elder son was intended to represent the Pharisees and scribes He is the model heir; working in the field; sweating away Surprised to find a party going on Jesus used the figure of the older brother to condemn the attitude of the Pharisees and scribes who found His fraternization with the publicans and sinner completely unacceptable. It is ok to look with disgust at the sins of the world But we also have to guard the way we look at the sinners God hates sin; God is not willing for any to perish; He welcomes lost sheep back to the fold Our attitude toward sinners now comes into focus Our view of sinners should be one of great sorrow Sometimes we are skeptical when a lost soul repents and turns to God We need to be careful lest we partake of the elder brother syndrome The story is not a contrast between the two brothers, but between the attitudes of the older brother and the father. The older brother is loaded with bitterness; pent up feelings building up through the years He has his laundry list of everything he had done and was not rewarded Five Problems of the Older Brother: There was no form of respect presented as the son addressed his father He saw himself as a slave (doulos) Did not seem to know what being a son really meant Reflected the outlook of the Pharisees: always obeyed Justified himself; proud and arrogant Accused the father of failing to reward his loyal obedience Character assassination Hurling accusations; irresponsible and immoral And rewarded with a celebration Did not refer to the younger as brother but your son The father did not deny the accusations; did not deny the wrong that had been done; did not deny the sins were heinous His focus was on love not justice His focus was on the son s return Even so, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repents. (15:10) Serving God involves more than slavish adherence to a legal code It includes love for one s fellow man We have no right to be selfish when it comes to the love and blessings of God. We should never be upset at another man s good fortune. THE PARABLE Story 3 Luke 15:11-32 Jesus main intent was to illustrate the folly of self-righteousness in contrast to the loving attitude of God THE DEMAND 15:11, 12 God will not always prevent you from stumbling Many adults carry the scars of their teenage years THE DEPRAVITY 15:12 James 4:8 Wasted his money in a generally immoral manner THE DESPERATION 15:14, 15 Moral desolation: tending pigs Leviticus 11:7; Deuteronomy 14:8 THE DEGRADATION 15:16 THE DISCOVERY 15:17-20a The First Step to true repentance: Hopelessness and futility came to himself literally: came into himself He comes to realize that a life of sin offers only destruction The Second Step to true repentance: Brokenness His only hope lies in the merciful, gracious nature of his father The Third Step to true repentance: Action 2 Corinthians 7:9-11 Leave no overturned stone exposed THE DEVIATION 15:20b-21 The father is pictured as anxiously waiting for his willful child Demonstration of humility and conviction Divine offense Personal offense Accept Consequences THE DEMONSTRATION 15:22-24 Signs of full restoration to the status as son Sinful man, alienated from God, are dead in God s eyes THE DECISION 15:25 The elder son was intended to represent the Pharisees and scribes Our view of sinners should be one of great sorrow Did not refer to the younger as brother but your son ============================================= === SERMON: message(142).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/1/2025 10:46:41 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message DID I HEAR THAT RIGHT? LUKE 16:1-9 INTRODUCTION I know you think you understand what you thought I said but I'm not sure you realize that what you heard is not what I meant ? Alan Greenspan We turn to a new chapter in our study of the gospel of Luke. It has taken a while, but we find ourselves in chapter 16 The change also takes us from a very familiar story of The Willful Son to perhaps the most perplexing story in all of Jesus teaching You may have the heading The Unjust Steward in your Scriptures Jesus does not expressly call this a parable; though most commentaries do It is the story of a steward (house or estate manager) who has been called in to give an account for wasting his employer s goods and money. Rather than being tossed to the streets, he calls in several of his master s debtors and adjusts their accounts, thereby making friends for the future. When the master learns of the action, he praises the steward for his prudence. If this was politics, he would have received a promotion The idea of greasing the skids is not new All of us would like to make things easier or smoother We do what we can to reduce friction or resistance When working on trucks with read dual wheels, we would find a piece of sheet metal, poor some heaven oil on it, and slide the tires and hub away from the truck. Cashing in : Biden s energy sec lands cushy gig at major utility her agency boosted with taxpayer cash 2-21-25 The Daily BS Former Energy Secretary Jennifer Granholm is joining the board of a major U.S. utility company that received millions of taxpayer dollars from Granholm s agency on her watch. The problem with the story is that Jesus seems to be put in the position of praising dishonesty, even extolling it as a virtue. Three things to note as we begin: 1. Different time and place. We are far removed from the historical setting and understanding of the listeners. Caution needs to be used in our interpretation. What did this mean to those listening? 2. Divine Illustration. Jesus used examples of unrighteous sinful men. Luke 18:6; Mark 3:27. Jesus is able to use even what is wrong to make a point. He is not glorifying wrong. 3. Determine the main point. I would treat this like a parable and not pick apart every detail. Note the connection with the preceding story He also said to His disciples There is a connection; from the lost and found to gaining possessions The subject of the chapter is the proper attitude toward material wealth waste : same verb in 16:1 as in 15:13 The accusation was made; the coverup is at work Sounds like the national news It has been said that if you throw a rock into a pack of wolves the one that squeals is the one that got hit. It is fascinating that so many express little concern about the misuse of money, but greatly concerned that it is being exposed. It has been recorded that Jesus spoke around 40 parables About one third of those dealt with money in some way Evidently, Jesus knew that the subject of money would be a dominant point of discussion. how to acquire it, how to acquire more of it, how to spend it, how to save it, how to invest it, how to borrow it, counting it, sometimes giving it away, loaning it. Many are climbing the ladder of success only to find out it is leaning against the wrong wall They profit on earth yet lose their soul Luke 16 tells of a guy who is running a scam operation He is out to get your money and will cook the books to get er done Jesus turns His attention from the Pharisees and scribes though they are still listening; 16:14; and will end with a warning; the story of Lazarus, 16:19-31 This story is about discipleship: how disciples are to live Remember: not all who are following Jesus are true disciples At this stage there were still many hanging around but have not place their faith in Jesus Many are still enjoying the benefits of proximity to the Master Luke 16 is discipleship time: how to handle your money God s perspective on riches How great is it that Jesus uses unrighteous men to tell the story The Unjust, Unrighteous Steward (16:8) The Rich Man (16:19) Both are unrighteous, evil, wicked, conniving Both are looking out for themselves Jesus says we can learn even from pagans This guy saw what was coming and prepared for the future A faithful steward will use his Master s money carefully in preparing for eternity This is where your treasure is there will your heart be also is fleshed out This is laying up your treasure in heaven If the ungodly are shrewd, forward looking; how much more so should we who actually have a future to look forward to THE MANAGER Responsibility Steward; house manager; could be a slave or in this case a free man He had full administrator responsibilities of the estate Lands, crops, assets, liabilities In all things he has the right to act on behalf of the landowner Report Not a formal accusation but coming from friends, acquaintances Accused, reported, bring a complaint (1x, NT) Noun diabollos: means slanderer, devil Even used of wife of deacons: 1 Tim. 3:11 not slanderers The charge of mismanagement; squandering, wasting It was a habitual practice of misuse We go from Prodigal Son to Prodigal Steward Throwing away money with no sense of accountability Requirement: give an account An examination and presentation of the records A formal accounting of one s actions Do it right now; you have been exposed The command was not to try and save his job In most companies an infraction of this nature would probably not be accompanied by a two-week notice The owner gave the guy some time to get a report together This is a story and not real life You are a steward of God s possessions It is God who gives the ability to gain wealth Deuteronomy 8:17-18 and thou say in thy heart, My power and the might of my hand has procured me this wealth. But thou shalt remember Jehovah thy God, that it is he who giveth thee power to get wealth, that he may establish his covenant which he swore unto thy fathers, as it is this day. We will all one day be called to give an account 2 Corinthians 5:10 Any reward will bring glory to His name Vance Havner often said, "God called us to play the game, not keep the score." This accounting is not about your sins; that debt has been paid This accounting is how you used the talents, time, money and influence in this life How disappointed will we be when in heaven we realized we could have done so much more? Think of the Prodigal Son and his brokenness over wasting what was entrusted to him Give an account of your stewardship are words we all will hear Life is a stewardship DID I HEAR THAT RIGHT? LUKE 16:1-9 Jesus seems to be put in the position of praising dishonesty Three things to note as we begin: Different time and place Divine Illustration Determine the main point The subject of the chapter is the proper attitude toward material wealth This story is about discipleship Jesus says we can learn even from pagans A faithful steward will use his Master s money carefully in preparing for eternity THE MANAGER Responsibility Report Throwing away money with no sense of accountability Requirement Deuteronomy 8:17-18 We will all one day be called to give an account 2 Corinthians 5:10 This accounting is how you used the talents, time, money and influence in this life Life is a stewardship ============================================= === SERMON: message(143).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/7/2025 12:32:51 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE R.O.I. Luke 16:1-13 INTRODUCTION We are looking at a parable that at first glance seems to be rewarding and encouraging dishonesty. As we saw last week, Jesus is not honoring the dishonesty of the steward but the motivation behind what he did. The point last week is that Life is a Stewardship We are to give an account of the use of our time, talents, money, and influence. THE MANAGER Responsibility Report Requirement Give an Account A crisis does not make a man, but it does show what he is made of Dilemma: soon to have no job, no money, no home Deliberation: talks to himself; what to do? Disinclination: not strong enough to dig; not a disability His lazy lifestyle left him too weak for labor Could not find a shovel to fit his hands There is little doubt that our own country is guilty of making people lazy by making it too easy to live off what Uncle Sam supplies Big flap about cuts to Medicare Dignity: ashamed to beg; the only form of welfare in the ancient world Language of pride; should have been more ashamed of cheating his master than begging for food Dealing 16:4-7 A sudden flash of inspiration; unrighteous and wrong, yet resourceful Present planning to provide a welcome mat for later Let s make a deal Many debt owers but only two mentioned International Standard Bible Encyclopedia: "It would seem that as Israel came into contact with the surrounding nations, debt became increasingly a commercial matter. The Mosaic laws regarding clemency toward the poor who were compelled for the time being to become debtors were utterly disregarded, and the poor were oppressed by the rich." Put each debtor now under obligation to him Scratch me back, I ll scratch yours The words don t mean anything to us but the debts were significant Oil: Measure = 8-9 gallons; 1,000 denarius; common day s wage Wheat: 10-12 bushels; 2,500 denarius; enough wheat to make over 200,000 loaves of bread He had doubtless forgiven debts in the past; normal course of business His dishonesty lies in using his master s goods solely for his own benefit. Steward is clever, shrewd and dishonest 16:8 The commendation comes from his boss, not that of Jesus Not approving of the loss of revenue but the shrewdness of behavior Praised for acting with foresight and insight He clearly calls him unrighteous ; he was still crook, not praise for that The story is an example of prudence, not practice Using present opportunities to prepare for future needs Priorities: if the doctor told you tomorrow, you have six months to live, how would that change your plans? the commendation was not for acting dishonestly, but cleverly Jesus is looking for good men with this man s dishonest business sense Romans 16:19 wise to what is good; innocent to what is evil The story is not about cheating your boss on your way out the door, but looking ahead RESPONSE Four related applications to the parable This is our lesson on the relationship of the Christian to material possessions Lesson #1 16:8 Be Shrewd: common sense The word is morally neutral meaning common sense, prudent the sons of this age the children of this world They are lost, living only for earthly goods not for eternity They can only see time Children of light : Believers are to Live with eternity s values in view Believers should make far better use of opportunities MacArthur: Take care of your future. We hear that incessantly pounded into us. We're subject to all that advertising all the time. Take care of your future, take care of your future, and you go through life and you live and you save and you save and you save and at 65 you quit working and there it is, there's your retirement and you die at 66. What a ridiculous deal that is.
