All those years of sending it forward and sending it forward and when you get there it's a year, or maybe you live three, or maybe you enjoy it for five and then you hit 70 and you can't see like you could see, and you can't hear like you could hear, and you can't taste anything and who cares, you know? Maybe you could buy the car of your dreams, but they take your license away. And you don't want to take a long trip because you don't want to go through the airport lines with a walker. "Alistair Begg tells the story of a couple who had their car stolen. In the morning the car was returned with a note of apology and two tickets to the theatre. The note invited the couple to enjoy a night out as their way of apologizing for stealing their car. The couple took the tickets, went to the show, and when they returned their home had been cleaned out by the robbers! Be honest. Don t you just a little admire the creativity of the crooks? 1 Peter 4:10 we are stewards of the manifold grace of God The yield of the sinner ends with death while the reward of the saved begins with death and lasts forever. Why can t that be our motivation? The story tells us clearly the sons of light do not have a sense of need and urgency We are careful to have regular checkups with our optometrist, we also need correction for spiritual vision If we believe it, we will act upon it Adrian Rogers: We're a generation that is amusing ourselves into oblivion. We live in a day in which a "hero" is a sandwich, "Life" is a magazine, "Power" is a candy bar, "Joy" is a detergent, "My sin" is a perfume, a "Star" is an actress marrried three times, and "The real thing" is a soft drink. What can we learn from an unjust steward? He knew he would be called upon to give an account He took advantage of the present to prepare for the future Ephesians 5:15-17 Therefore watch carefully how you walk, not as unwise, but as wise; redeeming the time, because the days are evil. Therefore don't be foolish, but understand what the will of the Lord is. Lesson #2 16:9 Wealth is to be used I say to you disciples make do it now; don t delay Make friends with the use of unrighteous manna money can buy influence Not of the earthly variety but heavenly friends who will receive you into glory Use your money to make friends with those who will greet you when you get to heaven Lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven The call is to be generous and kind: we will have two examples, bad and good 16:11- the rich man 19:1-10 Zaccheus 1 Thessalonians 2:19, 20 What brought Paul joy? Our first response would be Jesus And to be sure there is the assurance of being in the presence of Jesus, but he adds that it would also be seeing all the believers he ministered to in Thessalonia. 2 Corinthians 9:12-14 as a result of your ministry they will bring glory to God There was a great need and the church folks of Corinth provided 1 Timothy 6:17-19 eternal investments with dividends mammon of unrighteousness used by the world for unholy purposes, but greatly blessed through the hands of God s people Thank you for giving to the Lord. I am a life that was changed Thank you for giving to the Lord. I am so glad you gave when it fails not if, but when. It will fail someday Proverbs 23:4,5 Don't weary yourself to be rich. In your wisdom, show restraint. Why do you set your eyes on that which is not? For it certainly sprouts wings like an eagle and flies in the sky. But think of your R.O.I. Return On Investment Infinitely valuable forever Only one life, twill soon be past, Only what s done for Christ will last. -- C. T. Studd William Barclay says that if only the Christian was as eager and ingenious in attaining goodness as the man of the world is in his drive to attain money and comfort. We must pray for the lost but we can also pay for the lost Invest in ministries, people, agencies who are actively in that business When earth becomes our heaven -- when we see God's blessings as being primarily immediate and temporal -- we lose sight of who we are, why we are here, and what awaits us beyond the horizons of this world. * Randy Alcorn Lesson #3 16:10-12 God notes our faithfulness Our faithfulness: reveals our motivations Luke 12:42; 1 Cor. 4:2 requirements of stewards Contrast two types of wealth The worldly level of materialism The true riches in spiritual values Do we live by the flesh or the Spirit? Romans 8:5, 6 The one who proves unfaithful with another s wealth has no right to claim anything of his own If we prove unfaithful in this God-given task, what right do we have to claim our heavenly reward, that which is ours forever? 1 Cor, 3:21-23; Luke 12:20, 21 Lesson #4 16:13 Impossible to Serve Two Masters Slavery is not a part-time relationship A slave is obligated to serve his master at any and all times The Christian must yield himself totally to the service of God There is really no such a thing as part-time Christianity We must keep our priorities straight. Wealth is to be used, not served If all our efforts are to go to serving God, then the proper use of wealth is in service to God, no self In a sense the Unjust Steward is an illustration of this, for he certainly did not worry about wealth, but rather used it as a tool for his own self-preservation. The Christian does not serve self and therefore any wealth he controls should be used for God s purposes Matt. 6:24; 1 Tim. 6:6-10 The song writer put it this way: 1. Take my life and let it be Consecrated, Lord, to Thee. Take my moments and my days, Let them flow in endless praise. 2. Take my hands and let them move At the impulse of Thy love. Take my feet and let them be Swift and beautiful for Thee. 3. Take my voice and let me sing, Always, only for my King. Take my lips and let them be Filled with messages from Thee. 4. Take my silver and my gold, Not a mite would I withhold. Take my intellect and use Every pow r as Thou shalt choose. 5. Take my will and make it Thine, It shall be no longer mine. Take my heart, it is Thine own, It shall be Thy royal throne. 6. Take my love, my Lord, I pour At Thy feet its treasure store. Take myself and I will be Ever, only, all for Thee. A Puritan Prayer: Turn my heart from vanity, from dissatisfactions, from uncertainties of the present state, to an eternal interest in Christ. Let me remember that life is SHORT AND UNFORESEEN, and is only an OPPORTUNITY FOR USEFULNESS; GIVE ME A HOLY AVARICE TO REDEEM THE TIME , to awake at every call to charity and piety, so that I may feed the hungry, clothe the naked, instruct the ignorant, reclaim the vicious, forgive the offender, diffuse the Gospel, show neighborly love to all. Let me live a life of self-distrust, dependence on Thy Spirit, mortification, crucifixion, prayer." In Jesus' Name. Amen (From Valley of Vision) R.O.I. Luke 16:1-13 THE MANAGER Responsibility Report Requirement Dilemma Deliberation Disinclination Dignity Dealing 16:4-7 Praised for acting with foresight and insight Using present opportunities to prepare for future needs RESPONSE Lesson #1 16:8 Be Shrewd Common sense Live with eternity s values in view 1 Peter 4:10 The yield of the sinner ends with death The reward of the saved begins with death Ephesians 5:15-17 Lesson #2 16:9 Wealth is to Be Used Use your money to make friends with those who will greet you when you get to heaven 1 Thessalonians 2:19, 20 2 Corinthians 9:12-14 1Timothy 6:17-19 Proverbs 23:4,5 Lesson #3 16:10-12 God Notes Our Faithfulness Romans 8:5, 6 Lesson #4 16:13 Impossible to Serve Two Masters Wealth is to be used, not served ============================================= === SERMON: message(144).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 3/22/2025 10:44:02 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: MESSAGE I ANTICIPATE THIS TO BE A TWO-PARTER ATTITUDE ADJUSTMENT Luke 16:14-18 INTRODUCTION I guess if have finally arrived at the age where I am tempted to say, Well, back in my day. No, I did not walk to school uphill both ways. Yes, I did drink out of a garden hose, rode my bike without a helmet, sat in the front seat of a car without a seatbelt, drank juice from a wax bottle, had candy cigarettes, would go for a day-long bike ride without any means of being tracked, or calling home. We talked on rotary phones mounted to a wall in the kitchen, talked to an operator, made collect calls, used pay phones, looked up stuff in Encyclopedias, knew TV channels signed off at midnight, put tinfoil on the TV antenna, had to walk across the room to change the channel, had a bike with a banana seat and baseball cards attached to the spokes, wore bell bottom pants, had a transistor radio, used a manual typewriter, shifted the transmission with 3-on-the-tree, watch a movie at the drive-in, and drove station wagons with wood-grain paneling. There was no McDonalds, businesses closed on Sundays, you could leave your house unlocked, churches were packed on Easter and Christmas, there were no interstate highways, and everyone stood when the flag passed by. No air conditioning in your car, no cruise control, paid extra for an automatic transmission and electric windows, and seldom did a car get to 100 K. They were the old days, but not necessarily the good old days Cigarette smoking was everywhere, people died of causes easily preventable today, segregation was real, wars were still fought, liberal theology was a problem. Before the Lord saved me, I was part of the beginning of a Christian Coffee House in my hometown. My understanding of theology was so limited in some regards I got sucked into it. They would have music, refreshments, and some attempt of sharing the Scriptures. I have to dig deep to remember much about the goings on or what I role I even had. But one thing I remember was on occasion as a group was gathered, someone would call out, Attitude Adjustment. The response was Praise the Lord. I have no idea why I remember that, but it came to mind in preparing today s message. The real topic of Luke 16 is attitude adjustment. This is what began the discussion with the Pharisees in the first place, for Jesus was not willing to tolerate their self-righteous attitude toward sinners. (15:1, 2) He is now expanding the teaching to include the proper use of wealth. Jesus moves from a teaching time directed to the disciples (16:1) back to a confrontation with the Pharisees. THE RELIGIOUS ELITE 16:14 They were listening alright. Not to learn anything, not to heed and change, but to ridicule This section is about questioning the authority of Jesus There used to see a t-shirt that said, Question Authority The problem of the Pharisees is exposed: they only appear to keep the Law The appearance of religion and a closeness to God may not be an accurate reflection of a person s response to the authority of God They had no interest in laying up for others 16:9 Covetous wealth was to be desired as a sign of God s favor lovers of silver ; there was no paper money Last week: wealth is not a sign of God s blessing and poverty is not a sign of God s disfavor. Two things: Following biblical principles is no guarantee of being rich Wealth can be a major stumbling block to spiritual living When Jesus said you cannot (v.13) that is exactly what He meant Jesus was saying that a man must not give himself to seek the comfort and ease and pleasures and possessions of the world. Jesus was going against the philosophy of the world, a philosophy that had even permeated religious circles: that money and possessions are a sign of the blessings of God. That definitely was the case for the Pharisees God is smiling on me today, because of my wonderful bank account There can be no greater hypocrisy than to appear to be pious merely to gain the acclaim of men. Their reaction to the teaching time: sneering, scoffing, ridiculing Worded as their usual response; not the first time Of course we love God! But we love our money also. How dare you say we can t to both. Prov. 13:1 a scoffer does not listen to rebuke Prov. 15:12 a scoffer does not love one who reproves him A scoffer will honor God with their lips, but will live like the world A person can appear to most religious, but have a heart full of covetousness Philippians 3:18, 19 THE RELIGIOUS INSPECTION 16:15 Key to passage: God knows your heart For the hypocrite this fact is unbearable, for he realizes that his actions fool neither himself nor God God know the attitudes and the motives Exposed by the Word of God Hebrews 4:12, 13 sharper than a two-edge sword, piercing; discern between the thoughts and intents; the thoughts and motives behind the thoughts And there is no creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him with whom we have to do. Jesus lays bare the fundamental sin of the Pharisees: they sought to justify themselves Only God justifies; declares a person righteous The Pharisees worked at it: prayer, fasting, almsgiving In reality, they were false, fake, and phone They looked good outside but there was no transformation of the heart The problem was that they were too righteous for God; they felt they did not need His grace because they were already perfect. Their self-righteousness puffed them up with vanity making them unable to receive God s grace. It is easy to justify yourself when you are using the wrong comparison God s view and man s view are different S. Cole: The instant you get a glimpse of God in His absolute holiness, the light of His glory also shows you how vile and filthy your own heart is before Him. Rather than boasting in your good deeds and parading your supposed righteousness before others, you shrink back in fear of being instantly consumed. You despair of ever being righteous enough to present yourself before God, because you know how deceitful and desperately wicked your heart is. That s why we need to gospel. We need a Savior and Mediator We need a righteousness other than our own. Problem addressed by Paul Romans 13:1-3 Brethren, my heart s desire and my prayer to God for them (THE JEWS) is for their salvation. 2 For I testify about them that they have a zeal for God, but not in accordance with knowledge. 3 For not knowing about God s righteousness (IN OTHER WORDS THEY DON'T UNDERSTAND HOW HOLY GOD IS AND THAT HE REQUIRES ABSOLUTE PERFECT RIGHTEOUSNESS IN SINFUL MEN) and seeking to establish their own (LIKE THE PHARISEES IN Lk 16:15 ), they did not subject themselves to the righteousness of God. Self-righteousness claims to be both law giver and judge We must judge our sin on the thought level highly esteemed among men - being conformed to this world The value system of this world is an abomination to God; i.e. detestable Originally meaning that which offends the sense of smell; it stinks Don t let the world squeeze into its mold RELIGIOUS EVOLUTION 16:16 This is a tuffy The Law and Prophets until John many insertions suggested were are were proclaimed were in force were in effect The Pharisees had access to the truth I think we can identify two main points First: The preaching of the gospel ushered in a new era Law and prophets shorthand for the OT Jesus is saying that time period is over No longer would men be able to appear righteous because of their adherence to the Law while their motives and hearts were wrong. So guess what Pharisees, your gig is up Second: The Law still stands Paul declared the Law was holy . . .spiritual . . . good Rom. 7:12, 14, 16 It would be easier for the natural order to become void than for the Law to change in the slightest A tittle was the tiny extension, or a horn , on some letters of the Hebrew alphabet. The Greek has the iota subscript; a tiny symbol at the bottom of a letter The standard of righteousness before God and other men has not changed. Jesus came to fulfill every requirement of the Law He was not out to destroy it, to undercut it, to short cut it, to alter it (Matt. 5:17-20) 3 Different Types of Law in the OT Moral Law: 10 Commandments purpose of pleasing God and others Still in effect; morals are morals Civil Law: governing the nation as a nation Do not apply today because the church is not a state Great principles for those who do govern Ceremonial Law: religious festivals and temple worship Abolished as all pointed to and fulfilled in Christ John performed a transitional ministry: He preached the promise of the OT and he preached fulfillment He preached the coming and he preached the appearance He preached the good news of the Kingdom The Kingdom is God s rule in action The Jews were looking for a King But before that can happen - Luke 17:20, 21 is within you The kingdom was more than just declaring and obeying God s Word found in the OT The kingdom is now something you enter into Now what does it mean to enter the kingdom violently? Some translation use a passive verb: subject is the recipient of the action is urged to enter it Others use an active verb: the subject initiates the action is forcing his way in is pressing into it Basically: Everyone is greatly urged to enter the kingdom Or: Everyone is forcing their way into the kingdom Entering the Kingdom is a forceful, radical act It requires a renouncing of self and dedication to God It is a hard and difficult struggle You present your body as a living sacrifice You deny yourself, take up your cross daily, follow True repentance involves the will acting in self-denial The tax collectors and sinners, for instance, had to jump over the roadblocks set up by the Pharisees It is incompatible with the hypocrisy which Jesus was attacking in the Pharisees Matt. 11:12-15 Even though the Pharisees reject the message of John and now Jesus, many are embracing it Those who wish to be saved must imitate those who take the city by storm. Rush into it without delay - Calvin Remember how the crowds gathered around Jesus so much He retreated to a boat Jesus is saying to the Pharisees, While you guys sit around scoffing, the very people whom you despise are stampeding into the kingdom! S. Cole: If you are not saved, nothing in life should matter to you more than how you can get saved. You won t accidentally get saved while you devote yourself to everything else under the sun, but never devote any effort to understanding spiritual matters. While we are not saved by our efforts, but only through faith in the blood of Jesus, the mark of a person who has come to genuine saving faith is a subsequent life of increasing submission to the Lord Jesus Christ. RELIGIOUS ILLUSTRATION 16:18 This statement looks totally out of place What is Jesus after? Why did look include it here? This is not a teaching on divorce Obligation of the moral law is still enforce Here is an example of the binding nature of the Law You Pharisees really do have a problem with covetousness You covet another s woman The Pharisees cannot make the claim Jesus destroyed the Law There is no violation in accepting the harlots, publicans and sinners There is no violation of the clean and unclean Pharisees strictly observed the lesser duties to avoid pollution They are the ones violating the Law through breaking the 7th commandment Jesus is to make His point even more forcefully about correct attitudes Divorce would have been a vivid example of the pharisaic attitude toward the Law; their own lust was looking for a way to get around it The main teaching of Jesus was that of maintaining a right relationship to God and to men That was the true purpose of the Law Divorce was an example of how this concept could be twisted and yet retain the appearance of legality. The Jews could easily divorce their wives (Deut. 24:1) But to do so was a striking example of disregard for one s relationship to God and to his fellow man You must violate a covenant made before God, a spouse, and other men So, Men are great at seeking a work-around of any passage of Scripture that interferes with the way they want to live their life. Scripture elsewhere calls it the hardness of the heart The Scriptures are about maintaining a right relationship to God and men Jesus says He is not the one guilty of destroying the Law and Prophets The Pharisees are doing that through their own disobedience This is not the time for us to do an intensive study of divorce Jeff did that in Sunday School recently and Jesus is using it here for an illustration of an unrighteous attitude by violating the Law and Prophets Our position is the so called Exception Clause was understood by the listeners as sexual activity prior to marriage But if we truly allowed Scripture to interpret Scripture, think of the poor bloke who only had Luke 16. He would know nothing of some imaginary loophole and think he had to remain married. ATTITUDE ADJUSTMENT Luke 16:14-18 THE RELIGIOUS ELITE 16:14 This section is about questioning the authority of Jesus Covetous lovers of silver Following biblical principles is no guarantee of being rich Wealth can be a major stumbling block to spiritual living THE RELIGIOUS INSPECTION 16:15 Key to passage: God knows your heart Hebrews 4:12, 13 It is easy to justify yourself when you are using the wrong comparison We need a righteousness other than our own. Romans 13:1-3 highly esteemed among men - being conformed to this world RELIGIOUS EVOLUTION 16:16 The preaching of the gospel ushered in a new era The Law still stands It would be easier for the natural order to become void than for the Law to change in the slightest 3 Different Types of Law in the OT Moral Law: 10 Commandments Civil Law: governing the nation as a nation Ceremonial Law: religious festivals and temple worship The Kingdom is God s rule in action Everyone is greatly urged to enter the kingdom OR Everyone is forcing their way into the kingdom Entering the Kingdom requires a renouncing of self and dedication to God Those who wish to be saved must imitate those who take the city by storm. Rush into it without delay - Calvin RELIGIOUS ILLUSTRATION 16:18 Obligation of the moral law is still enforced The Pharisees cannot make the claim Jesus destroyed the Law The main teaching of Jesus was that of maintaining a right relationship to God and to men Men are great at seeking a work-around of any passage of Scripture that interferes with the way they want to live their life. ============================================= === SERMON: message(145).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/4/2025 8:57:58 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message PROFESSION AND PRACTICE 1 Samuel 16; Malachi 1 INTRODUCTION For the past two weeks, I have mentioned taking a Rabbit Trail from our text in Luke 16. This is the week we are going to explore that Trail. At a previous time, I mentioned the favorite Bible verse of those who are outside the Christian family. That verse is in Matthew 7 where it says, Judge not lest ye be judged. It is strategically thrown around as if that is supposed to end any argument to which they disagree. It is meant to shut up the opposition. We know they use the verse as their proof text to elevate their superior position. What it does is tell us they have no idea what they are talking about. The abuse and misuse of Scripture is nothing new. I have seen several video presentations lately with someone presenting an argument from Scripture, but it only shows their attempt of God s approval with willful disobedience and ignorance. They are looking for a way to sooth a guilty conscience. Unfortunately, proof texting is not limited to unbelievers. We are going to see one today. This application of the verse began during the church growth movement and has permeated the church. It is interesting that the verse has been around for thousands of years but had no such application until the 1970 s. There was no such teaching when I grew up, in fact, it was the opposite. It has been heavy on my heart for some time, but I decided to not take time out from a series but to wait until a verse warranted such a discussion. We now have it in Luke 16:15 God knows your hearts God knows your heart. 1 Kings 8:39, 40; (Proverbs 24:12) From the message: It is easy to justify yourself when you are using the wrong comparison That which is highly esteemed among men stinks before God God gives us grace not to do what we want, but to do what we ought Titus 3:5 The grace of God that brings salvation has appeared to all men, teaching them to deny ungodliness and worldly lusts, but to live soberly, righteously, godly in the present age PROFESSION 1 Samuel 16 I have noticed through the years, that many believers also have a favorite, go-to verse. 1 Samuel 16:7 But God said unto Samuel, Look not on his countenance, or on the height of his stature; because I have rejected him: for God sees not as man sees; for man looks on the outward appearance, but God looks on the heart. This was the proof text of the church growth movement leading to great advertising slogans like: come as you are casual dress, serious faith The only thing the verse says is that the prophet only was using physical appearance in evaluating the suitability for a man to be a king. God has the unique ability to look on the inside. And What caught Samuel s attention: countenance and his height He was a good looking, tall dude I am glad God accepts the good looking, the homely, the white, the black, the brown, the blondes, the brunettes, the bald, the short, and tall The verse has nothing to do with what he was wearing But that is how the verse is applied God cared how Adam and Eve were covered. God cared how the High Priest was dressed in the performance of His duties God cared how all the priests were dressed including the wearing of shorts to cover their nakedness. (If you have never done a study on nakedness, you should) The church growth movement used the verse for the purpose of getting people into the building. It was not based on Scripture. Their mode of operation was to do surveys to see why people did not come to church; what bothered them; and to eliminate such offenses. You don t want to dress up; we have a verse for that! I know the immediate reaction; but I could dress up and be every bit the hypocrite the Pharisees were. That is true. But is that the comparison you really want to make? 16:15 justified themselves before men What is highly esteemed among men stinks in the sight of God The NT is clear We can know a person s heart by observing them Matthew 7:16 you will know them by their fruit Every word and action is fruit from our heart; your heart is on display Matthew 22:11-13 The Parable of the Wedding Feast But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw a man there who did not have on a wedding garment. and he said to him, 'Friend, how did you come in here not wearing wedding clothing?' He was speechless. Then the king said to the servants, 'Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and throw him into the outer darkness; there is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.' My plea is not about a dress code. My plea: Don t proof text. 1 Samuel 16:7 has been in the inspired Scriptures for a long time Not until the church growth movement was it used to please the lost It was all part of dumbing down Christianity seeking to make it more palatable for the lost. What it did was destroy the doctrine of the holiness of God; the absolute purity of God. It is robbing God of the glory rightly His. The lost were not called to deny themselves and take up a cross They were told to come as you are because God s love is unconditional Several months ago, an Amazon delivery arrived as we returned home from church. The fellow made a comment about our wearing our Sunday best I have not heard that for a long time Some of us grew up hearing, those are your Sunday clothes The church growth movement left us with three problems Sloppy Doctrine, Sloppy Worship, Sloppy Dress That is why I implore you when reading Leviticus look beyond the sacrifices and see God. God has not changed. He is jealous of His glory. He was particular with the presentation of offerings, both animal and grain; incense preparation Everything commandment was consistent with His glory It is no wonder He will determine how we are dressed when we get to glory But remember, you will be dressed in your own righteousnesses Rev. 19:8 The Pharisees needed an attitude adjustment They were careless in how they handled the Scriptures They twisted the application to fit their own agenda God knew their motives; He knew their heart PRACTICE Malachi 1: 6-14 The prophet uses a general principle from daily life and applies it to a believers attitude which they ought to assume toward God. Here is an earthly analogy applied to Divine relationships He addresses the reproof to the priests as they are the ones which should a pattern to the people in obedience and holiness God reminds us of the reverence due to himself from the fear and honor expected by them They were guilty of cheap religion which costs them nothing But Such a religion is costly in that it is rejected by God It is a lawless, thoughtless spirit. It is the sin of irreverence: a lack of respect for the presence, power and majesty of God. God has a right to expect from us the fullest tribute of veneration which we can offer Irreverence in Malachi's days was shown by the character of the offerings made to God They were offering to God that which cost them nothing So, we fall into the trap of believing that if my heart is right, it matters little of the external circumstances when we worship Do we really demonstrate reverence for God s presence? The infection of such irreverence can spread slowly, silently, and universally Malachi presents to us a good question, God asks, where is My honor? Would you do the same for your governor? Would he accept you? Would he accept your leftovers? Wouldn t he expect the proper decor? We just saw that on Zelensky s last visit to the White House. He was criticized for not dressing up. (John Fetterman; hoody and shorts) I have seen folks dress up for a wedding on Saturday and dress down for church on Sunday. Do you have more respect for the couple than God? I have seen guys dress nicer to play golf than they do for church They make no complaint when a shirt with collar is required by the golf course but any such recommendation by a church is suddenly legalism. Is it legalism when the dress code at work is business or business casual? Why are we so willing to adopt to a work requirement but when we worship God, we pick up a tee shirt off the floor, give a quick sniff test, and out the door we go with the excuse it is no big deal because God looks at the heart God IS looking at your heart. Nothing escapes His notice. He is asking the question, Where is My honor; Where is My fear? We serve the same God who appeared on the mountain to the children of Israel by lightening, thunder, cloud and fire Sloppy Doctrine gave us God loves us Unconditionally It also gave us proof texting for a sloppy approach to a glorious God. Rightly divide the Word of Truth; that is all I am seeking. We make the Right Profession; let s do the Right Practice With lip we call him Master, In life oppose his Word, We ev'ry day deny him, And yet we call him Lord! No more is our religion Like His in soul or deed Than painted grain on canvas Is like the living seed. In the balance we are weigh'd, And wanting we are found, In all that's true and Christly the universe around. PROFESSION AND PRACTICE 1 Samuel 16; Malachi 1 It is easy to justify yourself when you are using the wrong comparison That which is highly esteemed among men stinks before God Titus 3:5 PROFESSION 1 Samuel 16 What caught Samuel s attention: countenance and his height The verse has nothing to do with what he was wearing We can know a person s heart by observing them Matthew 7:16 Don t proof text God has not changed. He is jealous of His glory Everything commandment was consistent with His glory PRACTICE Malachi 1: 6-14 Here is an earthly analogy applied to Divine relationships Such a religion is costly in that it is rejected by God It is the sin of irreverence They were offering to God that which cost them nothing The infection of such irreverence can spread slowly, silently, and universally God IS looking at your heart Where is My honor; Where is My fear? ============================================= === SERMON: message(146).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/12/2025 11:23:03 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message Hope are feeling better SHOULD HAVE KNOWN Luke 19:42 INTRODUCTION On this date in 1950 the number 1 song was If I knew you were coming, I d have baked a cake. And for full disclosure, the number 1 song for the year of 1950 was Rudolph the Red Nosed Reindeer. Somewhere in your time upon this earth you have heard or used the statement, If only I had known. Many times, it is used to ease anxiety over missing out on a significant event. It could have been anything ranging from a birthday to funeral. We did not know, we were never informed, we were not in a position to be made aware of something that had happened. I am sure there are times the statement is made to excuse ourselves from something we really didn t want to do anyway. And other times, we use it to put the blame on another person who we think should have made us aware of what is going on. If I had only known . . As if to make the person think we really would have readily responded to such a notice. OR it has also been used to justify a decision when something is not what we thought it would be. If I had known it was going to be this bad, I would not have come or save my money or warned others What we are saying is that if we had had additional information, it would have changed our decision making, our behavior, our schedule; to the place where life would have much different. Each of the gospel writers record for us the events of what our calendar calls Palm Sunday. I would like to look at Luke s account in chapter 19. Verses 41-44 is Luke s record of the words of Jesus as He weeps over the city of Jerusalem. v. 42 If you had known, even you, especially in this your day, the things that make for your peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes. How did Jesus use this statement as He lamented over the lostness of the people of the city and, in fact, the entire nation of Israel. A TURNING POINT If only you had known .. The implication from Jesus is that the people should have known The people could have known It is fascinating that all the pronouns in this passage are not plural (as one might expect) but singular, as if He is addressing the personified city of Jerusalem as representative of the entire Jewish nation. Any result from their lack of knowledge would be realized upon the whole Jesus came unto His own and His own received Him not Although there were a few who responded to the message of the kingdom, the majority did not And we know that even those who were singing about coronation today would be shouting crucify Him by the end of the week. In this your day literally: in the day of yours, this one This is a huge day for the nation This is not just any day but a very specific, unique day, a day the Jews could have and should have known! Jesus is presenting Himself as King fulfilling the OT prophecies The OT records over 300 Right down to this very day, Behold, your King is coming to you; He is just and having salvation, Lowly and riding on a donkey, A colt, the foal of a donkey. (Zechariah 9:9) A TELLING POINT I say Telling point because the people are now fully exposed They can t hide behind their traditions, their twisting of the Scriptures The leaders can t hide behind their robes, the exalted positions They are being told on No Messiah Now a single word but announcing a curtain drop Jesus is announcing the final scene of a play He is announcing the beginning of a time of judicial spiritual blindness This is one of the most tragic verses in all of Scripture You don t want to see, so I will make it so you can t see You don t recognize your Messiah; so now you cannot recognize your Messiah No Peace The City of Peace had no clue what it took to have real peace Ultimately, it is not what things make for peace but Who makes for peace Messiah is the Prince of Peace; Isaiah 9:6 But becomes a word of contrast What could have been vs. the consequences of rejecting truth Such will be the state of the nation until Messiah returns Individuals will be saved National Peace: the Peace of Jerusalem for which we pray Romans 11:26-29; Zechariah 12:10 Note the irony. Jesus weeps at His first Triumphal Entry; the nation at His Second The word to us is to be ready We do not know, nor can we know the time of the Lord s return We have seen and will see the warning of alertness required of us Luke 12:37 Blessed are those servants whom the master, when he comes, will find watching Luke 21:34 TEACHING POINT How can Jesus say, they should have known, they could have known? How can Scripture be so definitive as to say, in this your day ? Are we looking at a specific timeline or just in the ballpark somewhere? Adrian Rogers: "Jesus came on time; He died on time; He was buried on time; He rose on time; and He is coming on time. You can just bank on it." When is the day, this one ? not just any day but a very specific, unique day, And why should they have known ? The day was foretold in Daniel 9:24-27 the 70 Weeks of Daniel a critical text for our discussion A great prophecy is given to Daniel because of his great prayer; vs. 3-19 He is making intercession before God on himself personally and the nation Why? He had been reading Jeremiah; vs. 1, 2 There is much debate over the interpretation of the prophecy Much of it because of the tendency to spiritualize, allegorize, or take away from the literal reading of the text. 9:24-27 9:25 know and understand ; know and discern expresses the idea of knowing the reason for something by looking at it or giving attention to it. It is collecting the pieces into a puzzle and putting them together A puzzle may require some work, but it can be done; God is the revelator There was sufficient material here that the Jews in Jesus day should have and could have known We will summarize weeks is a term meaning 7 s; not the usual word for a week v. 24 Seventy 7 s; v. 25 seven 7 s; sixty two 7 s v. 27 one 7 490 years; 49 years; 483 years; 7 years The starting point is the issuing of a decree to restore and rebuild Jerusalem which conservative scholars agree was Artaxerxes' decree in Nehemiah 2:1-8 . Then after 62 sevens plus 7 sevens or 483 years Messiah the Prince would enter Jerusalem and allow the Jews for the first time to proclaim Him as their King, the so-called "Triumphal Entry." The Messiah would be cut off which refers to His crucifixion. It was the study of this passage by Rabbi Leopold that led to his conversion and the beginning of Chosen People Ministries. A man who was part of that ministry for years left and began Jews for Jesus. Their study of the timeline led to the realization that the Messiah came 2,000 years ago Rabbi Cohn asked himself, Is it possible that the time which God had fixed for the appearance of our Messiah had passed away without the promise of our true and Living God being fulfilled? He was encouraged to come to America and he was saved The 69 weeks (seven and sixty-two) were fulfilled in Luke 19:41-44 Reginald Showers of the Friends of Israel Gospel Ministry writes that Daniel 9:24-27 is "one of the most significant prophecies in all the Old Testament Scriptures" and is also "one of the strongest biblical evidences to the effect that Jesus of Nazareth is the Messiah....What significant thing happened to Jesus, the Messiah, on April 6, 32 AD? When referring to the end of the first 483 years, Gabriel said: until Messiah the Prince. Whatever happened to Jesus on April 6, 32 AD, it must have been related significantly to His being the Prince, the King of Israel. He would be identified by arriving on the foal of a donkey Brother Showers: Jesus language indicated that that particular day had been marked out by God as the time of Jerusalem s visitation by her Messiah Prince-the day which could have brought lasting peace to that city if its people had accepted Jesus for who He was. It also indicated that the Jews should have recognized that that particular day (April 6, 32 AD) was the day on which Messiah would visit Jerusalem as Prince. Why should they have recognized this? Because several centuries earlier in Daniel 9:25 , God had revealed the exact time" CONCLUSION This passage has brought many to salvation When once they were blind, now they see There are still many people blind to what makes for peace Romans 5:1 Therefore, having been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ Are you willing to ask, what is it that blinds me in this my day ? If you had known culpable responsibility; God holds us responsible He has given to us everything we need for life and godliness There is a day when we will know; we will see 1 Corinthians 13:12 For now we see in a mirror dimly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known. Until then: May our prayer be Open my eyes so that I may behold wondrous things from Thy Law SHOULD HAVE KNOWN Luke 19:42 A TURNING POINT The people should have known; The people could have known This is not just any day but a very specific, unique day A TELLING POINT No Messiah The beginning of a time of judicial spiritual blindness No Peace it is not what things make for peace but Who makes for peace What could have been vs. the consequences of rejecting truth National Peace: Romans 11:26-29; Zechariah 12:10 Jesus weeps at His first Triumphal Entry; the nation weeps at His Second Luke 12:37 TEACHING POINT Daniel 9:24-27 9:25 know and discern weeks is a term meaning 7 s; The starting point restore and rebuild Jerusalem Nehemiah 2:1-8 . The 69 weeks were fulfilled in Luke 19:41-44 SHOULD HAVE KNOWN Luke 19:42 A TURNING POINT The people __________ have known; The people __________ have known This is not just any day but a very specific, ____________ day A TELLING POINT No Messiah The beginning of a time of judicial ____________ blindness No Peace it is not what things make for peace but _________ makes for peace What could have been vs. the _______________ of rejecting truth National Peace: _____________ 11:26-29; Zechariah 12:10 Jesus weeps at His first Triumphal Entry; the ________ weeps at His Second Luke 12:37 TEACHING POINT ____________ 9:24-27 9:25 know and discern weeks is a term meaning ________ The starting point is the _____________ of Jerusalem Nehemiah 2:1-8 . The 69 weeks were _____________ in Luke 19:41-44 ============================================= === SERMON: message(147).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/19/2025 10:23:36 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message RESURRECTION AT WORK Philippians 3:9-11 INTRODUCTION Acts 23:6 Every saint s great hope is in the resurrection of the dead!" Our hope is not a hope so but a hope sure (1 Peter 1:3) Sir Walter Raleigh wrote on the night before he was beheaded: From this earth, this grave, this dust, my God shall raise me up! In John 11, Martha knew from the OT there would be a resurrection in which she had hope for her brother Lazarus. Jesus moved the subject from doctrine to personal: I AM the RESURRECTION and the LIFE. He who believes in Me will live even if he dies. (11:25) Jesus took the resurrection from an It to an I John 14:19 Because I live, you shall live also Philippians 3:21 He will transform the body of our humble state into conformity with the body of His glory For believers, death is not a period but a comma Psalm 17:15 David: I will be satisfied with Thy likeness when I awake Job 19:26 Even after my skin is destroyed, yet from my flesh I shall see God. All of God s attributes will be on display for our consideration for all eternity Spurgeon: His works, His gifts, His love to us, and His glory in all His purposes, and in all His actions, these shall make an eternal theme which will be ever new. 1 John 3:2 Beloved, now we are children of God, and it has not appeared as yet what we shall be. We know that, when He appears, we shall be like Him, because we shall see Him just as He is. John Stott: Christianity is in its very essence a RESURRECTION religion. The concept of the RESURRECTION lies at its heart. If you remove it, Christianity is destroyed. The resurrection of Jesus Christ after making His Atonement demonstrates clearly that His offering was acceptable to the Father. On the cross Jesus cried out It is finished and on Sunday morning the Father said, Amen The whole system of Christianity rests upon the fact that Christ is risen from the dead. (Spurgeon 1 Corinthians 15: If Christ is not risen, we are of all men most miserable If Christ was not risen, we would have every right to question His deity Romans 4:25 Our justification is directly linked to the resurrection He was delivered up for our transgressions, and was raised for our justification. Our regeneration is connected to His resurrection; 1 Peter 1:3 born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead. Jesus announced His victory: Revelation 1:17, 18 I am the first and the last, and the living One; and I was dead, and behold, I am alive forevermore, and I have the keys of death and of Hades. GLORIOUS DAY 1 Corinthians 15 Death is swallowed up in victory! One day the grave could conceal Him no longer. One day the stone rolled away from the door. Then He arose over death He had conquered. Now He's ascended, My Lord evermore. Living He loved me, Dying He saved me, Buried He carried, My sins far away! Rising He justified, Freely forever. One day He's coming. Oh glorious day! Oh glorious day! Whenever we gather around the grave of a believer in the Lord Jesus, we do so with the promise from God that that very grave will open one day and in the twinkling of an eye that mortal, corruptible body will be changed and will become immortal and uncorruptible. Therefore, we are sorrowful, but not like those who have no hope. That is the future for all of us who have place our trust in Jesus sacrifice on the cross for our sins. He took on our sins; we received His righteousness. But does the resurrection have anything to do with my present life? It is wonderful to have such promises for the future, but what about my day-to-day life? What does the resurrection mean to me? VICTORIOUS DAY Ephesians 1:15-20 1:3-14 is one long sentence and based on the truth cited there, Paul lets the Ephesian believers know how he is praying for them 1:15-20 the surpassing greatness of His POWER toward us who believe is the same RESURRECTION POWER that brought Christ up from the grave and is now operative within all those who belong to Christ (Ro 6:5 , 8-12 -note , cf Php 3:10-11 -note ). In no uncertain terms, Paul tells his desire for this church: 3 things Know the hope of His calling The riches of His glory in the inheritance The surpassing greatness of His power It all begins with the opening the eyes of the heart How is that going to happen? The Word and Prayer Philippians 3:10 that I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death; Amplified: [For my determined purpose is] that I may know Him [that I may progressively become more deeply and intimately acquainted with Him, perceiving and recognizing and understanding the wonders of His Person more strongly and more clearly], and that I may in that same way come to know the power outflowing from His resurrection [by which it exerts over believers], and that I may so share His sufferings as to be continually transformed [in spirit into His likeness even] to His death, [in the hope] Here is Paul revealing the eyes of his heart The order here is important: That I may know Him The word know is an intimate knowledge This is not knowledge needed for salvation; Paul was saved and had been active in ministry The word know was used in the OT: Adam knew Eve and she conceived NT: Joseph did not know Mary until after the birth of Jesus For this power to be effective in our lives, there must be a desire to pursue knowing Jesus. We read His Word; it is the revelation of Himself that He wanted us to know. Everything we need for life and godliness Divine power begins with knowing Jesus John Piper: Education about God precedes and serves exultation in God. Learning truth precedes loving truth. Right reflection on God precedes right affection for God. Seeing the glory of Christ precedes savoring the glory of Christ. Good theology is the foundation of great doxology. But Paul wanted to live a righteous life. He just said that all that he had previously pursued was rubbish He knew a righteous life was first found in knowing Jesus But a key element is knowing the power of the resurrection What is the connection between that power and the life I now live? NEW DAY Romans 6:3-5 When we received Jesus as Savior we identified (baptized) into His death We died; Galatians 2:20 we have been crucified with Christ We also identify with Christ s resurrection with a new life old things have passed away, all things become new (2 Cor. 5:17) We are now enabled to live a brand-new quality of life we were never able to do before The power of the resurrection enables me to life on a higher level of obedience according to the righteousness of Christ The power of the resurrection motivates me in my daily life It is a new life; the old life died and was buried And the life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God who loved me and died for me According to Romans 6, I must know this to be true; I recognize the application of such to my life and yield the members of my body accordingly. The Holy Spirit activates the power of the resurrection; new life So, we pray daily for the power of the resurrection to be present in my life We pray the same for one another The power of the resurrection in your life is Victorious Living Phil 3:11 If by any means I may attain to the resurrection from the dead For me to fully arrive to the resurrection from the dead (ones), I consider any righteousness of my own but rubbish and yield to the life-giving Holy Spirit daily. I want to challenge you that for the next 30 days, you make it a habit to recite Paul s prayer for you and your church family. Put a note beside your bed, the bathroom mirror, top of your dresser All the note needs to say is Philippians 3:10 The Resurrection is... * the foundation of our faith, * the focal point of our fervency, * the focus of our faithfulness and flourishing growth, * the fundamental fact of our fellowship with God and our future with Him, * the footing of our fortitude and going forward for Christ, * the fire in our hearts and fuel for our zeal. Easter Sunday used to be the time for new clothes It is really the time for a new life; not new for the outside but the inside Colossians 3:1 If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God RESURRECTION AT WORK Philippians 3:9-11 John Stott: Christianity is in its very essence a RESURRECTION religion. GLORIOUS DAY 1 Corinthians 15 We are sorrowful, but not like those who have no hope. VICTORIOUS DAY Ephesians 1:15-20 The same RESURRECTION POWER that brought Christ up from the grave and is now operative within all those who belong to Christ Philippians 3:10 For this power to be effective in our lives, there must be a desire to pursue knowing Jesus. NEW DAY Romans 6:3-5 We identify with Christ s resurrection with a new life The power of the resurrection motivates me in my daily life The power of the resurrection in your life is Victorious Living Colossians 3:1 ============================================= === SERMON: message(148).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 4/26/2025 9:10:42 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE HELL, YOU SAY Luke 16:19-31 INTRODUCTION This next account is not as straight forward as it might appear Is it a real-life account or a teaching parable? Many will say a parable because it begins much the same as other parables certain 7:41; 10:30; 12:16; 13:6; 13:31; 14:16; 15:11; 19:12 But certain is used in real accounts: 14:2; John 5:5; 11:1; Acts 3:2; 5:1 8:9; 9:33; 10:1; 14:8; Some claim it is a true story as it has an element no other parable has - Names: Lazarus, Abraham, Moses They will also say the rich man is not named as a courtesy Shows Jesus would not expose wicked men to the public This story is narrated like real history Hades is real; not a figure of speech This passage talks of things outside of human experience; unlike other parables; birds, seed, fields, pearls, wheat, barns, leaven, fish A parable is an earthly story with heavenly implications. This passage transcends human reality Wiersbe: "Luke did not say that this narrative was a parable; perhaps it was an actual occurrence." We generally regard parables as teaching one primary spiritual truth and don t push the details too far But even those who consider this a parable treat it like a real story They give considerable attention to the details of this story including teaching about heaven and hell The Doctrine of Eternal Punishment Apostle s Creed: does not use the word hell but states of Jesus, He descended to the dead. Nicene Creed (325 A.D.) He suffered and was buried; and the third day He rose again, according to the Scriptures; and ascended into heaven, and sits on the right hand of the Father; and He shall come again, with glory, to judge the quick and the dead; whose kingdom shall have no end." Nothing is said where the quick and dead go after judgment The Athanasian Creed (450-600 AD) has an interesting section on judgment declaring that Christ "suffered for our salvation, descended into hell, rose again the third day from the dead; He ascended into heaven, He sits on the right hand of the Father, God, Almighty; From thence He shall come to judge the quick and the dead. At whose coming all men shall rise again with their bodies; and shall give account of their own works. And they that have done good shall go into life everlasting and they that have done evil into everlasting fire." Westminster Shorter Catechism: Q. 19. What is the misery of that estate whereinto man fell? A. All mankind by their fall lost communion with God, are under his wrath and curse, and so made liable to all the miseries of this life, to death itself, and to the pains of hell forever. In the last 20 years, belief in heaven and hell have remained steady among those who call themselves religious Pew Research: (2023-2024) of all adults 67% in heaven; 55% in hell Among the religious people in America; 81% believe in heaven; 72% in hell Jesus spoke more on hell than any other in Scripture Erwin Lutzer begins his book "One Minute After You Die" with this quote "Hell disappeared. And no one noticed. It was a sermon on hell that brought about American s First Great Awakening. Billy Graham - As hell was becoming for many no more than a swear word, sin was also an accepted way of life. . . . If people can ignore what the Bible calls sin, then they can quite logically discount what it says about the reality of hell. The Distortions in Public Opinion Distortion Sources 1. It is not a fun subject. Why talk about something no one wants to hear? Originally, I was going to skip most of this and go straight to v. 31 But that would violate the principle of verse by verse expositional proclamation of the Word of God. We explored the topic some in our study of Revelation, but that has been years ago. Hell is physical pain, loneliness, darkness that accentuates fear, regret, separation from God, and the absence of a second chance. 2. The difficulty of reconciling hell with the love of God One man said that he would not want to be in heaven with a God who sends people to hell. To some: the punishment in hell does not fit the crime 3. False doctrines from the within the church Universalism; Annihilationism; non-biblical descriptions "flames of fire gushed out from his ears and eyes and nostrils and at every pore." But while the church is speaking of hell less, modern culture is using it more It is used glibly, tritely, and flippantly which has desensitized and even eliminated the concept of torture It is hard to scare the hell out of someone when they have such a laze faire attitude toward the whole idea. Distortion Results The wicked have no hesitation to tell you to go to hell when the church is most reluctant to tell people not to go to hell. The truth of hell as taught in the Bible is to bring fear, dread, terror and panic. It is intended to bring fright and horror Hell is full of surprised people John Lennon wrote: Imagine there's no heaven It's easy if you try No hell below us Above us only sky Imagine all the people living for today. He no longer has to imagine; He is not dreaming An old Puritan writer: "Meditate much on hell. Let us go into hell by contemplation that we may not go into hell by condemnation! We must look at this passage through the lens of the culture As one writer puts it, Put on your toga and sandals Jesus is still addressing the Pharisees; scribes; religious leaders He has spoken about the danger of the love of money; 16:1, 2 The deceitful and treacherous nature of riches; 16:9-11 What they highly esteemed was hateful to God; 16:15 The proper use of mammon would welcome them into heaven; 16:11, 12 Those who thought themselves to be righteous; followers of the Law v.15 That they were guilty of misusing Scripture; 16:17, 18 This story is going to rock the Pharisees In their eyes, the rich man was successful; Lazarus a loser The rich man is a true son of Abraham; the rich man is blessed of God They would have identified with the rich man and imagine their shock when he was in torment The rich man was not in hell because he was rich Lazarus was not in heaven because he was poor For the Pharisees, this was a major switcheroo Rich people go to heaven; poor people go to hell The prosperity gospel is not new The point of the story is to appropriate God s method of deliverance now Don t wait until then; then is now The rich man and his brothers were busy living one world at a time Ecclesiastes 12:1 Remember your creator in the days of your youth 12:13 Fear God, and keep His commands, for this is the whole of man Many texts fill in with the word duty not in the original This is the wholeness of man; this is what makes man whole Some will be carried by angels; others will awake in torment The fate of a person s eternal destiny is fixed by his choices in this life The Doctrinal Summary 13:23-30 The Road to Hell is walked by the Lazy, the Late, the Lofty We are stewards of the manifold grace of God (1 Peter 4:10) We must not shy away from warning others of the reality of hell It is the most loving, compassionate, and gracious thing you can do May no acquaintance of ours ever say, I wish you would have told me What do we tell them? Here is a summary for recall Fact #1 - Hell is a place of horrible fire torment. Luke 16:23 , 24 , 28 torment and agony Eternal burning pain and suffering Fact #2 - Hell is a place of total consciousness. Luke 16:23b The rich man knew his whereabout and pain Fact #3 - Hell is a place of no mercy. Luke 16:24 No grace; no mercy Fact #4 - Hell is a place of complete memory. Luke 16:25 , 27-31 No dementia; no Alzheimer s; no memory lapses He remembered his previous life. Lk 16:25 He remembered his lost family Lk. 16:27, 28 He remembered God s Word 16:29, 31 Fact #5 - Hell is a place of no possible escape. Luke 16:26 No purgatory; no second chances ILLUSTRATION Fanny Crosby tells the story of how she came to write Rescue the Perishing... It was written in the year 1869, when I was forty-nine years old. Many of my hymns were written after experiences in New York mission work. This one was thus written. I was addressing a large company of working men one hot summer evening, when the thought kept forcing itself on my mind that some mother's boy must be rescued that night or not at all. So I made a pressing plea that if there was a boy present who had wandered from his mother's home and teaching, he would come to me at the close of the service. A young man of eighteen came forward and said, 'Did you mean me? I promised my mother to meet her in heaven, but as I am now living that will be impossible.' We prayed for him and he finally arose with a new light in his eyes and exclaimed in triumph, 'Now I can meet my mother in heaven, for I have found God!' A few days before, Mr. Doane had sent me the subject Rescue the Perishing, and while I sat there that evening the line came to me, Rescue the perishing, care for the dying. I could think of nothing else that night. When I arrived it my home I went to work on it at once; and before I retired the entire hymn was ready for a melody. The next day my words were written and forwarded to Mr. Doane, who wrote the beautiful and touching music as it now stands. In November, 1903, I went to Lynn, Massachusetts, to speak before the Young Men s Christian Association. I told them the incident that led me to write Rescue the Perishing," as I have just related it. After the meeting a large number of men shook hands with me, and among them was a man, who seemed to be deeply moved. You may imagine my surprise when he said, Miss Crosby, I was the boy, who told you more than thirty-five years ago that I had wandered from my mother s God. The evening that you spoke at the mission I sought and found peace, and I have tried to live a consistent Christian life ever since. If we never meet again on earth, we will meet up yonder. (Fanny Crosby's personal testimony) HELL, YOU SAY Luke 16:19-31 The Doctrine of Eternal Punishment Q. 19. What is the misery of that estate whereinto man fell? A. All mankind by their fall lost communion with God, are under his wrath and curse, and so made liable to all the miseries of this life, to death itself, and to the pains of hell forever. Jesus spoke more on hell than any other in Scripture Billy Graham - If people can ignore what the Bible calls sin, then they can quite logically discount what it says about the reality of hell. The Distortions in Public Opinion Distortion Sources 4. It is not a fun subject 5. The difficulty of reconciling hell with the love of God 6. False doctrines from within the church 7. Distortion Results Hell is full of surprised people "Meditate much on hell. Let us go into hell by contemplation that we may not go into hell by condemnation! The rich man was not in hell because he was rich Lazarus was not in heaven because he was poor The fate of a person s eternal destiny is fixed by his choices in this life The Doctrinal Summary We must not shy away from warning others of the reality of hell Fact #1 - Hell is a place of horrible fire torment. Luke 16:23 , 24 , 28 Fact #2 - Hell is a place of total consciousness. Luke 16:23b Fact #3 - Hell is a place of no mercy. Luke 16:24 Fact #4 - Hell is a place of complete memory. Luke 16:25 , 27-31 Fact #5 - Hell is a place of no possible escape. Luke 16:26 HELL, YOU SAY Luke 16:19-31 The Doctrine of Eternal Punishment Q. 19. What is the misery of that estate whereinto man fell? A. All mankind by their fall lost communion with God, are under his wrath and curse, and so made liable to all the miseries of this life, to death itself, and to the pains of hell forever. ___________ spoke more on hell than any other in Scripture Billy Graham - If people can ___________ what the Bible calls sin, then they can quite logically discount what it says about the reality of _________ The Distortions in Public Opinion Distortion Sources 1. It is not a _________ subject 2. The difficulty of reconciling hell with the ___________ of God 3. False doctrines from ____________ the church 4. Distortion Results Hell is full of ______________ people "Meditate much on hell. Let us go into hell by _______________ that we may not go into hell by __________________! The rich man was not in hell because he was _________ Lazarus was not in heaven because he was __________ The fate of a person s eternal destiny is fixed by his choices in ________ life The Doctrinal Summary We must _________ shy away from warning others of the reality of hell Fact #1 - Hell is a place of horrible fire ____________ Luke 16:23 , 24 , 28 Fact #2 - Hell is a place of total ______________ Luke 16:23b Fact #3 - Hell is a place of no ____________ Luke 16:24 Fact #4 - Hell is a place of complete ______________ Luke 16:25 , 27-31 Fact #5 - Hell is a place of no possible _____________ Luke 16:26 ============================================= === SERMON: message(149).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/3/2025 10:49:11 AM To: "Hill Jonathan" Subject: message HELL, YOU SAY! Luke 16:19-31 INTRODUCTION Jesus pulls back the curtain to give us a glimpse of life on the other side of death Luke 15 focused on God s love for the lost and the poor Luke 16 is the spiritual dangers of the wealthy and powerful The Unjust Steward: use the money of this world to prepare for the next The Rich Man and Lazarus: the possibility of major role reversals TWO REACTIONS when a wicked person dies: First: Gloating Careful that the old Adam in us does not gloat knowing the retribution will come to the wicked. They will receive their comeuppance Heaven and hell are not compensation for what happens in this life; or in other words to square accounts Second: Recoil Eternal punishment is not an accurate teaching of the character of God A W Tozer -The vague and tenuous hope that God is too kind to punish the ungodly has become a deadly opiate for the consciences of millions. There is no doubt this passage is from the mouth of Jesus The Doctrine of Eternal Damnation is sobering and requires care and compassion in the presentation. Such a teaching is often received with scorn and ridicule. We must search the Scriptures to see that these things are so; we must rightly divide the Word of truth as approved workmen. We were all born in sin; we are all under the just condemnation of God for our sin; we will be forever separated from God upon our physical death unless we embrace the saving message of Jesus Christ who died for our sins, was buried, and rose again the third day. We may not understand speaking in tongues, we may not understand the timing of the Rapture with little consequences. If we miss the Bible s true instruction on hell, we do so with eternal consequences. We must remind ourselves that this world is the best thing that an unbeliever will ever experience. And this world is the worst thing to be experienced by a believer. TWO MEN 16:19-21 Rich man: nameless; he did not know God No suggestion of finances gained unjustly No mention of any hideous sin He is rich, self-centered, self-indulgent, self-gratification His life on earth is summed up in a single sentence The culture views him as greatly blessed by God Day after day clothed himself with incredibly luxurious garb right down to the best underwear money could buy; not Fruit of the Loom for this guy 16:15 that which is highly esteemed among men is detestable in the sight of God." Revelation 3:17-20 church of Laodicea Many a man s gold has lost him his God. - George Swinnock Lazarus: named; the Hebrew meaning helped of God "if anyone loves God, he is known by Him." (1 Cor. 8:3) No record of anything he said Lazarus physically poor but he was also poor in spirit and thus became far richer in death than the rich man was in life was laid literally was thrown down ; flung there, not gently laid there Placed there assuming a rich guy would be of help Not just any old gate but marked by impressive construction and stature Same word used for the gates in the New Jerusalem; Rev. 21 Covered with ulcers; contrast to rich man covered in purple Too weak as he was brought there; paralyzed? Continual state of hunger; In those days they ate with their hands, as most of the world has done for most of its history; and, typically, you took bread and you dipped it in some kind of stew or thick soup and you ate that way. It is a little messy; and they didn t have paper napkins so they used bread that was stale...So they would use the bread to clean their hands and then throw it under the table. You say, Not at my house, they wouldn t."...They also had a built-in cleanup crew. Mt 15:26 , Jesus says this, It is not good to take the children s bread and throw it to the dogs. What did Jesus mean Throw it to the dogs ? In Mt 15:27 Yes, Lord; but even the dogs feed on the crumbs which fall from their masters table. Who s the cleanup crew? Dogs. Lazarus was longing to be under the table eating with the dogs beggar, full of sores, laid at the gate, crumbs, top it off with dogs licking sores (we don t know if the licking was of benefit or added to pain TWO PLACES 16:22, 23 The beginning of The Great Reversal; two deaths resulting in Two Places Here is a list of contrasts presented by J. MacArthur 1. The poor man then becomes rich; and the rich man becomes poor; and the poor man becomes richer than the rich man ever was; and the rich man becomes poorer than the poor man ever was. 2. You have a poor man on the outside of the house, and you have a rich man on the inside. Then comes death, and you have a poor man on the inside and rich man on the outside. 3. You have a poor man with no food, and a rich man with all the food he can possibly need; and then you have a poor man at the great heavenly banquet, and a rich man with absolutely nothing. 4. You have a poor man with needs, and a rich man with no needs; and then you have a poor man with no needs, and a rich man with needs. 5. You have a poor man who desires everything. You have a rich man who desires nothing, and then you have a rich man who will never have his desires fulfilled, and a poor man who has all his desires fulfilled. 6. You have a poor man who suffers, and a rich man who is satisfied; and then you have a rich man who suffers, and a poor man who s satisfied. 7. You have a poor man who s tormented, and a rich man who s happy; and then you have a poor man who s happy, and a rich man who s tormented. 8. You have a poor man who is humiliated, a rich man who s honored. Then you have a rich man who is humiliated, and a poor man who is honored. 9. You have a poor man who wants a crumb, a rich man who feasts; and then you have a poor man who s at a feast, and a rich man who wants a drop of water. 10. You have a poor man who seeks help, a rich man who gives none. Then you have a rich man who seeks help, and a poor man who gives none. 11. You have a poor man who is a nobody, a rich man who is well-known; and then you have a poor man who has a name, and a rich man who has none. 12. You have a poor man who has no dignity in death, not even a burial. You have a rich man who has dignity in death. 13. Then you have a poor man who has dignity after death, and a rich man who has no dignity after death, not even a name. 14. You have a poor man with no hope, and a rich man with all hope. Then you have a rich man with no hope, and a poor man who is hope realized. Death of Lazarus: no record of burial; probably taken to Gehenna; fire dump Death of Rich Man: was buried no doubt with great fanfare; not told if he was sick or death was sudden; would not change the story I read where the concept of two compartments in Sheol (OT word for the place of the departed) progressed during the intertestamental years The NT word Hades refers to the same place There is much about the two-compartment concept we know nothing about Also note: v. 23 Abraham was far away where was that? I can offer no dogmatic statement Abraham s bosom = paradise The place of privilege; honor; reclining so as the head was near the chest of the host A place of rest, contentment, peace This is the only occasion in Scripture where angels carried one in death And what did the angels carry? 2 Cor. 5; Paul; to be absent from the body is to present with the LORD Heb. 1:14 angels are ministering spirits This had to be a shock to the listeners Transported from the gutter to the head table The shock was about to get greater 16:23 in Hades Hades has been described as the holding cell for unbelievers until the Great White Throne judgment Rev. 20:14 The rich man knew where he was; no soul sleep; not an unconscious state being present and forever tense Hades is not to be confused with hell, even though that is often the translation Notice: his thoughts were clear, his emotions were intact, his mind was functional even in the midst of torment Their minds will forever condemn them, their emotions will forever be anger and hatred; their know they are separated from God forever Which is what they wanted all along; not knowing the devastating results 2 Thessalonians 1:6-9 And to the person who asks, How can a loving God permit such a place as hell to exist? simply reveals they know little of God s holiness or the wickedness of sin. If God is righteous and just, how can He not have a designated place for those who reject His love and holiness? Sin is rebellion against a holy and loving God. God sends no one to hell. Man desires to live His life without God and God grants him his desire. Man sends himself to hell by refusing to believe Rev. 21:8 unbelievers are second on the list Tony Evans defines Hell - Hell is the place of eternal exile where the ungodly will experience God s righteous retribution against sin forever....Someone has said that hell is the answer to the sinner s prayer. Jesus taught us to pray to God, Thy will be done. But a rebellious sinner says to God, My will be done. And God grants that person s request. TWO PRAYERS 16:24-31 Relief for his body 16:24-26 Abandon all hope ye who enter here The rich man now becomes the beggar David Thompson: the irony is that one drop of living water can keep a person out of hell He who had no mercy now pleads for mercy Luke 6:24, 25 Depending on his lineage; child of Abraham to validate his legitimacy He is still making demands; still sees Lazarus as a tool He still views things as he did during his life Hell is not remedial; hell does not fix anybody (MacArthur) There is no remorse in hell; no humility; no brokenness Interesting, he does not scream let me out of here I don t deserve this While it is not likely that his cry for water refers to a literal drop of water, Jesus' use of this figure of speech does point to the reality that the horror of hell is so terrible that this portrayal of even licking water from a fingertip would bring some relief. This is not about a real drop of water, but is about real relief. There will be no relief from the eternal torment of Hell! That's a horrible thought! Not even one drip from a fingertip of relief will ever hit the tongue of a soul eternally tormented in hell. Any argument over whether the flame was literal or figurative misses the point. The point is hell is a place of torment No one can ever say, that hurts like hell it s hot as hell they have no idea If the symbol is terrifying, how must more be the real thing? Luke 3:17 chasm v. 26 indicates the impossibility of change Exit is not a word found in the vocabulary of hell No escape hatches, no exit signs, no second chances It is not so much a visual gulf as the concept of permanence It is a truth that after death all men are eternally secure: heaven or hell A person s eternal destiny is fixed in this present life The Second Prayer 16:27-31 You have heard it said hell won t be so bad because all my friends will be there Hell is torment, loneliness, darkness The rich man is now an evangelist; before it was about material things I wonder if we as believers had a more vivid picture of the eternal tortures of the damned, would it not serve to motivate a more urgent style of evangelism? The rich man now has a concern for his brothers Could it be the torment or could it be conviction of an ungodly influence he had on his brothers? The denial is not because God is not interested in the salvation of his brothers, but that the plan presented is useless The man is now a firm believer; There are no agnostics in hell If you never believe on earth, you will believe in hell." Hell is nothing more than truth known too late J. C. Ryle Abraham speaks of Moses and the Prophets as if they are still speaking Abraham and Moses are living voices And why not because they are speaking God s truth: the living Word of God if they hear not since they are not listening; they will not hear Acts 7:56, 57 at Stephen s message the conviction was so great and the message so offensive to their souls, they covered their ears They will not hear; they will not be persuaded The negative is absolute. Miracles do not melt stony hearts Numbers 14:11 How long will you refuse to believe in me? 1 Kings 18:16-46 continued rebellion of the Northern Kingdom Matthew 11:20-24 John 11:46-53 Acts 14:16 even after admitting the miraculous had taken place Matthew 28:11-15 explaining away the resurrection Jonathan Edwards writes "The warnings of God s Word are more fitted to obtain the ends of awakening sinners, and bringing them to repentance, than the rising of one from the dead to warn them. In this passage, Moses and the prophets seem not only to be equalized to the warnings of one from the invisible world, but to be preferred before them. They have Moses and the prophets, let them hear them. They have already those means which God in his infinite wisdom hath seen to be fittest for them, and more suitable to their nature and circumstances, than the rising of one from the dead. The primary purpose of miracles in the Bible was not to convince men of the truth but to confirm the truth People are responsible for hearing There is one great inescapable responsibility: take God at His Word The grave works no miracles; you die and enter eternity True power evangelism involves not the doing of miracles but the proclamation of God s truth It is the Scripture that makes one wise for salvation through faith in Jesus Christ. Miracles may attest to the authority of the speaker, but they do not bring conviction Lazarus was raised from the death and the leaders wanted to kill him This rich man had the prophets; he had the good life Romans 2:4, 5 Even the torment of Hades did not change his heart; it was still self-centered He argued rather than honoring the wisdom of Abraham Wiersbe: This indicates that the punishment of lost sinners is not remedial; it does not improve them. Hades and hell are not hospitals for the sick; they are prisons for the condemned. The Bible knows of no other methodology for reaching the lost and growing a church other than the message of the Bible. It is not about figuring out a person s felt needs It is not about entertaining Take away everything from a church service than the expositional proclamation of the Word of God and you will soon discover who is the church and who is along for the ride. John 6 assures us that many will find another means of entertainment The only reason people go to hell is their rejection of Biblical revelation Romans 10:17 "faith comes from hearing, and hearing by the Word of Christ." 1 Peter 1:23 "you have been born again not of seed which is perishable but imperishable, that is, through the living and enduring Word of God."
