Unbelief is a moral issue not an intellectual one No amount of evidence can turn unbelief to belief Repentance is not about having all of your intellectual questions answered It takes a power only found in the Word of God; Hebrews 4:12 The issue is not a lack of information but a rejection of the truth John 3:19 This is the judgment, that the Light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the Light, for their deeds were evil The real problem is for man to humble himself and admit he is a sinner and in need of a Savior C S Lewis - The safest road to Hell is the gradual one the gentle slope, soft underfoot, without sudden turnings, without milestones, without signposts. (Lewis, C S, The Screwtape Letters) If a person sees a house on fire and does everything he can to rescue the occupants, he is heralded as a hero. And that s the way it should be. Why is it, then, that a Christian who accepts what God says in the Bible about hell and tries to warn people is ridiculed as an alarmist? There is a way to stay out of hell, but no way to get out HELL, YOU SAY! Luke 16:19-31 TWO REACTIONS Gloating Recoil TWO MEN 16:19-21 Rich man: His life on earth is summed up in a single sentence Lazarus: helped of God was laid literally was thrown down ; Lazarus was longing to be under the table eating with the dogs TWO PLACES 16:22, 23 Abraham s bosom = paradise; place of privilege; place of rest Transported from the gutter to the head table Hades the holding cell for unbelievers Hades is not to be confused with hell 2 Thessalonians 1:6-9 Sin is rebellion against a holy and loving God Man sends himself to hell by refusing to believe TWO PRAYERS 16:24-31 Relief for his body 16:24-26 David Thompson: the irony is that one drop of living water can keep a person out of hell Depending on his lineage Exit is not a word found in the vocabulary of hell Evangelism 16:27-31 if they hear not since they are not listening Miracles do not melt stony hearts Numbers 14:11 1 Kings 18:16-46 Matthew 11:20-24 John 11:46-53 Acts 14:16 Matthew 28:11-15 The primary purpose of miracles in the Bible was not to convince men of the truth but to confirm the truth He argued rather than honoring the wisdom of Abraham Unbelief is a moral issue not an intellectual one John 3:19 HELL, YOU SAY! Luke 16:19-31 TWO REACTIONS ____________ ____________ TWO MEN 16:19-21 Rich man: His life on earth is summed up in a ____________ sentence Lazarus: ____________ of God was laid - literally was ____________ down ; Lazarus was longing to be ____________ the table eating with the dogs TWO PLACES 16:22, 23 Abraham s bosom = paradise; place of ____________; place of ________ Transported from the gutter to the ____________ table Hades is the____________ cell for unbelievers Hades is not to be confused with ____________ 2 Thessalonians 1:_________ Sin is _____________ against a holy and loving God Man sends himself to hell by refusing to ____________ TWO PRAYERS 16:24-31 ____________ for his body 16:24-26 David Thompson: the irony is that one drop of living water can keep a person out of hell Depending on his ____________ ____________ is not a word found in the vocabulary of hell Evangelism 16:27-31 if they hear not ____________ they are not listening Miracles do not melt ____________ hearts Numbers 14:11 1 Kings 18:16-46 Matthew 11:20-24 John 11:46-53 Acts 14:16 Matthew 28:11-15 The primary purpose of miracles in the Bible was not to convince men of the truth but to ____________ the truth He argued rather than honoring the ____________ of Abraham Unbelief is a ____________ issue not an intellectual one John 3:19 ============================================= === SERMON: message(150).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/10/2025 5:47:53 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FORGIVENESS LUKE 17:1-6 Wiersbe: Luke 17:1-6 Forgiveness Luke 17:7-10 Faithfulness Luke 17:11-19 Thankfulness Luke 17:20-37 Preparedness INTRODUCTION It has been a long Sabbath Day. Beginning in Luke 14 Jesus has been teaching about the cost of discipleship, the value of individual souls, the lack of true spirituality in the religious leaders, and preparing for eternity by providing a look at life after death. He now turns His attention squarely to His disciples. Note in v. 1 they are disciples; in vs. 5 they are apostles He is truly narrowing His focus on those who will carry forth the message He is about to address principles related to behavior, attitudes, and relationships. This is about ministry to others and to the Lord Jesus The Guarantee 17:1 It is impossible for stumbling blocks not to come (YLT) They are unavoidable, certain The path of following Christ was never said to be easy They will continually come to trip up a believer We have enemies: the world, the flesh and devil Woe that is a solemn warning; notice it is not defined The Woe is not the millstone; that is the easy way out Since the alternative is better , the woe is something to avoid The entrance of sin into the world brought with it differences and disputes Nature has its lustings and lustings lead to strivings - Spurgeon When we are offended, be reminded Jesus said it would happen Let us be careful we are not the cause Regarding unity: the Psalm writer tells us Behold, how good and pleasant it is Stumbling blocks vary: a harsh word, the thoughtless deed, the frivolous remark We sin but are so quick to play the blame game We did it as children and the pattern continued into adulthood It just comes naturally Stumbling blocks is the Greek word for scandal: cause of displeasure, offense; a trap stick, lure Root idea is a snap trap; wood help open to catch an animal The animal would hit the bait stick, trigger the trap and be ensnared The false teaching, superficial religion of the Pharisees would be included But just because the inevitable is going to happen does not mean we just live with it; just go with the flow We should do all we can to avoid sinning against others and taking offense with others sin against us Romans 14:13 the case of the weaker brother Matthew 16:23 Peter seeking to prevent Jesus death The Condemnation 17:2 Great sorrow for those who are the cause of stumbling Millstone: the large stone used for grinding grain, turned by a donkey 4-5 feet in diameter, 1 foot thick, weighing 3300 pounds the concrete is there for the weight (Mack the Knife) It is like being fitted with a pair of concrete shoes Jesus is not a torturer, but trying to get across the seriousness of causing another to sin It would be more advantageous for you to be weighted down with concrete and die a horrible death of drowning than to cause one to sin This is vivid, sobering language Better to suffer a Mafia-style death than to cause another believer to stumble It does highlight the seriousness of seeking forgiveness If we are involved in leading another to sin, we are to do everything we can to seek forgiveness or the restoration of another who sins Matthew 5:23, 24 sin hinders worship The Pattern 17:3, 4 Verses 1 and 2 address the offender; v. 3 addresses the one offended be on guard pay attention If your brother, one with whom you have an intimate relationship with, trespasses (misses the mark) If a potential action rebuke command; admonish, warn, reprimand, may include censure A frank but gentle admonition Don t harbor a grudge, deal with it Not done with glee, not with an air of arrogance if potential action forgive command; a specific action A refusal to forgive not puts you in the position of sinning Be on guard look first into your own heart; remove any log Matt.7:3-5 Stephen Cole: When relational conflicts erupt, the first thing you should do is to ask God to show you what part you are responsible for. If you think that, being generous, you re responsible for ten percent of the problem, you can safely multiply that number by four or five! We all are prone to justify ourselves and blame others. But healing will not begin in damaged relationships until each person allows the Spirit of God through the Word of God to shine into his or her own heart and reveal the sin that is there. We must be on guard against relational sins because we are so prone towards them." Rebuke is tied to forgiveness Mattoon: This rebuke is to be strong enough to inspire repentance. To "rebuke" does not mean to point out every sin, for Jesus also warns against being judgmental. Rebuke is always to be done in love and compassion, not in a censoring and judgmental spirit. Its purpose is to bring the sin to the attention of the offending person and restore them to fellowship with the Lord and other believers. In this passage, this refers to sin that could pull that person or others away from God, and result in the horrible judgment. When a person feels that he or she must rebuke another Christian for a sin, it is wise and essential for that person to check his or her attitudes and motivations first. He needs to take a good look at himself to make sure there are no faults in his own life that he has not made right with God or other people. The goal is always repentance and restoration; not to prove you are right Cole: "While biblical forgiveness is a quick decision, the restoration of trust usually takes time proportionate to the seriousness of the offense. . . Trust is gradually restored as a person demonstrates growth in godliness. Also, granting forgiveness does not necessarily mean removing all of the consequences of the person s wrongful actions. God forgave David, but He imposed heavy consequences for his sin so that he and others would see the seriousness of what he had done (2 Sam. 12:14 ) This is serious but very tough to do It is much easier to walk away from a strained relationship than to give biblical rebuke. It does not mean we walk around looking to be offended It you enjoy rebuking others, you are not doing it right Ephesians 4:2 Ephesians 4:32 1 Peter 4:8 Romans 15:1 Proverbs 19:11 The discretion of a man delays his anger; and it is his glory to overlook a transgression How do we know when to bear with someone s sin and when to rebuke it? (The Peacemaker by Ken Sande) Are you aware that the offender has something against you? We are not to be passive about strained relationships Don t just shrug it off Is the other person s sin bringing dishonor to God? It is about God s glory and your brother s holiness Is the other person s sin damaging your relationship with him? You find yourself avoiding them whenever possible Is the other person s sin seriously hurting others? You cannot ignore destructive behavior Is the other person s sin seriously hurting himself? James 5:19, 20 Is the person s sin an often repeated pattern? Anger, lust, greed, selfishness, insensitivity to others, laziness or a lack of self-discipline Forgive without Limits 17:4 Jesus is giving us another potential action if Here is a picture of a person willing to repent We are asked to do nothing more than what God does for us everyday Seven is the number for completeness, perfectness This is a quick look into one s soul; are they walking in the Spirit It is your job to be always ready to forgive. Forgiveness can not be granted until one comes seeking it If there was ever an area where you can go overboard or extravagant , here it is Is there ever a time when God says, ok, that s it! You have gone to far this time! Biblical forgiveness is a decision, not a feeling The idea of forgiveness is to dismiss a case in court, to release Your promise to forgive means you will never bring it up again To others, to the person, to yourself, to God The Request 17:5,6 The response of those specifically called by Jesus Those soon to be the eyewitnesses of the resurrection and the foundation of the church There was an instant realization that the demand is tough We are not dealing with automatic behaviors They realized their level of faith was not up to the challenge It was an honest request with the right motives But they are to learn that more faith is not the issue Faith is not measured by its quantity, but simply by its presence A mustard seed sized faith will accomplish impossible things. The real need is for more obedience and humility Even Paul one time asked, who is adequate for these things? Every demand of discipleship is beyond our capabilities humanly speaking It is not about the measure of your faith but the obedience your faith instills Lord, increase my faith that I may be able to obey it Faith is not measured by how big it is but how living and active it is Once you learn what God wants, you obey. v. 6 if called a first class condition or since you have mustard sized faith A living faith, even though small, is able to forgive your brother 7 times. God will work in that faith with supernatural power It is your little faith with the power of God behind it CONCLUSION The story is told of a man who was buried in a tomb who requested a great mass of granite over the top of his grave when he died. He had said if there ever was a resurrection it might be certain he should never rise! The granite stone weighing tons is there but split in two! - Before they could place that large granite stone on the grave a bird happened to drop an acorn right there. An oak tree sprang up & the living power of the acorn split the granite in two. [small & weak, but has life!] FORGIVENESS LUKE 17:1-6 The Guarantee 17:1 The path of following Christ was never said to be easy The Woe is not the millstone; the Woe is something to avoid Stumbling blocks is the Greek word for scandal Root idea is a snap trap; wood help open to catch an animal The Condemnation 17:2 It is like being fitted with a pair of concrete shoes Better to suffer a Mafia-style death than to cause another believer to stumble It does highlight the seriousness of seeking forgiveness The Pattern 17:3, 4 rebuke command; admonish, warn, reprimand A refusal to forgive not puts you in the position of sinning The goal is always repentance and restoration; not to prove you are right It does not mean we walk around looking to be offended How do we know when to bear with someone s sin and when to rebuke it? * Ken Sande Are you aware that the offender has something against you? Is the other person s sin bringing dishonor to God? Is the other person s sin damaging your relationship with him? Is the other person s sin seriously hurting others? Is the other person s sin seriously hurting himself? Is the person s sin an often repeated pattern? We are asked to do nothing more than what God does for us everyday Biblical forgiveness is a decision, not a feeling The Request 17:5,6 There was an instant realization that the demand is tough Faith is not measured by its quantity, but simply by its presence Faith is not measured by how big it is but how living and active it is It is your little faith with the power of God behind it ============================================= === SERMON: message(151).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/16/2025 1:02:11 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FAITHFULNESS Luke 17:7-10 INTRODUCTION Jesus continues His final journey to Jerusalem and His offering as the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world. Luke has us in the final months of year 3 of Jesus public ministry. The opposition is resolute in their desire to kill Him; reasons for accusations The believing disciples are wrestling with the instructions they are receiving The concept of crucifixion remains foreign to them The remaining uncommitted disciples are still along for the ride The apostles have just been told the issue of forgiveness does not depend on a specific quantity of faith but the practice of faith. Just a little bit can go a long way. Just a spoon full of sugar A little dab will do ya Just one look A little leaven Someone wrote: It doesn t take much of a man for God to use, just all of him So, make sure we understand the connection: forgiveness and faithfulness It would be easy for a person whose faith uproots trees to become proud Miraculous faith must be balanced with faithful day-to-day ordinary service Faith is the root of saving religion Heb. 11:6 Faith is a grace which is evident in degrees Weak faith Romans 4:19; 14:1 strong faith Romans 4:20 little faith Matthew 8:26 great faith Matthew 8:10; 15:28; Luke 7:9_ Faith does not come in full measure at salvation If you have faith, pray for more. 2 Thess. 3:2 not all men have faith Jesus now directs their attention from Forgiveness to Faithfulness It is the nature of faith to be put into action THE PLOWING SERVANT The point that Jesus is trying to make is not about how God treats His servants, but rather the spirit in which we should serve God. The focus is not about some commendation for a job well done, but a humble attitude of simply doing our duty. v. 7, 8 has an interesting introduction: but which of you Luke has used this intro 3 other times; each addressing a larger audience 11:5; 14:28; 15:4 To paraphrase: Can you possibly imagine The expected answer is of course not! The end result is well, why don t you act this way then? A major emphasis in Jesus ministry was attacks against the danger of self-righteousness This is a general warning to all those listening Spurgeon: This is the time for us to serve, even as Jesus did when he was here; and we are to serve right on till the close of the day, even as Jesus did......See, brethren, our position as believers; we are here as servants. It is not the time for feasting yet. Whatever work we have done, even if it is getting towards the evening of our life s day, we must not think of sitting down yet, and expecting our Master to wait upon us. No, we must go on with our service, and reckon it to be our highest privilege still to gird ourselves, and wait on him. This is not the place of resting or of feasting; this is the day of our holy servitude. THE DINING MASTER The servant comes in from the field, cleans himself up, makes the food, sets the table, and serves his master while standing off in the corner 17:9 We might tend to react to such a description, but this was a perfectly normal description of the slave-master relationship Jesus made no comment on it, neither to condemn nor to approve He merely used this relationship to illustrate His teaching on the life of faithfulness The slave received and deserved no special treatment or thanks The Apostle Paul clearly exemplified this kind of attitude Often referred to himself as a slave (doulos) Romans 1:1 a servant of Jesus Christ (Galatians 1:10) Paul suffered great hardship; 2 Cor. 11:24-28 But never once did he complain or expect special remuneration for his efforts 1 Corinthians 9:16 For though I preach the gospel, I have nothing to glory of God owes us nothing We are called to reject materialism, oppose injustice, tell all about Jesus for necessity is laid upon me; yea, woe in unto me, if I preach not the gospel (1 Cor. 9:16) 17:10 Put on your slave shoes What a blow to self-righteousness; The point is to do your duty The teaching applied directly to the pharisaic attitude of Jesus day The self-righteous Pharisees believed their good works constituted a claim upon God They felt that God rewarded the righteous materially Hence, the story of the rich man and Lazarus The scribes and Pharisees desired the attention and honor (20:46, 47) J. C. Ryle: Seldom will a saint be found who is not at seasons tempted to be satisfied and pleased with himself. There is such a thing as a pride which wears the cloak of humility. There is not a heart upon earth which does not contain a piece of the Pharisee's character. Jesus repudiated this attitude, saying good works are our duty Salvation is a gracious gift of God Justification is by faith, not by works Rom 1:17; Gal. 3:11 Salvation is a free gift Eph. 2 A living, vibrant faith will produce good works as a natural outgrowth God may richly reward your service, but at the end of the day you were only doing your duty. There may be times when you will go beyond expectations or directions You saw something that needed to be done while fulfilling your assigned duties It does not mean there will be special recognition or payment We are bondslaves of Christ; bought with a price I dare say we have yet to accomplish all those things which you are commanded (v. 9) Unworthy - unprofitable useless, nothing special 1 Corinthians 4:7 what do you have that you did not receive? Try preaching that in a day of self-esteem, self-worth We remain debtors toward God We must decrease but He must increase We have a duty, responsibility, obligation H. A. Ironside, the late beloved pastor of the historic Moody Memorial Church in Chicago, Illinois, used to tell his students of the maid who was asked how she knew she had really become a Christian. She replied, I know I m a Christian because I sweep under the rugs now! All this to say it does not mean that God does not value our service Jesus is looking for a selfless attitude, humble demeanor Matthew 25:21 is our goal Well done, thou good and faithful servant Things to remember when serving God God is our absolute Lord and Master; He owes us nothing We are bond-servants; ransomed slaves We are not our own, we have been bought with a price We do not serve for reward; we are only doing our duty The disciples prayed for an increase of faith Jesus said to use what faith you have; obey what you know 2 Peter 1:5 add to your faith, virtue moral excellence (vs. 1:3) David Thompson has an interesting thought that "Most people see themselves as unworthy when they face their sin, but Christ is challenging a disciple to see himself as unworthy when they are in His service." Did the disciples learn this lesson? Matthew19:27 We left everything. What then will we have? Matthew 20:21 Put one son on your right and one on your left Luke 22:24 which of them was considered to be the greatest We are to declare ourselves as unprofitable servants Jesus may one day say, Well done, thou good and faithful servant It is Give of your best to the Master Living for Jesus My life, my love I give to Thee, Thou Lamb of God who died for me; O may I ever faithful be, My Savior and my God! Hudson FAITHFULNESS Luke 17:7-10 Miraculous faith must be balanced with faithful day-to-day ordinary service It is the nature of faith to be put into action THE PLOWING SERVANT The focus is a humble attitude of simply doing our duty. THE DINING MASTER God owes us nothing A living, vibrant faith will produce good works as a natural outgrowth Unworthy - unprofitable useless Jesus is looking for a selfless attitude, humble demeanor Things to remember when serving God God is our absolute Lord and Master We are bond-servants; ransomed slaves We are to declare ourselves as unprofitable servants ============================================= === SERMON: message(152).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 5/30/2025 12:01:34 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message COMMUNION AFTER THE MESSAGE WHERE IS MY HONOR? Luke 17:11-18 INTRODUCTION Jesus is going. Beginning in 9:51, the direction of His travel is to Jerusalem. We have found Him at times on the other side of the Jordan River. Today, Luke tells us He is traveling between Samaria and Galilee. In recent chapters of Luke s gospel, Jesus has helped our view of how we look at unbelievers He was often accused of eating with publicans and sinners (15:1,2) He has traveled through Samaria when most Jews avoided that part of the country. The people were of a mixed race due to the Assyrian captivity. When they offered to assist with the rebuilding of the temple, the Jews in Jerusalem refused their help. They built their own place of worship on Mt. Gerizim. His home was Galilee which was not recognized as a place of great spirituality. You remember the question, Can anything good come out of Naareth? Jesus was going to finish His mission. You got to love the apostle Paul s wording, I have finished my course. That is a great way to conclude one s life. Luke 13:22; we are reminded of His course of direction So 17:11 is the third time we read of the purpose of His travel 10:38, Jesus was in Bethany; only a short distance from Jerusalem So, either His path to the city was not in a straight line or Luke was writing according to His theme and not chronologically. We do know that from 9:51 to 19:27, 60% of what we read here is unique to Luke John 11:54 MacArthur: It is likely that this location is the general area to which Luke is referring with the phrase between Samaria and Galilee. THE APPEARANCE 17:11 The place is not important; just some village Lepers: near to the entrance but far enough away because of their condition Lev. 13:45; Num. 5:2, 3 We need to see ourselves in the lives of these men Once we were at a distance from Jesus; by choice and sin Yet, He still ministered to us They were there to meet Jesus, but still kept their distance They no doubt believed He could heal them Leprosy was common in Jesus day There is a fascinating history of which we should be familiar Here is a summary of an article: The Three Messianic Miracles The author is Arnold Fruchtenbaum The priests divided up miracles into 2 categories: those which anyone led by the Holy Spirit could do and those reserved specifically for the Messiah Jesus, of course, performed both, but the ones receiving the most attention were those reserved for the Messiah; leprosy being one Something to keep in mind: from the time of the Mosaic law was completed there is no record of any Jew being healed of leprosy Miriam, Moses s sister, was healed but before the Law Naaman was healed but he was a Syrian Gentile Leviticus 13, 14 give directions upon the healing of a leper The priests were to keep detailed records of those who had the disease Anyone healed was to appear before a priest; offer two bird sacrifice There was a 7 day observation period On day 8, a whole series of offerings were presented Remember, the miraculous healing of a leper was reserved for the Messiah Also remember, the priests had never seen a miraculous healing Now there are 10 lepers coming for healing recognition If I had been Jesus, I would have sent them about 10 minutes apart Every 10 minutes a leper presented himself for inspection Every 10 minutes a priest was confronted with the reality that the Messiah was there 10 times a priest had to confirm that Jesus performed a miracle It was not by the mouth of 2 or 3 witnesses the truth is confirmed It is 10 And confirmed by the same body of spiritual leaders that was bent on Jesus destruction THE APPEAL 17:13 raised their voices shouting as loud as one with leprosy can shout Shouting because of the distance Knowing vocal ability affected; leprosy dries the throat, the voice is low and husky have mercy - desperate petition Must have heard of His reputation; knew He had the ability to heal Jesus, Master Master is one who stands over, a commander A recognition of superior power, but one who is approachable Power and compassion The verb tense is often used as a command; here it expresses an urgency Mercy: feel sympathy with the misery of another THE ORDER 17:14 Interesting it was not that Jesus heard them He saw them; literally seeing them as no other eyes could see them To everyone else they were repulsive, unattractive To Jesus, they are objects of compassion Spurgeon: Before we were saved, Jesus saw us in our pitiable condition of sin and spiritual death on the highway to hell without His intervention. But even after we are saved, Jesus still sees us and knows the trials and afflictions we are going through and stands ever ready to come to our aid. Hebrews 2:18; 4:15 Go and show The priests are the health inspectors; a long process for leprosy Lev. 13:4-6 Leprosy was a God-thing often viewed as punishment from God 2 Chron. 26:19-21 King Uzziah the healing is viewed as miraculous; Messianic (contrast 5:14; Jesus touched; tell no one; show the priest) Jesus kept the Law; show the priests; fulfill the requirements For 7 days the priests would be observing the miracle The miracle was complete; not a 7-day process of skin healing Luke 5:14 as a testimony to them Acts 6:7 states a few priests did become believers; but most continued rejecting the message and miracles Another reminder miracles do not lead to salvation; Luke 16 Their going was an act of faith; they were healed as they went It was only a shell of a promise but they cracked it Here is faith Spurgeon: A grand faith this! You are to come to Christ before you feel any grace in you; you are not to wait until you feel you are healed, and then come to him. Come just as you are, without any sense of grace, or any kind of feeling within you that is worth the having. Come just as you are. As the sinner believes, he is saved. As a man begins to go towards the Saviour, the Saviour s grace meets him Go and show there had to be some confidence in the person of Jesus Your faith is only as good as the object It is not You are clean. Now, go show the priests Jesus could heal anyway He chose Power in our life comes as we obey It was not much faith; just like a mustard seed; just a little faith in a Healer There were times Jesus healed because people believed There were times Jesus healed and they never believed There were dead people raised who could not believe THE UTURN 17:15, 16 Imagine walking along and observing in yourself or the others healing Imagine walking along and the irritation of sores being exposed to the air or clothing going away Imagine walking along and you look at the bloke next to you and see clean skin where once an oozing blister was One Turned Back: walking in step with the others His turning was not so much about the healing as it was the Healer He had to get back to the One who had compassion And he took his megaphone: loud voice Imagine those with Jesus: they saw this man walk away and now running back; he left a leper and came back healed glorifying God he had his voice back and put it to good use fell on his face in Scripture a picture of worshipping God Maybe (MacArthur) the other nine thought they would be worshipping God in the temple. God had already departed Here is a Samaritan who grasped what the woman at the well had to learn John 4:21-24 neither in this mountain or in the temple Worship will be in spirit and truth ; God dwells in Jesus Jesus did not reject the worship; He did not tell him he was wrong The man now had a voice and he was going to use it Spurgeon: We have sometimes heard complaints that, at certain revival meetings, the singing was very loud and there was even shouting. Let the converts shout, brother, let them shout! They have good reason to shout, for Christ has made them whole. We have a great deal too much of respectable death about us, let us have a little even noisy life. I would sooner by half hear the praises of God shouted with a loud voice, than hear the mockery of praise in a tone that is scarcely to be heard, while some machine grinds out music to God s glory, and men forget to sing or are drowned in loud bursts of wind from the instrument. WWW: I would rather tone down a fanatic than resurrect a corpse Unfortunately, there is often a lot of noise but little walking straight when the music stops. THE AMAZEMENT 17:17, 18 Rhetorical questions A testimony against the Jews and their lack of even a little faith Those who worship much know they have been saved from much It was the foreigner just like Luke 10:33 John 1:11-13 Eph. 2:11, 12 CONCLUSION In this chapter, Jesus has spoken of the value of even a little faith Then it was about faith and duty; obedience Now, the lesson is about faith and gratefulness We recently looked briefly at Malachi 1 when God corrected the Israelites attitude toward worship. In a nutshell, we learned that God told them not to bring their blind goat as a sacrifice. He prefaced the section by asking the question, where is My honor? Jesus simply asked where are the nine? Why are they not here bowing before me? Why are they showing no gratitude? We may not have leprosy, but we were at a distance from God When we consider the marvelous nature of our salvation, that we were once dead, at war with God, and He still drew us to Himself; are we properly giving honor to whom honor is due? WHERE IS MY HONOR? Luke 17:11-18 John 11:54 THE APPEARANCE 17:11 Once we were at a distance from Jesus; by choice and sin There is no record of any Jew being healed of leprosy Leviticus 13, 14 10 times a priest had to confirm that Jesus performed a miracle THE APPEAL 17:13 have mercy - desperate petition Jesus, Master A recognition of superior power THE ORDER 17:14 Objects of compassion Hebrews 2:18; 4:15 Leprosy was a God-thing the healing is miraculous Their going was an act of faith Power in our life comes as we obey THE UTURN 17:15, 16 He had to get back to the One who had compassion The man now had a voice, and he was going to use it THE AMAZEMENT 17:17, 18 Those who worship much know they have been saved from much ============================================= === SERMON: message(153).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/7/2025 10:51:56 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 INTRODUCTION It is unclear to me the connection with this next section with what we have previously studied. Jesus has been talking about faith; that doing the hard things like forgiving others only requires a little faith. That same faith will carry you through various steps of servanthood and doing your duty. Faith will also lead you to be grateful for the blessings that come your way. I suppose we could stretch this section to speak of faith and the Lord s return to set up His kingdom. But we don t know that this section flows chronologically. We do know how this kingdom section fits. When it comes to the Kingdom we have two Different Dimensions The first, is the already now 20, 21 Note such words as come , is coming , here it is , there it is The Second is the not yet 22-37 We learn that participation in the future-not-yet dimension is dependent on entering the already-now dimension. Israel s major feasts, especially Passover, heightened the expectancy of deliverance from bondage. The fact that He was headed to Jerusalem brought more excitement; 19:11 The Inquiry of the Time demanded of the Pharisees a strengthened form of the word ask So in this context it means to be persistent, earnest in questioning Knowing the Pharisees, you could translate the word pestering Were they genuinely interested or seeking to get Jesus in trouble? Were they responding to the many occasions when Jesus spoke of the Kingdom? 6:1, 2, 7; 11:20; 11:53, 54; 15:1, 2 There is nothing wrong with the question Just not from the ones who asked The Jews had a real interest in the Kingdom The general Jewish belief was that the kingdom of God would be incredibly wonderful, with a powerful Messiah establishing His rule in Israel and delivering the nation from her enemies Jesus keeps talking about it; performs a few miracles; but no kingdom 19:11; expectations heightened at the Triumphal Entry, 19:35-41 All focus was on a grand and glorious kingdom The Pharisees were Premillennialist The Confusion of the Time comes not with observation They were looking for signs in the heavens, cataclysmic signs The already now kingdom would be inward The already now kingdom would not come slowly as a political movement which could be seen to evolve (John 18:36) Emil Schurer (1844-1910) The History of the Jewish People in the Time of Christ (1885-89) Pieced together the Jewish eschatology from the time of Jesus This is from volume 2: The Messianic Hope The coming would be proceed by a time of tribulation There would appear a prophet like Elijah Messiah would establish His Kingdom The nations would ally to fight the Messiah All opposing nations would be destroyed; becomes subjects Jerusalem would be made new and magnificent Scattered Jews would return Israel would become the center of the world The kingdom would be a time of eternal peace, righteousness and glory. That is the results of His study and sounds like modern day theology A list of events some say was not developed until the 1800 s but he identifies them as part of Jewish thought at the time of Jesus What the Jews missed was two comings They missed the spiritual kingdom; They were not looking for a Savior because they did not have need of one It is easy for us to be critical of the Jews and the hardness of their heart But has there ever been a time in your life when God did not operate the way you thought He should? Maybe a time when the God you put in a box did not respond according to your desires? 21 Jesus counters the belief of a great showing for the Kingdom arrival He slows them down to correct their theology of the kingdom There first needs to be an understanding of the invisible Kingdom John 3:3 unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God The hearts of the people had become dull so they could not hear with their ears or see with their eyes. The fact that you do see, you do hear; that God has opened your heart to receive the gospel should bring indescribable joy. You are privileged; you are blessed You can understand the Kingdom which is a complete mystery to so many Our testimony: once I was blind but now I see Simply put: if you do not recognize King Jesus you will not see the Kingdom By the time many people do see, it will be too late the kingdom is within you Conveys the idea of that which is inside, within one s reach, or in one s midst Colossians 1:13; 4:1; presents the spiritual aspect of the Kingdom We rule that out because Jesus is speaking to the Pharisees whose only relationship to the Kingdom is standing on the outside But there were more listening than just the Pharisees Jesus was saying His kingdom had already begun in the hearts of His followers; it was in the midst of the Pharisees; it was all around them in the lives of the people who have responded to the message Emphasizing the fact that the spiritual kingdom is internal and not manifested by visible signs Jesus was saying, Don t look for the kingdom out there unless it is first in your own heart I, the King, am standing before you. How can you enter the Kingdom when you reject the King? Paul writes of this internal Kingdom: Romans 14:17 Luke 13:18, 19 the Kingdom begins relatively unnoticed; mustard seed The Sign of the Time 17:22 Jesus begins to give a general description of His return Chronology is not the focus; we have more of that in chapter 21 Here are some general characteristics This is far from the glamorous, victorious view of the Jews This is frightening, terrifying; even deadly Jesus gives us clear timing when these days will come: Mark 13:32,33 Acts 1:6 reveals remaining confusion They were looking: 1 Thess. 1:10; Titus 2:11-14; 2 Tim. 4:8 This is not the time to rehash the book of Revelation I stand by what I perceived to be a straightforward system of interpretation "When the plain sense makes good sense, seek no other sense lest it result in nonsense" The desire to see: the longing for the beginning of the Kingdom one of the days can mean the first of the days Believers should be longing (desire) for that day 2 Peter 3:13 2 Thessalonians 1:4-10 Revelation 6:9, 10 Ye shall not see it the kingdom would come only at the appointed time rather than whenever they desired it UNDERSTANING THE TIME 17:23-25 We have had many through the years make money predicting the return of Jesus. 88 Reasons Why the Rapture Will Be in 1998 There was a day if you conducted a prophecy conference attendance would be through the roof. Not so much of a conference on the Holy Spirit. People like to be thrilled, not chilled Dynamic of Time 17:23, 24 Don t jump every time someone declares its arrival The coming of the Son of Man (Messianic title) will be an open event as lightening which flashes from one part of the heaven to the other Matthew 24:27 His coming will not be in obscurity or limited to one locality It will not be subtle but obvious; not slow but quick all the tribes of the earth will see His coming in power Matt. 24:30 Revelation 1:7 every eye shall see Him At that time there will be no time to change one s mind about Jesus That s why we have warning after warning Have you ever changed your mind about a certain activity because you thought, Jesus might come His day refers back to the first of the days of the Son of Man v. 22 This term is not identical to the day of Christ 1 Cor. 1:8; 5:5; 2 Cor. 1:14 That speaks of the rapture This day will be part of the Day of the Lord Tribulation Period through the Kingdom age The suddenness calls for patience, hope, diligence, moderation, personal holiness Delay of Time 17:25 The next event is not setting up a kingdom; it is rejection and death must not optional, binding, inevitable Suffering and rejection were never understood by the disciples, although predicted many times 9:44, 45 24:44 it is there; the Law, the Prophets, the Psalms rejected generation: race, clan, or to one s contemporaries John 1:11 the Jews living at the time of Jesus Matthew 12:39 evil generation Matthew 12:45 wicked generation Matthew 16:4 adulterous generation Matthew 17:17 faithless and perverse generation Jesus could not come until He fulfilled His purpose for coming Jesus cannot return until the Jews repent Acts 3:19 repent, return, time of refreshing Acts 3:21 the period of restoration of all things Acts 3:25 you are the sons of the prophets not were the sons Acts 3:26 for you first God raised His Servant God promised a time of Kingdom and that time will yet come. Zechariah 12:10 And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the Spirit of grace and of supplications: and they shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for him, as one mourns for his only son, and shall be in bitterness for him, as one that is in bitterness for his firstborn. THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 Different Dimensions of the Kingdom The first, is the already now 17:20, 21 The Second is the not yet 17:22-37 The Inquiry of the Time The Jews had a real interest in the Kingdom The Pharisees were Premillennialist The Confusion of the Time The already now kingdom would be inward Emil Schurer (1844-1910) The History of the Jewish People in the Time of Christ (1885-89) They were not looking for a Savior because they did not need one There first needs to be an understanding of the invisible Kingdom John 3:3 If you do not recognize King Jesus, you will not see the Kingdom Colossians 1:13; 4:1 How can you enter the Kingdom when you reject the King? Luke 13:18, 19 The Sign of the Time 17:22 This is far from the glamorous, victorious view of the Jews Mark 13:32,33 Believers should be longing for that day 2 Peter 3:13 2 Thessalonians 1:4-10 Revelation 6:9, 10 UNDERSTANING THE TIME 17:23-25 Dynamic of Time 17:23, 24 His coming will not be in obscurity or limited to one locality Revelation 1:7 Delay of Time 17:25 must not optional, binding, inevitable Jesus could not come until He fulfilled His purpose for coming Jesus cannot return until the Jews repent ============================================= === SERMON: message(154).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/14/2025 1:28:25 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 INTRODUCTION Last week in verses 20-25, Jesus is responding to the pestering question from the Pharisees as to when the kingdom He keeps speaking of will arrive The fact of the return of the Lord Jesus Christ is well documented in Scripture In fact, the return of the Lord Jesus Christ must take place to fulfill the words of God the Father, God the Son and God the Holy Spirit There will be a physical kingdom on this earth; all the prophecies speaking to that will be as completed as the 300-some prophecies for the first coming. As Scripture said Jesus would be born of a virgin, it happened Jesus would be born in Bethlehem, it happened Jesus would be the Son of David, it took place Everything exactly as the Old Testament said it would Act 1:9-11 will so come in like manner visibly, bodily, cloud, Mt.of Olives Rev. 1:7 cloud, visibly, bodily Zechariah 14:4 Mt. of Olives I acknowledge there is much debate as the when Jesus will come There are some who believe the world will become such a fine place that Jesus will come There are those who believe Jesus will come to establish His kingdom There are those who reject the whole concept and say there will be no kingdom My approach to interpretation on the doctrine of the end times: eschatology When common sense makes perfect sense, to look for any other sense is nonsense. A 1,000 year reign of Christ from a throne in Jerusalem is expressly predicted in Scripture Sinful man does not have the capacity to establish world-wide peace So, for any kingdom to become reality, Jesus must come and do it. Luke s record in chapter 17 does not give to us a chronological table for the Second Coming but it does speak to a primary characteristic: judgment Beginning in verse 20, Jesus is correcting the Jewish mindset They have willingly overlooked a major aspect of the Messiah setting up a kingdom He must first clean house; remove all who have rejected His kingship The Inquiry of the Time The Confusion of the Time The Sign of the Time Understanding the Time A simple review states there will be two comings: the already now and the not yet The already now is comes without observation and is internal The not yet will arrive in a grand display of power to be seen by everyone. There will be admittance in the not yet kingdom if there is no participation in the already now kingdom Jesus yet needs to face the suffering and rejection by the generation He was ministering to The End Times discussion continues by highlighting the lack of anticipation by the peoples We will continue by adding to the last major point from last week: UNDERSTANDING THE TIME The Dynamic of Time: Every eye shall see Him The Delay of Time: Suffering and Rejection The Denial of Time Jesus goes back to the book of Genesis and uses two events as illustrations Noah s Flood and the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah Note first of all, Jesus uses two events which many today refuse to accept as real He does not say, just as it may have happened But as in the days of Noah likewise in the days of Lot Here is the standard of comparison; It is certain and it is sudden People were unprepared as they went about their daily lives 2 Peter 2:5 Noah is described as a teacher of righteousness We cannot be definitive on the timing of building the ark We do know: Gen. 5:32 we meet Noah at 500 years old Gen. 7:6 Noah is 600 years old when the flood began That is all we know The 120 years you may have heard about probably comes from Genesis 6:3 the days of man were set at 120 years There were warnings What warnings before His Second Coming; 3 major ones Revelation 7:3-9 144,000 sealed Jews Revelation 11:3-14 the two Jewish witnesses Revelation 14:6, 7 a flying angel Fear God, and give Him glory, because the hour of His judgment has come; worship Him who made the heaven and the earth and sea and springs of waters. Matthew 24:14 the gospel of the kingdom will be preached in the whole world as a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come Cataclysmic judgment is all around them; special messengers are sent to give warning; and the world goes on with its busy routines One would think that by the time all the judgments of tribulation were poured out upon the people: Seal, Trumpet, Bowel; the people would be on their face repenting. Rev. 16:9, 11, 21 Miracles do not translate to conversions A legitimate question is How in the world can one not repent in the face of such horrible judgment? 2 Thessalonians 1:8 2 Thessalonians 2:10-12 They rejected the clear message of the gospel when presented They are devoted to themselves These are the days of the return of the Son of Man; the Messiah The wickedness of man will reach new depths of depravity 2 Thess. 2:7 the restrainer will be removed The Holy Spirit s present ministry will be lifted and sin will reach an all-time low v. 29 the same deal with the destruction of Sodom some warning but the judgment came suddenly men will be as blind as those in Sodom who wanted to rape the angels Lot s family was part of the culture of the city to the place where his sons-in-law laughed at the warning; Gen. 19:14 Much of Lot s life is a picture of the consequences of greed and the negative influence of a sinful environment. Lot knew God, but he chose to live among people who would lead his family into sin and complacency. But Lot s story is also an illustration of God s great mercy in spite of Lot s poor choices, God saved him and his daughters from a violent end in Sodom and preserved his line throughout the ages. As God delivered 8 before the flood; here it is only 3 Lot and daughters were delivered but not without supernatural help Gen. 19:16 But he (LOT) hesitated. So the men seized his hand and the hand of his wife and the hands of his two daughters, for the compassion of the LORD was upon him; and they brought him out, and put him outside the city." I am convinced the deliverance of Noah s family and Lot s family is a picture of God s deliverance of His bride, the church, before the coming days of tribulation. 2 Peter 2:7-9 and delivered righteous Lot, who was very distressed by the lustful life of the wicked (for that righteous man dwelling among them, was tormented in his righteous soul from day to day with seeing and hearing lawless deeds): the Lord knows how to deliver the godly out of temptation and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment; 1 Thessalonians 4:16, 17 Each event: flood and brimstone was preceded by warning from God, a rejection of God by the wicked, the longsuffering of God, and the judgment of God It was life as usual: were eating, were drinking, were marrying, were being given in marriage; were eating were drinking, were buying, were selling, were planting, were building No wonder Jesus coming is described as a thief in the night (Rev. 16:15) Humanity will be carrying on life as normal without considering God will destroy them all Destruction came suddenly and decisively A separation will take place: To the one side: Come, you who are blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world To the other: Depart from Me, accursed ones, into the eternal fire which has been prepared for the devil and his angels CONCLUSION We have more to do to get through verse 37, but the theme will continue When the King comes to set up His kingdom, only those who will be part of the inner kingdom will be allowed to enter the physical kingdom An incredible judgment will remove all the wicked, unbelievers, lost, children of wrath, those part of the kingdom of darkness THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 The Inquiry of the Time The Confusion of the Time The Sign of the Time UNDERSTANDING THE TIME The Dynamic of Time The Delay of Time The Denial of Time People were unprepared as they went about their daily lives 3 Major Warnings Revelation 7:3-9 144,000 sealed Jews Revelation 11:3-14 the two Jewish witnesses Revelation 14:6, 7 a flying angel Cataclysmic judgment is all around them They rejected the clear message of the gospel when presented The wickedness of man will reach new depths of depravity The deliverance of Noah s family and Lot s family is a picture of God s deliverance of His bride, the church, before the coming days of tribulation 2 Peter 2:7-9 Humanity will be carrying on life as normal without considering God Destruction came suddenly and decisively ============================================= === SERMON: message(155).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 6/28/2025 10:20:48 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 THE INQUIRY OF THE TIME THE CONFUSION OF THE TIME THE SIGN OF THE TIME INTRODUCTION Many of us remember the time of going to the movies whether in a theatre or drive in and knowing that before the special feature they would show previews of coming attractions. Some of you probably remember when they showed headline news clippings from the war Coming Attractions were just that; to attract you enough to return to part with another $1.50. You could put on your calendar and anticipate another evening of entertainment My point of reference is the early 70 s; no idea the present cost Jesus has shared with us some Previews of His Second Coming The alerts came from illustrations of OT historical accounts of the world-wide flood and the destruction of Sodom and surrounding cities As in the days of Noah ; Likewise also in the days of Lot Most people will reject the warnings and continue life as usual Only those who are part of the inner kingdom will be welcomed into the physical kingdom Some gospel songs have contributed to the confusion of the two comings of Jesus; one in the air and one to the earth I will always try to distinguish the comings by saying Rapture or Second Coming Rapture being the translation of the word caught up in 1 Thess. 4:17 Meaning to seize, to snatch, to pluck, to take by force, to be carried away UNDERSTANDING THE TIME The Dynamic of Time: Every Eye Shall See Him The Delay of Time: Suffering and Rejection The Denial of Time: Noah and Lot The Disclosure at the Time: 17:30-37 The Disclosure will Bring Death 30-33 The Disclosure will Bring Division 34-37 The Disclosure at the Time 17:30-37 Similar conditions will prevail when the Son of Man is revealed revealed to uncover, unveil, take the lid off English = apocalypse Also used in the NT to speak of the rapture; 1 Cor. 1:7; 1 Pet. 1:7, 13; 4:13 Life will go on as usual; focus on all the temporary stuff People will be prosperous but unprepared Matthew 24:10, 12 many will fall away; the love of many will grow cold If the Rapture of the church would happen today, in the next 7 years most of the Jesus rejecting folks we know will soon be killed, some will be saved and die, and some will be saved and survive the horrors of tribulation (Rev. 6-19) and enter the kingdom. those who endure until the end shall be saved Matt. 24:13 v. 31 Matthew 24:15 reveals that the flight of 17:31 follows the abomination of desolation spoken in Daniel 9:27 The defilement of that temple in the middle of the 7-year tribulation period Luk 21:31-33 (Matt. 24:15-22) describe the conditions of that day A time known as the Great Tribulation (Matt. 24:21) This will be A time of great persecution for the Jews because the Antichrist will break his covenant with Israel and will then seek to destroy the Jewish people (Daniel 9:27) People are warned to flee without grabbing any valuables Nothing can slow down the hasty escape This is the time when hearts will be revealed The unveiling of Jesus will unveil the hearts of men. Do I love His appearing or do I love the things the world provides? How easily can I be separated from things I consider valuable? Not about people but things We know when folks escape because of a coming hurricane or disaster where some planning is involved, preparations can be made and some valuables can be retrieved or protected Not so when such disaster comes suddenly Jesus says to flee and leave all behind; don t even think about hesitating Here will be the Disclosure of hearts: occupied by stuff or Jesus Disclosure Will Bring Death 32 Remember Lot s wife Genesis 19:17, 26- Exihibit A The danger of looking back Those who seek to carry their possessions will lose their lives even as Lot s wife lost her life because she longed for the things of Sodom She came out physically but her heart was still in Sodom How many people today have joined a church but have not joined to Jesus? Romans 7:4 Therefore, my brethren, you also were made to die to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you might be joined to another, to Him who was raised from the dead, in order that we might bear fruit for God. Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world Lot s wife was like the seed on rocky soil. She took a few steps toward God but the cares of this world soon choked out the seed. J. C. Ryle: Lot's wife is meant to be a beacon and a warning to all professing Christians. It may be feared that many will be found like her in the day of Christ's second advent. There are many in the present day who go a certain length in religion. They conform to the outward ways of Christian relatives and friends. They speak the "language of Canaan." They use all the outward ordinances of religion. But all this time their souls are not right in the sight of God. The world is in their hearts, and their hearts are in the world. And by and by, in the day of sifting, their unsoundness will be exposed to all the world. Their Christianity will prove rotten at the core. The case of Lot's wife will not stand alone. Remember this is in the context of the abomination of desolation in the temple. The antichrist will be turning on the Jews which will make Hitler s holocaust look lame. It is completely Jewish in the context, but the principle remains for us 33 We are to turn our backs to that which the world offers The reference to Lot s wife leads to the principle that whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lost it; and whosoever shall loses his life shall save it (Matt. 10:39; Luke 9:24) When one lives for the things of the world, he loses the life God intended him to live When one dies to the things of the world, he lives the life God intends him to live John 12:24, 25 a grain of wheat must die before it can reproduce Paul often taught the truth that life comes from death 2 Corinthians 1:8-11; 4:10-18; 6:9 Galatians 2:20; 5:24, 25 When Jesus returns whether it is the Rapture or Second Coming, hearts will be revealed It will then be known who loves Jesus and who loves the world It will then be known who took up his cross and who played the game. Scripture records the stories of those who died physically because they sought to live for the things of this world Lot s wife, Achan, Joshua 7; Ananias and Sapphira, Acts 5 Demas is an example of a believer who sought to save his life but lost it in the sense of missing the life God intended 2 Timothy 4:10 Luke 18:18-27 we will read of the rich young ruler who lost eternal life Disclosure will Bring Division 17:34-37 The whole world will be involved at the Return of Jesus Nighttime, noon, day; sleeping, grinding, working The separation which many experience even now will be permanent All will be impacted; some taken, others left Using the illustration of Noah and Lot: the ones left are the believers Compare Matthew 24:37-41 I also acknowledge disagreements among Bible students v. 37 no specific timetable presented Just know that where you see vultures, there lay the dead CONCLUSION One may come to different conclusions as the exact timing of the text One thing that must be agreed upon is the command to be expecting and looking for Jesus to return. I am looking for the Rapture; others may be looking for a physical kingdom. Still others may be looking for an antichrist. Jesus simply expects occupy until I come (19:13) John Newton: Our work is great; our time is short; the consequences of our labor are infinite. Work, for the night is coming, Work thro' the morning hours; Work while the dew is sparkling, Work 'mid springing flow'rs. Work when the day grows brighter, Under the glowing sun; Work, for the night is coming, When man's work is done. Work for the night is coming, Work thro' the sunny noon; Fill brightest hours with labor-- Rest comes sure and soon. Give every flying minute Something to keep in store; Work, for the night is coming, When man works no more. Work for the night is coming, Under the sunset skies: While their bright tints are glowing, Work, for daylight flies. Work till the last beam fadeth, Fadeth to shine no more; Work, while the night is dark'ning, When man's work is o'er. Written by Annie Walker, age 18, after reading John 9:4 John 9:4 I must work the works of him who sent me, while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work. THE END TIMES Luke 17:20-37 UNDERSTANDING THE TIME The Dynamic of Time The Delay of Time The Denial of Time The Disclosure at the Time 17:30-37 revealed to uncover, apocalypse Matthew 24:15 the abomination of desolation Daniel 9:27 A time of great persecution for the Jews This is the time when hearts will be revealed Do I love His appearing, or do I love the things the world provides? Jesus says to flee and leave all behind Disclosure Will Bring Death Remember Lot s wife Genesis 19 Romans 7:4 Lot s wife was like the seed on rocky soil When one lives for the things of the world, he loses the life God intended him to live Scripture records the stories of those who died physically because they sought to live for the things of this world Achan, Joshua 7; Ananias and Sapphira, Acts 5 Disclosure will Bring Division 17:34-37 Just know that where you see vultures, there lay the dead ============================================= === SERMON: message(156).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/5/2025 9:01:01 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message LUKE 18 BELIEVING PRAYER Luke 18:1-8 INTRODUCTION It is easy to assume this parable is presented to encourage us in our praying to be persistent; continuing to storm the gates of heaven until we receive our requests. Now, there is nothing wrong with fervent persistence in seeking the Lord in prayer But this section is about contrast, not comparison The contrast is this: (J. Vernon McGee) If this unjust judge would hear a poor widow because she kept coming continually, then (BY WAY OF CONTRAST) why do you get discouraged going to God who is not an unjust judge, but Who actually wants to hear and answer prayer?" And the context must also be considered: 17:20 The implication is that one might lose heart because of the delay in Christ s coming WWW: This parable is not urging us to pester God until He acts; it is saying that we do not need to pester Him because He is ready and willing to answer our prayers We continue in our praying because we know God wants to hear; He cares Don t fall into the trap of believing that the accumulation of your prayers will ultimately reach the place where God cannot ignore. That places God in the same category as the unjust judge The contrast here is between the worst of man (judge) and the best of God God is not like the judge; God is good and gracious We are not like the widow, for we are His chosen ones. Kent Huges: so a distressed bugging of God is in fact inadequate prayer Prayer is not bugging God, it is loving God Our Lord is saying, When you come to God in prayer, do you think that God is an unjust judge? When you come to Him in prayer, do you think He is a cheap politician? Do you think God is doing things just for political reasons? (McGee) THE TARGET Audience: Disciples 17:22 By application that is you and me Subject: the days of the Son of Man 17:22 Desire to see Lesson: Parable 18:1 He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up An earthly story with a heavenly application Purpose: Pray Some parables were explained at the conclusion: 8:9 This lesson is stated at the beginning Matthew Henry: the key is hanging at the door The promise of the Second Coming of Jesus has been given, but the delay is long It would be so easy to be tempted to give up one s faith and cease to pray with confidence that the day of Disclosure will come Hebrews 10:35, 36 carries some of the same weight Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompense of reward. For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise" Always At all times whenever, wherever; on any occasion 18:7 day and night Continual prayer: frequently, regular intervals Not Continuous prayer: nonstop It is the atmosphere in which we live. Our hearts are constantly desiring before Him without even saying a word. The holy desire of the heart, living life in the will of God, constant communion It is setting the mind on things above The attitude of our heart because Jesus can come at any moment 21:36 We pray for Jesus to come We pray that we will be living well when He comes Prayer is a necessity, it is Plan A, it is a priority Alternative: lose heart; fall, feint, be weary, be discouraged, quit as our society becomes more like a rotting corpse, (17:37; WWW) The slow pollution will affect our spiritual lives Don t give in to the bad, but breathe the fresh air of heaven It is easy to lose heart in prayer; prayer involves work Prayer often becomes a Last Resort rather than a First Resource (Guzik) D. Jeremiah: Prayer should be preventative, not only remedial Prayer is not the last thought; it is the first thought Pray that we do not enter into temptation; that s prevention Jesus gave us a model prayer: thy kingdom come What do we do in the meantime? Pray When you are weary in body and soul Cumbered with many a care When work is claiming its strength-taking toll Make it a matter of prayer. And when you're discouraged, distraught or dismayed Sinking almost in despair Remember there's One who will come to your aid, If you'll make it a matter of prayer. And when you are lost in this world's tangled maze When life seems a hopeless affair Direction will come for all of your ways If you'll make it a matter of prayer. We often hear Take time to pray It should be: We don t have time not to pray THE QUESTION 18:8 Will the Son of Man find faith on the earth? Brings us back to the original question of the kingdom Some assume the answer is No , but the question is open ended Luke is the only one who includes this question He places it at the end of the parable of the widow how does not take no for an answer Parable lesson: should always pray and not lose heart The widow had faith in her petition; she knew she was on the right side, she knew she was in a system where women were not recognized, she had no one working on her behalf, the odds were stacked against her Yet, she did not let up; she had faith she was right The judge unjust, wicked; no fear of God, no respect of man Yet he yielded to the persistence of the widow, not because of justice but simply to get her off his back How much more will a loving Father listen to the plea of His elect children in their cry for vindication? Revelation 6:10 And they cried with a loud voice, saying, How long, O Lord, holy and true, do you not judge and avenge our blood on them that dwell on the earth? Then comes the Big Question The day of Christ s coming is quick, not calendar quick, but when it finally happens There are many examples of a time delay in God s promises Noah: going to be a flood; 100 years and no rain Abraham: have a son; at least 25 years and beyond childbearing age Joseph: family bow before; raised out of an Egyptian prison Israel: great nation; 400 years in Egypt Malachi: coming Messiah; 400 years When the time of the Second Coming is right, will there be anyone still knocking on the throne room of heaven pleading for that coming? The context suggests the answer is NO It will be like the time of Noah and Lot; people will be looking after their own affairs, accumulating wealth, wrapped up in their own little world The lesson impressed by this discourse on the listeners is that they must keep on the alert and be ready for that day when it comes. They must trust God and not lose heart CONCLUSION It is not easy to persevere in prayer; we are part of the instant gratification crowd Paul: remove this thorn ; God: I have something better for you God often delays for us to fine tune our requests We can pray for something when the emotions are high and later realize that might not have been the best approach. James 5:1-8 Jesus is calling us to a persevering faith Will He find that when He returns to earth? Believers have been waiting and while suffering at the hands of sinners Sin in society is getting worse; corruption within the church is evident Jesus is not saying that when He returns there will be no believers He is saying the characteristic of the world at that time will not be faith Will you make a regular part of your praying: Thy kingdom come ? It has been promised He will come; we believe He will come Here He says, keep praying for it to come He will come; He will be faithful to His elect; He will bring vindication; He will establish His throne; He will be exalted Revelation 22:20 He who testifies these things says, "Yes, I come quickly." Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus BELIEVING PRAYER Luke 18:1-8 This section is about contrast, not comparison The context must also be considered: 17:20 Prayer is not bugging God, it is loving God THE TARGET Disciples 17:22 Subject: the days of the Son of Man 17:22 Lesson: Parable 18:1 Purpose: Pray Hebrews 10:35, 36 Continual prayer: setting the mind on things above Don t give in to the bad, but breathe the fresh air of heaven Prayer often becomes a Last Resort rather than a First Resource THE QUESTION 18:8 How much more will a loving Father listen to the plea of His elect children in their cry for vindication? Revelation 6:10 When the time of the Second Coming is right, will there be anyone still knocking on the throne room of heaven pleading for that coming? The lesson impressed by this discourse on the listeners is that they must keep on the alert and be ready for that day when it comes. Revelation 22:20 He who testifies these things says, "Yes, I come quickly." Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus ============================================= === SERMON: message(157).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/12/2025 9:57:56 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FULL OF YOURSELF Luke 18:10-30 INTRODUCTION The warning: Be careful who thinks he stands, lest he fall God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble Humility comes before honor Looking at our chapter, it appears Luke strings together 3 stories teaching the same lesson 18:14 They were addressed to v. 9 trusted in themselves that they were righteous He begins by addressing another aspect of prayer; adding to the illustration of the widow; teaching us that if she can continue her pursuit of justice knowing she was right, how much more should we who have the promise of the Lord s return continue praying Thy kingdom come Will Jesus find that kind of faith when He returns? Or will life be like the days of Noah and Lot where life goes on despite the warnings of coming judgment? Will our faith remain in tune with Scripture, or will we grow weary because of the delay? So, we have three accounts: the parable of the two men praying in the temple; the disciples forbidding the presence of children; and the young rich guy. Each account speaks to the initial problem in v. 9 trusted in themselves PRAYER, PEONS 18:15-17; POSSESSIONS 18:18-30 PEOPLE CAN SHOW THEY ARE FULL OF THEMSELVES WHEN THEY PRAY 18:10-14 Puffed Up Pharisee After speaking to the matter of Faith in our praying Luke records Jesus thoughts about the attitude one must have in prayer We understand his thoughts about himself because of how he thinks of others: despised - to count as nothing; look down upon others literally the rest Self-righteous people boast of their achievements and despise all others Trusted in themselves other translations relied on themselves conceited confident of their own piety full of confidence in themselves Full of themselves Pharisees: separated ones , known for their separation from Greek culture Major concern with the externals of religion Publicans: tax collectors of the day Often classified with sinners Matthew 9:10, 11; 11:19 Or harlots Matt. 21:32, 33 And Gentiles Matt. 18:17 The scene is probably either the morning or evening sacrifice: 9 and 3 There is an animal blood sacrifice, they symbol of atonement So it is more than just praying; it is worship; seeking God s forgiveness Some attend to seek God s favor; others to seek the favor of other men 18:11, 12 Pharisee: no doubt standing in a prominent place Matthew 6:5 with himself does not mean he was praying silently Wholly self-centered and self-righteous Literally: towards himself these things he was praying I five times in two sentences; no doubt who is on the throne in his heart Any praise was directed to himself, not to God Edersheim on I thank You - Never, perhaps, were words of thanksgiving spoken in less thankfulness than these We are told a common prayer for a Jew: I thank you God that I am not a Gentile, not an unlearned man, and I am not a woman It would be the same as if we would come to the end of this account and pray, Thank you, God, that I am not like this Pharisee His religion became his ruin. How sad to walk away from a religious service and feel better about your sin. Extortioner: guilty of robbery Not unjust: conformed to what is right No adulterer To top it off: not like this publican even as this the taxcollector Judging others even as he was praying Jews were only commanded to fast one day a year; Day of Atonement Lev. 16:19, 20 Isaiah 38:1-7 the nation was rebuked for going through a fast without a changed life Matt. 6:16-18 Jesus warned of fasting to impress others Tithing of possessions: refers to what one obtains; income Going beyond the Biblical command to tithe only certain crops and flocks Num 18:21; Deut. 14:22 Humble Publican 18:13 Tax collector: occupied with his own sinfulness He knows he is unworthy, the weight of being alienated from God Common prayer posture was arms crossed one s chest; He is beating his Mourning his sinful state; expression of extreme sorrow Only other occurrence; 23:48 as people departed the cross be propitious to me, the sinner a holy telegram 6 words to his prayer Dealing with God on a personal basis; not about others Prayer content is the opposite of the Pharisee Speaks of mercy, atonement; God would be appeased of his sin Propitiation: the satisfaction of God s righteous anger Trusting the blood of the offered lamb would cover Please apply the atonement to me Have you ever pled guilty before God? I am a sinner in need of a Savior Forgiving Savior 18:14 Tone of authority It is not the man who is "good" who is justified, but the man who knows he is wicked that is justified. That had to stun the listeners because that is not what the rabbis taught. Tax collector returns justified: being made right or righteous Both men went up. Both men went down One went up exalting himself but came down downgraded that one One went up in humility but came down exalted by God Romans 3:24; 5:9 Don t miss this: the tax collector was justified declared righteous He received a righteousness not his own MacArthur: Jesus just announced that in one moment an extreme sinner can be pronounced instantly righteous without any works, without any merit, without any worthiness, without any law-keeping, without any moral achievement, religious achievement, spiritual accomplishment or ritual. All the sinner does is receive the gift. Note: there is no cross, no resurrection. This is an OT conversion The sacrifice of Christ was pictured in the animal Righteousness and justification, salvation, is a gift from God No works, no time lapse, no penance, no ceremony, no sacrament It s a gift. That s the gospel. The atonement was worthless to the self-righteous one. He came down more wretched than when he went up The self-righteous have no need of a Savior The sinner, however, yearns for grace The Central Truth 16:15 Spiritual principle: exalt self shall be abased; humble self shall be exalted Exalted here is a synonym for salvation, righteousness Everyone who tries to save himself will suffer eternal loss and punishment The damned think they're good. The saved know they're wicked. The damned believe the kingdom of God is for those worthy of it. The saved know the kingdom of God is for those who know they're unworthy of it. The damned believe eternal life is earned. The saved know it's a gift. The damned seek God's commendation. The saved seek His forgiveness. Note: this goes for everyone there is no fine print James 4:6 Isaiah 57:15 (LXX) Thus saith the Most High, Who dwells on high for ever, Holy in the holies, is His Name, the Most High resting in the holies, and giving patience (makrothumia ) to the faint-hearted (oligopsuchos ), and giving life (zoe ) to the brokenhearted (suntribo ): Pharaoh, Goliath, Haman, Sennacherib, Nebuchadnezzar, Herod, are all cases in point. Isaiah 66:2 Luke 14:7-11 Let s take a quick Humility Test 1. Do you feel joy when others are honored? 2. Do you honestly and openly admit sin? 3. Do you seek truth from others regarding your weaknesses? 4. Do you accept criticism graciously? 5. Do you turn all worry, anxiety and concern over to the Lord? 6. Do you respond with humility when you have been replaced? 7. Do you pursue godliness in all that you do? FULL OF YOURSELF Luke 18:10-30 PEOPLE CAN SHOW THEY ARE FULL OF THEMSELVES WHEN THEY PRAY 18:10-14 Puffed Up Pharisee despised - to count as nothing with himself - towards himself these things he was praying Edersheim Never, perhaps, were words of thanksgiving spoken in less thankfulness than these His religion became his ruin Humble Publican 18:13 a holy telegram 6 words to his prayer Propitiation: the satisfaction of God s righteous anger Forgiving Savior 18:14 It is not the man who is "good" who is justified, but the man who knows he is wicked that is justified. Romans 3:24; 5:9 justified declared righteous All the sinner does is receive the gift. The sacrifice of Christ was pictured in the animal The Central Truth 16:15 Exalted here is a synonym for salvation James 4:6 Isaiah 57:15 ============================================= === SERMON: message(158).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 7/18/2025 2:42:11 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message PEOPLE CAN BE FULL OF THEMSELVES Luke 18:9-30 INTRODUCTION Looking at a section of Luke which began with a question in 17:20 When is the kingdom coming? Jesus corrects their thinking announcing a delay for the necessary rejection and suffering. The warning comes as He speaks directly on the nation s lack of urgency as they will be living just as they did in the day judgments came in the time of Noah and Lot. People then were even full of themselves as living their lives was much more important than taking heed to whatever warnings were given. 9:33 is your key verse Jesus continues identifying the lack of faith in His return using a parable of a widow who knew her cause was just and hounded the judge until he made it right. Jesus is looking for that kind of faith and wonders if there will be any evidence of such when He does return. Key Verse: Don t grown weary waiting and praying Last week we started looking at 3 stories highlighting how easy it is for man to become wrapped up in his own world just like the days of Noah and Lot I entitled the section FULL OF THEMSELVES , noting how pride can easily slip into every part of our lives Vs. 9-14; we can be Full of Ourselves when we pray Two men: one of the religious elite; the other a collaborator with Rome in collecting taxes. A job noted for cheating and selling out their countrymen. One full of pride; the other repentant Key verse: 14 Our attitude in prayer reflects our heart s condition We pray for Christ s return, but are we interested in His glory or how it might best be beneficial to us?
