FULL OF THEMSELVES IN PRAYER FULL OF THEMSELVES AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 WHEN AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 Peon definition: compulsory servitude; a person who does hard or dull work A person of low social status, insignificant Within Jewish culture the woman in vs. 1-8 would be the Peon And Jesus has just given us an example of one acceptable to God He goes from a parable to a living demonstration A practical illustration of His delight in humility Little children: Luke infants; Matthew and Mark little children Infant to very small child William Barclay: It was the custom for mothers to bring their children to some distinguished Rabbi on their first birthday that he might bless them. (There is no baptism here) Jesus was upset with the disciples behavior Luke rebuke = admonish, indignant Mark 10:14 indignant ; greatly afflicted; a strong word of deep emotion Matthew 21:15 the chief priests reaction to the Triumphal Entry crowd Mark 10:41 Disciples against James and John Jesus was greatly grieved akin to a "holy furor rebuking imperfect tense; He kept on rebuking. Repeated scene: A child is brought; Disciples send away; Jesus rebukes The 12 did not have the compassion found in Jesus Matt. 15:21ff sought for Jesus to send away the Canaanite woman seeking healing for her daughter Matt 14:15ff desired to send the hungry crowd away Common to seek a rabbi s blessing in prayer Suffer permit, allow, do not hinder; command requiring quick action Now, in defense of the disciples: what did they know about children? Do we know anything that would give us a clue? We know Peter was stuck with a MIL. Sorry, Peter had a sick MIL. Fishermen, tax collector, zealot: never a mention about family The disciples could easily see children as a distraction, annoyance, a bother Saw their job as to promote the important adult stuff You probably know of invitations to some event with the notation: No Children Please Children The kingdom of God is characterized by childlike faith Saving faith has the simplicity of trust which characterizes a child Matthew 18:1 who is the greatest in the kingdom? Jesus calls a child Except you be converted and become as little children, you shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven 1 Peter 2:2 as newborn babies desire the sincere milk of the Word of God Disciples saw themselves as guardians, protectors, keep out the lowlifes Jesus saw children as precious to the Kingdom Don t ignore the children; don t despise the presence of children Christianity has always been the religion to safeguard the rights of children Or it may be one who is young in the faith; a weaker believer It could be someone struggling in understanding even basic biblical truth While we are talking about our missionary budget, I would challenge us to give careful consideration to reaching children: LifeWise Academy; Camp ministry D. L. Moody once returned from a meeting and reported two and a half conversions. Two adults and a child, I suppose? asked his host. No, said Moody, two children and an adult. The children gave their whole lives. The adult had only half of his left to give. The Kingdom belongs to such as these This is the Kingdom within you Children are sinners, selfish, loud, disobedient Children possess a spiritual capacity for helpless dependence Jesus is not talking innocence; not even so much about trustfulness, wonder The key quality for entrance into the kingdom is total dependence, humility Challenge: Somewhere amid all the demands on you as a servant of Jesus Christ, take the time to hold the hand of an aging believer, to comfort a tired mother, or to cradle a child until she sleeps. Hudson Taylor, the pioneer missionary to China, scrawled this note as he neared the end of his life: I am so weak that I cannot work; I cannot read my Bible; I cannot even pray. I can only lie still in God s arms like a child, and trust. v. 17 without such quality: shall not not enter ; shall absolutely not No other way for entrance into the kingdom than as a childlike converted turn around; be simple, dependent, helpless, unaffected, unpretentious, unambitious. CONCLUSION Let the children come to Me There are plenty of surveys repeating the news that most people come to Christ for salvation in their younger years. All of us were impacted by the number of pictures and videos of the recent flooding in Texas, especially the focus on the young girls at Camp Mystic. You may have seen memes of little girls getting off a bus in heaven and running into the arms of Jesus. Things that tug at the emotions; not necessarily what is true When it comes to what we don t know, I lean on the truth that whatever My God does is right. What do know is from our text We as adults cannot become so Full of Ourselves as to not appreciate what children bring to a church ministry And whenever possible, we are to be the tools God uses to encourage, instruct, and model Christ before them In recent passages: 16:15 17:2 17:33 18:14 PEOPLE CAN BE FULL OF THEMSELVES Luke 18:9-30 FULL OF THEMSELVES AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 Peon A person of low social status, insignificant Little children: Infant to very small child Luke rebuke = admonish, indignant The 12 did not have the compassion found in Jesus The disciples could easily see children as a distraction The kingdom of God is characterized by childlike faith Matthew 18:1 Disciples saw themselves as guardians Jesus saw children as precious to the Kingdom Children possess a spiritual capacity for helpless dependence v. 17 without such quality: shall not not enter PEOPLE CAN BE FULL OF THEMSELVES Luke 18:9-30 FULL OF THEMSELVES AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 Peon A person of low social status, _______________ Little children: Infant to very __________ child Luke rebuke = admonish, ______________ The 12 did not have the _______________ found in Jesus The disciples could easily see children as a ______________ The kingdom of God is characterized by ______________ faith Matthew _______ Disciples saw themselves as ______________ Jesus saw children as ______________ to the Kingdom Children possess a spiritual capacity for _______________ dependence v. 17 without such quality: shall ______ _______ enter ============================================= === SERMON: message(159).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/1/2025 12:27:19 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message FULL OF YOURSELF Luke 18:9-30 INTRODUCTION FULL OF YOURSELF IN PRAYER 18:9-14 FULL OF YOURSELF AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 FULL OF YOURSELF AND POSSESSIONS 18:18-30 Presentation of the Ruler Presentation of Respect Presentation of Request Presentation of Righteousness 18:19-30 Righteous Prodding 18:19 Question of Goodness; Designation of God Righteous Examination 18:20,21 Jesus answers his question, What must I do? You know the commandments Matt. 19:17 Keep the commandments Makes it sound like Jesus is teaching salvation is by works This is what the Jews taught; that is all they ever heard Leviticus 18:15 The Law plays a vital part in your salvation Galatians 3:24- The Law had a purpose: Romans 3:20; James 2:10 The young ruler missed the purpose of the Law; we are all sinners Leon Morris: "When anyone takes seriously the requirements of the law, he is on the way to coming to Christ." Jesus highlights commandments 5-9 Matthew 19:19 summarizes: Love your neighbor as yourself Basically asking: Have you really loved your neighbor? Why did Jesus leave out #10 Thou shall not covet ? One reason might be that this commandment does not deal with external, observable actions Easier to check off the other commandments Impulsive Reply: I believe he is totally sincere From every external measurement, he has done good I have kept a guard or senty; nothing had escaped his attention He was ignorant of the teaching of the Sermon on the Mt where Jesus clarified the purpose of the Law was to examine the heart not murder applies even the level of becoming angry His testimony is part of his self-deception If he had done all of this, why was his heart still empty; why the doubt? Within the eyes of man he was guiltless; but guilty before the eyes of God from my youth his bar mitzvah Please show me a teenager that has never reacted to the instructions of a parent Philippians 3:6 the testimony of Paul 3:9 turning away for external righteousness Righteous Exposure 18:22, 23 C. S. Lewis: Prosperity knits a man to the world. He feels like he is finding his place in it, while really it is finding its place in him. Do you own possessions or do possessions own you? Mark 10:21 And Jesus looking upon him loved him Fixed His gaze upon him; looking intently at him Jesus saw his heart and knew the turmoil His next words would bring I know you are not going to like this but . . . I am only doing this because I love you . . Matthew 19:21 If you wish to be complete attain your goal; teleios Matt. 5:48 perfect be perfect as your heavenly Father is perfect One thing you lack it is like saying you have climbed the ladder of success only to find out it is leaning against the wrong building The one thing is a failure to understand God's goodness and that it was perfect, setting a standard that no human being could possibly achieve by human effort! The Law that he was so diligent about obeying was never meant to save him, but to show his great need for forgiveness. The one thing he needed was a self-sacrificing devotion which characterizes each follower of Jesus Self-surrender implies a renunciation of his own achievement The rich young ruler is exposed He loves his possessions more than God; Commandment 1-4 He loves his possession more than his neighbor: 5-10 As he broke the two great commandments, he broke all 10 It is not about buying his way into heaven Jesus simply held up a mirror so the young man could see himself Jesus held up commandment #10 James 1:23-25 Sell and distribute do it now; don t go home and think about it Would demonstrate the humility of the tax collector; trustfulness of a child Such a response would reveal the presence of a new heart Ezekiel 36:26, 27 Oswald Chambers: notes a General Reference and a General Principle The General Reference here is selling material goods. The rich young ruler had deliberately to be destitute, deliberately to distribute, deliberately to discern where his treasure was, and devote himself to Jesus Christ. The principle underlying it is that I must detach myself from everything I possess. Many of us suppress our sense of property, we don t starve it, we suppress it. Undress yourself morally before God of everything that might be a possession until you are a mere conscious human being, and then give God that. Guzik - We may make two mistakes here. The one is to believe this applies to everyone, when Jesus never made this a general command to all who would follow Him, but especially to this one rich man whose riches were clearly an obstacle to his discipleship. Instead, many rich people can do more good in the world by continuing to make money and using those resources for the glory of God and the good of others. The second mistake is to believe this applies to no one, when there are clearly those today for whom the best thing they could do for themselves spiritually is to radically forsake the materialism that is ruining them. Treasure on Earth 12:33, 34; 17:33 and 18:14 are now illustrated Jesus is not talking about losing everything but gaining everything Everything that really counts Kent Hughes - Jesus always demands that those who come to him put away their gods, whether they be possessions, position, power, a person, or a passion! This man had an idol; he had a false god Jesus was calling him to faith; trust in Him Jesus is calling him to repentance; 1 Thess. 1:9 turn from idols to serve Oswald Chambers calls it being abandoned to God and very few achieve it The only turning here is the man turning his back on Jesus The cost was too high; he came running, he left broken He was slain by commandment #10; Romans 7:7-12 Righteous Entrance 18:24-27 A sobering statement to the disciples; Matt. 19:23 The listeners were shocked; if riches can t do it, then who can be saved? It is not the fact that a man has riches that keeps him from the kingdom of God but the fact that his riches had him. Money is a wonderful servant but a terrible master There is no mark of God s special favor in possessions, nor in the lack of them. Ray Stedman - This is a very remarkable statement that Jesus makes. In it he highlights...the terrible danger of affluence, of riches, of seeking to get rich and loving the things money can buy. This, he says, does terrible things to the soul. Most of us, if not openly then at least secretly, are envious of rich people. We wish we had money. We say so ourselves. And yet, if we really understood what Jesus is saying, we would not feel that way. We would feel sorry for them. We think them over privileged; Jesus says they are underprivileged. They are deprived people. There is so much they are robbed of by the things they have. Materialism is a deadly enemy of the kingdom of God Those with riches have a greater stake in this world Revelation 18:9-19 the fall of material Babylon And the Jesus presents a rhetorical hyperbolic statement The disciples reaction tells us this was to be taken literally Jesus consistently viewed the rich as underprivileged Wealth is a major hindrance for entrance into the kingdom In Luke 12, we had the story of the man building bigger barns In Luke 16, we had the story of the rich man and Lazarus You fool! This very night your soul is required of you; and now who will own what you have prepared? So is the man who stores up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God. (12:20, 21) Wealth naturally works at perverting one s values Hughes: We soon know the price of everything and the value of nothing. Paul tells Timothy, Command those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant (1 Timothy 6:17 ). Pride, arrogance, insensitivity, indifference, self-satisfaction, worldliness, and other ungodly mind-sets feed on affluence. And let s face it folks, all of us are richer than the majority of the world Jesus is not saying a rich man cannot be saved; it is only harder But a person who is ruled by money cannot be ruled by God Jesus never speaks of wealth as a blessing The needle is not a narrow gate in the city wall The "Eye of the Needle" has been claimed to be a gate in Jerusalem, which opened after the main gate was closed at night. A camel could only pass through this smaller gate if it was stooped and had its baggage removed. This story has been put forth since at least the 15th century, and possibly as far back as the 9th century. However, there is no widely accepted evidence for the existence of such a gate. Wikipedia Question: If the wealthy cannot get into heaven, (the cultural assumption) what possible chance does a poor man have? This is now beyond the rich to just anyone Answer: Possible only by God. Salvation is a miracle Phillips: Salvation is beyond purchase; beyond money; beyond price; and beyond all human standards of religion, morality, good works, and self-effort. Salvation is a miracle for the rich and poor Romans 1:16 Jonathan Edwards once wrote, To take on yourself to work out redemption, is a greater thing than if you had taken it upon you to create a world. And why not when we know at salvation we become a new creation As long as money is the measure of your life, you will be empty Satisfaction will only come when fully trusting Jesus (Like a little child) Righteous Reward 18:28-30 What s in it for us? WWW: Peter has a rather commercial view of discipleship He wants reassurance that sacrifice of the disciples has been noticed God is not obligated to bless us; we surely deserve eternal punishment Yet, Peter is correct. They left all; their source of income even as profitable fishermen; Matthew had a lucrative business; they have given up family and friends Absolute abandonment (Chambers) They had answered the terms as established by Jesus: Follow me v. 29 God is not only blessing you now, but will in the future No One absolutely no one will miss out on this promise Anyone who responds to the Lordship of Christ To serve the cause of the Kingdom; for My sake and the gospel s sake We do not serve Christ for pay but for the privilege It is all of grace from beginning to end Blessings are already at work: Colossians 3:4 2 Peter 1:3 Philippians 4:13 Godliness with contentment is great gain It is having a peace beyond all understanding (Phil 4:8), having a joy unspeakable and full of glory (1 Pet. 1;8), "the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Christ" (2 Cor. 4:6 ), and "the love of God poured out into our hearts through the Holy Spirit" (Ro. 5:5 ) Mark 10:29-31 Jesus said, Truly I say to you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or farms, for My sake and for the gospel s sake, 31 but that he will receive a hundred times as much now in the present age, houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and farms, along with persecutions; and in the age to come, eternal life. 31 But many who are first will be last, and the last, first. Hughes on Mark's passage - I like Jesus math. He does not say 100 percent more, but a hundredfold more! One house gone; but a hundred doors are open! One brother in the flesh lost; but a thousand brothers in the spirit, whose love is deeper and whose kinship is profounder. Identity with Christ will bring persecutions First is last, the last is first equal footing Jim Elliott - "He is no fool who gives up that which he cannot hold to gain that which he can never lose". What will you receive? Gold in exchange for clay; eternal life in exchange for a few fleeting years of selfish pleasure Only one life Twill soon pass Only what's done for Christ will last CONCLUSION Scripture calls us to empty ourselves not be full of ourselves When we Pray: Thy will be done When around Peons: Sober Assessment [[Sacrifice, Separation, Sober Assessment, Serve One Another in Love, Supernatural Ministry]] Children are part of a church family. To be loved, accepted, even tolerated When considering Possessions: What do we have that we have not received? Does it all belong to Him or are we hanging on? FULL OF YOURSELF Luke 18:9-30 INTRODUCTION FULL OF YOURSELF IN PRAYER 18:9-14 FULL OF YOURSELF AROUND PEONS 18:15-17 FULL OF YOURSELF AND POSSESSIONS 18:18-30 Presentation of the Ruler Presentation of Respect Presentation of Request Presentation of Righteousness 18:19-30 Righteous Prodding 18:19 Righteous Examination 18:20,21 Leviticus 18:15 The Law had a purpose: Romans 3:20; James 2:10 His testimony is part of his self-deception Philippians 3:6 Righteous Exposure 18:22, 23 Mark 10:21 Matthew 19:21 Self-surrender implies a renunciation of his own achievement Jesus simply held up a mirror so the young man could see himself Sell and distribute do it now Ezekiel 36:26, 27 Jesus is not talking about losing everything but gaining everything Jesus is calling him to repentance; 1 Thess. 1:9 Righteous Entrance 18:24-27 Money is a wonderful servant but a terrible master Materialism is a deadly enemy of the kingdom of God Revelation 18:9-19 The disciples reaction tells us this was to be taken literally Wealth naturally works at perverting one s values But a person who is ruled by money cannot be ruled by God Jesus never speaks of wealth as a blessing Salvation is a miracle for the rich and poor Romans 1:16 Righteous Reward 18:28-30 Peter wants reassurance that the sacrifice of the disciples has been noticed No One absolutely no one will miss out on this promise We do not serve Christ for pay but for the privilege Colossians 3:4 2 Peter 1:3 Philippians 4:13 Mark 10:29-31 Jim Elliott - "He is no fool who gives up that which he cannot hold to gain that which he can never lose". ============================================= === SERMON: message(160).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/23/2025 10:29:20 AM To: Subject: message IF I TOLD YOU ONCE . .. Luke 18:31-34 Cf. Matthew 20:17-19; Mark 10:32-34 INTRODUCTION Let s see if you can finish these popular slogans: From the business world: Choosy moms choose (Jif) A diamond is (forever) When you care enough (to send the very best) Where s the (beef)? Finger linkin (good) There are some things money can t buy. For everything else there s Put a tiger in your (tank) Fly the friendly skies of (United) Mm..Mm.. (good) I can t believe I (ate the whole thing) On the home front : Money doesn t grow (on trees) Because I (said so) Close the door! Were you born (in a barn)? Stop crying before (I give you something to cry about) This hurts me more than (it hurts you) Just wait until (your father gets home) Wipe that smile off your face (or I will wipe it off for you) If I told you once, (I ve told you a thousand times) TRY AGAIN Behold listen up; pay attention; do not miss this Not the first time for discussion 3rd time speaking directly of His death Luke 9:22 , 44-45 allusions: 12:50 ; 13:32-34 ; 17:25 This is the most detailed of them all The predictions are bookends of the journey: 9:51 19:27 Why the repetitions? Fighting against the common teaching of the day A dying Messiah was completely foreign to their understanding Have you ever made up your mind about something that nothing could change your mind? Even though you have blown it out of proportion? I will never eat at Taco Bell I will never own a Ford Can anything good come out of Michigan? Contrast in response: 9:44, 45 and Matthew 16 Matthew emphasizes the fear and astonishment of the disciples Luke focuses on the misunderstanding The trip is coming to a conclusion 18:35 Jericho is soon to be reached; 19:11 near Jerusalem The previous predictions said nothing about the location; now they know The city of the great King (Ps. 48:2) will be the place of death son of man Messianic title; Daniel 7:13- accomplished complete, execute, finish Joshua 23:14 And, behold, this day I am going the way of all the earth: and ye know in all your hearts and in all your souls, that not one thing hath failed of all the good things which Jehovah your God spake concerning you; all are come to pass unto you, not one thing hath failed thereof. Isaiah 14:24 Yahweh of Armies has sworn, saying, "Surely, as I have thought, so shall it happen; and as I have purposed, so shall it stand: Even after His death and resurrection, Jesus had to remind the disciples that He had predicted His coming death and resurrection 22:44, 46 It would be good to place ourselves in the culture of the day The Greeks resisted the idea of a God-man being truly human Luke presents the evidence the Jesus is a real man The Jews stumbled over the fact Jesus was truly God Matthew stresses the deity of Christ Luke shows the Gentiles responsibility for His death Matthew and Mark point out the role of the chief priests and scribes Matt. 20:18; Mark 10:33 Peter emphasized the Jews were responsible Acts 2:22, 23, 36; 3:13-15; 4:10 It is believed Luke was writing for the benefit of Gentiles Remember who the gospel was directed to: Theophilus; probably a Roman TRIAL DETAILS All things must be accomplished Psalm 2:2 the Son of Man would be handed over to the Gentiles Psalm 22:6, 7 Time for the Son of Man to be mocked (Isa. 53:3) Isaiah 50:6 Time for the Son of Man to be mistreated Isaiah 50:6 Time for the Son of Man to be spit upon Psalm 22:16 Time for the Son of Man to be killed Psalm 16:10 Time for the Son of Man to be resurrected Mocked 23:37 literally: to play as a child Ridicule, make fun of, to trick, to taunt, to play a game with Insulted: Spitefully treated, reproach hubrizo Our English word hubris refers to exaggerated pride or self-confidence means act with insolence, wantonness, wicked violence Spit on: a gesture of extreme contempt Scourging: and after whipping Him Scourging always preceded capital punishment in Roman law Many times a person died just from the scourging The whole purpose was to weaken one for crucifixion or they potentially could hang on a cross for hours or even days for a strong man scourge: deadly weapon with leather thongs in a wooden handle The whip would rip one s back to ribbons John 19:1-7 Pilot was hoping such punishment would suffice Jesus knew exactly what was going to take place His omniscience is on display Jesus knew Peter would catch a fish with a coin in its mouth Jesus knew the woman to whom He was speaking had 5 husbands Jesus knew where a colt would be tied Jesus knew He will rise again middle voice; implies He would raise Himself Spurgeon: It would be hard to imagine our Lord speaking more plainly. He tells them of his sufferings in detail describes exactly what is to happen. But their thoughts did not run that way, and when you are not expecting a thing, it matters little how plainly you may be told of it. TRIFECTA OF UNBELIEF 18:34 The disciples were so puzzled at the words of Jesus that it took Luke 3 attempts to describe their unbelief And they understood none of these things; and this saying was hid from them, and they perceived not the things that were said. It is a huge hurdle to grasp something so foreign to your own thoughts The words of Jesus ran so counter to their own vision of the future that was easy to refuse no matter how many times they are told There were some lessons they handled: Matthew 13:16,16 But here what they were hearing did not match their theology This is not the way the kingdom was going to happen The idea of a cross is still foolishness to unbelievers: 1 Cor. 1:18 It was a massive barrier to Jew; 1:23 Matthew 16; remember Peter s strong reaction to what was taught And again it may be hard to comprehend a teaching because of how it was presented, but here is Truth personified presenting the lesson; the Divine Logos Think back through the Gospel His parents; 2:49, 50 Disciples never did understand until 24:45; John 12:16 That had this mental block that needed removed It is interesting that the disciples did not grasp the lesson until after the resurrection Even the enemies of Jesus had a greater respect for the teaching Matt. 27:62-64 they wanted a guard set up God had a purpose: it was hidden from them They were prevented from understanding 9:44, 45 they did not know what was said It would be easy from our vantage point to wonder at their unbelief How could anyone be so entrenched in what they believe as to ignore all the things taught and all that was seen? But it could easily be said of any of us How many times did we ignore a warning, saying it would never happen to me, I can handle it, maybe for others but not for me How many times have you tried to teach someone important truths, but they could not grasp the details or simply refused to accept it? And more than once I have prayed that a person would not wind up with scars that will impact their lives for years to come We have to deal patiently and graciously with others who are slow to learn God may have His own reasons for keeping them in the dark 19:41, 42 We are like the disciples in that we view life in the present It is all about my comfort, happiness, joy now It is hard to grasp lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven when you think those resources would be better put to use now Even the lesson on eternal life: as a believer in Christ, you have it now It is not something you receive at death, yet it will be not be real to us until we die How can we get to living by faith when it is so easy to live by sight ? Peter tells us it may be the will of God for you to suffer; 3:17; 4:19 Can you accept that as a necessary part of discipleship? The disciples of Jesus Christ were listening and learning. They apparently were losing sight of some reality pertaining to Jesus Christ. They could not grasp the fact that the whole program of God was contingent upon Jesus Christ going to the cross and dying as a sacrifice for sin. We must stay focused on the whole counsel of God The death of Christ has been predicted throughout our Scriptures Genesis 3:15; 22 Exodus 12 Leviticus These disciples were apparently so caught up with themselves that they actually did not or could not see and understand the whole truth of God. Just because someone has been saved many years does not that person really knows God s Word well. IF I TOLD YOU ONCE . .. Luke 18:31-34 TRY AGAIN 3rd time speaking directly of His death Luke 9:22 , 44-45 Fighting against the common teaching of the day The Greeks resisted the idea of a God-man being truly human The Jews stumbled over the fact Jesus was truly God TRIAL DETAILS All things must be accomplished Psalm 2:2 Psalm 22:6, 7 Isaiah 50:6 Isaiah 50:6 Psalm 22:16 Psalm 16:10 Mocked: Ridicule, make fun of Insulted: exaggerated pride or self-confidence Spit on: a gesture of extreme contempt Scourging: deadly weapon with leather thongs TRIFECTA OF UNBELIEF 18:34 The idea of a cross is still foolishness to unbelievers: 1 Cor. 1:18 It was a massive barrier to Jew; 1:23 Disciples never understood until 24:45 God had a purpose: it was hidden from them We have to deal patiently and graciously with others who are slow to learn We are like the disciples in that we view life in the present ============================================= === SERMON: message(161).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 8/29/2025 3:31:33 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHEN JESUS PASSED BY Luke 18:35-43 INTRODUCTION As we come to the end of chapter 18 of this wonderful gospel provided to us by Luke, we have his last recorded miracle performed by Jesus before His crucifixion. Most of you by now should be able to state when He began His journey to Jerusalem; 9:51. And last week, we looked at the 3rd time Jesus specifically spoke of His coming death to the disciples. They did not understand and through a purpose unknown to us, God withheld that knowledge from them until after the resurrection. I believe such delay only ignited a fire in their soul to the place where they truly burned out for Jesus. The Apostle Paul had a similar experience through his temporary physical blindness which led him to say, Phil. 3:10 Gal. 6:14 2 Tim. 4:7 WHEN JESUS PASSED BY AS THE BLIND MAN WAS SITTING THERE BY THE WAY, HE CRIED TO JESUS, FOR MERCY THAT DAY. JESUS, COMMANDED AND GAVE HIM HIS SIGHT. SO HE FOLLOWED JESUS AND I'M SURE HE CRIED. JESUS PASSED BY MY WAY AND HE GAVE ME HOPE THAT DAY. JUST A SINNER WAS I BUT THEN JESUS PASSED BY, AND OH WHAT A CHANGE IN MY LIFE, SINCE JESUS PASSED BY. JUST LIKE THE BLIND MAN, I WONDERED ALONE, IN DARKNESS OF SIN I WAS ALWAYS ALONE. BUT ONE DAY I MET HIM AND HE MADE THINGS RIGHT, AND OH WHAT A DIFFERENCE SINCE JESUS PASSED BY. WHEN JESUS PASSES BY They brought the blind to Jesus' side Touch me now I pray So Jesus led him down, out of town And touched his eyes that day He said I see men as trees walking So Jesus touched him once again And these blinded eyes received their sight When Jesus passes by Oh, it always makes a difference When Jesus passes by The devil trembles, the enemy flees When Jesus comes on the scene He always shines a ray of light The darkening clouds must fly It always makes the difference When Jesus passes by They took him to the tomb that day Lazarus was his name His loved ones wept, for death had crept Into their lives with pain Oh, but someone sent a message And soon Jesus did reply And even death could have no power When Jesus passes by Oh, it always makes a difference When Jesus passes by The devil trembles, the enemy flees When Jesus comes on the scene He always shines a ray of light The darkening clouds must fly It always makes a difference When Jesus passes by. Our story is recorded also by Matthew and Mark. You may remember when special dignitaries were visiting in California recently, preparation included removing the homeless, the drug addicts and washed the sidewalks so as to present a more pleasant experience. No such thing took place as Jesus travelled. He did not send his disciples ahead to remove the riffraff We know there were times some went ahead to make preparations for the arrival of a larger crowd (9:52); we know Jesus will yet send some ahead to make preparation for the Passover. But no such work will take place to make His travel a more pleasant experience. THE PROBLEMS Jesus is coming near Jericho His trip to Jerusalem had Him on the other side of the Jordan River in Perea He is now back across the river and about to make the ascent to the City Jericho is about 700 feet below sea level; Jerusalem about 3,300 feet higher than Jericho Matthew and Mark both say there was a large crowd following The Location Matthew 20 and Mark 10 both have Jesus leaving Jericho Luke says He is approaching Most writers believe the difference has to do with two Jericho s The old Jericho, the ruins of which have been discovered The area was referred to as "a little paradise," with its palm trees, rose gardens, etc. Its winter climate was delightful, making it a winter residence fit for a king. The new Jericho built by Herod Our story locates Jesus between the two Jericho s In Ohio, we have Sandusky and Upper Sandusky. I have no idea why Upper is two hours south of the other one In KY, there are two Pleasurevilles. One is a city, the other unincorporated Different zip codes The Blind Another difference in the gospels is the number of blind men present Matthew says there was 2; 20:30 Mark and Luke speak of one, but do say there was only one Mark gives his name: Bartimaeus Perhaps known by his readers How many were there? There no doubt were two men there; Mark names the one who was the more vocal The Miracle From the OT, only the Messiah would heal the blind Throughout Scripture, only Jesus healed the blind Ps 146:8 The LORD opens the eyes of the blind; The LORD raises up those who are bowed down; The LORD loves the righteous; Isa 29:18 On that day the deaf will hear words of a book, And out of their gloom and darkness the eyes of the blind will see. Isa 35:5 + Then the eyes of the blind will be opened and the ears of the deaf will be unstopped. It was this miracle that was to be the testimony before John the Baptist 7:22, 23 Matthew 9:27-31; 2 blind men Mark 8:22-26; blind man at Bethsaida John 9:1-12; the man born blind Matthew 12:22, 23; Luke 11:14-23 blind, mute demoniac We know in Scripture that physical blindness is a metaphor to spiritual blindness Isaiah 59:9, 10 John 9:35-41 We just learned the disciples were blinded to the reality of the coming suffering Maybe this is an illustration of their blindness We do know that in Israel there were many blind and many beggars From John 9 the common thought was that blindness was a judgment from God and that they were reduced to begging The large crowd got the attention of the blind men Their ears more attuned to changes around them Began asking questions; imperfect tense: continued asking; persistence THE PROBABILITY 18:37 Opportunity: Latin word used of a ship waiting for the timing of the tide to make it safely into the harbor Thus "OB PORTU," described the ship waiting "FOR PORT," God gives each of us many "ob portu's", but we must be spiritually wise and Spirit filled in order to see and seize them. The beggar sensed this was his opportunity, not knowing this would be the last time Jesus would ever pass by Isaiah 55:6 Seek ye Jehovah while he may be found; call ye upon him while he is near: THE PROTEST 18:38-40 he called out a loud cry expressing deep emotion Jesus, Son of David Jehovah saves with the messianic title This man knew something; he had heard something Even though this man was blind, he had spiritual vision Suggesting here an element of faith The only one in the gospel of Luke who uses that title Matthew 9:27 the testimony of another blind man Cry for mercy Help to those in distress The plea was not from merit but out of desperation Pleading mercy is humbling; a good pattern for all of us It is a reverent, urgent appeal Here is man in the dark crying out to the light of the world Matthew: crowd ; Mark: many Trying to silence the beggar Yelled at him to keep quiet He was continuing to cry out; they were continuing to silence Don t be surprised when those who seek the Lord find opposition do not be surprised that the majority will not be for you but against you when you seek the Lord, for the majority are themselves not on the right road (cf Mt 7:13 , 14 + ) (the gate to life is narrow) We learned that this week after the school shootings The city mayor and news personality both attacked the value of prayer The tragedy in this scene is that those who were following Jesus had little if any of the compassion which moved their Master. Much like the disciples seeking to keep children away, these folks saw no value in two blind men Even though this physically blind man saw more than the spiritually blind crowd who tried to prevent him for crying out to Jesus! Whenever you trust in God, you will find hindrances Ruth and I experienced that by having children It again happened when we decided to homeschool The more they protested the more he yelled v. 39 Kradzo: scream, shriek loudly; inarticulate scream He could not be silenced in seeking Jesus' help! The opposition did not alter his doctrine: Jesus is the Messiah Bartimaeus sensed the urgency of the moment: Jesus is here now He had a desperate need and the determination to have it met Jesus stopped On the final part of His journey to Jerusalem to die for the sins of these same people; He takes time to stop for a blind beggar In chapter 8, Jesus was on His way to heal the daughter of Jairus, when He stopped to minister to one woman THE PLEA 18:41 Compare to the rich young ruler: What must I do? What can I do for you? The King becomes the servant 2 Corinthians 4:4 we were all once blind beggars before God Revelation 3:17 Jesus description of the church of Laodicea This is perhaps the major stumbling block that keeps people from coming to Christ: they want to commend themselves and their good deeds. God has to open our eyes to our true condition before Him. We have nothing in ourselves to merit His salvation. We are spiritually blind sinners, and the only way we can come to Him is to ask for mercy, not for merit. S. Cole Jesus knew Bartimaeus was blind; Jesus knew what he wanted Yet, Jesus still wants to hear it from us Psalm 81:10 Open your mouth wide and I will fill it James 4:2 we have not because we ask not It is an act of humility to declare your need The need must be known before it can be met There can be no salvation for those who are unawaken to their true condition. His faith is on display: first he called him the Messiah: son of David Here, he calls him Lord He knew his condition and his desperate need for Jesus I want to receive my sight can mean to recover uncertain if he once saw and seeks it again THE PROVISION 18:42 He made a specific prayer; he received a specific answer Three times in three verses the word regain or receive shows up. Each word is used in a different mood that really adds color to his narrative: Verse 41 the word regain is in the subjunctive mood - which means the blind beggar knows that receiving sight is a possibility. Verse 42 the word receive is in the imperative mood - which means receiving sight is commanded. Verse 43 the word regained is in the indicative mood - which means receiving sight is a factual reality. David Thompson Your faith has saved you This does not mean that the man s faith created the cure, but that it was the means by which he received it. (L Morris) The recipient of the gift of faith (Eph, 2:8) is rewarded for exercising it His faith saved him physically and spiritually Physically; he could see again Spiritually: he praises and follows The way of Jesus becomes his way, especially significant because Jesus is on the way to the cross. THE PRINCIPLES It seems to me the story of the healing of the blind man summarizes the chapter. Bartimaeus possessed the humility of the Tax Collector; pleading for mercy Bartimaeus showed the persistence of the widow; seeking relief from the one designed to provide Bartimaeus shows us the value of each individual; like a child Bartimaeus is a great contrast to the rich young ruler: What can I do? Or What can Jesus do? Bartimaeus provides for us the qualities of a true disciple WHEN JESUS PASSED BY Luke 18:35-43 THE PROBLEMS The Location Our story locates Jesus between the two Jericho s The Blind Mark names the one who was the more vocal The Miracle From the OT, only the Messiah would heal the blind Throughout Scripture, only Jesus healed the blind Psalm 146:8 Isaiah 29:18 Isaiah 35:5 THE PROBABILITY 18:37 The beggar sensed this was his opportunity Isaiah 55:6 THE PROTEST 18:38-40 A loud cry expressing deep emotion The plea was not from merit but out of desperation Whenever you trust in God, you will find hindrances Kradzo: scream, shriek loudly THE PLEA 18:41 2 Corinthians 4:4 Revelation 3:17 Jesus still wants to hear it from us Psalm 81:10 It is an act of humility to declare your need THE PROVISION 18:42 He made a specific prayer; he received a specific answer THE PRINCIPLES Bartimaeus possessed the humility of the Tax Collector Bartimaeus showed the persistence of the widow Bartimaeus shows us the value of each individual Bartimaeus is a great contrast to the rich young ruler: Bartimaeus provides for us the qualities of a true disciple ============================================= === SERMON: message(162).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/6/2025 4:21:50 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message Communion after the message. sorry for the delay. forgot before heading to wilminton MEETING THE WEE LITTLE MAN Luke 19:1-10 INTRODUCTION The end of the journey is approaching Jerusalem is 15 miles and 3,300 feet climb away As you see from the chapter, we are soon to see Jesus Coming to Jerusalem Crying Over Jerusalem Cleansing the Temple But first there are two important occasions to study The Conversion of Zaccheus The Counsel About Stewardship; The postponement of the Kingdom Jericho: the Eden of Palestine Rich soil, perfect climate; just where you expect to find the tax commissioner It had a great palm forest and world-famous balsam groves which perfumed the air for miles around. Its gardens of roses were known far and wide. Men called it The City of Palms. Josephus called it a divine region, the fattest in Palestine. The Romans carried its dates and balsam to world-wide trade and fame. Antony gifted to Cleopatra who sold it to Herod who built his summer palace Interesting contrast to Bartimaeus Bartimaeus could not see because of physical blindness Zaccheus could not see Jesus because of physical stature; the lack thereof Bartimaeus sought Jesus be screaming loudly Zaccheus sought Jesus by climbing a tree Would Jesus extend the same courtesy to a rich man as He did the blind man? If you really want to see Jesus, you can. It is about your heart s desire The rich young ruler wanted to see Jesus, but came on his own terms The idol of his heart kept him from a personal relationship. Idols of the heart still keep even believers from a close relationship It will disturb your sense of nearness to Jesus; idols will grieve and/or quench His Spirit! Ezekiel 14:1-8 As the blind guys took advantage of the opportunity given to them as Jesus passed by, so likewise we see another tax collector seek his time All the days seem alike as they come to us; but each one is really individual and peculiar, coming with its own opportunities, privileges, and blessings. If we do not take just then the gifts it offers, we never shall have another chance to get them, and always shall be poorer for what we have missed. J. R. Miller How many times have we missed the opportunity because of dullness of spirit, an idol of the heart? My spiritual eyes were blind We have repeated warnings and admonitions: fight the good fight of faith 1 Ti 1:18 , 6:12 by the Spirit putting to death the deeds of the body, Ro 8:13 abstaining from evil 1 Th 5:22 to abstain from fleshly lusts which wage war against our soul 1 Pe 2:11 You ve got to enjoy Warren Wiersbe s outline of this passage A Man became a Child: 19:2-4 (running down the street) A Seeking Man Becomes Found: 19:5 A Small Man Becomes Big 19:7-8 A Poor Man Becomes Rich 19:9,10 Do you ever view yourself as rich? Ephesians 1:3 every spiritual blessing Eph. 1:7 the riches of God s mercy and grace Ephesians 3:8 The unsearchable riches; not fully understood Phil. 4:19 the riches of His glory Rom. 11:33 the riches of His wisdom When a day begins, you never know how it will end The Man s Biography 19:2-4 Name: Zaccheus: bright, clean, pure, innocent Occupation: chief tax collector Seen an unclean ; certainly not a candidate for salvation Luke s mentioning of tax collectors: Lk 3:12 ; 5:27 ; 7:29 ; 15:1 ; 18:10 God sees not as man sees Zaccheus has 3 strikes against him He is a tax collector He was crooked and cheated He worked for the Romans A crooked tax collector, representing everything wrong about life The whole system was abused and used for extortion It was not the collecting of taxes that was the problem; it was cheating Luke 3:13 Not told to quit their job; just don t overcharge Luke 20:25 pay what you owe to Caesar Zaccheus had position and possessions which were usually a problem preventing salvation We are about to see the camel go through the eye of a needle "things that are impossible with people are possible with God. 18:26 , 27 Statistics: Short Easy to guess he was only around 5 ft. tall; maybe a little less trying deliberately pursue after, to go in search of To find by any means possible which in this case was a tree You can picture him standing on tiptoes Here is a very short rich guy who no one likes trying to weasel his way through the crowd to see Jesus. Not too hard to assume he received little sympathy In fact, you would assume the glee to keep the slimy little shyster away Maybe Zaccheus had known Matthew from previous tax collector meetings Maybe the word got around that one of his brethren walked away from a lucrative gig And it wasn t just about seeing Jesus, but seeing who He was What makes Him different? Why do so many follow? Why would someone give up everything; job, family; to follow Him? Who is this man who just healed a couple of blind men? To any man who truly wants to see Jesus, there will be obstacles Not just physical obstacles of people getting in the way Also spiritual obstacles: 1 Thessalonians 2:14-18 For Zaccheus there was a solid wall of onlookers When they saw Zaccheus the elbows became like barbwire There was no way anyone would allow him up front TRANSITION Truth be told, not a one of us measures up We are all pipsqueaks in the eyes of God We are short on integrity and tall on sin (Bell) all have sinned and come short of the glory of God The Man s Determination 19:4 Picture a grown, rich man running down the street like a little boy He fits Jesus description in 18:17 Like Bartimaeus, he was a man on a mission; rich guys don t climb trees I wonder how many saw him climb the tree; how many may have mocked him; how many yelled unkind things; Hey, shorty! Spurgeon: his curiosity overcame his dignity The Trinity at work: the Father drawing him through the inner working of the Spirit to seek the Son Who ultimately was really the One seeking Zaccheus! Can you say, Amazing Grace ? John Stott: [My faith is] due to Jesus Christ himself, Who pursued me relentlessly even when I was running away from Him in order to go my own way. And if it were not for the gracious pursuit of the Hound of Heaven I would today be on the scrap-heap of wasted and discarded lives. As an aside lest you be offended by the metaphor of God portrayed as a "hound" as Stott explained "there are good hounds as well as bad hounds, and that especially admirable are collies, which range the Scottish Highlands in search of lost sheep. In Psalm 23:6 David wrote "Surely goodness and mercy will follow me all the days of my life." Pastor Ray Stedman commenting on that passage wrote "Some quaint commentator has said that those two words goodness and mercy (hesed) are God's "sheep dogs". This is the Shepherd's Psalm. David wrote it when he was but a lad, keeping sheep. In referring to the goodness and mercy of God, he is referring to the sheep dogs that nip at the heels of the flock and keep them in line, driving them into place. "Surely Goodness and Mercy shall follow me all the days of my life," nipping at my heels, humiliating me, turning me back from that which looks good but is really evil, keeping me from getting what I think I need, and what I think I want. But in the end we must name these what God names them -- goodness and mercy!" It was goodness and mercy that chased Zack up a tree Can you say with absolute confidence you are in the fold? There is no doubt that because of your faith in the Lord Jesus Christ that should you die even today, your soul would be with Jesus? Not because of any merit on your part, but wholly through faith in the sacrifice of Jesus on the cross? But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world. (Galatians 6:14) these things are written that you may KNOW you have eternal life Identifying the sovereignty of God at work; God is always at work Sycamore tree: a sycamore fig tree has low branches A study in 2015 indicated that the sycamore tree was brought to Israel by Philistines during the Iron Age (which lasted from about 1200 BC to 550 BC). Now think about this for a moment. Was this tree on the side of that particular street in Jericho by accident or chance? Who created this tree? Why did God allow this tree to grow here at this time? What if the tree had not been beside the road? What if it had been a palm tree instead of a sycamore known for its low hanging branches? (It is notable that Jericho was known for its palm trees not its sycamore trees for Dt 34:3 calls it "the city of palm trees"!) And so, this blessed sycamore tree was no mere accident of "mother nature". The sycamore tree was the purposeful planting by Father God! While you may think it a bit far fetched, it is clear that God had already provided a "front row" seat for this little man who was seeking a big God! I believe in the doctrine of divine providence God is always behind the scenes and controls the scenes He is behind. Psalm 139:23, 24 The rich young ruler: self was the idol of his heart Self is best biggest obstacle to salvation I am reminded that self is the very thing we are asked to sacrifice The curiosity of Zaccheaus overcame his dignity LORD S SUPPER MEETING THE WEE LITTLE MAN Luke 19:1-10 Idols of the Heart Ezekiel 14:1-8 Our Riches in Christ Ephesians 1:3, 7; 3:8 Philippians 4:19 Rom. 11:33 The MAN S BIOGRAPHY 19:2-4 The whole system was abused and used for extortion Zaccheus had position and possessions which were usually a problem preventing salvation trying deliberately pursue after To any man who truly wants to see Jesus, there will be obstacles 1 Thessalonians 2:14-18 We are short on integrity and tall on sin THE MAN S DETERMINATION 19:4 Spurgeon: his curiosity overcame his dignity It was goodness and mercy that chased Zack up a tree The sycamore tree was the purposeful planting by Father God God is always behind the scenes and controls the scenes He is behind. ============================================= === SERMON: message(163).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/20/2025 11:19:52 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message MEETING THE WEE LITTLE MAN Luke 19:1-10 INTRODUCTION When last we visited this passage, we focused on Zaccheaus. THE MAN S BIOGRPAHY Chief tax collector, a cheat, Roman employee THE MAN S DETERMINATION Small stature and hostile crowd THE MAN S SALVATION 19:5-10 The Attention of Jesus The text would have us believe that Zaccheaus was looking for Jesus What is really happening is Jesus is looking for Zack Adam hid among the trees when God came looking Zack was not hiding but seeking Among all the people around Jesus, He zeroes in on a man up a tree The work of grace: calls him by name This is where salvation begins How did Jesus know His name? From Philippians 2, we learned what is popularly called the kenosis theory Jesus laid aside the independent exercise of His divine attributes in becoming man. He is still fully divine as the God-man He only used miraculous powers under the authority of His Father God knows everyone He knew the name and the heart It was not Heh, you in the tree! Have you ever been surprised when someone you did not know called you by name? Even more concerning when they add I heard about you! This is what v. 10 is all about Dick Walker, former dean of men, had a great capacity for learning names He knew his name but sought personal fellowship Did not say He would like some time, but must have some time A divine necessity; a divine appointment We have many divine necessities in the Scriptures John 3 you must be born again John 4:4 must needs go through Samaria; seeking another lost sinner Not optional Jesus was often invited into the homes of others but this is the only occasion where He invited Himself Jesus works in the hearts of individuals; not the masses He calls individuals by name; His call is effectual; His call bear fruit It is the call to a personal relationship Jesus knows you. He knows your heart. He knows your desires, your passions, your problems, your heart aches Let s summarize the call of Jesus from the notes of John Butler: A Gracious Call: no one deserves it An Urgent Call: Make haste and come down the day of salvation A Humbling Call: come down a recognition of need A Personal Call: requires a personal encounter The Activity of Zaccheaus Not only called him by name Receive Him gladly; joyfully; to entertain, receive under one s roof Received Him while continuing to rejoice No hesitation, no debating A man in motion on a mission; he was running; now he is hurrying Today is the day of salvation No - almost you make me to be a Christian No let me think about it Jesus was not coming back this way Life is a vapor; there may be no more opportunities The human response to the grace of God There was nothing about the life of Zack that merited the attention of Jesus He had done nothing good; far from it Nothing in my hand I bring, simply to thy cross I cling. (Rock of Ages) "when the reality of God s grace floods your soul, great joy will be your response." Just like blind Bartimaeus; 18:43 There is no other way to receive Jesus than with great joy The Agitated Crowd 19:7 The legal spirit of self-righteous men Just a continuation of the grumbling they have been doing There was no pleasing these people no matter how great a work was done Jesus did not go to commend Zack in his sin but to convert him from his sin There will always be the naysayers and critical spirits The wording is the murmuring spread throughout the crowd these grumblers "overlooked the fact that, coming into a world like ours, He was limited exclusively to such homes!" (Believer's Bible Commentary) The Acknowledgment of Zaccheaus 19:8 Here we see Zaccheus a "son of Abraham" physically bear fruits in keeping with repentance demonstrating that he is a "son of Abraham" spiritually. John the Baptist: bear fruits in keeping with repentance A faith that demonstrates no faith is a dead faith James 2 faith alone saves but show me your faith by your works Perhaps Zack heard the murmurs of the crowd; he knew his position in society He has a message for all the grumblers to hear We are not even told they got to the house; we know nothing about any other communication between Jesus and Zach Assuming it was during a meal as common to have a crowd around Zack, Stood We are not sure what brought about the conviction; maybe the noise of the crowd. But in that moment he saw it all; his past and present; what he was And now what the future must hold Here is his personal, public testimony of a heart supernaturally changed Testimony of a soul saved from eternal punishment The whole current of his life changed He did not defend himself before the crowd No blame shifting to justify his behavior the Roman s made me do it Pritchard - This is the only place in the Bible where Zaccheus is recorded saying anything. Not bad for a new convert. Can a person change that quickly? YES 2 Corinthians 5:17 becomes a new creation Not every bad habit disappears immediately But change happens; new creation in Christ with new desires, new tastes, new goals You know when salvation takes place when it hits the wallet His confession agreed with the accusations against him; a cheat, dishonest No mere sentimentalism that does not change a life Think of the influence of such a testimony on the city He had no problem acknowledging the Lordship of Christ And it was not merely: the past is past; let it go; we cannot change it Just move on and do right That is right to a point, but when possible make the wrongs right It may be confessing a wrong committed; try to set things right Sorrow for sin is not enough, if we can in any way make right, that which we have marred. Zack is now God s workmanship ; created to good works Note the word if ; if I falsely accused anyone Grammatically called a first class conditional clause ; properly since and since I have wrongly taken Falsely accused is a great Greek word; meaning to be a fig informer A. T. Robertson says that to export figs from Attica was against the law, but it was a lucrative trade, so there were people whose business it was to inform the authorities about the illegal traffic and they were called sukophantai. Vine does not dispute this, but also traces its meaning from the practice of shaking a fig tree to make it give up its ripe fruit, so Zacchaeus and men like him were adept at shaking down rich men for their wealth. Restitution is a great example of a changed heart How do you show the reality of Christ in your life? Ray Pritchard: Here s the principle. The reality of your new life in Jesus Christ will be seen at precisely the point of your old weakness. Zaccheaus: problem of greed so deal with that Paul: Ephesians 4 If lying is a problem: practice speaking the truth If anger is a problem: let not the sun go down upon your wrath If stealing is a problem: steal no more, but labor and give Corrupt communication: speak what is edifying Pornography: pursue purity; capture every thought; bring your body under subjection; find an accountability partner 3 Reasons for Restitution It demonstrates humility It provides an opportunity for restoration of relationships A changed behavior demonstrates a changed life It makes it less likely you will do it again It costs you something; admitting you were wrong God may impress upon you to: Take inventory: really look at your life Make a list of the people you have hurt Make amends whenever possible Someone called it joining the ROZA - The Royal Order of Zaccheus Anonymous It is accepting the challenge to show the world that Jesus has really made a difference in your life The Acceptance from Jesus 19:9, 10 Declaration: salvation has come to this house In a moment, Zach was transformed from a vile chief tax collector to a child of God This is the second to last conversion recorded; the thief on the cross to this house typically when the head gets saved others do as well All require a personal confession The physical son was not also a spiritual son He believed God and it was reckoned to him as righteousness Zach is now delivered; he is now safely in the arms of Jesus Dedication: that is why I came The Jews failed in being a light to the nations Isa. 42:6 God took on the job of seeking the lost Ezekiel 34:6, 11 Jesus did not come to condemn the world but that the world through Him might be saved You cannot find a more simple statement; not even a word with 2 syllables No man can be found until he knows he is lost No reason to be saved until he knows he is in danger The bad news precedes the good news Doctors don t treat healthy people; Luke 5:31 Son of Man the divine God-man; the Messiah has come - His condescension to seek - His compassion for the horrible state of sinners to save - to rescue one from great peril To deliver from the power of sin To heal from the wages of sin which is death To be made whole lost - a descriptive term for all of mankind; not a special group conveys the sense of utter destruction of one's purpose for which they were created which is to glorify God and enjoy Him forever. The end result is not cessation, annihilation but alone in hell fully conscious of its torments As Adrian Rogers said "There are only two places where there's no hope. One is in Hell because when you go to Hell, you've lost hope. The other is in Heaven because when you're in Heaven, you don't need hope!" If the words, lost, sinner, ungodly, fit you, then you can have hope, because Christ came to save such people from their sins. But if you say, I may have my faults, but I m not lost, then I cannot offer you a Savior. Jesus came to save the lost. If you say, I m only human, of course, but I m not a sinner, then Christ did not come to save you. He came into this world to save sinners. If you say, I know that I have done plenty of wrong things, but I wouldn t call myself ungodly, then I m afraid that Christ did not die for you. Scripture says that Christ died for the ungodly. S. Cole No one is too bad to be saved It may be the little things that count Zack was running and tree climbing out of curiosity Jesus has compassion and power to change men s hearts Converted sinners will always give evidence of a change life MEETING THE WEE LITTLE MAN Luke 19:1-10 THE MAN S BIOGRPAHY THE MAN S DETERMINATION THE MAN S SALVATION 19:5-10 The Attention of Jesus The work of grace: calls him by name must A divine necessity; a divine appointment Jesus works in the hearts of individuals The Activity of Zaccheaus Received Him while continuing to rejoice The human response to the grace of God There is no other way to receive Jesus than with great joy The Agitated Crowd 19:7 There will always be the naysayers and critical spirits The Acknowledgment of Zaccheaus 19:8 A faith that demonstrates no faith is a dead faith He has a message for all the grumblers to hear Testimony of a soul saved from eternal punishment 2 Corinthians 5:17 You know salvation takes place when it hits the wallet When possible make the wrongs right and since I have wrongly taken The reality of your new life in Jesus Christ will be seen at precisely the point of your old weakness 3 Reasons for Restitution It demonstrates humility It provides an opportunity for restoration of relationships It makes it less likely you will do it again The Acceptance from Jesus 19:9, 10 Declaration: salvation has come to this house Dedication: that is why I came Ezekiel 34:6, 11 No man can be found until he knows he is lost lost - a descriptive term for all of mankind ============================================= === SERMON: message(164).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 9/27/2025 9:21:28 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message 10 LITTLE MINAS Luke 19:11-27 INTRODUCTION We come to a part of Luke s gospel of a fun little parable. I call it fun because it requires some extra background work and some serious cross reference work. It is fun because we have to engage another very similar parable in Matthew s gospel; 25:14-30; commonly called the Parable of the Talents Are we looking at the same teaching, just a different angle from two writers? It is also fun wondering if Pastor Hill can cover 16 verse in one message. O ye of little faith We have just witnessed the salvation testimony of Zacheus; the cheating chief tax collector. Scripture does not record the impact of that on the community of Jericho. And if there was any question regarding the seriousness of his conversion, Jeus puts His stamp of approval on it. We are getting so close the arrival of Jesus in Jerusalem. You see from the paragraph headings the Triumphal Entry, Weeping Over Jerusalem; The Cleansing of the Temple. Verses 11-17 are for us and all those who await the return of Jesus. CONTEXT While listening to these things The marvelous demonstration of salvation The incredible restoration of stolen money; like winning the lottery Jesus own statement: He came to seek and save the lost Luke give us two reasons for this parable He is near Jerusalem; 17 miles, about 6 hour walk The Jews were expecting the establishment of the Kingdom If Jesus can turn Zacheus, think what He can do for the nation! We must not confuse this parable of the minas (found only in Luke) with the parable of the talents in Matthew 25:14-30 , That parable was given several days later to a different audience, with different details and a different application. The parable of the Talents followed the Parable of the Virgins: Wise and Foolish. There the message was having faith; being prepared when the Bridegroom comes. The message of the parable of the Talents is to be faithful The distribution of talents was not equal: 5, 2 and 1 to each according to his own ability 25:15 Jesus used receiving money as the test of responsibility We often look at this as the distribution of spiritual gifts 1 Cor. 12:11 the Spirit distributing to each one as He wills There are different gifts, different ministries, different activities The message is if you try, you will receive commendation God has gifted; develop it, use it, God will reward For the variety of responsibilities which have been assigned in accordance to ability to be carried out during His absence, the Messiah will return to proportionately reward the faithful who will enter the kingdom and to judge the wicked who are excluded. Let s look at the Parable of the Minas Mina is actually Hebrew; the Greek word is mna Some translations use the word pound It is about 1/60 of a talent A talent was about 6,000 days wages Mina was about 90 days salary Important note: each received the same amount; 10 minas HISTORY 19:12 Jesus used an event that was known to the residents of Jericho Josephus has given a detailed account of which we summarize This took place about 30 prior Herod the Great died about 6 B.C. His territory was divided among his sons; Archelaus was to rule over Judea He is mentioned in Matthew 2:22 His desire was to receive the title of King but that required a trip to Rome and meeting with Caesar Before he could leave, an uprising took place in Jersualem during Passover, 4 B.C.. The people were mourning the killing of some of their religious leaders by King Herod When Archelaus sent a group of soldiers to quiet the crowd, the Jews turned and stoned the soldiers. In reaction, Archelaus ordered the soldiers to invade the Temple with the result of 3,000 Jews killed Passover was then officially cancelled. Archelaus sailed to Rome to get approval for his kingship but there he was opposed by his brother Antipater and a group of dissident Jews protesting his killing of 3000 countrymen Because of this resistance to Archelaus, Caesar would only grant him the title of "ethnarch " (ruler over an ethnic group) and not the title of king, He was eventually deposed and banished in 6 A.D. Archelaus had a palace in Jericho. The event was not that long ago No wonder, Jospeh and family settled in Galilee APPLICATION Even though the people are about to pronounce Him as King Jesus says, not so fast. Jesus knows the events soon to come will not put Him on the throne Since the journey began in 9:51, all teaching has been about the proper use of possessions, the Jewish rejection of Jesus, the return of the Son of Man, the kingship of Jesus, the delay of the Parousia, the unveiling He has also explained the duties of the disciples That emphasis continues here It is fascinating as you study the Gospels that this idea of an earthly kingdom resurfaces again and again in the minds of the Jews, even though Jesus repeatedly emphasized that His first coming was not to establish a literal earthly kingdom. Can you imagine anyone so stuck on a narrative they stick to it even when it has been continually rejected? We are well beyond the teaching in 17:21, the kingdom of God is in you The disciples never got over the anticipation of a literal, earthly kingdom They were still of that mindset after the resurrection after attending a 40 day seminar on the kingdom of God; Acts 1:3, 6 The parable seeks to slow the emotions, appeal to the mind by highlighting a return using an historical event well known to them It is safe to say the Jews were not believers in amillennialism Progressive revelation has given us Revelation 20:4-6 How long is the delay? Peter in Acts 3:21 Which will include the repentance of Israel; 3:26; Zechariah 12:10 Jeus is the nobleman; He is going to receive a kingdom; He will return What are the minas? Each of the servants received the same amount This is what one writer called meantime living What are we to do in the meantime? busy yourself until I come What is a mina? It is something of equal value? What do all of us from God that that is the same? What do we have in common with Luther, Calvin, MacArthur, Kirk? We have the Word of God; we have the gospel; we have grace We are told to do business with it while the King is away K. Hughes: We are to multiply our spiritual capital invest the gospel increase the yield of the good news of salvation through Christ! This is not a matter of gifts but of investment. And not only invest it in the lives of others but preach it daily to ourselves We are to grow in grace Our investment is not just for salvation but also sanctification Paul: 1 Thessalonians 2:4 (WEB) But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts. 1 Timothy 1:11 (WEB) according to the Good News of the glory of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust. 1 Timothy 6:20; 2 Timothy 1:13, 14; 2 Timothy 2:1, 2 The time of our "occupation" is transient (Ps 39:14 ) so we do well to frequently beseech the Lord of the harvest to "teach us to number our days so that we may present to Him a heart of wisdom." (Ps 90:12 ). Work, do business, occupy for the night is coming when your work is done We will work until ; and then times up You say, I have not seen much for my work Galatians 6:9-10 (ASV) And let us not be weary in well-doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not. So then, as we have opportunity, let us work that which is good toward all men, and especially toward them that are of the household of the faith. OPPOSITION 19:14 We are living between verses 14 and 15 Notice the phrase his citizens, where the possessive pronoun "his" indicates they belonged to him. MacArthur explains that "Everybody belongs in His kingdom. You may reject Christ, you may hate Christ; He owns you. You may be an atheist, a Muslim, a Buddhist but Christ owns you. You live in His country for He made this world. It is His, and He made you. By creation, He owns you....I think this is a message that people don't quite understand. They think that if they reject Christ, then Christ has nothing to do with them. You reject Christ and He has everything to do with you. You accept Christ and He has everything to do with you. You do nothing with Christ and He has everything to do with you. You are in His world...You can reject Him, you can ignore Him, but He owns you." John 1 He came unto His own and His own receive Him not John 19 We have no king but Caesar John 3:20 For everyone who does evil hates the Light, and does not come to the Light for fear that his deeds will be exposed. John 7:7 The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me because I testify of it, that its deeds are evil. John 15:18 If the world hates you, you know that it has hated Me before it hated you. John 15:23 He who hates Me hates My Father also. John 15:24 , 25 If I had not done among them the works which no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have both seen and hated Me and My Father as well. But they have done this to fulfill the word that is written in their Law, They hated me without a cause CONCLUSION We will have to Settle Accounts another time We have this plaque on our bathroom wall: This is the beginning of a New Day God has given me this day to use as I will I can waste it or grow in its light and be Of service to others. But what I do with this day is important. Because I have exchanged a day of my life for it When tomorrow comes, today will be gone forever. I hope I will not regret the price I paid for it. None of us will probably have the platform of Voddie Bacham. D. L. Moody, or Charlie Kirt. But we have the same gospel entrusted to us. Do Business with it by sharing it with others and having it conform your own life to that of Christ. 10 LITTLE MINAS Luke 19:11-27 CONTEXT We must not confuse this parable of the minas with the parable of the talents in Matthew 25:14-30 Mina is actually Hebrew; the Greek word is mna A talent was about 6,000 days wages Mina was about 90 days salary HISTORY 19:12 Herod the Great died about 6 B.C. An uprising took place in Jersualem during Passover, 4 B.C. Archelaus ordered the soldiers to invade the Temple with the result of 3,000 Jews killed Archelaus had a palace in Jericho APPLICATION Jesus knows the events soon to come will not put Him on the throne The disciples never got over the anticipation of a literal, earthly kingdom Acts 1:3, 6 It is safe to say the Jews were not believers in amillennialism How long is the delay? Acts 3:21, 26 This is what one writer called meantime living We have the Word of God; we have the gospel; we have grace Our investment is not just for salvation but also sanctification 1 Thessalonians 2:4 1 Timothy 1:11 Galatians 6:9-10 OPPOSITION 19:14 We are living between verses 14 and 15 John 3:20 John 7:7 John 15:24 , 25 ============================================= === SERMON: message(165).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/3/2025 12:17:33 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message TEN LITTLE MINAS Luke 19:11-27 INTRODUCTION Zacheus has made his profession of faith through his works Jesus has clearly stated His purpose for coming to earth The masses have the intention of crowning Him king once they arrive in Jerusalem CONTEXT, HISTORY, APPLICATION, OPPOSTION Jesus seeks to pour cold water on their plan through a parable using an event from their recent history about a nobleman traveling to a distant country seeking the position of king. He is no longer speaking of Archelaus, but Himself The kingdom is delayed, because no matter the excitement of the crowd, they will soon declare their rejection of His conditions for Kingship There will be no bowing of the knee We don t want this man to reign over us The parable describes to us mean time living What we are to be doing in the meantime, the time until Jesus returns He has entrusted equally to us the responsibility to invest the gospel That is the mina ; one Hebrew word for money That distinguishes this parable from the parable of the Talents, as those were distributed as led by the Spirit. Monetarily speaking, a talent is much greater value than a mina (mna) Please do not start a church by proclaiming your spiritual gift is greater than the gospel. These are parables EVALUATION 19:15 Not referencing the size of the settlement; but who is in charge The Return As we arrive at v. 15, Jesus has returned after receiving the kingdom It does not say if but when He returns That was a problem which Peter had to address; 2 Peter 3:1-4 Jesus is coming to earth; there will be a kingdom; the throne will be in Jerusalem; the duration is 1,000 years The Kingdom is given to the Son of Man by God, the Father Psalm 2:4-6 Revelation 19:11-16 The Accounting Not a suggestion: it was a command, an order Here is a reckoning for the servants; bondslaves; doulos An early order of business is examining what the servants had accomplished How they had invested the mina; the gospel What they had gained by trading; doing business What had they earned with the Master s mina The assumption is they had used the money wisely and had earned interest This is about accountability; faithfulness; stewardship The word for money is for silver coins; same word for the 30 pieces of silver given to Judas for the betrayal Peter to the lame beggar: Silver and gold have I none 1 Peter: we are not redeemed by silver or gold, but the blood of Christ This is not the Judgment Seat of Christ; 2 Corinthians 5 That describes the judging by fire the works of believers Yet, this should give us pause knowing that an exact accounting is coming 1 Cor. 4: Faithfulness is required in a steward The gospel, spiritual gifts, and blessings have been richly bestowed upon us Our evaluation will be by fire; our works as a believer will be instantly judged We will know in an instant whether our work is good or worthless A pastor s work will be put to the fire to see whether he built on the foundation or Jesus Christ with wood, hay, stubble, or gold, silver, precious stones 1 Cor. 3 SUMMATION 19:16 The original dispersion of minas was to 10 servants We have recorded the report of 3 Slave #1 Hopefully you can see the character of the slave Your mina had made 10 more minas The servant took no credit, not his ingenuity, not his personality Not his business sense Not boasting, no pride Master recognizing his place; bought one; considered this a high honor to bring glory to his Master He reports a yield of 1,000% (and he is servant, not a member of Congress) Folks, this is not a competition between saints to see who can gain the biggest yield We each are called to run the race set before us Stay in your lane and stop looking at the fruit gained by someone else Abide in the vine John 15 The power of the gospel is in the message, not in the skill of the messenger Your mina the power is in the mina, not in the servants If you are depending on slick salesmanship, marketing techniques, and entertainment to advance your message, what does that say about your confidence in the power of the gospel? Do business with this mina until I return Are you doing business for the Master with His gospel? You may ask, but did not Paul say he became all things to all people to win them to the gospel? Good question. 1 Corinthians 9:22 Part of a verse is often quoted but the context of the chapter is ignored The chapter speaks of the things Paul did not do; the things he avoided or denied to himself, including proper privileges So, you may decide not to marry to further the gospel You may refuse a job promotion to further the gospel You may move to a less desirable location to further the gospel It is not let s have a rodeo so we can present the gospel Let s have the pastor ride his Harley onto the platform That has become the primary application of 1 Cor. 9:22 It is not our job to make the gospel palatable to the masses Praise God for 10 mina Christians!
