Your fruitfulness in ministry is not about how much fruit had been produced but how much fruit could have been produced Well done the commendation you want to hear The slave has been trustworthy, reliable, and dependable The reward is grace upon grace Great investment: one mina, not even his, yields an incredible investment Authority over 10 cities Ephesians 3:20 Luke 16:10-12 right and might have been granted to the fruitful slave Faithful work is rewarded with more work But what a great compliment to be entrusted with so much But you say, I thought heaven was about rest Revelation 22:3 His servants shall serve Him Will there be a literal 10 cities during the Millennial Reign? Remember, this is a parable We do know saints will rule with Christ; Revelation 20:4 He who does God s work will receive God s pay We all start with 10 minas SLAVE #2 Showing his love and honor given to his Master Words and actions 500% return; still faithful in the handling of the Master s mina Future rewards will vary The reward of work well done is more work SLAVE #3 19:20, 21 Another slave there are two words in Greek meaning another Another of the same kind and another of a different kind This is a slave of a different kind His Reluctance 19:20 The first two were rewarded for their investments Neither showed a hint of animosity toward the Master The debate continues as to whether this servant is a believer or not Does he lose any reward or was never in the ballgame? What is apparent is a wasted opportunity to use the Master s mina for profit Storing money in a handkerchief is not wise then and not wise now Here is a servant who has no heart for Christ He has his mina, his gospel, his get out of hell free card His life revolves around his interests and activities He kept laid up in a napkin ; did not use the Master s gift Colossians 1:4, 5 the hope which is laid up for you in heaven 2 Timothy 4:8 the crown of righteousness which is laid up to all who love His appearing Hebrews 9:27 it is laid up for man once to die We are to have a joyous look toward a bright future His Reasoning 19:21 Dominated by fear; he knew his day of reckoning would come He feared the day he would appear before his Master He seeks to shift the blame from himself to the Master An austere man exacting, rough, strict, uncompromising He accuses the nobleman of being cruel, harsh, severe, and unfair What was his problem? He perhaps feared than any profit gained would be taken by the Master Or feared if there was any loss, he would have had to make it up "I was afraid of you. You're a tough person to get along with. You take what isn't yours and harvest grain you haven't planted." An accusation of stealing J. C. Ryle: Hard thoughts of God are a common mark of all unconverted people. They first misrepresent Him, and then try to excuse themselves for not loving and serving Him. Think back to the OT: Israel murmuring in the wilderness, finding fault with God s appointments and dealings, charges God with hardness and injustice CONDEMNATION 19:22-27 v. 22 you think I am hard? I will treat you just like you think I am The words of the wicked slave come back on his head as his own condemnation. wicked servant evil The nobleman judged the wicked servant on the basis of his own presuppositions. v. 23 potential wasted the wicked slave has no respect for the nobleman, no relationship he was indifferent and did not care Ray Pritchard: The master is not angry because the servant failed to make ten minas. That is not the issue. Profit (or the lack of it) is not the point. After all, the king rules the empire. He s got all the money he needs. He ll never spend the money he has. Then why is the master angry? BECAUSE THIS SERVANT DIDN T EVEN TRY TO USE HIS MONEY FOR THE MASTER S ADVANTAGE. It s not the amount. That s not the issue. But this man was first unbelieving, then disobedient, then overly-cautious, and finally just plain thumb-sucking lazy. HE DIDN T TRY BECAUSE HE DIDN T CARE. That s why the master is angry. Nobleman proves he is harsh, hard, severe man Spurgeon: "It is always so, the gracious and faithful man obtains more grace and more means of usefulness, while the unfaithful man sinks lower and lower and grows worse and worse. We must either make progress or else lose what we have attained. There is no such thing as standing still in religion. The bystanders failed to understand and thought the nobleman was unfair They failed to understand that the master was operating on the principle that faithfulness in little means faithfulness in much. He can take it away because it was his money to start with Have you never moved money from one bank to another for a greater return? Did you ever ask, how do they like me now? Leon Morris emphasizes that "Jesus is not saying that the rich will get richer and the poor poorer. The words must be seen in their context. It is the man whose abundance shows that he has made good use of what he has who will get more." If Zacheus was still listening, he would have been encouraged to not fall back on his promise of giving and refunding What you don t use, you lose faithful in little is faithful in much This other slave lost the opportunity to do more The smallest gift must be put to good use Use it or lose it; don t sit on your mina If you ever do any outside reading of this parable, you will find much disagreement as to salvation of this other slave Some believe he was saved so as by fire ; loss of reward but not salvation I lean on the side he is not a believer: He is called another ; not identical to the others He was afraid of the master He calls his master strict, harsh, severe He accuses his master of being a thief His master calls him evil, wicked 19:27 Now addresses the group from earlier in the parable we do not want this man to reign over us Such will be the end of all those listening who do not seek Jesus as did Zacheus. T. W. Manson comments We may be horrified by the fierceness of the conclusion; but beneath the grim imagery is an equally grim fact, the fact that the coming of Jesus to the world puts every man to the test, compels every man to a decision. And that decision is no light matter. It is a matter of life and death." TEN LITTLE MINAS Luke 19:11-27 EVALUATION 19:15 The Return It does not say if but when He returns Psalm 2:4-6 Revelation 19:11-16 The Accounting SUMMATION 19:16 Slave #1 The servant took no credit We each are called to run the race set before us The power of the gospel is in the message It is not our job to make the gospel palatable to the masses Ephesians 3:20 He who does God s work will receive God s pay SLAVE #2 Words and actions The reward of work well done is more work SLAVE #3 19:20, 21 His Reluctance 19:20 His Reasoning 19:21 CONDEMNATION 19:22-27 The words of the wicked slave come back on his head the wicked slave has no respect for the nobleman What you don t use, you lose ============================================= === SERMON: message(166).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/10/2025 9:21:37 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message TRIUMPHAL ENTRY Luke 19:28-40 cf. Matthew 21:1-11; Mark 11:1-11 INTRODUCTION There is something about coming to the end of a journey that is so rewarding. It may be a landmark or other familiar sign that says, You are getting close. I always enjoyed coming north on I-71 into Cincinnati. You come around a hillside and as you start down the cut of the hill in Kentucky the city opens before you. Or the state sign greets you after your trip. Welcome to Ohio. The Heart of it All. You students have a similar joy when you are returning home for fall break or after a semester at school. It is nice to be back to the familiar, nice to be home. Jesus is completing His journey to Jerusalem. Does anyone remember the chapter and verse in Luke where it said He steadfastly set His face to go to Jerusalem. (9:51) In 19:28-40, He arrives. It is popularly called the Triumphal Entry. It caused quite a stir on the city. There was the retrieval of the donkey, shouting of those coming with Him, the antagonism of the Pharisees, the weeping over the city, people asking who is this? , and cleansing the temple. All of these are familiar accounts that you have read and heard messages about since your early years in a church. Many times children in churches are waving palm branches. This really is a big deal. The familiarity also brings a challenge as to how to approach the section. After last Sunday s service, Jon asked if I was going on in the chapter. I said we would be looking at the Triumphal Entry but was not sure yet how I was going to approach it. One very astute Cedarville student suggested, You could talk about the Triumphal Entry. Said student has already read the passage to you, so now I invite you to turn to Daniel 9. There are other OT passages related to Luke 19:28-40, mostly notably Zechariah 9:9 Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion; shout, O daughter of Jerusalem: behold, thy king cometh unto thee; he is just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass, even upon a colt the foal of an ass. I want to take a stab at Daniel 9. Much has been written. There are several interpretations. But Gabriel s introduction to the interpretation gives us an indication of its significance. 9:23 consider and understand are the same word Diligently discern; give heed to the message 9:25 know therefore and understand One writer: "The history of the interpretation of these verses is confirmation of the fact that this prophecy is difficult and requires spiritual discernment." The challenge from the angel is to be "intimately" acquainted with the important truth and timing of events to follow. We are not here to accumulate facts, but to know by experience the revelation of God The word understand is used in 1:4 and 1:17 as the men were gifted by God The process of thinking through a complex arrangement of thoughts Reginald Showers of the Friends of Israel Gospel Ministry writes that Daniel 9:24-27 is "one of the most significant prophecies in all the Old Testament Scriptures" and is also "one of the strongest biblical evidences to the effect that Jesus of Nazareth is the Messiah." In our discussion of how do we know the Bible is God s Word , one of the items often used is the fact of fulfilled prophecies. Though not the final piece to the puzzle, it does add weight to the argument. Daniel 9 prophecies the Triumphal Entry. THE COUNTDOWN 9:25 Here is the key to the interpretation of the entire passage: From the issuing of a decree to restore and rebuild Jerusalem The decree, edict, word originated in heaven and made manifest on earth THE CONFUSION The Interpretation there are several approaches 1. literal interpretation with a gap between the 69th and 70th seven year period 2 symbolic interpretation (which several variations) and 3 literal interpretation of the seventy weeks as 490 consecutive years The Edict The arguments are over which decree marks the beginning of the 70 weeks? 490 years Decree of Cyrus: 538 B.C, Ezra 1:1-3 Cyrus' decree was for rebuilding of the house of the Lord, not the city, Jerusalem That math would leave us in the middle of the first century; years before Jesus was born Decree of Darius I: 520/519 B.C. Ezra 6:3-12 This decree was a confirmation of Decree #1; building the temple Decree Artaxerxes: 458/457 B.C. Ezra 7:11-13 Ezra s response is important; 7:27 All focus is on the temple and nothing about the city Decree of Artaxerxes: 444 B.C. Rebuild the city with plaza and moats Nehemiah 2:1-8 2:16-18 We believe this date can be determined Neh. 1:1 20th year of the reign of Artaxerxes His father died 465 B.C, the accession-year system was used the first year of Artaxerxes reign the report to Nehemiah would have been November/December 445 the decree of Artaxerxes was in the spring of 444 B.C. THE CONSTRUCTION 9:25 Plaza and moat The plaza was a public square Genesis 19:2 the angels told Lot they would stay in the square It is the center of city life The moat 1X OT A dig, a cut out, a trench, a cut out around the city for fortification Why is this important? A city needed protection and including these items pictures a completed restoration In the LXX, the word for wall, teichos, is used to translate moat The idea is the city was built with some type of defensive barrier The starting date for the 490 years countdown is found in Nehemiah 2 Dr. Charles Ray: 7 Reasons for the 444 B.C. date 1. The most severe times of distress took place during Nehemiah s day. 9:25 troublesome times 2. There were no later decrees by Persian kings about rebuilding the city 3. The first three decrees say nothing about rebuilding the city 4. This decree literally specifies rebuilding the city 5. All three stages are recorded in history: city, plaza, wall 6. The 4th decree best explains Luke 19:42 in this your day 7. The math works THE CHRONOLOGY until Messiah the Prince Up to the time; up to the point in time; up to the stipulated time To be sure there are some who see this fulfilled in the lives of others Some Bibles do not support a Messianic interpretation As mentioned earlier, there are disagreements on how to interpret 70 weeks I am looking at 69 weeks of years or 483 years This is where some math calculations enter How many days in a Biblical year? Genesis 7:11 8:4 the flood lasted 5 months Gen. 7:24; 8:3 says the same time is 150 days 30 days per month Daniel 9:24 70 weeks; literally 70 7 s or 490 years 9:27 the middle of the week: 3 years Rev. 12:14 time, time, and half a time Compared to Rev. 12:6, 14 equals 1,260 days Rev. 13 5; the same time is 42 months Using a 360 day per year calendar and subtracting 483 years from 444 B.C. and your arrive at 32-33 A.D. At the end of the 69 Sevens is the appearance of Christ, the Messiah, the anointed One Various Opinions The Birth of Jesus The magi spoke of Him as the King of the Jews But Gabriel announced His purpose: You shall name Him Jesus for He shall save His people from their sins The Baptism of Jesus Luke 3:21, 22 He was not presenting Himself to the nation as the Messiah but presenting Himself to the Father for approval This is my beloved Son The people did not see anything special about His baptism John the Baptist was alone is observing the descending of the Holy Spirit The Triumphal Entry [another time we will study the usage of Messiah in the OT] Prince: commander, prince, captain; not the usual word for king Throughout His public ministry Jesus refused the title of King He avoided the desire of the populace to anoint Him king But now in Luke 19, Jesus is riding as a king, receives all the honor and Hosannas as the Prince of the house of David He took to Himself all the authority and majesty of the rightful King and Lord of the chosen people, their Temple and their state And when the officials objected, He stated, I tell you that if these should keep silent, the stones would immediately cry out He had to be presented to the nation as its rightful and anointed King; and this is when and how it was done. We find the exact terminating-point of the angel's four hundred and eighty-three years. CONCLUSION I submit to you the exact timing of the appearing of Jesus as King in Luke 19 was laid out 483 years earlier. I submit to you that Jesus is never early and He is never late If such exactness is evident throughout the Scriptures, you can surely expect it in your own life. God will be there in your time of need God will be there when your life on this earth is over Our times are in His hands ps. 31:15 So teach us to number our days that we may gain a heart of wisdom 90:12 TRIUMPHAL ENTRY Luke 19:28-40 Daniel 9 prophecies the Triumphal Entry THE COUNTDOWN 9:25 The key to the interpretation of the entire passage THE CONFUSION The Edict Decree of Cyrus: 538 B.C, Ezra 1:1-3 Decree of Darius I: 520/519 B.C. Ezra 6:3-12 confirmation of Decree #1 Decree Artaxerxes: 458/457 B.C. Ezra 7:11-13 focus is on the temple Decree of Artaxerxes: 444 B.C. Rebuild the city Nehemiah 2:1-8 The decree of Artaxerxes was in the spring of 444 B.C. THE CONSTRUCTION 9:25 The plaza was a public square Genesis 19:2 The moat The city was built with some type of defensive barrier THE CHRONOLOGY until Messiah the Prince Using a 360 day per year calendar and subtracting 483 years from 444 B.C. and your arrive at 32-33 A.D. Various Opinions The Birth of Jesus The Baptism of Jesus Luke 3:21, 22 The Triumphal Entry Prince: commander, prince, captain ============================================= === SERMON: message(167).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/18/2025 8:30:13 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message BEHIND THE SCENES Luke 19:41-44 only NT record INTRODUCTION You have heard the statement: Not everything is as it seems , or there is more to this than meets the eye The idea is there are hidden aspects or deeper meanings that are not immediately visible Don t make any snap judgments based solely on surface appearances There are some people who are great at seeing behind the scenes There are others of us who tend to take things as they are presented without looking behind the curtain Some people are always questioning the motives of others Why did they do that? What did they mean? What are they up to? There s something fishy going on here! I remember while in high school reading literature like Les Miserables And the class discussion would be the motives, the innuendos, the meaning behind everything I read. I m sitting there thinking, I never thought of that! In another class we looked at the rock opera Tommy and the popular song Pinball Wizzard . Again, much of the discussion was what caused him to behave as he did. I don t fret any when someone pulls the wool over my eyes I have learned that many things get exposed and the truth will be learned The reality is that some things are not as simple as they seem. Jesus has arrived in Jerusalem. He is presenting Himself as King of the Jews. His mode of transportation was spoken of in Zechariah 9. His day of arrival was predicted in Daniel 9. The previous 3 years was sufficient for the nation to know He is the promised Messiah. He taught, the healed, the performed miracles of various sorts, including the restoration of sight to the blind, which was specifically identified with the Messiah. Many folks followed Him; some were committed to Him and His message The masses for the most part remained indifferent with the religious leaders maintaining an antagonistic position. But He is here, receiving all the accolades, praises, attention of a King The next section of Luke 19 provides for us the rest of the story DIVINE JUDGMENT 19:41, 42 As he ascended from the other side of the Mount of Olives from Bethphage and Bethany the city would come into view as He reached the top of the mountain and could see the Temple on Temple Mount Now take a moment and image this incredible scene. The crowd is rejoicing and shouting the words from the Messianic Psalm 118:26 , even proclaiming Him as the "King of Israel" and yet Jesus, as He sees the city, begins sobbing deeply, a visible show of emotion which would have been obvious to all who could see Him. What a contrast! On one hand, the Jews are expressing unbridled jubilance , while on the other hand, Jesus was expressing profound sorrow Jesus wept Not like John 11:35 at the tomb of Lazarus; that word means shedding tears This word means sobbing 34x NT Rachel weeping for her children (the killing by Herod) Peter weeping over his denial It is the wailing over the death of a loved one This sobbing was not done in a corner All in the vicinity of Jesus would have witnessed the tearing of His heart He knows what is to come; He knows these same people are soon to turn on Him The word that comes to mind is fickle : defined as "marked by erratic changeableness in affections or attachments." Here is a deep, inward anguish; weeping while riding in triumph He knew his joyous entrance into Jerusalem would be followed by a mournful procession out of it Bible Exposition Commentary puts it: No matter where Jesus looked, He found cause for weeping. If He looked back, He saw how the nation had wasted opportunities. If He looked within, He saw spiritual ignorance and blindness as He looked around, Jesus saw religious activity that accomplished very little as Jesus looked ahead, He wept as He saw the terrible judgment that was coming to the nation, the city, and the temple. Do you ever wonder if Jesus weeps over the United States of America? Turning Point v. 42 this day the day prophesied in Daniel 9 A very specific day, a unique day, a day the Jews could have and should have known Adrian Rogers said "Jesus came on time; He died on time; He was buried on time; He rose on time; and He is coming on time. You can just bank on it." Daniel: You are to know and discern You should be able to assemble the individual facts into an organized whole Like putting pieces in a puzzle They had sufficient ability to know and understand what is happening There are two organizations operating today because their leaders studied Daniel 9; Jews for Jesus and Chosen People Ministries The Jews in the city of peace did not understand the basis for true peace It is not about politics, no matter how many peace deals are signed It is not what things but Who makes for peace but now an expression of time; announcing the beginning of a time of judicial blindness They did not recognize and now they cannot recognize The antithesis of Paul s prayer; Ephesians 1:18, 19 "I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened, so that you will know what is the hope of His calling, what are the riches of the glory of His inheritance in the saints, and what is the surpassing greatness of His power toward us who believe." Key phrase: us who believe The irony is that He is making this judicial declaration at the very time the nation of Israel is seemingly receiving Him as their Messiah and their King The nation wanted to defeat Rome; Jesus had a greater enemy to conquer: sin, death, Satan This judicial blindness will last until there is a national repentance Zechariah 12:10 VISIBLE JUDGMENT 19:43, 44 for Jesus is about to explain something Here is the consequence of their spiritual blindness, spiritual judgment This judgment will not be hidden The judgment is future: will 4x The judgment is personal: you singular; city personified the judgment is thorough: days the Judgment is certain: when , not if; it is destined to happen your enemy will be the very Roman government you want defeated How? Throw up a barricade: throwing up a rampart of earthworks A standard Roman military tactic It describes a fortified fence constructed of wooden poles with earth, stones, pieces of wood packed between, the purpose being to prevent escape from the besieged city. Josephus in the 'Jewish War': "In the morning Titus commanded that the fires should be put out and that a road should be built to the gates to allow entry for his troops. His generals then came together to discuss what should be done with the temple. Some wanted to destroy it, because it would give the Jews a reason for uprising. Others argued that if the Jews would clear out of the temple it should be allowed to stand, but if they were to use it as a fortress, it should be destroyed. Titus then gave the command that no matter what happened, the temple should be spared, because it would always be a great tribute to the empire. Three of his chief generals agreed, and the meeting was disbanded......Titus then went into Antonia, intending the next morning to attack and overwhelm the temple with his entire force. But on that day, the tenth of Lous [August 30, 70 AD], the same day on which Solomon s temple had been destroyed by the king of Babylon, the structure was doomed. The rebels again attacked the Romans after Titus retreated, and a battle took place between the temple guards and the Roman troops who were trying to put out the flames in the inner court. The Romans scattered the Jews and pursued them into the sanctuary. At the same time, a soldier recklessly grabbed a torch . . . He hurled the firestick through the doors made of gold on the north side which allowed entry to chambers around the sanctuary. On seeing the flames, a cry went up from the Jews, and caring nothing for their lives, they rushed forward to put out the fire. A messenger rushed to the tent of Titus to inform him of the fire. Immediately, Titus ran to the temple to put out the flames. But because of the battle that raged on, the soldiers either could not or would not hear his commands. The wrath of his troops could not be stopped, and at the doorway many soldiers were trampled by their own forces. There among the burning ruins they fell, sharing the same fate as their enemies. Pretending not to hear the commands of their general, and filled with hatred, the soldiers rushed on, hurling their torches into the temple. The helpless rebels made no attempt at defense. Fleeing for their lives, with bloodshed all around, many civilians were caught in the battle. Even the steps of the altar were stained with the blood of the dead. When Caesar could not hold back his troops, he and his generals entered the temple and viewed for the last time the Most Holy Place. Since the fire had not yet reached the inside, but was still feeding the outside chambers, Titus made one last effort to save the structure. Ordering a centurion to club anyone if they disobeyed his commands, he rushed forward and pleaded with his soldiers to put out the flames. But because of their hatred of the Jews and their desire for riches, the soldiers disregarded the orders of their general. Seeing that all the surroundings were made of gold, they assumed that inside there would be great treasures. Titus then ran out to hold back his troops, but one of those who had entered with him thrust a torch into the hinges of the temple gate, and mighty fire shot up inside. Caesar and his generals fled for safety, and thus, against his wishes, the sanctuary was burned."....... The city and the temple was then leveled to the ground by the command of Caesar. Only the highest towers and part of the western wall remained to show all mankind how the Romans overpowered such a strong fortress." CONCLUSION Many of the same Jews listening to Jesus would come to experience the very reason Jesus was sobbing They had their chance Do you ever think Jesus crying over the present situation in our country or the world? Scripture is still clear: people still have a chance, and some have already been given over to the hardness of their own hearts What do we learn about God? Hebrews 3:12,13 Hebrews 10:31 Jesus is Prophet, Priest, and King Triumphal Entry: King Weeping Over Jerusalem: Prophet Cleansing the Temple: Priest BEHIND THE SCENES Luke 19:41-44 DIVINE JUDGMENT 19:41, 42 On one hand, the Jews are expressing unbridled jubilance , while on the other hand, Jesus was expressing profound sorrow Jesus wept means sobbing; a deep, inward anguish this day the day prophesied in Daniel 9 but now They did not recognize and now they cannot recognize VISIBLE JUDGMENT 19:43, 44 The consequence of their spiritual blindness The judgment is future The judgment is personal The judgment is thorough The Judgment is certain Hebrews 3:12,13 Hebrews 10:31 ============================================= === SERMON: message(168).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/24/2025 8:19:15 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message I would like to use the temple picture at the beginning. Or one like it SETTING THE STANDARD Luke 19:45-48 cf. Matthew 21; Mark 11 INTRODUCTION EXPULSION IN GOD S HOUSE This is fascinating - the King of Glory entered into the very place from which His Glory had departed over 500 years earlier, just prior to the destruction of the First (Solomon's) Temple in 586 BC by the Babylonians and King Nebuchadnezzar Recall John 1 Jesus is described as the Word of God 1:14 we beheld His glory Here for a brief moment, as in John 2, the glory returns It was corruption and idolatry that led to the first departure Jeremiah 7:1-10 Jeremiah s day: the Jews were oppressing aliens, stealing, murdering, etc. Nevertheless, they continued to offer their sacrifices in the temple; as if such merely formalistic worship of Jehovah would do any good, and as if the very presence of the temple would protect them from the outpouring of God's wrath. Glory departed: Ezekiel 10:4, 18; 11:23 Failure to recognize Jehovah as God Now, corruption and failure have surfaced again Jesus has entered the city; proclaimed King by the masses He enters the temple area, probably through the Eastern Gate Mark 11:11 Day of Triumphal Entry: entered city, looked around, went to Bethany for the night, returned the next day and cleaned house The people may have expected Him to head straight for the Antonia Fortress which housed the Roman soldiers Instead, He confronts religious corruption taking place in the Court of the Gentiles The Eastern Gate: the only one of the city gates presently sealed This was done about 1530 by Arabs thinking to prevent any Jewish Messiah would enter there. They also planted a cemetery in front of the gate thinking the Messiah would not step foot there. The Court of the Gentiles This area was primarily a bazaar, with vendors selling souvenirs, sacrificial animals, food, as well as currency changers, exchanging Roman for Tyrian money because the Jews were not allowed to coin their own money and they viewed Roman currency as an abomination to the Lord,[19] as also mentioned in the New Testament account of Jesus and the Money Changers when Jerusalem was packed with Jews who had come for Passover, numbered by some 1-2 million Jesus also stopped anyone from using the temple area for a shortcut Jesus exercised His authority: Prophet, Priest and King Adding fuel to the fire of the religious leaders against Jesus drive out those who were selling Upsetting tables, opening cages, releasing animals and doves Coins of money rolling around, mass confusion with the added noise People running for the exits No whip mentioned here; John 2 The picture is Jesus single handedly clearing out an area of several football fields This is holy indignation moving mountains Some may have self-deported Whatever reformation happened in John 2 was short-lived; three years later all practices had returned This was a glorious piece of real estate; a temple built by Herod taking 40 years. It was the center piece of worship for the Jews and a place of interest for all. Yet Christ s anger that its courts had become a den of robbers reminds us that the true significance of any house of worship is not found in how it looks but in what happens there." Matthew, Mark, and Luke record the cleansing without any hint of resistance This was a major disruption to the business of extorting money from worshippers People traveling in for the Passover had limited options They were ready made customers to pay whatever price was necessary for the perfect animal or paying the temple tax You have been in places where you had to pay the price They had you over a barrel, because of no other options Zeal for your house consumes me jealous, envy Psalm 69:9 John 2:14-17 zeal to be hot, indignation His anger was pure and completely justified because at its root was concern for God s holiness and worship There are some things that must make you angry Ephesians 4:26 be angry and sin not deal with it properly in a timely manner For Jesus: His anger had the proper motivation no selfishness involved His anger had the proper focus sinful behavior He had the proper control His emotions did not control Him His anger had the proper result God s glory EXPECTATION OF GOD S HOUSE 19:46 My house shall be called a house of prayer My house the Temple is His; He is God Isaiah 56:7; Jeremiah 7:11 Mark 11:17 nations = Gentiles It is hard to fulfill the commission to be a light to the Gentiles (Acts 26:17) when you turn your temple courtyard into the local stockyards made it into a robber s den literally cave The robber, (Barabbas) conducting his operations on a large and systematic scale, and with the aid of bands, is thus to be distinguished from the kleptes, or thief (Judas) who purloins or pilfers whatever comes to hand. A den would be appropriate to a band of robbers, not thieves. We know there were robbers along the Jericho road, but nothing compares to the wholesale robbery taking place on the Temple Mount The robberies were two-fold Robbing the people financially Robbing the people spiritually: stealing from them the opportunity to know God genuinely The religious leaders have perverted God s right purposes for their own gain What should have been a sanctuary for the righteous had become a refuge for the wicked There are ministries today with a lot of show but no depth Their people are moved with the music, entertained with the production, but are receiving a Ted Talk instead of biblical truth. how to have a happy life instead of thus saith the Lord I am not going to start bashing other ministries but I do have a responsibility to warn Cypress was just approved to build a church on Kenny Blvd. It is much more of an entertainment center than a bible teaching church. To call their service a circus is not too far off A.W. Tozer: We cannot afford to let down our Christian standards just to hold the interest of people who want to go to hell and still belong to a church. Quality of God s House: Prayer They were going through the motions of worship while at the same time abusing the people around them God saw through their pretense, however, and promised to deal with the thieves in His sanctified house. Jeremiah 7:4 do not trust in lying words the words of the false teachers Now Jesus is dealing with the same attitudes The temple is run by thieves but thieves who do not even have to hide in caves. They run the whole operation Matthew 21:14-16 the children had a better understanding of the Scriptures It s always good to remember the Lord s purpose for what He makes. Whether it s the temple, the church, marriage, the family, or life itself, we should follow God s design and seek to honor Him. Any twisting or perverting of God s design for selfish purposes will draw the Lord s righteous anger. Jesus insisted on reverence Jesus rebuked fraud and demanded honesty Jesus endorsed missions for all nations Prayer is a significant part of all nations entering into a relationship with God Prayer is communication with God. Prayer is worship Matthew 11:28 the invitation If you are part of the family of God, not only are you His household, but you are also His house of prayer Ephesians 2:19-22 EXAMINATION OF GOD S HOUSE It is time to examine our house Hebrews 3:6 but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end. 1 Peter 2:5 You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ. 1 Corinthians 6:19, 20 Or don't you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit which is in you, which you have from God? You are not your own, for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God's. God still expects purity in His house What is the basis of the examination? 19:47 Jesus was teaching Not how to have a happy marriage, nice kids, get along with your boss Jesus is now in the last days of His life and involves Himself with what He considers of highest value: teaching 20:1 taught the people and preached the gospel There is no greater calling we can have 1 Peter 2:21 follow in His steps 1 John 2:6 walk just like He walked In Scripture to teach means to pass on the truth about the Word of God, the God of the Word and the faith of the saints, with the goal of influencing the understanding and stimulating obedience to the truth taught and resultant Spirit energized transformation and Christ-likeness Religious people won t like it Others will listen closely: hanging on every word Literally: hang on the lips of the speaker Paying careful attention If Jesus would come to the US today, He would not go to Washington He would go to the church 1 Peter 4:17 For the time is come that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if it first begins with us, what shall the end be of them that obey not the gospel of God? CONCLUSION Would anyone describe our church as a house of prayer? Not whether or not we pray, but are we the army of God that advances on its knees? Are we faithfully and without apology teaching the Word of God? Have we who are the house of God allowed elements of corruption to rob our soul and, through influence, the souls of others to settle for going through the motions of religion without truly engaging the heart? Do we presently practice something that is actually a detriment to someone growing in grace and knowledge? What must we do better? The challenge of the text is more than not selling books and tapes in a church building. When Jesus revealed His Examination of the churches in Revelation 2 and 3, it was not about selling merchandise. ============================================= === SERMON: message(169).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 10/31/2025 1:40:05 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message WHOSE THE BOSS? Luke 20:1-8 cf. Matthew 21:23-27; Mark 11:27-33 INTRODUCTION Question Authority Benjamin Franklin is often cited as making the original quote: It is the first responsibility of every citizen to question authority. However, there is no historical evidence that he ever made the statement. The statement was no doubt popularized by Timothy Leary who was an advocate of psychedelic drug research and a proponent of the beneficial effects of LSD. some people have suggested that the idea behind the slogan can be traced back to the ancient Greek philosopher Socrates . One of the most influential icons in the counterculture movement which formed in the late 1960s out of opposition to the Vietnam War 's escalation, Leary gained influence among much of the youth by advocating the use of LSD as a way of mind expansion and the revelation of personal truth. Not only was there the war in Vietnam, but we lived through the Watergate scandal. It led to bumper stickers and other forms of anarchy against the government and other authority figures including parents and employers. The idea of questioning authority is today most often seen in an adversarial role taken on by the media, congressional hearings, and no kings rallies. It is that adversarial role we see in Luke 20. 20:1 came upon stand upon; a variety of applications including assault Much has taken place in the last couple of days to raise their BP, provide their lack of sleep, schedule late night meetings, and add fuel to their hatred Religious leaders of different stripes are working together Sadducees ran the priesthood Elders were the traditionalists Scribes were the theologians; included among the elders They have a common enemy: Jesus These groups comprised the Supreme Court of Israel, the Sanhedrin This was the evil trio responsible for the death of Jesus 9:22; 22:66 What should have been the smartest group in Israel was the most foolish They knew the Law but was blinded as to its real meaning They were the custodians of the temple; Jesus claimed superior jurisdiction Jesus was back in the temple; teaching, evangelizing, healing (Matt) Not sitting as a teacher but Matthew says He was walking in the temple area Now, He is asked for His credentials Jesus is confronted; to stand upon ; suddenly appear; in your face assault An interesting word: 2 Tim. 4:2 "preach the word, be ready (ephistemi) in season and out of season" The same word is used in 2:38 when the angels suddenly stood before the shepherds Jesus spoke with authority; the people recognized that; 4:36 The leaders were ignorant of it either QUESTION 20:2 From those who were assigned authority these things immediate context: Entry, testimonies, cleansing, healings "Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?" How might that be said today? Who do you think you are? How dare you wreak havoc over our well-oiled machine? What makes you think you can do that? How dare you come in here and throw your weight around? Who made you judge and jury? This is our turf, and you are not permitted to tread on it Not the first occasion in Scripture of Questioning God? Isaiah 29:16 Isaiah 45:9 Romans 9:20 How often have any of us questioned God? Are you sure? Really? You think maybe this slipped passed you? Why? or more specifically, Why me? Authority: a look at two NT words ????s?a exous a, ex-oo-see'-ah: the right to act, freedom of action when used of God, it is absolute, unrestricted When used of man: it is delegated; Revelation 22:14 The right to exercise authority; jurisdiction, competency Jesus is soon to tell the disciples, All authority has been given to Me Matthew 28:18 Power and authority (right and might, power and privilege) Luke 4:32, 36; 5:24 the people recognized it The teachers of the day based their teaching on the authority of others Another rabbi who trained them d??a ?? dunamis, doo'-nam-is: power, might power in action; Romans 1:16; 1 Corinthians 1:18 The question was not about His power but His right to perform action What credentials do you have; Who gave you this authority? What denomination ordained you? Where did you go to seminary? What is your degree? Since when did you become a rabbi? Who trained you? If Jesus said He was acting on His own authority, they could have called Him crazy If, on the other hand, He claimed His authority came from God, they could accuse Him of blasphemy If they had been paying attention, He told them of His authority 19:46 My house Jesus spoke with His own authority and proceeded to quote God s Word, the final authority 19:46 Steven Cole: The problem that those Jewish religious leaders faced is the same problem that every person who comes into contact with Jesus faces: The Problem: His authority confronts my authority. It may begin with a message you don t like; church or chapel Then a verse of Scripture drops on your toes Where you were once able to brush it off, now it starts to work on you You know if you yield to His Word, changes will have to happen You are not sure you want to yield control So, you duck, you dodge, your argue, you make excuses Sooner or later, you have to ask, who is going to order my life? Authority is a fundamental question of life: Who has the right to govern? These questioners were watching their influence, their authority evaporate They were panicking They had been watching, they had been listening; they could not compete So, they attacked His authority Has Jesus upended any tables to interrupt your selfish life? Have you been challenged as to who is your ultimate authority? You could have been doing the best you could, obeying the best you knew how, not doing anything sinful or immoral; then Jesus steps in and with one jerk everything is upended And your reaction is, who do You think You are to upset my life like this? THE COUNTER QUESTION 20:3-6 This was a common rabbinic practice If you ever watched a Charle Kirk debate, he often did this The intent is to get things to a deeper level 20:22, 24 The correct answer to the counter question will satisfy the initial inquiry John the Baptist He testified to the divine source of his mission In demanding an answer from them, our Lord was claiming an answer as from authorized teachers who were acquainted with the facts The implication: there are only two sources of authority: God or man Jesus is not dodging their question Simply put: If you properly answer my question, you will have the answer to your question If John was God s prophet and he pointed to Jesus as Messiah, then Jesus was acting under God s authority. Why did Jesus focus on John s baptism? the baptism of John was a type of baptism that had never been practiced in Israel prior to John's ministry the Jews had their baptisms but they were all ritualistic, symbolic, external John s baptism was associated with genuine repentance and a radical change of one's heart. John s baptism called for a confession of sins and repentance of heart shown in visible fruit Luke 3:3 preaching the baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins The baptism was a testimony to a changed heart telling the people to believe in the One who was to come after him 3:16, 17 The essence of John s baptism was not the physical act of immersion in water, but it is what this act symbolized which was belief in the coming Messiah, confession of sins, repentance and forgiveness of sins resulting in genuine salvation The group before Jesus had already rejected John s baptism 7:28-30 Question: by what authority did John practice his ministry? THE ANSWERS 20:7, 8 Answer #1 the response of cowards An honest answer would have been: we don t want to answer That would have been cowardly, but honest we do not know is a lie Stein: "it was not ignorance but insincerity and hypocrisy that shaped their answer." They were exposed before all observers They were not interested in truth They had 3 years of observation of Jesus public ministry They were looking to do damage, find fault, find something to use against Him Answer #2 the response of judgment v. 8 when you refuse to believe the teachings of Jesus, there will come a time when all teaching will cease and the only thing left is judgment 22:67-69 There was no point in continuing to cast pearls before swine Kent Hughes: This was another authoritative slam dunk by gentle Jesus, meek and mild and in one of the temple courts! The implied judgment upon the chief priests and elders is sustained in the parable of the vineyard which immediately follows CONCLUSION Before we share the Lord s table, who is your authority? There are only two: God or yourself Are you in danger of judgement because you reject God s authority? Power and the Lord s Table John 10:17, 18 Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again. No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father." WHOSE THE BOSS? Luke 20:1-8 Religious leaders of different stripes are working together Jesus is confronted: suddenly appear THE QUESTION 20:2 Questioning God Isaiah 29:16 Isaiah 45:9 Romans 9:20 Authority: exous a The right to exercise authority dunamis Power in action Romans 1:16 Jesus spoke with His own authority and proceeded to quote God s Word, the final authority 19:46 The Problem: His authority confronts my authority. Authority is a fundamental question of life THE COUNTER QUESTION 20:3-6 The intent is to get things to a deeper level 20:22, 24 If you properly answer my question, you will have the answer to your question John s baptism was associated with genuine repentance and a radical change of one's heart. Luke 3:3; 3:16, 17 The group before Jesus had already rejected John s baptism 7:28-30 THE ANSWERS 20:7, 8 Answer #1 the response of cowards Answer #2 the response of judgment Luke 22:67-69 WHOSE THE BOSS? Luke 20:1-8 Religious leaders of different stripes are working together Jesus is confronted: suddenly appear THE QUESTION 20:2 Questioning God Isaiah 29:16 Isaiah 45:9 Romans 9:20 Authority: exous a The right to exercise authority dunamis Power in action Romans 1:16 Jesus spoke with His own authority and proceeded to quote God s Word, the final authority 19:46 The Problem: His authority confronts my authority. Authority is a fundamental question of life THE COUNTER QUESTION 20:3-6 The intent is to get things to a deeper level 20:22, 24 If you properly answer my question, you will have the answer to your question John s baptism was associated with genuine repentance and a radical change of one's heart. Luke 3:3; 3:16, 17 The group before Jesus had already rejected John s baptism 7:28-30 THE ANSWERS 20:7, 8 Answer #1 the response of cowards Answer #2 the response of judgment Luke 22:67-69 ============================================= === SERMON: message(170).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 11/7/2025 12:00:37 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE THE COST OF REJECTING AUTHORITY Luke 20:9-19 cf. Matt. 21:33-46; Mark 12:1-12 INTRODUCTION The religious authorities are mad. I looked up some slang terms Filled with rage; steamed; furious, irate; aggro; seeing red Blow a fuse; lose one s marbles; go bonkers; have a meltdown From the South: dills my pickle; fit to be tied; hissy fit He s got his tail up, bless his heart From the 1970 s: ticked to the max They are about to snap. They are liars. They have been exposed. Jesus is laying them out for all the people to see just who they are. They lord it over the people. Yet, they are afraid of the people. The whole chapter becomes a stand-off between Jesus and the leaders of Jewish worship Shortly, as recorded in Matthew 23, Jesus will pronounce woes upon the scribes and Pharisees Jesus never attacks the political leaders or Rome the way He undresses the religious people. If our country continues its direction toward socialism, it will become all the more imperative for us to focus on the spiritual and not the political world. Rome was as corrupt as the day is long; Jesus preached the kingdom of God Folks, proper worship must always be forefront on our minds What does God expect? What is acceptable worship? What must be avoided? I know we are to worship in spirit and in truth (Jn. 4) I know we are told to come boldly before the throne of grace (Heb. 4) But have we lost the element of fear: Psalm 5:7 (KJV) But as for me, I will come into thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple. Psalm 96:9 (KJV) O worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth. Revelation 14:7 Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters. Revelation 15:4 Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for thou only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before thee; for thy judgments are made manifest. Church history tells us it is easy to get out of balance Some find liturgical services too stuffy; others find contemporary worship too unorganized. Some scream legalism while others are shouting freedom Some sit in silence until the spirit moves while others are full of noise and the louder the better What most have in common is what appeals to the individual and little consideration to what God wants Worship in Jesus day had become so regulated that it required a wholesale upending of the tables of money changers and those selling approved sacrificial animals. Worship had deteriorated to the place where Jesus pronounces judgment upon those responsible. Worship is Jesus day was not from the heart They heard and read the words, but their heart was far from God Some refer to this event in the temple as the Day of Antagonism Matthew records another parable spoken before this one: 21:28-32 The Parable of the Two Sons TRANSLATION 20:9 This parable is unlike some in that there is little doubt who the principle players are Even those listening understood who this parable is pointing to; 20:19 * The Owner of the Vineyard = God the Father * The Vineyard = The nation of Israel * Vine-growers = The religious rulers of Israel entrusted with leading Israel - chief priests, scribes and Pharisees, and the elders. * Slaves = The prophets of God that were persecuted and killed. * Son = The Lord Jesus The Design of the Parable Indirectly answer the question of His authority Show them He knew about their plan to kill Him Reveal the awaiting judgment for rejecting Him as the Messiah Steve Cole: To follow up the challenge of the Jewish leaders to Jesus about the source of His authority, He tells a parable about some wicked tenants of a vineyard, who had wrongfully assumed ownership of that which was not their own. It is one of only three parables that occur in all three synoptic gospels (the sower and the mustard seed are the other two). The parable answers the question that the leaders had just asked Jesus: By what authority are you doing these things? If God owns the vineyard and Jesus is the Son and rightful heir to it, then He is acting under God s authority. TRANSGRESS 20:10-15 The fundamental question is: Who owns the vineyard? Isaiah 5:1-7; Jeremiah 25:4-11 The history of Israel is summed up in this dark picture Alfred Plummer - The uniform hostility of kings, priests, and people to the Prophets is one of the most remarkable features in history of the Jews. The amount of hostility varied, and it expressed itself in different ways, on the whole increasing in intensity; but it was always there In this parable Jesus is not only reminding the religious establishment what they were like, but He was putting in their minds a question: how could they claim obedience as God s people and still reject His messengers? Countless prophets had been sent but all have been rejected Now they are guilty of rejecting the only Son of the Landowner What will the owner do to the evil tenants? Jesus is forcing the religious leaders to declare their own miserable fate Not an unusual approach when presenting a parable: let the people finish Matthew s account: Jesus ask the question and the people answer Matthew 21:41 He will bring those wretches to a wretched end, and will rent out the vineyard to other vine-growers, who will pay him the proceeds at the proper seasons. In Luke s account, Jesus affirms the response They knew exactly who the last messenger was: the heir It was not a question of evidence They heard, they saw, they witnessed miracles; they witnessed power John 12:42 many among the chief rulers did believe But with Jesus out of the way, who would dispute their authority? Kill the Son and the vineyard would be theirs This is the depravity of the human heart They did not kill Him because they thought He was a counterfeit Messiah but because they knew He was the Messiah! TRANSFER 20:16 In the process of being left out of the kingdom, they learn that the kingdom will be given to other tenants Matt. 21:41, 43 those others will producing the fruit of it This new people will have a personal relationship with the Landowner the original tenants rejected The new tenants will produce fruit for the kingdom in spreading God s Word to all people Exodus 19:5, 6 Who are these new tenants? Many say Gentiles who will believe But the word others is allos ; others of the same kind So, Jesus could be referring to the 12 apostles and Paul Jewish apostles replace Jewish religious leaders And the early church was primarily saved Jews A change of metaphors: from a Vineyard and a Rejected Son To a Building and Rejected Stone While the Resurrection of Christ is not specifically mentioned in the parable, it is suggested by the mention of Cornerstone Recorded in their own Scriptures: Psalm 118:22, 23 The very Psalm from which originated the Hosannas at the Triumphal Entry TRAUMA 20:17-19 He fixed His gaze on them literally, He saw in them Eyeball to eyeball 22:61 Jesus looking at Peter after his denial Sticks the knife in: have you never read? You just don t say that to a leading theologian Of course they had, they were recited every year The bitter irony is that the very psalm used to hail Him as Messiah and King of Israel, Jesus uses to foretell Israel's rejection of Him! What are they going to do with the chief cornerstone? The main piece of the foundation is soon to be cast aside By rejecting the Son, the foundation of a beautiful building, that same Stone will be used to crush and destroy It is interesting to note that in either case the individual is broken By falling on the stone or having the stone fall on them When we come to Christ for salvation, we come as a sinner, broken in spirit, broken in heart We are built upon the foundation Those who reject will be pulverized; not annihilated, but cease to exist in God s presence God offers a choice of brokenness Broken before Him or crushed by Him The Application is simple enough Have you come to know Christ as your Lord and Savior, or have you rejected Him like the Jewish leadership did? The listeners to this parable would have been shocked that a landowner would send another after another who would be so mistreated And then send his own so to be killed? Second, if you are a believer, what have you done with Jesus? Are you like the bad tenants, rejecting His Word and living a life of disobedience? It goes back to who is the Authority in your life? We have been given a great privilege and position and God expects production We here in America are the most spiritually privileged people alive We have God s Word in our language; we have unlimited spiritual resources; we have plenty of leisure time; we have financial resources to support God s work around the globe Are we laboring for what we can get out of the vineyard or what we can produce for the owner? Is not it a marvelous thing that God sends to us a multitude of teachers to call us to salvation and obedience? God could have called a halt to His patience long before we ever got our house in order. But He didn t. He is indeed longsuffering. Ephesians 3:14-21 THE COST OF REJECTING AUTHORITY Luke 20:9-19 The whole chapter becomes a stand-off between Jesus and the leaders of Jewish worship Psalm 5:7 Psalm 96:9 Revelation 14:7 Revelation 15:4 Worship is Jesus day was not from the heart TRANSLATION 20:9 The Design of the Parable Indirectly answer the question of His authority Show them He knew about their plan to kill Him Reveal the awaiting judgment for rejecting Him as the Messiah TRANSGRESS 20:10-15 Jesus is forcing the religious leaders to declare their own miserable fate They knew exactly who the last messenger was This is the depravity of the human heart TRANSFER 20:16 The new tenants will produce fruit for the kingdom Who are these new tenants? Gentiles Jewish apostles replace Jewish religious leaders Psalm 118:22, 23 The very Psalm from which originated the Hosannas at the Triumphal Entry TRAUMA 20:17-19 He fixed His gaze on them literally, He saw in them It is interesting that in either case the individual is broken God offers a choice of brokenness Broken before Him or crushed by Him It goes back to who is the Authority in your life? THE COST OF REJECTING AUTHORITY Luke 20:9-19 The whole chapter becomes a _____________ between Jesus and the leaders of Jewish worship Psalm 5:7 Psalm 96:9 Revelation 14:7 Revelation 15:4 Worship is Jesus day was _________ from the heart TRANSLATION 20:9 The Design of the Parable Indirectly answer the question of His ____________ Show them He __________ about their plan to kill Him Reveal the awaiting _____________ for rejecting Him as the Messiah TRANSGRESS 20:10-15 Jesus is forcing the religious leaders to declare their own miserable _______ They knew ____________ who the last messenger was This is the _____________ of the human heart TRANSFER 20:16 The new tenants will produce ___________ for the kingdom Who are these new tenants? ______________ ______________ apostles replace Jewish religious leaders Psalm 118:_________ The very Psalm from which originated the Hosannas at the Triumphal Entry TRAUMA 20:17-19 He fixed His gaze on them literally, He saw ______ them It is interesting that in either case the individual is _____________ God offers a ____________ of brokenness Broken before Him or ____________ by Him It goes back to who is the Authority in your life? ============================================= === SERMON: message(171).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 11/14/2025 7:47:02 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message CITIZENS AND SAINTS Luke 20:20-26 INTRODUCTION Derangement Syndrome: a pejorative term used to describe negative reactions; incapable of accurately perceiving the world Originally coined by Charles Krauthammer and defined as "the acute onset of paranoia in otherwise normal people in reaction to the policies even a persons very existence First used of President Bush and now often of President Trump It seems to me the term accurately describes the religious leaders in Jesus day Their power base is slipping away; they have been exposed They have no answers; every attempt to discredit is rejected They have Jesus derangement syndrome They hate Him so much they can t see straight; can t think coherently To prove they no longer can think straight, after many failed attempts, they come up with another effort to find fault. Why did they think they could trap Him now? Psalm 66:3 How terrible art thou in thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee submit is not the best translation; the word means to feign obedience, deceive, deny Psalm 81:15 YLT: Those hating Jehovah feign obedience to Him They had been watching closely; 6:7; 14:1 Watching desperately for some slip of the tongue Anything to convince the Romans to arrest Him Why the Roman governor? Only Rome could carry out capital punishment The religious leaders could wash their hands of the whole situation and blame Rome Warning to Us: the enemies of Christ are watching for our downfall Eager to hear or tell of some evil thing about us Ready to cast the stone of slander against us Ephesians 5:15, 16 Psalm 119:117 Do we ever pretend to be righteous before others? A make-believe performance Where we get the word hypocrite: to play a part The Light had exposed the darkness of their heart It is a fool who comes before He who is the wisdom of God believing he could entice Jesus into a trap Here they try the Tribute Trap They must have sat up all night coming up with this A lot of conversation; a lot of ideas discussed and they settle on this one Matthew 22:1-14 has the Parable of the Marriage Feast prior THE SETUP 20:21 A 1968 song by Lynn Anderson said flattery will get you everywhere Others insist flattery will get you nowhere Whether it is butter up, kiss up, curry favor; might work for a while but will soon be found out Hughes: Flattery is the reverse mirror image of gossip Gossip involves saying behind a person s back what you would never say to his face. Flattery is saying to a person s face what you would never say behind his back. (Proverbs 26:28; 29:5; Psalm 12:3) Orthodox truth was in their lips, but hypocrisy was in their hearts we know your teaching is orthodox The Bible teaches us what is right; that is orthodoxy Or the word orthopedics: straight bones Mark 12:14 we know that you are truthful lying through their teeth Consistent Also non-partial no respecter of persons receive the face to show partiality; (James 2:1-3) We don t change the message depending on who we are talking to Jesus teaches the way the manner in which we are to think and live We all want to look good to others Remember when you started dating? Best behavior, clean breath, didn t pick your nose; no belching; didn t use a key to clean the wax out of your ear ILLUSTRATION: two old folks marry after their spouses have died Upon entering the bridal chamber the old fellow stood there aghast This was his account: We got married last Friday, my girl was right there beside me; Our friends were all gone, we were alone; side by side. We were so happily wed when, she got ready for bed then Her teeth and her hair she placed in a chair, side by side. One glass eye so tiny, one hearing aid so small, Then she took one leg off and placed on the chair by the wall. I stood there broken hearted, most of my girl had departed, I slept on the chair, there was more of her there, side by side. THE SUCKER PUNCH 20:22 is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not? they were hoping for a yes or no answer Yes and they could stir up the people against Him No and they report Him to the Roman officials Paying taxes has always been a complaint of citizens Even Rome used some tax money to provide roads, like all governments they take more than they return There were three taxes which were collected: 1. Ground Tax: Made up of 10% of all the grain and 20% of all the wine and fruit produced. 2. Income Tax: 1% of a man s income. 3. Poll Tax: A flat tax of one denarius (a day s wage) was paid by all men from 14 to 65 years of age and on all women between the ages of 12 to 65. Paying the poll tax was especially offensive to them The question is it lawful is really is it right ? is it allowed ? Here the Jews were not discussing the legality of paying poll tax to Caesar, but whether a Jew should do so in view of his theocratic relationship to God. This money is used to support a pagan government and pagan gods Paying taxes is a symbol of submission and dependence They believed that they and the land belonged to God It was offensive to live as if they belonged to Caesar They thought they had Jesus cornered They even used this against Jesus before Pilate; 23:2 THE SETTLEMENT 20:23-24 He quickly proved them wrong. Again. v. 23 Trap quickly detected perceived to observe fully, discover Matthew: malice; Mark: hypocrisy Folks, you can t wear a mask thick enough to hide your motives from God It is a fearful thing to fall as hypocrites into the hands of the living God I could ask, by show of hands, have you ever played the part of a hypocrite? Hebrews 4:13 There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him with whom we have to do. And as Jesus has done before: answer a question with a question Show me a penny a Roman coin with the stamp of Caesar And the words Tiberius Caesar , son of the divine Augustus. This is the coin used to pay the dreaded poll tax Now this is interesting. Did Jesus know someone in the crowd was in possession of such a coin? Yea, I got one right here! Herodians probably were more likely to carry Or since only Jewish coins were used in the temple, it took some time for a coin to be retrieved? Simple question: Whose image (likeness) is this? Colossians 1:15 same word who is the image of the invisible God Genesis 1:25 same word in LXX Jesus did not see it as a political issue but a spiritual one By using the coin, you recognize Caesar s authority Just as the coin bears the image of Caesar, you have a responsibility to him As you bear the image of God, you have a responsibility to Him It is not an either/or but a both/and If you are using your government s money to buy & sell; then you are in debt to the government under which you live & enjoy its benefits. Human government was instituted by God Romans 13:1-7 Give to the government what is his and give to God what is His God and Caesar each have legitimate realms of authority Give Caesar his due; you enjoy some benefits provided Jesus affirmed that it would be wrong to go along with Caesar s blasphemous claim to deity, which was stamped on each coin. One side read, Tiberius Caesar , son of the divine Augustus ("divine Augustus"? Woe!); the other read, Pontifex Maximus ( Chief Priest ). In Jesus answer He states God is above Caesar We do not give to Caesar what would violate our obligation to God Whose image do you bear? By creation all men bear the image of God Gen. 1:26, 27 Only by redemption do we bear the image of Christ 1 Cor. 6:19, 20 Or don't you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit which is in you, which you have from God? You are not your own, for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God's. Romans 12:1 present that body as a living sacrifice Are you rendering to God what is due to Him alone? We are to be loyal citizens and real saints We owe God our very existence He owns us, our money, our possessions, our time As Daniel 4 repeats three times, The Most High is ruler over the realm of mankind, and bestows it on whom He wishes (Da 4:17 , 25 , 32 ) Even Nebuchadnezzar came to understand that A few years ago, the late Senate Chaplain Richard Halverson told about a senator who was speaking at a church men s dinner. The senator asked how many men believed in prayer in the public schools. Almost every hand went up. He then asked, How many of you pray daily with your children in your home? Citizens and saints Give your coin to Caesar but give your life to God Caesar has some authority, but only God reigns supreme Roman coinage was not even accepted in the temple; hence money changers Temple coins should be put in the Temple box; not to pay Romans taxes Who showed up to ask the question: Herodians and Pharisees Herodians: supported Herod and the Roman Government Pharisees: strict observance of the Law and traditions Opposite ends of the political spectrum; common hatred for Jesus Each had a stake in the question and answer Jesus drew a sharp distinction between the two kingdoms We are part of both worlds, at least temporarily Some things are material; some things are eternal Romans 13:1-8 1 Timothy 1:17 Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen. CITIZENS AND SAINTS Luke 20:20-26 THE SETUP 20:21 Flattery is the reverse mirror image of gossip Orthodox The Bible teaches us what is right Consistent the way the manner in which we are to think and live THE SUCKER PUNCH 20:22 They were hoping for a yes or no answer Paying the poll tax was especially offensive to them is it lawful is really is it right ? Paying taxes is a symbol of submission and dependence THE SETTLEMENT 20:23-24 Hebrews 4:13 Colossians 1:15 Genesis 1:25 Jesus did not see it as a political issue but a spiritual one Human government was instituted by God Romans 13:1-7 We do not give to Caesar what would violate our obligation to God 1 Cor. 6:19, 20 Romans 12:1 We are to be loyal citizens and real saints Give your coin to Caesar but give your life to God ============================================= === SERMON: message(172).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 11/21/2025 12:36:23 PM To: Subject: message SAD-SACK SADDUCEES Luke 20:27-40 INTRODUCTION Sad Sack: a blundering, inept person a sad sack is one so unlucky in love that when his girlfriend splits, his family sides with her. Rand Richards Cooper Interesting when you read verse 27 where you put the emphasis Regular reading: Now there came to Him some Sadducees Tired of the hassle reading: NOW, here come the SADDUCEES! The attacks just wont stop! First came the chief priests, scribes, and elders 20:1-8 Following the prophetic parable spoken against them (9-19), the Herodians make their attempt (20-26) about paying taxes Now the Sadducees come to question Jesus Described by Luke: which deny that there is any resurrection That is not just some well-reasoned, philosophical disagreement Ecclesiastes 3:11 "He has made everything appropriate in its time. He has also set eternity [Lxx = aion ] in their heart, yet so that man will not find out the work which God has done from the beginning even to the end." They cannot deny what God has placed in their heart They obviously simply refused to believe this divine desire in their heart! RESURRECTION RIDDLE 20:28-33 They were mainly a political group but maintained control of the high priestly line They were dominant in the Sanhedrin They were concerned with the wealthy in opposition to Jesus and the Pharisees One may assume their approach and questioning of Jesus was condescending Pharisees were ritualists; Sadducees were rationalists Legalists liberals Separatists compromisers, opportunists Regarded the Pentateuch more highly than the rest of the Scriptures They were not looking for a Messiah They were philosophically sophisticated. Have you ever noticed that when people achieve a certain social strata, they often stop believing in certain things? They depend on reason as the establishment of religious truth If something went beyond human reason, such as raising the dead, they rejected it Believing in God transcends human reason The way we know the truth of Scripture is first by being born from above by God s power so that we come to know Him and then by submitting our reason and our will to God s revelation in Scripture. We must hold to all that God s Word reveals, even if it doesn t fit with our finite reasoning, or we will fall into serious doctrinal error. Rationalism undermines God s power; faith in His Word affirms it." As my former pastor use to say: We rationalize until our rationalizations become rational lies. The put their belief system in the Pentateuch They did not see the resurrection there, so they easily dismissed any reference in the rest of the OT Job 19:25-27; Psalm 16:9-11; Isa. 26:19; Daniel 12:2, 13 Their question for Jesus and His answer came from the Pentateuch Deuteronomy 25:5; Exodus 3:6 Absurd: contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly. Inconsistent with reason, inviting ridicule; manifestly false, ludicrous. Can make a rock so big He cannot move it? How many angels can sit on the head of a pin? There have been Hollywood stars married 8 times: E. Taylor; M. Rooney Spurgeon: This was one of the stock stories they were in the habit of telling in order to cast ridicule upon the resurrection. v. 29 levirate marriage Latin "levir" which means a "husband s brother" God made a provision for a family to be raised up in name and property rights for a husband who dies with no male heir. with this hypothetical case they chose to silence this self-made rabbi and the pharisaical stance on bodily resurrection after quoting the OT, the Sadducees gave an extreme example to show the absurdity of bodily resurrection and to test its validity Fascinating that a family had 8 boys No one seems to be suspicious of the woman I guess they have never watched Dateline This is the consummate widow maker. If I had been the third or fourth brother, I would have been asking questions! She dies and there is a collective sigh The Sadducees presupposition: "that resurrection life is an exact counterpart to earthly life The woman would be guilty of incestuous marriages or arbitrarily assigned to one of the brothers Or the whole notion of a resurrection is absurd To them the question shows a serious flaw in believing a resurrection And let s face it, there will be many in heaven who have had more than one spouse RESURRECTION REALIZATION 20:34-36 Resurrection for the Jew was a prolongation of this life with a few modifications such as the absence of pain, troubles, enemies, and addition of peace, rest, and bliss Jesus explains what you see is not what you are going to get There is a difference this age (34) and that age (35) He sets them straight: Matthew and Mark accounts: Jesus accused the Sadducees they neither understood the Scriptures nor the power of God Jesus deals with both of the mistakes of the Sadducees 1. He corrects their misunderstanding God s power = Luke 20:34-36 2. He corrects their misunderstanding of the Scripture = Luke 20:37-38 The same reasons people run around today speaking lies about God Here is what Jesus wants us to REALIZE, know about heaven There is no Marriage in Heaven v. 34 common in this age; normal part of life v. 35 no marriages The purpose of marriage, companionship and reproduction, will no longer exist This destroys Islamic and Morman theology Yes, I believe family relationships will still be known, but the glory of heaven will be a relationship and connection with God that surpasses anything else The pleasures of this world will be far surpassed by the joys of heaven There are Qualifications for Heaven 35 That world will be for those who are considered or counted worthy of the age to come Resurrection of the dead will be experienced by everyone Resurrection out of the dead ones refers to the righteous who are raised to everlasting life What Jesus says about the resurrection relates to believers Worthy because saved by grace, saved by the blood of the crucified One Our worthiness comes from an outside source Worthy 4x NT 21:36; Acts 5:41; 2 Thess. 1:5 And not made worthy but declared worthy The word attain is also troublesome to our understanding Attain: gain with effort can never be said of our salvation The word means to hit the mark We are deemed worthy because we have attained the gospel ticket There is no Death in Heaven Will not die anymore The quality of resurrection life: it is eternal Preserving life will no longer be a priority through procreation Jesus did not say, they will not die but they cannot die Death is swallowed up in victory Christians will never meet for the last time! This a direct assault on the Sadducees denying the power of God Not only will He raise the dead, they cannot ever die We will not be Angels Like angels: in the same manner as, after the fashion of Similarity and equality limited to the restrictions of the context which speaks of marriage and the propagation of the race Human beings will still be human beings with resurrected bodies You will NOT become an angel Sons of God being sons of the resurrection Essential nature, defining quality v. 34 sons of this age the present passing world Sons of God: the character or quality of the life of God Sons of the resurrection: characterized by resurrection from the dead To be a son of God is to experience the closeness and security of a Father Who loves us, accepts us, and wants to know us personally and intimately. Galatians 3:26 1 John 3:1 Of all the titles you may have after your name: titles, degrees, etc There will none better than child of God Paul is saying (Ephesians 5) that the marriage relationship, and especially the one flesh aspect of marriage, is the closest earthly picture that we have to our union with Christ, which will be consummated in heaven RESURRECTION RULE 20:37-40 Mark is straightforward in his record: 12:24 you are mistaken 12:27 you are badly mistaken You are greatly roaming from the truth The question of authority has been predominant in this chapter Jesus could have used several OT verses but He answered in terms of the Pentateuch He knew the Sadducees preference for the Pentateuch over the rest of the OT Further, they saw no place in the Pentateuch for the resurrection Jesus quotes Exodus 3:6 He (Jehovah) said also, I am (Lxx = "ego eimi" = present tense = continually) the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look at God. God is God of the living though He mentions those already dead The Sadducees could not claim He quoted from an invalid source If God is the God of these 3 men that means they are still alive and if they are still alive the resurrection is true Jesus further stated that there is relationship between God and the living in that all live to Him All live to Him He quoted the Pentateuch and showed that even Moses attested to a life after death Matthew: the crowds were astonished They had probably never heard anyone silence the Sadducees on this point of the Resurrection Even the scribes had to admit that what Jesus said was well-spoken 40 end of discussion Matthew and Mark do add one more question in their gospels Which is the great commandment of the Law?
