They record a simple answer with no further discussion SAD-SACK SADDUCEES Luke 20:27-40 Sadducees which deny that there is any resurrection Ecclesiastes 3:11 RESURRECTION RIDDLE 20:28-33 Sadducees were rationalists, liberals, compromisers Regarded the Pentateuch more highly than the rest of the Scriptures Rationalism undermines God s power; faith in His Word affirms it." RESURRECTION REALIZATION 20:34-36 There is no Marriage in Heaven The pleasures of this world will be far surpassed by the joys of heaven There are Qualifications for Heaven 35 Worthy because saved by grace We are deemed worthy because we have attained the gospel ticket There is no Death in Heaven Jesus did not say, they will not die but they cannot die We are not Angels Human beings will still be human beings with resurrected bodies Sons of God: the character or quality of the life of God RESURRECTION RULE 20:37-40 Mark 12:24, 27 Exodus 3:6 The Sadducees could not claim He quoted from an invalid source ============================================= === SERMON: message(173).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 11/29/2025 12:56:04 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THANKSGIVING LEFTOVERS Psalm 100 INTRODUCTION I admit being a little perplexed getting ready for this morning. College students are gone, but I also knew that several of our folks would be out of town. It was a busy week with a holiday and family My focus was not so much on Luke 20 and the next verses seemed to be a nice beginning to the month of December. And to top it off, I was not yet ready to yield over the subject of Thanksgiving before getting into the nativity season. And using the fact that some of us at least have turkey and pie left over from family gatherings, I chose to title the message Thanksgiving Leftovers . I don t want to give the impression that offering God praise is something done after everything else has been done. We get to the end of a day without giving God much thought and as we lay down to sleep it is Oh, thank you God for the day. Praising God is not some P.S. we add to the end of the day But neither is it to be a day on the calendar And I don t know about you, but it is another spiritual activity that gets lost in the day-to-day grind. Life is busy and praise becomes an add-on rather than the direction Revelation 4:11 Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honor and power: for thou hast created all things, and for thy pleasure they are and were created. Pleasure is an interesting word. Used 60x in the NT 58x it is translated will Thy will be done an apostle by the will of God 1x desires Eph 2:3 fulfilling the desires of the flesh 1x pleasure It means choice; unsaved people choose the desires of the flesh; We are to understand what the will of the Lord is (5:17) and then as servants of Christ doing the will of God from the heart (6:6) Whatever God wills brings Him pleasure We should find the same pleasure in seeking and doing His will God is worthy to receive praise from us To add some additional thoughts, please open your Scriptures to Psalm 100 A favorite Psalm of many; often memorized; often cited at least in part There are two main headings in understanding this Psalm There is an Invitation to Praise and the Foundation of Praise Not only does the psalmist invite us into a life of Praise, but he also presents to us the basis, the reason, the ground from which Praise should be given. THE INVITATION 100:1, 2, 4 For all people: all ye lands It is a call to activity: make noise, serve, come, enter The call is to jubilant worship Isaiah 51:11 they shall obtain gladness and joy; and sorrow and mourning shall flee away. The first part of the verse; a song from about 1972 Therefore the redeemed of the LORD shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy shall be upon their head: But what is the basis for such an invitation? THE FOUNDATION 100:3 Know In your bulletin there is the statement: Worship is our response to God s revelation That is why we begin the service with Scripture The Revelation from God should set the agenda for the day Music has a role, but no musical selection is God-breathed, no matter how inspirational it may be Make a joyful noise, serve the Lord, come before His presence Has depth to it because of what we know You cannot worship God with an empty head Worship does not start in the heart but the head Worship does not start with emotionalism Emotions will be involved but worship goes from the head to the heart, not from the heart to the head Serving the Lord with gladness is not based on feelings or my circumstances The real basis for thanksgiving is knowing that the Lord is God Jehovah is Elohim The first thing to know is He is God The second thing to know: He has made us ; we are His Not that we are part of the creative order for He made all things We are His people, the sheep of His pasture We were once not the people of God but now we are We are His by His choice 1 Peter 2:9 But ye are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of darkness into his marvelous light: Psalm 40:2, 3 He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings. And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the LORD. We belong to Him; we have been purchased; we are not our own That is why we can sing: I know whom I have believed and am persuaded that He is able to keep that which I ve committed to Him against that day And I know, yes, I know; Jesus' blood can make the vilest sinner clean C. S. Lewis on praise: when he first become a Christian, he couldn t get the praise thing at all. He couldn t understand why it was that anybody would ask to be praised all the time. After all, he said, if you go to a party and somebody wants to be the center of attention all the time, you instinctively don t like that person. So why would I then like a God who wanted to be praised all the time and who wanted everybody to go around talking to each other and say, Hey why don t we praise God? I I couldn t get it figured out at all. But the most obvious fact about praise whether of God or anything strangely escaped me. I [had] thought of it in terms of compliment, approval, or the giving of honor. I had never noticed that all enjoyment spontaneously overflows into praise unless (sometimes even if) shyness or the fear of boring others is deliberately brought in to check it. The world rings with praise lovers readers walkers praising the countryside, players praising their favorite game praise of weather, [praise of] wines, dishes, actors, motors, horses, colleges, countries, historical personages, children, flowers, mountains, rare stamps, rare beetles, even sometimes politicians or scholars He says the whole world is about praise; a spontaneous acknowledgement of what is valuable to them So, if you have an assembly of the people of God and no expression of praise in singing, there is a problem People express what is most valuable to them Lewis: I [hadn t] noticed how the humblest, and at the same time most balanced and capacious, minds, praised most, while the cranks, misfits and malcontents praised least. And he adds this: Heaven knows, many poems of praise addressed to an earthly beloved are as bad as our bad hymns. I had not noticed either that just as men spontaneously praise whatever they value, so they spontaneously urge us to join them in praising it: Isn t she lovely? Wasn t it glorious? Don t you think that magnificent? The psalmists in telling everyone to praise God are doing what all men do when they speak of what they care about. Third thing to know: He is Good He is good in His mercy Endures forever Exodus 34:6 The Lord, the Lord compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in love and faithfulness, maintaining love to thousands, and forgiving wickedness, rebellion and sin. Mercy: lovingkindness Covenant of love; He saves; He keeps O love that will not let me go, I rest my weary soul in Thee He is good in His truth His Word abides forever to all generations It will always be truth Why is this important? 100:4 enter into His gates with thanksgiving and into His courts with praise. Be thankful unto Him and bless His name Thanksgiving and thankful; not the exact same word but one is found in the other It is sometimes used when people are under duress Thankful: Psalm 42:5, 11 depression Thanksgiving: used in the title to the Psalm praise But also Jonah 2:9 I will sacrifice to Thee with the voice of thanksgiving from the belly of a great fish What if you had no family for Thanksgiving? What if your circumstances were different? You were a Christian in Nigeria, the middle east? What if you lost your job, your health? The foundation of thanksgiving is what you know, not how you feel 1 Thess. 5 In everything give thanks Phil. 4:6 Be anxious for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God. The gates are still open. The invitation to praise is still ours The do what make you happy culture is so toxic to Christians. We are not called to do what makes us happy. We are called to do what glorifies God. Christianity is not all sunshine and happiness. It is hard work and dedication to Him, not us. Do. What. Glorifies. God. THANKSGIVING LEFTOVERS Psalm 100 THE INVITATION 100:1, 2, 4 The call is to jubilant worship THE FOUNDATION 100:3 Worship is our response to God s revelation You cannot worship God with an empty head The first thing to know is He is God The second thing to know: He has made us 1 Peter 2:9 Psalm 40:2, 3 Praise; a spontaneous acknowledgement of what is valuable to them Third thing to know: He is Good He is good in His mercy Mercy: lovingkindness He is good in His truth Thanksgiving and thankful sometimes used when people are under duress Psalm 42:5, 11 Jonah 2:9 The foundation of thanksgiving is what you know, not how you feel THANKSGIVING LEFTOVERS Psalm 100 THE INVITATION 100:1, 2, 4 The call is to _____________ worship THE FOUNDATION 100:3 Worship is our response to God s ______________ You cannot worship God with an _____________ head The ___________ thing to know is He is God The ___________ thing to know: He has made us 1 Peter 2:9 Psalm 40:2, 3 Praise; a _______________ acknowledgement of what is valuable to them _____________ thing to know: He is Good He is good in His mercy Mercy: _______________ He is good in His truth Thanksgiving and thankful sometimes used when people are under _____________ ____________ 42:5, 11 ____________ 2:9 The foundation of thanksgiving is what you __________, not how you ___________ ============================================= === SERMON: message(174).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/6/2025 9:59:02 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message THE TEACHER TEACHES LUKE 20:41-44 INTRODUCTION Jesus had patiently responded to the questions posed by the spiritual leaders of Israel. Even the absurd question posed by the Sadducees about the resurrection; a question I believe they had used before to silence the Pharisees. They had to be surprised that Jesus not only had a answer, but a complete destruction of their denial of a resurrection. It is hard to yield over a long-held belief The debates in the temple conclude with 3 occasions where Jesus challenges and rebukes the religious leaders The question about David s Son A rebuke of the teacher s of the Law The account of the widow s offering The Jews believed the Messiah would be human There is little doubt that the Jews did extensive research to determine that Jesus was a descendent of David 2 Samuel 7:12-14 They surely would have used such knowledge against Him if it proved to be false There is no record of them refuting His origin They never questioned the authenticity of His miraculous works Only questioned the source of His power Lazarus was dramatic proof that Jesus could raise the dead It is believed that all genealogical records were destroyed in 70 A.D. Thus, they have no genealogy records to prove any future Messiah candidate is actually from David's line! Judaism 101 is on online encyclopedia of Judaism covering Jewish beliefs, people, places, things, language, scripture, holidays, practices and customs, written from a traditional perspective in conversational language you can understand. This apparently is the view of modern orthodox Jews: The mashiach will be a great political leader descended from King David (Jeremiah 23:5 ) The mashiach is often referred to as "mashiach ben David" (mashiach, son of David). He will be well-versed in Jewish law, and observant of its commandments. He will be a charismatic leader, inspiring others to follow his example. He will be a great military leader, who will win battles for Israel. He will be a great judge, who makes righteous decisions. But above all, he will be a human being, not a god, demi-god or other supernatural being. It has been said that in every generation, a person is born with the potential to be the mashiach. If the time is right for the messianic age within that person's lifetime, then that person will be the mashiach. But if that person dies before he completes the mission of the mashiach, then that person is not the mashiach.... Jews do not believe that Jesus was the mashiach. Assuming that he existed, and assuming that the Christian scriptures are accurate in describing him (both matters that are debatable), he simply did not fulfill the mission of the mashiach as it is described in the biblical passages cited above. Jesus did not do any of the things that the scriptures said the messiah would do. (Source: Judaism 101 ) That brings us to Luke 20 The leaders have exhausted their attacks; they are out of ammunition DISCERNING QUESTION In this case, He takes an often quoted verse and questions their understanding Mark, how is it that the scribes say . . Matthew, what do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Mark 12:35 He is still teaching; still presenting truth; still seeking lost ones He was desirous, then, before the end came, to show from an acknowledged Messianic psalm that if he was Messiah and unquestionably a large proportion of the people received him as such he was also Divine. Psalm 110:1 The verse clearly teaches that if He is the Messiah, He is Divine Someone might ask: Is not this casting pearls before swine? Mark 12:34 you are not far from the kingdom of God Interesting that Jesus does not provide an answer It is the people s responsibility to seek out the truth Here is one final evangelistic effort 19:11 He came to seek and save the lost 19:41 He wept over the hardness of their hearts The Bible clearly presents Jesus with two natures: human and divine Often described as fully God and fully man ; or wholly God and wholly man ; or truly God and truly man In Matthew s account The religious leaders had consistently failed to see the incredible truth: that Christ, anointed one, Messiah, was to be God as well as man As man He was to be David s son; as God He was to be David s Lord Jesus has exposed their ignorance J. C. Ryle: We may well believe that of all the defeats which our Lord's malicious enemies met with, none galled them more than this. Nothing so mortifies the pride of man, as to be publicly proved ignorant of that which he fancies is his own peculiar department of knowledge. DEFICIENT ANSWER The Pharisees give the correct answer: the Son of David Partial credit The human qualification of Jesus is readily recognized by all the people 19:38 I can picture them standing before Jesus full of themselves There is no way they could expect part 2 to the question How could David describe his son as his Lord? Jesus distinctly claims divinity for Himself It has been a popular theme for a couple of chapters Blind Bartemaus: 18:38, 39 In the parable of the wicked husbandman 20:9-19 In His latest acts in the temple: driving out the sellers and buyers the children welcoming Him with Messianic language Matt. 21:15 receiving the praise of all at the triumphal entry Jesus was aware of the charge that would be brought against Him John 5:18 John 10:33 Luke 22:71 This cuts to a fundamental question of Christianity: What is the nature of Jesus Christ? If Jesus is just another man, and not God, Christianity is a dead belief system, for the founder is a liar Here is one last try to get them to open their eyes to the prophecy of Psalm 110:1 And don t miss out on the patience in the teaching ministry of Jesus David did not refer to the Messiah as his Son, but rather reveals the words of the Father Himself ( The LORD, Ps 110:1 = Jehovah or Yahweh ), who speaks to Messiah, His Son and David s Lord ( my Lord, Ps 110:1 = 'Adonay ). It was taught in Scripture that Messiah would be the son of David, and yet David himself refers to Messiah as his Lord. How can this be? The Incarnation DIVINE ANSWER Psalm 110 is the most quoted or referenced psalm in the NT 110:1 is directly quoted 5x Matthew 22:44 ; Mark 12:36 ; Luke 20:42 ; Acts 2:34 ; Hebrews 1:13 ; Alluded to 6x Matthew 26:64 ; Mark 14:62 ; Luke 22:69 ; Ephesians 1:20 ; Colossians 3:1 ; and Hebrews 8:1 . 110:4 is mentioned another 6x Hebrews 5:6 ; 7:17 ; Hebrews 5:10 ; 6:20 ; and 7:11 , 15 Peter quoted the psalm to Jewish hearers: Acts 2:34-36 Mark adds, David himself said in the Holy Spirit These were not just David s words, but words given to him Jehovah says to my Lord; the Self-existent One says to the Sovereign One Occupy the place of honor Enemies will be conquered David recognizes the Messiah as his Lord He recognizes Him as the Sovereign of the OT; His Deity The Discerning Question: how can Messiah be both Deity and human? The problem was a puzzle to the people but a simple solution was right there in their Scriptures The Incarnation is the Answer; it has always been there but ignored whether through ignorance, stumbling block, or laziness of study They knew the text but denied it s meaning. John 5:18 on of the charges brought before Jesus was His claim to Deity He called God His Father, making Himself equal with God There were times Jesus had to dampen the enthusiasm of the people, He never denied who He is. We have the Trinity in Psalm 110 Spirit provided the Revelation; Jehovah; and Adonay THE TEACHER TEACHES LUKE 20:41-44 From Judaism 101 But above all, he will be a human being, not a god, demi-god or other supernatural being. Jesus did not do any of the things that the scriptures said the messiah would do DISCERNING QUESTION Psalm 110:1 The verse clearly teaches that if He is the Messiah, He is Divine truly God and truly man DEFICIENT ANSWER The human qualification of Jesus is readily recognized by all the people 19:38 Jesus distinctly claims divinity for Himself Jesus was aware of the charge that would be brought against Him John 5:18 John 10:33 Luke 22:71 If Jesus is just another man, and not God, Christianity is a dead belief system, for the founder is a liar David did not refer to the Messiah as his Son, but rather reveals the words of the Father Himself DIVINE ANSWER Psalm 110 is the most quoted or referenced psalm in the NT The Self-existent One says to the Sovereign One The Incarnation is the Answer ============================================= === SERMON: message(175).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/12/2025 6:37:24 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2 INTRODUCTION Rather than look into the gospels for lessons from the nativity, I decided to jump to Philippians and look at a passage that is seen as the greatest Christological passage in the New Testament that sounds the depths of the incarnation. Among scholars it is known as the Kenosis passage, speaking of the self-emptying of the Son of God as He became incarnate in humanity. - Wuest Paul uses the Incarnation as the Example for his Exhortation to humilty Gromacki: "Paradoxically, he illustrated exhortation with doctrine, whereas most preachers try to make their doctrinal sermons practical." We are given the mind of Christ and then told what to do with it If you want to know what humility looks like, here you go Here is the illustration of voluntary humiliation The passage combines Christian practice with Christian doctrine Let this mind may be interpreted by some as a suggestion or optional Grammatically, it is a command This be ye constantly thinking in you which also was in Christ Jesus Exercise the mind, direct one s mind to a thing This is the key to unlocking the doctrinal portion of the letter As a command, you choose Life Application Bible: You can choose your attitude. You can approach life expecting to be served, or you can look for opportunities to serve others. May the mind of Christ, my Savior, Live in me from day to day, By His love and power controlling All I do and say. This is a text that requires the renewing of the mind My flesh naturally gravitates toward selfishness and pride I will not achieve Christ s example of humility on my own O to grace how great a debtor daily I m constrained to be! Let Thy goodness, like a fetter, bind my wandering heart to Thee. Prone to wander, Lord, I feel it, prone to leave the God I love. Here s my heart. O take and seal it. Seal it for Thy courts above God has not left us alone to try and figure this out on our own Through His grace I now have the desire and power to carry out God s commands Philippians 2:12, 13 God is at work We must stop being conformed to this world s mold 1 Corinthians 2:16 we have the mind of Christ 1 John 2:6 The one who says he abides in Him ought himself to walk in the same manner as He walked 2 Corinthians 3:18 But we all, with unveiled face beholding as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit. Do you have the mind of Christ? How can you know? Romans 8:9 We must bear a resemblance to Christ s life if we are to benefit from His death A T Robertson comments that "Paul presents Jesus as the supreme example of humility. He had no tears for His own grief, but sweat drops of blood for mine The temptation is to jump right in to verse 5, but let s lay a foundation THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Paul is concerned about unity in the church How the church can stand and strive against outside pressure There are NO doctrinal issues at stake here; more personal preferences Mentioned in 1:27; hence the therefore in 2:1 The threat is external; 1:28 His focus is internal unity and the attitudes and actions they are to practice in order to counter the tendency for divisions in the body Edwards: The Earthly Conduct of Heavenly Citizens The Lowly Mind is Selfless 2:1,2 The appeal is to selflessness Look at 4 terms: Encouragement which is in Christ Comfort which comes from divine love Oneness through the Holy Spirit Tender mercies in believers This unity is not the result of natural oneness but supernatural bonding. Basically: don t let selfishness sneak in and destroy your oneness Living Bible ( 2:1-4 ) "Is there any such thing as Christians cheering each other up? Do you love me enough to want to help me? Does it mean anything to you that we are brothers in the Lord, sharing the same Spirit? Are your hearts tender and sympathetic at all? Then make me truly happy by loving each other and agreeing wholeheartedly with each other, working together with one heart and mind and purpose." Swindoll: What a wonderful way to live one s life! That one heart and mind and purpose suggests unity, a genuine Spirit-filled unselfishness that breeds strength and spreads cheer. Paul is not calling for uniformity; that is pressure from without Dress alike, look alike, sound alike, think alike Unity is spirit energized; cooperation for same objectives And don t get excited when you see the word if ; 4x in verse 1 It does not imply these things do not exist, but since they exist, in view of the fact The if s state the source of the resource Adequate Spiritual Resources + Appropriate Spirit enabled Response = Unity Unity was the prayer of Jesus: John 17:11, 21-23 To be like-minded requires me to be lowly-minded after the pattern of the Lord Jesus A quick look at your resources Encouragement in Christ: 3:9 the blessing of being found in Christ 3:10 the blessing of knowing Christ 1:29 the gift of faith In the midst of trial and suffering, find encouragement in your relationship with Jesus. Barnabas was the Son of Encouragement Comfort of Love He is ours; we are His We love because He loves us Fellowship of the Spirit 1:5 1:17 unites us in the family of God 3:3 directly impacts our worship Fellowship: we have things in common How can we have anything in common with the Holy Spirit? 2 Peter 1:4 we are partakers of the divine nature partaker = fellowship Shared affection and mercy Items which flow from Christ Tenderness and compassion How can this work practically? They say it is almost impossible to tune two pianos to each other. But if you use a tuning fork to tune each piano, they will be in tune with each other If you are in tune with Jesus and I am in tune with Jesus, we will be in tune with each other When we are seeking God together, unity will happen When the Word of God is the common standard, unity will result The Lowly Mind Serves Others 2:3, 4 Do nothing No self-promotion, no competitive spirit D. L. Moody said, Selfishness is tearing others down and vain conceit is building ourselves up. The best way to forget other people s faults is to remember your own Humility you might say, that s for weasels, I m an eagle! Then this quotes for you { Eagles may soar, but weasels aren t sucked into jet engines! } Lowliness of mind tapeinophros ne, tap-i-nof-ros-oo'-nay Does not mean we fail to recognize our talents You may well be more educated, more skilled, smarter than someone else The NASB translates this, Do nothing from selfishness or empty conceit, but with humility of mind let each one regard one another as more important than himself. It is very difficult to say, That person is better than me when he may not be in various ways, but we can say, That person s interests are more important than mine. Humility is realizing that God and others are responsible for the achievements in my life. Look not 2:4 not to be quoted when taking a test John Phillips - To seek one's own advancement is worldly. To seek the prosperity, good, and promotion of others is divine. Abraham said, We are brethren. You choose which area you will settle. Lot was looking out for himself when he chose the well-watered plains And lost his family, fortune, and almost his faith "During my second month of nursing school, our professor gave us a pop quiz. I was a conscientious student and had breezed through the questions, until I read the last one: 'What is the first name of the woman who cleans the school?" Surely this was some kind of joke. I had seen the cleaning woman several times. She was tall, dark-haired and in her 50's, but how would I know her name? I handed in my paper, leaving the last question blank. Before class ended, one student asked if the last question would count toward our quiz grade. Absolutely, said the professor. "In your careers you will meet many people. All are significant. They deserve your attention and care, even if all you do is smile and say hello". I've never forgotten that lesson. I also learned her name was Dorothy." Do you have a Dorothy in your life that needs attention? KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2 Here is the illustration of voluntary humiliation My flesh naturally gravitates toward selfishness and pride THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Paul is concerned about unity in the church The Lowly Mind is Selfless Unity is spirit energized To be like-minded requires me to be lowly-minded after the pattern of the Lord Jesus When we are seeking God together, unity will happen The Lowly Mind Serves Others 2:3, 4 The best way to forget other people s faults is to remember your own That person s interests are more important than mine. KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2 Here is the illustration of _________________ humiliation My flesh __________________ gravitates toward selfishness and pride THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Paul is concerned about ____________ in the church The Lowly Mind is ______________ Unity is ______________ energized To be like-minded _________________ me to be lowly-minded after the pattern of the Lord Jesus When we are _________________ God together, unity will happen The Lowly Mind _________________ Others 2:3, 4 The best way to forget other people s faults is to _________________ your own That person s interests are more ___________________ than mine. ============================================= === SERMON: message(176).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/19/2025 8:04:50 PM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: message KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 INTRODUCTION We appreciate the number of you who took time to watch the message from last week. I looked at the stream on Monday, and it said there had been 65 views. I asked Ruth where all those people came from. I was expecting it to say 12 15. Of course, Jon popped my bubble by saying people could have pulled it up multiple times. They got bored and returned later hoping it improved. So, for any of you who took 3 or 4 times to work your way through it, thanks. It's the most wonderful time of the year; liberals are high jacking Christmas for their own political gain Emmy Griffin: cheapening the Nativity story to forward a political agenda through emotional and spiritual manipulation. In Massachusetts, a Catholic church, St. Susanna, removed baby Jesus from His rightful place at the center of the Nativity display, replacing Him with a sign stating, ICE was here. Another sign added, The Holy Family is safe in The Sanctuary of our Church. Outside of Chicago, Lake Street Church of Evanston s Nativity display showed baby Jesus cuffed with zip ties and wrapped in an emergency blanket, while Mary and Joseph were clad in gas masks as if they were protesters anticipating getting tear-gassed during an anti-deportation protest. It s the most wonderful time for music. I love most of the songs of the season. But one has become a little troubling. All I want for Christmas is you . Sounds lovely, doesn t it? All her hopes are wrapped up in one fellow. But how does the song begin: I don t want a lot for Christmas. Some poor bloke is a gift for a girl not looking for much! On the flip side, the girl has set the bar pretty low, so he is off the hook. After I began studying Philippians 2, I looked in my file for some old notes to see what was there and was somewhat amazed at the absence of much content. How could I have been preaching for 37 years without doing a serious study of Philippians 2? It looks like I may have to postpone retirement because there is so much, I have not covered yet. As mentioned last week, Philippians 2 is seen as the greatest Christological passage in the New Testament that sounds the depths of the incarnation. Among scholars it is known as the Kenosis passage, speaking of the self-emptying of the Son of God as He became incarnate in humanity. - Wuest Alva McClain made this observation: Nothing beyond a cursory review of the astonishingly numerous interpretations of this Philippian passage is enough, as someone has suggested, to afflict the student with intellectual paralysis. As we are soon to celebrate Incarnation Day, Philippians 2 adds some Theology to our understanding of the narrative provided in the gospels. There is so much in the Old and New Testaments outside of the synoptic gospels that add to the story. God is God and He can do anything He wills. It is one thing to become made in the image of man and quite another to die. It is a wonderful thing to heal the sick, restore sight to the blind, and raise the dead. People expect miracles from God. The stumbling block comes when those same people are confronted with the why of Jesus coming. I have come to seek and save that which is lost. Many in Jesus day rejected His evaluation of their spiritual condition and it has not changed much today. And it doesn t matter what part of the world you visit. The heart of man is deceitful above all things. Many suppress the truth in unrighteousness and worship the creature more than the creator. All have sinned and come short of the glory of God And the worst thing this preacher could do, is to overlook spiritual needs of those sitting before him. I know you have a testimony of salvation. But the exhortation from the apostle Paul is, examine yourselves to see if you be in the faith. Your salvation testimony can be simply based on some prayer you made sometime in the past. What did salvation look like in your life this week? To use the text from last week: were you Selfless and were you Serving? Our Incarnation study is about the voluntary humiliation of Jesus We are looking at the Lowliness of Mind we are to have because that is what is required of us as we let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus. We have examined the Definition of a Lowly Mind This week is the Illustration of a Lowly Mind And we will conclude the section looking at the Exultation of the Lowly Mind Belief is not just an intellectual exercise of a statement of fact It is our personal trust and confidence in Him of whom the fact is true. F. B. Meyer: We are not saved merely because we believe that Jesus Christ died for us on the Cross, but because we trust in Him who died. It is the personal touch between Christ and us that causes His life to pass into our nature, making us sound and healthy, as well as secure and safe. In each of us there is a strong self-will Strong enough to think I can be good and give up bad habits But it will never happen that way It is only when I see myself standing before the Cross that I understand my inability to change and bring an end to myself For it is at the cross where I can enter into and live His eternal life It is at the cross where I see my pride and sin nailed to One who entirely yielded up His own will It is seeing His strength that breaks my pride It is only when we are weak that we are really strong THE ILLUSTRATION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:5-8 Kenosis comes from Philippians 2:7 of no reputation (KJV) BY DEFINTION The Greek word ?e??? emptied , - used by most translators to abase, of none effect, void 5x Romans 4:14; 1 Cor. 1:17 Root word: kenos 16x Luke 1:53; 20:10, 11; Acts 4:25; 1 Cor. 15:10 NIV: made himself nothing NLT: gave up his divine privileges AMPLIFIED: but emptied Himself (without renouncing or diminishing His deity, but only temporarily giving up the outward expression of divine equality and His rightful dignity) No where in Philippians does it say what He emptied Himself from We must be careful not to go beyond what the Scriptures say The verse says nothing about yielding attributes and it is obvious from the gospels that Jesus possessed the wisdom and power of God Whatever emptiness entails, Jesus remained truly God He did not cease to be God; He did not become a lesser god Colossians 2:9 For in him dwells all the fulness of the Godhead bodily Here is where the NLT shines: gave up His divine privileges Jesus laid aside the divine privileges He had in heaven; He veiled Himself thought it not robbery to be equal with God thought equality with God not a thing to seize ??p??? harp zo to seize, to catch away, pull, to take by force (robber) The kenosis was a self-renunciation, not an emptying Himself of deity lowliness of mind selfless, serving; considered the other person s needs more important than His Neither was it an exchange: Jesus never ceased to be God during His earthly ministry He also voluntarily refrained from using His divinity to make His way easier. He always did the will of His Father John 5:19 Jesus sometimes operated within the limitations of humanity He was tired; John 4:6; 19:28 At least on one occasion, He surrendered the use of omniscience; Matt. 24:36 Other times, His omniscience was on full display: Luke 6:8; John 13:11; 18:4 BY OPPOSITION What kenosis is not Like so many other areas, there is some false teaching by taking kenosis too far this heresy is sometimes referred to as the kenosis theory, but a better term is kenoticism or kenotic theology, to distinguish it from biblical understanding of the kenosis. It is easy to see how the terms could be confused Kenotic theology teaches that the divinity of the Son of God was somehow lessened or altered, when the Lord took on human flesh and entered our world. The thought is that Jesus actually laid aside some of His divinity in order to be more like human beings According to this teaching, Jesus laid aside His divine attributes Problems with Kenoticism Emptying Himself of any divinity would make Jesus less than truly God God cannot stop being God even for a moment Also, a problem would arise for those who follow Christ No mere human being could fulfill the role of Savior The sacrifice of a mere human would not be sufficient to pay the price of sin the efficacy of His sacrifice on the cross would be nullified BY REVELATION The danger is to focus too much on what He gave up The Kenosis also deals with what Jesus took on Jesus added to His divine nature a human nature as He humbled Himself Jesus went from being the glory of glories in heaven to being a human being who was put to death on the cross. 2:7, 8 Kenosis is Jesus taking on a human nature with all of its limitations but without sin The union of the two natures is called the Hypostatic union (essential nature) Jesus is truly God and truly man with the divine nature coexisting with the human nature without confusion or separation. Kenosis is Christ laying aside the privileges of divinity He chose to leave a position of infinite honor, glory and adoration He made Himself nothing ; He chose to occupy the position of a slave Jesus never ceased to be God; He did not exchange divinity for humanity Miracles were not done to benefit Himself but for the good of others From our college Theology Survey class: Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes When He healed the sick, walked on water, fed the 5,000, and raised Lazarus from the dead, Jesus divine nature was fully evident. When He allowed Himself to be hungry, thirsty, beaten, abused, and crucified without retaliating, He was intentionally restricting His divine power. He did not give up His power; rather, He chose to subjugate it for a greater good. But at no time during His life was Christ ever without the fullness of divinity First, He emptied Himself of His pristine position in relation to the Law. Although personally without sin, He willingly took the sins of the people He loves Second, He emptied Himself of His rightful ownership of everything. Jesus borrowed a place to be born Borrowed homes to sleep; No place to lay His head, used boats from which to preach, borrowed an animal to ride into Jerusalem, used another s upper room for the Passover, and even a tomb to be buried in Third, He emptied Himself of His heavenly glory that He shared with the Father John 17:4, 5 Last, Jesus emptied Himself of His divine knowledge Matthew 24:36 no knowledge of when He returns John 8:26 John 8:28 John 15:15 Jesus did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped Jesus was Selfless and Served John 13:14, 15; 2 Cor. 8:9 How are we to respond? Vs. 1-5 describes a mindset we should adopt A mindset which was also in Christ Jesus Demonstrate humility, lowliness of mind and the same sacrificial mindset Jesus provides an example of how we are to live Complete dependence upon God for everything, including the wisdom and knowledge we need We can set aside our supposed privileges in order to minister to others Jesus did not cling to His heavenly throne, why should we hang on to earthly treasures? Can we empty ourselves as Jesus did? He urges humility on the Philippians as the only way to secure unity." CONCLUSION A former missionary told the story of two rugged, powerful mountain goats who met on a narrow pathway joining two mountain ridges. On one side was a chasm 1,000 feet deep; on the other, a steep cliff rising straight up. So narrow was the trail that there was no room to turn around, and the goats could not back up without falling. What would they do? Finally, instead of fighting for the right to pass, one of the goats knelt down and made himself as flat as possible. The other goat then walked over him, and they both proceeded safely. In a sense, this is what Jesus Christ did for us when He left heaven's glory and came to this earth to die for our sins. He saw us trapped between our sin and God's righteousness with no way to help ourselves. He humbled Himself by giving up His right to use His divine power. He came in the likeness of men and took the form of a servant (Phil. 2:5-8 ). Then, by dying for sinful mankind, He let us "walk over Him" so that we could experience forgiveness and receive eternal life. D C Egner KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 Our Incarnation study is about the voluntary humiliation of Jesus For it is at the cross where I can enter into and live His eternal life THE ILLUSTRATION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:5-8 BY DEFINTION ?e??? - emptied , of none effect No where in Philippians does it say what He emptied Himself from NLT: gave up His divine privileges Jesus never ceased to be God during His earthly ministry Jesus sometimes operated within the limitations of humanity John 4:6; 19:28 BY OPPOSITION What kenosis is not kenotic theology: the divinity of the Son of God was somehow lessened or altered Problems with Kenoticism God cannot stop being God even for a moment No mere human being could fulfill the role of Savior BY REVELATION Kenosis also deals with what Jesus took on The union of the two natures is called the Hypostatic union Kenosis is Christ laying aside the privileges of divinity Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes He emptied Himself of His pristine position in relation to the Law He emptied Himself of His rightful ownership of everything He emptied Himself of His heavenly glory that He shared with the Father John 17:4, 5 Jesus emptied Himself of His divine knowledge ============================================= === SERMON: message(177).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 12/26/2025 8:55:34 PM To: Subject: message KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 INTRODUCTION In Philippians 2, Paul is exhorting the folks to work at church unity THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 We saw where the Lowly Mind was Selfless and Serving The Lowly Mind acknowledges that other people are more important The Lowly Mind is not about ignoring your own needs but also taking time to note the needs of others And then Paul uses Jesus as the perfect Illustration of a Lowly Mind Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus We are looking at the Kenosis of the Lord Jesus Exploring the text referred to causing intellectual paralysis Some bullet points from last week: Kenosis means emptying Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes. At no time was Jesus ever less than truly God. The humiliation of Jesus was voluntarily self-imposed. The kenosis not only consisted the act of self-emptying, but involved a continuous self-renunciation, which began with Jesus becoming man and culminated in His death on the cross. He left the Splendor of Heaven knowing His destiny Was the lonely hill of Golgotha, there to lay down His life for me. If that isn't love, the ocean is dry, There's no stars in the sky and the sparrow can't fly; If that isn't love, then Heavens a myth There's no feeling like this, if that isn't love We are going to look more into Jesus becoming man today Next Sunday, we will look at His death on the cross THE ASSUMPTION OF SERVANTHOOD 2:7 The Method of Self-Emptying The form of a slave taking (aapnms) The dramatic act of the incarnation is described in the word empty Yielding over the independent exercise of His divine attributes It did not change the person and attributes of Christ in His divine nature, but added to it a complete human nature Christ voluntarily submitted moment by moment to the human limitations apart from sin The humiliation was temporary; the incarnation was everlasting Jesus became a man and not just a typical man Jesus became the lowest position a person could become in the Roman world The more you think of this, the more staggering it becomes Jesus went as low as you can possibly go You will never possess a level lower than Jesus He can identify with you in any situation: poverty, loneliness, homelessness, rejection The human nature of Jesus was exactly like ours; without sin There was nothing about His appearance to suggest He was a god or a super man He was undiminished deity and perfect humanity; truly God and truly man To deny or limit either aspect is a move into serious heresy Taking does not imply an exchange but addition form is the same as in v. 6; it refers to the nature or character of something It is both internal and external Form indicates character, kind, and quality, rather than a specific slave It refers to the outward display of the inner reality or the essential form of something which never alters. He Who was the Sovereign manifested Himself as a lowly bondservant. As a bondslave, Jesus surrendered His rights to the will of the Father Christ Jesus changed His mode of expression from that of the glory of Deity to that of the humiliation of a bondservant, and in doing that, He set His legitimate desire of being glorified aside, thus setting self aside to express Himself as a bondservant, receiving instead of the worship of the angels, the curses and hatred of mankind. When He kneeled on the floor to wash the disciples feet, He was still the Lord of glory although He looked like a bondservant. The desire to serve was part of both natures A rule of Greek grammar says that the action of an aorist participle precedes the action of the leading verb. The leading verb here is emptied. That means that the act of taking preceded the act of emptying. That in turn means that the act of taking upon Himself the form of a servant preceded and was the cause of the emptying. The translation so far could read, emptied Himself, having taken the form of a bondslave. - Wuest So, Jesus exchanges one form of expression God , for another form of expression bondslave This is the direct opposite of what took place at the transfiguration And the mode of His outward expression was changed before them, and His face did shine as the sun, and His raiment was white as the light (Matt. 17:2 ) In Philippians, the outward expression is that of a servant Christ laid aside the form of God The Medium of Self-Emptying was made in the likeness of man Jesus entered into a new state of being when He became man And always keep in mind, at no time did His becoming man exclude His Deity His likeness was real, not a phantom, not an incomplete copy His mode of manifestation resembled what men are Was made in likeness not exactness; He was without sin Sinfulness is not a necessary characteristic of humanity, though it happens to be a universal characteristic. Jesus was neither a divine man nor a human god G. Campbell Morgan: The Word passed from government to obedience, from independent cooperation in the equality of deity, to dependent submission to the will of God. A servant is known for obedience Romans 6:16 Isaiah 42:1 Ephesians 6:6 1 Thess. 1:9 Revelation 22:3 Made in the likeness of man in likeness of man being made (ampnms) The Creator is the creature; The Son of God is the Son of Man The Infinite is joined to the infant The Omnipotent is joined to the newborn babe (Spurgeon) Jesus never used His power to make His life easier All the miracles He did was for the benefit of others The power was there; Matthew 26:53 Tony Evans: The reason is that Jesus had to experience every pain and temptation we face (Hebrews 4:15 ), so He could reverse the first Adam s failure and win the spiritual battle Adam lost for mankind in Eden. Jesus lived out the will of God on earth, that He might be an acceptable substitute for man. Hebrews 10:5 really underscores the nature of Christ s self-emptying. Speaking of Christ, the author said, When He comes into the world, He says, Sacrifice and offering Thou hast not desired, but a body Thou hast prepared for Me. When Jesus Christ emptied Himself, what He did was pour His deity into a container called a human body. This is what we call the Incarnation, Jesus taking on human flesh. The second person of the Trinity was encased in a body that God prepared for Him through the Virgin Birth, when the Holy Spirit conceived a baby in Mary s womb. Notice again that Jesus did the emptying Himself. It was voluntary. He didn t have to do it, but He did it to save people like you and me, just because He loved us! We need to meditate on that regularly, because it will give us an attitude of gratitude, love, and humility the attitude of Jesus! (Who is this King of Glory) Hebrews 2:14, 15 (Chalcedonian Creed) 451 A.D. "We, then, following the holy Fathers, all with one consent, teach men to confess one and the same Son, our Lord Jesus Christ, the same perfect in Godhead and also perfect in manhood; truly God and truly man, of a reasonable soul and body; consubstantial with the Father according to the Godhead, and consubstantial with us according to the Manhood; in all things like unto us, without sin; begotten before all ages of the Father according to the Godhead, and in these latter days, for us and for our salvation, born of the Virgin Mary, the Mother of God, according to the Manhood; one and the same Christ, Son, Lord, Only-begotten, to be acknowledged in two natures, inconfusedly, unchangeably, indivisibly, inseparably; the distinction of natures being by no means taken away by the union, but rather the property of each nature being preserved, and concurring in one Person and one Subsistence, not parted or divided into two persons, but one and the same Son, and only begotten, God the Word, the Lord Jesus Christ, as the prophets from the beginning have declared concerning him, and the Lord Jesus Christ himself has taught us, and the Creed of the holy Fathers has handed down to us. He is the Word made flesh in order that men might see and know the true God John 14:8 He is the Word made flesh in order that the world might know what life in its essential perfection truly is. He is the Word made flesh in order to die for sinners and to share His own eternal life with men. He is the Word made flesh in order that He might be a merciful and faithful High Priest. Hebrews 2:18 Dr. McClain: We must at last fall down upon our knees before the mystery of God in Christ and confess humbly that we cannot explain Him. But thank God that we know Him. KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Selfless and Serving Kenosis Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes. At no time was Jesus ever less than truly God. The humiliation of Jesus was voluntarily self-imposed. THE ASSUMPTION OF SERVANTHOOD 2:7 The Method of Self-Emptying The form of a slave You will never possess a level lower than Jesus Form indicates character, kind, and quality The Medium of Self-Emptying Jesus entered into a new state of being Jesus was neither a divine man nor a human god A servant is known for obedience Jesus never used His power to make His life easier He is the Word made flesh in order that men might see and know the true God He is the Word made flesh in order that the world might know what life in its essential perfection truly is. He is the Word made flesh in order to die for sinners and to share His own eternal life with men. He is the Word made flesh in order that He might be a merciful and faithful High Priest. ============================================= === SERMON: message(178).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/2/2026 11:46:50 AM To: Subject: message KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 INTRODUCTION This series of message from Philippians 2 has not gone as planned. One was presented to an empty auditorium and I had to miss last week. The original plan was to share verse 7 last week, verse 8 this week, and finish 9-11 next week. So, I am going to take some thoughts from my notes from verse 7 and add them to verse 8 as we prepare to share the Lord s Supper together. And in the interest of full disclosure, I did have a message prepared last week. Our topic is Kenosis or the Self-Emptying of Jesus Christ and His Incarnation We are not looking at the birth narrative as provide by in the gospels, but an illustration that Paul uses in his plea for unity in the church at Philippi. Few illustrations take on a life of their own like this one. Few illustrations are the subject of volumes of material exploring the intricacies of the topic. We will never exhaust the depths of Philippians 2. It reminds me of Paul s statement in Romans 11 after the salvation of Israel, O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and the knowledge of God! how unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past finding out! We are entering an arena beyond our understanding. We are engage with a text where we trust, belief the God of the Word. Dr. McClain: We must at last fall down upon our knees before the mystery of God in Christ and confess humbly that we cannot explain Him. But thank God that we know Him. THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Selfless and Serving Kenosis Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes. At no time was Jesus ever less than truly God. The humiliation of Jesus was voluntarily self-imposed. THE ILLUSTRATION OF A LOWLY MIND THE ASSUMPTION OF SERVANTHOOD 2:7 The Method of Self-Emptying The form of a slave You will never possess a level lower than Jesus Form indicates character, kind, and quality The Medium of Self-Emptying Jesus entered into a new state of being Jesus was neither a divine man nor a human god A servant is known for obedience THE MODEL FOR OBEDIENCE 2:7, 8 The Condition for Obedience Barclay: And when he came in appearance as a man for all to recognise, he became obedient even to the extent of accepting death, even the death of a cross. Wuest: And being found to be in outward guise as man, He stooped very low, having become obedient to the extent of death, even such a death as that upon a cross. 3 Key Words Form - Morphe: 2:6 ??f? morph? 3x (Mark 16:12) form of God; 2:7 form of a servant refers to possessing the essential attributes which belong to the essence or nature It refers to the essential form of something which never alters. Truly God and truly man taking does not imply an exchange but addition to A rule of Greek grammar says that the action of an aorist participle precedes the action of the leading verb. The leading verb here is emptied. That means that the act of taking preceded the act of emptying. He emptied Himself after having taken the form of a servant Likeness 2:7 ? ??? a homo oma; 6x implies a true human likeness Not a mere phantom as some Gnostics suggested Fashion: 2:8 s?? a schema; 2x (1 Cor. 7:31 fashion of this world ) appearance; His outward appearance was definitely that of a man came in appearance of man for all to recognize Jesus never used His power to make His life easier Tony Evans: The reason is that Jesus had to experience every pain and temptation we face (Hebrews 4:15 ), so He could reverse the first Adam s failure and win the spiritual battle Adam lost for mankind in Eden. Jesus lived out the will of God on earth, that He might be an acceptable substitute for man. Hebrews 10:5 really underscores the nature of Christ s self-emptying. Speaking of Christ, the author said, When He comes into the world, He says, Sacrifice and offering Thou hast not desired, but a body Thou hast prepared for Me. When Jesus Christ emptied Himself, what He did was pour His deity into a container called a human body. This is what we call the Incarnation, Jesus taking on human flesh. The second person of the Trinity was encased in a body that God prepared for Him through the Virgin Birth, when the Holy Spirit conceived a baby in Mary s womb. Notice again that Jesus did the emptying Himself. It was voluntary. He didn t have to do it, but He did it to save people like you and me, just because He loved us! We need to meditate on that regularly, because it will give us an attitude of gratitude, love, and humility the attitude of Jesus! (Who is this King of Glory) Hebrews 2:14, 15 The Course of Obedience He humbled Himself Obedience carried to its limit Humbled: abase, bring low same word in v. 3 Lowliness of mind Not obedience through coercion, or brow beating, or yielding to a yoke All voluntary choice; prompted by love Galatians 2:20 McClain: He assumed servanthood and died on the cross for us, not because of any compulsion external to Himself, but according to His free and loving choice of His own will. Obedient: literally to hear under Under: descriptive of His humble state of slavehood Hear: His obedient attentiveness to His Father s will It is impossible for us to realize the tremendous descent of our Lord s humiliation Christ not only lived a human life, He died a human death From the throne of God to the grave of man He repelled any suggestion to alter His purpose for coming John 8:14; 12:27 for this cause came I to this hour He had the power to lay down His life; no man could take it from Him Lawlor: with deliberate consideration, with full knowledge of all it would mean for Him, our Lord yielded Himself willingly to death. The Climax of Obedience death of the cross No common ordinary death He died our death; He entered into our doom He entered into the darkness alone; He was forsaken by the Father A death of intense pain, but extreme degradation It was torture in public view Galatians 3:13 The demands of the Law were satisfied in Him As the sin bearer, God s wrath was poured out upon Him We were under the curse, Christ took the curse upon Himself, so that when divine judgment fell it fell on Him instead of us. This is God s justice. Sin is never ignored. Sin is never glossed over. Payment had to be made. Sin deserves retribution God would have acted in flat contradiction to His nature had He glossed over the sin of mankind. The great and glorious proclamation of the gospel is Christ has purchased our freedom Galatians 3:13 redeemed; it is an historical fact Because of Christ the curse will never fall on us That is the purpose of the Incarnation Christ came into the world to save sinners; 1 Timothy 1:15 2 Cor. 5:19 Col. 1:22, 23 Christ did not come as an influencer ; an example of how to live He did not come to be a martyr, or a great person of humanity An influencer will not lead you into paths of holiness The daily life of godliness comes only after the atoning sacrifice has been made for our sins. The suffering of Christ was not simply to reveal the love of God, but also to satisfy His justice. He is the Word made flesh in order that men might see and know the true God John 14:8 He is the Word made flesh in order that the world might know what life in its essential perfection truly is. He is the Word made flesh in order to die for sinners and to share His own eternal life with men. He is the Word made flesh in order that He might be a merciful and faithful High Priest. Hebrews 2:18 CONCLUSION This is an illustration of Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus This humble mind is in v. 3 in lowliness of mind each counting other better than himself I surely wish I could say follow my example This is hard. Jesus is the perfect example of considering others more important than you The Lord s table is our regular reminder of God demonstrating His love toward us The Table is a reminder of God s righteousness and justice The Table heightens our awareness of God s holiness and our sin KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 THE DEFINITION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:1-4 Selfless and Serving Jesus surrendered the independent exercise of His divine attributes. THE ILLUSTRATION OF A LOWLY MIND 2:5-8 THE ASSUMPTION OF SERVANTHOOD 2:7 The Method of Self-Emptying You will never possess a level lower than Jesus The Medium of Self-Emptying Jesus was neither a divine man nor a human god THE MODEL FOR OBEDIENCE 2:7, 8 The Condition for Obedience Form morphe; the essential form of something which never alters Likeness 2:7 implies a true human likeness Fashion: 2:8 schema; appearance The Course of Obedience Humbled: abase, bring low All voluntary choice; prompted by love Obedient: to hear under It is impossible for us to realize the tremendous descent of our Lord s humiliation The Climax of Obedience 2:8 He died our death Galatians 3:13 This is God s justice. Sin is never ignored Because of Christ the curse will never fall on us 1 Timothy 1:15 2 Corinthians 5:19 Colossians 1:22, 23 He is the Word made flesh in order that men might see and know the true God He is the Word made flesh in order that the world might know what life in its essential perfection truly is. He is the Word made flesh in order to die for sinners and to share His own eternal life with men. He is the Word made flesh in order that He might be a merciful and faithful High Priest. ============================================= === SERMON: message(179).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/8/2026 11:07:27 AM To: "Jonathan Hill" Subject: MESSAGE KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 INTRODUCTION Kenosis: the self-emptying of Jesus; the Incarnation Jesus voluntarily took on human flesh to die for the sins of mankind A death sufficient that all who come to Him by faith in His substitutionary death will receive forgiveness and everlasting life. Paul s interest in his letter to Philippi is the requirements for Unity in the Local Church The Definition of the Lowly Mind: 2:1-4; Selfless, Serving The Illustration of the Lowly Mind: 2:5-8; Servanthood, Obedience 2:9 Wuest: Because of which voluntary act of supreme self-renunciation, God also super-eminently exalted Him to the highest rank and power, and graciously bestowed upon Him THE NAME, the one which is above every name, therefore - To show the cause and effect of His self-renunciation and His exultation There was the cradle, then the cross, then the crown As Jesus was declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead; (Romans 1:4) His exultation was God s final stamp of approval on His earthly ministry Acts 2:33-36 Being therefore exalted by the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this, which you now see and hear. For David didn't ascend into the heavens, but he says himself, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit by my right hand, Until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."' "Let all the house of Israel therefore know assuredly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified." Spurgeon: He suffered and He reigned; He stooped to conquer, and He conquered for He stooped, and was exalted for He conquered. [[[psalm 45; 72 Paul is presenting the divine paradox, foolish to the natural man, that the way up is down. That a cross precedes a crown. That the road of exaltation by the Father is paved by humble service to others for the Father's glory. It runs counter to the pursuit of Satan: his ascent began his descent James 4:10 humble yourself in the sight of God and He will exalt you Proverbs 15:33 before honor is humility THE DEFINITION OF THE LOWLY MIND THE ILLUSTRATION OF THE LOWLY MIND THE EXALTATION OF THE LOWLY MIND EXALTED POSITION 2:9 Exalt to the highest rank; raise to supreme majesty The highest exaltation possible; in a class by Himself A T Robertson discussing the phrase "God highly exalted Him" writes that... Because of Christ s voluntary humiliation God lifted Him above or beyond (huper) the state of glory which He enjoyed before the Incarnation. What glory did Christ have after the Ascension that He did not have before in heaven? What did He take back to heaven that He did not bring? His humanity. He returned to heaven the Son of Man as well as the Son of God Why is that significant? It means that Jesus Christ still bears the scars of His crucifixion in His hands, side and feet An eternal testimony to the New Covenant A testimony of once saved, always, saved; Jesus will never break the Covenant It is the testimony of eternal security Revelation 5:6-12 (6, 9, 12) 5:6 perfect tense: has been slain; done in the past with continual results It has been said, the only man-made thing in heaven will be the scars of the Savior. When my lifework is ended, and I cross the swelling tide, When the bright and glorious morning I shall see; I shall know my Redeemer when I reach the other side, And His smile will be the first to welcome me. * Refrain: I shall know Him, I shall know Him, And redeemed by His side I shall stand, I shall know Him, I shall know Him, By the print of the nails in His hand. EXALTED NAME Hebrews 1:3, 4 His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself made purification for our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high; having become so much better than the angels, as he has inherited a more excellent name than they have. gave to Him a name freely and generously; gift of grace You may ask, What s in a name? plenty Jesus, name above all names There s just something about that name The name is profanity to the sinner, but heaven s password to the saint Name by which one is known; not just a title but speaks of the whole person The name of the Lord is what He is Some translations say the name (Wescott/Hort) Name: denoting office, rank, dignity The Name of God in the Old Testament, denotes the divine Presence, the divine Majesty, especially as the object of adoration and praise John 17:5 Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed. 4 Things about the Name Transcendent: Above every name No other name like it in the universe No other name can be compared to it Universal Acknowledgement Every knee will bow in homage and confession A name that sends humans to their knees Sovereign Corresponds the His position, honor, dignity, and glory God-glorifying: to the glory of God the Father The source of deity and authority manifested in the Son the Man Christ Jesus, who as Very God had voluntarily laid aside His expression of the glory of Deity during His incarnation, now has placed upon His shoulders all the majesty, dignity, and glory of Deity itself. It is the God-Man Who stooped to the depths of humiliation, Who is raised, not as God now, although He was all that, but as Man, to the infinite height of exaltation possessed only by Deity. We hear about Trump Derangement Syndrome That is nothing compared to the attacks on the name of Jesus for last 2,200 years TRANSITION: EXALTED POSITION, EXALTED NAME EXALTED PRAISE 2:10 at the name of Jesus Greek: in the name of Jesus What is the name ? Opinion is divided as to its identity First: Jesus; Jehovah is Salvation The name assigned at birth; the name of His redemptive mission His is the conquering name; the name which shall become victorious; the name which is destined to supremacy Second: the Lord Jesus Christ Lord with the Messianic title, Christ, and the name of His humanity Third: Lord Kennedy laments that the term Lord has become one of the most lifeless in the Christian vocabulary, whereas it really declares the true character and dignity of Jesus Christ and is the basis and the object of worship. How was it understood by the early church who operated by the LXX? Kurios was consistently used to translate Yahweh or Jehovah declares the true character and dignity of Jesus Christ and is the basis and the object of worship. Yahweh: a name the Jews held to be so sacred that then never mentioned it, never even wrote it wherefore v.9; the name was given because of His humiliation So that rules out Jesus; the name for His humiliation Kendrick: It refers to a name intrinsically significant of preeminent exaltation, a name which God bestowed, conferred upon Him as a mark of favor, and which accompanied His elevation. LORD is the confession which the apostle urges unbelievers must make when they receive Him as their Savior is the confession of Jesus as Lord Romans 10:9 Jehovah is the God of revelation; Exodus 3:14, 15 The tongue confesses to that to which the knee bows Isaiah 45:23 45:17, 18 It is Yahweh who is unique; the only One who creates, redeems, sustains It is this supremacy which is conferred on Jesus Used by Paul in Romans 14:11 For it is written, "'As I live,' says the Lord, 'to me every knee will bow. Every tongue will confess to God.'" The combination of tongues confessing and knees bowing gives evidence that the idea is a complete submission to Jesus, both in word and in action, and one that is required of all. Every knee and tongue: whether by choice or force MacDonald phrases it "Those who will not be reconciled in the day of His grace will be subjugated in the day of His judgment Divine Presence, Divine Majesty; the object of adoration and praise The day is coming when all who mocked Him, spit on Him, denied Him, used His name in vulgarity, denied His role as creator of the world, the creator of male and female, the One who defined family, will all be on their face before Him. Every knee and tongue without exception Edwards note "Again we see the vindication of servanthood. Christ was willing to make others more important than Himself and now God has made Christ eternally more important than anyone else." 2:11 The confession is not for salvation; that blessing has already been forfeited Someday, the entire universe (including Satan) will publicly declare and openly acknowledge Jesus as Lord, agreeing with God the Father regarding the testimony which He has given concerning His Son. Today is the day of salvation. All who confess Jesus as Lord do it by faith. The day is coming when ALL MEN and EVERY ANGEL will confess BY SIGHT that Jesus is Lord! CONCLUSION Remember this is a passage pleading for unity in the local church Jesus humbled Himself and the result was glory to God And the text of declaring Jesus as Lord is written to believers Is He Lord? Is He Lord of all? Without exception Thomas Constable writes that... The exaltation of Jesus Christ is as much a motivation for the Christian to live a life of submissive humility as is His incarnation. God will reward a life of self-denial. Motivation is the key. If we submit to God and to one another for the glory of God rather than for selfish glory, as Jesus did, our motivation is correct. The Definition of a Lowly Mind was to SELFLESS AND SERVING Our cure is maintaining the Mind of Christ To our unbelieving friends What if I asked you to place your money on Ohio State to beat Miami? It would be utter foolishness to bet on something when the outcome has already been determined. And yet millions of people bet their eternal destiny against an outcome that God has declared absolutely certain. Jesus Christ has been raised from the dead and is ascended to the right hand of God the Father where He awaits all of His enemies to be made His footstool (Ps 110:1 ). God s Word assures us that every knee will bow to acknowledge Jesus Christ as Lord. And yet people go on betting their eternal destiny against this sure word from God, living as their own lords and saviors, as if God s Word were uncertain or not true. KENOSIS AND THE INCARNATION Philippians 2:1-11 There was the cradle, then the cross, then the crown Acts 2:33-36 THE DEFINTION OF THE LOWLY MIND SELFLESS AND SERVING THE ILLUSTRATION OF THE LOWLY MIND SERVANTHOOD AND OBEDIENCE THE EXALTATION OF THE LOWLY MIND EXALTED POSITION 2:9 The highest exaltation possible Jesus Christ still bears the scars of His crucifixion Revelation 5:6-12 EXALTED NAME Name: denoting office, rank, dignity Transcendent Universal Acknowledgement Sovereign God-glorifying EXALTED PRAISE 2:10 Jesus; Jehovah is Salvation Lord Jesus Christ Lord Kurios: consistently used to translate Yahweh or Jehovah Jehovah is the God of revelation; Exodus 3:14, 15 Isaiah 45:23 Every knee and tongue: whether by choice or force The confession is not for salvation ============================================= === SERMON: message(180).txt ============================================= From: "Dwain Hill" Sent: 1/15/2026 1:44:39 PM To: Subject: message THE SCRIBE SLAM Luke 20:45-47 INTRODUCTION This text is an amazing departure from our study in Philippians 2 We go from lowliness of mind , consider the needs of others , humble , servant , death To long robes, best seats, best places The conversation turns from a local church to religious leaders The plea for unity in Philippians turns to one of warning to those who divide In Philippians, we saw Jesus as the perfect example of humility and now we see scribes as the perfect example of pride In Philippians, it was being selfless, serving, obedient, servanthood In Luke, it is the condemnation of the great pretenders The proud are all around us. In politics, in sports, in entertainment People of all stripes who have come to expect special privileges due to their self-elevated status Somehow they think their opinion is more important than the average Joe The entertainers use their award shows as a platform to share their ignorance with millions Politicians use what they call a hearing to pontificate for their 30 second video clip for the evening news Unfortunately, the church has not escaped the presence of those who consider themselves God s gift to the church, some who expect absolute loyalty from their followers, those who financially drain the people for their personal enrichment Leaders come in all shapes and sizes; egos naturally follow None of us are exempt from pride; from taking personal credit for our social status, or personal possessions. We pat ourselves on the back if our children turn out well, if we retire early, take exotic vacations, drive a Lamborghini and not a lemon. Review of Chapter 20 3 times the leaders have attempted to trap Jesus using word games The Question of Authority The Question of Taxes The Question of Marriage The whole chapter becomes a stand-off between Jesus and the leaders of Jewish worship Jesus is still sharing with the all the people. But a transition is coming From here, Jesus works with His disciples Here is one final message Not the first time in Luke where Jesus was critical of the leaders: 11:37-54 The Attention of the Crowd The text says all the crowd was listening ; all were hearing They were listening, enjoying what they were hearing But not necessarily to the place where they would believe or become followers They would soon become part of the Crucify crowd They were in one ear and out the other listeners May we not fall into the same trap: God, may give us ears to hear The Attack on the Scribes Both the Pharisees and Sadducees had scribes Luke provides a summary of the denunciation of the hypocritical religious leaders; Matthew 23 is the long version; 8 Woes Jesus is concluding His public ministry strong by pronouncing judgment on the leaders and their entire religious system, including the destruction of the symbol of the corrupt religious system, the temple and the whole city Matthew 23:37-39 note how Matthew concludes the teaching Luke 13:34, 35 very similar wording Luke 12:1 Jesus earlier warning of hypocrisy Hypocrisy: to wear a mask; assuming an identity one is not; play a part Impersonating; practice of deception Our relationship with Christ requires us to be an open book It is not about airing your dirty laundry, but having a sense of accountability In His denunciation, Jesus moves from doctrine to practice in public exposure; Using religion to advance their personal ambitions Beware these guys are like snakes in the grass; a fox in the hen house Condemned for the attitudes and actions contrary to OT Law Not every Bible expert is a Bible believer Acts 20:28-31 Paul issues a similar warning to the church Beware: present tense; a continual warning Take heed, pay attention, be alert, be on guard Be on guard because there is a real danger here Moses received the law and gave it to Joshua. Joshua received the law and gave it to the elders. The elders received the law and gave it to the prophets. The prophets received the law and gave it to the Pharisees and the Scribes. They were the treasurers of the Law of God; they were the experts They were the agents of God; they became the final authority In reality, they do not know God; they have no true spiritual wisdom So these men are to be avoided because they are always one thing on the outside and something else on the inside. They have nothing to offer spiritually and are destructive...deadly...dangerous. Do not get near them, because you will get singed, stained." - MacArthur Psalm 1:1 J C Ryle - This was a bold and remarkable warning. It was a public denunciation, we must remember, of men who sat in Moses seat, and were the recognized teachers of the Jewish people. It teaches us clearly that there may be times when the sins of people in high places make it a positive duty to protest publicly against them. It shows us that it is possible to speak out, and yet not to speak evil of dignities. Hypocrisy is the one sin on which Jesus comes down the hardest He was full of mercy for Zaccheus, the woman taken in adultery, Matthew the publican, the penitent thief, Saul, a persecutor of the church. But when He saw Scribes and Pharisees wearing a mere cloak of religion, and pretending to great outward sanctity, while their hearts were full of wickedness, His righteous soul seems to have been full of indignation. Whatever else we may be in religion, may we be true J. C. Ryle: However feeble our faith, and hope, and love, and obedience may be, let us see to it that they are real, genuine, and sincere. Let us abhor the very idea of part-acting and mask-wearing in our Christianity. At any rate let us be thorough. It is a striking fact that the very first piece of armor which St. Paul recommends to the Christian soldier is truth. Stand therefore, he says, having your loins girt about with truth. The Attention on the Scribes Putting on Airs: long robes; strutting around like roosters in a barnyard Long flowing robe with special markings of identification Matthew 23:5 But all their works they do to be seen by men.
